 वादेर्लोपि *etc* वादिलुप्ता is again आर्थी in the broader sense, since here neither of the two kinds (श्रौत and आर्थ) of उपमाद्योतक is stated, the उपमा is imphed only It is possible only in a समास, कर्मणि क्यच्, आधारे क्यच् क्यङ्, कर्माणि णमुल्, कर्तरि णमुल् In समास and णमुल there is real वादिलोप But as regards वादिलोप is क्यच् and क्यङ्, there as a difference of opinion among अलङ्कारिकाऽ *e g* साहित्यदर्पण -इह च यथादितुल्यादिविरहाच्छ्रौत्यादि विशेषचिन्ता नास्ति। इदं च केचिदौपम्यप्रतिपादवकस्येवादेर्लोपमुदाहरन्ति। तदयुत्तम्। क्यडादेरपि तदर्थविधितत्वेन औपम्यप्रतिपादकत्वाद् ! ननु वयडादिषु सम्यगौपम्यप्रतीतिर्नास्ति। प्रत्ययेन अस्वतन्त्रत्वादिवादिप्रयोगाभावाञ्च, इति न वाच्यम्। कल्पवादावपि तथा प्रभगात्।

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708340517वा.PNG"/>

 वादे gen sing वादि *i e *उपमाप्रतिपादकऽ like वा ( = इव), यथा, इव, सदृश etc

 वाशब्द etc वाशब्द उपमाद्योतव We have already given an instance of of used as उपमाद्योतक Another instance from the रामायणसिंह कुञ्जररुद्धो वा आस्थित (उत्तरवाद 36 42)

 वादे = उपमाप्रतिपादकस्य पद *viz* (1) समासेन, (2-3) कर्मणः उत्पन्नेन क्यचा and अधिकरणात् उपन्नेन क्यचा, (4) कर्तु क्यडा, (5-6) कर्मैण उपपदस्य णमुला and कर्तुम् उपपदस्य णमुल क्यचा, क्यडा, णमुला are instru. sings of क्यच्, क्यङ् and णमुल् कर्मण अधिकरणाच्च उत्पन्नेन क्यचा – The सूत्र in question is उपमानादाचारे, (३-९-९०) उपमानात्कर्मण सुबन्तादाचारार्थे क्यच् स्यात्। पुत्रभिवाचरति पुत्रीयति छात्रम् (बी) क्यच् is affixed to a noun which is an उपमान in the accusative, like which the उपमान is treated अधिकरणाच्चेति वक्तव्यम्, is a वार्तिक which means क्यच् is also affixed to a noun which is an उपमान in the locative, like which the उपमेय Is treated *e g* प्रासादीपति कुट्याभिक्षु' (को) कर्तु क्यदृा— The सूत्र in question कर्तुं क्यङ् (३-१-११) उपमानात्कर्तु मुबन्तादाचारे क्यङ् (वा) स्यात् (कौ) क्यङ् is applied to a noun which is an उपमान, like which the उपमेय (in the nom) behaves e g कृष्ण इव आचरति कृष्णायते् \[Nouns ending in स् like यशस्, drop their final स before it, e g यशायते, ओावायते \] कर्मकर्त्रोरपपदयोर्णमुला The सूत्र in question is उपमाने कर्मणि च (३-४-४५) चार पुतनिधाय निहित जलन घृतमिव सुरक्षितमित्यर्थ। अजकनाश नष्टः अजक इव नष्ट इत्यर्थ। णमुल is added to an उपमान, in the accu or nom which therefore is the object or subject of the action embodied in the णमुल कर्मवर्त्रो उपपदयो Loc Absolute कर्मकर्त्रो उपपदयो सतो when कर्मन् and कर्तृ are the उपपदऽ or the words prefixed to the णमुल्,

 ** St. 10 तत कुमुदनाथेन** *etc* Illustrates वादिलुप्ता समासगा (आर्थी, of course) where the साधारणधर्म and उपमान are compounded Description of moon rise कुमुदनाथेन in āpposition to चन्द्रेण (कुमुद = the moon lotus.) माहेन्द्री दिक् = the East. कामिनीगण्ड the cheek of a lovely lady चन्द्रेण कामिनीगण्डपाण्डुना embodies the उपमा

 Here चन्द्र गऽ the उपमेय, कामिनीगण्ड the उपमान, पाण्डुता the साधारणधर्म The वादि is dropped Since कामिनीगण्डपाण्डु is a समास, \[formed according to the सूत्र – उपमानानि सामान्यवचनै २-१-५५\], therefore वादिलुप्ता समासगा Note that in नेत्रानन्देन we have अतिशयोक्ति.

 ** St. 11 असित** *etc* Illustrates again वादिलुप्ता समासगा but where the उपमान and उपमेय along with the साधारणधर्म are compounded Description of a king getting fierce when confronted by enemies असित पत्र whose sword (असिपत्र) became fierce like a black (असित) cobra The black sword resembles the black cobra रुहरुहिका etc रुहरुहिकया (cf मराठी— रुखरुख) उत्पठया आहित (seized) चित्त तेन तूर्णं (quick) चार (movements) यस्य उत्कपोलकान्ति— उद्गता कपोलयो कान्ति यस्य प्रति भटविक्रम Valour of the hostile warriors

 Here असिपत्र is उपमेय, असितभुजग उपमान, भीषणत्व साधारणधर्म उपमाप्रतिपादक is dropped उपमेय, उपमान and साधारणधर्म being in a compound the उपमा is वादिलुप्ता समासगा

 **St 12 पौर सुतीयति** *etc* Illustrates वादिलुप्ता कर्मणि क्यचि in the first पाद वादिलुप्ता आधारे क्यचि in the second पाद and वादिलुप्ता क्याङ in the third पाद Description of the love of a king for his subjects, his bravery and its effect on hus enemies पौर सुतीयति – He treats the citzen(s) as if they were his sons \[सुत इव आचरति\]  
 Here पौर is उपमेय सुत उपमान, आचार (expressed by क्यच्) साधारणधर्म, the उपमाप्रतिपादक is dropped Since सुतीयति is a क्यच् formation with सुत as उपमान in the accusative (सुत इव आचरति) it is वादिलुप्ता कर्मणि क्यचि उपमा

 अन्त पुरीयति— Construe असौ समरान्तरे अन्त पुरीयति He behaves in the midst of a battle as in a harein Here समरान्तर in उपमेय अन्तपुर the उपमान आचार expressed by क्यच् (=freedom of movement) साधारणधर्म वादि is dropped अन्त पुरीयति is अधिकरणे क्यच् from अन्तपुर अन्त पुरे इव आचरति इति अन्त पुरीयति; therefore वादिलुप्ता अधिकरणे क्यचि विचित्रचरित्रचुञ्चु (*v l*) विचित्रचरित्रचर्च चुञ्चु an affix conveying the sense of well known for, reputed for. नारीयते *etc* Construe तस्य कृपाणपणे समरसीम्नि चरितानि आलोक्य सपनसेना नारीयते सपत्नमेना नारीयते— the hostile army becomes (as nervous) as a woman नारीयते = नारी इव आचरति is क्यङ् from नारी. The उपमेय is सपत्नसेना; उपमान नारी, साधारणधर्म आचार (मीनत्व) वादि is dropped Therefore वादिलुप्ता कर्तरि क्यङि, चरितानि *v l* लसितानि

 **St 13 मृधे निदाघ** *etc* Illustrates वादिलुप्ता कर्मणि उपपदे णमुलि and वादिलुप्ता कर्तरि उपपदे णमुलि Description of the terrifying influence of a king on the battle-field and his free movements there. मृधे (मृध n) in the battle परे enemmes निदाघधर्मांशुदर्शे is णमुल् gerund As they would look at the sun (घर्मांशु) in summer

 Here न is उपमेय निदाघघर्मांशु उपमान, दर्शन साधारणधर्म both embodied in णमुल्, वादि is dropped Therefore वादिलुप्ता कर्मणि उपपदे णमुलि (णमुल् with the object निदाघधर्मांशु as the prefixed word) स पुन पार्थसचारं सचरति— Illustrates वादिलुप्ता कर्तरि उपपदे णमुल पार्थसचार-पार्थं सचरति तथा is णमुल with कर्तृ prefixed in other words कर्तरि उपपदे णमुल, Here स is उपमेय, पार्थ उपमान, सचार साधारणधर्म, वादि is dropped Therefore वारिलुप्ता कर्तरि उपपदे णमुलि

 As said above, there is a controversy among rhetoricians as to whether in such cases we have real वादिलोप Are not these affixes by themselves taking the place, partly at least of the वादि ? सा द fully recapitulates the position in इह च etc cited above

 Really speaking वादिलोप takes place only in the compound and the णमुल् which also practically amounts to being a compound.

 **§ सूत्र ७ एवद्द्विलोपे** *etc* This gives धर्मवादिलुप्ता which is of 2 kinds एतयो द्वयो लोपे एतयो according to वृति, धर्मवाद्यो Note, however, that if एतयो were to refer to the two that have immediately preceded they are उपमान and वादि of सूत्रऽ ५ and ६ But we are to take them as the वृत्ति explains The fault lies not in the वृत्ति but in the कारिका

 धर्मवादिलुप्ता is possible in क्विप् and समास क्विप् is just like क्यच्, क्यङ् But, by a technicality of grammar, this affix is supposed to be elided according to the सूत्र-वेरपृक्तस्य— (६-१-६७) The simple fact is that क्विप् represents those denominative forms where the usual आय is absent *e.g* विधवति *etc* Read रामशरण तर्कवागीश,- यत्र कालपादिभि

 **उपमितसमास *and* रूपकसमास**— This leads us to the question of उपमितसमास and रूपकसमास मुखचन्द्र may be dissolved in two ways (1) as an उपमा (2) as a रूपक As an उपमा, it will be dissolved as मुख चन्द्र इव in which मुख i e उपमेय is प्रधान As a रूपक, it will be dissolved as मुख एवं चन्द्र where चन्द्र i e उपमान is प्रधान उपमासमास is formed as seen above, according to the सूत्र 'उपमित व्याघ्रादिभिः सामान्याप्रयोगे’ (२-१-५५) A noun denoting the उपमेय is compounded with the उपमान such as व्याघ्र, सिंह *etc* the common property not being stated

 रूपकसमास also called मयूरव्यंसकादिसमास is formed according to the सूत्र 'मयूरव्यंसकादयश्च' २-१-७२ (मयूरो व्यसवः धूर्त) which gives us some irregular कर्मधारयऽ Whether a compound like मुखचन्द्र should be dissolved as उपमा or रूपक depends on context If the context does not decide either way, then the आलङ्कारिकऽ would give it as an instance of सन्देहसङ्कर of उपमा and रूपक \[See further\]

 **§ सूत्र ८** Gives धर्मोपमानलुप्ता It is possible in two sub-divisions (1) in a compound (वृतौ), (2) and in a sentence It will be आर्थी since in the absence of the उपमान इव, यथा etc cannot be used We can understand the dropping of धर्म, which when it is well known can be dropped But the remarks made above in relation to उपमानलुप्ता apply equally to this present case.

 वृत्तौ मम्मट uses the word here in the sense of ‘compound’ though compound is only one of the five वृति or complex formations. (viz गृद्वृत्ति, धातुवृत्ति समासवृत्ति and एकदेशवृत्ति, \]

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708342088लुव.PNG"/>

 ** St 16 दुण्दुण्णन्तो** *etc* Illustrates धर्मोपमानलुप्ता समासगा, सं छाया -
दुदुलायमानो गरिष्यति A piece of advice to the Bee, not to hover about the leaving the blossom दुण्दुलायमान or दुदुलायमान may refer to the sound of the bee or mean merely परिभ्रमन् bristle with thorns. The s may also be an for a lover, not to carry on with one who may land him into trouble He should lavish his love on (possibly) and wherever it may be— are not stated Hence धर्मोपमानलुप्ता समासगा \[See उपमानलुप्ता above\]

 मालता कुसुमेन सम (सदृश) will give धर्मोपमानलुप्ता वाक्यगा So the वृति कुसुमेण सममिति etc The author suggests सम which is as good as सदृश (सरिच्छ) only for keeping the rmetre unimpared

 **§ सूत्र ९** क्यचि *etc* आसे = निरासे = लोपे This gives वादि-उपमेयलुप्ता which is possible only in क्यच् We saw above that had it not been. for a grammatical technicality this sub division had no justification To drop the उपमेय in an उपमा is to knock out the very bottom of an उपमा

 **St 17** **अराति** *etc* Illustrates क्यचि वाद्युपमेयलुप्ता Description of the bravery of a king in the battle अराति विलोचन whose eyes are dilated (विकस्वर) at the sight (आलोक) of the valour of the enemies कृपाणेन उदग्र भीषण दोर्दण्ड दण्डसदृशो बाहुर्यस्य सहस्त्रायुधीयति— सहस्रयुध इव आमान आचरति। सहस्रायुध is कार्तवीर्य अर्जुन of a thousand (arms. and) weapons

 Here सहस्रायुध is the उपमान, आचार, as in वादिलुप्ता, is the साधारणधर्म वादि is dropped, and the उपमेय too स cannot be grammatically the उपमय For सहस्रायुधीयति is a क्यच् which has to be dissolved as सहस्रायुध इव आचरति (not सहस्रायुध इव आचरति when it will be सहस्रायुधायने a क्यङ्) सहस्रायुध, the उपमान, has no उपमेय mentioned स cannot be उपमेय, because स is in the nom while सहस्रायुध is in the accu— the rule being that उपमान and उपमेय must be in the same case i e सामानाधिकरण्य) Therefore we have to fill in the gap by inserting आत्मानं स (आसान) सहस्रायुध (इव) आचरति Thus we get वादि उपमेयलुप्ता

 वृत्ति— अत्रात्मा उपमेय As seen above आमान has to be supplied to ensure सामानाधिकरण्य betweers स and सहस्रायुधम् Thus आमा (आमान) is the उपमेय, not स Grammatically स may be different from आ मा But from a practical stand point are they not the same? Some read सुसहस्रायुधयति and thus explain it as वाद्युपमेयलुप्ता since the उपमेय is dropped Read however सा द - अत्र सहस्रायुधमिवामानमाचरति इति वाक्ये उपमेयस्यामनो लोप। अत्र केचिदादुः। सहस्रायुधेन सह वर्तते इति ससहस्रायुध स इव आचरति इति वाक्याससहस्त्रायुधीयतीति पतसिद्धौ विशेष्यस्य शब्दानुपात्तत्वादिदोषमेयलोप इति तन्न विचारासहन् । कर्तार क्यच अनुशासनविरुद्धत्वात्।

 ** § सूत्र १०** विलोपे च *etc* When three *viz* वाति , धर्म and उपमान are dropped there can be समासगा उपमा only Note that this is also a gram matical possibility, not a rhetorical one The लोप of वादि and धर्म cars be well imagined as taking place in a compound; but the लोप of उपमान is a grammatical fiction, pure and simple. In fact, the उपमान— at least a trace of it-is present in this variety.

 **St. 18**. तरुणिमनि *etc.* Illustrates वादिधर्मोपमानलुप्ता. Description of a youthful maiden that ravished the mind of a sage. तरुणिमनि कृतावलोकना who had just glanced at youth i. e. was on the threshold of youth, ललित.............विग्रहा who had given (वितीर्ण) her person (विग्रह) to graceful charms i e, who exhibited graceful charms in her person, स्मर .........अन्तरा whose heart was bristling with showers of arrows of Cupid.

 Here the word मृगनयना illustrates वादिधर्मोपमानलुप्ता नयन is the उपमेय; मृगनयन the उपमान, but it is dropped-though in part-(मृगनयन being reduced to मृग în in the compound). The वादि is dropped; the साधारणधर्म *viz.* चञ्चलत्व is also dropped being well known. Thus it is वादिधर्मोपमान लुप्ता.

 अत्र सप्तम्युपमान *etc.* मृगनयना has to be dissolved as मृगनयने इव नयने यत्साः सा. Normally we would have expected मृगनयननयना . But due to a वार्तिक of कात्यायनः सप्तम्युपमानपूर्वपदस्य वहुव्रीहिरुत्तरपदलोपश्च, गृगनयना is reduced to मृग and thus we get the compound मृगनयना .

 But there are some, who explain æqual in another way. They say, मृग stands for मृगनयन by the process of लक्षणा. Therefore we have no उपमानलोप, they argue. According to them this will be merely an instance of वादिधर्मलुप्ता, रं गं thinks otherwise. He maintains that even supposing that मृग by लक्षणा stands for मृगनयन the compound मृगनयना continues to be त्रिलोपा उपमा— मृगपदस्य मृगचक्षुसदृश्यलक्षणिकत्वपक्षे वृत्तेर्विशिष्टार्थवाचकपक्षेऽपि स्वरूपमात्रबोधकपदभावात् त्रयाणां लोपः

 मृगनयन will be an instance of वादिधर्म उपमानलुप्ता only if we looked at it as compound formed according to the वार्तिक— सप्तभ्युपमान etc. That is why मम्मट says तदा इद उदाहरणम्, not if we explain मृग as मृगनयन by लक्षणा, the whole standing for a part.

 The वार्तिक means, a compound word, the first member of which is a noun in the locative or an उपमान, forms a वहुव्रीहि with another compound word. But in doing so, its (of the compound word) latter constituent is dropped.

 कण्ठेस्थ यस्य becomes ( = शिव), स्थ which is the latter part of कण्ठेस्थ being dropped, since कण्ठे is locative. This illustrates सप्तमीपूर्वपदस्प समीपदस्पतिरीरध मृगनयने इव नयने यस्या becomes मृगनयना Of मृगनयने, नयने which is the latter part is dropped, since मृग (though roughly speaking) is the उपमान This illustrates उपमानपूर्वेपदस्य बहुव्रीहिरुत्तरपदलोपश्च

 क्रूरस्याचारस्य etc We have seen one variety of त्रिलोपा, where उपमान, साधारणधर्म and वादि are dropped मम्मट refers to and refutes the view of some who give another त्रिलोपा wherein the उपमेय, साधारणथर्मं and वादि are dropped मम्मट shows that what they give as वादि धर्म उपमेयलुप्ता is no more than अतिशयोक्ति There is a word आय शूल्कि (one who is cruel in his behaviour) which is explained as अथ शूलेन अन्विच्छति (स्वीय) हितम् असौ Here अय शूल is recognused to stand for cruel conduct Therefore some look upon अय शूल – from which आय शूल्कि is derived as an instance of उपमेय साधारणधर्म वादिलुप्ता उपमा अय शूल which is उपमान is stated while क्रूर आचार the उपमेय, तैक्ष्ण्य the साधारणधर्म and वादि are dropped they argue

 मम्मट says that here we have no उपमा as such It is a case of अध्यवसान (= अध्यवसाय) *i e* supersession of the उपमेय by the उपमान which gives rise to अतिशयोक्ति To put it technically क्रूर आचार is अध्यवसित as अय-शूल, in this case In fact मम्मट seems to say that if this त्रिलोपा were admitted, no field will be left for the figure अतिशयोक्ति

 As we saw earlier, to deny उपमेय in an उपमा Is self contradictory Whatever pseudo instance is given has only a grammatical value not rhetorical Where the उपमेय is not there, but the उपमान is there, as in चन्द्रोऽयम्, we get अतिशयोक्ति due to the उपमेय being अध्यवसित as the उपमान

 एवमेकोनविंशतिर्लुप्ता पूर्णाभिः सह पञ्चविंशतिः।

पूर्णा 6 {3 श्रौती पूर्णा ( वाक्यगा, समासगा, सद्धितगा )  
     {3 आर्थी पूर्णा (वाक्यगा, समासगा, तद्धितगा )  

लुप्ता 19 {2 श्रौती धर्मलुप्ता ( वाक्यगा, समामगा )  
      {3 आर्थी धर्मलुप्ता ( वाक्यगा, समासगा, तद्धितगा )  
      {2 उपमानलुप्ता ( वाक्यगा, समासगा )--आर्थी only  
      {6 वादिलुप्ता (1 समासे, 2 क्यचि, 1 क्याडि, 2 णमुलि ) - आर्थी only  
      {2 धर्मवादिलुप्ता (क्विपि, समासे / आर्थी only  
      {2 धर्मोपमानतुप्ता ( समासे, वाक्ये ) – आर्थी only  
      {1 वाद्युपमेयलुप्ता ( क्यचि )-- आर्थी only  
      {1 त्रिलोपा *i e* वादिधर्मोपमानलुप्ता (समासे) -- आर्थी only.

 मम्मट now proceeds to draw attention to मालोपमा and रशनोपमा. In मालोपमा one उपमेय comes to be compared with several उपमानऽ (एकस्यैव बहुपमानोपादाने); while in रशनोपमा, we have a string of उपमानऽ, wherein the उपमेय in each preceding उपमा becomes the उपमान in the following one, the scheme being a: b; b: c; c; d; and so on. Each उपमा here is thus like a link interconnected with another in a chain or girdle, hence the name रशनोपमा \[आलङ्कारिकऽ do not admit a रशनोपमा where the उपमान of each preceding उपमा becomes उपमेय of each succeeding one, presumably because to turn an उपमान into उपमेय means lowering its status. Otherwise there is no reason why such a रशनोपमा should not be admitted.\]

 मालोपमा is possible in two ways. When the साधारणधर्म between the उपमेय and the different उपमानऽ is the same, or when the साधारणधर्मऽ are भिन्न, in the case of the different उपमानऽ. Similarly, रशनोपमा is possible when the साधारणधर्म is the same or different in the different constituent उपमाऽ

 मम्मट says he has not taken the trouble to define (न लक्षिता) these, because it is possible to have a thousand and one variations (वैचित्र्यसहस्र) like these, though fundamentally they do not go beyond the varieties specified (उक्तभेदानविक्रमाच्च)

 **St. 19.** अनयेनेव अभिन्ने साधारणे धर्मे मालोपमा. Description of a maiden who grew pale in sadness, दैन्येनेव मनस्विता — as high spirit gets cooled by poverty or helplessness. Here the same उपमेय viz. सा comes to be compared with several उपमानऽ viz राज्यश्री, मनस्विता and पद्मिनी; hence मालोपमा. The साधारणधर्म is ग्लानि which is अभिन्न in the different उपमाऽ.

 ** St. 20.** ज्योत्स्नेव *etc.* Illustrates भिन्ने साधारणे धर्मे मालोपमा. Description of a charming lady. प्रभुदेव समाकृष्टसर्वलोका who has drawn the whole world towards herself as sovereignty takes the whole world under its sway. नितम्बिनी beautiful lady. Here नितम्बिनी is उपमेय; ज्योत्स्ना, सुरा, प्रभुताः the उपमानऽ The साधारणधर्मऽ are different for the different उपमानऽ. viz. नयनानन्दत्व, मदकारणत्व, and समाकृष्टसर्वलोकत्व respectively.

 यथोत्तरमुपमानस्य etc. This explains रशनोपमा where the उपमेय of the preceding उपमा becomes उपमान of the following one ( यथोत्तरम् उत्तरोत्तरम्) पूर्वपदभिन्नभिन्नधर्मेत्वे = पूर्ववत् *i. c.* as in मालोपमा, here also the धर्म. *i. c.* साधारणधर्म may be अभिन्न or भिन्न

 **St. 21.** अनवरत etc. Illustrates अभिन्ने साधारणधर्मे रसनोपमा. Description of a king who is distinguished by his generosity, eloquence, intelligence *etc* अनवरत यत् कनकवितरण सुवर्णदान तदर्थं जललवभृते जलबिन्दुपूर्णे बरे हस्ते तरङ्गिता तरङ्गवमिलिता अर्थिनति याचकगण यस्य नृपस्य Note the रशना - भणति - मति-भति- चेष्टा-चेष्टा-कीर्ति Each उपमेय of the preceding उपमा becomes उपमान in the following उपमा The साधारणधर्म अतिविमला is the same for all उपमाऽ i e भणतिरिव मनिरतिविमला, मतिरिव चेष्टा अतिविमला, चेष्टेव कीर्तिरतिविमला

 **St 22 मतिरिव** *etc* Illustrates भिन्ने साधारणधर्मे रशनोपमा Description of the king continued तस्य नृपस्य goes with मति, मूर्ति, सभा and जयश्री Note the रशना - मति- मूर्ति- मूर्ति- सभा-नभा - जयश्री The साधारणधर्म for the different उपमाऽ are respectively मधुरत्व प्रमावचितत्व and परेषां जेतुमशक्यत्व

 इत्यादिका रशनोपमा च - Note how the वृत्ति reads  

 अनयेन इत्यभिन्ने साधारणे धर्मे, ज्योत्स्नेव इति भिन्ने च तस्मिन् (*i e* साधारणधर्मे), एकस्यैव बहुपमानोपादाने मालोपमा, यथोत्तरम् उपमेयस्योपमानत्वे पूर्वपदभिन्नभिन्नधर्मत्वे अनवरत इत्यादिया रशनोपमा च न लक्षिता

 एवविधवैचित्र्य *etc* One may just take note of एकदेशविवर्ति उपमा In this, we have the sunt explicit in some parts, implicit in others. Read मा च and also रुय्यक (अलङ्कारसर्वस्व P 92) मा च - एवविधेति भणनात् एकदेश-विवर्तिनी उपमा। यथा— नैत्रैग्विोत्पले पद्मै मुखैरिव सर. श्रिय। पदे पदे विभान्ति रम चक्रवाकैः स्तनैरिव॥अत्र सरश्रीणा नायिकात्व न समासोक्त्या विशेषणसाम्याभावात्। तस्मात् उपमैकदेशविवर्तिनी वाच्चैव। यथा अन्यै उक्ता।

 Regarding these माला and रशना forms मम्मट seems to have scant respect. He relegates them to the वृत्ति (*e g* मालारूपक, मालाप्रतिवस्तूपमा, मालानिदर्शना, मालादृष्टान्त) and does not define them except in the case of मालादीपक which, as we shall see, is not दीपक in माला form but a separate figure altogether Note how he brusquely brushes aside रशनारूपक (under रूपक) as having no charm भामह also says once— मालीपमादि सर्वोऽपि न ज्यायान् विस्तरो मुधा। का (२ ३८)

 उक्तभेदानतिक्रमाच्च— मम्मट is right in holding that in मालोपमा or रशनोपमा we have merely a combination of different उपमाऽ already dealt with Therefore, there is no point in separately defining मालोपमा and रशनोपना, he argues

**२ — अनन्वय**

 The name अनन्वय— absence of connection-gives but remotely an idea of the figure The figure consists in comparing a thing with itself it is Self comparison, One thing can be compared with another To compare a thing with itself appears, therefore, anomalous. But it is exactly in this anomaly that the essence of the figure lies When a thing is compared with itself, the natural conclusion one draws is that there is nothing else with which it can be compared In other words it is unparalleled. The name अनन्वय is explained in two slightly different ways मम्मट himself in the वृत्ति says that it is so called because it involves the negation of connection (सम्बधाभावः) with any उपमान excepting itself Others like रुय्यक (अलङ्कारसर्वस्व), अप्पय्य दीक्षित (कुवलयानद) explain the name as refering to the incongruity (अनन्वितत्व) inherent in comparing a thing with itself The word अनन्वित, in Marathi, does still convey this idea of ‘unmatched,' ‘unequalled' (e g अनन्वित छल, अनन्वित कृर्त्ये)

 भरत and दण्डिन् admitted the figure in substance, if not in name, when they respectively recognised सदृशी उपमा and असाधारणोपमा भामह was the first to recognise the name उद्भट admitted it रुद्रट listed it as a variety of उपमा Later आलकारिक uniformly admit it.

 ** Essentials**— अनन्वय requires the following things (1) The उपमान and उपमेय will be one and the same, (2) and that too in the same sentence e g रामरावणयोर्युद्ध रामरावणयोरिव In उपमेयोपमा, which follows this figure we have the same thing as उपमान and उपमेय but not in one वाक्य as here but in two (3) Again the उपमान and उपमेय should be in the same words, as far as possible Synonyms, though not forbidden, detract from the charm of the figure Cf सा द किन्तु अत्र उचितत्वात् एकशब्दप्रयोग एव श्रेयान्। He cates अनत्वये च शब्देक्यमौचित्या दानुषङ्गिकम्।

 It would thus seem that अनन्वय which, in fact, was a development of the उपमा, (See जगन्नाथ's treatment of the classified figure), must be put in the form of an उपमा— तस्या मुख तस्या मुखमिव This औपम्य may be आर्थ also as in मुख आत्मनैव तुल्यमभवत्. But it should not be put as स न केनापि अन्येन तुल्य Though in essence अनन्वय implies the status without parallel, of a thing, स न केनापि तुल्य will be relegated to the figure असम by some (मम्मट does not admit it), while others will bring it under उपमानलुप्ता उपमा

 अनन्वय and उपमा— Though in both of them comparison is there, in उपमा, there is real comparison, for one thing is compared with other e g मुख चन्द्र इव In अनन्वय on the other hand, मुख मुख इव, there is merely a semblance of comparison for to compare a thing with itself is unnatural in the extreme Thus in उपमा is admitted the comparability of a thing with another, while in , अनन्वय is implied the incomparability of the thing in question

 अनन्वय and उपमेयोपमा— See under उपमेयोपमा

 वृत्ति— उपमानान्तर etc This explains the name of the figure. अन् + अन्वय implies the negation (अभाव) of connection (अन्वय = सम्बन्ध) with any other उपमान, excepting itself We have already seen how रुय्यक and अप्पय्य दीक्षित explain the name in a sightly different manner

 § सूत्र ११ Construe एकस्यैव एकवाक्यगे उपमानोपमेयत्वे अनन्वय भवति।

 एकवाक्यगे उपमानोपमेयत्वे may be taken as Loc Sing meaning when the same thing is उपमान and उपमेय both, in one sentence the figure is अनन्वय Or एकवाक्यगे उपमानोपमेयत्वे may be Nom Dual standing in apposition to अनन्वय अनन्वय consists in the उपमानत्व and उपमेयत्व of one and the same thing in a sentence In whatever way we construe, the sense is the same

 **St 23** न केवल *etc* Illustrates अनन्वय Description of a lady of unrivalled beauty and grace The अनन्यय consists in नितम्बिनी नद्विलासा नितम्विनीव मानि। तद्विलासा इव (भान्ति) Here the same thing नितम्बिनी Is उपमान and उपमेय both Also the same thing नद्विलासा is उपमान and उपमेय both This naturally results in conveying the idea that the नितम्विनी is matchless and so the विलासऽ also Cf मा च - अत्र नितम्बिनी निम्बिनी इति विलासा विलासा इव इति भेदाभावात् उपमानतया अनुपपद्यमान अयव्यावृत्तौ लक्षणया अवतिष्ठते।

**३ - उपमेयोपमा**

 In उपमेयोपमा, we have two things compared one with the other, each in turn beng उपमान and उपमेय like बमलेव मति मति इव कमल, with the ultimate purpose of showing that they cannot be compared with any third thing If I say तस्या मुख चन्द्र इव चन्द्रस्तस्वा मुख इव, I imply thereby, generally speaking, that there is no third thing with which they can be compared— Read मा चम् अत्र पूर्ववाक्यात्मादृश्यावगतौ पुनरिह सादृश्यमुच्यमानम् अनन्यार्थत्वे सति अन्यव्यावृतिं लक्ष्यति। अत्र पूर्वत्र च ण्तयो सदृश नन्यदति भाव।

 In a normal उपमा we have उपमानेन उपना Here we have उपमेयेत उपमा, as मम्मट himself says in the वृत्ति Due to this extraordinary feature it is called उपमेयोता In यहानेर मति मति एक बहान्न, what was उपमेय in the first उपमा viz मति becomes उपमान in the following

 दण्डिन accepted a division of उपमा called अयोयोमा which approximates to उपमेयोता भामह admitted उपयोपमा for the first time in name and substance उद्भट, बामन and रुद्रट have all admitted it.

 Essentials— (उपमेयेन उपमा) In उपमेयोपमा (1) there are two उपमाऽ, (2) in these two the उपमान and उपमेय exchange places the उपमान in one being the उपमेय in the other and rice versa, (3) the purpose underlying this exchange of places of the उपमान and उपमेय is to exclude any third thing as being similar to them अस्याश्च उपमानान्तरतिरस्कार एव फलम्, says जयरथ This requirement results in excluding सविता विधवति विधुरपि सवितरति from the scope of this figure For there though उपमान and उपमेय exchange places, it is not to exclude a third उपमान but only to show the perverse effect of सवितृ and विधु on one who is सुखदुखवशीकृतमनस् (4) Since there are two उपमाऽ two वाक्यऽ are necessary But the two वाक्यऽ may be explicit or implicit Read जयरथ।

 स च वाक्यभेद शाघ्य आर्थश्च। तत्र शाब्दो यथा— रसोभि स्यदनोद्धूतै गजैश्च धनसनिभै। भुवस्तलमिव व्योम कुर्वन्व्योमेव भूतलम्॥ आर्थो यथा— भवपादाश्रयादेव गप भक्तिश्च शाश्चता। इतरेतरसादृश्यसुभगामेति वन्द्यताम्॥अत्र स्फुटेऽपि शाब्दे एकवाक्यत्वे गङ्गा भक्तिवत् भक्तिश्च गङ्गावत् वन्द्या इति अस्ति एवम् आर्थो वाक्यभेद। अस्याश्च उपमानान्तरतिरस्कार एव फलम् । अत एव उपमेयेन उपमा इति अस्या अन्वर्थाभिधानम्। यत्र पुन उपमानान्तरतिरस्कारो न प्रतीयते यत्र नायमलङ्कार यथा सविता विधवति विधुरपि सवितरति इयत्र। रं ग llustrates आर्थ वाक्यद्वय with नयनद्वय परस्परामना तुलनामुदञ्चति।

 (5) The *sameness* of words will indeed heighten its charm तस्या मुखं तस्या मति इव तस्या मति तरया मुखमिव is better than तस्या मुख तस्या मति इव तस्या प्रज्ञा तस्या मुख इव or तस्या मति तस्या वदनमिव (6) The figure must tend to exalt an object Read रं ग— श्य च उपमेयोपमा यदि वस्यापि अर्थस्य उत्कर्षधायिका तदा अलङ्कार।

 उपमेयोपमा *and* अनन्वय

 अनन्वय— तस्या मुखं तस्या मुरा इव।

 उपमेयोषमा— तस्या मुख तस्या मति इव तस्या मति तस्या मुखमिव ।

 In both no doubt the same thing is उपमान and उपमेय But while in अनन्वय, the same thing is उपमान and उपमेय in one वाक्यऽ In अनन्वय the effect Is द्वितीयसदृशव्यवच्छेद In उपमेयोपमा the effect is तृतीयसदृशव्यवच्छेद

 § सूत्र १२ विपर्यास etc Construe तयो (उपमानोपमेययो) विपर्यास उपमेयोपमा विपर्यास is परिवृत्ति exchange of places The same thing becomes उपमान and उपमेय both

 वृत्ति— परिवृत्ति अथात् वाक्यद्वये – The exchange of places of उपमान and उपमेय naturally presupposes two वाक्यऽ. In one and the same वाक्य, भिन्न उपमान and उपमेय cannot exchange places जयरथ (and जगन्नाथ also) say that the वाक्यद्वय may be explicit or implicit

 इतरोपमानव्यवच्छेदपरा— This is the very life of the figure As we saw above, mere exchange of places will not suffice as in मविता विधवति etc The exchange of places must be with the intent of excluding a third thing as उपमान Read मा चं cited above

 रं ग says एकेन धर्मेण एक्प्रतियोगिके परानुयोगिके सादृश्ये निरूपिते अपरप्रतियोगि कस्य एकानुयोगिकस्यापि तेन धर्मेण सादृश्यस्य अर्धन सिद्धतया शब्देन पुन तदुक्तिः स्वनैरर्थक्य परिहाराय तृतीयसदृशव्यवच्छेदमाक्षिपति ।

 उपमेयेन उपमा— As seen above, this is the distinctive feature of this figure

 **St 24** कमलेव *etc* Illustrates उपमेयोपमा Description of the wealth etc of a king (यस्य) यस्य to be construed with all कमला wealth. विभा splendour कमला comes to be compared with मनि and vice versa Similarly and with similar effect, तनु comes to be compared with विभा and vice versa धरणी with धृति and vice versa This tends to show that they cannot be compared with a third thing. Construe यस्य कमलेव मति मतिरिव कमला, and so on.

**४ - उत्प्रेक्षा**

 All the three figures dealt with until now were उपमाऽ, if not in substance at least in form In fact, as we said earlier, Rhetoricians like दण्डिन् looked upon them as varieties of उपमा With उत्प्रेक्षा, we pass on to a figure which has the element of उपमा in it, no doubt, but which is highly more poetical than उपमा.

 Its higher poetical effect is to be explained thus उपमा, अनन्वय and उपमेयोपमा are all भेदाभेदप्रधान. We have seen how उपमा is such, and as the other two also are उपमाऽ in form, they too show the same feature In उत्प्रेक्षा, we get a figure wuch is अभेदप्रधान and if अतिशयोक्ति, in which the उपमेय is swallowed up by the उपमान, be taken to be the height of poetical effect in the direction of अभेद, then उत्प्रेक्षा is indeed, a milestone on the way and, therefore, is to be assessed at a higher poetical value than उपमा In fact रुय्यक says that in उत्प्रेक्षा we have साध्य अध्यवसाय, while in अतिशयोक्ति it is सिद्ध,

 Rhetoricians say that in उत्प्रेक्षा, the poet makes a headway towards अभेद, in so far as उत्प्रेक्षा is based on सम्भावन *i e* poetical fancy सम्भावन, say the आलङ्कारिक, the poet's mind is in a state of suspense or incertitude of course आहार्य or intentional and not स्वारसिक (or वास्तविक or actual) wavering between the उपमान and the उपमेय but with a greater bias towards the उपमान In other words in उत्प्रेक्षा the poet seems to say It may be the उपमान मुख चन्द्र मन्ये Thus state of Incertitude of the mind may be conveyed by नून, मन्ये, शङ्के, ध्रुव, प्राय etc The majority of such words are given by दण्डिन् and others—

मन्ये-शङ्के ध्रुव-प्रायो-नूनमित्येवमादिमि। उत्प्रेक्षा व्यज्यते शब्दैरिवशब्दोऽपि तादृश॥ The name is explained as उत्कटा प्रकृष्टत्य (= उपमानस्य) ईक्षा ज्ञानमुत्प्रेक्षा

 Rhetoricians generally classify उत्प्रेक्षा as—

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708348771उ.PNG"/>

 In स्वरूपोत्प्रेक्षा, as in मुख चन्द्र मन्ये, one thing comes to be fancied as another in हेतूपेक्षा, the cause of a particular fact comes to be fancred as something other than the real one in फ्लोत्प्रेक्षा, the effect of some thing comes to be fancied as other than the real one So many rhetoricians multiply these divisions by basing them on the classification anto जाति, गुण, क्रिया, and द्रव्य सा द gives 96

 स्वरूपोत्प्रेक्षा is illustrated by लिम्पनीव etc, हेतूत्प्रेक्षा by उन्मेषं यो etc फलोत्प्रेक्षा may be illustrated by प्रायोऽब्ज त्वत्पदेनैक्य प्राप्तु तोथे तपस्यति। where the फल, कामिनीचरणप्राप्ति is उत्प्रेक्षित

 But, as र ग says, the charm hes only in these three divisions चमत्कारवैलक्षण्यं पुनर्हेतुफलस्वरूपाभवाना त्रयाणा प्रकाराणामेवेति। मम्मट does not enter anto these divisions at all Nonetheless his definition - प्रकृतस्य समेन सम्भावन is comprehensive and yet precise enough to include all is these divisions. For, it not सम्भावन or poetical fancy that plays part in all of these?

 उत्प्रेक्षा *and* उपमा - As we saw above, उपमा is भेदाभेदप्रधान, while उत्प्रेक्षा IS अभेदप्रधान In one resides औपम्य in the other सम्भावन In उपमा like मुखं चन्द्र इव we have a certain knowledge that मुख and चन्द्र are भिन्न, while in at there is a certain incertitude (of course, intentional or आहार्य) This incertitude nught be conveyed by the use of verbs like मन्ये, उद्दे, तर्कयामि, शङ्के or particles like ध्रुव, नूनं प्राय It may also be conveyed by इव used after verbs For, the Grammanans have laid it down that इव used after verbse e g लिम्पति इव conveys सम्भावन and not औपम्प नोपमानं तिङन्तेन they say Read दण्डिन् का II 227 ff

 But there are so many उत्प्रेक्षा where इव is used after nouns. How are such cases to be distinguished from उपमा। There is a safe criterion When the उपमान is such as subsists only in the poet s fancy an other words, is लोकानेकान्तगोचर, we should decade the figure to be उत्प्रेक्षा When कालिदास describes the हिमलय as पृथिव्या इव मानदण्ड *i. e.* measuring rod of the earth, as though, we can see that be refers to

a possibility of the poetic realm, when अग्निमित्र, at the end of Act I of the मालविकाग्निमित्र, describes the मुरजवाद्यनाद as स्वमनोरथम्य शब्द इव, we have again an instance of उत्प्रेक्षा For, none but a poet can fancy a measuring rod of earth, and none but a poet can fancy a 'call' of aspirations We know of measuring rods but not of earth, we know of calls but not of aspirations To put it otherwise, when इव has to be interpreted as ‘as if’ and not ‘like’ the figure is उत्प्रेक्षा So पाक्शासन इव is उपमा, but अपर पाक्शासन इव is उत्प्रेक्षा, for अपर पाक्शासन— is possible only in कविसृष्टि

 Like उपमा, उत्प्रेक्षा has been recognised by all ancient Rhetoricians, except भरत भामह and दण्डिन् admitted it and so did the rhetoriciar's that followed them भामह, possibly by the presence of इव etc in उत्प्रेक्षा, was led to describe it as ‘उपमया सह’. It may be stated in passing that the word सम्भावन which मम्मट uses was first used in that sense by उद्भट (circa 800 A D) ‘सम्भावनेयमुप्रेक्षा’

 For उत्प्रेक्षा *vs* भ्रान्तिमत् see under भ्रान्तिमत् For उत्प्रेक्षा vs ससन्देह see under ससन्देह For उत्प्रेक्षा vs अतिशयोक्ति see under अतिशयोक्ति For उत्प्रेक्षा vs अनुमान see under अनुमान

 **Essentials**— \[उत्कटा प्रकृष्टस्य (उपमानस्य) ईक्षा ज्ञानमुत्प्रक्षा\] (1) As we saw above, in उत्प्रेक्षा, the स्वरुप, हेतु or कार्य may be fancied to be other than the actual one, (2) being a fancy the idea is आहार्य or intentional (3) The fancy may be expressed by used after verbs, by इव used after उपमानऽ that are possible in the realm of the poet, by words like मन्ये, शङ्के, ऊहे, तर्कयामि. In the case of these words मन्ये etc however, one must be cautious Read रय्यक— किन्तु उत्प्रेक्षासामग्यभावे मन्येशब्दप्रयोगो वितर्कमेव प्रतिपादयति। *i e* मन्ये must be used to convey poetical fancy and not mere conjecture (4) In some cases e g त्वत्कीर्तिभ्रमण श्रान्ता विवेश स्वर्गानिम्नगाम्। none of the words may be there, and yet we get उत्प्रेक्षा which is, therefore, गम्या as distinct from the वाच्या where these words are used See below

 § सूत्र १३ Construe अथ प्रकृतस्य समेन यत् सभावन सा उत्प्रेक्षा

 In उत्प्रेक्षा, प्रकृत or matter under description is fancied as being something else similar to it

 अथ— Until now the three figures dealt with were based on साधर्म्य which is भेदाभेदप्रधान Now the author proceeds to deal with figures that are अभेदप्रधान Hence the use of अथ समेन *i e* अप्रकृत

 सम्भावन— As we saw above, it involves a state of suspense, with a greater bias towards the उपमान जयरथ in hus gloss on रुय्यक's

अल्कारसर्वभ्व explains it as follows: सभावना हि एकतरपक्षशिथिलीकारेण पक्षान्तरदाढर्थेन। च प्रादुर्भवतीत्यस्या साध्याध्यवसायतुल्यक्क्षत्यम्।

 **St. 25** उन्मेषं etc. Illustrates उत्प्रेक्षा (हेतुत्प्रेक्षा). A poetical fancy about the lotus like grace of the feet of a maiden. The poet imagines that पद्मलक्ष्मी is clinging to the feet of the नायिका, because the नायिका has vanquished the moon— a born enemy of the day lotus— by the beauty of her face. Construe : य (इन्दु) मम (कमलस्य) जातिवेरी (मन्) निशाया उन्मेषं (= विराम) न सहते, तत्य (इन्दोः) सौन्दर्यदर्पः इन्दीवरदलदृशा (कमलपत्रदृशा) अनया (नायिकया) वक्त्रकान्त्या प्रसभ शान्ति नीत इनि हर्षात् ते (नायिकाया) पादयो पद्मलक्ष्मीः लग्ना इति मन्ये। उन्मेष bloom. प्रसभे शान्तिं नीतः was at once quelled, or suppressed.

 This is an उत्प्रेक्षा (to be more precise हेतुत्प्रेक्षा) where the lotus-like beauty of the feet of the lady, though due to nature, comes to be fancied as being due to joy on the part of the lotus. The real हेतु for the beauty is Nature. The poet fancies (c p.the word मन्ये) it to be joy of the lotus due to the नायिका defeating the moon

 **St 26** लिम्पतीव etc. Another illustration of उत्प्रेक्षा (स्वरूपो प्रेक्षा)— in the second line, however, we have an उपमा One fails to see why मम्मट, who does not bother about the divisions of उत्प्रेक्षा, should give one more instance. Possibly, the instance that received so much attention at the hands of earlier Rhetoricians— particularly दण्डिन्— could not be well ignored by मम्मट.

 Description from the मृच्छकटिक, of the dark night when वसन्तसेना was, as an अभिसारिका, moving towards चारुदत्त's abode. तमः अङ्गानि लिम्पतीवः— So pitchy dark it was that the limbs seemed to get coated with it नभ अञ्जनं पर्यतीय- the sky seemed to shower collynum-such was the all pervading darkness, दृष्टिर्विफलता गता *i. e.* one cannot see with one's eyes due to darkness.

 The उत्प्रेक्षा is present in the first half; in the second, we have उपमा. इव following a verb, as we have already seen, gives rise to उत्प्रेक्षा. Here and अधप्रसरण of come to be fancied as अङ्गत्वेन or अञ्जनधारा
This seems to be the उत्प्रेक्षा, according to मम्मट, to judge from his वृत्ति

 But there are others who say that the उत्प्रेक्षा not in looking upon ध्यायन *etc*. as लेपन and so on, but in attributing to the inanimate तमग् and नमन् They say and नमन being inanimate cannot be of acts. Here they are so described, and

there lies the उत्प्रेक्षा मा चं seems to be of this view, which he justifies But कुवलयानन्दकार seems to favour the former view, though he refers to the latter in passing

 अत्र व्यापनादि *etc* आदि includes अधप्रसरण व्यापन comes to be looked upon as लेपन, अधप्रसरण as (अञ्जन) वर्षण But कुवलयानन्द seems to take व्यापन alone fancied in two ways as लेपन and वर्षण As we saw above, मा च does not agree with me in his estimate of the उत्प्रेक्षा in this stanza

 Here it may be noted that in both these instances the matter under consideration, the प्रकृत thing, is not stated. स्वभावलग्नव and तमोव्यापन both of which are are not stated in these particular stanzas Later we shall come to the figure अतिशयोक्ति of the first variety where also the प्रकृत thing is अन्तर्निगीर्ण by the अप्रकृत What then, is the difference between the two?

 We have already seen that in उत्प्रेक्षा and अतिशयोक्ति (I), we have अध्यवसान but whereas in अतिशयोक्ति it is accomplished (सिद्ध), in उत्प्रेक्षा it is in the making (साध्य) This साध्यत्व is conveyed by words like इव, मन्ये, ध्रुव etc all of which equally hint at the hesitating tone of the poet which is conspicuously absent in अतिशयोक्ति (I) Thus even where the प्रकृत is not mentioned the bare presence of words like इव etc is enough to distinguish the figure in question from अतिशयोक्ति 

 Thus though in the norm मुख चन्द्र मन्ये, the प्रकृत is mentioned it may be dropped in cases where it can be easily imagined To be more categorical, in स्वरुपोत्प्रेक्षा, the प्रकृत may or may not be stated, as in मुख चन्द्र मन्ये and लिम्पतीव तमोऽङ्गानि respectively In हेतूप्रेक्षा and फलोत्प्रेक्षा the प्रकृत is not stated at all To state the real हेतु or फल will detract from the poetical effect But then the words like इव etc are enough to convey the उत्प्रेक्षा

 This brings us to the so-called गग्या उत्प्रेक्षा admitted by र ग and कुवलयानद e g त्वकीर्ति- र्भ्रमणश्रान्ता विवेश स्वर्गनिम्नगाम् When इव etc. are not there when the प्रकृत is not there, one may venture to say that here we have, for all practical purposes cases of अतिशयोक्ति , for the case becomes one of अध्यवसान and not सभावन

**५ - ससन्देह**

 We have already seen that in मालोपमा one and the same उपमेय comes to be compared with several उपमानऽ ससन्देह is, in essence, मालोपमा Therein the उपमेय comes to be compared with several उपमानऽ But whereas in मालोपमा the poet straightway compares the

उपमेय with several उपमानऽ, in ससन्देह the transition from one उपमान to another is rendered more poetical by being put in the form of a doubt, the different उपमानऽ being merely the various alternatives between which and the उपमेय the poet s mind wavers

 It is true that मुख वा चन्द्रो वा is an instance of ससन्देह But an effective ससन्देह arises only when we have the उपमेय doubted as being one उपमान after another Note that the आलङ्कारिकऽ do not, as a rule, recognise मालाससन्देह indicating thereby that several उपमानऽ form but the essence of ससन्देह

 But it is to भिन्ने साधारणे धर्मे मालोपमा, that ससन्देह bears close affinity and for obvious reasons. The very purpose of ससन्देह is to focus our attention to the different facets of the The उपमेय may be exalted from various angles. From each angle it comes to be compared with a different उपमान The comparisons are set forth in the form of doubts with the sole purpose of facilitating transition from one उपमान to another,र ग however, admits ससन्देह where the same साधारणधर्म may be present in the different alternatives, Read र ग— अस्मिंश्च संशये नानाकोटिषु क्वचिदेक एन समानो धर्म।

 Thus the instance of भिन्ने साधारणे धर्मे मालोपमा—

**ज्योत्स्नेव नयनानन्द सुरेव मदकारणम्।  
प्रभुतेव समाकृष्टसर्वलोका नितम्बिनी॥**

 may be put in the ससन्देह form as—

**इय ज्योत्स्ना किम् इय सुरा किम्, इय प्रभुता किम्**

 When विक्रम (विक्रमोर्वशीय IV 62 ) finds the सगमनीयमणि what arrests his attention is the extreme red colour and extreme refulgence So he doubts in his mind as to whether it is a morsel of flesh (well known for its redness) or a spark of fire (well known for its refulgence) प्रमालेपी नाय हरिहतमृगस्यामिपलव, स्फुलिङ्ग स्यादग्नेर्गगनमभिकृष्ट पुनरिदम्। In this way an effective ससन्देह aims at drawing our attention to the different traits present in the प्रकृत thing

 We see that there is doubt in ससन्देह But the question arises is the doubt genuine or simulated? To put it in आलङ्कारिक terminology is it आहार्य or स्वारसिक(= वास्तविक)? र ग is specific when he says अय च क्वचिदाहार्य। यत्र हि कविना परनिष्ठमयो निर्बाध्यते प्रायस्तत्रानाहार्य।

 यत्र च स्वगत एव तत्राहार्यम्। What is meant is that the poet may pretend that he is in doubt, or he may describe another party like विकास, in the above instance as being in *genuune* doubt. In both

cases the result secured is the same, viz. drawing the reader's attention to the various facets of the उपमेय

 **Essentials**— (ससन्देह= संशयेन सह वर्तते इति) (1) There must be a doubt, genuine (स्वारसिक) or simulated (आहार्य), with the mind wavering between the different alternatives (2) It must be poetical; mere स्थाणुर्वा पुरुषो वा cannot enjoy the status of an अलकार (3) The प्रकृत or उपमेय must, as far as possible, b one as possesses different angles on account of which it is suspected to be this उपमान and that. (4) While passing from one उपमान as it was suspected to be (or differentiating circumstance) may or may not be given. Thus in the above Instance प्रभालेपी etc, प्रभालेपी gives the reason why the प्रकृत is not आभिषलव (Flesh is always dull red) अभिवृष्ट वनमिद gives the reason why it is not, (which cannot burn when it is raining) In अस्या सर्गविधौ etc, no reason s given, the poet's mind only' wavering between one possibility and another चन्द्र, मदन and पुष्पाकरमास

 Divisions of ससन्देह–This brings us to the divisions of ससन्देह In some cases there may be mere transition from one उपमान to another without a statement of the difference (भेद), this is शुद्ध variety where no निश्चय as to the identity of the प्रकृत is arrived at In this absence of निश्चय, शुद्ध resembles निश्चयगर्भ In some cases the transition may be accompanied and accounted for by the difference (भेद ) between उपमेय and उपमान (भेदोक्तौ) When thus भेद is stated, sometimes the poet may go on indulging in doubts one after another, without coming to a final decision as to the identity of the उपमेय (निश्चयगर्भ) Sometimes, he may come to such a decision as to the identity (निश्चयान्त) This gives us the following scheme—

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1707747917ससन्देह.PNG"/>

रुय्यक, in his अलङ्कारसर्वस्व, is explicit on these divisions Read— च त्रिविध। शुद्धो निश्चयगर्भो निश्चयान्तश्च। शुद्धो यस्य सशये एव पर्यवसानम्। निश्चयगर्भं य संशयोपक्रम निश्चयमध्य संशयान्तश्च।

 मम्मट, unlike उद्भट, seems to be for admutting निश्रयान्त उद्भद holds, according to the वृत्ति, that in निश्चयान्त the identity of the उपमेय being established, nothing is left to our suggestion निश्रयान्त, therefore, is less poetical than निश्रयान्त, argues उद्भट - so the वृत्ति says

 The term निश्चयगर्भ requires elucadation निश्चय गर्भे मध्ये यस्य सा द calls it निश्रयान्त When passing from one उपमान to another, the poet arrives at a निश्चय, that the उपमेय is not the first उपमान and so proceeds to a second and so on In this way,निश्चय -negative though it be that it is not the first उपमान second उपमान and so on-lies sandwiched between two संशयऽ Hence it is called निश्चयगर्भ Read सा द — यत्र आदौ अन्ते च सशय एक, मध्ये निश्चय स निश्चयमध्य। Really speaking, निश्चयगर्भ, if interpreted in this way, is not exclusive of निश्रयान्त For in निश्चयगर्भ also do we not get निश्चय sandwiched between two सशय ? Thus just as in अय मानण्ड etc there is निश्चय between the two सशयऽ viz अय मार्तण्ड किं and अय कृशानु किम्, So in इन्दु किं etc there is निश्चय between इन्दु कि and सरसिज किम् Will it not be proper, therefore to take—though against accepted tradition — निश्चयगर्भ as व्यङ्ग्यनिश्चय and निश्रयात as वाच्यनिश्चय, निश्चय being final decision ?

 **ससन्देह** *and* **उत्प्रेक्षा** - We have seen that in सम्भावन which forms the basis of उत्प्रेक्षा, there is a state of incertitude with a greater leaning towards the उपमान To put it in तार्किक terminology, we have there an उह्न which is described as उत्कटैक्तोटिक (wherein one extremity is felt more prominently) In ससन्देह, on the other hand, we have a state of incertitude wherein the mind wavers equally between the उपमान and the उपमेय, without a special leaning towards either If the poet were imagined as marching towards (or अध्यवसान) that is reached in the figure अतिशयोक्ति, then ससन्देह marks a stage earlier than उत्प्रेक्षा Since the mind wavers equally between the उपमान and the उपमेय in ससन्देह, whereas it leans more towards the उपमान in उत्प्रेक्षा, ससन्देह is one stage earlier than उत्प्रेक्षा This difference manifests itself in the different modes of expression While in ससन्देह, the suspense is expressed in the form of a doubt इद किं, इद किं, in उत्प्रेक्षा it is put in the form of a fancy expressed by means of words like इव, ध्रुव, नु etc It must be confessed, however that in certain cases even with these words we get ससन्देह e g अस्या सर्गविधी etc where we find नु is used to express doubt रुय्यक does refer to a view according to which these also are taken as instances of उत्प्रेक्षा— अन्ये तु सुशब्दस्य सम्भावनाद्योतकमत्त्वात्प्रेक्षामकारमिममाचक्षते।

 **ससन्देह** *and* **उपमा** – As we saw at the outset, ससन्देह is *inessence* मालोपमा itself But whereas in मालोपमा, there is no element of incertitude or doubt, In ससन्देह we have doubt मुख चन्द्र इव has no element of doubt therein, while in मुख वा चन्द्रो वा we have doubt Again ससन्देह is अभेदप्रधान for संशय itself is an outcome of अभेदबुद्धि, while उपमा is भेदाभेदप्रधान

 भामह first recognised the figure ससन्देह दण्डिन् has no figure ससन्देह, but has सशयोपमा instead उद्भट, रुद्रट, रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, and others recognise the figure some styling it ससन्देह, others सन्देह रुद्रट alone names it सशय According to the Trivendrum edition which reads स ससन्देहस्तु etc, मम्मट himself, it seems, recognised the figure under the name सन्देह

 § सूत्र १४— ससन्देहस्तु संशय। (Trivendrum edition reads स सन्देहस्तु etc) ससन्देह means doubt with or without the statement of difference तु— merely introduces a new figure भेदोक्तौ तदनुक्तौ— the भेद between the उपमान and उपमेय is mentioned in some cases while in others it is not stated It must be borne in mind that if the poet once gives भेद while passing from one उपमान to another, it is necessary that he should consistently do so in the case of all the different उपमानऽ If he does not give the भेद in one case, he should not give it in others

 भेद e g in इन्दु किं क्व कलङ्क, कलङ्क is the भेद between इन्दु and मुख.. In अय मार्तण्ड किं स खलु तुरगै सप्तभिरित, स खलु तुरतै सप्तभिरित gives the भेद between मार्तण्ड and प्रस्तुत नायक

 **St 27** अय मार्तण्ड कि etc Illustrates निश्चयगर्भ ससन्देह It describes the doubts on the part of the enemy at the sight of the prowess lustre and deadliness of a king on the battlefield मार्ताण्ड or मार्तण्ड the sun स खलु etc The sun is possessed (इत) of seven horses, whereas the प्रकृत नायक possesses one horse only Thus the sun s dependence on the horse is more than that of the king who therefore, is superior to the sun Some commentators interpret in a different way the sun possesses seven horses only whereas this king possesses innumerable horses- hardly convincing कृशानु fire प्रसरति etc— The fire does not advance in all directions since it flares up only in the direction of the wind The king, on the other hand, advances in all directions. कृशान्त God of Death Yama Construe साक्षात् कृतान्त किम् महिपवहनोऽसा - Buffalo is the mount of यम, whereas the प्रकृत नायक has a horse a superior animal, for his mount Con

strue. त्वाम् आजी (समरे) समालोक्य प्रतिभटा चिर इति विकल्पान् विदधति। विकल्प doubt इति thus, as described in the earlier lines.

 Herein we have different doubts regarding the particular, नायक, viz whether he is the Sun, or Fire, or Yama Each doubt is set aside by the statement of a distinguishing circumstance. Thus that he is not the Sun is decided by his not having the seven horses, that he is not the Fire is decided by his flaring in all directions, that he is not Yama is decided by his mount being other than a buffalo In this way, one doubt after another is dispelled, but the question of his Identity is left to our suggestion Thus it is निश्चयगर्भ भेदोक्तो ससन्देह-प्रताप, तेजस्, and क्रूरत्व are the three aspects of the king that the poet wants to bring out.

 Though undeniably there is a vein of व्यतिरेक running through the stanza in so far as the उपमेय is described as being superior to the several उपमानऽ, the statement is yet in the form of doubts and hence the figure is ससन्देह

 भेदोक्तावित्यनेन *etc* In the सूत्र, ससन्देहस्तु भेदोक्तौ तदनुक्तौ च सशय, the word भेदोक्तौ indicates that the author admits (स्वीकृत) not only the निश्चयगर्भ but also the निश्चयान्त variety of the figure For, भेदोक्ति or statement of the differentiating circumstance is present in both We have already seen how भेदोक्ति is present in निश्चयगर्भ variety illustrated by अय मार्तण्ड किम् etc That भेदोक्ति can be present in निश्चयगर्भ also is borne out by the instance for इन्दु किं क्व कलङ्क etc.

 The वृत्ति wants to lay emphasis on मम्मट’s admitting the निश्चयान्त variety which उद्भट, as the वृत्तिकार(मम्मट?) says later, did not admit This threefold division it may be noted, has been admitted by रुद्रट's काव्यालङ्कार, रुय्यक’s अलङ्कारसर्वस्व, साहित्यदर्पण, रसगङ्गाधर As to उद्भट's own view point see further

 **St 28** इन्दु किं etc Illustrates निश्चयान्त ससन्देह Different doubts regarding the face of a नायिका, whose real identity is ultimately ascertained इन्दु किम् means अय इन्दु किम् अम्बु कुत्र गतम् where is water, a necessary circumstance for lotus Construe हरिणाक्षि परन (अन्तत) ललितमविलसवचनैर्मुग्नमिति निश्चितम्।

 Here the नायिकामुख is doubted, in turn to be इन्दु and सरसिज, both these doubts are discarded, in turn, owing to the distinguishing curcumstances viz the absence of कलङ्क and अम्बु which are invariably associated with इन्दु and सरसिज respectively, but are conspicuously absent in the case of the प्रकृत In fine, the प्रकृत is ascertained to be

मुख due to the elegant and amorous words proceeding therefrom. Thus here we have निश्चयान्त ससन्देह

 **किन्तु निश्चयगर्भ इव** etc— The वृत्तिकार (मम्मट) claims to give the attitude of उद्भट (circa 800 A D) towards निश्चयान्त ससन्देह, In निश्चयान्त ससन्देह the identity of the प्रकृत thung is ascertained at the close As this identity is stated in this variety and not left to be guessed (प्रतीयमान) as in निश्चयगर्भ, निश्चयान्त variety appears less poetical than निश्चयगर्भ and hence, says मम्मट, it has been ignored by उद्भट Poetical taste values suggestion more than expression.

 Does उद्भट really assume this attitude? Looking to his extant work, काव्यालकारसारसग्रह, it may be said that therein he does not specsfically condemn निश्चयान्त Nay, his definition of ससन्देह does refer to भेदोक्ति, just like मम्मट's, and as such mught comprehend निश्चयान्त variety also He defines it as उपमानेन तत्त्व च भेद च वदत पुन। ससन्देह वच स्तुत्यै ससन्देह विदुर्बुधा। Only the instance that उद्भट gives is of निश्चयगर्भ and not निश्चयान्त To that extent only, मम्मट's statement may be said to correctly set forth उद्भट's view. It is significant that there are views attributed to उद्भट by आलकारिकऽ like आनन्दवर्धन and रुय्यक which find no place in his काव्यालङ्कारसारसङ्ग्रह There is a possibility, therefore, of some other work of उद्भट on अल्कार which is no more extant and on which मम्मट and others base their views (read Prof Banhatti's Introduction to का सा सं p, XIII)

 तदनुक्तौ यथा तदनुक्तौ = भेदानुक्तौ अस्या etc Illustrates शुद्ध or भेदानुक्तौ ससन्देह Poetical doubts regarding the identity of the creator of उर्वशी, the heroine of the विक्रमोर्वशीय अस्या सर्गविधौ चन्द्र प्रजापति अभूत् नु i e was it the moon that played the creator s role in creating उर्वशी? कान्तिप्रद चन्द्र hints at उर्वशी's कान्ति or elegance शृङ्गारैकरस मदन नु i e was it Cupid who takes delight solely in romance who created her? This hints at उर्वशी's fitness for शृङ्गार पुष्पाकर मास नु was it the Vernal Season who played the part of the creator? This hints at उर्वशी being as delicate as a flower वेदाभ्यासजड विषयव्यावृत्तकौतूहल adj of पुराणो मुनि i e नारायण, the creator of उर्वशी. जड grown cold (नई) owing to the study of the Vedas The study of the Vedas makes one staid unemotional, unimpulsive विषयव्यावृत्तकौतूहल whose curiosity has turned away from objects of sense Construe पुराण मुनि "जन "कौतूहल सन् इद मनोहर रूप निर्मातु कथ प्रभवेत्

 In the first two lines alone is the figure शुद्धससन्देह present Is the creator of उर्वशी the Moon, or is it Cupid, or is it the Vernal

Season? Such are the different doubts raised, in view of उर्वशी being exquisitely elegant (like the moon), suited for romance (like Cupid), beautiful (like the vernal season). While passing from one उपमान to another, no भेद is mentioned as in the instances अथ मार्तण्ड etc. and इन्दु किं etc. Thus the first half of the stanza illustrates भेदानुक्तौ ससन्देह otherwise called शुद्ध In शुद्ध ससन्देह as in निश्चयगर्भ, no निश्चय is arrived at. Read रुय्यक's quotation given above The second half of the stanza embodies अतिशयोक्ति. Hence रुय्यक cites the stanza to illustrate अतिशयोक्ति (सम्बन्धेऽय्यसव्ध) रुय्यक adverts to a view already cited, according to which, due to the use of नु (surely) these instances will come under उत्प्रेक्षा

**६ - रूपक**

 In उल्लास II we have dealt with a division of लक्षण, *VIZ* गौणी सारोपा ....................strated by गौर्वाहीक. Vāhīka, the bull. Here वाहीक (some person) is identified with गौ on account of the सादृश्य, in point of stupidity etc. between the two वाहीक being dull like the bull. It is this variety of लक्षणा based on सादृश्य, that at the bottom of रूपक But not each and every गोणी सारोपा can be रूपक , enjoying the status of an अलकार, demands that गोणी सारोपा must also be वैचित्रयवत्

 What about शुद्धा सारोपा लक्षणा of the type आयुर्धृतम् wherein there is आरोप based on सम्बन्ध other than सादृश्य ? In आयुर्घृतम्, for instance, it is based on कार्यकारणभाव, आयु being the कार्य of घृत It is easy to lapse into the error of looking upon it as a case of रुपक But मम्मट categorically repudiates its claim to be looked upon as an अल्कार while discussing the impropriety of accepting हेत्वालङ्कार, in the course of his वृति on कारणमाला (p. १६४)

 Like उत्प्रेक्षा and ससन्देह, रूपर is अभेदप्रधान For the poet, when he rėmarks मुख चन्द्र, wants to drive at the अभेद or identity between the two In उपमा e g मुख चन्द्र इव, on the other hand, the poet while showing the अभेद is also conscious of the भेद, as we have already seen माणिजयचन्द्र says साम्यमात्रे उपमा। अतिसाम्ये तु रूपकम्। What is the exact stage that रुपक represents in the poet's progress towards अवयवमान, the last stage of अभेद? We have seen how ससन्देह and उत्प्रेक्षा mark two successive stages leading to अध्यवसान रूपक marks a stage further Inasmuch as, in ससन्देह and उत्प्रेक्षा there is an-element of incertitude, while, in रूपक, that element is conspicuously absent. There is a definiteness in रूपक.

 It is equally easy to mistake what are acknowledged to be

cases of अतिशयोक्ति for रूपक Note the following description of श्रीकृष्णः— मल्लानामशनिर्नृणा नरवर स्त्रीणां स्मरो मूर्तिमान्, गोपानां स्वजनोऽसता क्षितिभुजां शास्ता स्वपित्रोः शिशु। मृत्युर्भोजपनतेर्विराजविदुषा तत्त्व पर योगिना वृष्णीनां परदेवनेति विदितो रङ्ग गत साग्रज। Here, to take one unit from the series, साग्रज (= वलरामसहित श्रीकृष्ण) मल्लानामशनि इति विदित i e. श्रीकृष्ण who was regarded as ‘thunderbolt’ to themselves by the wrestlers, we have a case of अतिशयोक्ति, for मल्लानामशनि इति विदित makes this a case of अध्यवसान, so far as the मल्ल's are concerned The मल्लऽ think अशनिरय (अय not standing for श्रीकृष्ण) where the उपमेय is निगीर्ण by the उपमान It may be noted, however, that with a slight modification, this itself will be a case of रूपक. Let the poet himself think : श्रीकृष्ण मल्लानामशनि and it becomes a case of रूपक, for here there is आरोप on श्रीकृष्ण of being मल्लानामशनि, the उपमेय is not निगोर्ण and hence there is no अध्यवसान

 There is another case of अतिशयोक्ति – likely to be mistaken for रूपक Describing the रूप of a king, apoet says तदीय रूप शत्रणा वित्रासनम्, रूप वित्रासनम्, one might suggest, is a case of रूपफ, since here is आरोप. he might urge, of वित्रासन on रूप But, on a closer scrutiny, it will be seen that the case is of अध्यवसान and not of आरोप The रूप is really speaking वित्रासनहेतु (the source of terror) not वित्रासन, So the हेतु is in effect, निगीर्ण by the कार्य Instead of saying रूप वित्रासनहेतु the poet says रूप वित्रासनम् That the अध्यवसान is based on सादृश्येतरसम्बन्ध (here कार्यकारणभाव) is another circumstance to mark the case off from a रूपक based on सादृश्य only.

 While discussing उपमितसमास, we have already drawn attention. to the point of contact between उपमा and रूपक It is too easy to mistake मुख चन्द्र as a case of वादिधर्मलुप्ता उपमा But, fortunately for us, the Grammarians have laid down that where the साधारणधर्म is dropped in an उपमा, the उपमान and उपमेय are necessarily compounded (उपमित सामान्याप्रयोगे) If it were a case of वादिधर्मलुप्ता उपमा, we would have got मुखचद्र and not मुख चन्द्र That मुखचन्द्र may be, according to the particular circumstances, रुपक or उपमा is a different matter. One thing is certain and that is मुख चद्र can never be an उपमा

 It is interesting to note that रूपक is confined to the आरोप of one शब्दार्थ over another In मुख चन्द्र, the शब्दार्थ of चद्र is आरोपित on the शब्दार्थ of मुख What about the आरोप of one वाक्यार्थ over another ? It has crystallised into a separate figure viz वाक्यार्थनिदर्शना य ते गुणान् गदितुमुद्यममादधाति स मेरु लिलङ्घयिषति is, essentially, वाक्यार्थ रूपक, one वाक्यार्थ being आरोपित on another But now who dare call it रूपक ? वाक्यार्थ

निदर्शना, though a scion of रूपक, has carved out a separate kingdom for itself! Incidentally, it may be noticed that there is an analagous case of वाक्यार्थीपमाऽ crystallising into independent figures like दृष्टान्त, प्रतिवस्तूपमा, अर्थान्तरन्यास

 रूपक is a figure of great antiquity. भरत includes it in the four figures he recognised After him भामह, दण्डिन्, उद्भट, वामन, रुद्रट all recognised the figure. दण्डिन् understands the figure in a far wider sense than other rhetoricians, bringing figures like अपह्नुति, आक्षेप, and व्यतिरेक withun its scope

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708341111रू.PNG"/>

A रूपक may be detached (निरङ्ग) or sustained रुपक again may be such as is made up of constituent रुपकऽ either inter related as cause and effect (परम्परित) or not so related (साङ्ग) In the case of साङ्गरूपक all the constituent units may be expressly stated (समस्तवस्तुविषय) or one alone might be so stated, the others being impied (एकदेशनिवर्ति) The परम्परित may or may not be based on श्लेष (श्लिष्ट or अश्लिष्ट) The subdivision occurs where the प्रकृत or उपमेय is identified with one उपमान only In माला one उपमेय comes to be identified with several उपमानऽ.

 **Essentials**— \[विषयिणा विषयस्य रूपात करणात्व रूपकम्— रूय्यक अन्यरूपेण रुपवत् करणात्— कुवल्यानन्द रूपयेते उपमानोपमेयोरभेदारोपणम्\] (1) There must be the अरोप of the उपमान on the उपमेय, based on सादृश्य and no other सम्बन्ध In other words, there must be धन of the type मुखं चन्द्र (2) The must be अन्ये intentional or simulated and not स्वरनिय or genuince. The poet must vidulge therein with a full consciousness that *really* मुख is from चन्द्र (3) The आरोप of course must be striking enough to be poctical. (Vähika is a bull) though cannot

enjoy the status of a रूपक as it lacks वैचित्र्य (4) The आरोप must be शब्दगत् and not वाक्यगत In मुख चन्द्र the आरोपित and आरोपविषय are शब्दऽ only आरोप of one वाक्यार्थ over another will be निदर्शना (5) As there Is आरोप, no उपमाप्रतिपादकऽ like इव, यथा, वा. are present (6) The साधारणधर्म between the प्रकृत and अप्रकृत must be obvious enough making it unnecessary to mention it In मुख चन्द्र the साम्य in point of रम्यत्व, etc is too obvious Sometimes however the साधारणधर्म may be mentioned as in नरानन्व त्रातु त्वमिह परमं भेषजमसि O Mother Ganges you are a supreme remedy to save men Here नरान् त्रातु is the साधारणधर्म

 रूपक and उपमा - As seen above, माणिक्यचन्द्र says that if in उपमा we have साम्य, in रूपक we have अतिसाम्य This former is based on अभेदाभेद, the latter on अभेद If in उपमा it is the साधम्य that charms us, in रूपक it is अभेदारोप, one step further than साधर्म्य, that becomes the source of charm If उपमा becomes the starting point of the poet's march towards अध्यवसान, in अतिशयोक्ति, to wit, चन्द्रोऽयम्, रूपक, aiming at अभेदारोप, marks an advanced stage in that march This eventually results in the distinct forms assumed by the two In मुख चन्द्र इव we have the उपमाप्रतिपादक mentioned in मुख चन्द्र as there is अभेदाराप, no room is left for an उपमाप्रतिपादक That रुपक cannot be mistaken for वादिधर्मलुप्ता we have already seen

 रूपक *and* ससन्देह and उत्प्रेक्षा - We have seen that in ससन्देह there is सशय, the mind wavering equally between the different alternatives In उत्प्रेक्षा, there is उह्न, the mind wavering with a greater bias towards the उपमान In the case of रुपक, on the other hand,. such an incertitude and wavering of the mind is conspicuously absent There is no अनिश्चितता whatsoever

 रूपक *and* स्मरण - See under स्मरण

 § सूत्र १५. Constrpe उपमानोपमेययो य अभेद तद्रूपकम्. As we have already seen, उपमा is based on भेदाभेद रुपक, however, is based on अभेद between the उपमान and उपमेय In मुख चन्द्र इव we take the उपमान and उपमेय to be भिन्न in so far as there is comparison which requires two objects while we look upon them as अभिन्न in so far as they have the same common property In a रtपक like मुख चन्द्र, on the other hand there is no consciousness of भेद due to the absence of comparison. For here we progress to superimposition (आरोप), a step further than comparison But there is still a consciousness of distinctness as is presupposed by superimposition itself Superimposition re-

quires two objects a thing superimposed and another superimposed upon To that extent, in रुपक, there is a consciousness of distinct ness Even this distinctness vanishes in अभिशयोक्ति चन्दोऽयम् In short, in रुपक we have not the extreme भेद of an उपमा nor the extreme अभेद of अभिशयोक्ति \[See the वृति\].

अतिसाम्यात् अभेद अतिसाम्यात् goes with अभेद The अभेद is due to extrerne similarity. अतिसाम्य—अति is inserted to show that the साम्य in रूपक is more than that in उपमा

 अनपहुतभेदयो उपमान and उपमेय whose distinctness is not disguised (अपह्नुत) Though there is अभेद it is not of that absolute type which presents itself in अतिशयोक्ति चन्द्रोऽयम् In अतिशयोक्ति चन्द्रोऽयम्, the उपमान swallows up the उपमेय, in रूपक मुख चन्द्र both are intact Read मा च— अयोग्यमानमेवयन्तु तुम यती खालन्। Further, तथा मुख चन्द्र इयदिरुपक चन्द्रेण स्वगुणा लक्ष्यते।

 सूत्र १६ & १७ define समस्तवस्तुविषय and उपदेश विवर्ति two subdivisions of साङ्ग रूपक

 § सूत्र १६ समस्तपशु पदा Construe यदा आरापिता (1) समग्रवस्तुविषयं रूपकं (मरत) Wherever in a complex रुपक, all the उपमानऽ (=आरोपित) are explicity stated like the उपमान, we get This is the definition of refera sur wich, as we saw above forms a sub-division of साङ्ग रुपक, is one which consists of parts, all together forming a complex or sustained रुपक Here we have a set of as and a corresponding set of s sin In terraſtra all the saras are explicitly mentioned This is in contrast to where only some stated the others being inferable That all the stated in both the varieties goes without sayırg or as are directly mus are directly

ed (विषय) i e expressly stated in this variety All वस्तुऽ i e components उपमानऽ & उपमेयऽ of the रूपक are expressly stated.

 वृत्ति— आरोपविषया समस्तवस्तुविषयम्। This elucidates the सूत्र समस्तवस्तुविषय etc आरोपविषया इव शब्दोपात्ता when the उपमानऽ (आरोप्यमाणा) are explictly stated (शब्दोपात्ता) like the उपमेयऽ (आरोपविषया) शब्दोपात्त explains श्रौता of the सूत्र आरोप्यमाणा the आरोपिता of the सूत्र समस्तानि वस्तूनि समस्तवस्तुविषयम्। This explans the name समस्तवस्तुविषय It is so called because all constituents (वस्तु) are encompassed by it (विषय) Encompassed in so far as they are directly stated In this variety no उपमान nor उपमेय is left unexpressed In एकदेशविवर्ति, on the other hand some उपमान or उपमानऽ are implied and not expressed आरोपिता इति etc This defines the scope of the word आरोपिता in the सूत्र If the सूत्र were rigorously interpreted it would mean where three or more than three आरोपितऽ are expressly stated the रूपक is समस्तवस्तुविषय For the plural of आरोपिता stands for three or more than three' as all plurals do If the plural were rigorously interpreted it will result in excluding cases of two आरोपिताऽ or उपमानऽ It may just happen that in a complex रूपक only two component रूपकऽ may exist with two उपमान and two उपमेयऽ Such cases might not be embraced by the definition with the आरोपिताऽ in plural To avert such a contingency the वृत्ति says that the plural number of आरोपिता should not be taken too rigorously, it is of no consequence it is not meant (अविवक्षित) to be taken too strictly It comprehends dual also

 **St 30** Illustrates समस्तवस्तुविषयरूपक Description of रात्रि with its attendant circumstances as a कापालिकी with the appurtenances peculiar to the sect कापालिक a kind of Saiva ascetic who carries a human skull and uses it as a receptacle for his food Here रात्रि is conceived as a nun belonging to this order Construe इय रात्रिकापालिकी ज्योत्स्ना धवला तारकास्थीनि विभ्रती अन्तर्धानव्यसनरसिका, चन्द्रमुद्राकपाले लाछनस्य छलेन न्यस्त सिद्धाजनपरिमल दधती, द्वीपाद् द्वीप भ्रमति। ज्योत्स्ना etc White as she is besmeared with the ashes in the form of moonlight The कापालिकऽ smear their bodies with ashes तारका etc The stars are thought to be the bones (अस्थीनि) that the कापालिकऽ carry for purposes of magic अन्तर्धानव्यसनरसिका The कापालिकऽ were credited wuh powers to conceal themselves it had become a hobby with them (व्यसन) The night also was अन्त रसिका in so far as it concealed other objects Objects become invisible during night time Acc to सम्प्रदायप्रकाशिनी, अन्तर्धान as applied to कापालिकी means 'death (अवसान) The कापालिकऽ

indulged in the pastime of bringing about (?) deaths द्वीपाद्द्वीप भ्रमति Ref to the night passing swiftly from one territory to another. The कापालिकऽ also were notorious tramps. चन्द्रमुद्रा etc The spot on the moon is conceived to be the magic collyrium (सिद्धापन) carried by the कापालिकी in the skull bowl (मुद्राकपाल) in the shape of the moon The चन्द्र is the मुद्राकपाल लाठन or spot is the निद्धाशन in the form of in the guise of. मुद्राकपाल has been differently explained.. मुद्रेव हस्तग्राह्यमित्यर्थ (स प्र) skull as handy as a seal Some take the word मुद्रा, as signifying objects assumed after the imitation of the heretics Thus मुद्राकुण्डल means a कुण्डल assumed on initiation सिद्धाञ्जनपरिमल परिमल only signifies the scent of the अथन Acc. to स प्र st signified ‘sign' (चिह्न), the mark of collyrium सिद्धाञ्जन Collyrium with magic (सिद्ध) powers.

 One is reminded of the कथा of मित्रगुप्त in दशकुमारचरित्र 3 VI. where an अथ makes a beautiful lady look like a monkey.

 Here (1) जोत्स्नना ण्व भम्म (2) तारका ण्व अस्थीनि (3) रत्रिक ण्व कापालिकी and चद्र ण्व मुद्राकपाल are the different रूपक.s Since in each one of them the उपमान and उपमेय are explicitly stated, together, they form a complex or भाङ्ग रूपक of the समवस्तुविषय variety, it may be noted, embodies an आहुति to which we shall come later. In fact this stanza is given to illustrate आरोपपूर्वक अपह्नुति by कथय the अलवारगर्वभार

 One question presents itself to our minds as we think of complex रुपकऽ like these. We easily lapse into a belief that there is a central रुपक of which the other different रुपकऽ are limbs or Is this belief justified? In other words, can we look upon one of these रुपकऽ as and the other, or subservient to it? To revert to our instance, the temptation is too strong to look upon as the प्रधान रुपक to which all the other three are subservient for, is it not the that possesses etc?

 But, on a closer scrutiny, it will be realised that in a complex रुपक like this, al the component enjoy the same status 7.gether they form the total रूपक which, therefore, is no more than

an aggregate of the different components Thus रात्रि ण्व कापालिकी is on no higher level than ज्योत्स्ना ण्व भस्म or other रूपकऽ. That कापालिकी possesses भस्म etc. has nothing to do with its status. Even without रात्रिकापालिकी, the other रूपकऽ could have remained-giving rise to एकदशविवर्ति रूपक

 अत्र पादत्रये...... न कार्या – We have already seen how a compound like मुखचद्र' can be dissolved in two ways मुख चन्द्र इव or मुखमेव चन्द्र, the former being an उपमा, the latter Unless there is some conclusive circumstance (साधक), confining us definitely either to रुपक or उपमा, a compound like मुखचद्र may, with equal justice, be interpreted alternately as उपमा, or रूपक This gives rise to मदेहसकर, a complex figure dealt with at the close of the Ullasa, which arises when there is absence of certainty of one figure due to the absence of a circumstance favourable (न्याय, साधक), for the acceptance of one figure, or unfavourable, (वाधक) for another figure, leading to its rejection (एकस्य च ग्रहे न्यायदाषाभावादनिश्चय)

 One might just suspect that in the stanza ज्योत्स्नाभरम etc. which is given to Illustrate समस्तवस्तुविषयरूपक, we have a case of सन्देहमकर rather than of रूपक exclusively. The four compounds embodied in the first three quarters (पादवये) of the stanza,— ज्योत्स्नाभस्म, तारकास्थीनि, रात्रिकापालिक and चन्द्रमुद्राकपाल— may be dissolved either as उपमाऽ or रूपकऽ and not exclusively as रूपकऽ Thus, to take one instance, ज्योत्स्नाभस्म may be dissolved either as ज्योत्स्ना ण्व भस्म ( रूपक) or ज्योत्स्ना भस्म इव (उपमा) \[ Trvm Edition reads अत्र पादत्रयम्। *sc.* रूपकात्मनम् \]

 मम्मट, however, justifies the instance as one exclusively of रुपक and not उपमा There is a circumstance, he argues, which makes out a case (साधक) for रूपक, ruling out thereby the possibility of उपमा That circumstance is the epithet, अन्तर्धानव्यसनरसिव taking delight in अन्तर्धान रसिकत्व pertains more appropriately to the कापालिकी, whose hobby it is to vanish, who is the आरोपित or उपमान in the present case So, to suit the धर्म of अन्तर्धानव्यसनरमिकत्व कापालिकी becomes the प्रधान member in the it is a रूपक, रात्रिरेव कापालिका, and not an उपमा There is no room, therefore, for a doubt that herein we have a case of भार of रूपक (तत्) with उपमा

 Since in रात्रिकापालिकी कापालिकी is the प्रधान member, it naturally follows that in ज्योत्स्नाभस्म, तारवारस्थीति and स्कन्द्रमुद्रापपस्य, the 2nd member of each as प्रधान, for भस्म, अग्ध and मुद्राकपाल pertain to कापालिकी All these compounds also therefore become रुपकऽ

 One might just pose मम्मट with the question is not अन्तर्धानव्यसन-रात्रिधम also? Because, रात्रि covers up or obscures things There are two objections to this view First, अन्तर्धान stands primarily for mysterious disappearance which is solely the feature of a कापालिकी, and secondly, रमिवद्धन is a चेतनधर्म more properly connected with the चैतन कापालिकी than the अचेतन रात्रि

**एकदेशविवर्ति रूपक**

 एकदेशविवर्ति which dates back from भामह is another sub-division of the साङ्ग रुपक. Whereas in समस्तवस्तुविषय the उपमानऽ one and all, are expressly stated in एकदेशविवर्ति, some उपमानऽ are merely suggested on the strength of others that are expressed That is to say, the उपमान or उपमानऽ that are expressed may just hint at others, which thus are suggested and as such poetically more effective Thus in एकदेशविवर्ति रुपक, which like समस्तवस्तुविषय consists of several रुपकऽ, all the उपमेयऽ are expressed and so some उपमानऽ or उपमानऽ . Other उपमानऽ are left to our suggestion The name एकदेशविवर्ति is explained by मम्मट as एकभेदा , which subsists more apparently in one portion (where the उपमान or उपमेयऽ are expressed) than in others

 For एकदेशविवर्ति रुपकऽ. परम्परित see below

 § गू १७ Construe ते (अरोप्यम..) परिनर् आर्यश्च That is cairt where the 75 (mit = wom) are some of them expressed others implied (r) refers to the mentiamaul in the resendina ar tamundra boer senerkran a

pedantry than accuracy : एकन्दा एकस्मिन् काले ईश शाब्दत्वात् समर्थ विवर्त रूपणविधियतेति व्युत्पत्ति Compare, प्रतीहारेन्दुराज on काव्यालङ्कारसारसङ्ग्रह १.१४..... for a similar derivation.

 St 31, जस्स etc Illustrates एकदेशविवर्ति रूपक Description of a king whose enemies used to take to their heels when he put his hand on his sword. स छाया— यस्य रणान्त पुरे करे कुर्वतो मण्डलाग्रलताम्। रसममुरयपि सहसा पराङ्मुखीभवति रिपुसेना॥यस्य sc राज्ञ रणान्त पुरे In the harein of battle मण्डलाग्रलता करे कुर्बत as soon as he handled the sword (मण्डल गोलाकार अग्र यस्य मण्डलाग्र,, a kind of sword with arched tip) लता only hints at the length रससमुखी i e वीररससमुखी adj of रिपुसेना that was prone to the heroic sentiment पराङ्मुखीभवति flees away.

 Here रणातपुर is a रूपक wherein रण comes to be identified with अन्त पुर. In thus रूपक, the आरोपित (अन्तपुर) is expressed and enables us to guess other आरोपितऽ viz नायिका in relation to मण्डलाग्रलता and प्रतिनायिका in relation to रिपुसेना What is meant is As soon as we find रण identified with अत् पुर, by an easy surmise we identify मण्डलाग्रलता with नायिका and रिपुसेना with प्रतिनायिका For is it not usual with a king, tn the harein. to take up the hand of one consort, the sight of which becomes repugnant to another? This understanding is facilitated by the fernmnine gender of रिपुसेना and मण्डलग्रलता as also by the word रसममुखी hinting at शृगारसामुख्य

 Thus in thus complex रूपक, the आरोपित viz अन्तपुर is expressed The others viz नायिका and प्रतिनायिका are implied Hence it is एकदेशविवर्ति Note that, viewed from a different angle, this instance can with justice, be one of समासोक्ति See under समासोक्ति

 वृत्ति— अत्र *etc* शब्दोपात्तम् *i e* explicatly mentioned in the compound रणान्त पुर अर्थसामर्थ्यादवसीयते Because, the आरोप of अन्तपुर on रण at once puts us in mind of the scene in a royal harein, where the king takes (करेकृ) the नायिका by the hand, thereby making the प्रतिनायिका turn away in huff
एकदेशे विशेषेण etc Explanation of the name एकदेशविवर्ति विशेषेण वतन is, in रसगङ्गाधरकार's words, स्फुटतया, because the उपमान viz अतपुर is expressly stated.

 § सू १८ साङ्गमेतत् एतत् *i e* the one of which समस्तवस्तुविषय and एकदेशविवर्ति are sub-divisions

 Here an interesting question presents itself to our minds We have seen above that what is साङ्ग रूपक is no more than an *aggregate*

of several constituent रूपकऽ. If it is an aggregate, are we justified in looking upon साङ्ग रूपक as a sub-division of रूपक ? For sub-divisions of figures must be based on a higher principle than mere aggregation जगन्नाथ, in his र गं, does refer to such a view. But he takes pains to contradict it by saying that aggregation also is sometimes charming Read. अत्र च चमत्कारविशेषजनक्तया रूपकमधातात्मकमपि सावयवरूपक रूपकालकृतिभेदगणनायां गण्यते। यथा मौक्तिकालकृतिभेद्गणनायाभेक नासामौक्तिकमिव सवातात्मकमौक्तिकमञ्जर्यादयोऽपि गण्यन्ते। अन्य (था ?) मालारूपस्योपमादेस्त- द्भेदगणनेऽगणनप्रसङ्गात्। एतेन गवा सघातो गोभेदाना कपितादीना गणनाया यथा न गण्यते तथा रूपकभेदगणनामस्तुतौ न तत्सधातात्मक सावयव गणनीय इति परास्तम्।

 We have already seen how आलकारिकऽ like रुय्यक and the commen tator माणिक्यचन्द्र are inchned to admit एकदेशविवर्तिनी उपमा, a counterpart of एकदेशविवर्ति रूपक,

 वृत्तिः उत्तद्विभेद सावयवम्। साङ्ग is सावयव रसगङ्गाधर calls it such उत्तद्विभेद The two subdivisions of which (समस्तवस्तुविषय and एकदेशविवर्ति) are stated just now.

**निरङ्ग रूपक**

 निरङ्ग रूपक is one which unlike साङ्ग, does not consist of parts or constituent रूपकऽ It has two sub divisions शुद्ध and माला शुद्ध occurs where there is one रुपक, absolute and unrelated to another माला occurs where on one आरोपविषय several आरोपितऽ are superimposed Note that माला is consistent with निरङ्गत्व Because in माला, though we have several रूपकऽ, yet the उपमेय or आरोपविषय is the same in all

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708059554निरङ्ग.PNG"/>

 § स् १९ निरङ्गं तु शुद्ध Correct Translation— What is known as शुद्ध is sub-division of the निरङ्ग रूपफ शुद्ध रूपक is one where we have one simple रुपक, not forming part of an aggregate It is an absolute unit The epithet शुद्ध (opposed to माला) as apphed to figures (ep शुद्धा निदर्शना, शुद्धा प्रतिवस्तूपमा, शुद्ध दृष्टान्त, शुद्ध दीपक, शुद्ध व्यतिरेक) conveys the idea of an absolute unit, not related to another.

 Let us not forget that it is an easy transition from शुद्ध to मुखचन्द्र may be शुद्ध. But मुखचन्द्र हामज्योत्स्ना तनोति will be साङ्ग (समस्तवस्तुविषय ).

सा प्रिया सौन्दर्यस्य तरङ्गिगी etc ललनाचूडामणि The crest jewel of women *i e* pre-eminent amongst them

 Here the उपमेय or आरोपविषय is सा प्रिया She comes to be identified with the eight उपमानऽ or आरोपित viz ॰तरङ्गिणी, ॰हर्षोद्गम, ॰कार्मणकर्म, ॰उल्लास, नावासभू, ॰विद्या, ॰प्रावीण्यसाक्षात्क्रिया, ॰बाणा and ललनाचूडामणि

**परम्परित रूपक**

 After having dealt with साङ्ग and निरङ्ग रूपक, मम्मट now proceeds to another sub-division of रूपक viz परम्परित The term परम्परित is given to this sub-division owing to the presence here of the परम्परा of कार्यरुपक and कारणरूपक In other words here the superimposition of one thing upon another becomes the occasion for the superimposition of some thing else on a third thing Thus we have here two or more रुपकऽ one of which is responsible for others Thus परम्परित रूपक is made up of a set of रूपकऽ related as कार्यरूपक and कारणरूपकऽ While describing a king s powerful arm I say तब भुज जयकुञ्जरस्य आलानम् Your arm is the tying post of the elephant of triumph Here I could identify भुज with आलान only because I had identified जय (Victory or triumph) with कुञ्जर For an आलान cannot be thought of unless there is an elephant to be tied thereto That is why the name परम्परित, which as explained by प्रदीप means कार्यकारणभावरूपा परम्परा सञ्जता अव is given to this variety काव्यालङ्कार रुद्रट (arca 850 A D) seems to have been the first to give the name a to this variety उद्भट the author of काव्यालङ्कारसारसग्रह रुद्रट (circa 800 A D) recognised the variety for the first time but had given the name एकदेशवृत्ति (different from एकदेशविवर्ति)
