 Above we saw that the आरोप of कुञ्जरत्व on जय was the cause of the आरोप of आलानत्व on भुज Here in the कारणरुपक कुञ्जर and जय are two distinct words But there can be cases where instead of two words in the कारणरूपक we may have one word with a double significance so that one sense of it can be आरोपित on another Thus if I use मानस with a double significance (1) mind and (2) the मानस lake it will enable me to indentify a king with a swan the king enshrined in the mind being identified with the swan in the मानस lake I can then talk of विद्वमानसे इस नृप Here the आरोप on मानस the mind मानस of in the sense of the lake मानस is the cause which has made possible the आरोप of हसत्व on the नृप Thus परस्परित रूपक can be possible with श्लेष in the कारणरुपक This means that परम्परित रुपक can be one which is not based on श्लेष or one which is based on श्लेष In other

words it may be अश्लिष्ट or श्लिष्ट. It is easy to imagine how each one of these can be शुद्ध or मालारूप So we get the following scheme—

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708356904पर.PNG"/>  



 In a परम्परितरूपक how are we to decide a रूपक as कारणरूपक or कार्यरूपक ? It is easy to do so The poets intention is to exalt (maybe, also to run down in some cases) a particular object which is the प्रकृत thing In order to exalt it he identifies it with some उपमान This is the कार्यरूपक to justify which कारणरूपकऽ are resorted to In भास's ( ? ) प्रतिमानाटक (१२८) राम wants to exalt वल्कलऽ in the eyes of लक्ष्मण with the words तप सग्रामकवच नियमद्विरदाकुश। खलीनमिन्द्रियाश्वानां गृह्यता धर्मसारथि॥Here since बल्कल is the प्रकृत thing वल्कल कवच, वल्कल अकुश, वल्कल खलीन, वल्कल सारथि are कार्यरूपकऽ to Justify which the poet resorts to the कारणरूपक viz तप एव सग्राम, नियम एवं द्विरद, इन्द्रियाणि एव अश्वा' and धम एव (रथी)— the last being also एकदेशविवर्ति

 § सू २१ नियतारोपणो etc Defines and divides परम्परित रूपक Construe य परस्य आरोप नियतारोपणोपाय स्यात् तत् परम्परितम्। (तत्) बाचके श्लिष्टे भेदभाजि वा (भवेत्)। Where the आरोप (on one thing) of something else is occasioned by a specific supernmposition the figure is परम्परित रूपक It may occur either when the word expressive (of the thing super imposed in the कारणरूपक) as distinct or paronomastic (श्लिष्ट)

 To apply the definition to the above instances the आरोप of आलानत्व on बाहु is caused by the नियतारोपण viz of कुञ्जरत्व on जय So the आरोप of हसत्व on the king is caused by the नियतारोपण viz . of मानस the lake, on the mind In the former The वाचक of the thing superimposed viz In the latter मानस the वाचक of the thing superimposed in the कारणरूपक is श्लिष्ट i e. punningly used

 Thus—  

                 कारणरूपक               
          कार्यरूपक  
      (कुजर is अश्लिष्ट जय एव कुजर          
    भुज एव आलानम्

      (मानस is श्लिष्ट) मानस (mind) एव मानसं (lake)
  रावा एव हंस

 नियतारोपणोपायः to judge from मम्मट’s वृत्ति is a बहुव्रीहि compound
आरोपण उपाय यस्य cf एवमरोपनिमित्त हमरारोप of the वृत्ति on p. ५४

which makes out a case for बहुव्रीहि. What is meant is that some specific (नियत) आरोप like that of कुञ्जरत्व on नय becomes the उपाय or cause of the आरोप of something else (परस्य) like आलानत्व on मुख In plain words, where a certain superimposition is responsible for some other superimposition प्रदीप refers to an explanation of नियतारोपणोपाय as a षष्ठीपुरूष \[नियतस्य मुखस्य आरोपणस्य उपाय (e g of आलानत्व on भुज) य' परम्य अमुख्यस्य आरोप (*e g* of कुञ्जरत्व on जय) तत् परम्परितम्\] which is far fetched, unnatural and not in keeping with the वृत्ति Though the resultant sense is the same, the interpretation that is put on नियत्व and पर seems to be unnatural नियतारोपण is taken to refer to the कायरूपक and परस्य आरोप to कारणरूपक प्रदीप nghtly ignores thus view वाचके Read प्रदीप उपायभूतरूपके आरोग्याराविषययो वाचकस्य श्लिष्टवाद्भिन्नरूपत्वाञ्चेत्यर्थ। वाचके, in plain words, means शब्दे When the शब्द expressive of the उपमान and उपमेय in the कारणरूपक is श्लिष्ट or when the शब्दऽ are भिन्न भेदभाजि Loc. sing भेदभाज् (= भिन्न) Note that प्रदीप takes वाचके as आरोप्यस्य वाचक and आरोपविषयस्य वाचक We have taken it as आरोप्यस्य वाचक only For, firstly, in the preceding line of the कारिका, we have परस्य (आरोप्यस्य) आरोप and secondly, वाचके (singular) can with propriety be taken with this आरोप्य

 परम्परित रूपक and एकदेशविवर्ति रूपक - The question arises As in परम्परित रूपक one आरोप is responsible for another soth एवदेशविवर्ति also one आरोप is responsible for another Thus, to revert to our instance of रणान्तपुर, the आरोप of अन्तपुरत्व on रण is responsible for the आरोप of नायिकात्व and प्रतिनायिकात्व on मण्डलाग्रलला and रिपुसेना respectively What, then is the dividing line between the two varieties of रूपक ?

 Firstly in परम्परित रूपक the आरोपित are all expressed, while in एकदेशविवर्ति some are expressed and others implied Thus कुञ्जरत्व is as much expressed as आलानत्व in आलान जयकुञ्जरस्य Whereas in यस्य रणान्त पुरे etc, अन्त पुरत्व alone is expressed while नायिकात्व and प्रतिनायिकात्व are implied Secondly, in परम्परित रूपक, one रूपक is exclusively the cause of another So much so that in the absence of one, the other could not be there If there were not the आरोप of कुञ्जरत्व on जय, there could not have been the आरोप of आलानत्व on भुज Whereas in यस्य रणान्त पुरे etc even supposing there were not the आरोप of अन्त पुरत्व on रण, we could have guessed that there is the आरोप of नायिकात्व etc, on the strength of the feminine gender and the words रससमुखी and पराहमुखी Only the figure would have turned into a समासोक्ति Thus in यस्य रणे करे कुर्वतो मण्डलाग्रलताम्। रससमुख्यपि सहसा पराङ्मुखीभवति रियुसेना॥even in the absence of the आरोप of अन्तपुर we get the idea of नायिका and प्रतिनायिका Read जगन्नाथ (रसगङ्गा

धर) :— यद्यपि सावयवेऽप्यारोपे (प १) आरोपान्तरस्योपायस्तथापि तत्रारापानिरिक्तेन कविसमय सिद्धसादृश्येनाप्यारोपान्तरसिद्धि समवति।

 परपरितरूपक and समस्तवस्तुविषय— It is not so easy to distinguishr between the two. For in both, all the आरोपितऽ are expressed But whereas in परम्परित रूपक one रूपक is necessarily the cause of another, in समस्तवस्तुविषय it may not be necessarily so The आरोप of कुवरल is necessarily the cause of the आरोप of आलानत्व on भुज, but the आरोप of कापालिकात्व on रात्रि is not the exclusive cause of the आरोप of भस्मत्व on ज्योत्स्ना etc. For who knows that also the white colour of the moon light was not responsible for the आरोेप of भस्मत्व or that the round shape of the moon was also not responsible for the आराप of मुद्राकपालत्व on it? Read र ग cited above.

 This gives us a clue to a further point of distinction Whereas. in परम्परित, the कार्यरूपक enjoys a comparatively higher status than the कारणरूपक, in समस्तवस्तुविषय all constituents of the aggregate enjoy the same status.

 It cannot be gainsaid, however, that the line of demarcation between the two varieties is too faint For instance, one might, without fear of serious contradiction, say that the आरोेप of कापालिकीत्व on रात्रि is responsible for the आरोप of अस्थित्व on तारका etc And one cannot resist the temptation to look upon समस्तवस्तुविषय as the broader class, from which परम्परित रूपक, though a sub-division of it, has developed into a separate figure That भामह has समस्तवस्तुविषय but no परम्परित is a case in point.

 **St 34** विद्वन्मानसहस *etc* Illustrates श्लिष्ट परम्परित मालारूपक Vocatives addressed to a king Description of the different excellences of a king विद्वन्मानसहस विदुषा मानस (mind) एव मानस (Lake) तस्मिन् हस वैरि etc वैरिण कमलाया (wealth) सङ्कोच (shrinking) एवं कमलाना असकोच (blooming) तस्मिन् दीप्तद्युति (Sun). He makes enemy's wealth shrink, the sun makes the lotuses bloom कमलासङ्कोच is split in two ways. कमलाया सकोच as appied to the king कमलाना असङ्कोच as applied to the sun. (दीप्तद्युति ) So in the following epithets. दुर्गामार्गणनीललोहित दुर्गाणा अमागणनेव दुर्गाया मार्गण तस्मिन् नीललोहित (= शिव). The king was so powerful that he never sought refuge (अमार्गण) behind forts शिव has occasions to go in quest (मार्गण) of दुर्गा or पार्वती नीललोहित = शिव is नील at his कण्ठ due to हालाहल and लोहित with his hair समिन्स्वीकारवैश्वानर समिता (सग्रामणा) स्वीकार एव समिधा (इ धनस्य) स्वीकार तस्मिन् वैश्वानर (अग्नि) The king had no difficulty in accepting a challenge for battle. The

fire also eats up the fuel सत्यप्रीतिविधानदक्ष सत्ये प्रीति एव सत्या (पार्वत्या) अप्रीति तस्य विधाने दक्ष (प्रजापति ) The king inculcated a love for truth (among his subjects ?) दक्ष, as the पुराणऽ aver treated his daughter पार्वती (the same as सती) with disfavour (अप्रीति) विजयप्राग्भावभीम विजय परपराभव एव विजयो अर्जुन तस्य प्राग्भाव तस्मिन् भीम The king is the prime origin of victory भीम was born earlier (प्राग्भाव) than अर्जुन साम्राज्यं etc construe वरवीर वैरिञ्च वत्सरशतं साम्राज्य उच्चै क्रिया O pre eminent, hero ! May you rule sublimely for a hundred years of ब्रह्मा (विरिञ्चि) A day of ब्रह्मा = 1000 युगऽ Note that दक्ष and भीम are not to be inter preted as ‘clever' and 'fierce' but as proper nouns

 Here (1) मानसमेव मानसं, (2) कमलाया सङ्कोच एव कमलाना असकोच, (3) दुर्गाणाम मार्गणमेव दुर्गाया मार्गणम, (4) समिता स्वीकार एक समिधा स्वीकार (5) सत्ये प्रीतिरेक सत्या अप्रीति, (6) विजय (victory) एवं विनय (अर्जुन) are the कारणरूपकऽ responsible for the कार्यरूपकऽ viz राजा एवम् इस, राजा एवं दीप्तद्युति, राजा एवं नीललोहित, राजा एव वैश्वानर, राजा एव दक्ष and राजा एव भीम respectively Since there is श्लेष in the कारणरूपकऽ viz in मानस, कमलामवोच, दुर्गामार्गण समित्स्वीकार, सत्यप्रीति and विजयप्राग्भाव, it is श्लिष्ट परपरित Since there is आरोप of many e g हस, दीप्तद्युति etc on one viz the king it is मालारूपक So the figure here is श्लिष्ट परपरित मालारूपक

 वृत्ति Thus requres no further elucidation एव आरोपणनिमित्त = एव आरोपण निमित्तं यस्य Paraphrase of नियतारोपणोपाय which thus is a बहुव्रीहि The आरोप of हस etc on the king is rendered possible by the आरोप of मानस (the lake) on मानस (the mind ) and so on

 यद्यपि etc मम्मट is explaining why श्लिष्ट परम्परित is finding a place in the midst of अर्थालकारऽ In so far as this variety is based on रूप, it is a शब्दालङ्कार For, the charm depends on the presence of particular words like मानस, दुर्गामार्गण etc How vitally necessary these words are becomes clear from the fact that we could not replace them by their synonyms (परिवृत्यसह) For instance of we were to use चित्त instead of मानस, the कारणरूपक, viz मानसमेव मानस, will disappear altogether Thus these words are vital to the figure To that extent it is शब्दालङ्कार It is also अर्थालङ्कार in so far as there is आरोप of one अथ over another In the कार्यरूपक राजा एवं हस, we have आरोप of हस on राजन् This depends on अर्थ, for one अर्थ is आरोपित on another To that extent we have in परम्परित रूपक an अर्थालङ्कार also Taken together therefore, परपरितरूपक embodies in it a शब्दार्थलङ्कार *i e* it is an उभयालङ्कार

 मम्मट had occasion to discuss in general the question of उभयालङ्कारऽ in the 9th Ullāsa in the course of his treatment of शब्दश्लेष \[It

must be admitted, however, that he does not specifically refer to श्लिष्ट परपरितरुपक there, as one is likely to believe, due to his words, शब्दार्थालङ्कारोऽयमित्युत्तम्\] मम्मट will again revert (वक्ष्यते च) to the topic of उभयालकारऽ at the close of this उल्लास (see p 222) where he, will state that (श्लिष्ट) परम्परितरुपक is an उभयालङ्कार. If श्लिष्ट परम्परितरुपक is thus an उभयालङ्कार, it ought not to have found a place here in the midst of अर्थालकारऽ, one might argue मम्मट replies -Though its right place should have been in the midst of उभयालङ्कारऽ, it comes to be dealt with here, owing to the usage (प्रसिद्धि) of Rhetoricians to assign it a place among अर्थालङ्कारऽ. For श्लिष्ट परपरित of ours is none else than what is styled as एकदेशवृत्ति (not एकदेशविवर्ति) by others like उद्भट who assign it a place among अर्थलङ्कारऽ

 It is difficult to say whether it is corrupt mss tradition or inaccuracy on the part of मम्मट that is responsible for the reading एवदेशविवर्ति But one thing is certain that उद्भट was the first to refer to एकदेशवृत्ति which is the same as परपरित रूपक He defines it with the words एकदेशवृत्ति स्यात्पररूपेण रूपणान् where पररूपेण रूपणान् refers to one रुपक being responsible for another. मम्मट seems to argue that श्लिष्ट variety, pertaining as it does to परपरित class, will come under its prototype viz the एकदेशवृत्ति of उद्भट And since एकदेशवृत्ति is assigned a place among अर्थालङ्कारऽ, to श्लिष्ट परपरित also has been conceded a like position

 There is also another inaccuracy in the statement. To judge from उद्भट's उदाहरण आसारधाराविशिसे etc, which is an अश्लिष्ट परम्परित it is evident that श्लिष्ट परम्परित was not being considered by him under एकदेशवृत्ति So मम्मट's 'इद अन्यै अभिधीयते' does not give us a correct picture of उद्भट's view point, if अन्यै refers to उद्भट's as in all probability it does

 It may be noted that एकदेशवृत्ति of others like उद्भट's is the same as मम्मटऽ एकदेशविवर्ति and, therefore, the reading एक्देशविवर्ति is incorrect

 अन्यै It is उद्भट only, so far as we know, who first recognised the परपरित variety though under the style of एकदेशवृत्ति भेदभाजि यथा भेदभाजि वाचके i e भिन्ने वाचके

 ** St. 35** आलान etc llustrates अश्लिष्ट परम्परित मालारूपक Eulogy of the arm of a powerful king जयकुञ्जरस्य आलान Victory never ships from the arm as an elephant can never slip away from the tying post विपद्वारिधे दृषदा सेतु A stone embankment to hold back the ocean of adversity करवालचण्डमहस पूर्वाद्रि The rising mountain of the Sun (चण्डमहस) in the form of sword (करवाल) The sword grows more

and more triumphant as it comes into the hand धिय लीलोपधान The pleasure-cushion of Wealth The royal glory of the king derives comfort from the arm सग्राम मन्दर the मन्दर mountain in the अमृतमथन in the form of battle मन्दर played a prominent part in the अमृतमथन The arm plays a prominent part in the battle. क्रीडाविधि hints at the ease and facility with wich the king fights. वीरवैरिवनिता वैधव्यद The arm reduces to widow-hood the wives of the heroic enemies who are slain on the battle field

 Here the आरोप of कुञ्जरत्व on जय of वाशिधत्व on विपद् of चण्डमहत्त्व on करवाल and of सागरप्रमथनत्व on सग्राम is responsible for the आरोप of आलानत्व, मेतुव पूर्वाद्रित्व and मन्दरत्व on the भुज Since some रूपकऽ are कारणऽ of certain others thus is परपरित रूपक Since there is no श्लेष जय being भिन्न from कुञ्जर, विषद् from वारिधि etc It Is अश्लिष्ट Since the same भुज is आरोपविषय of आलानत्व मैतुत्व etc the रूपक is मालरूपक Thus we have अश्लिष्ट परपरित मालारूपक Note that लीलोपधानं श्रिय is rather एकदेशविवर्ति रूपक Read मा घं लीलोपधानं श्रिय इति परम्परितमध्ये विवर्ति। श्रिय स्वीत्वानुत्ते।

 अग्र *etc* जय etc are भिन्नशब्दवाच्य from कुञ्जर ete They are not अभिन्नशब्दवाय or श्लिष्टवाच्य as in मानस दुर्गाम र्गण etc Only when on जय etc कुञ्जरत्व etc are superimposed does the superimposition of आलानत्व etc on भुज become plausible In plain words the आरोप of कुञ्जरत्व etc on जय etc is the कारण of the आरोप of आलानत्व etc on भुज

 **st 36** अलौकिक etc. Illustrates श्लिष्ट परम्परित शुद्ध रूपक Pra se of a king अलौकिक etc अलौलिक य महान् आलोक (प्रकाश) तेन प्रकाशित जगतश्य येन ad) of ०रत्न स्तूयते etc Construe वैभवान् न स्तूयते सदृशमुक्ता एर्न in apposition to भवान् Pearl of the excellent bamboo in the form of eminent family Bamboos are supposed to have pearls in them

 Here the रूपक सदृश (eminent family) ण्व सद्वश (excellent bamboo) is responsible for the कार्यरूपक दव ण्व मुक्तारानम् Both form परपरित रूपक Since सद्वत is श्लिष्ट it is श्लिष्ट परम्परित रूपक Since there is आरोप of one thing only viz मुक्तारत्न on देव it is शुद्ध Thus thus allustrates श्लिष्ट परम्परित शुद्धरूपक

 St. 37 निरवधि etc Illustrates अश्लिष्ट परम्परित शुद्धरूपक विष्णु praised as the origin of the world Construe निरवधि निराश्रयं च यस्य विवर्ष र म मूर्त इह प्रथम मान् उपति। निरवधि निरामयं च स्थित whose presence is infiute and unsupported विष्णु the of all does not require the support of anything else. अनिवर्तित *etc*. भनि कम्य प्राय देन who has not ceased causing a

maze of wonder or continued surprise इह प्रथम The first in this world, chronologically or influentially कूममूर्ति ref to कूर्मावतार. चतुर्दशलोक *etc* चतुर्दशलाका (भूर्भुवरादय) स्व वलि (लता) तस्या कन्द (मूलम्) For चतुर्दशलोक read उद्योत The bulbous root of the creeper in the form of the fourteen worlds, i e the origin of the fourteen worlds

 Here चतुदशलोका एव वल्लिः is the कारणरूपक which accounts for the आरोप of कन्दत्व on विष्णु For विष्णु could not be identified with a कन्द unless there were the आरोप of वल्लिच on ºलोक्ऽ This illustrates परम्परित रूपक It is अश्लिष्ट for ºलोका एव बल्लि has got no श्लेष It is शुद्ध for on विष्णु is superimposed only one thing viz वन्दत्व Thus here we have अश्लिष्ट परपरित शुद्ध रूपक

 इति च etc अनालारूपक *i e* शुद्ध रूपक

 **St. 38** किसल्य *etc* Illustrates रशनारूपक which variety मम्मट condemns as void of वैचित्र्य Description of the different agencies that मदन employs to win the hearts of lovers. They are लनाना किसलयकरऽ, (योषिता) करकमलऽ नलिनाना कमलमुखऽ and योषिता मुखेन्दुऽ Construe योषिता with करकमलै and मुखान्दुभि both

 Here there is रशना *viz* किसलया एव करा— करा एवं कमलानि— कमलानि एव मुखानि- मुखानि एव इन्दव Thus here we have a set of रूपकऽ knit together like a girdle the आरोपित of each preceding रूपक becoming the आरोपविषय of each following one

 इत्यादि रशनारूपक न वैचित्र्यवत् We have already seen at the close of उपमा, that मम्मट is intolerant of figures in रशना forms One fails to see what consideration weighed with मम्मट in his condemnation of रशनाऽ Is it, as is suggested, the lowering of the उपमान to the status of उपमेय that he disliked or is he perturbed over the inability of poets to give as रशनाऽ anything more than a clumsy patch work? That the very form of रशना imposed handicaps on the free play of the poets genius goes without saying It is again significant that किसलयकरे etc is given in the काव्यालङ्कार of रुद्रट from whom मम्मट has the honour to differ sometimes \[Co pp १०० १६४\] सम्प्रदायप्रकाशिनी finds fault with किसलय etc because here we have a series of things rather hanging loose- अत्र किसलयत्वादय भिन्नसम्बन्धिन करत्वादिरूपेण स्थानप्रतीतयश्च इति रशनात्वेनोपनिबद्धा अपि न वैचित्र्याय कल्पन्ते But thus is finding fault with the particular stanza, not the figure विश्वनाथ and जगन्नाथ, it may be noted have not recognised रशनरूपक See भामह cited at p 28 on मालोपमा इति न लक्षितम् So a separate definition of रशनारूपक is not given

**७ — अपह्नुति**

 Like रूपक which we have just dealt with, अपह्नुति is a figure based on अभेद In fact, it is रूपक supplemented by an अपह्नव or denial. मुख चन्द्र is रूपक, नेद मुख, चन्द्रोऽयन्, becomes अपह्नुति If in रूपक we have आरोप of one thing over another, in अपह्नुति we have आरोप backed by अपह्नव to the effect that the प्रकृत is not what it is To adopt मीमांसा terminology here we have विधि supported by प्रतिषध Needless to say that such a प्रतिषेध gives emphasis to the विधि चन्द्रोऽयम् Is not as emphatic as चन्द्र मुख चन्द्रोऽयम् That way, it could be said that, in अपह्नुति the poet goes one step farther, in his progress towards the goal of अध्यवसान reached in the figure अतिशयोक्ति

  Scope of the figure— (1) Primarly the figure is based on सादृश्य and मम्मट, to judge from his वृत्ति उपमेय असत्य कृत्वा etc (p ५९), seems to recognise the figure only as सादृश्यमूल In नेदं मुखं, चन्द्रोऽयम् the सादृश्य between मुख चन्द्र is विवक्षित In fact the earliest concept of the figure as given by भामह is based on सदृश्य or औपम्य (cp अपह्नुतिमीष्टा च विचिदन्तर्गतोपमा )

 (2) But rhetoricians were not to remain contented with such a restricted scope of the figure. In श्लिष्ट परपरित रूपक typified by विद्वन्मानस हस (p ५३) we saw how the poet superimposed मानस (the lake) on मानस (the mind) even when strictly speaking there is no सादृश्य between the lake and the mind. That both are conveyed by the same word मानस was enough for the poet to superimpose one on the other In other words एकशब्दप्रतिपद्यत्व was the bare modicum of सादृश्य between मानस (the lake) and मानस (the mind) with which the poet was satisfied and which he appreciated as highly as the सादृश्य between मुख and चन्द्र It mattered very little if in the former pair the सादृश्य was शब्दगत *i e* श्लेषमूलक while in the latter it was भयगत

different senses\] Similarly, ताप कराति सोत्कप, ज्वर कि, न सखि स्मर' \["It causes me fever with shiver" "Is it fever ?" "No, it is love, O friend!"\] is another case of अपह्नुति, for ताप करोति सोत्कप is denied of fever, but asserted of love, though in a different sense

 (3) Another extension of the figure is found illustrated by नाय सुधाशु, किं तहिं सुधाशु प्रेयसीमुखम् \[This is not the moon; rather the face of my beloved is the moon !\] where the उपमान (सुधाशु) is demed as being what it in fact is and the उपमेय (प्रेयसीमुख) is looked upon as being the same as उपमान (सुधाशु) This does hint at सादृश्य

 दण्डिन् has a definition comprehensive enough to include all such cases He defines the figure as अपह्नुतिरपह्नुत्य किञ्चिदन्यार्थसूचनम्। 'न पञ्चेषु स्मरस्नस्य सहस्र पत्रिणामिति' \[Cupid is not five arrowed, his arrows number a thousand\] is an अपह्नुति according to him.

 मम्मट's definition of the figure is strictly confined to (1) viz to those cases of सादृश्य, where the उपमेय is denied and उपमान is established instead The वृत्ति उपमेय असत्यकृत्वा उपमान सत्यतया यत् स्थाप्यते bears this out Case (3) though based on w falls outside the scope of मम्मटऽ definition Case (2) based on शब्दसादृश्य only cannot be embraced by the definition in the light of मम्मट's वृत्ति

 Divisions— To judge from मम्मट’s वृत्ति and उदाहरणऽ, he seems to have adopted divisions of अपह्नुति as follows – The निषेध and आरोप may be embodied in two वाक्यऽ भेद मुख चन्द्रोऽयम् (or even more than two when the denial assumes the form of opinions of others from whom the poet differs as in अफ केपि शशङ्किरे etc) This results in भिन्नवाक्यगत अपह्नुति Sometimes the निषेध and आरोप may be put in nutshell by the use of words like छल, छद्म, कपट, व्याज, बपु, परिणाम, कैतव, मित्र, आत्मा, नाम, which are embodied in one वाक्य only. मुसच्छद्म चन्द्रोऽय means, in effect नेद मुख चन्द्रोऽयम्, भृगच्छलेन कालकूटमिद means नेमे भृङ्गा कालकूटमिदम्- This gives rise to एकवाक्यगता अपह्नुति Thus we get the following scheme—

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708232760अप.PNG"/>

 Read चित्रमीमांसा - इय च साक्षात्रिषेधकनमादियोगे परपक्षत्वाद्युपन्यासे निषेधसामर्थ्याद्गम्यत्वे च वाक्यभेदेन प्रेवनन, विषयासत्यत्वप्रतिपादव छद्म-निभ-कपट व्याजादिशब्दप्रयोगे

विषयासत्यत्वार्थवमाविरूप-वपु-नाम- भङ्ग्यादिशब्दप्रयागे च तदभेदेन। Again र ग.— अस्यां च नत्रादिभि साक्षात्परमनसिद्धत्वाद्युपन्यामिश्च किञ्चिद् व्यवधानेन विषयम्य निषेधे बोध्यमाने प्रायशो वाक्यस्य भेद। मिप-छल-छद् -कपट-व्याज-वपु-आत्मादिशब्देस्तु तस्मिंस्तस्यैक्यम्।

 कुवलयानद gives as many as sx sub-divisions of the figure— (1) शुद्धा where the उपमेय is denied or उपमान established instead, नाय मुर्षात्तु किं तर्हि ष्यामगङ्गामरोरुहम् (This is not the moon, but it is a lotus in the heavenly Ganges!) (2) हेत्यपद्भुति where the reason is given for denying the उपमेय नेन्दुर्स्नाद्रो न निश्यवले सिन्धोरौर्वोयमुत्थित \[It is not the moon, for it is not hot, nor is it the sun, for it is not found at night time, it is the sub-marine fire that has shot up from the ocean — said about the moon by a विरहिजन\] ( 3 ) पर्यस्तापह्नुति where उपमान is denied as उपमान and the उपमेय is established as उपमान. नाय सुधांशु कि तर्हि सुधाशु प्रेयसीमुखम् (4) भ्रान्तापह्नुति where delusion of another party is removed ताप करोति सोत्कम्प ज्वर कि न मखि रमर। Here a विरहिणी नायिका says, ताप करोति सोत्यस्यम्. Another as misled into thinking that it is ज्वर that does it The first speaker corrects saying that it is स्मर, not that is responsible ! (5) छेकापह्नुति (छेकः = विदग्ध an astute person) is the exact reverse of Here in order to put a person off the scent, an irrelevant thing is asserted and the relevant thing denied मजयन् मदेम का किन कि नपुर Here नायिका says to her friend fe .............. लग्न The सरगै asks कान्त किम् The नायिका replies न नपुर Here कान्त was relevant to the mind of the नायिका, but to put her friend off the scent, she referred to नूपर the relevant (6) ..............where wonds like ...., मित्र, ...... etc are used .................

 अपह्नुति was first recognised by मम्मट As said above, मम्मट looked upon it as ....................... . दण्डिन् widened Its scope to include all cases of denial with assertion, inespective of सदृश्य उद्भट,......, रुद्रट understood the figure to be सदृश्यन्. That मम्मट also held the same view has been made clear रुह्यक,..........,.......scemed like दण्डिन् to take a broader view of the figure मम्मट based it on सदृश्य.

 अपह्नुति and व्याजोक्ति See व्याजोक्ति

 ***Essentials***— \[किञ्चिदपह्नुयते अत्र) In अपह्नुति (1) one thing must be denied and another established instead In other words there must be निषेध and आरोप (2) The denial must be आहार्य or intentional and not स्वारसिक In नेद मुख चन्द्रोऽय, the poet *purposely* denies that it is not मुख when he is fully conscious that it is मुख (3) The aim of the denial and assertion may be to convey सादृश्य The सादृश्य may be अर्थगत or even शब्दगत as we saw above (4) The निषेध may be (a) in the form of a simple negation नेद मुख, or (b) may be given in the form of an opinion of some, which the poet finds unacceptable, as in अङ्क केऽपि शशङ्किरे, जलनिध पङ्कपरे मोनरे wherein the different views re the spot on the moon are given by the poet only to refute them with his own view तन्सान्द्र निशि पीतमधनसम कुक्षिस्थमाचक्ष्महे॥, or (c) may be expressed by words like छल, छ्द्म, कैतव, कपट, व्याज which are विषयामत्यत्व प्रतिपादक *i e* which directly convey the असत्यत्व of the प्रकृत thing, *e g* मुखच्छद्मा चन्द्रोऽयम, or it may be indicated by words like रूप, वपु, नाम, परिणाम etc. which are in the words of चित्रमीमांसा, विषयासत्यत्वपर्यवसायि i e whose upshot is that the प्रकृत is असत्य (5) This results in the अपह्नुनि being embodied in two वाक्यऽ if the निषेध and आरोप are explicitly given as in नेदम् मुखम्, चद्रोऽयम् or in वाक्यऽ two or more than two, if the निषेध is given in the form of different views as in अङ्क केऽपि etc \[If only one view is given, one वाक्य for निषेध, one for आरोप, if more than one view are given, as many वाक्यऽ for निषेध as the views, and one वाक्य for आरोप\] or in one वाक्य like रामाभिधान शशाङ्क प्रतिमानाटक I. 4 where words like रूप, वपु etc are used (6) The निषेध may precede the आरोप or vice versa Read रय्यक – अपह्नवपूर्वक आरोप, आरोपपूर्वकोऽपह्नवछलादिरमत्यव प्रतिपादकैर्वाऽपह्नवनिर्देश। पूर्वोक्तभेददये वाक्यभेद। तृतीयभेदे विवत्वापयम्।

 § सू २२ Construe. प्रकृत निषिध्य अन्यत् यत् साध्यते सा तु अपह्नुति..

 प्रकृत is the उपमेय, as the वृत्ति says. It is significant that the सूत्र is sufficiently vague to comprehend अपह्नुति whether सादृश्यमूलक or otherwise. And had it not been for the वृत्ति and one could have claimed मम्मट as one among the followers of दण्डिन्ऽ wider view on अपह्नुति निषिध्य after denying But as we saw above (Essential 6), the denial may either follow or precede. So निषिध्य need not be taken too strictly as implying that it precedes आरोप Read साहित्यदर्पण for a justification of मम्मट's indifference on this point „- अपह्नुतिपूर्वक

मारापस्य आरोपपूर्वकत्व चापह्नवस्येति द्वैविध्य चमत्कारविशेषासिद्धेरवधीरित ग्रन्थकारेण। निषेध also called निह्नव or अपह्नव in the form नेद मुख is आहार्य अन्यत्- प्रकृतात् अन्यत् = अप्रकृत= उपमान as the वृत्ति says साध्यते is estabished in the form चन्द्रोऽयं तु marks the transition to a new figure from the one preceding

 वृत्ति— उपमेय असत्य कृत्वा etc Note how the वृत्ति has narrowed down the scope of the सूत्र by making it refer to सादृश्यमूल figure only असत्य कृत्वा explains निषिध्य of the सूत्र The उपमेय is demed (intentionally of course)

 उपमान सत्यतया स्थाप्यते *e g* चन्द्रोऽय where the उपमान is established as the real thing i e there is आरोप of उपमान on the उपमेय which also as आहाय intentional Note that चद्रोऽय is a case of आरोप at should not be mistaken for अध्यवसान of अतिशयोक्ति which also assumes the form चन्दाऽय but is different from the former In चद्रोऽय in the अपह्नुति, the उपमेय is not lost sight of it is not निगीर्ण In चद्रोऽय in the अतिशयोक्ति the उपमेय is lost sight of it is निगीर्ण by the उपमान Note that मम्मट does not refer to the divisions of अपह्नुति though his उदाहरणऽ take note of them

 **St 39** अवाप्त etc Illustrates भिन्नवाक्यगत अपह्नुत The कलङ्क on the moon is denied as being कलङ्क and established as रजानेरमणी resting on the person of her lover the Moon after dalliance! Addressed by शिव, in one of his happy moments to पार्वती Construe– शैलतनये परिणत रुच शशाङ्कस्थ वपुषि प्रागल्भ्य अवाप्त नायकलङ्क विलसति। अमुष्य (चद्रस्य) बिङ्गल्दमृतस्य शिशिरे उरमि रविश्रान्ता रजनिरमणी गाढ शेते (इति) मन्ये। परिणतरुच शशाङ्कस्य of the moon whose splendour is at its height, प्रागल्स्य अवास that has attained such prominence or conspicuousness ॰शिशिरे उरसि on the bosom cool with the trickle of ambrosia that is dripping रजनि after her fatigue due to dallance derives comfort from the cool touch of the bosom The Moon is अमृतमय, hence the coolness रजनिरमणी रजनि एव रमणी (a case of रूपक.)

 Here there is अपह्नव of the उपमेय viz नाय कलङ्क and आरोप of the उपमान viz इय रजनिरमणी The अपह्नव and आरोप are embodied in different वाक्यऽ Hence भिन्नवाक्यगता अपह्नुति Is cd a case of उपेक्षा? The word मन्ये points to its presence. But the उत्प्रेक्षा is only confined to रतिभ्रान्ता शेते इति मन्ये It is not रजनिरणी इति मन्ये It at were so it would be a case of सम्भावन and not आरोप that is necessary for अपह्नुति So the आरोप is रजनिरमणी इयम्

 **St 40** यत सखि *etc* Illustrates एकवाक्यगा अपह्नुति The grievance that a विरहिणी makes of the tantalizing effects of the sight of a mango blossom and bees Construe— स्मरस्य अस्मिन् रागिलोके कियद् वैर पश्य। अमुष्मिन् रागिलोके typified by herself बत Alas! cd Construe अनेन च्छलेन प्रतिविशिख कालकूट उट्टकितम्। उपवने यानि सहकाराणि तेषु उद्भाासन ये भृङ्गा तेषा छलेन In the deceptive form (छल) of the glistening bees on the mango blossoms in the garden affare on every arrow (in the form of the mango blossom) सहकार or चूत 1s one of the five arrows of स्मर उट्टकितम् - विनिहितं टक् ( 10th conj) to cover The idea is Not only has Cupid kept his arrows ready he has also made them deadly by tipping them with poison in the form of the black bees!

 Here उपवनसहकारोद्भासिभृङ्गच्छलेन pithily conveys the double idea of अपह्वव and आरोप For it means न इमानि सहकाराणि विशिखा इमे। न इमे भृङ्गा कालकूटमिदम्। The word छल (like छद्म व्याज etc ) can concentrate so much meaning in itself As the अपह्नुति is thus in one वाक्य (viz उपवनसहवारोद्भासिभृङ्गच्छलेन प्रतिविशिख कालकूट उट्टङ्कित) this is एकवाक्यगा अपह्नुनि As seen above चित्रमीमांसाकार looks upon छल and similar words as विषयामत्यत्वप्रतिपादक because by them is *expressed* the idea of owing to the shade of deception implicit in their meaning वपु अभिधान रूप etc on the other hand are विषयामयत्पर्यवसायि *i e* they do not express अपह्वव bat imply it, as there is no shade of deception in their meaning

 वृत्ति अत्र दि etc unfolds the अपह्नुति that is tersely condensed in the word छल

 **St 41** अमुष्मिन् etc Gives another instance of एकवाक्यगा अपह्नुति The line of hair at the navel of a lovely lady is denied as being such and established as a line of smoke coming out of the charred remains of Cupid who when burnt by Siva must have in utter agony taken a plunge in the region of her hips Construe नून शर्मप्लुष स्मर मृगदृश अमुष्मिन् लक्भ्यामृतसरराम प्रयुजवनभागे निपचित (भवेत्) यद् (अस्या) नानिकुहरे अङ्गाङ्गाराणां प्रशम पिशुना इय भूमस्य शिखा रोमावलिपु परिणमनि। शवप्लुष्ट burnt by शिव (= शव) ºसरसि पृथुजघनभागे A comphment to the beauty of the hips which are in fact a lake of ambrosia of beauty स्मर must have taken a plunge therein to get relief from the acute agony of hus burring body अङ्गाङ्गराणां प्रशमपिशुना adj of धूमस्य शिखर Indicating (पिशुन) the extinction of the embers of the charred body The embers got extinguished as मदन plunged in the lake. When

embers are extinguished they send forth a line of smoke रोमावलिवपु
परिणमति assumes the form of the line of hair at the navel (नाभिकुहरे)

 Here धूमस्य शिखा रोमावलिवपु परिणमति embodies अपह्नुति. For, it means, as the वृत्ति says न रोमावलि, धूमशिखा श्यम् As the अपह्नुति is in the body of a वाक्य, it is एकवाक्यगा वपु condenses in itself both निषेध and आरोप Acc to चित्रमीमांसा, it belongs to the असत्यत्वपर्यवमायि class

 सम्प्रदायप्रकाशिनी calls अपह्नुतिऽ that are condensed in words like छद्म, वपु etc as प्रतीयमान as distinct from वाच्य अपहृतिऽ where denial and assertion are explicitly stated. अपह्नुतिऽ assuming the form of opinions of others may come under प्रतीयमान division, though the Com.. does not specifically say so

 एवमिय etc On these very lines (एव), this figure (इय = अपह्नुति) is conceivable (उह्या= तर्क्या√ उह1 P A to conceive) in other modes (भङ्गि or भङ्गी) also The other modes may refer to the use of other similar words छद्म, कैतव, अभिधान, etc or, it may just comprehend that mode of अपह्नुति where the denial assumes the form of परमत as in अङ्क केऽपि etc But it can be said with certainty that the भङ्ग्यन्तरऽ cannot refer to the varieties (as in the कुवलयानन्द), not based on सादृश्य For मम्मट's वृत्ति उपमेयममत्य सत्यतया यत् स्थाप्यते सा अपह्नुति has, once for all narrowed down the scope of the figure to सादृश्य

**८ - श्लेष**

 With श्लेष we pass on to a figure the विच्छिति (= वैचित्र्य) of which lies in one and the same statement conveying senses more than one That way it is fundamentally different from the figures that have preceded At best it may be said that while the preceding figures were based on अर्थगl सादृश्य in श्लेष we come to a figure based on शब्दगतसादृश्य Languages generally speaking, uniformly present the phenomenon of fluidity of meaning Words originally meant strictly to convey a particular idea happen later to be used in a different connotation As we saw in उद्गम II, रुढि लक्षणा typifies a tendency similar in nature कुशल that meant one thing (“handler of kuśa grass") when the word was first used, came to acquire another meaning ("expert") at a later stage. In the Rgveda रम् meant to stop (cf a पर्यवन् प्राप्नुवन् अभवत्), in classical Sanskrit, the word came to mean 'to amuse’ ‘to sport'-an easy transition from the physical

domain to the psychical one ‘Ardent' (etymologically ‘burning') represents a similar transfer from the physical to the psychical sphere But, in all these cases, the *original* meaning, in course of time, is lost sight of Who but an etymologist is today aware of the original signification of कुशल, रम, 'Ardent', 'Camouflage' ‘Boycott’?

 But many words, even when they are adopted in other spheres, still coninue to hold on to their original meaning. Though today we never talk of ‘ardent' fire as we talk of 'ardent' love we can yet speak of ‘burning fire’ and ‘burning love’ or ‘burning question' So रक्त may mean ‘passionate' and 'red' both अम्बर may mean sky' and ‘garment’ both उदय may mean ‘rise’ and ‘prosperity' both It is precisely such words that he at the root of the figure श्लेष श्लिष्ट word, in fact, means one where two meanings cling together (लिप्यत अर्थौ अस्मिन्) Though every language in general, possesses such cases of double entendre, it may be justifiably claimed for Sanskrit that it of all languages, has explored and exploited its possibilities best Sanskrit Subhasitas regale us with श्लेषऽ and so do the writers of Sanskrit romances, like सुबन्धु and बाणभट्ट

 From the position assumed above it would seem that the same word comes to convey a double meaning The word उदय meaning ‘sunrise' is the same as the one meaning prosperity Sanskrit Rhetoricians even admit this to be the view of 'some' (?) As against this there is another view according to which a word like उदय meaning 'sunrise' is only similar to— and not the same as— another, meaning ‘prosperity' प्रतिप्रवृत्तिनिमित्तं शब्दभेद (for every different meaning, a different word is used), they argue Thus प्रतीहारेन्दुराज on का सा सं ४ ९ states अर्थभेदेन तावच्छन्दा भिद्यन्न इति भट्टोद्रटम्प सिद्धान्त मम्मट, to judge from the वृत्ति एकार्थ etc, seems to hold the same view

सभङ्गश्लेष and अभङ्गश्लेष— Until now we saw how a word like उदय is capable of conveying two meanings. Here the two meanings are to be had from the same word without splitting it which, therefore, is called अभङ्गश्लेष But sometimes we find the poets making use of the same set of syllables which when split up differently resolve into different component words yielding different meanings. We have already seen (St. 34 ) how दुर्गामार्गण can be split up as दुर्ग + अमार्गण or दुर्ग + मर्गण  
the meaning varying in the two cases. So, in the same

 Is श्लेष an independent अलङ्कार ? Wherever श्लेष (शाब्द or आर्थ) occurs it is found accompanied by some other figure like उपमा, रूपक, दीपक, तुल्ययोगिता etc. \[In the instance स्ताकेत etc given above we have उपमा along with श्लेष\] This has tended to give rise to three schools of thought re the status of श्लेष (1) Some say that its presence annuls the other co-existing figure (2) Some others say that श्लेष can subsist as an independent अलङ्कार along with another (3) Others hold that it is itself annulled by the other figure र ग thus sets forth these views - अय अलकार प्रायेणालकारान्तरस्य विषयमभिनिविशेत तत्र किमम्य बाधकत्व स्यात् आहोस्वित् माणिक्यत्व उताहो बाध्यत्वं सा द अल्कारसर्वस्व and र ग hold that श्लेष can claim an independent status Others (?) believe that it is annulled by the other co-existing figure while उद्भट is of opinion that it annuls the other co-existing figure मम्मट apparently admits श्लेष as an independent figure

 भामह recognised though faintly under the name श्लिष्ट after him दण्डिन् treated श्लिष्ट as a काव्यगुण and also as an अलकार उद्भट also recognised the figure as श्लिष्ट वामन was the the first to use the term श्लेष After him रूदट, रुय्यक, मम्मट, विश्वनाथ and others admitted the figure

 The scope of the figure— मम्मट in his treatment of व्यञ्जना (उ II) had occasion to touch upon the topic of अभिधाव्यञ्जना, where a statement having a double significance had one meaning वाच्य and other व्यङ्ग्य due to one thing being प्रकृत and another अप्रकृत In श्लेषालकार on the other hand both the meanings are वाच्य and refer to things which are both प्रकृत or both अप्रकृत It is easy to understand how both the meanings may be प्रकृतविषय In etc. (St. 42) we have a statement with two meanings one applying to the sun another to a king विभाकर by name Both the sun and the king are प्रकृत as the poet intends to describe both Hence it is an instance of श्लेष

 But how can we have both meanings अप्रकृतविषय ? Is it not self contradictory to say that a poet describes something अप्रकृत ? For do we not think that whatever the poet describes is प्रकृत ? This difficulty is obviated when we understand that in अप्रकृतविषयक श्लेष both the meanings apply to things that are both उपमानऽ So अप्रकृतविषयक is precisely speaking उपमानविषयक The प्रकृत may be there and is there but the statement with double significance does not refer to it. It refers to two उपमानऽ with which the उपमेय is compared Thus in अब्जेन त्वन्मुर्ग तुल्य हरिणाहिव्यक्तिना \[Your face is similar (1) to the moon (अब्ज) with which the deer (spot) has established contact, and (2) to the lotus

(अब्ज) with which the sun (हरि) has established contact \] We have अप्रकृतविषयक श्लेष, because अब्ज, the lotus and the moon, are both उपमानऽ in relation to त्वन्मुख which does not come within the purview of the श्लेष अब्जेन हरिणाहितभक्तिना to which the figure is confined refers with its two meanings exclusively to the two उपमानऽ That this is शब्दश्लेष is a different matter Later when we come to the figure तुल्ययोगिता, we shall get another case of an अप्राकृतविषयक figure This gives us the following divisions of अर्थश्लेष—

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708262280्््.PNG"/>

 **Essentials**— \[रिध्यत अर्थी अस्मिन्\] (i) The statement must be such as has a double significance, applying to two things. (ii) Both the things must be प्रकृत or both अप्रकृत (iii) Both the meanings must be वाच्य otherwise it becomes a case of अभिधामूलव्यञ्जना (iv) In the statement, the विशेषणऽ and विशेष्य must both be श्लिष्ट That is to say, the attributes and the thing qualified by them must be conveyed by words having a double significance. In विभाकर उदयमयन, the विशेषण उदयमयने means (1) rises (2) prospers, the विशेष्य विभाकर means (1) the sun (2) king Vibhakara. (v) The words in the statement must be पर्यायपरिवृत्तिसह *i e* they must be such as could be replaced by their synonyms

 अर्थश्लेष *vs* समासोक्ति See under समासोक्ति

 § सू २३ श्लेष स etc Construe यत्र स्वस्मिन् वाक्य प्रतीकार्थता भवत् स श्लेष। where, in one statement, we have senses more than one, the figure is श्लेष वाक्य statement अनेकार्थता capacity to yield senses more than one.

 वृति— यत्र To judge from this वृत्ति मम्मट seems to belong to that school which held that a word can convey one and one meaning alone. was the gunding principle of this school The question arises as to how to reconcile The reply seems to be in the fact that though a word conveys only one meaning still there may be another word just similar to it which possesses a different meaning Thus two words just simular conveying two meanings seem to be one word conveying two meanings. prosperity is different from 'rise'. But the two words being quite similar, we are lulled into believing

that one word उदय conveys a double sense, while, as a matter of fact, here are two words conveying two meanings The wording एकार्थ प्रतिपादकानामेव शब्दानां यत्र अनेकोऽर्थः can well exclude सभङ्गश्लेष, a शब्दालङ्कार, from the scope of अर्थश्लेष, but it cannot exclude a word like, मानस used in a double sense (1) mind (2) the Mānasa lake, which is also a शब्दालकार as we said above सभङ्गश्लेष is excluded because therein we have two sets of words not at all similar, one to the other दुगाया मार्गण is not at all similar to दुगाया अमार्गण, in the sense in which उदय ‘prosperity' is similar to ‘rise’ But a case like the use of मानस in a double sense though it is शब्दश्लेष is not excluded because here we have two similar words, मानस-mind, मानस-Manasa lake, conveying two meanings To that extent मम्मट's वृत्ति seems to suffer from अतिव्याप्तिदोष

 **St 42** उदयमयते etc Illustrates अर्थश्लेष Description of the meritorious excellence of the Sun and a king named विभाकर उदय (1) rise,(2) prosperity दिङमालिम्य (1) The gloom of the quarters, (2) the sullenness of quarters due to misery prevailing therein

 निद्रामुद्रा निधन नयति (1) Puts an end to the seal of sleep (2) Puts an end to languid looks प्रवर्तयति क्रिया (1) Enables the activities of creatures to proceed, (2) Sets afoot the different activities, religious or otherwise, in the kingdom

 तरा in निराकुरुनेतरा and रचयतितरा expresses the effectiveness of the actions. स्वैराचारप्रवर्तनकर्तन (1) The स्वैराचार of night is put an end to by the Sun, (2) the king puts a stop to all frivolous acts विभाकर (1) the Sun, (2) King विभाकर

 Here one statement yields a double sense, one applying to the sun, another to a king named विभाकर Both the sun and विभाकर are equally प्रकृत and, therefore, both the meanings are वाच्य (not one वाच्य and another व्यग्य when one thing is प्रकृत and another अप्रकृत as in अभिधामूल व्यञ्जना also called शब्दशक्तिमूलध्वनि) The words उदयमयते etc. are परिवृत्तिसह i e the meaning does not suffer if we use their synonyms. This, therefore, is an instance of अर्थश्लेष There is one word, however, which is परिवृत्त्यमह and it is विभाकर विभाकर, the name of a particular king can not be replaced by any other word and, to that extent, in the श्लेष we have शब्दश्लेष To tide over this drawback, some (e g Jhalkikar) interpret विभाकर as meaning a king in general (विना नव्या राज्यस्य प्रकाशं करोतीति विभाकर)

 It should be bome in mind, however, that this same instance will cease to be one of श्लेष, if there were the least indication that one of

the two— the sun or the king-alone is प्रकृत and the other अप्रकृत It will then be a case of अभिधामूलव्यञ्जना If one were to assert that here king विभाकर is प्रकृत and the sun is अप्रकृत or vice versa it would become a case of अभिधामूलव्यञ्जना

 अत्राभिधाया वाच्यौ— मम्मट says that in the instance उदयमयते etc both the senses are equally वाच्य for the expressive power of the words (अभिधा) is unrestricted In शब्दशक्तिमूलध्वनि (= अभिधामूलव्यञ्जना), illustrated by the etc. (उ II), one meaning is वाच्य another व्यङ्ग्य due to the son of the words being restricted by either of the circum stances enumerated in the कारिका-संयोगो विषयोगश्च etc (Vide उ II p 65) Here there is none of these restrictive circumstances operating and therefore both the meanings applying to the sun and the king are वाच्य That is why this becomes an instance of श्लेष

 अभिधाया अनियन्त्रणम् अभिधा is the power of a word by which the word conveys its primary or direct sense It is one of the three powers the other two being लक्षणा & व्यञ्जना (Vide उ II) अनियन्त्रण Circumstances like संयोग etc restrict the अभिधा of a word As soon as अभिधा is restricted one sense alone is वाच्य while the other becomes व्यग्य If in our instance उद्यभवने etc., one say king विभाकर, were प्रकृत then the meanings of the words उदय etc., as applying to king विभाकर will be वाच्य while as applying to the sun they would become व्यग्य Here none of these circumstances is operating and therefore both the meanings are वाच्य as they should be in श्लेष It may be noted in passing that शब्दशक्तिमूलध्वनि is not admitted by the कुवलयानन्दकार who says that even with these circumstances operating both the अर्थऽ are वाच्य द्वौ अपि अर्कभूपौ वाच्यौ *i e* the meanings as applying to the sun (अर्क) and the king are both वाच्य We are to suppose that when the poet uttered this stanza he was thinking equally of both the sun and the king विभाकर

**९ — समासोक्ति**

 In समासोक्ति we come to a figure based on शब्दगतसादृश्य like श्लेष In रूपक, मुखं चन्द्र there is the आरोप of the रूप or form of चन्द्र on मुख In समासोक्ति, on the other hand, there is the आरोप of the व्यवहार or behaviour of one thing over another It is customary with poets to see in the action of a particular thing the striking reflection of the action of something else When the king clutches the sword in his hand and makes the hostile army flee, the poet is put in mund of the action of a रूपक who as soon as he holds the makes the प्रतिनायिका turn away in huff And as soon as the poet remembers this, he

describes the king's action of clutching the sword and the consequent flight of the enemy's army with such ingenious transparency that the behaviour of the नायक, नायिका and प्रतिनायिका happens to be suggested thereby This is the achievement of समासोक्ति As कुवलयानन्द says समासोक्तावप्रस्तुतव्यवहारसमारोपश्चारुताहेतु। न तु रूपक इव प्रस्तुतेऽप्रस्तुनरूपसमारोपोऽस्ति। Again रुय्यक— अव छेदकत्व च व्यवहारसमारोपो न रूपसमारोप। रूपसमारोपे त्ववच्छादितत्वेन प्रकृतस्य तद्रूपरूपित्वादेव रूपकम्।

 So what the poet does in समासोक्ति is this. He describes a particular action of the प्रकृत thing in such a way that the सहृत्य is at once reminded of the corresponding action of the अप्रकृत thing even when the the अप्रकृत thing is not mentioned in so many words In fact the charm of the figure lies in the poet so describing the action of the प्रकृत that the अप्रकृत is at once suggested In यस्य रण करे कुर्वतो मण्डलग्रलताम्। रसममुख्यपि महमा पराङ्मुग्रीभवति रिपुसेना॥that नायिका and प्रतिनायिका are the अप्रकृत is quite transparent. In अयमैन्द्रीमुख पश्य रक्तश्चुम्वति चन्द्रमा, we at once see how the व्यवहार (or वृत्तान्त to use the word of कुवलयानन्द) of the नायक is attributed to the moon This is as the आलकारिकऽ describe the आरोप of the व्यवहार of the अप्रकृत on the प्रकृत

 Can we set our finger on the mainspring of the poet's technique that enables him to make us conscious of the अप्रकृत, though it is unspecified? To begin with the poet is careful in the choice of the लिङ्ग of the प्रकृत, so as to make it correspond to that of the अप्रकृत In अयमैद्रीमुख पश्य रक्तश्चुम्पति चद्रमा the चन्द्रमा is masculine like the नायक, केन्द्री is feminine like the नायिका Then the poet attributes such transparent qualifications (विशेषणऽ) to प्रकृत the as can easily suggest the अप्रकृत रक्त in the instance is such a विशेषण, it applies to the ruddy (रक्त) moon it also applies to the sanguine (रक्त) नायक. The qualifications stated are therefore, such as apply to the प्रकृत as well as the अप्रकृत They may apply to the प्रकृत and the अप्रकृत in the same sense that is विशेषणऽ the may be साधारण as in अघेन पातर्भान्या सञ्चरना विषमविषयेषु। दृढमिदं मया गृहीता निम्नगिरिशृङ्गारदुपगता गङ्गा॥ Where विशेषणऽ the apply to the प्रकृत गङ्गा and the अप्रकृत यष्टि almost in the same sense. Or they may apply to the प्रकृत and the अप्रकृत in different senses that is the विशेषणऽ may be श्लिष्ट रक्त in अयमैद्रौमुख etc. is श्लिष्ट. This gives us the following scheme—  


<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708405538समा.PNG"/>

 विश्वनाथ, the author of the साहित्यदर्पण, descnbes समासोक्ति as समै कायलिङ्ग विशेषणे व्यवहारसमारोप meaning thereby that there can be समासोक्ति based on लिङ्गसाम्य, कार्यसाम्य and विशेषणसाम्य On a closer scrutiny, it will be found that these classes are not mutually exclusive and boil down to one class viz. विशेषणसाम्य We cannot have a समासोक्ति based on mere लिङ्गसाम्य without विशेषणसाम्य also we cannot have a समासोक्ति of कार्यसाम्य which is not also one of विशेषणसाम्य, if we understand the word विशेषण in its wider meaning of words giving attributes- and not its strictly grammatical sense of an adjective

 समासोक्ति is a figure dating as far back as भामह दण्डिन् recognised it, though his definition of it was far from precise. वामन, रुद्रट, उद्भट, मम्मट, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ admitted it दण्डिन्, वामन, and रुद्रट, however, seem to look upon समासोक्ति as अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा of a more suggestive type

 समासोक्ति is essentially based on गम्य औपम्य The विशेषणऽ by their transparency suggest the अप्रकृत and the सादृश्य is led to guess the likeness between the प्रकृत and the अप्रकृत The उपमान is never mentioned, nor is there any word like इव or यथा to convey औपम्य The औपम्प In समासोक्ति being thus suggested becomes more effective than expressed औपम्य अय ऐन्द्रीमुखं पश्य रक्तश्चुम्बी चन्द्रमा is more effective than अय ऐन्द्रीमुखं पश्य रक्तश्चिम्बी चन्द्रमा नायक इव

 Essentials - \[समसो प्रस्तुतप्रस्तुतवृत्तान्वया उक्तिः अत्र नभा समासेन अभ्युपरात् समासोक्तिः रामचरण सर्वतागदा gives a fantastic explanation समस्य कार्य दे आसन (1) There must be the statement of some व्यवहार or वृत्तान्त (behaviour action) of something which is प्रकृत; (2) This statement must be so transparent that it must at once put us in mind of the behaviour of the अप्रकृत, (3) this transparency should be the result of लिङ्गमान्य and विहारगम्य , (4) the विशेषणऽ may be श्लिष्ट or शुद्ध (5) The विशेषणऽ alone must be applicable to the प्रकृत and अप्रकृत both the विशेष्य referring to the प्रकृत only (6) The ध्यरगम्यामान्य must be only suggested and must in its turn, suggest the different corres-ponding suggests to us that action of the, is similar to that of a passionate lover This in its turn, suggests that, is compared to a lover and to his beloved \]

 समासोक्ति vs अर्थश्लेष -(i) In समासोक्ति, there is a statement of the प्रकृत suggesting an अप्रकृत in श्लेष the statement relates to two things both प्रकृत or both अप्रकृत (ii) In , only the or words expresave of attribres have a double significance, in, the

drive at their transparency ? परोक्ति used roughly for पराक्षेपः the suggestion of the पर (i. e. अप्रकृत). The पर is only hinted at, never mentioned. The word उक्ति, that way, is misleading.

 The definition, as it is, is none too precise. The word श्लिष्ट, strictly interpreted, would leave out शुद्धसाधारणविशेषणऽ which also, as seen above, may give us समासोक्ति Read कुवलयानन्दः अत एव श्लिष्टविशेषणायामिव साधारणविशेषणायामण्यप्रस्तुतव्यवहारसमारोप इत्येव प्राचीनानां प्रवादः। The word परोक्ति, as seen above, is a bit misleading. But the flagrant drawback of the definition seems to lie in its failure to indicate that it is व्यवहार or वृत्तान्त of the पर not the पर itself-that should be suggested, in the figure. परोक्ति has to be interpreted, therefore, as परवृत्तान्ताक्षेपः

 **वृत्तिः**— प्रकृतार्थ etc प्रकृतार्थ = प्रकृतवृत्तान्त श्लिष्टविशेषणमाहात्म्यात्— needless to say that माहात्य refers to the transparency of the epithets. न तु विशेष्यस्य सामर्थ्यादपि means that the विशेष्य, not being श्लिष्ट is not समर्थ for अप्रकृतार्थप्रतिपादन. As seen above, if the विशेष्य were also श्लिष्ट, the figure will be श्लेष, not समासोक्ति श्लिष्ट विशेषण should comprehend शुद्ध साधारणविशेषण also. अप्रकृतस्यार्थस्य अभिधानंः अभिधान means suggestion here. सा समासेन etc. The name is explained. समासेन सङ्क्षेपेण i. e. in brief. In one statement are concentrated two meanings (अर्थद्वय): one applicable to the प्रकृत, the other to the अप्रकृत. Note the fantastic explanation of the name by रामचरण तर्कवागीश, given above.

 St. 43. लहिउण etc. Illustrates समासोक्ति. The laments, on the death of her heroic husband, of a widow, who pictures to herself the consequent sad plight of जयलक्ष्मी (Glory-of-Victory, personified). संस्कृत छायाः लब्ध्वा तव वाहुस्पर्शं यस्याः स कोऽप्युल्लासः। जयलक्ष्मीस्तव न विरहे न खलूज्ज्वला, दुर्बला ननु सा॥

 तव बाहुस्पर्श लब्ध्वाा refers to victory (जयलक्ष्मी) being embraced by the hero. तव विरहे now that the hero is dead. न खलूज्ज्वला दुर्बला ननु सा when the hero has ceased to live, not only has जयलक्ष्मी ceased to be gay (उज्ज्वला) but she has also grown feeble-a widow's plight. The idea is: With your death, my beloved, chances for victories in battles have become precarious!

 Here the description primarily referring to the sad plight of जयलक्ष्मी becomes suggestive of the sad plight of a नायिका due to (1) The Feminine gender of जयलक्ष्मी (2) the phrases बाहुस्पर्शे लब्ध्वा बोध्यापि उत्पन्नः and तव विरहे न खलु उज्ज्वल दुर्बल ननु सा। These tho phrases are applicable to जयलक्ष्मी and नायिका though in slightly varying senses. Victory became effective (उल्लास) with the help of the hero's arm: a नायिका becomes

spightly with the touch of the arm of the नायक The chances of victory faded when the hero ceased to live the नायिका grows sullen when separated from the नायक The chances of victory became precarious when the hero was dead, the नायिका grows feeble when the नायक ceases to live

 Thus the femiune gender of जयलक्ष्मी which is प्रकृत and the transparent phrases, बाहुस्पर्शं लब्ध्वा etc, mentioned above convey to us the behaviour and plight of a नायिका on the death of the heroic नायक Thus we have here a समासोक्ति

 अत्र जयलक्ष्मी etc Here all the descriptive phrases (विशेषण) viz तव बाहुस्पर्श लब्ध्वा etc have a double meaning one applying to the प्रकृत (जयलक्ष्मी), the other to the अप्रकृत (viz नायिका or कान्ता ) Only the प्रकृत विशेष्य (जयलक्ष्मी) conveys one meaning (viz Victory) the अप्रकृत viz कान्ता being only suggested, not expressed In short, the विशेषणऽ have a double meaning, the विशेष्य a single one That is why it is समासोक्ति If the विशेष्य also as in उदयमयते etc (St 42) had a double significance, the figure would have been श्लेष

 आलकारिकऽ, like विश्वनाथ जगन्नाथ, have made an attempt to classify the व्यवहारसमारोप i e to classify broadly the प्रस्तुतऽ and अप्रस्तुतऽ in समासोक्ति विश्वनाथ for instance says (1) क्वचिल्लौकिके वस्तुनि लौकिकवस्तुव्यवहारसमारोप (2) शास्त्रीये वस्तुनि शास्त्रीयवस्तुव्यवहारसमारोप (3) लौकिके वस्तुनि शास्त्रीयवस्तुव्यवहारसमारोप (4) शास्त्रीये वस्तुनि लौकिकवस्तुव्यवहारसमारोप इति चतुर्विध They support this classification with instances that are striking no doubt But it must be admitted that barring लेकिके वस्तुनि लैकिकवस्तुव्यवहारसमारोप other व्यवहारसमारोपऽ have a smack of pedantry and not poetry about them शास्त्रीये लौकिकस्य व्यवहारसमारोप illustrated (र ग) by कृत्वा सूत्रै सुगूढार्यै प्रकृते प्रत्यय परम्। आगमान् भावयन् भाति वैयाकरणपुगव (आरोप of राजव्यवहार which is लौकिक on the व्याकरणशास्त्र ) may be pedantically a success Assessed poetically is it worth anything?

**१० — निदर्शना**

 In समासोक्ति, we saw how औपम्य was conveyed by the use of विशेषणऽ with double significance In निदर्शना, we come to a figure wherein औपम्य between two things is conveyed, in a veiled manner, by one thing being described as being the same as the other When दुष्यन्त, at the sight of शकुन्तला's superb beauty, exclaims— 'शुद्धान्तदुर्लभमिद वपुराश्रमवासिनो यदि ननस्य।दूरीकृता खलु गुणैरुद्यानलना वनलताभि '॥, we find him Identifying one fact viz, आश्रमवासिन वपु सम्पन्नत्व with another viz, उद्यान लताना वनलताभि अभिमव One is as good as the other, says दुष्यन्त Thus

two sentences connected by correlatives (here यदि) are used, each containing an idea apparently *unconnected* with the idea in the other, but, for that very reason, suggesting the similanty between the two Ideas What has वपु मम्पन्नत्व to do with बनल्ताभि अभिभव, we ask ourselves And our आकाक्षा is satisfied when we conclude that the two apparently unconnected facts are juxtaposed only with a view to driving at their similarity Rustic beauty outstripping sophisticated refinement is tantamount to a wild creeper eclipsing the artificially cultivated garden creeper we conclude Another striking instance of निदर्शना embodied in two वाक्यऽ will be इद किलव्यानमनाहर वपु तप क्षम साधयितु य इच्छति। ध्रुव स नीलोत्पल्पत्रलेखया समिल्लना छेत्तुभृपिर्व्यवस्यति II, said by दुष्यन्त again

 The same effect may be produced in the body of a single sentence also दोर्भ्यो अब्धिं निनीर्यन्त तुष्टयुक्ते गुणार्गवम् Seeking to cross the ocean with the help of their arms, they praised thy ocean of ments— says a poet Here the attempt to enumerate the hero's merits is described as being as rashly ridiculous as an attempt to cross the ocean with nothing more than the two arms Thus गुणार्णवस्तुति and दोर्भ्याम् अब्धितरण, both apparently unconnected, are here juxtaposed in the body of a single sentence, with the eventual result that one thing comes to be looked upon as being tantamount to the other

 The same effect may be produced in the body of a single word also Admiring a lovely face the poet is found saying मुख पद्मशोभा विभर्ति Looking to a lordly elephant the poet says गज गिरित्यन्तं वहति Scanning the gait of a lady the poet says कामिनीपदभ्यां राहम गति स्वीकृता We ask ourselves How can मुख assume the charm of a lotus, the elephant the charm of the mountain the human feet the gait of a swan? And we conclude that these convey similarity To put it in the words of the साहित्यदर्पण अत्र अन्यन्य धर्मे मन्यो वहतु इति बिम्बप्रतिबिम्बभावे बोधयति In short, when we attribute to one thing the characteristics of another apparently unconnected with it, there results the निदर्शना in the body of a word Here too, it may be noted, that the juxtaposition of two apparently unconnected things like मुखचमच गजगिरि etc., by the employment of such words as eta, वपु, शोभा, results eventually in the similarity between the two being conveyed सा द called this variety एकवाक्यगा , the remaining two being In अपह्नुति, it should be clearly bome in mind, we use words like छद्म, छल, वपु with the shade of demal in them and compounded with the उपमेय Cf भृङ्गच्छेदेन रोमचरणेषु In निदर्शना the words

शोभा etc., have no trace of denial and are further compounded with the उपमान पद्मशोभा, गिरेलीला etc. Further, अपह्नुति appears as भृङ्गच्छन्न कालकूटमिदम्। निदर्शना will appear as भृङ्गा कालकूटशोभा दधते।

 One feature, it may be seen, is shared in common by all these three varieties viz therein facts apparently unconnected happen to be juxtaposed and this results in the साम्य between them being conveyed That is why these varieties of निदर्शना are called असभवद्वस्तु सबन्धा, *i e* those in which there is no apparently possible connection between the things stated.

 These varieties may be possible in शुद्ध or माला form In शुद्ध we have one प्रकृत Juxtaposed with one अप्रकृत In माल्य we have one प्रकृत Juxtaposed with several अप्रकृतऽ

 Again, though it is normal for the poet to attribute to the उपमेय the उपमानधर्म, मुख पद्मशोभा बिभर्ति, the poet sometunes produces the same poetical effect, possibly with a greater emphasis, by attributing to the उपमान the उपमेयधर्म Thus in वियोगे गौडनरीगा यो गण्डतल्पणिम्मा अदृश्यत सा खर्जूरीमपरागर्भरेणुषु ॥ or in योऽनुभूत कुरङ्गाक्ष्यास्नस्या मधुरिमाधरे। समास्वादि स मृद्वीकारसे रसविशरदै॥ the tables are turned and to the उपमानऽ viz खर्जूरीगर्भरेणुऽ and मुद्रीकारस, are attributed the characteristics of the respective उपमेयऽ viz, गण्डतल and अधर साहित्यदर्पणकार admits this possibility As जयरथ says in bus विमर्शिनी यथोपमानसम्बधी धर्म उपमेयगतत्वेनैव सभवति तथोपमेय सम्बधी। धम क्वचिदुपमानेगमे। रुय्यक (अ सर्वस्व) - इय सामान्येनैव असभवात् प्रतिपादित उपमेय वृत्तस्योपमाने सभवादपि भवति । उभयत्रापि सम्वन्धविनस्य विद्यमानत्वात्।

 If in the 3 varieties given above, we have असभवस्तुसम्बन्धत्व as the common feature there is a fourth variety of निदर्शना based, as the आलकारिकऽ say, on सभवद्वस्तुसम्बध The सम्बन्ध in question to judge from what the आलकारिकऽ say is none other than बोधकसम्बध Nay, रसगङ्गाधर goes to the extent of styling this vanety as बोधननिदर्शना कुवलयानद defines it as अपरा बोधन प्राहु

 In this variety, the poet describes some particular fact as illustrative of a general truth. In चूनमणिपदे धत्ते र्योऽवरे रविमागनम्। सना कायानिधेर्याति बोधयन् गृहमैधिन॥, the action of the उदयाचल in supporting the sun on its top is described as illustrative of the general rule that a householder should accord hospitality to the good See again उदयन्नेव सविता पद्मैष्वध्वयति श्रियम्। विभावयन् समृद्धीना फल सुहृदनुग्रह॥ That the particular fact is allustrative is conveyed, sometimes by the use of words like इति बोधयन्, निदर्शयन्, कथयन् etc or sometimes by the use of इति only tacatly conveying the idea of इति बोधयन् etc.

 It will not be wide of the mark if one were to say that असम्भवद्वस्तुसम्बन्धा वाक्यगा निदर्शना bears to दृष्टान्त the same relation that सभवद्वस्तु सम्बधा does to अर्थान्तर यास See under दृष्टान्त and अर्थान्तरन्यास

 Thus we get the following Scheme—

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708323342निद.PNG"/>

For निदर्शना vs दृष्टान्त, see दृष्टान्त For निदर्शना vs अर्थान्तरन्यास, see अर्थान्तरन्यास

 निदर्शना vs रूपक – There is no occasion for the पदार्थरूपक like मुख चन्द्र being mistaken for निदर्शना But निदर्शना is likely to be confused with वाकयाथरूपक admitted particularly by जगन्नाथ जगन्नाथ is at paths to prove that स्वत्पादनखरनाना यदक्रमार्जनम्। इदविधो॥ is a वावयार्थरूपक, because acc to him, there is an आरोप of पाण्डुरौकरण on अलवनमार्जन But the same idea put in the form of a निदर्शना will appear as त्वपादनखरत्नानि या रञ्जयति यावकै । इन्दु चन्दन नगन्नाथ contends that in रूपक we are concerned with आरोप while in निदर्शना, we have आर्थ अभेद which is quite exclusive of अरोप or अधयवसान \[ Read र ग— ण्वं चारोपाध्यवमानमार्गदर्भूत अर्थ ण्वभेदे निदर्शनाजीवितम् \] Judged by this standard, रानमेवा मनुष्याप्रमनधरान इनम्। unaandar Artazar, is just to be appraised as a वाक्यार्थरुपक, though the कुवल्यानन्दकार says it as वाक्यार्थवृत्तिनिदर्शना

 Is जगन्नाथ justified in his view point ? It seems he is not वाक्यार्थरुपक itself, for that matter, seems anomalous. So far as one can sec, no other आलङ्कारिक recognises it. In a वाक्य, क्रिया is प्रधान क्रिया is always in the making and, as such, it seems well nigh impossible for one representing something in the making to be wilt on another also representing something else in the

making riere one is put in mind of the reason why we cannot have उपमा when इव etc is put after a verb like लिम्पति in लिम्पतीव etc When जगन्नाथ talks of आरोप of वाक्यार्थऽ and वाक्यार्थरूपकऽ really speaking, he denies all scope to निदर्शना त्वत्पादनसरनाना यदलक्तकमार्जन is taken to be a निदर्शना by रुय्यक the अलङ्कारसर्वस्वकार.

 निदर्शना is a figure dating back from भामह whose defimtion only embraces the सभवद्वस्तुसबधा vanety After him, दण्डिन् referred to निदर्शना in terms sufficiently vague to comprehend सभवद्— as also असभवद्वस्तुसम्बधा उद्भट, of all आलङ्कारिकऽ names the figure as निदर्शना but explicitly talks of the two सबधऽ अभवत् and भवत् वामन seems to revert to भामह's narrow view about निदर्शना रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित and जगन्नाथ give definitions and corollaries wide enough to comprehend both types of सम्बन्ध

 Essentials— \[निदर्शन दृष्टान्तवरणम्— मम्मट\] (1) It, in असभवद्वस्तुसबध variety, is a figure based on सादृश्य between two things or sets of things. which have बिम्बप्रतिबिम्बभाव between them \[जयरथः-एव चात्र निदर्शनाया सादृश्या विनाभाव। र ग – अस्या च घटकपदार्थाना बिम्बप्रतिबिम्बभाव आवश्यक।\] (2) The सादृश्य must be गम्य only not वाच्य \[Cf भामह – ज्ञेया निदर्शना नाम यथेववतिभिर्विना। \] This makes particles like इव etc and other औपम्यप्रतिपादक words. and affixes conspicuously absent from निदर्शना (3) In असभववस्तुसबन्धा अनेकवाक्यगा, we have two (in माला form, more than two) sentences. connected by correlatives like यद् तद् यदि तर्हि, generally expressed but sometimes implied also (4) Correlatives introduce facts which apparently are unrelated but for that very reason, become औपम्यपर्य वसायि (5) In एकवाक्यगा (असभवद्वस्तुसम्बन्धा) we have the contents of अनेकवाक्यगा compressed in one by an adjustment of syntax The एकवाक्यगा दोर्भ्यो अब्धिं तितीर्पन्तस्तुष्टुवुस्ते गुणार्णवम् will be अनेकवावयगा ये ते गुणार्णव स्तुवन्ति ते दोर्भ्याम् अब्धिं तितीर्षन्ति। (6) In पदार्थगा the attribute of the उपमान is assigned to the उपमेय by the use of words (like लीला, शोभा) with the tenor the उपमेय assumes the grace (or disgrace when औपम्य relates to an अमत् thing) of the उपमान (7) In सभवद्वस्तुसम्बन्धा, some particular fact is stated to typify some general truth by the use of words like इति बोधयन्, इति निदशयन्, इति वधयन्, इति विभावयन् or by mere इति which tacitly means इति बाधयन् etc. (8) In सम्भवद्वस्तुसम्बन्धा, we have one वाक्य with the tenor typifying the general truth, here is a particular fact

 For माला and other instances see Appendix C

 § सू २५ Construe— (4) वस्तुसम्बध अभवन् (सन्) उपमापरिकल्पको (भवति) स निदर्शना भवति। निदर्शना is the connection between things which, because

it is improbable, tends to establish similarity between the things. As साहित्यचूडामणि says: तात्पर्यचिन्तायाम् उपमायाः परिकल्पकः The upshot of things, apparently unconnected, standing in juxtaposition, is to suggest their likeness. In other words, on the face of it, there is no relevancy in juxtaposing the things which are apparently unconnected. Hence we are forced to admit them as being similar (उपमापर्यवसायी), Note that मम्मट's definition is a good deal like that of रुद्रटः अभवन् वस्तुसबन्न्धो भवत् वा यत्र कल्पयेत्। उपमानोपमेयत्वं कथ्यते सा विदर्शना॥

 वृत्तिः निदर्शनं etc. explains the propriety of the name. निदर्शना is a figure based on निदर्शन i e adducing a similar instance (दृष्टान्तकरणम्). See the figure दृष्टान्त In निदर्शना, in all but one variety, the poet is found aiming at giving us things similar to the प्रकृत. In the सम्भवद्वस्तुसबन्न्धा, however, he aims at describing a particular fact as typifying a general truth.

 ** St. 44**. क्व सूर्य etc. Illustrates असम्बद्वस्तुसम्बन्धा अनेकवाक्यगा निदर्शना. The कविकुलगुरु--कालिदास -- in utter humility refers to the vastness of the task of tifa that he has set about achieving with his 'meagre’ intellect. सूर्यप्रभवः वंशः The Solar Race. अल्पविषया मति i.e. the मति can comprehend only petty things. Subjects as vast as the रघुवंशवर्णन will baffle it ! उडुप (पः or पं) उदुनः (=जन्यत्) पानि (=रक्षति) इति a boat, a raft.

 Here in two sentences two facts are juxtaposed: In one sentence— that the poet is attempting, with his petty intelligence, to describe the solar race. In the other-that the poet aspires to negotiate, out of delusion, the vast ocean with the help of a raft. As these two facts, standing in no apparent relation, are juxtaposed, we are led to conclude that one is as good as the other. In other words, they become औपम्यपर्यवसायिन्. Thus we have bere a निदर्शना which is अनेकवाक्यगा and असम्भवद्वस्तुसम्बन्धा. As there is one उपमान only, it is शुद्धा (not मात्र). In हा वंशः ... क्व मतिः we have विषमालङ्कार

 अत्र *etc.* मन्मत्या सूर्यावर्णनम् Note that this idea is only implicat in the first line of the श्लोकः क्व सूर्यप्रभवो वंशः It is not explicitly stated and, for this reason, sriadas like arzaziĝa (gazasin) have taken exception to this stanza being cited as an instance of निदर्शना, अध्यम्यक्ष calls it an instance of धन Read वानन्दः- ....गरम्॥अवविनिदान।विषयमा सूर्यकेतु दशी मनम्यानुरूप यार बिश्वभूमाद उद्द्येन मगर दिल्ली पुरानीदन प्प नियङ्गम्॥र्ग. details views

both for (नव्या) and against (प्राञ्ज ?) assuming farm in the stanza He, however, justifies it as निदर्शना (Read his remarks under निदर्शना) इति उपमाया i e इति औपम्ये, not actual उपमा इति i e talking the तात्पय into consideration

 **St 45** उदयति *etc* Illustrates पदार्थगा असभवद्वस्तुसबधा निदर्शना Description of the रैवतक mountain (near, the capital of the आनर्तऽ, North Kathiawar) at sunrise Construe अहिमरुचौ उदयति हिमधान्नि च अस्त याति अय गिरि वारणेन्द्रलीला वहति When the sun (अमरुचि hot lustred) rises and the moon (हिमधामन् cool lustred) sets this mountain wears the grace (लीला) of an elephant (वारणेन्द्र) embellished (परिवारित) with two bells dangling from its two sides. विततोर्ध्वरश्मिरत्नौ (with their upward rays stretched out like ropes) adj of अहिमरुचौ and हिमधाम्नि both Then only will विलम्बिधण्टाद्वय have its counterpart उदयति and याति are Loc. sing Pres Part Masc उदि and या

 Here to the गिरि is attributed the लीला (grace) of an elephant How can one wear the grace of another, we ask ourselves And we are satisfied when we interpret this juxtaposition as driving at the Similanty of the गिरि to the वारणेन्द्र Thus as गिरि (लीला) and वारणेन्द्रलीला though apparently unconnected are juxtaposed in so far as गिरि वारणेन्द्रलीला is attributed we have असभववस्तुसवधा निदर्शना As the निदर्शना occurs in the body of a पद viz वारणेन्द्रलीला, it is पदार्थनिदर्शना As the प्रकृत, गिरि, is identified only with one उपमान viz वारणेन्द्र it is शुद्धा Thus this is असभवद्वस्तुसम्बधा शुद्धा पदार्थनिदर्शना Note, however, सा द acc to whom these are एकवाक्यार्थनिदर्शनाऽ

 अत्र कथमन्यस्य etc अयस्य लीला *i e* वारणेन्द्रस्य लीलाम् अन्यो वहति *i e* गिरि वहति तसदृर्शी i e वारणेन्द्रललासदृशीम् उपमाया = औपम्ये likerness

 **St 46** दोर्भ्यां etc Illustrates माला form of अनेकवाक्यगा असभवद्वस्तुसम्बधा निदर्शना The poet aims at showing by three analogies that his patron's virtues are too vast to be narrated तरङ्गवतीभुजङ्ग the ocean (the lover of rivers)

 Here, with गुणाना गदनोद्यम are juxtaposed the three facts apparently unconnected with it They are तरङ्गवतीभुजङ्गतितीर्षा, हरिणाङ्कबिम्बमादशतुमिच्छा and मेरुल्लिङ्घिषा So in the result गुणाना गदनोद्यम, the sane प्रकृत becomes compared or identified with the three अप्रकृतऽ or, roughly speaking उपमानऽ That is why it is माला in form Being embodied in वाक्यऽ, more than one it is अनेकवाव्यगा Since the प्रकृत is apparently uncon nected with the अप्रकृतऽ, it is असभवद्वस्तुसम्बन्धा निदर्शना Note that it is not an

उत्प्रेक्षा though the word ध्रुव is there for सभावन or poetical fancy is absent

 इत्यादौ we have already seen how there can be मालारूप also in एकवाक्ययाः पार्थगा and सभवद्वस्तुसम्बधा

 § सू २६ स्वस्वहेतु *etc* Construe (या) क्रियर्यव स्व-स्वहेतु-अन्वयस्य उक्ति सा अपरा (निदर्शना)।That is the other variety of निदर्शना, wherein a particular action itself declares the relation (अन्वय) between its cause and itself This purports to mean Some particular act in the world is traced back by the poet to some general truth In other words here a particular fact as it occurs is described as typifying some general truth The definition is very much reminiscent of वामन's क्रिययैव स्वतदर्थान्वयख्यापन निदर्शनम्,

 वृत्ति— क्रिययैव etc. The एव possibly means— The thing only as it occurs declares its relation साहित्यचूडामणि gives an explanation of ण्व which is too fantastic to be cogent क्रियातदनौ सम्बन्धप्रतीतावपि यत्र सहकारिनैपेक्ष्य तत्र चमत्कारातिशय इत्याह— क्रिययैवेति। भावगम्यते is passive of the causal of गम् with अब Note वामन s word ख्यापन and मम्मट's वृत्ति on St 47 where he uses स्थाप्यते The poet makes the particular क्रिया announce or convey the relation that subsists between itself (स्वम्नुरूप) and its cause (स्वकारण) Read नागेश्वरी अवगम्यते णितलमेतत् तिलमेनन्। ख्याप्यते इत्यथ। अपरा i e समवद्वस्तुसम्बधा as distinct from the अभवद्वस्तुसम्बन्धा detailed above

 **St 47** उन्नत etc Illustrates समवद्वस्तुसम्बधा शुद्धा The topping down of a light stone from the hill top with a gentle breeze is given as typical of the fall from eminence of a man of levity Construe यसन्न पदमवाप्य पोतिन् शोसन छ्यलण चाम्माइलपुत (मन्) भय पनि। य हेचैन पतेन् Whosoever suffers from levity will suffer a fall easily (देव) if he were to be raised to eminence यं कुवल्यानन्द reads वृद्गण In place of इति वदर र गं reads इति ध्रुवं and remarks इति पये का न्यादा अपने

 Here the fact that the light particle is unseated with the slightest jerk of the wind is stated to account for itself as being due to the general truth Undeserved eminence precariously held succumbs to the slightest disturbance In other words -more accurately is made to convey the relation between fall and its

cause viz attainment of eminence on the part of one that is लधु (light or frivolous)

 Here the दृषत्पातक्रिया is capable of intimating the general truth of the cause effect relation between undeserved eminence and fall. That is why it is सभवद्वस्तुसबन्धा कुवलयानद and र ग are more categoncal and, for that matter, helpful also, when they call it बोधननिर्देशना That the समवत्सबध Is बोधनसबन्ध can be clearly made out from the words of र ग on the stanza चूरामणिपदे etc. cated above – गिरेश्च सूर्योदयदेशावच्छिन्न शिरखनारूपस्य गृहमेधिगतमदातिथ्यवरणविषयकत्वाधानुकूलाचरणस्य सभवात् मभवदस्तुसम्बध मूलापि निदर्शना सम्भवति। Again read जयरथ– अभ्यागतस्य रवेगिरिणा शिरसा धारण तत्पमर्थाचरणम्। अत एवात्र बोधयन्त्रिति णिचस्ममर्थाचरणे प्रयोगान्मयेव भवद्भिरपि अतिथिमपया कार्या इति सभवत्सम्बधमूलमत्रार्थमौपम्यम्॥मम्मट's definition स्वस्वहेत्वन्वयम्याक्ति क्रियवैव, is too vague to bring out the essential feature of the figure which, as seen above, consists in making a particular fact typify some general truth In fact, मम्मट, to judge from his वृत्ति, seems to take सभवद्वस्तुसबध as referring to two facts being related as cause and effect The क्रिया is the effect of some cause, to that extent there is सबधसभव The क्रिया itself intimates (or, is made to intimate) this सबध This seems मम्मट's view point about this variety.

 There arises a question due to the occurrence of words like इति
बोधयन् etc. in this variety of निदर्शना. When the poet attaches to the प्रकृत like उद्याचल, in the instance given above, the attnbute of being बोधक etc. is he not tndulging in an implied उत्प्रेक्षा ? For उदयाचल सना आतिथेया चार्या इति बोधयन् (इव) देव रविं चूनमणिपदे धत्ते, which is गम्य (इव being understood) उत्प्रेक्षा, seems to be the tenor There are two schools among आलङ्कारिकऽ that answer the question differently जयरथ, for instance says there is no उत्प्रेक्षा here, for बोधयन् does not mean बोधयन् इव but simply बोधयन् The rule being that the causal formation clearly indicates the capacity of the agent to carry out the action in question (तत्समर्थाचरणे प्रयोगात्) र ग also subscribes to this view but thinks it just possible that it may be a case of गम्य उत्प्रेक्षा, acc to the view by which a कर्तृ of किया is यनाश्रय while here the उदयाचल, say, makes no यत्न and is still fancred as making the यत्न in so far as it is a कर्तृ

 वृत्ति— अत्र पातकिया etc. पातक्रियया to be accurate दृषत्वणपानक्रियया Here the action of the stone's fall is made to intimate (ख्याप्यते causal ख्या) the relation (cause-effect) subsisting between fall and attainment of eminence (उन्ननपदप्राप्ति) by one suffering from levity (लघवे सति) लघवे सति उन्नतपदप्राप्ति is the कारण, पात is the कार्य As sard above, मम्मट seems to

have in view the मम्मट ofकार्यकारणभाव as lying at the root of this variety That is why it is aggan acc to him That this is not the view of other आलङ्कारिकऽ will be gathered from the remarks of र ग and जयरथ cited above. In fact,वामन from whose definition मम्मट seems to have partly borrowed also does not hold this view about सम्बन्ध स्वतदर्थान्वयख्यापन निदर्शनम् is his definition which he explains in his वृत्ति thus अतुल्यावधाय पतनायेर्थशास्त्रिना शमत्॥पततीति क्रिया। तस्या स्व पतनम्। तदर्थोऽयुचपदाध्यास पतनायेति दासनम्। Thus, acc to वामन also, the सम्बन्ध बोधनसम्बन्ध -- not कार्यकारणसम्बन्ध

**११ — अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा**

 In समासोक्ति, we saw how the अप्रस्तुत was suggested by the use of transparent विशषणऽ applied to the प्रस्तुत. In अप्रस्तुत, (statement (प्रशंसा) of the अप्रस्तुत) to be precise, in one variety (सादृश्यनिबन्धना) of it, we have an exact antithesis of समासोक्ति That is to say, we have such विशषणऽ applied to the अप्रस्तुत in अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, that they by their transparency produce in us a consciousness of the प्रस्तुत The poet, here, instead of making a direct statement about the प्रस्तुत, depends on the power of suggestion-not very powerful though-and makes the अप्रकृतव्यवहार suggest by its similanty the प्रकृतव्यवहार Thus in one variety of it. अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा is based on गम्य औपम्य Between the अप्रस्तुत and प्रस्तुत there is revealed सादृश्य in this variety

 But sometimes the relation between the अप्रस्तुत प्रस्तुत may be one of कार्य-कारण or कारण-कार्य also अप्रस्तुत being कारण or कार्य, and the प्रस्तुत, कार्य or कारण respectively So when the poet wants to hint at the कारण which therefore is प्रस्तुत he may describe the कार्य or vice versa

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708278301ननन.PNG"/>

 Are सारूप्य कार्यकारणभाव and सामान्यविशेषभाव the only possible relations between अप्रस्तुत and प्रस्तुत ? Read कुवलयानन्द— नह्यप्रस्तुतप्रशसाया प्रस्तुता प्रस्तुतयो पञ्चविध एव सबन्ध इति नियन्तु शक्यते। सम्बन्धान्तरेष्वपि दशनात्। सहोपत्त्या विमपि सम्बधान्तरमाश्रयणीयमेव।

 One might naturally ask why the poet takes a fancy to the description of the अप्रस्तुत, when he is primanly concerned with the प्रस्तुत The answer hes in the fact that in this figure the poet wants to be suggestive and at the same time regale us with a picturesque description of the अप्रस्तुत Thus when the poet describes the सदृश or कार्य or कारण or सामान्य or विशेष all अप्रस्तुतऽ, he means to suggest to us the प्रस्तुत सदृश or कारण or कार्य or विशेष or सामान्य respectively and, at the same time give the अप्रस्तुत in a picturesque setting Nay we may go a step further and say that in the last four varieties (based on कार्यकारणभाव or सामान्यविशेषभाव) he finds the अप्रस्तुत more picturesque and therefore describes it the suggestion of the प्रस्तुत being its natural sequel When र ग is inclined to look upon these cases as belonging to the गुर्णाभूतव्यङ्ग्य class he means as much In the case of सदृश्य प्रस्तुत and अप्रस्तुत (i e the first variety) how ever as both the प्रस्तुत and अप्रस्तुत are equally picturesque the poet s aim in describing the अप्रस्तुत seems to lie only in the suggestive effect Read र ग— एवं सादृश्यमूलप्रकारे द्वैतम् (i e ध्वनित्व वा न वा)।कार्यकारणभाव सामान्यविषयभावमूलास्तु चत्वार प्रकारा गुणभूतव्यङ्ग्यसैव भेदा।

 The first variety based on सादृश्य deserves a closer study par ticularly in the light of its wider implications assumed by र ग A stanza like मल्निऽपि रागपूर्णां विकसितवनामनपतल्पेऽपि। त्वयि चपऽपि च सरमा भ्रमर क्थ वा सरोजिनी त्यजति॥ apparently addressed to a भ्रमर, may be construed in two contexts (1) It mught be taken as addressed primarily to भ्रमर the प्रकृत suggesting the behaviour of अप्रकृत नायक—
