when it becomes a case of समासोक्ति, or (2) it might be *apparently* addressed to the भ्रमर, the अप्रस्तुत, when actually the नायक is प्रस्तुतः— when it becomes a case of अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, or (3) it may be addressed to the भ्रमर who is as much प्रस्तुत as the नायक For it may be just the remark of a ससी who actually sees before her the भ्रमरवृत्तान्त and नायकवृत्तान्त both and describing भ्रमरवृत्तान्त hints at the नायकवृत्तान्त both being प्रस्तुत Even in this case, it is अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा , acc to र ग. अप्रस्तुत, acc to र ग, connotes here whatever is मुख्यतात्पर्यं विषयातिरिक्त— whatever is not primarily aimed at by the poet \[अप्रस्तुतशब्देन हि मुख्यतात्पर्यविषयाभूताथानिरिक्तोऽर्थो विवक्षित।\] स च क्वचिदत्यन्ताप्रस्तुत
क्वचित्प्रस्तुतश्चेति न कोऽपि दोषः। Thus भ्रमरवृत्तान, though occurring before the सखी, is not तात्पर्यविषय, her primary aim beng to refer to the नायकवृत्तान्त Thus भ्रमरवृत्तान्त is अप्रस्तुत acc to र ग the नायकवृत्तान्त प्रस्तुत This also is therefore, a case of अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, acc to him कुबलयानन्द takes it as a separate figure, प्रस्तुताकुर

 We have seen above that the varieties based on कार्यकारणभाव and सामान्यविशेषभाव are, in essence of गूणीभूतव्यङ्ग्य class The variety based on सादृश्य, however, will be ध्वनि If both are प्रस्तुत, as seen above in the third interpretation of मलिनेऽपि etc If one is प्रस्तुत and another अप्रस्तुत as in the second interpretation it becomes, in all probability a case of गूणीभूतव्यङ्ग्य Read र ग— इद तु वाध्यम्— अत्यन्ताप्रस्तुतस्य वाच्यताया तस्मिन्नपयवलितया अभिषया प्रतीयमानार्थस्य ध्वनित्व न निर्वाधम्। द्वयो प्रस्तुतत्वे तु ध्वनित्व निर्विवादमेव। एव सादृश्यमूलप्रकारे द्वैतम्। One thing is clear viz, in अप्रस्तुतप्रशसा we have a वाच्य which is higher than an अधमकाव्य – शब्दचित्र or अर्थचित्र

 सादृश्याबधना variety interests us also because of its numerous forms The सादृश्य between the अप्रस्तुत and प्रस्तुत may be शुद्ध or श्लिष्ट (i e based on श्लेष) The श्लेषमूल सादृश्य may be again of two kinds (1) The श्लेष embracing the विशेषणऽ and विशेष्य both, or only (2) the विशेषणऽ As the अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा purports to hint at the प्रस्तुत that being the poet's main concern, the poet does not wait to see if the अप्रस्तुत वर्णन is plausible or otherwise The अप्रस्तुत वर्णन plausible or otherwise must bring the प्रस्तुत to our mind this is the poet's sole aim Thus the poet will make a tree speak and hint at the प्रस्तुत Is it not implausible (असभवत्) on the face of it that a tree should speak? Sometimes the अप्रस्तुतवर्णन may not be such an implausible thing but a plausible (सभवत्) one which may be अप्रस्तुत
Occasionally, again the अप्रस्तुतवर्णन may be a combination of plausible and implausible things These varieties could be

described to adopt रूय्यक's nomenclature, as वाच्यार्थस्य असमवे, वाच्यार्थस्य सभवे and वाच्यार्थस्य उभयरूपत्वे As what appears implausible when viewed in itself appears plausible when viewed in relation to the प्रस्तुत, वाच्यार्थस्य असभवे, ^(॰)सभवे and ^(॰)उभयरूपत्वे could be respectively described as वाच्येऽध्यारोपे, वाच्येऽनध्यारोपे and अशे अध्यारोपे अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा as मम्मट does Thus we get the following sub-divisions of the सारूप्यनिबधना (the same as सादृश्यनिबधना) अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708156861सारूप.PNG"/>

 Read र ग— अस्या च वाक्या (१ च्या) थे' क्वचित् प्रतीयमानार्थताटस्थ्येनैवावतिष्ठते। क्वचिच्च स्वगतविशेषणान्वययोग्यतामामायितु प्रतीयमानाभेदमपेक्षते। क्वचिच्च प्रतीयमानमपि किञ्चिदशे वाच्यनादास्य, वाच्य च किञ्चिदशे प्रतीयमानतादात्म्यमपेक्षते। मम्मट gives allustrations not of each and every one of these varieties but numerous enough to illustrate the underlying principle

 अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा more popularly known as अन्योक्ति, a name dating from रूद्भट has made rich contributions to the Sanskrit Literature Didactic writers have found therein an effective medium to give instruction and advice. Seen in a clear perspective, what are the stories in the हितोपदेश and पञ्चतन्त्र, if not so many अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसाऽ ? कुवलयानन्दकार says ण्व बृहत्वथादिषु सामान्यतः यच्चित्रं प्रस्तुत्य तद्विवरणार्थमप्रस्तुतकथा विशेषोदाहरणेषु इयमेवाप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा द्रष्टव्या। A classical example of अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसाऽ is AEsops Fables The कार्यकारणभावमूला variety is least useful that way the सादृश्यमूल and सामान्य विशेषभावमूला figure prominently in didactic Sanskrit literature

 It may be noted incidentally, that it is not always easy to detect the figure अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा The difficulty arises due to uncertainty as to whether what is stated as प्रस्तुत or अप्रस्तुत When on the face

of it we have a wholly implausible or partly implausible thing as the वाच्यार्थ we are certain that it is अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा for unless we thank of it in the hght of the प्रस्तुत, it will make no sense But where no implausibility is involved nothing else but the knowledge of context would decide प्रस्तुतत्व or अप्रस्तुतत्व We have seen above how मलिनेऽपि रामपूर्णां etc becomes a case of समासोक्ति or अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा ace to the view we take of the प्रस्तुत therein The task of detecting the figure becomes much more difficult in the variety where कार्य or कारण or विशेष or सामान्य is अप्रस्तुत and the other correlate प्रस्तुत For who can be sure that the statement made is in the light of something else viz its cause or effect or सामान्य or विशेष?

For **अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा **vs **पर्यायोक्त **see **पर्यायोक्त**.

For **अप्रस्तुतप्रशसा ** vs **दृष्टान्त **see **दृष्टान्त **

For **अ प्र** vs **अर्थान्तरन्यास **see **अर्थान्तरन्यास**

 **अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा *Vs* समासोक्ति—** The variety of अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा which is likely to be mistaken for समासोक्ति is श्लिष्टविशेषणमूलकसादृश्यनिबधना i e the one in which the अप्रस्तुत विशेष्य is qualified by विशेषणऽ with two meanings one applying to the अप्रस्तुत the other to the प्रस्तुत In समासोक्ति also as we saw the विशेषणऽ have a double sign ficance And the obvious point of difference between अ प्र of this variety and समासोक्ति is that whereas in the अ प्र the वाच्य is अप्रस्तुत and व्यङ्ग्य प्रस्तुत in समामोक्ति the वाच्य is प्रस्तुत and the व्यङ्ग्य अप्रस्तुत This makes it possible for one and the same stanza to be अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा or समासोक्ति acc as we take the वाच्य as अप्रस्तुत or प्रस्तुत respectively In other words difference of context will turn a stanza into a case of अ प्र Or समासोक्ति Thus in appraising the instance मलिनऽपि रागपूर्णां etc. र ग says इत्याद्यप्रस्तुतप्रशं- सामप्रकृतव्यवहार साक्षादुपात्तत्वाद्विशेष्येणापि उपस्थापित ण्व यदि तु भ्रमरवृत्तान्त ण्व प्रस्तुतरता भवत्येवेयं समासोक्ति। Read माणिक्यचन्द्र विशेषणानां श्लिष्टत्वे प्रस्तुतस्य वाच्यत्वे अन्यस्य तु गम्यत्वे समासोक्ति। अप्रस्तुतस्य वाच्यत्वे प्रस्तुतस्य गम्यत्वे अप्रस्तुतमेति विवेक।

 अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा vs श्लेष— Here again that variety of अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा wherein both the विशेषणऽ and विशेष्य are श्लिष्ट is likely to be mistaken for श्लेष Because as we saw earlier in श्लेष also both विशेषणऽ and विशेष्य are श्लिष्ट What dist ngushes अ प्र of this class from श्लेष is that while in अ प्र the वाच्य is अप्रस्तुत and the गम्य प्रस्तुत in श्लेष the बाच्य and the गम्य are both प्रस्तुत or अप्रस्तुत Here again context alone could show us what is प्रस्तुत or अप्रस्तुत मा च— प्रस्तुताप्रस्तुतयो (१ = प्रस्तुतयो अप्रस्तुतयो वा) वाच्यत्वे विशेष्यस्यापि श्लिष्टत्वेऽर्थरेष।

 **अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा** is a figure dating back from भामह भामह and दण्डिन्, however, confined its scope to अप्रस्तुतस्तुति only उद्भट followed suit वामन first broadened its scope to include any statement whether स्तुति or otherwise (किञ्चिदुक्तौ अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा) was more explicit when he named the figure as अत्योक्ति But for reasons unknown, the name अन्योक्ति could not grow popular and we find successive writers मम्मट, रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित (कुवलयानद) and जगन्नाथ sticking to the name अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, though in the broad sense of a statement of the अप्रस्तुत

 **Essentials** – \[अप्रस्तुनस्य प्रशंसा Read कुवलयानद for the significance of प्रशंसा - प्रशंसाशब्द स्तुतिनिन्दास्वरूपाख्यानमाधारणकीर्तनमात्रपरो द्रष्टव्यः। र ग explains the name as अप्रस्तुतेन प्रशंसा \] The statement need not be necessarily in the nature of praise प्रशंसा means , statement, only It may be praise censure or neither of the two (II) The statement (प्रशंसा) must relate to the अप्रस्तुत and must be transparent enough to suggest the प्रस्तुत (III) The प्रस्तुतत्व or अप्रस्तुतत्व will be determined by our knowledge of the context or by the statement being wholly or partly implausible (असभवत्) on the face of it. (iv) As the suggestion of the प्रस्तुत is the poet's primary concern the poet does not bother about the plausibility or otherwise of the वाच्यार्थ He will make a tree speak; he will make a fox converse with a lion, \[It should be noted however, that when the speaker himself, in an abnormal state of mind addresses an object not normally addressed by us the case need not necessarily be of असभव\] Read कुवल्यानद (under प्रस्तुतङ्कुरालकार)— न चानामत्रणीयामन्त्रणेन वाच्यामभवादप्रस्तुतमेव वाच्य मौग्ध्यादिना भृङ्गादावण्यामन्यणस्य लोके, दर्शनात् (v) The statement, if based on सादृश्य will convey the सादृश्य with or without the help of श्लेष If श्लेष is used, it may embrace the विशेषणऽ only or विशेषणऽ and विशेष्य both (vi) If the statement is based on कार्यकारणस्य on some occasions, the कार्यकारणस्य relation may be ascertained without external help But a knowledge of the context may be necessary otherwise to ascertain that the particular कार्य (अप्रस्तुत) is the outcome of some (प्रस्तुत) कारण, or that the particular thing is कारण (अप्रस्तुत), in relation to something else कार्य (प्रस्तुत) (vii) When the statement is based on may, generally speaking suffice to determine the प्रस्तुत

 § स् २० अप्रस्तुत etc Construc या अप्रस्तुतप्रंशसा सा प्रस्तुतप्रशंसा सा एव Whatever is known as the figure अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा is literally so,

bearing on the प्रस्तुत सा प्रस्तुताश्रया सा एवः It ( i. e. figure) is literally that, based on प्रस्तुत Literally that i e literally अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, i e the figure consists in the statement (प्रशंसा= कीर्तन) relating to the अप्रस्तुत and is therefore true to its name. The definition is taken as a pattern by चन्द्रालोक of जयदेव (13th cent.) अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा स्यात्सा यत्र प्रस्तुताश्रया Cf. मम्मट's definition of व्यतिरेकः स एव स प्रस्तुताश्रया based on प्रस्तुत i e suggesting it.

 वृत्ति – अप्राकरणिक etc. अप्राकरणिक = अप्रस्तुत अभिधानेन Explains the significance of the word प्रशंसा प्रशंसा need not be praise only though this was the meaning in the early stages of the development of the figure. Any statement is प्रशंसा here Read माणिक्यचन्द्र : स्तुतिनिन्दासाम्यैवर्णनादृष्टौ प्रशंसा। अत्र वर्णना न स्तुति। साहित्यचूडामणिः — प्रशंसा इति वर्णनोपलक्षण निन्द्राया अपि वर्ण्यमानत्वात्। प्राकरणिकस्य आक्षेप : The suggestion of the matter under description. Why is the प्राकरणिक suggested ? Read मा च— तच्च अप्रस्तुत अप्रस्तुतत्वादेव वक्तमयुक्त सत् प्रस्तुतपरतया उच्यमान युक्त स्यात् Again सा— अयुक्त खलु अप्रस्तुतस्य वर्णनमप्रस्तुतत्वादेव। तत् प्रस्तुततात्पर्ये यदाचित् युक्त स्यात्।  

 § सू २८. कार्ये निमित्ते etc Construe Construe (1) कार्य प्रस्तुते सति तदन्यस्य (i. e. कारणस्य) वचः (2) निमित्ते प्रस्तुते सति तदन्यस्य (i e कार्यस्य ) वच, (3) सामान्ये प्रस्तुते सति तदन्यस्य (विशेषस्य) वच, (4) विशेषे प्रस्तुते सति तदन्यस्व (सामान्यस्य) वच; (5) तुल्ये प्रस्तुते तुल्यस्य वच च इति पञ्चधा.

 Note the word वच meaning statement in general ‘प्रशंसा’ in the name of the figure means mere statement as seen above

 वृत्ति— तदन्यम्य i e of the other correlate, viz कार्यादन्यस्य कारणस्य, निमित्तादन्यस्य कार्यस्य, सामान्यादन्यस्य विशेषस्य, विशेषादन्यस्य सामान्यस्य, तुल्ये प्रस्तुते तुल्यस्य वच, says मम्मट Why does he not say तुल्ये प्रस्तुते तदन्यस्य वच ? Possibly because we might mistake तदन्य for अतुन्य, प्रस्तुते तदन्यस्य वच shows that whatever is described is the अप्रस्तुत, the प्रस्तुत being suggested. सामान्य विशेष a general truth and a particular fact bearing out the general truth तुल्य-तुल्य i e. सदृश things

 **St 48** याता etc Illustrates कार्ये प्रस्तुते कारणम्य अप्रस्तुतस्य वचः A lover describes how he was made to abandon his idea of going on travel by the soft but cynical smile of his beloved, which indicated that she would die if he were to leave her. याता कि न मिलन्ति etc. words of the lover about to depart. याताः किं न पुनः मिलन्ति i e they do not part company for good. नितरां कृशासि possibly due to the prospects of ampending separation लज्जामन्थरतारकेण

Her pupils (तारका) appeared slovenly as she felt abashed to confess her nervousness निपतत्पीताश्रु (चक्षु) i e she controlled her tears as they were about to fall Again due to bashfulness हसितेन etc she indicated to me by her (cynical) smile how eager she was to meet death that was imminent. ‘If we part company, I die' was her suggestion

 The stanza gives the कारण why the speaker dropped his idea of departure This must have been occasioned by somebody having referred to his abandonment of the projected trip (प्रस्थाननिवृत्ति) which therefore is the प्रस्तुत in relation to which the कारण given in the stanza is अप्रस्तुत This therefore is कार्ये प्रस्तुते कारणस्य अप्रस्तुतस्य प्रशंसा It may be noted that here one could easily judge even without the knowledge of context that since the speaker is giving the reasons why he deferred his departure the occasion (प्रस्तुत) must have been a reference to his प्रस्थाननिवृत्ति

 अत्र प्रस्थानात् etc इति कार्ये पृष्ट SC अत्र एव प्रस्तुते The काय VIZ प्रस्थाननिवृत्ति was the occasion (प्रस्तुत ) for a query प्रस्थानात् किमिति निवृत्तासि ? i e someone asked him the question referring to his प्रस्थाननिवृत्ति Thus प्रस्थाननिवृत्ति was प्रस्तुत किमिति निवृत्तोसि should not lead us to think that since the reason was asked the reason is प्रस्तुत and hence याना etc is प्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, not अ प्र For the reason itself comes to be asked in the light of the काय प्रस्थानात् निवृत्ति which is, therefore the प्रस्तुत Lest किमिति निवृत्तोऽमि might lead us to think that the कारण is प्रस्तुत प्रदीप proposes निवृत्तोऽमि किम् Unnecessary Read सा द— अत्र क्स्यचिदगमनरूप काय कारणमभिहितम्

 **St 49** राजन् etc. Illustrates कारण प्रस्तुते कार्यस्य अप्रस्तुतस्य अप्रशंसा
Pathetic description of a parrot addressing different portraits in the palace deserted by the enemy at the news of an imminent invasion by the king (नाथ) who is being addressed by the poet राजसुता मा न पाठयति the princess used to give lessons to the parrot. कुमारसचिवे the friends of the prince. नाद्यापि कि भुज्यते the parrot possibly took his grub in their company इत्थ *etc* Construe नाथ, तव अरिभवने अध्वर्ग एञ्जरात मुक्तः शुक शून्यवलभौ चित्रस्थान् (राजसुतार्थान्) अवलोक्य एकैक इथ आभाषने अध्वगै पञ्जरात् मुक्तः Passers-by took pity on the parrot left all alone by the enemy that had fled in panic. They set it free from the cage where else it would have starved शून्यबलभौ चित्रस्थान् अवलोक्य It looked at their paintings on the terrace that was deserted i e first he addressed the राजसुता then the देवी, कुब्जा and कुमारसचिवऽ

 'Here the poet wants to drive at the panic (काय) that seized the enemies when the king (addressed by the poet) started on his expedition This he does by graphically describing the effect of the panic, in so far as it was typified in the parrot's case Thus कारण being प्रस्तुत काय is stated

 अत्र etc. कारणे प्रस्तुत कारण of the शुक्ऽ plight The शुक्'s patheti plight was the result (a) of the sudden flight of your enemies, the कारण which is प्रस्तुत प्रस्थानोद्यत भवन्त ज्ञात्वा when the enemies came to know that you were about to start on an expedition against them Since the नाथ is addressed and his enemy's flight is referred to it is evident that the poet wants to pay a compliment to the king As soon as we know this we can guess that शुकवर्णन is not an end in itself but a means to an end and thus अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा Some आल्कारिकऽ like रुय्यक give this as an instance of पयायोक्त

 **St 50** एतत्तस्य etc Illustrates सामान्ये प्रस्तुते विशेषस्य अप्रस्तुतस्य प्रशंसा Description of the folly of a fool who not only mistook a drop of water on a कमलिनीपत्र for a pearl but passed sleepless nights when the drop vanished as he tried to snatch it तस्य मुखात् कियत् may be construed in two ways (1) तस्य वनस्य मुखात् वारिण वण मुक्तामणि इति अमल एतत् कियत् or (2) तस्य वडस्य मुखात् एतत् कियत् यत् स वण मुक्तामणि इति अमस्त In the first some one fools the stupid fellow that the drop was a pearl In the second the stupid fellow himself declares (मुखत्व वाङ्मात्र लक्ष्यते— सा चू) that he thought the drop to be a pearl अस्मादपि अन्यत् शृणु Listen something further Construe शतै आदीयमाने अङ्गुल्यग्रलघुक्रियाप्रविलयिनि वणे मम (मुक्तामणि) कुत्र उड्डीय गत इति अन्तःशुचा अनुदित न निद्राति। अङ्गुल्यग्रलघुक्रियाप्रविलयिनि when the drop vanished due to the light movement (क्रिया) of the finger tips. मम sc मुक्तामणि कुत्र उड्डीय गत where did it go flying?

 Here the poet wants to impress on his reader the general truth that fools lay claim to things they ought not to This he does by describing a particular fact viz fools behaviour Thus सामान्य being प्रस्तुत a विशेष is described

 अत्रास्थाने etc. जडाना अस्थाने ममत्वसभावना भवति the indiscreet cherish as their own what is not properly so (अस्थाने) i e cherish when the object is not really their own

 **St 51.** सुहृद्बधू etc illustrates विशेष प्रस्तुते सामान्यस्य अप्रस्तुतस्य प्रशंसा Acc to उद्योत these are the words of a minister of नरकामुर ad

dressed to his friend शाल्व The minister incites शाल्व to take vengeance on कृष्ण for killing नरकासुर by telling him (शाल्व) how only the persons that avenge their friend's death are esteemed highly. ab Construe य वैरप्रतियातनेन मुहृद्वधूवाष्पलप्रमार्तन करोति Whosoever wipes out the tears of the widows of his friend by avenging the hostile acts The widows feel relief when they find the culprit brought to book by the friend of their deceased husband स पुमान् : He is manly स नातिमान् i e be knows nght policy. श्रिय भाजन i e he wins glory

 Here, in order to convey the particular fact that शाल्व should wreak vengeance on कृष्ण, the speaker states the general truth viz that a person who avenges his friend's death covers himself with glory Thus here we have विशेषे प्रस्तुते सामान्यस्य अप्रस्तुतस्य प्रशंसा

 अत्र कृष्ण etc त्वमेव श्लाघ्य You alone will be praiseworthy. None else could stand comparison with you

 तुल्ये प्रस्तुते etc See the divisions given above of सारूप्यमुला अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा श्लेष समासोक्तिः मम्मट Is Using the term श्लेष merely to indicate cases of अ प्र where the विशेषणऽ and विशेष्य are both श्लिष्ट Similarly, he uses the term समासोक्ति, to indicate cases of अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा where the विशेषणऽ alone are श्लिष्ट Strictly speaking, the term श्लेष indicates the figure where both the वाच्यार्थऽ are प्रकृत or both अप्रकृत So also समासोक्ति indicates the figure where the वाच्यार्थऽ refers to प्रकृत and गम्यार्थ to अप्रकृत So both श्लेष and समासोक्ति, strictly taken, cannot well go hand in hand with अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, where the वाच्यार्थ refers to the अप्रस्तुत That is why मम्मट's use of the words श्लेष and समासोक्ति, in the present case, is unscientific though, what he means is clear सादृश्यमात्रं या मम्मट refers to सादृश्य not based on श्लेष तुल्यात् etc. are responsible for one thing suggesting another, similar to it

 **St 52** पुंस्त्वादपि etc Illustrates अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा based on श्लेषमूलक
सादृश्य, the श्लेष embracing the विशेषणऽ and विशेष्य both विष्णु's (पुरूषोत्तम) glonous example (अप्रस्तुत) described driving at the प्रस्तुत VIZ the glorious example of a Superman (पुरूषोत्तम) पुंस्त्वादपि प्रतिचलेत्, ref to विष्णु's assumption of a feminine role in the अमृतदरण episode For the double meanings of the different phrases, see the English Translation and उद्योत अधोऽपि यायात् Ref to बराहाततार as applied to विष्णु विष्णु as a boar went to पाताल and rescued the earth. प्रमयने नमहान् dwarf (वामनादतर) applied to विष्णुः

‘mean', as apphed to the Superman नमहान् a compound with न as the first member Trvm Edition reads प्रणयनेन महान् विष्णु became ‘great' by making a request to बलि पुरुषोत्तम (the Superman) will make himself great though he has to pray for it Not a happy reading For, all other विशेषण’s imply some sacrifice by विष्णु and Superman Only the reading नमहान् is in tune with them Construe यदि पुस्त्वादपि प्रविचलेन् तदपि विश्वमभ्युद्धरेत् *d* construe केनापि पुरुषोत्तमेन इय दिक् प्रकटिता केनापि By the inscrutable (विष्णु) By any (Superman) दिक् प्रकटिका Has shown the way, set an example The idea is विष्णु has set the example of surrendering his own interests to save the world The Superman sets the example of surrendering his own interests to save all

 Here the stanza describes पुरुषोत्तम विष्णु, the अप्रस्तुत, with विशेषणऽ that apply also to the Superman (पुरुषोत्तम) who is प्रस्तुत As पुरुषोत्तम primanly signifies विष्णु the वाच्यार्थ relates to विष्णु Since विष्णु is ordinarily an उपमान, he must be अप्रस्तुत in relation to the Superman the प्रस्तुत, who is similar to him Here we have श्लेष not only in the विशेषणऽ viz पुस्त्वादपि प्रविवलेत्, अर्थऽपि यायात्, प्रणयने नमहानपि स्यात्, तदपि विश्वमभ्युद्धरेत् but also in the विशेष्य केनापि पुरुषोत्तमेन Thus here सादृश्य between प्रस्तुत and अप्रस्तुत is conveyed by श्लेष of विशेषणऽ and विशेष्य both

 **St 53** येनास्यभ्युदितेन etc Illustrates to adopt मम्मटऽ musleading terminology, समासोक्तिमुलक अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, to describe it correctly, it is सादृश्यमूला अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, based on श्लेष that embraces the विशेषणऽ only A rebuke administered to the Moon who though obscured by the Sun (during day time), clings to his पादऽ (rays) and is जड (cool) enough to parade himself on high (at night time) ab construe येन (रविणा) अभ्युदितेन चन्द्र क्लान्तिं गमितोऽसि तत्र रवौ प्रतिकर्तुमेव युज्येत, न पुन तस्यैव (रवेरेव) पादग्रहं (कार्यं)। रवे पादग्रह Play on the word पाद (1) rays, (2) foot The moon, it is believed, takes in the rays of the sun which it diffuses during night time क्षीणेनैतद् etc. Construe यदि क्षीणेन (रवे पुरत तेजक्षीणेन) ण्तत् (पादग्रहण) कृत तत (=तर्हि) मनाक् नो लज्जसे किम्। The idea is Though you had grown क्षीण that was no reason why you should debase yourself by पादग्रह d Construe
एव अस्तु। Let that pass यद्वयौन्नि विस्फूर्जसे सा तु सवा जह धामता The idea is It is the height of जडधामता (cool lustredness: stupidity) that you parade on mugn with your borrowed rastrei

 Here, the transparent epithets क्लान्तिं गमित (sent into obscurity), पादग्रह कृ (to cling to the feet), क्षीण (impoverished), जडधामता

(stupidity ), व्योम्नि विस्फूर्जसे (parade on high) apply to a person who not only clings to the feet that kicked him into obscurity, but pleads that he did the abject act in poverty Nay, he has the stupid effrontery to parade himself in the borrowed feathers (that he earned with the पादग्रह) Thus the विशेषणऽ are श्लिष्ट applying to the अप्रस्तुत चन्द्र and प्रस्तुत जन पुरुष (कापुरुष— र ग) The विशेष्य viz चन्द्र is not श्लिष्ट Thus here we have सादृश्यमूल अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा based on श्लेष embracing the विशेषणऽ only

 The inaccuracy of मम्मट in styling thus vanety as समासोक्तिमूल is criticized by जगन्नाथ in the following terms –यत्तु 'येनास्यभ्युदितेन तस्यैव पादग्रह' इत्यत्र समासोक्तिनुग्राहिका इति मम्मटभट्टैरुक्त तत्र विचार्यते— अत्र विशेषण साम्यमहिम्ना प्रतीयमान कापुरुषवृत्तान्त किं प्रस्तुत आहोस्विदप्रस्तुत। आद्ये समासोक्तेर्विषय एव नास्ति। द्वितीयेऽप्रस्तुतप्रशंसाया नास्ति विषय। तस्माच्छ्लिष्टविशेषणोपक्षिप्त- द्वितीयार्थमात्र समासोक्तिरित्यभिप्रायेण यथाकथञ्चित्सङ्गमनीयम्।

 **St 54** आदाय वारि etc. Illustrates अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा based on शुद्ध सादृश्य, not श्लिष्ट The vile ocean receives the fresh water of the rivers only to turn it into brine यडवादहने हुत The ebb of the ocean is mythically explained as due to the water being consumed in the submarine fire (वडवानल)

 Here वारिधिवृत्तान्त, which is अप्रस्तुत as any shrewd सहृदय can see, puts us in mind of the प्रस्तुत viz the कापुरूष who has good things lavished on him only to be misused The सादृश्य is not based on श्लेष and hence शुद्ध. Thus thus illustrates तुत्ये प्रस्तुते तुल्यस्य अप्रस्तुतस्य प्रशंसा शुद्ध

 इयं च काचित् etc. मम्मट proceeds to allustrate वाच्यस्य सम्भव असम्भव and उभयरूपत्व इय च etc. इय i. e अप्रस्तुप्रशसा based on सदृश्य Read 'variety' in place of ‘figure’ in the translation मम्मट seems to take वच्यस्य सभवमभव as another independent principle of division giving 3 vaneties more of सादृस्यमूल अ प्र making a total of 6 varieties. Thus his classification would be

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708182014तुल्य.PNG"/>

But as seen above सभवासभव principle could be applied to each one of the three श्लेष**,** समासोक्ति and सादृश्यमात्र**,** giving 9 varieties (3x3) of सादृश्यनिबधना अ प्र

** ‘**वाच्ये प्रतीयमानार्थानध्यारोप=वाच्यस्य सभव वाच्ये प्रतीयमानार्थाध्यारोप=वाच्यास्य असभव अशेषु अध्यारोप=वाच्याशेषु प्रतीयमानार्थाध्यारोप = वाच्यस्य अशत समवासभवौ

 **वाच्ये** **प्रतीयमानार्थानध्यारोप** —Here the वाच्यार्थ describing the अप्रस्तुत is in itself plausible (सभवत्) and does not stand in need of beng construed in the light of (आरोप) the गम्यार्थ (=प्रतीयमानार्थ) applying to the प्रस्तुत प्रतीयमानार्थ = व्यङ्ग्यार्थ applying to the प्रस्तुत

 **St** **55** **अन्धेरम्भ** etc Illustrates वाच्चे प्रतीयमानार्थानाध्यारोपे सादृश्यमूला अ प्र A poetic conceit as to how dire the ocean would look if it were to be drained dry—the same ocean which it is childs play to cross when it is full ab Construe बहव पोतोपाया अम्भस्थगिनभुवनाभोगपातालकुक्षे अब्धे लङ्घनेऽपि क्षमन्ते।पोतोपाया (v l Trvm Ed पोतोपायाद) persons using boats अन्य स्थगित (covered) मुक्नाभोग पातालवुशिश्च येन cd Construe आहो (=यदि) ण्ण दैवात् कथमपि रिक्त भवेत् तदानीम् अस्य (उदधे) अवरकुहरालोक्नेऽपिक क्वल्प (=शक्त) स्यात्। अभं v l अर्ण **‘**Tvm अवर (2 m) pit आलोकनेऽपि in contrast to लङ्घनेऽपि of ab Now they are able even to trans st gress it**,** then they will be unable even to glance at it

 Here the अप्रस्तुत is the उदधिवृत्तान्त that suggests by सादृश्य the वृत्तान्त of the प्रस्तुत viz the person who when full**’** is tractable**,** but would become a deadly problem with his cupidity**,** if he were to be reduced to penury The उदधिवृत्तान्त is in itself plausible that is to say**,** has योग्यता It**,** therefore does not stand in need of being viewed in the light of (आरोप) of the suggested meaning though it is there viz सपन्न पुरुषवृत्तान्त Thus thus is वाच्ये प्रतीयमानार्थानध्यारोपे सादृश्यमूला अ प्र

   क्वचित्तु अध्यारोपैणैव — In some cases of सादृश्यमूला प्रस्तुतप्रशंसा the वाच्यार्थ appears plausible only when viewed in the light of (अध्यारोप) the व्यङ्ग्यार्थ In other words the वाच्यार्थ has no योग्यता in itself It gets योग्यता when viewed in relation to the व्यङ्ग्यार्थ The story of the hon and the mouse is implausible on the face of it. But it gets plausibility when viewed in the light of the व्यङ्ग्यार्थ viz तृणेन कार्य भवतीश्वराणाम् This in रुय्यक's terms**,** is वाच्यार्थस्य अमभवे

 **SL 56** कस्त्वं etc. Illustrates वाच्ये प्रतीयमानार्थाध्यारोपे सादृश्यमूला अ प्र Dialogue (प्रश्नोत्तरिवा) between the poet and a शाखोदक tree (haunted by ghosts)**,** wherein the tree dwells on the futility of its life.

 **शाखोटकः** (m.) A tree**,** haunted by ghosts (भूतावास)**,** growing on the burial ground acc. to commentaries. **मा**.**चंः**–इमशानाग्निदग्धएल्लवादिः तरूविशेषो हि शाखोटकः । To judge from **भावप्रकाश** cited in शब्दकल्पद्रुम**,** it is a particular tree having brown berries**:** शाखोटः पीतफलको भूतावासः खरच्छदः। शाखोटो रक्तपित्तार्शोवानश्लेष्मातिसारजित्॥Tvm. edition reads शाकोटकः. Will it be too fantastic**,** in the light of this reading**,** to suggest that शाखोटक (=शाकोट**,** **सम्प्रदायप्रकाशिनी**) is really शाक-वट**;** शाक being**,** **“**witch-craft**”** (cp**.** शक्ति**,** शक्त)**,** the very word that is the basis of the word शाकिनी—a witch**.** (**Cp.** **दशकुमारचरित** उ**.**द्द) Not that it was a वट (in c वट is separately mentioned)**;** but a tree resembling वट well-known as the haunt of ghosts**.** **वैराग्यादिव वक्षि** said by the poet because the शाखोटक described himself as दैवहतक**.** \[In the translation read **‘**an unlucky tree**’** in place of **‘**a tree**’.**\] **साधु** **विदितं** Well hit**:** said by the tree**.** **कस्मात्** SC. वैराग्यम्—asked by the poet**.** **इदं** **कथ्यते** said by the tree**.** The कथन is embodied in cd वामेनात्र etc. **वामेन,** acc. to मा**.** चं**.,** shows contempt and hints at the वट being of a lower class than himself**.** **छायापिः** The word अपि hints at the idea**:** **“**I can give berries (फल) and shade**.** The banian has only shade to give**.** But even my shade is not of service to the मथिकऽ**,** (it being a भूतावास)**.**

 Here we have a प्रश्नोत्तरिका between the कवि and the शाखोटक. As it is impossible for the tree to speak, it is implausible on the face of it**.** But it comes to have significance when viewed in the light of the व्यङ्ग्यार्थ viz. the truth**:** a serviceable अधम counts for nought to the people who prefer an उत्तम though less serviceable**.** cp**.** याच्ना मोधा वरमधिगुणे नाधमे लब्धकामा— **मेघदूत.** Read रुय्यकः अत्राचेतनेन सह प्रश्नोत्तरिका नोपपत्रेति वाच्यस्यासम्भव एव।

 **क्वचिदंशेषु** etc. In some cases the वाच्यार्थ is partly plausible**,** partly not so**.** That is**,** in some portions one has to read it in the light of the suggested meaning to see its plausibility**,** in others it is plausible in itself**.** अंशेषु अध्यारोप is वाच्याशे व्यङ्गयार्थस्य अध्यारोप**.**

 **St. 57.** *सोऽपूर्वो* etc. Illustrates अंशेषु अभ्यारोपे सादृश्यमूला अ. प्र. The bee asked to desist from hovering about the elephant in rut**,** for the reasons viz. (1) रसनाविपर्यय**,** (2) कर्णयोश्चापल**,** (3) मदविस्मृतस्वपरदिक् दृष्टि**,** (5) अन्तशून्यकरत्व**.** All these have a double significance one applying to the and the other to मत्वस्वामिन् (an insolent master). रसनाविपर्यय (1) The **‘**inversion**’** of the elephant's tongue due to a curse of अग्नि**.** \[The विपर्यय here only means the loss of the power

of speech So far as is known the elephant's tongue is normal**.**\] (2) The tendency to go back upon what he has said कर्णयोश्चापल (1) The unsteadiness of the ears (2) The tendency to ear to whatever is reported without waiting to sift truth from untruth मदविस्मृतस्वपरदिक् दृष्टिः (1) The sight that is blurred enough due to ichor to make the elephant oblivious of his own track and that of others (2) The outlook that in insolence fatis to discriminate between kinsmen and strangers अन्तशून्यकरत्व (1) Having a trunk hollow from inside (2) Having the hand empty from inside—1 e not inclined to give**.** कः ग्रह What sort of whim is it ?

 Here as the वृत्ति points out**,** so far as the भ्रमर is concerned**,** it is only one reason viz वर्णचापल that should persuade it to leave the elephant रसनाविपर्यय (tongue growing defunct) and शून्यकरत्व (hollowness of trunk) affect the भ्रमर least and are therefore**,** no reason why it should leave the elephant. (indicated by मदविस्मृतस्वपरदिक्) is actually the reason why the bee should not leave the elephant but should resort to it

 Thus वर्णचापल alone is in *tune with* the वाच्यार्थ viz. वारणस्य असेवनीयत्व रसनाविपर्यास and शून्यवरत्व are in no way helpful to it. and appear cogent only when viewed in the light of the व्यङ्ग्यार्थ That is**,** to भ्रमर**,** रसनाविपर्य स and शून्यवरत्व may be a matter of no concern**,** but to a सेवक the रसनावुपर्यय and शून्यवरत्व of his master are of consequence He should leave a master who goes back upon what he says and who is not inclined to give anything Thus here the art relating to wat वाच्यार्थ relating to भ्रमर assumes plausibility only when understood in the light of the व्यङ्ग्यार्थ So also in the case of मद It all suits the वाच्यार्थ Who will advise the bee not to resort to the elephant because of its ichor when we know that the ichor is the reason why the भ्रमर hovers about the elephant ? But मद becomes a plausible reason when viewed in the light of the व्यङ्ग्यार्थ A सेवक should leave a स्वमिन who is मत्त (insolent) Thus आरो्प of व्यङ्ग्यार्थ becomes necessary for रसनाविपर्यय**,** शून्यवरत्व and मद It is not necessary only in the case of वर्णचरय That is why this is an instance of अंहोपु अध्यारोप

 अत्र रसना etc. भ्रमरस्य अभेवने the न हेतु and hence योग्यता by व्यङ्ग्यर्थ्यध्यारोप हेतु and hence

ध्यारोप is not called for मद प्रत्युत सेवने निमित्तम् and hence requires व्यङ्गघार्थी यारोप

**१२- अतिशयोक्ति**

 In the मङ्गलोक to his • काव्यप्रकाश**,** मम्मटभट्ट describes the कवैर्भारती to be नियतिकृतनियमरहिता i e transcending the laws of the world And this feature of कविभारती (Poet's Speech) is nowhere else so con spicuous as in the figure अतिशयोक्ति As the name itself bears out**,** in अतिशयोक्ति we have a statement of something abnormal It is a statement hyperbolical enough to justify the verdict निरङ्कुशा कवय passed on poets When rhetoncians like भामह and दण्डिन् describe the उक्ति to be लोवातिकान्तगोचर or लोकमीमातिवर्तिनी or अतिलोक (रुद्रट)**,** it is not difficult to understand**,** though vaguely**,** the keynote of the figure

 This लोकातिक्रान्तगोचरच characterizes practically every figure जयरथ cites (3) —एक्वातिशयोक्तिव काव्यस्यालकृतिर्मता। भामह says—सैषा सर्वत्र वक्रोक्तिः (=अतिशयोक्ति)। This indicates how the different figures are but the various keys of the garnut of अतिशयोक्ति**.** This is अतिशयोक्ति in its broader sense**.**

 The figure अतिशयोक्ति**,** however**,** as limited in its scope According to मम्मट**,** it is limited to the four varieties he mentions

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1709121594r.PNG"/>

That these varieties with slight modifications, are admitted by a good many rhetoricians shows how there is a certain unanimity among them as to the scope of the figure The following scheme will make this clear

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1709306704s.PNG"/>

 Are the divisions heterogeneous ? उद्भट**,** so far as one can sce**,** was the first आलङ्कारिक to give us the divisions of अनिदायोक्ति which were accepted without or with a few additions by his successors**.** Looking at the divisions themselves one almost feels that they present a heterogeneous sorting To wit**,** मम्मट**’**s (I) is based on सादृश्य**,** or some such relation between प्रस्तूत and अप्रस्तूत**,** (II) on dental of the actual nature of a thing **;** (III) on a hypothetical relation **. **(IV) on धार्यकारणभाव That this grouping had baffled even the रिकऽ is clearly borne out by the difficulties they encounter in giving a comprehensive definition of the figure**.** मम्मट for instance is content fnerely with giving us an enumeration of the vinieties**,** without attempting to define it comprehensively जगन्नाथ । says that the neo– रिकऽ (नम्या) take exception to these divisions. नभ्यतु गियाङ्ग्यमानमेवादा मनेशन्सनुगम          

 On a closer scrutiny it would seem**,** however**,** that these divisions are not as heterogeneous as they appear at the first glance All of them embody भ and if one asks what अ means precisely**,** it is here अध्यक्षमाय determination of one thing as being another When जगन्नाथ defines the figure as..................................he exactly lays his finger on the essential feature of all the divisions रुय्यक is more explicat.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................

in number**,** practically the same as मम्मट**’**s**.** His भेदेऽभेद division is a bit vague in the light of the instance he gives वामन did not specify any divisions**,** nor did रूद्रट who**,** however**,** made an attempt to define अतिशय comprehensively यत्रार्थधर्मनियम प्रसिद्धिबाधाद विपर्यय याति। कश्चित् क्वचिदतिलोक स स्यादित्यतिशयस्तस्य मम्मट practically followed उद्भट स्य्यक**,** विश्वनाथ**,** नगन्नाथ gave 5 divisions in place of मम्मट's fourअप्पय्यदीक्षित, with his characteristic tendency for elaboration**,** gave 7 divisions.

 मम्मट**’**s four drerstons of अतिशयोक्ति—In the first**,** the उपमान swallows up the उपमेय**,** e g in चन्द्रोऽयम् the उपमान**,** चन्द्र**,** swallows up the उपमेय मुख We have seen**,** in उ II**,** that this is गौणी साध्यवसाना लक्षणा**,** (गौणी सारोपा मुख चन्द्र**,** forming the basis of रूपक) This is one step further than रूपक where we are conscious of both**.** उपमान and उपमेय It is even a step in advance of उत्प्रेक्षा**,** where we have साध्य अध्यवसान while here it is सिद्ध**.** As we saw earlier**,** it is the farthest limit to which the poet marches**,** in the field of सादृश्य

 What about शुद्धा साध्यवसाना**,** like नीलिमा अय for नील राम**,** where the relation between the प्रकृत and अप्रकृत 19 गुण-गुणिन्**,** or like सदीय रूप कामिनीना समोहन where the relation Is हेतु-कार्य (समोहन actually meaning सम्मोहनहेतु her beauty hypnotises) ? मम्मट**’**s कारिका is comprehensive enough to include these owing to the use of प्रकृतस्य परेण निगीर्याध्यवमानम् But his वृत्ति उपमानेन etc**.** narrows possibly through oversight**,** the scope to गौणी साध्यवसाना, where alone between the प्रकृत and अप्रकृत there is उपमेय–उपमान relation The tendency of the afts is**,** however**,** to comprehend साध्यवसाना**,** whether गौणी or शुद्ध**,** within the scope of this division of अतिशयोक्ति

 In the second division**,** a thing is described as being other than the norm**.** There is the normal beauty but this beauty is quite different—this is an instance of the tenor of this variety \[Cp मराठा त्यार्चे सगळें वेगळेञ्च **‘**or गुजराती\] In the third division**,** (यद्यर्थोक्तौ कल्पत)**,** the impossibility of a thing is conveyed by stating that it would occur only in case something else, which is impossible, occurs. This division**,** it may be noted**,** is utilised to convey the उत्व (or) of the प्रकृत over अलकृत It is signuficant that ट calls at अपत्या उपमा, aosa, vium In the fourth division which is based on causation, दण्डिन्, the effect is described as occurring even prior to the cause, in order to drive at the swiftness of action of the cause. ars like रुय्यक,

विश्वनाथ and जगन्नाथ give as instances of अतिशयोक्ति even those cases where effect is described as occurring simultaneously with the cause रुय्यक कार्यकारणयो समकालत्व पौर्वापर्यविपर्यये चातिशयोक्तिः। कायस्य चाशुभावाख्यविषये प्रतिपाद्यते। जयस्य speaks of another vanety where the normal कारण and कार्य are described as कार्य and कारण respectively Here We have कारणकार्यविपर्यय while above we had कारणकार्यपोर्वापर्यविपर्यय

 **अतिशयोक्ति** **and** **रूपक** —The first variety of अतिशयक्ति**,** If based on सादृश्य**,** can be compared with रूपक as both are based on आहार्यशान. But this अतिशयोक्ति takes the forms चन्द्रोऽय while the रूपक takes the form मुख चन्द्र In रूपक we have गौणी सायवराता That is to say**,** the poet is conscious of the distinctness of the two उपमेय मुख and उपमान चन्द्र**,** in रूपक**,** while in अतिशयोक्ति**,** the उपमान चद्र swallows up the उपमेय मुखम् That way अतिशयोक्ति goes ahead of रूपक

 **अतिशयोक्ति** and **उत्प्रेक्षा** — As we saw under उत्पेक्षा**,** रुय्यक holds that in both we have अध्यावसान But while in उत्प्रेक्षा**,** the अध्यावसान is in the making (साध्य)**,** in अतिशयोक्ति (I) it is consummated (सिद्ध) मुख चन्द्रं मन्ये is on its way to अध्यवसान चन्द्रोऽथ is अध्यीवसान itself To put it otherwise**,** in उत्प्रेक्षा, we have समावन**,** in अतिशयोक्ति**,** निश्चय— both, however, आहार्य

 ***Essentials***—\[अतिशयिता प्रसिद्धिमतिकान्ता लेकातीता उक्तिः।— झल्कीकार**,** or अतिशयस्य उक्तिः For the significance of अतिशय**,** see (ग and रुद्रट cited above.\] (i) All divisions are based on आहार्यज्ञान and वास्तवज्ञान (ii) The first division is based on सादृश्य (गौणी साध्यवसाना) (or सादृश्येतरसम्बन्ध, शुद्ध साध्यवसाना**,** acc. to other आलङ्कारिकऽ) (iii) There in the उपमान swallows up the उपमेय, or if based on सादृश्येतर सम्बध**,** the swallows up the प्रकृत (iv) In the second division**,** the प्रस्तुत is described as pertaining to a class different from (अन्य) the ordinary (v) In the third**,** the form is यदि तर्हि इदमप्यमवि**,** or its equivalent**,** and it aims at showing the impossibility of a thing by stating that it would occur if another impossible thing occurs. (vi) The third variety is utilized by the poet to drive at the उत्यर्थ (or अ) of the प्रकृत over the अप्रकृत which he puts thus If the अप्रकृत were to be this**,** then the प्रकृत would be like it and so on. (vii) In the fourth vanety the is described as occuring prior to the or simultaneously with it**.** This is a poetical way of expressing the prompt effectiveness of the (viii) The division which मम्मट does not refer to

but which is of usual occurrence viz सवधेऽप्यमवन्धं consists in denying a connection which is there e g वदाम्यामतड कथ नु विषय पुराणो मुनि We know that the पुराण मुनि is the creator of उर्वशी but this is questioned अत्र पुराणप्रजापतिनिर्माणसम्बधेऽप्यमबध उक्तम्।—रूय्यक

 § सू २९ Construe— (१) प्रकृतस्य परेण यत् निगीयाध्यवसान**,** (२) प्रस्तुतस्य यदन्यत्व (३) **‘**यदि**’**–अर्थोक्तौ (यत्) कल्पन**,** (४) कार्यकारणयोश्च (य) पौर्वापयविपर्यय सा अतिशयोक्तिः विज्ञेया।

 ( १ ) प्रकृतस्य परण (= अप्रकृतेन) as seen above**,** is more comprehensive and consistent with the usage of आलङ्कारिकऽ**,** than उपमेयस्य उपमानेन of the वृत्ति For**,** प्रकृतस्य परेण can comprehend गौणी साध्यवसाना as also बुद्धा साध्यवसाना**,** that is to say between the प्रकृत and अप्रकृत the relation may be सादृश्य or other सम्बधऽ like कार्यकारणभाव**,** गुण-गुणीभाव निगीर्य अध्यवसान supersession on being eclipsed or swallowed **‘**मुख**’** is **‘**swallowed up**’** by चन्द्र and results in the अध्यवसान चन्द्रोऽयम् मध्यवसान is dotermination in a particular form स्वरूपेण निश्चयः**,** says प्रभा प्रदीप द्रढीयमी वुद्धि। (२) प्रस्तुतस्य यदन्यत्व अन्यत्व from the norm**.** The प्रस्तुत is other (अन्य) than the ordinary or normal—imphes the poet**.** (३) यद्यर्थोक्तौ कल्पनम् Imagining a thing as contingent upon (the hypothetical occurrence of a thing stated by) an expression having the meaning (अर्थ) of यदि (in the event of) यद्यर्थोक्तौ is an elliptical way of saying **‘**यदि**’**–अक्ति–उपन्यस्त–वृत्तान्ते सति It takes the form If the अप्रकृत were this the would have been this यद्यर्थ the words यदित्तहि**,** or चेत कल्पन असभविन अर्थस्य says the वृत्ति दण्डिन् calls thus अद्भुतोपना रुद्रट calls this उत्पाद्या उपमा**,** making it clear that its charm lies in उत्कर्ष of the उपमेय over the**,** उपमान Cp रूद्रट**’**s definition viu 15 अनुपममेन्द्रलु इत्युपमान तद्विशेषण चामत्। सम्भाव्य सयद्यर्थे या कियते सोपमेत्याद्या । कुवलयानद**,** who calls this सभालङ्कार**, **widens its scope to include things not related as प्रकृत–अप्रकृत e**. **g यदि शेषो भवेद्वक्ता कथिता स्युर्गुणास्तव। (४) कार्यकारणयो पौर्वापर्यविपर्यय पैर्ववर्य is पूर्वपरीभाव i e. natural sequence. Normally the sequence is कारण first कार्य next**.** This sequence is upset in this variety It may be upset in two ways कार्य may be described for showing the prompt effectiveness of कारण**,** as taking place prior to the कारण or simul taneously with it**.** मम्मट takes note of the former only

 वृत्ति—उपमानेन etc. वृत्ति has unnecessarily limited the scope of the प्रकृत and पर of the **,** as seen above.

 **St. 58** कमानग्मसि etc**.** Illustrates अतिशयोक्ति—I Description of the superb beauty of a lady's face her eyes, her figure, अनग्मसि

यमदं viz the face**,** the मुखकमल Ordinary कमल grows only in water (अब्ज) कमले च कुवलये viz the pair of blue eyes (कुत्रत्वे Nom Dual) set in the lotus of the face (कमले Loc**.** Sing**.**) तानि मुखकमल नेत्रोत्पले च कनकलतिका viz the blond figure frame of the body उत्पातपरपरा viz array of freaks lotus growing on a dry spot two smaller lotuses growing on the lotus**,** the creeper of gold and yet velvet soft

 Here the उपमेयऽ**,** मुख**,** नेत्रे and तनु are eclipsed (अन्तनिङ्गीर्ण) by the उपमानऽ viz कमल**,** कुवलये and बनकलनिया Thus this is an instance of निगीर्याध्यवमानम् अतिशयोक्ति**,** माणिक्यचन्द्र rightly draws attention to the विरोध present in the stanza**,** विरोध implicit in अनम्मति कमल**,** कमले कुवलये**,** तानि कनकलनिकाया**,** and कनकलता सुकुमारसुभगा र ग sees विभावना in the figure— ण्व कमलमनम्ममीति परकीयपद्येऽतिशयोक्तयुशहरणेऽपि अन्त्येव विमावना। परं तु पप्ललप्रनम्मनि इत्यंशे शाब्दी। कमले च इत्यादौ आर्थी इति सक्षेप।

  अत्र मुखादि etc मुग्यादि i e मुख**,** नेत्रे and तनु कमलादि i e कमल**,** कुवलये and कलत्वविध अध्यववसितम् i e निश्चितम् the face etc are determined**,** of course poetically**,** to be कमल etc**.**

 यद्य etc. तदेव i e प्रकृतमेव—not उपमेयमेव अन्यद्येन अध्यवसीयते is determined to be different (from the ordinary**,** normal) अन्यस्येन i e सर्वजनीनात् अन्यचेन अध्यवसीयते note how अध्यक्षस्य (or न) is present in this vanety also See the remarks made above about the common feature of the divisions of अतिशयोक्ति**,**

 St. 59 अर्ण्ण लदहराणर्भ etc. Illustrates प्रस्तुतस्य अन्दन्वेन अध्यवसाने अतिशयोक्ति. Description of the superb charm and exquisite figure of a lady**.** सन्त या —अन्यष्टर्भ (इ) त्व अन्यैव बनाया। इदमा समनन्दप्र रमेन भवति ॥ अन्यत् i e of a different sort from the normal**.** स्टम ( ) =प्र —dignified bearing-सं प्र=पनाया शरीरं— उ—स प्र**,** मा चं वर्ग (१ ) दोना (conduct)। इपामा a lovely Iady Cp. उपेन सामान्य i. c. the creator who made the creatures. रो creation**,** delineation हेरा-ऐरा - मीन–सा पू **‘**She is not the limit (i.e not beyond the limit) of the lay creator.**’**

 Here in the first half we have अदाका based on The second half could गर, well illustrate सकसम, a vanety o itted by मम्मट she is stated as not being so. As said above उद‌शेष सध्दा comes under of other कुवलयानन्द calls this calls this 

 यद्यर्थस्य etc**.** Explanation of यद्यर्थोक्तौ of the सूत्र **“**When the sense of the word happens to be conveyed by the use of the word यदि or चेत्**”** **‘**यद्यर्थ**’**— रुद्रट uses the same term in the same sense कल्पन अर्थात् अममविन अर्थस्य Conceit of a matter of course**,** improbable अर्थात् असभविन Conceit can be apparently of a matter that appears unlikely As we saw above**,** generally the आल्कारिकऽ make use of this variety to convey the उत्कर्ष of the उपमेय over the उपमान Its form is If the उपमान were to be thus**,** then there would be resemblance between it and उपमेय दण्डिन् calls it अद्भुतोपमा**,** रुद्रट उत्पाद्या उपमा That way**,** it agrees with व्यतिरेक or प्रतिप in spirit**,** though not in form (See under व्यतिरेक)**.** कुवलयानाद**,** however**,** extends its scope in the figure सभावना where the occurrence of anything made contingent upon the occurrence of something else-of course poetical–is enough to constitute the figure Thus यदि शेषो भवेद्वक्ता कथिता स्युर्गुणास्तव**,** —the idea being your excellences are so numerous that they could be adequately expressed only if शेष with his thousand hoods were to speak—becomes an instance of it**,** though here there is no idea of the उत्कर्ष of उपमेय over उपमान मम्मट**’**s definition is vague enough to comprehend such cases also

 **St 60** राकायां etc**.** Illustrates यद्यर्थोक्तौ कल्पने अतिशयोक्ति Description of the surpassing charm of the face of some lovely woman राका पौजिम्मा The face is like the resplendent orb of the full moon But the orb is marred by कलङ्क**,** not so the face तस्या प्रकृताया नायिकाया साम्यपराभव अवाप्नुयात् That the full moon should be like the face is itself an indignity to the face which is so exalted !

 Here साम्यपराभव is the अमभवद् अर्थ which is imagined as con tingent on the occurring of राकाया इन्दो अपलङ्कत्व**,** a fact introduced by the word चेत् (**‘**यद्यर्थ**’**) This illustration indicates how**,** in this variety**,** we have the उ वर्ष of the उपमेय over उपमान The moon has a spot**,** the face has none ! Here**,** the अध्यवमान consists in thinking of a hypothetical fact**.**

 कारणस्य etc**.** Here कार्य is described as occurring even prior to (or simultaneously with) the कारण**,** with a view to indicating how the कारण is quick in its action (शीप्ररपिता) This is कार्यकारणयो पौर्वरयीपर्य \[रूय्यक calls this पौर्वपर्यतिविपर्ययविध्वन **,** to comprehend
also } कार्यस्य gigit 1. e. when the ai is described as (occurring) prior to the कारण चतुर्थी sc. अतिशयोक्तिः अतिशयोकि Gv)

** St 61** हृदयमधिष्टित etc Illustrates कार्यकारणपौर्वापर्यविपर्यये अतिशयोक्ति The words of a दूती to a नायक**,** No sooner did मालती a नायिका**,** see the नायक than she fell in love with him Construe हे रमणीवल्लभ**,** मालत्या हृदय आदौ कुसुमचापबाणेन अधिष्ठितम्। लोचनविषय भजता त्वया चरम (पश्चात्) अधिष्ठितम्। कुसुमचापबाणेन हृदय अधिष्टित** i** e she fell in love लोचनविषय भजता त्वया i e when you were seen by her

 Here**,** नायकदर्शन**,** which actually preceded मदनावेश is poetically described as following मदनावेश This is to suggest that the नायकदर्शन was so quick in its action**.** रूय्यक—कार्यस्य आशुभावारय विशेष दर्शन प्रतिपाद्यते । Vide जयरथ cited above to ascertain how there is अध्यवसाय here also रुद्रट calls this पूर्वालङ्कार कुवलयानद calls this अत्यन्तातिशयोक्ति कार्यकारणयो समकालत्व will be aptly illustrated by the well known stanza from the रघुवश —सममेव समाकान्त द्वय द्विरदगामिना। तेन सिंहासनं पित्र्य मण्डल च महीभृताम्॥where रघु**’**s coming to the throne as described as simultaneous with his achievement of establishing paramountcywhile**,** in fact रघु first came to the throne and then established his paramountcy

**१३ — प्रतिवस्तूपमा**

 निदर्शना (one variety of it) प्रतिवस्तूपमा and दृष्टान्त**,** are figures based on अनेकत्वाक्यगत सादृश्य In other words in these figures we have सादृश्य between one वाक्यार्थ and another that वाक्यगत सादृश्य wich requires two or more वाक्यऽ is different from क्क्यगा उपमा embodied in a single वाक्य has been already made clear Further**,** this वाक्यगत सादृश्य must be only suggested (गम्य or आर्थ) and never expressed by any उपमाप्रतिपादकं like इव**,** यथा**,** सदृश etc**.** See how भामह lays stress on this absence of वादि in the definition of प्रतिवस्तूपमा यथेवानभिधानेऽपि गुणसाम्यप्रतीतित । उद्भट also refers to उपमवाचिशून्यत of प्रतिवस्तूपमा

 And yet what distinguishes these three figures one from the other**,** is the particular mode employed by each to convey सादृश्य even in the absence of सादृश्यवाक्यऽ**.** Thus**,** as we saw**,** निदर्शना employs the expedient of juxtaposing two unrelated facts which become भौपम्यपर्यवसायि दृष्टान्त as we shall see later**,** suggests सादृश्य by pitting one against the other**,** धर्मऽ that are different but correspond प्रतिवस्तूपमा has a naiver method It gives us the same धर्म in the different sentences**,** thus driving at their similanty But**,** to avoid the दोष of repeating the same word or words**,**

(कथितपददोष viz**.** नैक पद द्वि प्रयोज्य प्रायेण— वामन)**,** the धर्म**,** though one and the same**,** happens here to be stated in different words This is what is meant when we say that**,** in प्रतिवस्तूपमा**,** we have वस्तुप्रतिवस्तुभाव**,** वस्तु–प्रतिवस्तु referring to the recurrence of the same वस्तु viz धर्म**,** though in different words Here the same वस्तु viz धर्म stands vis a vis itself though in a different form (प्रतिवस्तु)

 **वस्तु**–**प्रतिवस्तुभाव** and **बिम्ब** **प्रतिबिम्बभाव**—As seen above**,** वस्तु प्रतिवस्तुभाव**,** so far as it apphes to प्रतिवस्तूपमा**,** means the relation of the धर्म in one वाक्य to the धर्म 11m another, when the धर्म is the same**,** though couched in different words This is to be distinguished from बिम्व प्रतिबिम्बभाव lying at the back of दृष्टान्त**,** the figure that follows In विम्ब प्रतिबिम्वभाव**,** the धर्म in one वाक्य is different from that in the other**,** but still corresponds to it**,** just as the thing reflected is different from the réflection**,** though one corresponds to the other**.**

 Thus in स्थिरा शैली गुणवता खलबुध्द्या न बाध्यते। रत्नदीपस्य हि शिख्या वात्ययादि न नाश्यते॥the धर्म न बाध्यते is the same as न नाश्यते**,** though differently worded Between न बाध्यते and न नाश्यते there is वस्तु प्रतिवस्तुभाव In काम नृपा सन्तु सहस्रशोऽन्ये रानन्वतीमाहुरनेन भूमिम्। नक्षत्रताराग्रहमङ्कुलपि ज्योतिष्मती चन्द्रमसैव रात्रि**,** ज्योतिष्मतीत्व is a different धर्म from राजन्वतीत्व**,** and yet they correspond**,** one to the other There is thus विम्ब प्रतिबिम्बभाव between the two

 It must be conceded**,** however**,** that in some cases**,** it becomes difficult to judge precisely whether it is विम्ब प्रतिविम्बभाव or वस्तु-प्रतिवस्तुभाव that prevails The same धर्म stated in different words is likely to be mistaken for two corresponding धर्मऽ or vice versa Thus आपद्गत खलु महाशयचक्रवर्ती विस्तारयत्यतपूर्वमुदारभावम्। वालागुर्दहनमध्यगत समन्ताल्लोकोत्तरं परिमल प्रकटीकरोति॥**,** which is given as an instance of प्रतिवस्तूपमा by र ग**,** may justifiably be taken by another as a case of दृष्टान्त विस्तारयति only corresponds to**,** and is not the same as**,** प्रकटीकरोति**,** he may argue**.** In such dubious cases**,** there is bound to be divergence of opinions and one has only to justify his particular view point by proving the धर्म to be same**,** in the case of प्रतिवस्तूपमा**,** or धर्मऽ to be distinct**,** in case of दृष्टान्त

 Nay**.** in the earliest stages of their growth**,** दृष्टान्त and प्रतिवस्तूपमा were looked upon as identical**.** भामह and दण्डिन् did not admit दृष्टान्त as a separate figure**.** They admitted प्रतिवस्तूपमा**,** as a vanety of उपमा This variety was broad enough to comprehend दृष्टान्त also It was

उद्भट who first marked off the two figures one from the other His name for दृष्टान्त was वात्र्यदृष्टान्त वामन admitted प्रतिवस्तु as a variety of उपमा**,** but did not admit **‘**दृष्टान्त रुद्रट admitted उभययाम in place of प्रतिवस्तूपमा He also admitted दृष्टान्त रय्यक**,** विश्वनाथ**,** जगन्नाथ**,** अप्पय्यदीक्षित (कुवलयानन्द) all admitted the two figures मम्मट also admits them as separate figures

 The name प्रतिवस्तूपमा is explained by रुय्यक (अ स) as based upon the presence of the same धर्म in every वाक्य— प्रतिवाक्यार्थे उपमा साम्यमित्यन्वर्थाश्रयणात्। So he takes प्रतिवस्तु to mean प्रतिवाक्य**,** वस्तु being the same as वाक्यार्थ The word उपमा**,** he takes to mean साम्य i e औपम्य Some like कुवलयानन्द take उपमा to stand for साधारणधर्म प्रतिवाक्यार्थ मुपमा समानधर्मोऽस्यामिति व्युत्पत्ते— कुवलयानद मम्मट**,** to judge from his वृत्ति**,** seems to take उपमा to mean उपमान Where प्रतिवस्तु viz a वाक्याथ is उपमान in relation to another**’** But**,** as we saw above**,** the word उपमा in the name is reminiscent of the days when प्रतिवस्तूपमा was looked upon as a variety of उपमा

 **प्रतिवस्तूपमा *vs* वाक्यार्थनिदर्शना** — As we saw at the outset in both we have सादृश्य between one वाक्यार्थ and another**,** both are based on sameness of धर्म**,** and that in both the सादृश्य is गन्य onl But (1) whereas in निदर्शना the**,** वाक्यऽ are syntactically related य अल्पविषयया मत्या सूर्यवशवर्गन चिकीर्षति स उड्डपेन सगर निनीर्पति i e they are परस्परसापेक्ष in प्रतिवस्तूपमा**,** each one is independent of the other i e they are परस्परनिरपेक्ष (2) In निदर्शना the साधरणधर्म is not mentioned and has to be guessed on the strength of apparently unrelated facts being juxtaposed**.** In प्रतिवस्तूपमा**,** the साधारणधर्म is mentioned in cach वाक्य**,** though in varying terms**.**

 For प्रतिवस्तूपमा *vs* दृष्टान्त**,** see दृष्टान्त For प्रतिवस्तूपमा vs अर्थान्तरन्यास**,** see अर्थान्तरन्यास

 From the very fact that the figure is based on वाक्यार्थगत सादृश्यं It is easy to conclude that there must be one वाक्यार्थ प्रकृत in relation to another which is अप्रकृत This will be शुद्धा variety with one प्रकृत वाक्यार्थ and one अप्रकृत But it is possible to have the figure in the मात्र form also where there is one प्रकृत वाक्यार्थ compared with several अप्रकृत वाक्यार्थऽ**.** The साधर्म्य between the वाक्यार्थऽ may be conveyed negatively also i e**.** by वैध and पुम्पुर। नदि तुम्हारे बीच मराठी महिमानम्॥e & भन where पुम्पने of the is negatively put as ofend a meſ in the view Though uz does not talk of a sma, virtis like 127, स्र, For भतिवस्तूपमा s अर्थान्तरन्यास sce अर्थान्तरन्यास

विश्वनाथ जगन्नाथ**,** अप्पय्यदीक्षित have admitted it it Thus we get the following scheme—

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1709009079o.PNG"/>

** Essentials**—\[प्रतिवस्तु प्रतिवाक्यार्थ उपमा समानधर्मो अस्याम्—कुबलयानद वस्तुन वाक्यार्थस्य उपमानत्वात्—मम्मट who possibly sees in the prefix प्रति an indication of something pitted agasnst the उपमेय\] (1) There must be at least two वाक्यऽ mutually independent (2) In माला form**,** naturally**,** there will be more than two वाक्यऽ (3) Each वाक्यऽ must embody the साधारणधर्म (4) The साधारणधर्म though same must be stated in different by वस्तुप्रतिवस्तुभाव (5) One वाक्य must embody the प्रकृति**,** the other being अप्रकृत in relation thereto (6) The सादृश्य between the वाक्यार्थऽ must be implied only**,** never expressed (7) The सादृश्य may be stated positively (साधर्म्येण) or negatively (वैधर्म्येण) e g चकोर्य एव चतुरा चन्द्रिकापानकर्मणि। विनावन्तीर्न निपुणा सुदृशो रतनर्माती॥where the first वाक्य is positive in aspect**,** the second**,** But both have the same import viz क्स्यचिदेव कस्मिन्नपि negative विषये नैपुण्यम्

 § **स् ३० प्रतिवस्तूपमा** etc Construe Construe— सा तु प्रतिवस्तूपमा**,** यत्र एकस्य सामान्यस्य वाक्यद्वये द्वि स्थित। एकस्य सामान्यस्य द्वि स्थिति। e where one and the same साधारणधर्म is stated twice. द्वि = द्विवारम् Read **‘**twice**’** instead of **‘**n two forms**’** in the translation द्वि and वाक्यद्वये apply to शुद्ध प्रतिवस्तूपमा In मात्स**,** there will be three or more वाक्यऽ and the साधारणधर्म will be stated thrice or more Note that the सूत्र does not mention an important feature of प्रतिवस्तूपमा VIZ**.** that the साधारनधर्म must be conveyed by different modes of expression in the different वाक्यऽ**,** वाक्यद्वये one of the वाक्यऽ will be प्रकृतविषयक**,** तु marks transition to a new figure**.**

 वृत्ति—साधारणः धर्म mentioned as सामान्य in the सूत्र Where in the उपमेयवाक्य and the उपमानवाक्य**,** the सभारणधर्म is stated (उरानीयते) in different terms (शब्दभेदेन)**,** the figure is प्रतिवस्तूपमा Why the same साधारणधर्म should be stated in different terms is explained in

the वृत्ति कथितपदस्य दुष्टतयाभिहितत्वात्—since a word repeated over again (कथितपद) is declared to be defective If the same word or words were used over again to convey the साधारणधर्म**,** as it occurs of a in the different वाक्यऽ there will anse the defect or दोष of a चथिनपव To avoid it the साधारणधर्म is expressed in different words in the different sentences Note that the सूत्र overlooks this vital aspect of the figure कथितपद Repetition of word or words**,** पौनरूक्त्य मम्मट dose not define this दोष Vide उ VIl मा च explains it एकस्यैव पदस्य द्वि प्रयागकथितत्वम् See वामन cited above कथितपद स्य दुष्टतया अभिहितत्वात्—मम्मट like other आल्कारिवऽ has already (उ VII) averred that a कथितपद involves a poetical flaw सा वस्तुन वाक्यार्थस्य उपमानत्वात् मम्मटऽ own attempt to explain the propriety of the name प्रतिवन्तूपमा Therein one वाक्यार्थ is उपमान in relation to another which is मम्मट does not seem to see an अव्ययीभाव in प्रतिवस्तु as seen by रूय्यक or कुवलयानन्द प्रतिवस्तु=वाक्यार्थ**.** उपमा=उपमान acc**.** to him**.** Does he count on प्रति when he refers to since उपमान only since उपमान is what is set *against* (प्रति) the उपमेय ?

  **St 62** देवीभाग etc Illustrates शुद्धा साधर्म्ये प्रतिवस्तूपमा By a question of appeal is brought out the indignity inherent in a queen being reduced to the status of an attendant Construe देवीभव गमिता एषा कथ परिवरपद भजतु। दैवतरूपाङ्कित रत्न a piece of jewellery with the figures of deities carved therein **न** परिभोगयोग्यं does it mean Is not to be toyed with or trifled with ? Since we hardly look upon it as a sacrilege to wear (normal sense of परिभोग) Jewellery with divine figures engraved therein Icons do figure in our jewellery

 Here the same साधारणधर्म अयोग्यत्व is driven at**.** in the उपमेयवाक्य देवीभाणं ण्या by कथ उपमानवाक्य**,** न सतु रत्नम्**,** by न खलु That is why it is प्रतिवस्तूपमा It should not be mistaken for वैधर्म्ये**.** Since कत्रं and न सतु are identical मा चे says परिवारपत्व is the मधरणधर्म—अत्र परिवारपत्वं साधारणे धन परिभोगयोग्यमिति पर्यायो निम्नशब्देनोक्तपरिवरपरभोग्यत्ययोरेकार्थत्वात्। साहित्यचूडामणि also is of the same view सं प्र says महार्म्य is the साधारणधर्म It is conveyed by देवीभार्न and दैवारूपङ्किनं in the two वश्यऽ

 **St. 63** यदि दहस्यनले etc. Illustrites मल्यरूप सधर्म्ये प्रतिवस्तूपमा It is the nature of the good ones not to get dispinted as it is the nature of fire to burn**,** of hills to be bulky**,** of the brine to be saline

 Here as प्रकृतिरेव सतामविषादिता is the प्रकृतवाक्य or उपमेयवाक्य in relation to which the rest are अप्रकृतवाक्यऽ or उपमानवाक्यऽ**,** we have माला The words प्रकृतिरेव**,** in the उपमेयवाक्य and किमद्भुत**,** किं तत**,** सदैव (एव) convey the same साधारणधर्म viz स्वाभाविकत्व As all are stated in the same positive mode**,** the प्रतिवस्तूपमा is साधर्म्ये Thus this is मालारूपा साधर्म्ये प्रतिवस्तूपमा One should not suppose that**,** owing to प्रकृतिरेव सतामविपादिता we have an अर्थान्तरन्यास For प्रकृतिरेव etc is not generality or सामान्य of which अनलस्य दहनक्षमत्व**,** अद्रीणा गौरव and उदधिजल्स्य लवगत्व are particular cases or विशेषऽ For**,** as we shall see later**,** in अर्थान्तरन्यास**,** between the प्रकृत and अप्रकृत the relation is that of सामान्य विशेष or *vice versa*

** **एवमन्यनापि etc This principle of प्रतिवस्तूपमा viz**.** धर्मस्य शब्दभेदेन उपादान may be adopted elsewhere also What is the significance of अन्यत्र or elsewhere ? Acc to some like Jhalkikar**,** अन्यन अपि means वैधर्म्यस्थलेऽपि**,** as the instances given heretofore embrace साधर्म्ये प्रतिवस्तूपमा Acc to से प्र**,** it means in the complex formation of शृङ्खला प्रतिवस्तूपमा (? = रशना) मा च takes अन्यत्रापि tovmean माला**.** forms like this are possible in other figures like an also He says—यथा हरवन विषमदृष्टेि (St 77) इत्यादौ व्यतिरेके व्यतिरेकमाल Of all the interpretations वैधर्मस्थलेऽपि is helpful as it tends to make मम्मटऽ वृति embrace चैधर्म्ये प्रतिवस्तूपमा also**,** admitted by other आल्कारिकऽ like रुय्यक Still one wonders if this was the idea of मम्मट himself**,** for if it were so **, **certainly would have given us an instance of वैधर्म्ये प्रतिवस्तूपमा also

**१४ — दृष्टान्त**

 After whatever has been said above in reference to प्रतिवस्तुपमा**,** it will not be difficult to form an idea about the nature of this figure दृष्टान्त**,** like प्रतिवस्तूपमा and वाक्यार्थनिदर्शना**,** is based on वाक्यार्थमादृश्य.. This सादृश्य must be गम्य**,** not वाच्य Further**,** what is most import tant the धर्म in the प्रकृतविषयक वाक्य must be only similar to**,** and not the same as**,** that in the अप्रकृतविषयकवाक्य To put it otherwise**.** one धर्म must correspond to the other This is what the आलङ्कारिकऽ mean when they say that in दृष्टान्त we have the धर्मऽ in the प्रकृतविषयक and अप्रतविषयक वाक्यऽ

 विम्बप्रतिविम्बभाव— As seen above**,** when the धर्मऽ are भिन्न and yet correspond one to the other**,** the relation between them is विम्बप्रतिविम्बभाव as distinct from वस्तुप्रतिवस्तुभाव present in प्रतिवस्तूपमा**,** where the धर्म is the same**,** though couched in different words in different.

sentences Read कुवलयानद— यत्रोपमानोपमेयवाक्ययोर्भिन्नावेव धर्मो बिम्बप्रतिबिम्वभावेन निर्दिष्टौ तत्र दृष्टान्त। In त्वमेव कीर्तिमान् राजन् विधुरेव हि भ्रान्तिमान्**,** we have the धर्मऽ कीर्तिमान् and कान्तिमान् in the उपमेय and उपमान- वाक्यऽ**,** respectively**.** Both are different and yet correspond**,** one to the other**,** in the way in which a reflection (प्रतिबिम्व) corresponds to the object reflected (बिम्ब) though both are distinct Read जयरथ— लोके हि दर्पणादी विम्बात् प्रतिविम्बस्य भेदेऽपि मदीयमेवात्र वदन सक्रान्तमित्यभिमन्यन्ते। अन्यथा हि प्रतिबिम्बदर्शने वृशोह स्थूलोऽहमित्याद्यभिमानो नोदियात् भूषणविन्यासादौ च नायिका नाद्रियेरन्। Acc to him**,** बिम्ब प्रतिबिम्बभाव is the same as भेदेऽभेद.

 But it will be a mistake to suppose that बिम्बप्रतिबिम्बभाव in a दृष्टान्त is present only between the धर्मऽ To judge from the definitions of the figure given by different आलकारिकऽ it is clear that**,** in दृष्टान्त**,** there is as much बिम्बप्रतिबिम्बमात्र between the धर्मऽ as between the धर्मिन्ऽ also 1 e the objects in relation to which the धर्मऽ are given**.** Read जयस्थ —अतश्च धर्माणा धर्मिणा च बिम्बप्रतिविम्बभावेन निर्देशेऽयमलङ्कार। सा च — न तु प्रतिवस्तूपमायामिव साधारणधर्मस्यैव द्वि प्रयोग किं तु उपमेयादेरपि। मम्मट is specific on this point See his definition of the figure दृष्टान्त पुनरेतषां सर्वेषां प्रतिविम्बनम्। जगन्नाथ also lays stress on this point रुय्यक and अप्पय्यदीक्षित (कु)**,** however**,** insist on the बिम्बप्रतिबिम्बभाव between धर्मऽ only

 **दृष्टान्त and प्रतिवस्तृपमा** —This brings us to the main point of distinction between the two figures Though both are based on चाक्यार्थसादृश्य**,** though in both the वाक्यऽ are independent (unlike those in निदर्शना) though in both the सादृश्य is गम्य or tacit and not explicit still (1) in दृष्टान्त the धर्म in the उपमेयवाक्य is only similar to and not the same as that in the उपमानवाक्य That is there is between one धर्म and another the correspondence that is present between the बिम्ब and its प्रतिबिम्ब In प्रतिवस्तूपमा**,** on the other hand**,** the धर्म in both उपमेय-and उपमान-वाक्य is the same**,** and thus there is वस्तु-प्रतिवस्तुभाव between the धर्म in the उपमेयवाक्य and that in the उपमानवाक्य (2) In दृष्टान्त**,** the charm lies not only in the धर्मऽ corres ponding one to the other but also the धर्मिन्ऽ corresponding one to the other**,** while in प्रतिवस्तूपमा**, **the charm lies solely in the same धर्म presenting itself in one case and another Not that in प्रतिवस्तूपमा**, **we do not come across corresponding धर्मिन्ऽ. They may occur**,** but the poet does not stress them Thus in चवीर्य एव चतुराश्चन्द्रिकापानकर्मणि। व्याजन्त्य एव निपुणा सदृशी रतनर्मणि stress is land by the poet on the साधारणधर्म VIZ कस्यचिदेव कस्मिन्नपि विषये वैदग्ध्यं The correspondence between चकोर्य and आदन्त्य is hardly of any consequence to him in दृष्टान्त like

त्वमव कीर्तिमान् राजन् विधुरेव हि कान्तिमान् the poet lays as much emphasis on the correspondence between the धर्मऽ**,** कीर्तिमान् and कान्तिमान्**,** as on that between the धर्मिन्ऽ त्व and विधु

 But**,** as it has been already pointed out under प्रतिवस्तूपमा**,** not in all cases is it so easy to decide whether the धर्मऽ are same or only similar Further**,** one must not judge a case to be दृष्टान्त merely because to us a दृष्टान्त is any similar case He must decide whether the धर्म is भिन्न or अभिन्न and accordingly ascertain whether it is दृष्टान्त or प्रतिवस्तूपमा respectively**.** To a superficial observer चकोर्य एव etc or वसभवो गुणवानपि etc**.** may appear like दृष्टान्तऽ when they are really cases of प्रतिवस्तूपमा

** दृष्टान्त *and* निदर्शना** **—** Though in both**,** the सादृश्य IS वाक्यार्थगत**,** though in both there are वाक्यऽ more than one though in both the मादृश्य as implicit (गम्य)**,** still (1) in दुष्टान्त**,** the two (or more in मान्य) वाक्यऽ are grammatically independent**.** In निदर्शना**,** they are depend ent (य ते गुणान् वदति स मेरु लिलङ्घयिपति) (2) In दृष्टान्त**,** the धर्मऽ are भिन्न and mentioned**,** in निदर्शना the धर्म is अभिन्न and is implied That the धर्म is अभिन्न follows from the very fact that one thing is described as being tantamount to the other (3) In दृष्टान्त**,** the charm lies in the बिम्व प्रतिबिम्बभाव**,** persisting in the धर्म and धर्मन्ऽ also In निदर्शना**,** the charm lies in juxtaposing two (or more**,** in माला) facts having no apparent connection between them Read रय्यकः—निरपेक्षयोर्हि वाक्यार्थ योर्बिम्बप्रतिबिम्बभावोः दृष्टान्त। यत्र च प्रकृते वाक्यार्थे वाक्याथान्तरमारोप्यते सामानाधिकरण्येन तत्र सम्बधानुपपत्तिमूला निदर्शनव युक्ता। Read सा द— इह (निदर्शनाया) बिम्ब प्रतिबिम्बाक्षेपं विना वाक्यार्थापर्यवसानम्। दृष्टान्ते तु पर्यवसिनेन वाक्यार्थेन सामर्थ्यात् विम्बप्रतिविम्बताप्रत्यायनम्। It may be noted in passing that बिम्ब प्रतिबिम्बभाव is first hinted at by उद्भव**,** the originator of the figure**.** He talks of विस्पष्टपतिविम्बनिदर्शनं

 दृष्टान्त**,** as we saw under प्रतिवस्तूपमा**,** Is a younger figure than प्रतिवस्तूपमा It was who first admitted दृष्टान्त but under the significant name काव्यदृष्टान्त The name clearly suggests that the admission of this figure was in imitation of the दृष्टान्त that plays an important part in the Sanskrit syllogism After**,** all rheton cians except वामन admitted the figure

 For दृष्टान्त vs अर्थान्तरन्यास**,** see अर्थान्तरन्यास

 **दृष्टान्त *vs.* अप्रस्तुतप्रशसा** —Though in both the अप्रस्तुत is stated to bear out the प्रस्तुत in दृष्टान्त**,** प्रस्तुत and अप्रस्तुत are both stated or वाच्य**,** while in अप्रस्तुतप्रशसा only the अप्रस्तुत is stated**,** the प्रस्तुत being

गम्य. This naturally results in a second point of difference viz**.** in अ**.** प्र**.** the धर्म is stated once**;** in दृष्टान्त**,** the धर्म is stated both in the उपमेयवाक्य and the उपमानवाक्य**.** Read **स्टयक** **:**— सरूपयोस्तु वाच्यत्वे दृष्टान्तः। अप्रस्तुतस्य वाच्यत्वे प्रस्तुतस्य गम्यत्वे सर्वथा अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसेति निर्णयः।

** Divisions** **of** **the** **figure:** As the figure is practically analogous to प्रतिवस्तूपमा**,** the divisions of दृष्टान्त are the same as those of प्रतिवस्तूपमा**.** Thus we get the following scheme **:**—

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1709121473q.PNG"/>

It is to be noted that the वैधर्म्यमूला variety of दृष्टान्त is specifically stated and illustrated by मम्मट**, **though he did not mention a corresponding variety of प्रतिवस्तुपमा**.**

** .*Essentials*:**—\[दृष्टः अन्तः निश्चयः यत्र–मम्मट**.** On the statement of which instance**,** a definite idea (अन्त) of the matter under comsideration can be had \] (1) There must be सादृश्य between वाक्यार्थऽ**.** (2) This सादृश्य must be गम्य (यथेवादिपदैः शून्यं बुधैर्दृष्टान्त उच्यते—**उद्भट**) and hence उपमाप्रतिपादक like इव etc**.** must not be used. (3) There must be a मकृतविषयक वाक्य that bears सादृश्य to another अप्रकृतविषयक वाक्य**.** (4) These वाक्यऽ must be grammatically independent**.** (5) In माला the अप्रकृतविषयक वाक्यऽ are more than one (6) The धर्म in अप्रकृतविषयक बाक्य must be distinct from that in the प्रकृतविषयक but yet must correspond to it by बिम्ब-प्रतिबिम्बभाव**.** (7) This बिम्व-प्रतिविम्बभाव must be apparent also between the धर्मिनूऽ**.** (8) In वैधर्म्ये दृष्टान्त**,** the सादृश्य must be conveyed negatively**.** That is**,** if the उपमेयवाक्य is positive the उपमानवाक्य must be negative and vice versa.. (9) सा**.** द**.** insists**,** rather justifiably**,** on the instance being one which is well-known**.** For this reason**,** स्रगियं यदि जीवितापहा हृदये किं निहिता न हन्ति माम्। विषमप्यमृतं कचिद् भवेदमृत वा विषमीश्वरेच्छया॥is not दृष्टान्त**,** acc**.** to him**.** He says**:** इह तु विषामृतयोरभृतवियीभावस्याप्रसिद्धेर्न तस्य (दृष्टान्तस्य) सद्भावः।

 § सू**.** ३१**.** **दृष्टान्तः** **पुनरेतेपों** etc. दृष्टान्त consists in the reflection of all these**.** एतेषां सर्वेषां i**.** e**.** not only must the धर्मऽ correspond**,** but also the धर्मिन्ऽ**.** The charm of the figure lies in the correspondence between all units of the उपमेय- and उपमान वाक्यऽ**.** प्रतिबिम्यनं

I e correspondence**,** as seen above. Read **कुवलयानद**— वस्तुनो भिन्न योरप्युपमानोपमेयधर्मयो परस्परसादृश्यादभिन्नयो पृथगुपादान बिम्ब प्रतिबिम्वभाव इत्याल्कारिक समय। **र** **ग.** also insists upon the correspondence between all units—प्रकृतवाक्यार्थघटकानामुपमा (१ ना) दीना साधारणधर्मस्य च बिम्वप्रतिबिम्बभावे दृष्टान्त। सा द—भिन्ने बिम्बनुबिम्वत्वम्।

 **वृत्तिः**— एतेषा etc**.** **साधारणधर्मादीनाम्** आदि includes**,** as seen above**,** the धर्मिन् also in the उपमेयवाक्य and the उपमानवाक्य In त्वमेव कीर्तिमान् राजन् विधुरेव हि कान्तिमान्**,** just as there is correspondence between the धर्मऽ कार्निमत्त्व and कान्तिमत्त्र**,** so there is correspondence between त्व and विधु**,** the धर्मिन्ऽ

 **दृष्टोऽन्त** etc Explanation of the name means दृष्ट अन्त निश्चय यत्र means On the statement of which (यत्र=यम्मिन् उपादीयमाने) the matter on hand is decided with certainty An ambiguous thing becomes clear as soon as we get an instance to corroborate it. It is for this reason that सा द insists upon a प्रख्यात instance**.** In Sanskrit syllogism दृष्टान्त plays an important part in deciding an obscure fact**.** Read जयरथ— दृष्टान्ते पुनरेतादृशो वृत्तान्तोऽन्यत्रापि स्थित इति प्रकृतस्य अर्थस्य अविस्पष्टा प्रतीतिर्मा भूदिति प्रनीतिविशदीवरणार्थमर्थान्तरमुपादीयते।

 **St 64** स्वयि दृष्ट एव etc Illustrates साधर्म्ये शुद्ध दृष्टान्न A दूती pleads with the नायक as to how the नायिका**’**s mind gets soothed at the sight of hum निर्वाति is soothed derives comfort कुमुद्वत्या कुसुम=कुमुदम् आलोके=At the sight

 Here त्वयि मनोभवज्वलितम् is the उपमेयवाक्य**,** आलोके कुमुद्वत्या is the उपमानवाक्य The धमऽ conveyed by निर्वाति and विकसति are not the same**.** They are only similar and hence correspond उपमानवाक्य Thus there 25 विम्व प्रतिबिम्बभाव between the धर्मऽ. This correspondence also extends to the other units vis a vis viz त्व—हिमांशु तस्या—कुमुद्वत्या**,** मन कुमुम (=कुमुद) As there is only one उपमानवाक्य**,** it is शुद्ध (not माल्य) As both वाक्यऽ are positive**,** it is साधर्म्ये दृष्टान्त

 **St 65** तवाहवे *etc* Illustrates as the वृत्ति says**,** वैधर्म्ये दृष्टान्त of the शुद्ध vanety Someone (possibly the poet) describes to the king how the hostile warriors were put to rout as he (the king) was about to draw his sword Construe आरव कुरागान्तिर वरम् अनिनीयत माहमयमशर्मण**.** तत्र परेषां मग विशरानामगु। पासव दि अवाने रिवरनां दयते। कर आनिनीयत तव may be taken either as a Gen Absolute or may be construed with तव परे (of your enemes) The first scerns preferable**.** साहसकर्मशर्मन् Who takes delight in acts of daring

विशरारुता (वि + शू) = विशीर्णता A formation like वन्दारु from वन्द् (शृवन्द्योरारु । ३ २ १७३) पासव स्थिरता दधत (3rd plu Pres था) Particles of dust remain steady अवाते when there is no wind

 Here विशरारुता and स्थिरता are in exact antithesis That is why one वाक्य (उपमेयवाक्य) is positive and another (उपमानवाक्य) negative (अवाते) विशरारुता गच्छति put negatively will correspond to स्थिरतां गच्छति That is why it is वैधर्म्यं दृष्टान्त There is again correspondence between त्व and वात, मटा and पासव As there is only one उपमानवाक्य the दृष्टान्त is शुद्ध not माला Positively stated it would have been मपुद्विलासे तरला हि रेणव - सा चू

 Will it be wrong if this were taken to illustrate वैधर्म्यं प्रतिवस्तूपमा ? Possibly not Above we assessed स्थिरता as negatively *corresponding* to विशरारुता But we could as well take it as negatively the same (वस्तु प्रतिवस्तु) as विशरारुता and then the figure will be प्रतिवस्तूपमा

 The following remark of र ग will be helpful in understanding the significance of वैधर्म्यं दृष्टान्त (or प्रतिवस्तूपमा)— वैधर्म्योहरण हि प्रस्तुत धर्मिविशेषापारूढार्थदार्त्थाय स्वाक्षिप्तस्वव्यतिरेकसमानजातायस्य धर्म्यन्तरारूढस्य अप्रकृतार्थस्य कथनम्। It consists in the mention of an अप्रकृतार्थ in relation to some object, which (अप्रकृतार्थ) belongs to a class antithetical to the class to which प्रकृतार्थ belongs This is done to corroborate the fact stated in relation to the object under consideration See अर्थान्तरन्यास

**१५ — दीपक**

 In प्रतिवस्तूपमा and दृष्टान्त we saw that the interest centred round one and the same धर्म for corresponding धर्मऽ being stated in the प्रकृतवाक्य and the अप्रकृतवाक्य, leading to गम्य औपम्य In दीपक and तुल्ययोगिता that follows the central interest lies in several things being connected with the same धर्म in one and the same वाक्य and thus suggesting mutual similanty To put it otherwise while प्रतिवस्तूपमा and दृष्टान्त are based on वाक्यायौपम्य, तुल्ययोगिता and दीपक are based on पदार्थोपम्प That is why प्रतिवस्तूपमा and दृष्टान्त requure अनेकवाक्यऽ while तुल्ययोगिता and दीपक are embodied in one वाक्य only

 It is not difficult to understand how there results औपम्प by several पदार्थऽ being connected with the same धर्म in the body of a single sentence When a poet while describing a season says दिनानि पद्यानि च वृद्धिमीयु we get the idea that he wants to suggest औपम्य between दिनानि and पद्यानि by connecting both with the same धर्म viz वृद्धिगमन When कालिदास says in रघुवंश सचारपूतानि दिनन्तराणि कृत्वा

दिनान्ते निलयाय गन्तुम्। प्रचक्रमे पल्लवरगताम्रा प्रभा पतङ्गस्य मुनेश्च धेनु॥, he connects धेनु and प्रभा with the same धर्म viz, निल्याय गन्तु प्रचक्रमे and thus drives at their similarity

 दीपक and its divisions—

 Coming to दीपक, in particular, we find that therein the poet connects two or more पदार्थऽ with the same धर्म, but of these पदार्थऽ one is उपमेय or प्रकृत, the other or others being उपमान or अप्रकृत The may be a गुण or a क्रिया \[or a negative statement cp न मनागपि निश्चिन्त मण्डले शत्रुमित्रनो \] As the same धर्म illumines प्रकृत and अप्रकृत, the figure is called दीपक Sometimes, however, one and the same पदार्थ is made to stand in the same case relation (कारकसबन्ध) to different क्रियाऽ This is what is known as the कारक दीपक. In वसु दातु यशो धातु विधातुमरिमर्दनम्। नातु च मादृशान् राजन्नतीव निपुणो भवान्॥(जयरथ), we have भवान् as the कर्तृ (one of the कारक relations) of the different क्रियाऽ वसु दातु यशो धातु, अरिमर्दन विधातु and मादृशान् त्रातुम्

Thus we get the following scheme,—

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708495222दी.PNG"/>

 Many आलङ्कारिकऽ from भामह onwards have divided धर्मदीपक also on another principle viz according to the position of the धर्म, at the beginning, or in the middle or at the end of the वाक्य But some like विश्वनाथ and जगन्नाथ quite justifiably saw no special charm in this principle of place shifting जगन्नाथ,for instance, observes— वस्तुतस्तु धर्मस्यादिमध्यान्तगतत्वेऽपि चमत्कारवैलक्षाण्याभावात् vienkerarmäsit amendezrantara। मम्मट does not refer to this principle of division at all, nor does he go into the subdivision of धर्म into गुण, क्रिया or अभाव Some आलङ्कारिक like दण्डिन् and अप्पम्पदीक्षित speak of आवृतिदीपक where a पर or अर्थ or both are repeated This repetition, it may be seen, is against the very nature of दीपक.

 कारकदीपक—This deserves a closer study owing to some con troversial issues raised by it. To begin with, कारकदीपक is a corollary

to क्रियादीपक. If in क्रियादीपक, two or more पदार्थऽ are related to the same क्रिया, in कारकदीपक, several क्रियाऽ are related to the same पदार्थ. \[अत्र च यथा अनेककारकगतत्वेनैकक्रियादीपक तथानेकक्रियागतत्वेन एककारकमपि दीपकम्।-रुय्यक\] But the question arises should a कारकदीपक, like other दीपकऽ, suggest औपम्य between the different क्रियाऽ? That all the क्रियाऽ are related to the same कारक is a superficial similarity. Some आलङ्कारिकऽ are content with this semblance of similarity. मम्मट, for instance, has given an instance: स्विद्यति कूणति etc. where the various क्रियाऽ have no better साधर्म्य than एककारकत्व (here the कारक being subject). विश्वनाथ, in his साहित्यदर्पण, gives an instance....................तपस्विनी सा। उत्तिष्ठति, स्वपिति वासगृह त्वदीयमायाति याति श्वसिति क्षणेन॥ where also the similanty between the क्रियाऽ is only to the extent of एककर्तृकत्व दण्डिन् also, though he does not specify कारकदीपक, has given instances of दीपक wherein सादृश्य is no more than this i e एककारकत्व *Cp*. नृत्यन्ति निचुलेत्सगे गायन्ति च कलापिन। बध्नन्ति च पयोदेषु दृशो हर्षाश्रुगर्भिणीः॥ Where कलापिनः is the subject of the various क्रियाऽ. Cp. again another instance given by दण्डिन्ः हरत्याभोगमाशाना गृह्णाति ज्योतिषा गणम्। आदत्ते चाद्य मे प्राणानसौ जल्धरावली॥These instances show that here was a variety of दीपक depending upon कारकैकत्व only and did not require any further औपम्य between the क्रियाऽ. रुद्रट, who was the first to admit कारकदीपक also it may be noted, only insists on कारकैकत्व and nothing further. (Cp. यत्रैकमनेकेषां वाक्यार्थानां क्रियापद भवति। तद्वत्कारकपदमपि तदेतदिति दीपक द्वेधा॥).
