Because the persons are महामहिमशालिन This Is therefore उक्तनिमित्ता विशेषाक्ति In आहूतोऽपि महायैरमीत्युक्त्वा विमुक्तनिद्रोऽपि। गन्तुमना अपि पथिक सकोच नैव शिथिलयति॥(अ स) the पथिक who had closed his eyes (सकोच) did not open them even when hus sleep was over

This was due to his expecting a vis on of his beloved when his eyes were closed (प्रियतमाम्वप्नसमागम) The poet does not mention this निमित्त or विशेष which we are left to guess. This is अनुक्तनिमित्ता विशेषोक्ति

  But there are cases where the poet himself is not in a position to assign any reason why the effect does not take place even when the cause is there In such cases the sense of wonder the inscrutability of the of the reason itself constitutes the विशेष In हरनापि तनु यस्य (= स्मरस्य) शम्भुना न हत बल the बलहरण does not follow तनुहरण its normal cause How this comes about the poet himself is not in a position to judge To him as to the general reader the reason is incomprehensible Hence this is अचिन्त्यनिमित्ता विशेषोक्ति

 Thus we get the following scheme which is adopted by —  
<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708480381Screenshot2024-02-21072203.png"/>  
head रुय्यक - ....................................... But there seems to be greater justice in मम्मट’s classification Because ...............deserves to form a separate class by itself

  Scope of the figure— In विशेषोक्ति normally the form is The cause being there the effect is not there But, as in ................I have widened its scope also to include cases where a cause is followed by a phenomenon contrary to its normal effect. Instead of a we have while in this case. Read मम्मट’s ...........................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................

Viz स ण्व वर,मा एव क्षपा etc, and instead of कार्याभाव, Viz. अनुत्कण्ठा न उत्पद्यते we have the कार्यविरुद्धसद्भाव viz उत्पण्ठा नयते

  Such आर्थी विशेषोक्ति, it should be seen, cannot be distingushed from आर्थी विभावना (one variety) where instead of the cause being denied the contrary of the cause happens to be stated, e g शीतानुकिरणास्तर्वी हन्त सन्नापयन्ति ताम् Here one may argue with perfect justice, that we have आर्थी विशेषोक्ति also, the cause, शीत किरण, is there, but instead of कार्यभाव, say, मुख न आवहन्ति we have the कार्यविरुद्ध viz सन्तापयन्ति Similarly, in य कौमारहर' etc, which as said above, illustrates आर्थी विशेषोक्ति, one may find आर्थी विभावना also चेत् समुत्कण्ठने the कार्य is there, with the कारणविरोधिन्ऽ viz स ण्व वर etc In short, आर्थी विभावना and आर्थी विशेषोक्ति cannot be distinguished one from the other. Read रुय्यक— तथा ह्युत्वण्ठाकारणाभावे उत्कण्ठाया उपत्तौ विभावना स च कारणाभावो "य कौमारहर" इत्यादिना कारणविरुद्धमुखेन प्रतिपादित। तथा न “य कौमारहर" इत्याद्युत्कण्ठा कारणसद्भावेऽपि अनुत्कण्ठया अनुत्पत्तौ विशेषोक्ति। सा चानुत्पत्ति “समुत्कण्ठने" इति
विरोधो पत्तिमुखेनोक्ता।

  आलङ्कारिकऽ like रुय्य्क, विश्वनाथ and जगन्नाथ give these cases as सदेह मकर of विभावना and विशेषोक्ति मम्मट means as much when he says on य कौमारहर etc— अत्र स्फुटो न कश्चिदलकार Properly speaking these cases ought to have come under विरोध because, as we shall see later, there both the confucting things surprise us or to put it in आलङ्कारिक's words, are बाध्य, while in विभावना the फलभाव only surprises us, not कारणाभाव. And, in विशेषोक्ति फलाभाव only and not कारणसाकल्य causes surprise In these figures we have एकतोबाध, in विरोध मिथोबाध But when the entire field of कार्य-कारण-भाव was taken out of the sphere of विरोध, the आलकारिकऽ. were compelled to subsume such cases under विभावना or विशेषोक्ति This confusion would not have arisen if विभावना were strictly taken as embodying कारणाभाव— कार्यभाव, and विशेषोक्ति as embodying कारणसाकल्यकार्याभाव

  विशेषोक्ति is a figure dating back from भामह and दण्डिन् Both, however, were not explicit in their definitions An effect presenting itself despite a defective cause is in their view, and one can easily see how it borders on our विभावना उद्भट's is an explicit definition embodying the figure as later afers like मम्मट conceive it He talks of उक्तनिमित्ता and अनुक्तनिमित्ता varieties वामन understood the figure in a different light altogether To him it was based on औपम्य where the उपमेय is lacking in some feature of the उपमान द्युत नाम पुरुषस्य असिंहासन राज्य or अचतुर्वदनो ब्रह्मा is a विशेषोक्ति acc to

him He himself admits in his वृत्ति that thus has features of a रूपक – रूपक चैद प्रायेण, and रुय्यक and विश्वनाथ also criticise him showing that his विशेषोक्ति is really a रूपक रुद्रट, of all आलङ्कारिकऽ, did not admit विशेषोक्ति but his व्याघात corresponds to विशेषोक्ति रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित all admitted the figure मम्मट admitted it and was the frist to give the three divisions उक्तनिमित्ता, अनुक्तनिमित्ता and अचिन्त्यनिमित्ता.

  **विशेषोक्ति Vs. विभावना :— **Both the figures are based on विरोधाभास in the field of causation. But (i) while in विशेषोक्ति, the causes being there, the effect does not follow, in विभावना , the effect follows even when the cause is not there (ii) In विशेषोक्ति, the कार्यानुत्पत्ति surprises us , in विभावना, in विभावना, the कार्योत्पत्ति does so But, as seen above, आर्थी विभावना cannot be distinguished from आर्थी विशेषोक्ति.

   **Essentials—** \[ विशेष अभिव्यङ्क्तु प्रयुज्यमाना उक्ति — रुय्यक or विशपस्य अनुत्पत्तिनिमित्तस्य उक्ति अवगति यत्र— वैद्यनाथ तत्सत् ( कु ) \] . ( 1 ) The purest form will be. Even when the causes are present, the effect does not follow. (2) This violation of causation draws our attention to the circumstance that prevents the effect from taking place (3) This circumstance is the विशेष or निमित्त (4) This विशेष or निमित्त may or may not be mentioned When not mentioned, it is certainly more poetical (5) Sometimes theविशेष may be incomprehensible or mysterious, to the poet as to the reader In such cases the poet's aim is merely to present us with a mystery ( 6 ) Instead of फल्लभाव, the poet may give विरुद्धफल which as seen above gives rise to सहसकर of विभावना and विशेषोक्ति

  For विशेषोक्ति vs विरोध, see विरोध.

  § सू ३९ विशेषोक्ति etc Construe— अखण्डेषु कारणेषु फलावच विशेषोक्ति अखण्डेषु कारणेषु i e even when the causes are present in their entirety This corresponds to उद्भट's शक्तीनां सामग्येऽपि. This does not mean that there must be a plurality of causes mentioned Even a single cause without the effect following may give rise to this figure. फलावच literally, non mention (अवचसू) of the effect, i e non production of the effect.

  वृत्ति :— मिलितेष्वपि कारणेषु The कार्य is produced as a result of a number of causes operating together i e concomitance of causes This need not be taken too literally. There may be the mention of one cause even by the poet कार्यस्य अकथनं is कार्यानुत्पत्तिकथन अनुक्तनिमित्ता, उत्तनिमित्ता and अचिन्त्यनिमित्ता are its sub-divisions first conceived by

मम्मट In अनुक्तनिमित्ता, the निमित्त though conceivable is not stated. In अचिन्त्यनिमित्ता the निमित्त is inconceivable and hence not stated some rhetoricians would bring these under one head अनुक्तनिमित्ता with two sub-divisions –चिन्त्यनिमित्ता and अचिन्त्यनिमित्ता (See above)

 **St 83 निद्रानिवृत्तौ** etc Illustrates अनुक्तनिमित्ता विशेषोक्ति Description of a नायिका reluctant to move away from the नायक's embrace निद्रानिवृत्ति End of sleep द्युरत्न the sun पराप्त = प्राप्त An अपि is to be understood after all Locatives निद्रानिवृत्तौ अपि, सखीजने द्वारपद पराप्ते अपि, श्लथीकृताश्लेषरसे भुजगे अपि श्लथीकृताश्लेषरसे भुजगे— Even when the paramour's interest in embrace had grown slack भुजग Paramour

  Here the causes, निद्रानिवृत्ति, द्युरनोदय सखीजनप्राप्ति भुजगमस्य आश्लेषरमश्लथी करण, being there, the effect, viz आलिङ्गनत चलन does not follow (न चचल) This is due to the नायिका's ardent passion for the भुजग - a circumstance not stated Hence this is अनुक्तनिमित्ता विशेषोक्ति Note that though here several causes are given as being present even one cause would have been enough to produce विशेषोक्ति For instance the poet could have said निद्रानिवृत्तौ अपि आलिङ्गनत न चचाल and given us विशेषोक्ति though not as emphatic as the one under consideration

 ** St. 84** **कर्पूर इव** etc Illustrates उक्तनिमित्ता विशेषोक्ति Tribute to Cupid **कर्पूर इव दग्ध** Not कर्पूर इव शक्तिमान् Cupid though burnt by Śiva continues to be powerful in every man

  Here दाह Is the कारण its कार्य should be शक्तिनाश That there is no कार्य VIZ शक्तिनाश is indirectly conveyed by saying that Cupid is शक्तिमान् \[ The direct form would have been दग्धोऽपि न अशक्तः \] Why there is no शक्तिनाश is made clear by the epithet अकायवीर्य— Irresistible in vigour Therefore this is उक्तनिमित्ता विशेषोक्ति (आर्थी) Read रुय्यक— अत्र सत्यापि णेऽविकले कारणेऽशक्तत्वाख्यस्य कार्यंस्यानुपत्ति शक्तिस्वरूपेण विरुद्धेन धर्मेणोपानबद्धा।

  ** St 85 स एकः **etc Illustrates अचिन्त्यानिमित्ता विशेषोक्ति Tribute to Cupid Construe— एक त्रीणि जगन्ति जयति ।

   Here तनुहरण the cause, is there still the effect does not follow— बल न हृतम् Why कुसुमायुध even when deprived of the body does not lose his strength is a mystery as much to the poet as to the reader This is therefore, अचिन्त्यनिमित्ता विशेषोक्ति— Note that in एक त्रीणि नगन्ति जयति we have विरोध

**२२-यथासङ्ख्य**

 It is difficult to decide what made मम्मट place this figure after विभावना and विशेषोक्ति both based on कार्य-कारणभाव. The groupings by उद्भट also do not appear to have influenced him in the matter of the order of this figure. उद्भट takes यथासख्य, उत्प्रेक्षा and स्वभावोक्ति together and अर्थान्तरन्यास from his preceding group is get to be dealt with by मम्मट. It seems, therefore, that यथासख्य is put in this place only arbitrarily. Its proper place would have been with the शृखला figures like कारणमाला and एकावली

  The figure does not possess any great poetical charm. It consists in the members of one series being related, in the order in which they are mentioned, with the members of other senes— one or more. Thus in शत्रु मित्र विपत्तिं च जय रञ्जय भय, (कु.) शत्रु मित्र and विपत्तिं are respectively connected with नय, रक्षय and भञ्जय The figure becomes more telling when there are several series, the order of whose members is governed by the order of the members in the principal series.

   The figure, therefore, possesses no more charm than what pertains to uniformity of arrangement of the members of different series And this, in itself. does not require poetry of any high order. That is why there was a school of neo-rhetoricians who refused to allow it the status of an अलकार, जगन्नाथ refers to this school. Read र ग— .................................................................................................................... in his विमर्शिनी also refuses to look upon यथाम्न्य as a figure. It is says he, merely the negation of the दोप-अरकम Read विमर्शिनी :-न चाम्य अल्कारावं युक्तम्। द्वेषभावमयरूपचमिच्यो दोष प्रमयेन।..न्स्य ववियनारमव.विशेष पनि नु नव्या उद्दिष्टाना मण अनुन But it must be conceded that when we come across a number of scries with a certain uniformity running through them, we do feel a charm ahin to that of mosaic work

विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदाक्षित recognised it as यथामरश्च रुय्यक and जगन्नाथ speak of शाब्द and आर्थ varieties, according as the units of the series are uncompounded (असमम्न) or compounded (ममम्न) मम्मट does. recognise the figure but gives no divisions

  **Essentials—** \[सख्या अनतिकम्प वर्तते इति यथासख्यम्। र ग explains as
follows— सख्याया अनतिवृत्तिश्व प्रथमस्य प्रथमेनैव, द्वितीयस्य द्वितीयेनैव इत्यादि सम्बधे भवति।\] (1) There must be a poetically charming statement about two or more things, standing in a series (2) These things in the series must be connected, in the same order, with other equal in number standing in a different series. (3) The other series may be one or more Thus a, b may be respectively connected with c, d of a second series with e, f of a third series, with g, h of a fourth and so on Or a, b, c of the first series may be respectively connected with d, e, f of a second series and with g, h, i of a third and so on (4) The more numerous the members of the senes or the series them elves the more charming becomes the figure, with its mosaic effect. That is why रुद्रट says तद् द्विगुण त्रिगुण बहुषूद्दिष्टषु जायते रम्यम्। (5) There is one principal series on which the rest are pivoted

  § सू ४० यथासख्य etc. Construe क्रमिकाणा (तेन) क्रमेणैव (अन्ये) समन्वय यथासख्यम् Where things standing in a particular order (क्रमिक) are connected (with others) in the same order (क्रमेणैव), we get the figure यथासख्य The plural of क्रमिकाणा should also comprehend dual That is to say, we may have a series of two things also But, as said above, the more numerous the members the more charming the figure Read मा च— बहुवचन अतन्त्रम्। तेन द्वयोरप्यर्थयोर्ययासख्यम्।

 ** St 86** **एकस्त्रिधा** etc Illustrates यथासख्य A compliment paid to a patron king showing how he is cherished by maidens with love, by the learned with dehght (समद) and by the foes with annoyance, due to hus grace, culture (विनय) and prowess

 Here the units of the series शौर्योष्मणा, विनयेन, लीलया are, in this very order, connected with the units, ताप, समदरस and रतिं of a second series, and also with the units, द्विषा, विदुषा, मृगीदृशा of a third series,Thus the connection is द्विषा चेतमि शौयाष्मणा ताप पुष्णन्, विदुषा चेतसि विनयेन समदरस पुष्णन् and मृगीदृशा चेतसि लीलया रर्ति पुष्णन् (विधा वससि)। This is
therefore, यथासख्य द्विषा विदुषा मृगीदृशा is the pivotal series Note that in एकस्त्रिधा वससि, there is विरोध

 Why is it that one particular unit gets connected only with some other particular unit and not with any and every other unit? The reply is simple It is योग्यता that determines this connection Thus in the instance given above there is परस्परयोग्यता between द्विषा चेतसि,शौर्योष्मणा and ताप, between विदुषा चेतसि विनयेन and समदरसं and between मृगीदृशा चेतसि, लीलया and रतिं

**२३—अर्थान्तरम्यास**

  अर्थान्तरन्यास finds its place here because with विभावना, व्यतिरेक, आक्षेप समासोक्ति and अतिशयोक्ति fit formed a group both according to भामह and उद्भट It is a figure embodied like प्रतिवस्तूपमा and दृष्टान्त, in वाक्य’s mutually independent by syntax But whereas in those two figures, it is the poets aim to point out the between two as based on वस्तु-प्रतिवस्तुभाव or बिम्ब-प्रतिबिम्बभाव here, in अर्थान्तरन्यास, it is the poet's intention to corroborate some particular fact (विशेष) by citing the general truth or vice versa Thus while it is वस्तु प्रतिवस्तुभाव and बिम्ब-प्रतिबिम्बभाव that are respectively in evidence in प्रतिवस्तूपमा and दृष्टान्त, in अथान्तरन्यास it is समर्थ्य-समर्थकभाव Says a poet हनुमानन्धिमतरत् दुष्कर विं महामनाम्। ( कु) Here in order to corroborate the particular fact that Hanūmat could cross the ocean has been adduced the general truth Nothing will be difficult for the great to achieve

  Corroboration or समयन being the poet's motive in this figure he avails himself of the expedient of citing a general truth to corroborate a particular fact or vice versa That is a विशेष he supports by a सामान्य or a सामान्य by a विशेष Sometimes as in दृष्टान्त, he will produce the same result by वैधम्य i e by giving the विशेष positively and the सामान्य negatively or vice versa Thus We get the following Scheme—

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708523222Screenshot2024-02-21191634.png"/>



 समर्थ्य is the thing corroborated, समर्थक is the thing that corroborates or bears out the समर्थ्य Rhetoricians like उद्भट, make divisions based on the position of समर्थ्य-समर्थक, whether one or the other is stated first. \[समर्थकस्य पूर्वं यद्वचोऽन्यस्य च पृष्ठत। विपर्ययेण वा....उद्भट\] There is yet another principle of division adopted by some like उद्भट, जगन्नाथ If the समर्थकवाक्य expresses its समर्थन by words like हि यत्, यत् etc, it is शाब्द अर्थान्तरन्यास It becomes आर्थ if such words are absent Read र ग— अस्मिन्नलकारे समर्थ्य समर्थकभाव आर्थ शाब्दश्चालकारता प्रयोजक । हि, यत्, यत इत्यादे प्रतिपादकस्याभावे आर्थ। तसत्त्वे शब्द। उद्भट— हिशब्दोक्त्या अन्यथापि वा About such divisions रुय्यक’s seems to be the correct attitude He sees nothing striking in them Read अ स –हिशब्दाभिधान- अनभिधानाम्या समर्थक्पूर्वोपन्यास-उत्तरोपन्यासाभ्या च भेदान्तरसम्भवेपि न तद्गणना सहृदयहृदयहारिणी।

  **अर्थान्तरन्यास vs समवद्वस्तुसम्बन्धा निदर्शना**— We have already seen how, in this variety of निदर्शना, some particular fact is taken to convey some general truth It, therefore has very close affinities with अर्थान्तरन्यास, particularly where a general truth is corroborated by a particular fact The one could in fact, be reduced to the other vice versaThus चूडामणिपदे धत्ते यो देव रविमागतम्। सता कार्यातिथेयीति बोधयन् गृहमेधिन might become an अर्थान्तरन्यास If put as सना आतिथेयी काया। उदयाचल आगत रवि चूडामणिपदे धत्ते।

  But there is a difference of form between the two While in निदर्शना, the सामान्य and विशेष are welded together in one sentence in अर्थान्तरन्यास the two are embodied in two वाक्य’s mutually independent by syntax

  ** अथान्तरन्यास vs दृष्टान्त and प्रतिवस्तूपमा**— All of them are अनेक वाक्यगत and these वाक्यऽ are grammatically independent But (1) while in दृष्टान्त and प्रतिवस्तूपमा, there is औपम्य between the two वाक्यऽ in अर्थान्तरन्यास there is समर्थ्य समथकभाव between them (2) While in दृष्टान्त and प्रतिवस्तूपमा, औपम्य being the motif, both the वाक्यऽ refer to particular facts (विशेष) or both to generalities ( सामान्य ) , in अर्थान्तरन्यास, समर्थन being the motif one वाक्य refers to the सामान्य, the other to the विशेष The reason is plain Corroboration requires a particularity for a generality and vice versa (3) Therefore while in प्रतिवस्तूपमा there is वस्तु-प्रतिवस्तुभाव owing to the same धर्म being stated differently, while in दृष्टान्त, there is बिम्ब-प्रतिबिम्बभाव owing to the धमेऽ (and धार्मिन्ऽ also) corresponding one to the other in अर्थान्तरन्यास there is सामान्य विशेषभाव between one वाक्य and the other Read सा द— समर्थ्य समर्थकवाक्ययो सामान्यविशेषभावोऽर्थान्तरन्यास। प्रतिवस्तूपमा- दृष्टानयोस्तु न वधेति भेद।

 Is there no समर्थन in प्रतिवस्तूपमा and दृष्टान्त ? It is there but not as conclusive as in अर्थान्तरन्यास. One can decisively corroborate a particular case by a generality or a generality by a particular case (न निविशेष सामान्यम्). But an analogous case as in दृष्टान्त or प्रतिवस्तूपमा cannot corroborate as conclusively. It has a probative value only in so far as it tends to make us aware of another similar case.

  ** अर्थान्तरन्यास vs. अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा :** — अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा based on सामान्य-विशेष-भाव bears resemblance to अर्थान्तरन्यास which also, as we saw, is based on सामान्य-विशेषभाव But (1) while In अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, only the अप्रस्तुत् is mentioned, the प्रस्तुत being suggested, in अर्थान्तरन्यास, both the अप्रस्तुत and प्रस्तुत are mentioned. To put it otherwise, in अस्तुतप्रशंसा, only the समर्थक Is वाच्य, the समर्थ्य being गम्य; in अर्थान्तरन्यास, both समर्थ्य and समर्थक are वाच्य. Read रुय्यकः— द्वयोः सामान्यविशेषयोः यदा वाच्यत्वे भवति तदा अर्थान्तरन्यासाविर्भावः। अप्रस्तुतस्य वाच्यत्वे प्रस्तुतस्य गम्यत्वे सर्वथाऽप्रस्तुतप्रशमेति निर्णयः। (2) This entails a further point of difference between the two figures. In अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, we have a single statement pertaining to the अप्रस्तुत In अर्थान्तरन्यास we have a double statement, one relating to the अप्रस्तुत, other to the प्रस्तुत

  Can there be अर्थान्तरन्यास based 0n कार्य-कारणभाव ? : —आलङ्कारिकऽ like रुय्यक and विश्वनाथ hold the view that, for समर्थन such as is aimed at in अर्थान्तरन्यास, like सामान्य विशेषभाव, कार्य-कारणभाव also is useful. In other words, according to them, we get अर्थान्तरन्यास not only where the two वाक्यऽ are related as सामान्य-विशेष but also where they are related as कार्य-कारण. Thus the समर्थ्यं वाक्य being कार्य, the समर्थक may be बदरण, and vice versa. In पृथ्वि स्थिरा भव भुजगत धारमैनाम्देवघरोतहराज्यम्, देव etc. is the कारण of पृथ्वि स्थिरा भव etc. This, therefore, is an instance of अर्थान्तरन्यास, acc. to this view. \[अत्र कारणभूत .........................................- रुय्यक.\] As against this, there is the view of जयरथ and जगन्नाथ, both of whom say that समर्थन can be effected only on the basis of सामान्य-विशेषभाव and not कार्य-कारणभाव. Where the two वाक्यऽ are related as कार्य-कारण, the figure becomes according to them Read जयरथः-...................................................................................................................... मम्मट also contines his अर्थान्तरस्य अदम to सामन्यविशेषभाव,

  ** For अर्थान्तरन्यास vs. अनुमान, see अनुमान**

 अर्थान्तरन्यास is a figure dating back from भामह and दण्डिन्. The definitions of both are vague in so far as they fail to bring out the ravs-tart that figures in the अलङ्कार, neither उद्भट nor वामन

refers to this essential feature रुद्रट appears to have been the first to point out how सामान्य- विशेषभाव operates in the figure. मम्मट and the later आलङ्कारिकऽ admitted the figure as based on सामान्य-विशेषभाव Sorme like रुय्यक and विश्वनाथ spoke of अर्थान्तरन्यास also based on कार्यकारणभाव.

   **Essentials— **\[प्रस्तुतसाधनसमर्थस्य अर्थान्तरस्य न्यास अत्र\] (1) There must be two वाक्यऽ, grammatically independent (2) One of them must embody a सामान्य and the other a विशेष (3) One of the two is प्रकृत the other अप्रकृत. (4) The प्रकृत is समर्थ्य because it is to be corroborated, the अप्रकृत is समर्थक because it corroborates (5) If a सामान्य is प्रकृत, a विशेष will be अप्रकृत and vice versa (6) There may be cases, acc to र ग, where both, the समर्थ्य and समर्थक, are प्रकृत in कस्तृप्येन्मार्मिक्तन्वि रमणीयषु वस्तुषु। हित्वानिक सरोनिन्या पश्य याति न षट्पद॥ (र ग), both are प्रकृत (7) The समर्थकवाक्य may or may not have a word like हि, यत्, यत् connotang समथन (8) If an उपमाप्रतिपादक like इव occurs, as occurs, as in एको हि दायो गुणमन्निपाने निमज्जनीन्दो किरणेष्विाक (कुस ), though we have सामान्य-विशेषभाव, it is a case of उपमा (or, acc to some, of उदाहरणालकार)

** § स ४१ सामान्य वा** etc Construe— यत्र सामान्य वा विशेष वा साधर्म्येण इतरेण वा तदन्येन समध्यते म अथान्तरन्यास । सामान्य विशेष Generality, General truth विशेष a particular fact coming under the सामान्य Truth triumphs— will be a सामान्य, युधिष्ठिर, the truthful, triumphed— will be a विशेष coming under it. तदन्येन i e सामान्य by विशेष, विशेष by सामान्य समर्थ्यते is corroborated, is borne out. साधर्म्येण इतरेण वा i e साधर्म्येण वैधर्म्येण वा। वैधम्र्येण निम्तमाधम्र्म्यविरुद्धधर्म —रामचरण तर्कवागीश See notes on वैधर्म्ये दृष्टान्त under दृष्टान्न We can have साधर्म्ये अर्थान्तरन्यास and वैधयै अर्थान्तरन्याम् Fach again will have two divisions With सामान्य as the समर्थ्य, विशेष as समर्थव and Vice Versa This gives us four divisions in all. Note that the rhetoncians do not speak of माला अर्थान्तरन्यास When a विशेष Is समर्थ्य and सामान्य, समर्थक we cannot have माला अर्थान्तरन्यास Because we cannot state any more than one सामान्य. But when a सामान्य is समर्थ्य and विशेष, समयक, it is possible to have माल inasmuch as we could cate one विशेष after another as the समर्थक. Could I not say, for instance, "Overweening ambition comes to gnef. Napoleon came to grief Kaiser came to gnef" ?

   **St. 87 निजदोषाकृत** etc Illustrates साधर्म्ये अर्थान्तरन्यस where सामान्य is समर्थ्य, विशेष समर्थक It is our defective vision that makes us see things in other than ther true colours अतिसुन्दरमेव एव emphasises मोदय विपरीत भगत appears otherwise i e ugly

 Here the general truth (सामान्य) viz दोषावृत्तमनसां मुनर विपरीत भाति is समर्थ्य पित्तो परमपि पीत पश्यति which is a particular fact coming under the सामान्य— पित्तोपहृतत्व being a लोप— is समर्थक. Both are positive assertions Hence it is साधर्म्ये अर्थान्तरन्यास with सामान्य as समर्थ्य and विशेष as समर्थक

   **St 88 सुसितवसना**etc Illustrates साधर्म्ये अर्थान्तरन्यास where विशेष is समर्थ्य सामान्य समर्थक. An अभिसारिका stranded in pitch dark is able to resume her course and reach her destination when her path is lit up by the king s glory sung by coincidence ab Construe..................... When a lovely lady (....) was moving wantonly in moonlight ...................... The white colour of ornaments and apparel was in order to make the party inconspicuous in the moon light. कीति केनापि अगायत— The fame was so bright that it hit up the quarters मुकाशकर प्रियगृह अगात् ............ because in the pitch dark she was becoming conspicuous The .............was not because she would lose her way in darkness but because she might be made out clearly with her white omaments and clothing standing in sharp relief against the background of darkness!

 Here the particular incident of the अभिसारिका being obliged though indirectly by the king (because his glory was of service to her) is समर्थ्य The general truth that the king was a source of happiness every where is adr .................................. Therefore विशेष being in effect ....................................................

 Here the समर्थ्य is the सामान्य or general truth the virtuous have to suffer for their virtues The समर्थक is the विशेष The naughty bull enjoys a life of ease It is easy to see how this समर्थक negatively (enjoyment being the negation of suffering) bears out the general truth This is therefore वैधम्य अर्थान्तरन्यास with a सामान्य as समर्थ्य and विशेष समर्थक

  **St. 90 अहो हि** etc Illustrates वैधर्म्ये अर्थान्तरन्यास with विशेष as समर्थ्य and सामान्य समर्थक Someone breaking some sad news to ht friend Some say that it is युधिष्ठिर breaking the sad news of अभिमन्युऽ death to अजुन भ आयुषा बहु अपराद्ध i e it is my misfortune that I continue to live since I have to report such a sorry thing

  Here the अधन्यत्व of the particular individual who is reporting the sad news is the समर्थ्य and is a विशेष The समर्थक is the सामान्य VIZ धन्यत्व of those who have not to report a sad news अधन्यत्व being the negation of धन्यत्व we have वधर्न्ये अर्थान्तरन्यास in which the समर्थ्य is a विशेष and समर्थक सामान्य

   How are we to decide that a particular वाक्य is समर्थ्य or समर्थक? Whatever is put first is not necessarily समर्थ्य because it is equally permissible to place समर्थ्य after समर्थक It is easy to decide this matter where हि यत् यत् are used as they introduce a समर्थक But where they are not used each case will have to be judged on its own. merits. Thus in नि...पावृतमनसाetc one may justifiably argue पश्यति पित्तापहत etc is समर्थ्य and नि..दोषा विपरीत the समर्थक In मसितवसना etc on the other hand the case of the अभिसारिका alone would seem. to be समर्थ्य as it forms the प्रकृत But if we picture to ourselves a poet lavishing praise on his patron क्व नामि शुभप्र becomes a समर्थ्य and the particular incident of the अभिसारिका समर्थक The same applie to गुणानामेव etc But in अहो हि मे etc the particular thing alone is समर्थ्य because it is the matter under consideration. In short for deciding समर्थ्य—समर्थक no hard and fast rules could be la d down. It has to be decided by taking into consideration the context etc

**२४– विरोध**

  विरोध is really speaking विरोधभास semblance of conflict genuine विरोध being negation of योग्यता and therefore of fact has no place in poetry and one indulging therein will produce not real poetry but a travesty of it For poetic Licence also has its limits The poet in all his peregrinations must not deviate from योग्यता and genuine विरोध does involve such deviation

  In विरोधाभास the poet gives us a truth but by paradox or contradictory statement. Is it not paradoxical and yet true that इन्दुमती should lose her life due to the garland of flowers falling on her? Is it not paradoxical and yet true that the tender flower the arrow of Cupid should prove deadly to the lover— मृ तीक्ष्णतर यदुच्यते ममथ दृश्यते त्वयि? Is not दुष्यन्त telling us a truth though involving a semblance of contradiction when he says विसृतति हिमगर्भेरझिमिन्दुर्मयूखेगत्वमपि (i e स्मरोऽपि) कुसुमबाणान् वज्रमारीकरोपि।? In short in विरोधाभास we have a truth set forth as a paradox And because it is truth an explanation (समाधान) can be given for the paradox. Read at रुय्यक— स च समाधान विना प्ररूढो लोप। सति तु समाधाने प्रमुख एवाभासमानत्वात् विरोधाभास। भामह's words समाधौ मकरे सति relating to विभावना also apply here

  We have already seen how there is paradox at the root of the figure विभावना and विशेषोक्ति To have an effect in the absence of a cause and to have no effect when the cause is potent enough are paradoxical indeed That way विभावना and विशेषोक्ति are aspects of विरोध But as we saw विरोध in the field of causation has crystal sed into at विभावना and विशेषोक्ति and a few other figures which enyy a status independent of विरोध Read र ग— कार्यकारणा बुध्यना ने विरोधालङ्कार तदालीलस्तु विभावनादि

क्रिया, or (3) गुण and द्रव्य. There may be विरोधाभास also between (1) क्रिया and क्रिया or (2) क्रिया and द्रव्य Lastly, there may be विरोधाभास between (1) द्रव्य and द्रव्य. This gives ten (4+3+2+1) divisions in all मम्मट admits this principle of division. रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ and जगन्नाथ admitted it however is critical of it See below

   Before रुद्रट , neither भामह nor दण्डिन् nor उद्भट, nor वामन had adopted this principle of division After रुद्रट, अप्पय्यदीक्षित did not adopt it जयरथ is critical of it and sees no charm in the divisions Says he अनेन एषा लक्ष्ये तथा वैचियाभावात अनवक्लृप्तिर्ध्वनिता। अत एव अस्माभिरप्येते नोदाहृता। He would himself propose the following divisions— अनेनेह चिन्ननैरनुक्ता अपि वैचियाधायिनो भेदा अनुमर्तव्या इत्यपि सूचितम्। नेन (1) भावयो (2) अभावयो (3) नभावाभावयोश्च विरुद्धन्वोपनिवन्धे विरोधो शय इति। जगन्नाथ also is equally critical of divisions based on ताति etc He would rather adopt a twofold division based on the presence or absence of श्लेष Read र ग— वस्तुतो नान्यादिभेदानामहृद्यवात् शुद्धत्वश्लेषमूलत्वाभ्या द्विविध ज्ञेय। Further, जगन्नाथ would divide it as शाब्द and आर्थ If words like अपि are present, it is शाब्द, Otherwise आर्थ Read यत्र अपिशब्दिर्विरोधस्य द्यातकस्तत्र विरोध शाब्द, अन्यत्र त्वार्थ इति तावत् प्राचा सिद्धान्त।

 ** श्लेष and विरोध**— This brings us to the part played by श्लेष in विरोध Though it is possible to have विरोधाभास without श्लेष, e g, तस्या मृणालवलयानि दवरहनराशि भवति, still in Sanskrit literature, श्लेषमूलक विरोध is of frequent occurrence. Writers like बाणभट्ट have made a profuse use of it in their writings बाणभट्ट, in particular, makes it a point to round off his elaborate descriptions with श्लेषमूलक विरोधाभास

  It is not difficult to understand why श्लेष should bulk large in विरोधाभास As a श्लिष्ट word possesses two meanings, the poet finds at easy to effect विरोधाभास therewith Take, for instance, बाण's विरोधाभास used in the राजकुलवर्णन in the **कादम्बरी** He says that the राजकुल ( palace) was अवत्तिविषयगतमपि मागधननाधिष्ठितम् How can the palace in अवन्ति have people from मगध ? To say so is contradictory, paradoxical But that the contradiction is only apparent (विरोधभास) becomes clear as we recall the other sense of ’मागध’ viz bards The palace had so many bards therein Thus the विरोधभास is rendered possible by the double meaning of the word मागध With one meaning— inhabitants of मागध, there is real विरोध as that meaning does not apply to the राजकुल With the other meaning bards, there is no विरोध as it applies to the palace Thus it is a विरोधाभास Some आलङ्कारिकऽ like उद्भट call these cases of श्लेष Others hold them as cases of मकर of श्लेष and

विरोध Read **रुय्यक**— श्लेषगभत्वे विरोधप्रतिमोत्यत्तिहेतु श्लेष औद्भटानान्। दर्शनान्तरे तु मकरालकार।

   **विरोध vs विभावना and विशेषोक्ति—** Just as in विरोध there is only semblance of conflict so in विभावना and विशेषोक्ति, there is only a semblance of conflict Again in both the conflicting things are present in the same abode But (1) While in विभावना— विशेषोक्ति the विरोध is in other field of कार्य-कारण-भाव in विरोध it is in other fields (2) While in विभावना-विशेषोक्ति only one of the विरुद्ध things strikes us as wonderful to wit फलसत्त्व in विभावना and फलाभाव in विशेषोक्ति in विरोध both the विरुद्ध things strike us To put it otherwise on विभावना-विशेषोक्ति we have एकतावाध while in विरोध we have मिथोबाध Read सा द— विभावनाया कारणाभावेन उपनिबध्यमानत्वात् कार्यमेव बाध्यत्वेन प्रतीयते। विशेषोक्ति च कार्यभावन कारणमेव। इह त्वन्यान्य द्वयोरपि बाध्यत्वमिति मे। This is also the view of रुय्यक But see how र ग corrects it in the matter of विशेषोक्ति— वस्तुतस्तु कार्यनुत्पत्तिरेव अस्मिन्नलङ्कारे (i e in विशेषोक्ति) बाध्या।

   But an आर्थी विभावना— आर्थी विशेषोक्ति which give rise to सदेहसकर we get मिथोबाध and therefore there will be justification in looking upon them as cases of विरोध When अग्निमित्र addresses his sore afflicted heart प्रसक्त निर्वाणे हृदय परितापं वहसि किम् or when the poet says शखाद्भीणानिनाताऽयम् we get आर्थी विभावना or आर्थी विशेषोक्ति But it will not be quite wide of the mark if we say that it is a case of विरोध— though when we do so we just allow विरोध to trespass on the field of कार्यकारणभाव

 ** विरोध is रूपक**— If मृणालवलयानि लवलहनराशि or चन्द्र वच्चनल is a विरोध why should not मुख चन्द्र also be a case of विरोध since in both one thing is superimposed on another? The answer lies in the fact that in the former the poet wants to stress the विरोध between मृणालवलयानि and लवलहनराशि While in the latter though there as विरोध between मुख and चन्द्र still the poet wants to stress the सादृश्य between the two in order to attribute to मुख the धर्मऽ of चन्द्र such as .......... etc To put it otherwise in मृणालवलयानि ......... there is implicit the force of अपि मृणालवलयानि (अपि) ...... भवन while in मुख चन्द्र this force is absent Read र ग— एवं च रुपके मुख चन्द्र ................................................................................................... .................. ................. ............... ................................................................................................................................।

 For विरोध vs असङ्गति see असङ्गति

** विरोध** is a figure dating back from भामह and दण्डिन् Both of them give definitions that fail to bring out the fact that the विरोध must be apparent only All the same, the instances given by them are of विरोधाभास उद्भट also gives a definition equally vague on the point of आभास since he reproduces भामह verbatim वामन was the first to state categorically that विरोध is really विरोधाभास रुद्रट is silent over आभासत्व but gives divisions based on the fourfold सकेत of words मम्मट is explicit in the matter of आभास and adopts रुद्रटऽ principle of division based on सकेत रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ and जगन्नाथ follow मम्मट both in emphasizing आभासत्व and in giving the सकेतमूल divisions जगन्नाथ however, is loth to admit the divisions as poetical अप्पय्यदीक्षित ignores the divisions Some आलङ्कारिकऽ style the figure as विरोध, others as विरोधाभास

   **Essentials**— \[आभासते इत्याभास । विरोधश्चासौ आभासश्च।— **वैद्यनाथ तत्सत् **on कुवलयानन्द आभास is further explained by र ग— आ ईषद् भामह इत्याभास\] (1) Two mutually conflicting things must be set forth as abiding in one abode (2) The conflict must be in a field other than that of कार्यकारणभाव (3) The conflict must not be genuine but. apparent that is to say, we should have only विरोधाभास and not real विरोध (4) Since the विरोध is only apparent, it must be easily explicable— समाधौ सुकरे सति– भामह (on विभावना) (5) The विरोधाभास may or may not be based on श्लेष (6) The idea of conflict is explicit when अपि is used and implicit when it is not used but implied

    ** § सू ४२ विरोध** etc. Construe अविरोधेऽपि यत् विरुद्धवेन वच स विरोध। अविरोधेऽपि i e there must not be real conflict Real conflict is not permissible as it violates the requrement of योग्यता Thus the अलङ्कार विरोध is in effect विरोधाभास

  ** वृत्ति** वस्तुवृत्तेन अविरोधेऽपि i e वास्तविक अविरोधऽपि— Even when there is no real conflict **विरुद्धयोरिव अभिधानम्**. The two things are set. forth as if (इव) they are mutually confficting, incompatible इव emphasizes the आभासत्व the aspect of being a semblance

   § सू ४३ **जातिश्चतुर्भि** etc Construe जाति जात्याद्यै चतुर्भि विरुद्धा स्यात्। गुण त्रिभि विरुद्ध स्यात्। क्रिया द्वाभ्या विरुद्धा स्वात् । द्रव्यं द्रव्येणेव (विरुद्ध) स्यात्। इति ते (विरोधा) दश भवन्ति।

** There are four** जातिविरोधऽ VIZ (1) जाति vs जाति (2) जाति vs गुण (3) जाति vs क्रिया, (4) जाति vs द्रव्य रुद्रट does not admit the last viz जाति vs द्रव्य मा च rightly crithcises this view—नित्यमेव द्रव्यानितत्वात्

नानर्न जातिद्रव्ययोर्विरोध इति ब्रुवन् रुद्रट नवभेदी मन्यते। तदार्तम्। जातेर्गुणाद्याश्रितत्वस्यापि भावात्।

 Three गुणविरोधऽ are possible (1) गुण vs गुण (2) गुण vs क्रिया (3) गुण vs द्रव्य \[One more viz गुण vs जाति is जातिविरोध (2) \]

   Two क्रियाविरोधऽ are possible (1) क्रिया vs गुण, (2) क्रिया vs द्रव्य \[Two more viz क्रिया vs जाति is जातिविरोध (3) क्रिया vs गुण is गुणविरोध (2) \]

  One द्रव्यविरोध is possible द्रव्य vs द्रव्य \[Three more द्रव्य vs जाति is जातिविरोध (4) द्रव्य vs गुण is गुणविरोध (3) द्रव्य vs क्रिया vs क्रिया विरोध (2)\] The terms जातिविरोध, गुणविरोध क्रियाविरोध and द्रव्यविरोध here respectively connote a विरोध in which one of the विरुद्ध things is जाति गुण or द्रव्य इति ते दश— ने i e विरोधा दश 4+3+2+1 = 10

  जाति गुण क्रिया and द्रव्य is the fourfold सकेत of शब्द as admitted by the वैयाकरणऽ See उल्लास II for सकेत रुद्रट was the first to adopt this principle of division for विरोध भामह and उद्भट only talked of गुण and क्रिया

  ** St 91** अभिनव etc अभिनव etc Illustrates जाति नानि विरोध A दृती intimates to a नायक how the नायिका has been so stricken with the grief of separation that soothing remedies like the lotus leaves only tend to make her agony more acute Construe सभा विधिवान स्ववियोगपविपाते अस्या कुरङ्गदृश वलयानि दवलहनराशि भवति । पविपाते when there has fallen on her the bolt (पवि) of separation Dissolve नलिनीकिसलयश्च मृणालवलयानि च। दषदहन = दावानल (द्युनानि पीडयतीति तव)

  Here the विरोधाभास hes in नलिनीकिसलयश्च and मृणालवलयानि being लवदहन How can tender sprouts and fibre-coils become दावानल ask ourselves. The explanation (समाधान) lies in the fact that due to विरहवेलना soothing remedies aggravate the agony Since किसलय and वलयानि which represent particular जातिऽ stand here in antithesis to लवदहन also a जाति we have जाति जाति विरोध Note that there is implicat the idea of अपि in मृणालवलयानि (अपि) लवदहनराशि भवति।

   St. 92 गिरयोऽपि etc Illustrates जाति गुणविरोध A compliment paid to a king s eminence agility depth and forbearance तव........beside you as compared to you To be taken with गिग्य मरुत्, अब्धि and विश्वभरा लव अतिके गिरयाऽपि अनुप्रतियुक्त and so on विश्वभरा अपि अति लघु Compliment to the forbearance of the king Earth is generally taken as representing the height of forbearance.

  Here the विरोधाभास consists in associating गिरिऽ with अनुन्नलत्व, मरुत् with अचलत्व अग्धि with अ...........and विश्वभरा with अति........., It can be

explained away as due to the extraordinary eminence, agility. depth and forbearance of the king before whom these things are dwarfed into insignificance

   As गिरिऽ, मरुत्, अब्धि and विश्वभरा which are जातिऽ here stand in antithesis to गुणऽ viz. अनुन्नतत्व, अचलत्व, अगम्भीरत्व and अनिलघुत्व, we have जाति-गुण विरोध The antithesis is expressly conveyed by the word अपि used with all the four Is विश्वभरा (Earth) जातिवाचक ? Strictly speaking it is not जातिवाचक as earth is one only and जाति presupposes individuals more than one Nor can it be taken as द्रव्य, as प्रभा does For द्रव्य, as the वैयाकरणऽ conceived it, was सशा , proper noun The difficulty could be possibly removed by taking जाति conveying the notion implicit in the Common Noun of Grammar and none can deny that 'Earth', though one, is still a common noun To that extent, the word may be taken as जातिवाचक Some think of a class of विश्वभराऽ as due to there being an earth in every सर्ग and सर्गऽ being infinite in number At any rate वसुधरा cannot be द्रव्य which term the वैयाकारणऽ apply strictly to proper nouns Is the difficulty due to our judging वैयाकरण terminology by नैयायिक standards ?

   **St 93 येषा **etc. Illustrates जाति क्रिया विरोध The poet eulogises the valour of a king, thanks to which the hostile heroes suffer decapitation and are covered with dust. Construe येषां कण्ठपरिग्रह प्रणयिता समाप्य स धाराधर तीक्ष्ण अपि अनुरज्यते, कमपि च स्नेहं पराप्रति, तेषां सगरसङ्गमक्तमनसा राक्षां प्रसाधनविधि त्वया वासना पटले नियेने इति कौतुकम्। धाराधर sword. कण्ठपरिग्रहप्रणायिता प्राप्य Literally when it develops a liking (प्रणय) for the embrace of the necks of the enemies. Euphuistic expression fo decapitation of the enemy अनुरज्यते becomes red (with blood) स्नेह पराप्नोति becomes clammy (with blood) पासूनां पटलै प्रसाधनविधि The enemies are covered over with dust as they fall प्रसाधन Decoration

   Here the विरोधभास is between राज्ञां and पासूना पटलै प्रसाधनं, as सा चूडामणि understands The जाति of kings stands in antithesis to the क्रिया of dust toilette. The explanation lies in the fact that due to the prowess of the king they are covered over with the dust of the battle fickl Some commentators like माणिश्यर्थद्र see the विरोध between ........ and......... Some others think that the विरोध is between the जगति, धराधर and the क्रियाऽ,......and........of all ................ seems the best appreciation Note that there is गुण क्रिया विरोध

 ** St 94** सृजति च etc Illustrates जाति-द्रव्य विरोध The eminent विष्णु has to become a fish (मत्स्यावतार) by force of circumstances Construe य नियत हेल्यैव इद नगद सृजति अवनि सहरति च सोऽपि जनादन अवमरवशत शफर (अमृत्) इद चित्रम्। हेलयैव=लील्यैव अवसखशत' When the earth was submerged under water, मनु's ark with the creatures aboard was tied to the horn of this मत्स्य Thus the living creation could be saved from the प्रलय शफर fish

   Here जनादन which is मश or द्रव्य stands in antithesis to शफर, a जाति The explanation lies in the miracle worked by विष्णु

   **St 95** सतत मुसलासक्ता etc lilustrates गुण-गुण विरोध Description of the change made in the life of the wives of poor Brahmins by a king, a patron of learning coming into power सति भवति when you are there (on the throne) Construe— बहुतरगृहकमघ......सतत ............ (अत एव) कठिना करा

   Here there is विरोधाभास between the गुणऽ कठिनत्व and मुकुप्रषत्व The explanation lies in the fact that the women were spared the hard work of the household due to fortune smiling on them as a result of the kings munificence

 ** St 96** पेशलमपि etc lllustrates गुण-क्रिया विरोध The delicate words of a villain touch to the quick The harsh words of the good prove soothing सतत्वविदां मानस object of ...........and प्रभालयति सतायविदो तत्त्ववि. \[सदत्त्व तत्त्वम् इति च पर्याय- उद्योत ....... 

  Here there is विरोधाभास between the गुण पशलत्व and the क्रिया दहन and again between the गुण परुषत्व and the क्रिया प्रभेद the explanation is to be sought in the fact that the words of the wicked are mischievous and those of the good salutary

  St 97 त्रौद्याद्रिetc. Illustrates गुण-द्रव्य विरोध The well known miracle wrought by परशुराम when he pierced mount क्रोध with arrow Construe मय स ............ .... यत् ...................................(तस्य) ............. .......... अभूत्। मार्गणानर्गलशातपाते At the unstemmed (मार्गात्) and sharp (शति) shower of his arrows. ................ Solid with massive (उद्भट) boulders. अपूर्वसर्ग Creating umprecedented things.

   Here the विरोधभास lies between the द्रव्य (सद्वा) ऋषद्रि and the ..................The explanation is to be found in the Herculean strength of परशुराम

  St 98 परिच्छेदातीत etc Illustrates क्रिया क्रिया विरोध माधव narrating the state of his mind to मकरन्द परिच्छेदातीत (विकार) A feeling that is beyond (अतीत) measure b Construe य अस्मिन् नमनि पुन अनुभवपथ न गतवान्. Add in this life after repeated itself in the Translation विवेक etc i e the power of discernment is lost and in consequence profound infatuation has set in अन्त जडयति Makes me chill at heart

  Here the विराधाभास is between the क्रियाऽ नन्यति— नाप कुरुते जडयति has two meanings (1) नलयति cools down (2) paralyses a also has two meanings (1) Makes hot (2) torments How can a far which cools one down make him hot we ask And we are satisfied when we recall the other meaning of each of the two words It is common experience that a thing which paralyses also torments So what is felt as f in the light of the first meaning of each of the two जडयति and ताप कुमने proves to be mere विरोधाभास when we recall the other meaning

  St 99 अय वारामेको etc Illustrates क्रिया - द्रव्य विरोध The legend of ama who gulped down the vast ocean in the space of a moment तृष्णातरलितमनोभि with our minds disconcerted by thurst for water and also for the jewels ताम्यत्तिमिमक्रम् (उ) with the sharks (तिमि ) and crocodiles in agony (as their native element was being gulped down by the sage ) मुनि अगस्त्य

  Here there is far between (the meaning of ) a It is explained as Note that has ( - सज्ञा ) and the क्रिया of quaffing the ocean being due to the supreme eminence of an to be taken in the sense of अगस्त्य and then it becomes a द्रव्य or सा Some like h being one is take the विरोध to be between जननिधि and पनविया जलनिधि they argue That this view about is not correct we have seen at St 92 while discussing whether Note that this stanza could be also an द्रव्य being मनमरि अस्थिरत्वम्

  St 100 द्वयद्रयविरोध समदमतगज etc. Illustrates द्रव्य विरोध Ganges becomes dark like the Jumna owing to the ichor of a king s elephants being washed therein. स्थितितिलक addressed to the king The king was camping on the bank of the Ganges. शकरचूडापगा The nver ( आएगा ) on the head of free गङ्गा समद etc ये ...... यत् मदजल, तस्य च निष्यन्द .... या तरङ्गित (river) ............ (contact)

 Here the विरोधाभास lies between the द्रव्यऽ गङ्गा and यमुना (कालिन्दी) How can गङ्गा become यमुना, we ask ourselves, The explanation lies in the fact that the flow of black ichor was copious enough to turn the white current of the Ganges black like that of the Jumna

**२५— स्वभावोक्ति**

   स्वभावोक्ति means literally the description of some trait of an object, and the figure consists in the faithful portrayal of some object bringing into prominence traits that are charming enough in the poet's eye The poet aims at giving us a vivid pen picture, maybe, of some landscape, sun rise sun-set, some animal while it is stationary or in action, the innocent activities of a child, the amorous acts of a romantic maiden, and such other themes. What the artist does with the brush, the poet succeeds in achieving with the pen.

  Realism, not idealism, is the keynote of the figure. The poet's attention is arrested by some striking fact in nature and he sets about describing it as it is, without the slightest touch of idealism. That way, in स्वभावोक्ति we get the measure of the poet's power of observation कालिदास's graphic description of the कृष्णमृग in ग्रीवासङ्गाभिराम etc (शाकुन्तला १ ७), his portrayal of the actions of सर्वदमन who is yet a small boy (शाकुन्तला ७), भवभूति's description of the horse as put in the mouth of the unsophisticated वटु (उत्तररामचरित ४ २६) पश्चात् पुच्छ वहति etc., his description of when she was yet a small gırl in .............................. etc (उ रा १ २० ), बाण's description of the horse इन्द्रायुध and the elephant गन्धमादन at the palace ( राजकुल ) of तारापीड, his description of the चाण्डाल, the parrot, जाबालि the sage , the शाल्मलि् the evenings and mornings—these are some of the best specimens of स्वभावोक्ति.

  Is स्वभावोक्ति या अलङ्कार ? – Since in स्वभावोक्ति, there is only a realistic delineation of objects, some अलन्कारिकाare loth to concede to it the status of an अलकार भामह् does not seem to be very enthusiastic about its claim to be an आलङ्कारिका's, to judge from his words इति केचित् प्रचक्षते \[ स्वभवोक्तिरलङ्कार इति केचित् प्रचक्षते\] if this evidence is not concluive, one has only to see भामह's attitude towards 77, another figure closely akin to mirt Says He भामह — .............. ......... ....पक्षिणः। ................... प्रचक्षते॥ (२८७) In short, भामहऽ, a sponsor of वक्रोक्ति (............. विना) appears reluctant to admit , ....... स्वभावोक्ति. based on realism as .......... दण्डिन्'s one is apologetic when he talks of this figure ..................  


काव्येष्वप्येनदीप्सिनम् ।— says he He means in effect Though the proper sphere of realism is science it may play part in poetry also This implies that दण्डिन् also felt that स्वभावोक्ति due to its realism was not in tune with the rest of the figures वामन did not admit the figure जगन्नाथ does not refer to it but this may be due to us work र ग, having come to an abrupt end That he appreciated स्वभावोक्ति could be inferred from his remarks in his criticism of सा द's defintion of काव्य— वस्त्वलकारप्रधानाना काव्यानामकाव्यत्वापत्ते। न चेष्टापत्ति। महाकवि सप्रदायस्याकुलीभावप्रसगात्। तथा च जलप्रवाहवेगनिपतनात्पतनभ्रमणानि कविभिवार्णतानि। क्वपिवालनि विलसितानि च।

  As against these those that admit स्वभावोक्ति as a figure justify it by pointing out that though the figure is based on realism yet none but a poet could have effected it The matter delineated is सूक्ष्म which रुय्यक explains as कवित्वमात्रस्य गम्य It requires a poets genius to choose one out of the thousand things that exist or occur in the world outside It requires a poet s observation to observe it in every detail small or big And further it requires the poets power of expression to present the object with perfect vividness Read **रुय्यक**— इह वस्तुस्वभाववर्णनमात्र नालकार। तत्त्वे सति सर्वे चाव्यमलकार स्यात्। तदर्थं सूक्ष्मग्रहणम्। सूक्ष्म कवित्वमात्रम्य गम्य **सा द** also describes the matter as दुरूह wuch it explans as कविमात्रवेय Read **व्यत्तिविवेक** cited by साहित्यचूडामणि— कथ तर्हि स्वभावोक्तेरलकारत्वमिष्यते नहि स्वभावमात्रोक्तौ विशेष कश्चनानयो॥उच्यते वस्तुनस्तावद् दैरूप्यमिह विद्यते। तत्रैकमस्य सामान्य यद् विकल्पैक्गोचर। स एव मत्कविगिरा गोचर प्रतिभाभुवाम्। रसानुगुणशब्दार्थचिन्तास्तिमितचेतम। क्षण स्वरूप स्पर्शोथा प्रज्ञैव प्रतिभा कवे ॥

  स्वभावोक्ति to judge from भामह's wording is more ancient than भामह भामह defines and illustrates स्वभावोक्ति but appears to have no defines it and refers to its other name जाति He gives instances based on the fourfold significance of world जाति गुण क्रिया and द्रव्य उद्भट admits the figure वामन ts silent admits it रुद्रट admits it under the name जाति रुय्यक विश्वनाथ अप्पय्यदीक्षीत् (कु) admit the figure In the extant portion of the र ग the figure is not to be found

   **Essentials**— \[अथस्य ...स्थत्वं स्वभाव तस्य इति ।— **भामह\]** (i)There must be a faithful representation of a matter like a landscape an animal at rest or in action a child etc. (ii) The representation may relate to the complexion d position of limbs etc which must be characteristic of the object (iii) The theme must be poetically

striking (iv) The description must be graphic and bear testimony to the poets power of minute observation It must be detailed enough to be a Pen Picture Read व्यक्तिविवेक— अर्थस्वभावस्याक्तियां सालङ्कारतया मता । यत साश्रादिवाभान्ति तत्रार्था प्रतिभाषिता॥

  § सू ४४ स्वभावोक्तिस्तु etc तु marks transition from the earlier figure to the new one डिम्बादे of child and such other objects comprehends such objects as animals inanimate things etc रुद्रट attempts to make a list of some of these शिशु-मुग्धयुवति-कातर-सङ्घ्रान्त-हीनपात्राणाम्। सा वालावस्थोचि.... विशेषता रम्या॥ (हीनपात्र=abhor rent characters like ghosts) Note that उद्भट has मृगडिम्बाद in his definition and भामह's instance of the figure गा वारयति दण्डन डिम्भ.... Not unlikely, therefore, that's definition was influenced by भामह's instance, and मम्मटऽ reference to डिम्ब seems to have been induced by उद्भटऽ definition child has always been a source of abiding interest to the poet स्वकीया-रुपवर्णनं description of an action or feature (रुप) that are peculiar (स्व) to the object. The action etc. must be charactenstic of the object. This is what makes the figure striking It gives us the measure of the poet’s power of observation.क्रिया action The g in the स्व casts suspicious glances at the chanot contracts his limbs and skips in the air रुप Feature Comprehends as the वृत्ति says a ward स्व सन्धानं च वर्ग Complexion hue सन्न्धान posture of the limbs क्रियावर्णन gives us a verbal study of an object in action, it gives one of an रुपवर्गन object at rest.

सप्रदायप्रकाशिनी aims at giving the divisions comprehensively, 7 in number —क्रिया वर्ण संस्थानाना व्यस्तत्वे त्रय, किया वर्गयो, क्रिया-सस्थानयोः, वग सस्थानयोः, त्रयाणां च समस्तत्वन चत्वार इति सप्त अस्य भेदा। **दण्डिन्** gives divisions based on the fourfold समेत of शब्द VIZ नाति, गुण, क्रिया and द्रव्य

  St 101 qara etc पश्चादमी Illustrates स्वभावोक्ति The characteristic pose and action of a horse just awakened from sleep Construe शयनात् उत्थित तुरङ्ग —(१) पश्चादमी प्रसार्य — (२) त्रिकननिविनत अङ्ग उच्चै द्राघयित्वा (३) आभुग्नक्ण्ठो मुख उरति आमज्य — (४) धूलिधूमा भग विधूय —(५) घासप्रामामिलापात् अनवरतचल्योधतुण्ड (६) मन्द शब्दायमान—(७) क्ष्मा खुरेण लिखति। पञ्चादमी The hind legs. Bertandad (अङ्ग)—The body which appears extensive owing to the arch of the त्रिका i e spine त्रिक (क) spine or hips or loins उच्चै द्राघयित्वा ( Drawing out a good deal Add 'a good deal' after drawing out in the Translation. आभुघ्न bent प्रोथ(ध-थ) Nostrils.

   Here १-७ give us क्रियाऽ of the horse ४ (धूलिध्रूमा) gives us वर्ण सम्स्थानत् if interpreted as a pose' is illustrated by all except ६ संस्थान in the sense of 'configuration' is present in ........... अङ्ग All these details are characteristic of a horse and none but a poet could have observed and delineated them That is why we appreciate the stanza as a vivid pen picture a स्वभावोक्ति

**२६ - व्याजस्तुति**

  व्याजस्तुति was placed by उद्भट in the same group as विरोध and it is not unlikely that's treatment of the figure at this place might have been influenced by that grouping For reasons unknown and perhaps arbitrarily, he interposed स्वभावोक्ति that उद्भट had placed in a different group between विरोध and व्याजस्तुति

  In व्याजस्तुति, the poet s aim is either to praise an object or to dendu it. But the device he employs to effect it is ingenious. In order to make the स्तुति or निन्दा more striking the poet adopts an ironical mode of expression. When he aims at praise, he puts it in the setting of censure And when he wants to censure he gives it the garb of praise. The poet wants to praise the Ganges that is so sacred that at redeems even the sinners. He says How indiscreet you are O divine river that you lift the sinner unto heaven!—...................... The नि.... wants to rebuke the gh that was

treacherous enough to dally with the नायक She says Well done! O go between, well done There could not be any higher service than what you have done me How, for my sake, you got mauled with teeth and nails - साधु दूति पुन साधु कर्तव्य किमत परम्। यन्मदर्थे विल्नाभि ......... ॥(कु.) In both cases we find the poet resorting to irony. In the former case, it is sympathetic or appreciatory, in the latter, satiric or denunciatory. In one case, it is Praise by irony, in the other. Ironical Praise व्यानेन स्तुति, व्यानरूपा वा स्तुति— as the आलङ्कारिक's explain the name In the former, we have स्तुतिपर्यवमायिनी निन्दा, in the latter, निन्द्रापर्यवसायिनी स्तुति्

  In the early stages of its growth, व्याजस्तुति was designed to be onl व्याजेन स्तुति ्( निन्दा ) व्याजेन स्तुति, praise in the guise of censure a figure purely eulogistic in purpose. भामह, दण्टिन्, उद्भट and वामन conceived it as such was the first to think of the extension of निन्द्रापर्यवसायिनी स्तुति (cf. यस्मिन्निन्दा सुनिनो निन्दाया वा स्तुति प्रतीयेन।) His name for the figure was व्याजशेष, a variety of अर्थशेष, according to hum रुद्रट was followed by मम्मट, रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाय, अप्पय्यदीक्षित all under standing व्याजस्तुति as स्तुतिपर्यवसायिनी निन्दा and निन्द्रापर्यंवमायिनी स्तुति अप्पय्यदीक्षित wants to make a departure from tradition in his divisions of the figure According to him, the figure is present not only where स्तुति or निन्दा amount to निन्दा or स्तुति of the same party, ( निन्दास्तुत्योरेकविषयत्वे ), but also where of one results into of another, or fara of one results into of another, or where ft of one results into g of another, (मित्रविपयले ) ( Where निन्दा of one results into निन्दा of another, he has a separate figure व्याजनिन्दा, as it could not come under व्यापस्तुति ) man passes severe strictures on this departure on the part of the द्रविपुङ्गव (अप्पय्यदीक्षित ) and says - श्य च व्यानस्तुतिर्यस्यैव वस्तुन' स्तुतिनिन्दे प्रथममुपक्रम्येने तस्यैव चेत्रिन्दास्तुत्यो पर्यवमान भवेत्तदा भवति। वैयधिरण्ये तु न इति प्राचामत्कारशास्त्र प्रवर्तकानां समय तथा अन्यस्य स्तुत्या अन्यस्तुती अन्यनिन्दया वा अन्य निन्दायां गम्य मानाया नास्या अकृविषय \। पूर्वोत्तादेव हो। जगन्नाथ's criticism is justifiable, not only because it upholds tradition, but also because the extensions of the figure as proposed by are most apt to shade off into other figures like अप्रस्तुतप्रशमा In fact, one instance given by बुबय्यानद as one of व्याजस्तुति was looked upon as अप्रस्तुतनमा by दटिन् \[See कुवलयानन्द- व्याजस्तुति }

  व्याजस्तुति vs अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा— Since in वस्तुतप्रशंसा, there is the rention of one correlate like कार्य, सामान्य etc, culminating in the suggestion of the other, the temptation is too strong to look upon  
also as coming within the purview of that figure, because in, we get

स्तुति culminating in निन्दा and vice versa And to follow the trend of the arguments off, it seems that they looked upon it as differing from अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा only because it is confined to स्तुति - निन्दा  
Read रुय्यक, for instance स्तुतिनिन्दारूपत्वम्य विच्छित्तिविशेषस्य भावादप्रस्तुतप्रशसानी भेद। जयरथ's comments are एव स्तुतिनिन्दास्यामप्रस्तुताभ्या निन्दास्तुत्यो प्रस्तुत योर्गम्यत्वमित्यत्र सिद्धम्। यद्येव तत्विमियमप्रस्तुनप्रशसैव न भवतीत्याशङ्कय आह- स्तुतीत्यादि। तत्र हि सामान्यविशेषादीना गम्यत्वमुक्तम्। Again read कुवलयानन्द इयमेव अप्रस्तुतप्रशसा न कार्यकारणनिव धनेति दण्डी । वस्तुतस्तु अत्र व्यानस्तुतिरित्येव युक्त, स्तुत्या निन्दाभिव्यक्तिरित्य प्रस्तुतप्रशमानी वैचियविशेषसद्भावात्। अन्यथा प्रसिद्धव्यानस्तुत्युदाहरणेष्वपि अप्रस्तुताभ्या निन्दा स्तुतिभ्या प्रस्तुने स्तुतिनिन्दे गम्येते इत्येतावना व्यानस्तुतिमात्रमप्रस्तुनप्रशमा स्यात्।

  But, on a closer examination, it would be found that the difference between the two figures is more fundamental than that To say that in व्याजस्तुति, the वाच्य स्तुति or निन्दा is अप्रस्तुत and that it suggests the प्रस्तुत is not very correct The वाच्य, स्तुति निन्दा and the गम्य, निन्दास्तुनि are If स्तुति is given in the form of निन्दा, we are It is not related as अप्रस्तुत प्रस्तुत not justified in looking upon one as and the other as merely a difference in form, much the same as we find in पर्यायोक्ति ( Fig 32 ) To say that व स्वर्धुनि विवेक्ते पापिनो नयसे दिवम्। 15 अप्रस्तुत and that गङ्गाया महिमन् is प्रस्तुत is inaccurate Both निन्दा and स्तुति are here equally प्रस्तुन, since what is स्तुति in essence is put in the form of निन्दा How can thus be on a par with कार्यकारण सामान्य विशेष, where the two correlates are distinct enough to be अप्रस्तुत प्रस्तुत For व्याजस्तुति us पर्यायोक्त see पर्यायोक्त

Note the following scheme—

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1710865958Screenshot2024-03-19220201.png"/>

  जयरथ is inclined to look upon these two not as sub-divisions of one figure but tno independent व्याजस्तुतिऽ. Says he द्वे ण्वात्र व्याजस्तुती न पुनरेकैव द्विविधा व्याजस्तुतिरिति सूचितम्। प्रकारप्रकारिभावी हि सामान्यलक्षणमद्भावे न भवति॥His viewpoint is scientific no doubt, but it sets aside the conventional divisions As we said above, कुवलयानन्द admits 3 additional varieties. (1) अन्यस्य निन्दाया अन्यस्य स्तुती पर्यवमानम्, ( 2 ) अन्यस्य स्तुत्या अन्यस्य निन्दाया पर्यवमानम्, and ( 3 ) अन्यस्य स्तुत्या अन्यस्य स्तुतौ पर्यवसानम् \[ अन्यस्य निन्दया अन्यस्य निन्दाया पर्यवसान has been taken as a separate figure by hum\]. We saw how जगन्नाथ rules these out as being against आलङ्कारिक.....

** Essentials**— \[व्याजेन स्तुति, व्याजरूपा वा स्तुति— मम्मट रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ जगन्नाथ also is for this explanation of the name– तृतीयातत्पुरुष कर्मधारयाभ्या योगार्थद्वयेन द्वयोरपि शब्दार्थत्वम्\] (1) There must be the denunciation or appreciation of an object (2) This denunciation or appreciation must be only apparent or simulated and must at once culminate in appreciation or denunciation respectively i e निन्दा must be स्तुतिपर्यवसायिनी and स्तुति निन्द्रापर्यवासायिनी (3) That the निन्दा of स्तुति is only superfinal can be gathered by its being बाधित for want of योग्यता Whoever will condemn गङ्गा ? And we at once. see the स्तुति cloaked as निन्दा in क. स्तुति etc रुय्यक— यत्र स्तुतिरभिधीयमानापि प्रमाणान्तराद् बाधितरवरूपा निन्दाया पर्यवस्यति यत्रापि निन्दाशब्देन प्रतिपद्यमाना पूर्ववद् बाधितरूपा स्तुति पर्यवसिता भवति सा व्यानस्तुति। (4) That there is no योग्यता for स्तुति or निन्दा can be gathered from context the speaker, the party addressed etc Read जयरथ—प्रमणान्तरात् (इति) वक्तृ-वाच्य प्रकरणादियालीनामन (5) The निन्दा or स्तुति must be transparent and one must be able to see through it the underlying स्तुति or निन्दा (6) Because it is transparent, it is less subtle than ध्वनि, while in सुहृदयमात्रगम्य ध्वनि is felt without वाच्यार्थदाथ while in व्याचस्तुति we have वाच्यार्थबाथ Cp र ग— ध्वनौ हि निर्वाधेन वाच्येन आगूरणमहिम्ना अर्थान्तरमवगम्यते। न चैव प्रकृते।  

   **§ सू** **ध्यानस्तुतिर्मुखे** etc Construe (यत्र) मुखे निन्दा स्तुतिर्वा रूढि (च) अन्यथा (भवति सा) व्याजस्तुति। रूढि=पर्यवसानम्, पार्यन्निकी प्रतिष्ठा— सा चू अन्यथा i e निन्दा becomes स्तुतिपर्यवसायिनी and स्तुति निन्दापर्यवसायिनी In short, in व्याजोक्ति what appears as निन्दा on the surface culminates in स्तुति and vice versa As seen already, one results into the other owing to the वाच्यार्थ being बाधित for want of योग्यता रुय्यक and after him मा चं supposed that it is by विपरीतलक्षणा, that स्तुति becomes निन्दापर्यवसायिनी and निन्दा स्तुतिपर्यवसायिनी The definition which seems somewhat laboured is entirely of the making of मम्मट No predecessor of his has anything like it.

 ** वृत्ति— **व्याजेन व्याजरुपा वा स्तुति। The mss generally read व्याजरूप व्याजेन वा and commentators justify this order, which is against the order in the सूत्र by falling back upon the ..............., according to which, ............... gets the precedence व्याजरूपा स्तुति is the विग्रह of व्याजस्तुति taken as a ................., and is hence given first they say We have preferred the reading व्याजेन व्याजरूपा वा because it agrees with the order of the उदाहरणऽ of which the first is of व्याजेन स्तुति, and the second of व्याजरूपा स्तुति

  व्याजेन व्याजरूपा वा स्तुति The name explained so as to be applicable to the two divisions व्याजेन स्तुति is निन्दाव्याजेन स्तुति— where we have स्तुति given in the guise (व्याज) of निन्दा As we saw above भामह, दण्डिन्, उद्भट, वामन, thought of व्याजस्तुति only as व्याजेन स्तुति i e स्तुतिनिपर्यवसायिनी निन्दा व्याज is guise cloak, appearance

  व्याजरूपा स्तुति is निन्दापर्यवसायिनी स्तुति, whatever is स्तुति in appearance but boils down to निन्दा Here the compound is dissolved as a कर्मधारयव्याजश्वासौ स्तुतिश्व This was a later development, रुद्रट possibly being its originator जयरथ as we saw above, looks upon this as a second and independent व्याजस्तुति Evidently he is reluctant to look upon these varieties as one figure as they are so opposed in nature, one being स्तुतिपर्यवसायिनी and the other निन्द्रापर्यवसायिनी

  **माणिक्यचन्द्र **adopts the reading व्याजरूपा व्याजेन वा and explains व्याजरूपा as निन्दाद्वारिका and व्याजेन as वस्तुवृत्त्या (1) निन्दैवेयर्थ Obviously incorrect The order of उदाहरणऽ has musled मा च

  क्रमेण उदाहरणं क्रमेण only if we read व्याजेन व्याजरूपा वा With the other reading व्याजरूपा व्याजेन वा, it becomes ...... Or is it the क्रम in the सूत्र where मुखे निन्दा stands first and मुखे स्तुति second that the वृत्ति has got in view?

  ** St 102. हित्वा त्वा** etc Illustrates स्तुतिपर्यवसायिनी निन्दा The generosity of a king put in the form of निन्दा, to wit The king renounces लक्ष्मी and yet लक्ष्मी comes to him a Construe— त्व हित्वा पर उपरोधवध्यमनसा मौलिक न। मौलिक foremost त्वा हित्वा except yourself उपरोधेन (अनुकल्पया) वध्य (शून्य) मन यषा whose mind is void of pity Thus a means You are the most heartless person going b means none else is so shameless as रमा i e लक्ष्मी cd Explain the allegations contained in ab c you renounce wealth that comes to you by hundreds of channels d And yet Wealth resorts to you. even though you insult her by renouncing her मुखशतै एम्य आश्रिता coming by hundreds of ways and taking resort मुखशत refers to the numerous sources of revenue

  Here the king is described as heartless in his treatment of लक्ष्मी and लक्ष्मी is described as shameless in ber madness for the king This is निन्दा in appearance and results in स्तुति as it drives at the generosity of the king who is free with hus purse Thus it is ........... स्तुति or, more explicitly, निन्दाव्याजने स्तुति

  St. 103 हे ................... etc. Illustrates निन्दापर्यवसायिनी स्तुति A lampoon on the Ocean that shares with the desert the odium for

failing to quench the thirst of the thirsty traveller हलया (easily) जित बोधिसत्व (or त्वा) येन Adj of तोयधे बोधिसत्व (बोधि बोधवत् सत्व यस्य) of enlightened— intelligence— A Buddhist saint that has approximated to a Supreme Buddha and has to pass through one birth only before he becomes one Typifies the highest degree of दया The Ocean by its 'charity' has eclipsed even the बाधिसत्व— the charity as given in cd b Construe— परहिताधाने गृहीतव्रत त्वसदृश पर नास्ति परहिताधान Doing good to others cd तृष्यन्त ये ............. तेषाम् उपकार, तस्य घटना तस्या बैमुर....., तेन लब्ध अयश तस्य भार, तस्य प्राद्वहन The मरु or desert has to bear the burden of the odium due to its aversion (वैमुख्य) to do (घटना) an obligation to the thirsty traveller It fails to quench his thirst and has therefore become infamous कृपया भारप्रोद्वहने साहायक करोषि Out of pity you share the burden of infamy with the मरु or desert i e the Ocean also is infamous like the desert

  Here there is an apparent praise of the Ocean to the effect that it obliges the Desert by sharing with it the burden of infamy This praise boils down to denunciation viz the ocean is as infamous as the desert, as both fail to quench the thirst of the thirsty Thus this is व्याजरूपा स्तुति, or more explicitly निन्दापर्यवसायिनी स्तुति

  This cannot be taken as अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, since it does not suggest anything else as प्रस्तुत What could the behaviour of the ocean typify? मा च is therefore not justified in seeing अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा here (अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसासद्भावेऽपि says he) It is व्याजस्तुति pure and simple

**२७— सहोक्ति**

   सहोक्ति was placed by उद्भट in the same group as
व्याजस्तुति and possibly for that reason मम्मट deals with it after व्याजस्तुति Though some आलङ्कारिकऽ like रुय्यक and विश्वनाथ view the figure as of wider implications comprehending not only गम्य औपम्य but also कार्यकारणसाहित्य, the figure as conceived by a majority of them is strictly confined to गम्य औपम्य That way it is akin to दीपका-तुल्ययोगिता

   If, in दीपक-तुल्ययोगिता the poet drives at औपप्य between two धर्मिन्ऽ by mentioning the धर्म once only in direct relation to both in सहोक्ति he achieves similar result by mentioning the धर्म once only. but owing to the use of सह् or its equivalent the relation is direct in the case of one धर्मिन् and indirect in the case of the other In ....... ........ ... given by कुवलयानन्द to illustrate तुल्ययोगिता, the धर्मिन्ऽ .... and स्वैरिणीवदनानि are both directly connected with

सकुचन्ति. But were it to be put as सरोतानि स्वैरिणीवदनै सह सकुवन्ति, It would become a सहोक्ति, तरोजानि being directly connected with सकुचन्ति, and स्वैरिणीवदनानि indirectly, owing to the use of सह and the Instrumental This is no more than a difference of form The upshot of both is the same viz, औपम्य Read र ग— एषूद्राहरणेषु सहयोगनृतीयाप्रयुक्तो गुणप्रधानभाव। प्राधायेन क्रियान्वये तु यथायथ तुल्ययोगिता दीपक वा भवति।

 ** सहोत्ति** or **तुल्ययोगिता**— Though the figure is thus in a class with दीपक-तुल्ययोगिता, it is more closely related to तुल्ययोगिता than to दीपक This is due to the fact that in दीपक the धर्मिन्’s are प्रकृत and अप्रकृत both while in सहोक्ति we have only प्रकृत धर्मिन्’s That way तुल्ययोगिता of the first variety where all the धर्मिन्ऽ are प्रकृत is more akin to सहोक्ति And, as seen above in सकुवन्ति सरोतानि etc, one can be easily reduced to the other This is due to both being similar in essence, though they are divergent in form And the divergence is due to the use of सह (or its equivalent) in sal which results, as seen above, in making the धर्मिधर्मिसबन्ध direct in the case of one धर्मिन् and indirect in the case of the other In तुल्ययोगिता, on the other hand the धर्मिधर्मिसबन्ध is direct for both धर्मिन्’s

   As we can have a तुल्ययोगिता of शुद्ध अप्रकृत धमिन्ऽ, can we not have a सहोक्ति of all अप्रकृत’s ? Probably not अप्रकृतविषयक तुल्ययोगिता, as we saw, amounts to no more than उपमानविषयक तुल्ययोगिता , where some statement is made in common to all the was in their relation to the उपमेय As the उपमानऽ alone feature therein, and the stands outside the pale of the figure, अप्रकृतविषयक तुल्ययोगिता does not suggest genuine औपम्य and therefore stands on a different footing from सहोक्ति, where there is a suggestion of genuine औपम्य Let us take the instance त्वदगमार्दवे etc. given to illustrate अप्रस्तुतविषयक तुल्ययोगिता We may be able to put it in सहोक्ति form त्वदगमार्दवे दृष्ट कम्य चित्ते न भासते। मालती शशिलेखाम्या (सह) रम्भाया सुकलोरता, but that is no poetical सहोक्ति, as here सह only tends to bring the things together There is no room for So its more important function of औपम्यावगति So when रुय्यक says द्वयो प्राकरणित्वाद् अप्राकरणिकत्वाद् वा सहार्थमामर्थ्यादि तयो तुल्यवक्षत्वम्, his reference to द्वयो अप्राकरणिकत्वात् appears not quite correct Note that भामह,दण्डिन्, उद्भट, वामन clearly indicate that the धर्मिन्ऽ are प्रकृत in सहोक्ति when they all refer to तुल्यकालत्व of the क्रियाऽ in सहोक्ति

  If both the धर्मिन्ऽ in सहोक्ति are प्रकृत , which is the उपमान and which उपमेय? To a casual observer, the धर्मिन् in the Instrumental may seem to be उपमान, and that in the nominative उपमेय, nominative

showing that the धर्मिन् is प्रस्तुत But on a closer Scrutiny it will be found that, as in तुल्ययोगिता, both the things being प्रकृत, either may be taken as उपमान or उपमेय In other words, their औपम्य is optional The धर्मिन् in the instrumental, though appeanng as उपमान may be उपमेय and that in the nominative though appearing as उपमेय may be in fact उपमान Read रुय्यक— उपमानोपमेयत्व चात्र वैवक्षिकम्। तत्र तृनायान्नस्य गुणत्वादुपमानत्वम्। अर्थाच्च परिशिष्टस्य प्रधानत्वादुपमेयत्वम्। शाब्दश्चात्र गुणप्रधानभाव। वस्तुतस्तु विपर्ययोऽपि स्यात्। Thus in सकुवन्ति सरोतानि स्वैरिणीवदनै सह, though judged grammatically, स्वैरिणीवदऽ appear as उपमान and सरोजऽ as उपमेय's still it may be the other way also स्वैरिणीवदनऽ उपमेय and सरोजऽ उपमान Because both are equally प्रकृत

 ** सहोत्ति and its relation to अतिशयोक्ति** — We have seen that in सहोक्ति the poet by employing सह or its synonym (सहार्थ) associates the same धर्म with two धर्मिन् and thus hints at औपम्य between them In associating the same धर्म with the two धर्मिन्ऽ the poet may give an additional poetical touch, by making the धर्म applicable in one sense to one धर्मिन् and in a different sense to the other Thus in दिगन्तमगमत्तस्य कीर्ति प्रयर्थिभि सह (कु), दिगन्तमगमत् as appied to कीर्ति means spreading to the farthest end of the quarters as applied to प्रत्यर्थिन् (enemies) it means ‘Fleeing to the farthest end’ This means that two दिगन्तगमनऽ are presented as if they were one and the same by the use of सह This amounts to अध्यवसान and therefore thus is अतिशयोक्ति where, for instance the two मुख and चन्द्र, are presented by अध्यवसान as one, चद्रोऽयम्

   Here दिगन्तमगमत् was not श्लिष्ट and yet could apply in two slightly varying senses to the two कीर्ति and प्रत्यर्थिन्ऽ. But the poet may secure an identical effect by making use of a धर्म which is श्लिष्ट. In सहाधरदलेनम्या यौवने रागभाक् प्रिय ( सा द ) रागभाक् means 'red’ as applied to अथरलल and passionate as applied to प्रिय As above, here also we have one राग अध्यवसित् as another and hence अतिशयोक्ति based on श्लेष

  रुय्यक and विश्वनाथ thought of yet another form in whichअतिशयोक्ति may i e at the root of सहोक्ति. If कार्य and कारण are presented as occurring simultareously it becomes अतिशयोक्ति as we saw under अतिशयोक्ति. This variety is easily reduable to सहोक्ति Thus, in समर्थक समाक्रान्त etc., if we alter the second line to a तेन सिहासनं पित्र्य सहलेन (सह) महीभृताम्, it becomes a case of सहोक्ति acc to रुय्यक Here समक्रान्तं applies to सिहासनं and महत्व both due to the use of सह (or its equivalent)

तत्र नियमेन अतिशयोक्तिमूलत्वमस्या। सा च कार्य-कारण प्रतिनियमविपर्ययरूपा अभेदाध्यवसायरूपा च। अभेदाध्यवसायश्च श्लेषभित्तिकोऽन्यथा वा।— रुय्यक

  जगन्नाथ is loth to admut this कार्य-कारणसाहचर्ये सहोक्ति because acc. to him, its charm is not different from that of the corresponding अतिशयोक्ति But this argument would also apply to प्रकृतविषयिका सहोक्ति where the charm is the same as that in तुल्ययोगिता Then why should जगन्नाथ  
recognise प्रकृतविषयिका सहाक्ति if he is reluctant to admit कार्य-कारणसाहचर्ये सहोक्ति ? It would be more logical to admit both

  In the ught of the quotation of रुय्यक (नियमेन अतिशयोक्तिमूलत्व) it would appear that अतिशयोक्ति is essential for सहोक्ति सा द also means as much when he says ’लक्ष्मणेन सह राम कानन ययौ’ इयादौ च अतिशयोक्तिमूलत्वाभावन्नायमलकार But this is not a correct position In सह मणिवल्यैर्बाष्पधारा गलन्ति (St 104) we have no अतिशयोक्ति as गलन्ति applies in the same sense to मणिवलयऽ and बाष्पधाराऽ and still it is striking enough to be सहोक्ति Read रा तर्कवागीश cited below मम्मट to judge from his instance does not insist upon अतिशयोक्तिमूलकत्व His definition is vague on the point Thus we get the following scheme—

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708160613Screenshot2024-02-17143250.png"/>  

   मम्मटऽ definition excludes the second Some आलङ्कारिक's like रुद्रट admit further divisions such as कर्त्रो साहचर्ये कर्मणो साहचर्ये, क्रियाया साधारणधर्मत्व गुणस्य साधारणधमत्वे etc But as they involve no striking principle of division, they may be ignored Note that we can have a सहोक्तिऽ in माला form where round one thing there centre several सहोक्तिऽ Read जगन्नाथ— यत्रैक्रमेवोपमेयं विलक्षणसहोक्तायालम्वन सा मालामादृश्या मालासहोक्ति Thus in उक्षिप्त सह कौशिकस्य पुलकै सार्धं मुखैर्नामित भूपाना जनकस्य सशयधिया साक समालकालितम्। वैदेह्या मनसा सम च सहसाकृष्ट ततो भार्गवप्रौढाकृतिकललेन च सम तद्भग्नमैश धनु॥(अ स) धनु is the centre of several सहोक्तिऽ to wit पुलके सार्धम् उक्षिप्त मुखै सार्धं नामित संशयभिया साक समास्फालित मनसा मम आकृष्ट The सहोक्ति कन्दलेन सम भय has nothing to do with the माला It should be noted that in a मालासहोक्ति we require

several सहोक्तिऽ only If several धर्मिन्ऽ in the instrumental are related to one धर्म only that cannot be माला सहोक्ति. Thus ..र्वधूना सवकाप प्राणैश्च साक त्वया ज्या चकृपे will not be माला but शुद्ध सहोक्ति।

  सहोक्ति is a figure that dates from भामह and दण्डिन् उद्भट वामन, रुद्रट all admitted it. The last speaks of सहोक्ति based on वास्तव and औपम्य. But his instance of वास्तव is clearly one of औपम्य proving how औपम्य alone is the विच्छित्ति of the figure मम्मट रुय्यक विश्वनाथ जगन्नाथ अप्पय्यवीक्षित all admit the figure रुय्यक विश्वनाथ recognise the additional variety based on कार्यकारणसाहचर्य of which जगन्नाथ is critical

  **Essentials**— \[सह उक्तिः सहोक्ति— नमिसाधु or सहार्थेन उक्ति, or सह (भावस्य) उक्ति— हेमचन्द्र cited by झलर्काकर\] (1) Its object is to suggest औपम्य between two or more things नमिसाधु—एकवचनमिह अनन्त्रम्। तेन बहूनामप्यर्थाना सहोक्तिर्भवति। ( 2 ) This is achieved by stating the धर्म once only in direct connection with one धर्मिन् and in indirect connection with the other or others (3) The connection as direct with the धर्मिन् in the Nominative and indirect as regards the धर्मिन् that is in the Instrumental e g. भामहऽ वृद्धिमायान्ति यामिन्य कामिना प्रीतिभि सह Here यामिन्य is directly connected with वृद्धिमायान्ति while प्रीतिमि is indirectly connected therewith owing to the use of सह and the Instrumental (4) Though apparently the धर्मिन् in the Nom is उपमेय and that in the Instru the उपमान actually it may be otherwise also the one in the Nom being उपमान that in the Instru उपमेय (5) the धर्मिन् in the Instrumental will generally have सह or its equivalent with it \[Some of the equivalents भाव सार्धं मम सजु etc— रा तर्कवागीश \] (6) Sometimes mere Instrumental may be used to convey साहचर्य e g यामिनीभि प्रीतय वृद्धिमायान्ति सा द ण्व सहोक्तिरपि सहशब्द- प्रयागाभावेऽपि सहार्थ विवक्षया भवति इति बोध्यम्। (7) All cases where सह is used are not सहोक्तिऽ पुत्रेण सह पिता गच्छति is no सहोक्ति (8) There must be औपम्य and added thereto the poetical charm due to the same धर्म being applicable to the धर्मिन्ऽ in varying meanings or due to the साहचर्य between कार्य-कारण (9) The औपम्य when based on अतिशयोक्ति is more or less verbal as it consists in the same word going with the two धर्मिन्ऽ in different meanings. वृद्धि of यामिनीऽ and प्रीति is not the same to be real साधारणधर्म (10) The धर्मिन्ऽ in सहोक्ति are both प्रकृत, as in
प्रकृतविषयक तुल्ययोगिता (11) In माला form round one धर्मिन् several सहोक्तिऽ must be woven

 § सू ४६ सा सहोक्तिः etc Construe— सहार्थस्य बल द्विवाचक यश भवति सा सहोक्तिः सहार्थस्य ग्रलात् Due to the use of a word meaning

'together with' i. e सह or its equvalents like सम, माक, सजु सार्धं. These convey साहचर्य or साहित्य As seen above, sometimes mere Instrumental may convey साहित्य एक Sc. पदं One word, evidently that which conveys the धर्म द्विवाचक means 'significant for two धर्मिन्ऽ' and not 'conveying two senses' as stated in the Translation. That is, the same word applies to two, maybe in the same sense (as is सह मणिवलयैर्वाष्पधारा गलन्ति) or in different senses द्विवाचक interpreted as 'significant for two' gives scope to शुद्ध as well as अतिशयोक्तिमहिता सहोक्ति. द्वि need not be taken strictly. there may be more than two धर्मिन्ऽ also See नमिसाधु quoted at Essential 1

  वृत्ति— एकार्थाभिधायक is in effect एक्स्मै धर्मिणे अर्धाभिधायक applying to one धर्मिन् उभयस्यावगमक Siguficant for two धर्मिन्ऽ due to सहार्थ Here also उभयस्य stands for two or more Note that the interpretation in the वृत्ति एकस्य अर्थस्य अभिधायक and उभयस्य अर्थस्य अवगमक— where the word on the face of it expresses one meaning but actually conveys two unnecessarily restricts the scope of the figure to अतिशयोक्तिमूला सहोक्ति. Hence the modification of the translation, which however agrees with the वृत्ति.

  St 104. सह दिअह etc Illustrates सहोक्ति A दृती gives a vivid picture of the नायिका's sad plight due to विरह Sanskrit rendering सह दिवमनिशाभिदीर्घा श्वासदण्डा, सह मणिवलयैर्वाष्पधारा गलन्ति।तव सुभग सुभग वियोगे तस्या उद्विग्नाया सह च तनुलतया दुर्बला नीविताशा॥दिवसनिशाभि सह श्वासदण्डा दीर्घा i e The sighs became long The days and nights also appeared long to her, as time hung heavily Cf दण्डिन्'s सह दीर्घा मम श्वासैरिमा सप्रति रात्रय।

  Here दीर्घत्व is directly connected with श्रासदण्ड and means longI It is also indirectly connected with दिवमनिशाभि, owing to the use of He and the Instrumental and means 'prolonged Thus the same word applies in slightly varying meanings to the two In the same way, दुर्बला applies directly to जीविनाशा and indirectly to तनुलता दुर्बलत्व of जीविताशा is not the same as that of तनुल्ता दुर्बलत्व गलन्ति, st appies in the same sense to बाष्पधारा and मणिवलयानि. Hence रामचरण तर्कवागीश ( सा द ) says —वस्तुतस्तु यत्र वैचियमनुभूयते तत्रैवास्यालवारत्व मङ्गिकरणीयम्। अन्यथा सह मणिवलयेहि बाप्पधारा गलन्ति' इति काव्यप्रकाशकारदत्तोदाहरणममगत स्यात्। अत्र स्वस्थानाच्यवनरूप गमनमेकमेव 1. Thus means that अतिशयोक्ति need not always be present in a Here applies in th same sense to both, मणिवलयऽ and बाष्पधाराऽ, and hence no अभेदाभ्यवसाय is necessary Hence the मोके 15 अतिशयोक्तिमूल in a and d, it is with
