out अतिशयोक्ति in b मणिक्यचन्द्र mistakes this for मालासहोक्ति while it is merely सहोक्तिसमुच्चय श्वासदण्डादिगत etc शाब्द i e directly conveyed due to श्रासदण्ड etc being in the Nom दिवसनिशादिगत etc सामर्थ्यात् प्रतिपद्यते i e is indirectly conveyed on the strength of the words like सह or its equivalents As the अलङ्कारिकऽ put it otherwise with the धर्मिन् in the Instrumental the सबन्ध is गौण, with that m the Nominative it is प्रधान

**२८— विनोक्ति**

  The figure विनोक्ति was devised as a sequel to सहोक्ति, If, in सहोक्ति, the use of सह or its equivalent is at the root of the figure, in विनोक्ति, it is the use of विना or its equivalent that occasions it. If साहचर्य connoted by सह or its equivalent plays part in सहोक्ति, वियोग connoted by विना or its equivalent features in विनोक्ति.

  Though thus विनोक्ति is a sequel to सहोक्ति, it can hardly claim the poetical worth of the latter Because, in सहोक्ति, there is the suggetion of औपम्प which makes it poetical while in विनोक्ति there is no trace of suggestion The figure denves only that much charm as would attach to one thing being described as good or bad in the absence of some other In fact मणिक्यचन्द्र draws attention to the fact that some do not accept the figure at all while they admit सहोक्ति Read विनोक्ति
कृतवैचिन्याभावाच्छमात्रयागणर्लकारकल्पने हा धिगाद्युक्तावप्यारत्वप्राप्तेश्च विनोक्त्यलकार न मन्यनेऽन्ये। सहार्थबलात् साम्यप्रतीते सहोक्तिस्तु युक्तैव । र ग refers to a view acc, to which it is charming only when blended with some other figure, not otherwise. Read अलकारान्तरसमालिङ्गनाविर्भूतमेकास्या हृद्यत्व, न स्वत । तेन अलकारान्तरत्वमपि शिथिलमेवेत्यपि वदन्ति।

  And as we trace the history of the figure we find that neither भामह nor दण्डिन् had admitted it उद्भट, वामन, रुद्रट are all silent over it मम्मट was probably the first to conceive it After him रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित all accorded recognition to the figure जगन्नाथ widens its scope in his definition.  

  In विनोक्ति, the poet describes how a thing becomes good or bad when detached from some other thing In विद्य हृद्यपि सावद्या विना विनय सपदम् (कु) विद्या word of विनय is stated to be faulty In विना akdažn HÀNH सभग (कु), the समा is described as being glorious because of the absence of the wicked It is not that विनोक्ति alone can convey the idea of विषय implicit in विनोक्ति. There are other equivalents of it which may also produce a hundred result. Read र गं— इयं च न रक्त

विनाशब्दस्य सत्त्व एव भवति। अपि तु विनाशब्दार्थवाचकमात्रस्य। तेन तत्र निर् वि अन्तरेण ऋतेरहित विकल इत्यादिप्रयोगे इयमेव।

** Note the following scheme —**

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708136070Screenshot2024-02-16222251.png"/>  

  **Essentials—** \[विनार्थेन उक्ति\] (1) Its aim is to descnbe the effect of one thing being separated from another (2) The effect may be either good or bad, i e one object detached from another may turn out good or bad (3) To convey detachment, विना or its equivalents are used For equivalents see the quotation from र ग given above Read सा द— विना शब्दप्रयोगाभावेऽपि विनार्थविवक्षाया विनोक्तिरेव इयम् । (4) The figure may sometimes present itself in a subtle form e g निरर्थक जन्म गत नलिन्या यया न दृष्ट तुहिनाशुबिम्बम्। उत्पत्तिरिन्दोरपि निष्फलैव कृता विनिद्रा नलिनी नयेन॥(रु ) Here यया न दृष्ट and विनिद्रा न कृता both have the force of विनाभाव This र ग calls विनोक्तिध्वनि or suggested विनोक्ति (5) It may sometime be blended with other figures e, g राग विना विरानन्ते मुनयो प्रणयस्तु न। कौटिल्येन विना भाति नरा न कबरीभर॥(र ग) where at is blended with प्रतिवस्तूपमा.

  **§ सू ४७ विनोक्ति सा** etc Construe— यत्र अन्येन विना अन्येन सन् सा विनोक्तिः। यत्र अन्येन विना अन्य न इतर (i e न असत्) सा च विनोक्ति। न सत्=असत् Where in the absence of one, another becomes असत् Where in the one variety of निनोक्ति न इनर=ने असत्=सत् Where in the absence of one, another becomes सत्, that is the other variety The definition is clumsy indeed मा चन्द्र refers to a reading which is definitely more convenient यथान्योऽसन्नयेतर \[यत्र अन्य असत्, अथ इतर (i e सत्) \]

 वृत्ति— क्वचित् in some cases अशोभनः = असत्, न सत् of the सूत्र शोभनः = सत्, न इतर of the सूत्र

 **St. 105 अरुचिनिशया ** etc Illustrates असत्त्वपर्यवसायिनी विनोक्ति Without the night, the moon, and both together, there is no charm respectively in the moon, the night, and love निशया विना अरुचि The moon becomes obscure during the day. महत्तमः one stretch of darkness

 Here, without the निशा, the शशिन् becomes void of sple dour. Without the शशिन्, the विना becomes one stretch of darkness.

Without both, शशिन् and निशा, there is no glamour in love. Thus all निशा, शशिन् and मनोभवस्फुरति are stated to fare all in the absence of शशिन्, निशा and both together Hence this is असत्त्वपर्यवसायिनी विनोक्ति, Note that there is a particular charm in the मिथोविनोक्तिऽ or reciprocal विनोक्तिऽ like अरुचिनिंशया विना शशी, शशिना मापि विना महत्तम। रुय्यक aptly remarks about this source of charm— इय च (विनोक्ति) परस्परविनोक्ति भङ्गया चमत्कारातिशयकृत्।

  **St 106 मृगलोचनया** etc Illustrates सत्त्वपर्यवसायिनी विनोक्ति A prince when away from a particular lovely lady proves his competence for diverse affairs Without a certain companion, he becomes a prince charming प्रतिभाप्रभाप्रगल्भ He becomes conspicuous with the flash of his genus

  Here the prince is described as proving competent and generous when detached from the मृगलेचना and सुहृद. Hence it is सत्त्वपर्यवसायिनी विनोक्ति

**२९— परिवृत्ति**

  Both भामह and उद्भट deal with परिवृत्ति immediately after सहोक्ति मम्मट likewise discusses it after सहोक्ति but between them he treats of विनोक्ति, a sequel to सहोक्ति परिवृत्ति means Exchange, Barter, and in the figure the poet is found describing some select facts in life as if they were some barters or deals. मालविका, before giving a kick to the तपनीयशोक, has plucked a sprout from it. The poet imagines it as a barter and says आदाय वर्णकिसलयनियमरमै चरण मरणमर्पयति । In retum for the वर्णकिसलय, she gives a kick to the अशोक The generous giver has given wealth to the needy and has thus made a name for himself The poet conceives it as a bargain प्रदाय वित्तमर्थिग्य स यशोधनमादित । (भामह) The पथिकऽ chance upon creepers in blossom, the sight of which makes them uneasy The poet describes it as a deal लतास्वध्यन्यानामहह सहसा ललत्याधिम्।— The creepers receive the glances in return for the agony they gave !

 Thus in परिवृत्ति, we have a poetical bargain विनिमय, An actual bargain is poetically worthless. Read र ग— एषु दानादानव्यवहार कविकल्पित एव न तु वास्तव। यत्र स्वतन्त्र तत्र नालङ्कार। And subtler the fact, depicted as a bargain, the more poctical it is. Thus in the instances given above, the विनिमय between मालविका and the अशोक is not as poetical as that between the पथिकऽ and लताऽ or that between the दातृ the अर्थिन् s

 As they are poetical bargains, more often than not, the poet takes delight in describing such as appear inequitable कुवलयानन्दं and in fact recognises these alone as परिवृत्ति, ignoring those that are square deals. Thus a party may give a good thing in return for another that is bad This is न्यूनेन उत्तमस्य परिवृत्ति. रुय्यक calls it अधिकपरिवृत्ति When the enemmes receive blows and give the earth, it is परिवृत्ति of this sort Sometimes it may be the other way viz उत्तमेन न्यूनस्य परिवृत्ति or न्यूनपरिवृत्ति acc. to रुय्यक When जटायु gives up his crippled body and wins glory, he illustrates this type of परिवृत्ति Sometimes how ever, it may be a square deal, good things being exchanged for good, bad things for bad This is समेन समस्य परिवृत्ति or समपरिवृत्ति as रुय्यक calls it In अस्थिमालामयीं दत्वा मुण्डमालामयी तनुम्। गृह्णता त्वत्पुरस्थाना को लाभ स्मरशासन॥(र ग), bad things are exchanged for bad, in प्रदाय वित्त etc. a good thing (वित्त) is exchanged for another good one (यशोधन)

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708095366now.png"/>

  The goodness or badness of the things consists in their being उपादेय or अनुपादेय Those we like to have are good those we abhor are bad Read जयरथ— अधिकत्व न्यूनत्व च उत्कृष्टानुत्कृष्टत्वयोगात् They are good or bad absolutely, objectively, not relatively or subjectively, objectievely, not reletively or subjectively as in व्यतिरेक In the case of unequal deals, how are we to judge that it is न्यूनेन उत्तमस्य or उत्तमेन न्यूनस्य ? The point requires elucidation since there are two parties to a bargain, and so whatever is न्यूनेन उत्तमस्य to one party becomes उत्तमेन न्यूनस्य to the other Thus to revert to the instance of the पथिकऽ and लताऽ, the पथिकऽ receive agony in return for glances i e in relation to them it is न्यूनेन उत्तमस्य परिवृत्ति And in relation to the लताऽ it becomes उत्तमेन न्यूनस्य परिवृति, as they receive glances and give agony. In the case of such परिवृत्तिऽ to judge whether it is उत्तमेन न्यूनस्य or otherwise we have to take into consideration the subject of the sentence. Thus if लताऽ are the subject,

the परिवृत्ति is उत्तमेन न्यूनस्य. If पथिकऽ are the subject it is न्यूनेन उत्तमस्य \[Note that in the terminology न्यूनेन उत्तमस्य or उत्तमेन न्यूनस्य, as used by मम्मट, the Instrumental indicates the thing received and the genitive the thing given. रुय्यक, however, uses the cases just the other way The Instrumental indicates the thing given and Gen the thing taken, according to him प्रदीप understands these cases like रुय्यक :— विनिमयो हि केनचिद् वस्तुना दत्तेन कस्यचिदादानम्। So वैद्यनाथ तत्सत् also We have, of course, adopted मम्मट's standard

  Should there be two parties to the bargain ? :— जगन्नाथ, of all आल्कारिकऽ, is very positive in demanding that in thus figure, as there is an exchange there should be two parties thereto If the poet describes a party as giving up something and receiving some thing else, without mentioning the other party to the bargan, it as not परिवृत्ति according to him Thus in किमित्यपास्याभरणानि यौवने धूत त्वया वार्थवशोभि वल्कलम् (कुमारसभव ५४४), wluch is given by रुय्यक to illustrate परिवृत्ति, there is no other party to receive the आभरणानि and give the वल्कल For that reason, it is not an instance of परिवृत्ति, says जगन्नाथ— अत्र पररमै स्वकीययत्किञ्चिद्वस्तुसमर्पणमित्यतात्पर्यन्त लक्षणे विवक्षित, न तु स्वकीययत्किञ्चिद् वस्तुल्यागमात्रम्। \[His definition— परकीययत्किञ्चिद्वस्त्वादानविशिष्ट पररमै स्वकीय यत्किञ्चिद्वस्तुसमर्पणं परिवृत्ति .\]

  There is much to be said for this view of जगन्नाथ The majority of आलङ्कारिकऽ imply as much when they refer to परिवृत्ति as विनिमय, because विनिमय, requires two parties. But in some of the उदाहरणऽ, they appear to overlook this requirement and remain content with किञ्चित् त्यत्तवा वस्यचिदादानम् Thus वामन gives the instance विहाय सा हारे। बबध बालारुणबभ्रु बल्कलम् ॥ (कु स ५ ८), which, judged by जगन्नाथऽ standard, does not embody a परिवृत्ति

  But it must not be forgotten that too ngorous an application of जगन्नाथऽ standard will deprive us of some good specimens of काव्य which are defimtely परिवृत्तिऽ in every respect save the form demanded by जगन्नाथ Thus in अस्य हि प्रवयसो जटायुष स्वगिण्ण किमिव शोच्यते बुर्धम्। येन जर्जरकलेवरव्ययात् क्रीत..विरणोज्ज्वल यश॥(रु) जटायु is described as stnking a bargain in that he exchanges glory for a decrepit body Here we have a परिवृत्ति, even without the mention of the other party to the bargain.

 It will be safest, therefore, to conclude that in परिवृति, in its normal form there should be two parties. But in case the other party is not mentioned, the statement should clearly suggest that it

refers to a poetical deal (cp व्ययात् येन क्रीत in अस्य हि etc.) Mere assumption of one thing after parting with another is no परिवृत्ति It may be पर्याय (2)

   For परिवृत्ति vs पर्याय (2), see पर्याय.

  परिवृत्ति dates from भामह and दण्डिन् Their instances refer to a two party deal, though their definitions are vague on the point of two parties उद्भट draws attention to the three varieties सम, न्यून, विशिष्ट His instance refers to two parties वामन's definition is vague and his instance refers to a one-party bargain रुद्रट possibly conceives of two parties (cp अन्योन्य) in his definition His illustration refers to a two-party exchange रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित all recognise the figure All of these, except जगन्नाथ are vague on the point of reference to the second party जगन्नाथ, as we saw above, insists on it. अप्पय्यदीक्षित speaks only of न्यून-अभ्यथिकयो परिवृत्ति समयो परिवृत्ति possibly had no attraction for him

  **Essentials—** \[परिवृत्ति = परिवृत्ति = विनिमय\] (1) There must be and दान of one thing and आदान another (2) The दान and आदान must be such as involve no real barter, but a poetical one मुक्ताभि बाला बदरीफलानि क्रीणन्ति ( र ग ) is not परिवृत्ति because there the barter is real (3) The subtler the दान-आदान, the more poetical becomes the figure (4) Strictly speaking there should be a second party to the deal, expressed or implied (5) Where the second party is neither expressed nor implied, the statement should clearly indicate that the fact described is in the nature of a deal (6) The exchange may be of good-good, bad-bad, good-bad or bad-good things. (7) A square deal (समपरवृत्ति) is not poetically as striking as an inequitable one (8) The goodness or otherwise consists in the thing being acceptable (उपादेय) or unacceptable

  § सू ४८ परिवृत्ति etc. Construe अर्थाना समासमै य विनिमय सा परवृत्ति। That is परवृत्ति which consists in the exchange of objects for others that are equal or unequal to them. विनिमय• (वि + वि +**√**मे1A. to exchange, barter) Barter, exchange. अर्थानां comprehends objects two or more. Read मा च :– अत्र बहुवचनमनश्रम्। तत्रैक द्वे बहूनि वा दत्वा व्युत्क्रमेण विषमममसङ्ख्यानामादान यत्र तत्रेषा स्यात्।

  वृत्ति— परिवृत्तिरलकार Lest one might mistake विनिमय for the name of the figure, the author tells us that the name of the figure is परिवृत्ति and not विनिमय As परिवृत्ति and विनिमय are synonyms, this indication of the name was necessary

** St 107 लतानां** etc Illustrates समेन समस्य परिवृति The breeze that makes the creepers move becomes fragrant The creepers make the passers by that look at them break into sobs etc These facts are described as exchanges Construe उदितकुसुमाना लतानां मत लास्य दत्वा अय मरुद् असम आमोद भृश श्रयति। उदितकुसुम That are in blossom मत लास्य highly prized graceful movements आमोद श्रयति accepts their fragrance अध्वन्य a traveller आधिश्च व्याधिश्च भ्रमिश्च रुलितं च मोहश्च तेषा व्यतिकर (combination blend) भ्रमि Giddiness Not ‘defusion' as given in Translation

  Here in ab the exchange is between मरुत् and लताऽ मरुत gives लास्य to the लताऽ and receives आमोद (fragrance) in return Since लास्य and आमोद are both उपादेय this is समेन समस्य परिवृत्ति or समपरिवृत्ति as रुय्यक styles it

  In cd the लताऽ receive the glances of the travellers and give in exchange a blend of agony disease giddiness etc Glances being उपादेय and the blend of agony etc being अनुपालेय this is उत्तमेन न्यूनम्य परिवृत्ति, from the standpoint of the लताऽ the subject \[In relation to the अ वन्यऽ it would have become न्यूनेन उत्तमस्य परिवृत्ति\]

   प्रथमेऽधै in ab द्वितीये in cd

   St 108 नानाविधप्रहरणै etc Illustrates न्यूनेन उत्तमस्य परिवृत्ति The king's enemies received terrific blows at the hands of the king and surrendered for good thear kingdoms सप्रहार (m) battle दृप्तारिचिसर विसर host, multitude वसुधरा निर्विप्रलम्भपरिरम्भविधि वितीर्णा The enemy surrendered the Earth whose act (विधि) of embracing you (परिरम्भ) was not to be marred by separation (निर्विप्रलम्भ) निविप्रलम्भ निर्गत विप्रलम्भ यस्मात् स (परिरम्भविधि) The idea is The enemy parted with the earth for good

  Here the अरिविसर the subject in the Instrumental receives blows and gives the earth in return Blows being अनूपादेय and the earth उपादेय
this is न्यूनेन उक्तमस्य परिवृत्ति \[ If the king were the subject it would have become उक्तमेन न्यूनस्य परिवृत्ति

**३०— भाविक**

 The figure भाविक is a tangible result of the poets power of visualising things of the past or the future Sometimes the poet takes delight in recalling the vivid memories of the past and setting them forth before the reader as though they were events of the

present under his very nose. On occasions, a prophet that he is, the poet is found projecting his imagination into the future and giving us a vivid picture of the 'shape of things to come'. In short, the poet, in this figure, presents the past or the future as though it were the present A historian unfolds to us the past, no doubt, but he treats of it as no more than past A prophet gives us glimpses of the future but is too matter of fact to picture them as things of the present. Sometimes the poet may give us a falso by describing something in the nature of a miracle which he does not pretend to be a vision but which is presented in such lucid words that the सहृदय feels it as a thung of the present e g मुनिर्जयति योगीन्द्रा महात्मा कुम्भसमव। येनैकचुलुके दृष्टौ दिव्यौ तौ मत्स्य-कच्छपौ॥where the miracle of अगत्स्य witnessing मस्यावतार and कूर्मावतार in his act of drinking the ocean is described so effectively that the सहृदय feels the episode as being प्रत्यक्ष Read तर्कवागीश— मुनि वर्णनाविशेषवशेन श्रोतृृणा सन्निकृष्टवदवभासते

  The figure भाविक not only typifies the power of the poet to visualise the past or the future but something more Like स्वभावोक्ति, it demands a perspicuity of expression failing which the figure loses its force The vision may be there perfect in every detail, but if the poet is unable to reflect it in his words, it is still born. There must be a happy balance between idea and expression That is why आलङ्कारिकऽ like रुय्यक and जयरथ insist upon प्रसाद as a vital pre requsite of भाविक जयरथ, for instance, says— यद्यपि वाचामाकुलत्व सर्वत्रैव वर्जनीय तथापि तत्तत्र वैषम्येणार्थविशेषाप्रतीतर्विघ्नमात्रफलम्। यह तु तदाकुलत्वे नातीतानागतयो प्रत्यक्षायमाणत्वमेव न स्यादिति प्राधान्येनैतदुक्तम्। इह हि केचिदर्था कविवचसि सुस्पष्टमधिरूढा अपि निजमौमाम्याभावात् तृणशर्करावत् सहृदयानामवशास्तथा नावधानार्हा । केचिच्च सुभगा अपि दुर्भगशब्दोपारोहितया सहृदयानाभावर्तका एवेत्युभयमपीहावश्य माश्रयणीयम्।

   In fact, like श्लेष, भाविक was a काव्यगुण, not an अलङ्कार In the opinion of भामह and दण्डिन् दण्डिन् for instance, says तद्भाविकमिति प्राहु प्रबधविषय गुणम्। उद्भट was the first to assign to at the status of an अलङ्कार
Neither वामन nor रुद्रट recognised the figure भोत recongnised It. रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित all admitted it. रुय्यक and विश्वनाथ make an effort to prove that it is not a गुग, but an अलङ्कार It is unfortunate that in the रं गं in its extant portion, the figure is not to be found. nor does
जगन्नाथ reveal his attitude thereto while discussing प्रमाद as शब्दगुण and अर्थगुण

 भाविक vs स्वभावोक्ति— As in स्वभावोक्ति, in भाविक war we get a faithful portrayal of a theme which is arresting enough But whereas, in

भाविक, the charm consists in visualising a past or a future event as a fact of the present, in स्वभावोक्ति, this element of visualization is conspicyously absent. The poet is content, in स्वभावोक्ति, with giving us a graphic study of an object, without attempting to transfer it from the past or future to the present Read सा द —न च स्वभावोक्ति। तस्य लौकिकवस्तुगतसूक्ष्मधर्मस्वमावस्यैव यथावद् वर्णन स्वरूपम्। अन्य तु वस्तुन प्रत्यक्षायमाणत्वरूपो विच्छित्तिविशेषोऽस्तीति। But it has to be recognised that the two figures are closely allied and not unfrequently is भाविक found blended with स्वभावोक्ति giving rise to सकर.

  भाविक us उत्प्रेक्षा and अतिशयोक्ति —When the poet in भाविक, presents to us a भूत or भाविन् thing as though it were present one is likely to mistake it for उत्प्रेक्षा, गम्य of course, due to the absence of सभावनप्रतिपादकऽ like इव, मन्ये etc But there is a fundamental difference, between the two Whereas in उत्प्रेक्षा, there is a ( साध्य) अध्यवसाय, connoted by 'as if' 'as though', in भाविक, there is no अध्यवसाय at all Because when the poet transfers the past or the future to the present he and the reader both forget that it is a thing not of the present Both look upon it as a thing of the present, ipso facto, due to the high pitch of emotion Read रुय्यक —नापि भूतभाविनामप्रत्यक्षाणा प्रत्यक्षनयैव प्रतीते इवार्थगर्भीकारणाय प्रतीयमानोत्प्रेक्षा । तस्या अभिमानरूपाख्याध्यवमायन्वभावत्वात् । न ह्यप्रत्यक्षत्यक्ष प्रत्यक्षत्वेनाध्यवसीयते। किं तर्हि काव्यार्थविद्भि प्रत्यक्षत्वेन दृश्यत इति ।

  Nor is भाविक अतिशयोक्ति In अतिशयोक्ति, there is (सिद्ध) अध्यवसाय, here there is none Read रुय्यक— नहि भूतभावि अभूतभावित्वेन अध्यवसीयते, अभूतभावि,
भूतभाविक्येनापि, प्रत्यक्षमप्रत्यक्षगतत्वेन, अप्रत्यक्षमपि प्रत्यक्षत्वेन It is not अध्यवसाय when the अप्रत्यक्ष is visualised as the प्रत्यक्ष Because when the thing is visualised there is no element of आहार्यत्व there in, whereas अध्यवसाय is always आहार्य or intentional Like a योगिन्, the कवि actually gets the vision of the past or the future Read जयरथ —योगिना अतीन्द्रीयार्थदर्शने यथा भावना निमित्त तथैव काव्यार्थविदामपीत्यर्थ।  

  For भाविक 2s भ्रान्तिमान् See भ्रान्तिमान्

मम्मट’ s explanation reminds one of दण्डिन्’ s explanation भव कवेरभिप्राय काव्येष्वासिद्धि सस्थित। रुय्यक attempts two explanations (1) कविगतोभाव’ आशय श्रोतरि प्रतिबिन्दत्वेन अस्तीति, or ( 11 ) भावो भावना वा पुन पुन चेतसि निवेशन सोऽत्रास्तीति। The first explanation is based upon प्रमाद which.as we saw is necessary for the figure. The second draws attention to the play of भावना or mental impression in the figure and is akin to मम्मट's explanation\] (1) The poet like a mystic visualises as actually present before him a thing of the past or the future (2) This is due to the thing being stnking (अत्यद्भुत) enough to leave an impression (भावना ) on his mind (3) There cannot be a भाविक with a thing of the present (4) The poet's diction ought to be perspicuous (प्रसादयुक्त) Otherwise his own vision will not be communicated to the reader or श्रतु Read **प्रतीहारेन्दुराज—** तन वाचामनाकुलता झठिति अर्थप्रतीतिकारिता।तस्या हि सत्या कवे सम्बधी यो भाव स सहृदयै श्रोतृभि स्वाभिप्रायाभेदन तत्तत्काव्यप्रतिविम्विन-रूपतया साक्षात्क्रियते । (5) The poet and the reader actually feel the thing as one of the present There is no आहायत्व It as as much real to them as योगिप्रत्यक्ष is to the योगिन् (6) Just as it is not आहाय It is not भ्रान्त also In भ्रान्ति one gets perverted knowledge due to delusion though the experience is not आहाय In भाविक, there is no perverted knowledge at all Read रूय्यक— न चेय भ्रान्ति । भूतभाविन भूतभावितयैव प्रकाशनात्। (7) Or sometimes as in मुनिर्नयति etc the poet merely describes a wonderful thing of the past without claiming it to be a vision but so lucidly that it appeal to the सहृदय as if it were a thing of the present

 § सू ४९ प्रत्यक्षा इव etc प्रत्यक्षा इव As we saw above भाविक should not be mistaken for गम्या उत्प्रेक्षा with इव with etc. understood Possibly the definition would have become more precuse with प्रत्यक्षा एव The भाव s actually becorne प्रत्यक्ष to the poet ( and the सहृदय) Note that मम्मट overlooks प्रमाद on which earlier अल्पकारिकऽ like उद्भट had land so much stress Or, is प्रसाद presupposed in प्रत्यक्षा क्रियन्ते ? रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित, for that matter, do not refer to प्रसाद, in their definitions of the figure स प्रकाशिनी says that this omission has broadened the scope of the figure अत पल्वनवुलत्व विशेषमनषेभ्य सर्वभेदोपसग्रहाय प्रत्यक्षा —भूनभाविन इत्यनावव लक्षण कृतन्। भाषा facts. भूतभाविन Belonging to the past or the future There cannot be a भाविक with बनमान things Note that the भावऽ must be poeticall striking (विच्छित्तिविशेषवन्त )

 **वृत्ति —भूताश्व** etc This is to guard against भुतभाविन being interpreted as a कर्मधारय If interpreted as a कर्मधारय (भुत ते भाविनय )

it would mean those that occurred in the past and are again to occur in the future. Thus is not the intended meaning which is भूताश्च भाविनश्च —a द्वद्व —things of the past and the future भावः कवेरभिप्रायः etc The name explained. The figure is so called because therein is embodied the cherished intention (अभिप्राय) of the poet, which is to visualise the भूत or भाविन् as प्रत्यक्ष and to communicate the same to the सहृदय. Some explain this clause differently कवर्भाव श्रोतरि प्रति विम्वत्वेन अस्ति इति. In this figure, the भाव ( = भावना ) of the poet is reflected also in the सहृदय, of course due to प्रसाद or perspicuity of expression भाव मम्मट interprets as अभिप्राय others as भावना, the mental impression left by an अद्भुत पदार्थ which (impression) is responsible for its being recalled to mind again

**St 109 आसीदञ्जनं** etc Illustrates भाविक a illustrates भूतस्य प्रत्यक्षायमाणत्व b भाविन प्रत्यक्षायमाणत्वम् The eyes of a beautiful lady that had once collynum in them and her figure that has prospects of wearing ornaments in future are visualised as being in that state at present **आसीदञ्जनमनेति** as they teve when they had the अञ्जन in them **भाविभूषणसभारा आकृति** Your figure decked with prospective ornaments. Add “Here, in the first half there is a visualization of the past, in the second, of the future" in the Translation

 Here अञ्जनवत्त्व of the past and भूषणममारवत्त्व of the future are visualised as प्रत्यक्ष. Hence the stanza embodies two भाविकऽ भूतस्य प्रत्यक्षायमाणत्व and भाविन प्रत्यक्षायमाणत्वम् **रा तर्कवागीश ( सा द )** gives a picturesque context for the stanza —नयनशोभाविशेषवशेन अज्जनम्। सौन्दर्या तिशयवशीकृतस्य धनिन पत्युरादरातिशयेन भूषणसम्भारमनुमाय सख्या तथा अभिहित यथा भूत भाविनावपि अपनभूषणसम्भारौ सन्निकृष्टश्रोतृृणां प्रत्यक्षवद् भासेते। क्वेचित्तु यौवनोद्भेदादञ्जन विनापि चक्षुषि तच्छोभा भूषणाभावेऽपे वपुषि तच्चोभारम्भ इति व्याचक्षते तन्न। तादृशार्थम्य व्यञ्जनाभावेनाप्रतीते।

**३१-काव्यलिङ्ग**

 काव्यलिङ्ग or simpler still काव्यहेतु is put by उद्भवः in the same group as भाविक and this explains the sequence of the two figures in the काव्यप्रकाश The essence of the figure is better understood with the name काव्यहेतु, than काव्यलिङ्ग in fact उद्भवः in his enumeration of the group uses the name काव्यहेतु, though, in the definition, he uses the subtler and yet more popular name काव्यलिङ्ग — लिङ्ग being a probative हेतु in the तार्किक termunology लिङ्ग्यते गम्यने अनेन अर्थ इति लिङ्ग हेतु धूम is लिङ्ग in relation to वद्दिशन

 काव्यलिङ्ग or काव्यहेतु is a poetical cause. What is meant by the cause being poetical is that it is not the formal, dryasdust probative cause likeधूम, familiar to the तार्किक but has to be poetically charming This poetical charm when analysed still further resolves into two factors (1) the charm inherent in the theme itself, and (॥) the charm due to its हेतुत्व being suggested, and not expressed.

 इरावती in the **मालविकाग्निमित्र** ३ २३ slights अग्निमित्र by ignoring his प्रणिपात ( प्रणिपातलङ्घन) अग्निमित्र, at the end of the affair, says मन्ये प्रिया हृतमना तस्या प्रणिपातलङ्घन सेवाम् — I look upon the प्रणिपातलङ्घन as an obligation (सेवा) done to me' We feel puzzled to know how प्रणिपातलङ्घन, which is normally an insult, could be an obligation? But our doubts are set at rest as we scan the word प्रियाहृतमना What in ordinary circum stances, would have proved to be an insult becomes सेवा, because अग्निमित्र is s for the nonce involved in a love-intrigue with मालविका and to that intrigue प्रणिपातलङ्घन will be of utmost service So प्रियाहृतमना embodies a हेतु that explains why प्रणिपातलङ्घन is a that it is a सेवा And further, that it is a हेतु has to be guessed only, there is no Ablative like प्रियाहृतमनस्त्वात्, nor any word explicitly stating it to be a हेतु The हेतुत्व प्रियाहृतमना is therefore suggested, व्यग्य Thus, in effect, प्रियाहृतमना gives us a काव्यलिङ्ग for two reasons (1) because it is poetically striking, and (2) because its हेतुत्व is a matter for suggestion Whoever derues that suggestion is always at a premium in poetry? Read र ग गम्यमानहेतुत्वकस्यैव हेतो सुन्दरत्वेनालकारिकैः काव्यलिङ्गनाभ्युपगमात्।

 The illustration above gives us a काव्यलिङ्ग of a forceful character because it is employed in accounting for a paradox (विरोध) VIZ. प्रणिपातलङ्घनस्य सेवात्वम् And one must admit that the most pronounced काव्यलिङ्गऽ are those that feature with paradoxes and seek to explain them That way, उक्तनिमित्ता विभावना and उक्तनिमित्ता विशेषोक्ति must always embody काव्यलिङ्ग, provided, of course, the fact that the particular thing is the निमित्त is implied (गम्य) and not expressed Thus in the instance—कर्पूर इव दग्धोऽपि शक्तिमान् यो जने जने। नमोऽस्त्ववार्यवीर्याय तस्मै मकरयेतवे॥given to illustrate उक्तनिमित्ता विशेषोक्ति, we have a काव्यलिङ्ग Because, अवार्यवीर्याय, which is the निर्मित्त for कारणभावे कार्याभाव, is a गम्य निमित्त If it were a वाच्य निर्मित्त like अवार्यवीर्यत्वात् there would not have resuned my काव्यलिङ्ग

 But काव्यलिङ्ग may appear even in Jess pronounced forms. This it does when it features as an adjunct not of anyविरोध, but of some other fact, poetically striking Whenअश्व says (शाकुन्तल ४ २०३ ) शममेष्यति मम

शोक कथ नु वत्से त्वया रचितपूर्वम्। उटजद्वारि विरूढ नीवारबलि विलोक्यत॥we have a काव्यलिङ्ग in b not as an adjunct to any विरोध कण्व says that his grief would be sustamed as he would look at the नीवारऽ offered by शकुन्कला in worship growing into shoots Here the sight of नीवारबलि is the हेतु for शोकाशमन and in शोकाशमन there is no विरोध involved Similariy in जितोऽसि मन्द बन्दर्प, मच्चित्तेऽस्ति त्रिलोचन।(कु), मच्चित्तेऽस्ति त्रिलोचन is the हेतु of मदनजय and in मदनजय there is no विरोध standing in need of being explained.

 काव्यलिङ्ग may present itself in the body of a single word or in a set of words or in the body of a sentence In मन्ये प्रियाहृतमना etc at is embodied in the single word albert compounded प्रियाहृत ढढमना In शममेष्यति मम etc it presents itself in the set of words उटजद्वारि विरूढ नीवारवर्लि विलोकयत, which is only a phrase and not a complete वाक्य In जितोऽसि म
etc the figure is incorporated in the वाक्य, मच्चित्तेऽस्ति त्रिलोचन।

 This gives us the following scheme—

**काव्यलिङ्ग**  
।

।————————————————————————।

            ** पदार्थगत **                 
           **वाक्यार्थगत**

                ।

         ।———————————————————— ।

   **एकपदार्थगत**                     
**अनेकपदार्थगत  
       1                            
  2**

 ** 1s काव्यलिङ्ग an अलङ्कार ?** Since in काव्यलिङ्ग we have nothing more striking than a हेतु embodied in a पदार्थ oवाक्यार्थ and since this हेतु only serves to explain a fact that would otherwise have remained un accounted for and thus left a lacuma ( अपेक्षा ) in our knowledge of the fact some आलङ्कारिकऽ like जयरथ question its claim to be an अलङ्कार At best it could be looked upon as immunity from the दोष of हेत्वभाव but not as a positive अलकार they argue. Read जयरथ- ननु हेतोर्वाक्यपदार्थोभयो-पनिवन्धे न कश्चिद् विच्छित्तिविशेष प्रतीयत इति कथमस्य अलङ्कारमुत्तम्। कविप्रतिभामकस्य चिच्छित्तिविशेषात्मवस्यालकारत्वेन उक्तत्वात्। सत्यम्। यद्यपि एवमुपनिबधस्य वस्तुवृत्तेर समवान्न कश्चितिनय प्रतीयते, तथापि ग्रन्थकृता (—रूय्येकण) प्राच्यैर्लक्षितत्वा. ... । लक्षितम्। जगन्नाथ admits the figure but refers casually to this view ---अत्र वदन्ति काव्यलिङ्ग ना.....र। वैचित्र्यात्मनो विच्छित्तिविशेषस्य अभावात्। हेतुद्देतुसद्भावस्य वस्तु सिद्धत्त्वेन कविप्रतिभानिर्वर्त्यत्वायोगात्। अत एव चमत्कृतिरपि दुर्लभा। तस्मात् निर्हतुरूपदोषा भाव काव्यलिङ्गम् इत्यपि वदन्ति।

 There is much to be said for this view. काव्यलिङ्ग is no doubt an a large number of cases of its occurrence too colourless to deserve the distinction of an अलङ्कार That commentators like मल्लिनाथ, while commenting on the काव्यऽ, should stumble upon this figure in every nook and corner of theकाव्यऽ, shows its cheapness But with all that, it can not be gainsaid that sometimes the figure does present itself in a striking form indeed as in the stanza from the शाकुन्तल, शममेष्यति etc. cated above. Even such a stalwart आलकारिक as दण्डिन् had to concede that there is an अलङ्कार in such हेतुऽ \[ दण्डिन् had no काव्यलिङ्ग, but हेतुऽ of a wide range \] अयमान्दोलितप्रौढ-चन्द्रूनमपल्लव। उत्पादयति सर्वस्य प्रीतिं मलयमारत॥प्रीत्युत्पादनयोग्यस्य रूपस्या...पवृहणम्। अलङ्कारतयोद्दिष्टम्ः . (का द २,२३६-२३७ ) Earlier दण्डिन् had said हेतुश्च सूक्ष्मलेशौ च वाचामुत्तमभूषणम्। (का द २२३५ )

 Though the name काव्यलिङ्ग was first introduced by उद्भट, who also gives it the other name काव्यहेतु, still the figure in essence goes back to the days of दण्डिन् who, as seen above, names it हेतु भामह did not recognuse हेतु \[ हेतुश्च सूक्ष्मो लेशोऽथ नालकारतया मत। २.८६ \] वामन has neither हेतु nor काव्यलिङ्ग रुद्रट has हेतु but it is different in essence from काव्यलिङ्ग, as it is based on अध्यवमान of कारण as काय रूय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित all admit the figure काव्यलिङ्ग विश्वनाथ admits हेतु besides which is the same as रुद्रट's.

 काव्यलिङ्ग ts. अर्थान्तरन्यास— वाक्यार्थगत काव्यलिङ्ग bears a slight resemblance to अर्थान्तरन्यास, in view of the fact that in both one वाक्य is adduced to explain a fact embodied in the other Just as मच्चित्तेऽस्ति त्रिलोचन explains निनोऽसि मन्द बन्दर्प so दुष्वर कि महात्मनाम् explains हनुमानग्धिमतरत् That way, in both the figures, the poet aims at समर्थन But (1) whereas in काव्यलिङ्ग, the समर्थन takes the form of a fact being due to some crcumstance in अर्थान्तन्यास, the समर्थन takes the form of a particular fact being typical of some general truth, or a general maxim being illustrated by a particular fact. (2) This results in the two वाक्यऽ being related as कार्य-कारण in काव्यलिङ्ग and सामान्य -विशेष in अर्थान्तरन्यास How कार्य कारणभाव as absolutely different from सामान्य-विशेषनव needs no elaboration. Read कुवल्यानन्द --समथ्य समर्थवसबन्धेनऽर्थान्तरन्यास। तदितरसम्बन्धे काव्यलिङ्गम् Whers विश्वनाथ says: निष्पादक हेतु काव्यलिङ्गस्य समर्थतः अर्थान्तरन्यासस्य, he seems to take an un justifiably narrow view of समर्थात्व, which is equally present in काव्यलिङ्ग जगन्नाथ, while enticasing अप्पय्यदीक्षित, does apper to concede that in काव्यलिङ्ग also we have समर्थन. but without सामान्य विदोषभाव, (3) În काव्यलिङ्ग both the वाक्यऽ relate to the प्रस्तुत For as the कार्यं In much प्रस्तुत as the कारण in अर्थान्तरन्यास, one वाक्य relates to the

प्रस्तुत, the other to the अप्रस्तुत If the सामान्य 1s प्रस्तुत, the विशेष 15 अप्रस्तुत and vice versa

**For काव्यलिङ्ग vs परिकर, See परिकर  
For काव्यलिङ्ग vs अनुमान, see अनुमान**

 ** Essentials —**\[ काव्याभिमत लिङ्गम् — A cause poetically concerved लिङ्ग्यते गम्यते अनेन अर्थम् इति लिङ्ग हेतु \] (1) Here the poet aims at elucidating some fact and thereby removing the lacuna in our under standing of it by assigning some cause for it (2) Though this is the aim, the poet will never declare openly, nor formally, ( न ह्यत्र व्याप्तिपक्षधर्मतोपसहारादय क्रियन्ते। —रूय्यक) that a particular thing is occasioned by some other thing In other words the cause will not be put in the Instr or Abl, nor will the poet use words like हि यत् etc. Thus in अनुमतगमना शकुन्तला, तरूभिरिव बनवासवधुभि। परमृतविरुत कल यत, प्रतिवचनीकृतमेभिरीदृशम्॥- शाकुन्तल ४ ९ owing to the use of यत in c, there is no काव्यलिङ्ग Read सा द— न धत्ते शिरसा गङ्गा भूरिमारभिया हर ! त्वद्वाजिराजिनिर्धूतधूलिभि पङ्किला हि सा \।\। इत्यत्र हि—शब्दोपादानेन पङ्किलत्वादितिवद्धेतुत्वस्य सुटतया नायमलकार। वैचित्र्यस्यैवालवारत्वात् \।
Sumilarly, र ग. पचम्यादिशब्दप्रति पादितहेतुत्वकस्य हेतोरेत्र वारणायोपपादकत्वेन विवक्षित इति। न तु शब्दातेन रूपेण बोधित इत्येतदर्थफलकम्। तेन ‘भयानकत्वात् परिवर्तनीयो, दयाश्रयत्वादसि देव सेव्य’ इत्यादौ नायमलकार। गम्यमानहेतुकत्वस्यैव हेतो सुन्दरत्वेन आकारिकै काव्यलिङ्गताभ्युपगमात्। (3) When हेतुत्व is thus imphed it becomes poetical (4) Also the हेतु becomes must be striking in itself (5) This means that the हेतु poetical for two reasons (a) because it is striking in itself, and (b) because its हेतुत्व is suggested (6) When the हेतु explains a विरोध or paradox it is more striking than when it merely accounts for a simple thing (उपवृहण - as दण्डिन् says ) (7) The हेतु may be embodied in a पद (simple or compounded ) in a group of पदऽ or in a वाक्य (8) When embodied in a वाक्य there are bound to be at least two बाक्यऽ, for हेतु one, for कार्य the other (9) The way in which हेतुत्व is suggested by the poet is in the cash of पदऽ he reduces the हेतु to adjectives, which may sometimes take the form of locative and genitive absolutes In the case of वाक्यऽ, he studiously omuts particles like हि, यत, and words like यस्मात् etc. Cp प्रियाहृतमना नीवारबलिं विलोकयत मच्चित्तेऽस्ति त्रिलोचन in the उदाहरणऽ above

 १ **सू ५० काव्यलिङ्ग हेतो** etc. Construe हेतो वाक्य-पदार्थता काव्यलिङ्गम्। काव्यलिङ्ग occurs where a cause is embodied in a वाक्यार्थ or पदार्थ In the definition मम्मट overlooks the requrement of the हेतुत्व being गम्य

 St 110 **वपुःप्रादुर्भावात्** etc Illustrates वाक्यार्थगत काव्यलिङ्ग A devotee of शिव, sings in praise of a प्रणति to शिव He failed to make a bow to शिव, and became a ससारिन् Later he saluted शिव and became मुक्त ab Construe —पुरारे, वपुःप्रादुर्भावाद् इद अनुमित ( यत् ) प्राय पुरा जमनि क्वचिदपि भवन्त न प्रणतवान्। **वपुःप्रादुर्भाव** The fact that I have a body i e I am a ससारिन् I became a ससारिन् because, in all probability, I did not salute you in my births past cd Construe सप्रति नमन् मुक्त अह अतनु सन् अग्रे अपि अनतिभाक् ( भविष्यामि )। महेश, तदिद अपराधद्वय अपि क्षन्तव्यम्। The idea is Now that I salute you I shall become मुक्त in the future ( अग्रे ) And once I become मुक्त, I shall be unable, in the absence of a body, to salute you मुक्त persons have no body अनतिभाक् failing to salute **तदिद अपराधद्वय** viz. the अपराध of अनमन in the past lives due to which I became a ससारिन् and the अपराध of अनमन ( =अ-नति ) in future due to my being bodiless in my state of मुक्ति

 ** Here** अपराधद्वय mentioned in d calls for an explanation and it is given in the two वाक्यऽ पुरा भवन्त न प्रणतवान् and अग्रेऽपि अनतिभाक् ( भविष्यामि ) Had this explanation not been given we would have been puzzled to know what the twoअपराधऽ were But even when the explanation is given the poet nowhere says openly that it is owing. to the facts in abc that the arts were committed. That is why it is a काव्यलिङ्ग embodied in the वाक्यार्थऽ in abc Note that मोक्षनामनि महामोहे embodies an अपह्नुति मा च calls it रूपक. Incorrect

 St. 111 प्रणयिसखी etc. Illustrates अनेकपदार्थङ्गत काव्यनि माधव deals a deadly blow to अधोरपृष्ट who is about to slay मालती Conslrue –यत् ( मालत्या वपु ) प्रणयिसखीस.....परिहासरमाधिगतै शिरीषपुष्पनहननैरपि ताम्यति सत्र वपुषि बधाय शस्त्रमुपक्षियत तव शिरसि अकाण्डयमदण्ड इव एवं भुज पततु। ab मालती's figure is so tender that even when struck by the delicate शिरीष flowers it used to get hurt प्रणयिसम्वी etc. The strokes of शिरीष flowers which she got (अधिगत) owing to the love for jokes (परिहासरस ) on the part of her loving fnends They in therr merriment threw flowers at her प्रणयिसखी indicates that their floral attack could not have been harmful अकाण्डयमदण्ड The Death's Rod suddenly brought into use  

 Here तव शिरसि एवं भुज एतत्तु is accounted for by तत्र वपुषि बधाय शस्त्रमुपदिशा I ply this arm against you because you are kalling her—19 the adea. The हेतुत्व in वपुषि etc. 19 only suggested. \[ It would have been expressed if the words were something like यत

शस्त्रमुपक्षिपति तस्मात् ........\]. Thus the हेतु, which is suggested as such, beng present in तन्त्र वपुषि वधाय शस्त्रमुपक्षिपत we get अनेकपदार्थङ्गत काव्यलिङ्गं Note that the अनेकपदऽ are not a वाक्य for want of a fimte verb

 ** St. 112 भस्मोद्धूलन** etc Illustrates एक्पदार्थगत काव्यलिङ्ग A मुक्त person bids farewell to all the appurtenances of भक्ति which are no more useful to him When a person has attamed मोक्ष, the highest goal, the साधनऽ have no use for him भस्मोद्धुलन (उद्धूलन from the Denom. धूल्य from धूलि ) besmearing of ashes शुभ = भद्र farewell to thee b The idea is I shall no more make use of the flight. of steps leading to the temple of शिव cd Construe —अद्य आराधन तोषितेन विभुना युष्मत्सपर्यामुखालोकोच्छेदिनि मोक्षनामनि महामोहे निधीयामहे। Today, we (1 e I) are being placed in that great delusion named मोक्ष , which puts an end to the light of pleasure due to our attending on you ( 1 e भस्मोद्धूलन, रुद्राक्षमाला and सोपानपरपरा ), by शिव ( = विभु) who is pleased with the worship. युष्मत् etc when in the stage of a मुमुक्षु, yet to become मुक्त, a person gets pleasure from a devoted attention (सपर्या) to the साधनऽ like भस्मोद्धूलन, रुद्राक्षमाला etc. This pleasure is conceived as आलोक, light मोक्षनामनि महामोहे मोक्ष Is ironically conceived as a serious delusion, or better still, a serious swoon because, as in a swoon, the भुक्त gets blinded for want of the light (आलोक ) of सपर्यामुख।

 Here, when we are perplexed at the exalted मोक्ष being described 38 महामोह, युष्मत्सपर्यालोकोच्छेदिनि explains the propriety, as seen above. That युष्मत्सपर्यालोकोच्छेद is the cause of महामोह is only suggested Hence this is एकपदार्थगत काव्यलिङ्ग, युष्मत्सपयलोकोच्छेदिनि being one पद albeit compounded Note that here काव्यलिङ्ग explains the विरोध be tween मोक्ष and महामोहत्व

 ** एषु अपराधद्वये** etc—एषु sc उदाहरणेषु Construe -अपराधद्वये पूर्वापरजन्मनो अनमत उक्तरूप ( 1 e वाक्यार्थात्मक ) हेतु। भुतपाते शस्त्रोपक्षेप उक्तरूप (i e अनेकपदार्थात्मक ) हेतु। महामोहे च सुखालोकोच्छेदित्वम् उक्तरूप (i e एकपदार्थात्मक ) हेतु। For explanation see the appraisal of the उदाहरणऽ given above पूर्वापरजन्मनो अनमनम् In former births he failed to salute, hence he became a ससारिन् Later (अपर..न्मन्) when he became मुक्त, he could not salute for want of a शरीर अपरजन्मन् i e the one in which he became मुक्त, उत्तरूप i e वाक्यार्थरूप, अनेकपदार्थरूप and एक्पदार्थरूप

**३२—पर्यायोक्त**

 पर्यायोक्त is circumlocution a euphustic expression. It consists in the poet's conveying his message not by direct mention, but by

resorting to an indirect mode of expression which he does to secure a greater poetical effect रघु was victorious against the हूणऽ A historian would have said रघु won a victory over the हूणऽ.कालिदास means to convey the same idea But a poet that he is,he is not to remain content with so bleak an expression as that Rather would he say रघु's exploit ordered a ruddy tinge (of sorrow) to appear on the cheeks of the हूण beauties तत्र हूणावराधाना भर्तृषु व्यक्तविक्रमम्। कपोलपाटलदेशि बभूव रघुचेष्टितम्॥**रघुवंश** ४६८ So when the poet **मेण्ठ**(?) wants to refer to the conquest of स्वर्ग by हयग्रीव, he says स्पृष्टास्ता नन्दने शच्या वेशमभोगलालिता। सावश पारिनातस्य मञ्जर्यो यस्य सैनिकै॥(अ स ) His warriors handled the पारिजात blossoms which until now only went to adorn शची's hair It has almost become a mannerism with the Sanskrit कविऽ to drive at the prowess of their heroes by dilating upon its consequences such as the sad plight of the enemies widows (cp St 179)

 This makes it clear how a poet can adopt a picturesque mode of expression to convey some simple message of his This picturesque mode is पर्याय, which as दण्डिन् explains is प्रकारान्तर, भङ्ग्यन्तर a high flown style different (अन्तर) from the direct expression And the state ment employing this mode is therefore called पर्यायोक्त. Some like अभिनवगुप्त interpret the word पर्याय as व्यङ्गय पर्यायेण वाच्यादति-रिक्तप्रकारेण व्यङ्ग्येनोपणक्षितमुक्त अभिहित पर्यायोक्तम्। ( Cited in र ग ) They explain the name in the light of the message which the poet suggests in the figure.

 The **व्यङ्ग्य in पर्यायोक्त** —This bings us to the question of ब्यङ्ग्य in पर्यायोक्त The message the poet wants to give is the व्यङ्ग्य, in the figure. Normally we have seen that theवाच्यार्थ and व्यङ्ग्यार्थ of a वाक्य stand wide apart. But in the case of पर्यायोक्त, वाच्यार्थ and ब्यङ्ग्यार्थे are almost identical. This seems peculiar enough, but अ...... explain it by saying that here we get the identity of वाच्यार्थ and व्यङ्ग्यार्थं due to the poet adopting a mode for the वाच्य which is different from that of the व्यङ्ग्य. Read र गं —तेन पर्यायेण भङ्गयन्तरेण उक्तम् अनिहित व्यङ्ग्य यत्रति प्राचीनैर्निर्मित लक्षण व्यङ्ग्यत्व वाच्य...योर्विरोधगतमिति नाशङ्कनयन्। एकस्यैव प्रकारभेदेन वाच्य.........विराधत्।

 What is meant by **वाच्य--व्यङ्ग्ययोरैक्य** in पर्ययोक्त is that the two do not stand wide apart. One is practicilly the same as the other Since there is no rule defining the scope of व्यङ्ग्य within particular himits, we get व्यङ्ग्यऽ and व्यङ्ग्यऽ, some nearer the वाच्य than others.

Thus in तत्र हणावरोधाना etc., the वाच्य { that रघु by his exploit brought a ruddy hue to the cheeks of the हूण women) and the व्यङ्ग्य (that रघु vanquished the हूणऽ) are not as wide apart as the वाच्य and व्यङ्ग्य in निःशेषच्युतचन्दन स्तनतट etc. (3।2)

 **वाच्य— व्यङ्ग्य सम्बन्धऽ पर्यायोक्त** आलङ्कारिकऽ have attempted to analyse this identity in diversity As seen above they account for वाच्य and व्यङ्ग्य being (practically) the same as a result of प्रकारान्तराश्रयण And what is meant by प्रकारान्तराश्रयण will be clear if we recall the heads to which जगन्नाथ reduces all the प्रकारऽ He says (1) The वाच्य may be कार्य, the व्यङ्ग्य कारण (2) The बाच्य may be कारण, the व्यङ्ग्य कार्य, or ( 3 ) The वाच्य may be in some other relation like साहचर्य with the व्यङ्ग्य Read अयं चालङ्कार क्वचित् कारणेन वाच्येन कार्यस्य गम्यत्वे, क्वचिद् कार्यण कारणस्य, कचिद् उभयोदासीनेन सबधिमात्रेण सवधिमात्रस्य च इनि विपुलविषय। Of these the first is of frequent occurrence तत्र हूणावरोधाना illustrates it because therein the कार्य viz हूणसुदरीकपोल पाटलिमा is वाच्य and the कारण viz हूणपरानय is व्यङ्ग्य The second is illustrated by अपकुर्वद्भिरनिदा धृतराष्ट्र तवामतौ। उप्यन्ते मृत्युबीनानि पाण्डुपुत्रेषु निश्चितम्॥( र ग ) Here the कारण, कौरवनाशबीतावाय is वाच्य the वार्य, कौरवनाश is गम्य The third is illustrated by सूर्याचन्द्रमसौ यस्य वासो रज्जयन करै। अङ्गरागं सृतत्यग्निस्त वन्द परमेश्वरम्। Here सूर्याचद्रमसौ यस्य वासो रक्षयत have the वाच्यार्थ one whose garment is tinged by the Sun and the Moon and the व्यङ्ग्यार्थ one whose garment is sky and still these are not related by कार्यकारण सम्बन्ध, but by साहचर्य the sun and the moon with आकाश

 As said above of these the first is of usual occurrence In fact आलङ्कारिकऽ like **रुय्यक जयरथ** seem to refer to it alone as playing part in पर्यायोक्त Says **रुय्यक**— नहि तस्यैव तदैव तयैव विच्छित्त्या गम्यत्व वाच्यत्व च सभवति। अत कार्यमुखद्वारेणाभिधानम्। In fact जगन्नाथ refers to a view according to which the second कारण वाच्य and कार्य व्यङ्ग्य is thrown out for want of वैचित्र्य— एतेन कार्यात् कारणप्रतीतिवत् कारणात् कार्यप्रतीतेर्वै-चित्र्याभावात्—इति टीकारोक्तमपास्तम् The third head, though mentioned by र ग does not seem to be much in vogue among आलङ्कारिकऽ

 The question anses if वाच्य-व्यङ्ग are related as कार्य-कारण etc how are they regarded as अभिन्न in पयायोक्त? The answer lies in the fact that though the two are related as कार्य-कारण etc both are equally prominent in the poets eye In other words to the poet the वाच्य कार्य is as much प्रस्तुत as the व्यङ्ग्य कारण, to speak or one of the three प्रकारऽ Both are equally प्रतिपिपादयियित by him, so much so that there is no rish of one becoming अप्रस्तुत and the other प्रस्तुत It

would be helpful to recall in this connection the analogous case of व्याजस्तुति, where, as we pointed out, though the वाच्यार्थ and व्यङ्ग्यार्थ are स्तुति and निन्दा or orce versa, both are equally प्रकृत Read र ग–अप्रस्तुतेन कार्येण प्रस्तुतकारणावगमने अप्रस्तुतप्रशसैव। प्रस्तुतेन कार्येण प्रस्तुतस्यैव कारणस्यावगमन तु पर्यायोक्तस्य विषय इति **अलङ्कारसर्वस्वकारादिभि** प्राचीनै कृत विषयविभाग।

 **पर्यायोक्त vs व्याजस्तुति**—This brings us to the distinction between the two figures. Though in both there is व्यङ्ग्यार्थ not too sharply divided from the वाच्यार्थ, though in both there is भङ्ग्यन्तरसमाश्रय in the matter of the वाच्य, still (।) whereas in पर्यायोक्त, the वाच्य-व्यङ्ग्य are related as कार्य-कारण, कारण-कार्य, or by some other सबन्ध in व्याजस्तुति, the वाच्य-व्यङ्ग्य are related as निन्दा-स्तुति, or स्तुति-निन्दा (॥) That way पर्यायोक्त may be taken as a broader class, from which व्याजस्तुति, a smaller class developed into a separate figure.

 **पर्यायोक्त vs_अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा**– Though in पर्यायोक्त, the वाच्य and व्यङ्ग्य are related as कार्य-कारण or कारण कार्य, as in अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा in two varieties of which कार्य-कारण and करण कार्य are related as अप्रस्तुत-प्रस्तुत, still, as seen above (।) in पर्यायोक्त, both वाच्य and व्यङ्ग्य are प्रस्तुत, while in अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा वाच्य is अप्रस्तुत व्यङ्ग्य प्रस्तुत Read रुय्यक अत्र कार्यमुखद्वारेणाभिधानम्। काया देरपि तत्र प्रस्तुतत्वेन वर्णनार्हत्वात्। अत एव अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसातो भेद। See र ग cited above सा द –इह तु वर्णनीयस्य प्रभावतिशयबोधकत्वेन कार्यमपि कारणवत् प्रस्तुतनम्। (॥ ) According to the followers of the ध्वनि- school, in पर्यायोक्त, व्यङ्ग्य is वाच्यपर i e the व्यङ्ग्य is subserient to the वाच्य while in अ प्र, the वाच्य is व्यङ्ग्यपर i e, the वाच्य is subservient to the व्यङ्ग्य Read र ग—अस्मिश्चालङ्कारे व्यङ्ग्य वाच्यपरम्। अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसाया तु वाच्यं व्ययपरम्, इति ध्वनिकारानुयायिन।

 Though thus the two figures are differentiated, occasionally it is possible for one and the same stanza being assessed with equal Justice by one as पर्यायोक्त and by another as अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा Thus we find राजन् राजसुता etc. (St. 49) given by some आलङ्कारिकऽ like रुय्यक to illustrate पर्यायोक्त, while मम्मट gives it as an instance of अ. प्र In such doubtful cases, one has only to justify one’s view point One making a plea for पर्यायोक्त must show that both वाच्य-व्यङ्ग्य are equally प्रस्तुत and that वाच्य 15 merely a भङ्गयन्नर of the व्यङ्ग्य and more striking than it. And one who claims it to be must prove that the वाच्य is अप्रस्तुत व्यङ्ग्य प्रस्तुत due to there being a wide gulf between the वाच्य and व्यङ्ग्य Read मा च ..........................................................................................................................................

 पर्यायोक्त dates back from भामह and दण्डिन् भामह conceived it on lines adopted by fater आलङ्कारिकऽ. दण्डिन्’s पर्यायोक्त is different from that of others It consists in the employment of a pretext to achieve one’s aim. उद्भट’s definition is like भामह’s with slight new additions बामन did not admit the figure रुद्रट admitted it under the name पर्याय (१) रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित all admit it अप्पय्यदीक्षित admits in addition, a second variety on the pattern of दण्डिन्ऽ पर्यायोक्त

 **Essentials**— \पर्यायेण (भङ्ग्यन्तरेण) कथनात् पर्यायोक्तम्—मम्मट \[^2] The poet must resort to a वाच्य which, though circumlocutory, is more picturesque than the व्यङ्ग्य (2) The वाच्य व्यङ्ग्य are generally related as कार्य-कारण ( 3 ) In some cases, they may be related by कारण—कार्य सम्बन्ध or even by some other सम्बन्ध like साहचर्य. (4) In the poet's eye and to the सहृदय both वाच्य व्यङ्ग्य must be almost identical and both प्रतिपिपादयिषित e sought to be conveyed (5) Both must therefore be प्रस्तुत

[^2]: ।

 § **सू ५१ पर्यायोक्त** etc Construe— **पर्यायोक्त** etc वाच्य वाचकत्वेन विना यद् वच i e A statement not by a direct mode of expression (वाच्य-वाचकत्व ), but by suggestion (अवगमनव्यापार– as the वृत्ति says )

 **वृत्ति**– वाच्यवाचकभावय्यतिरिक्तेन **अवगमनव्यापारेण** By the process of suggestion which is distinct from straightforward expression वाच्य—वाचकभाव operates where the matter sought to be conveyed is conveyed directly **पर्यायेण भङ्गयन्तरेण कथनात् पर्यायोक्तम्** The name explained पर्याय is भङ्ग्यन्तर-प्रकारान्तर a different mode, a stylistic vanant र ग explains येन रूपेण विवक्षितोऽर्थस्त-दतिरिक्त प्रकारोभङ्ग्यन्तरम्। आक्षेपो वा। We have already seen how this भङ्ग्यन्तर amounts to (1) कारणे प्रतिपिपादयिषिते कार्यप्रतिपादन, or ( 2 ) कार्ये प्रतिपिषादयिषित कारणप्रतिपादन Or (3) सम्बध्यन्तरे प्रतिपिपादयिषिते सम्बध्यन्तरप्रतिपादन Of these the first प्रकार is the most often referred to, as shown already. Read **जयरथ**— अभिधयिमान हि कार्यं तदविनाभावित्वात् स्वसिद्ध्ये कारणमाक्षिपति इति गम्यमपि तद्वाच्यायमान इति यदेव गम्यते तस्यैव भङ्ग्यन्तरेणाभिधानम् भङ्गि (or ङ्गी ) 13 प्रकार a roundabout way of expression Note that acc. to the आलङ्कारिकऽ the वाच्यार्थ is more striking than व्यङ्ग्य, in this figure कु.—यदेव गम्य विवक्षित तस्यैव भङ्ग्यन्तरेण विवक्षितरूपादपि चारुतरेण केनचिद् रूपान्तरेण अभिधान पर्यायोक्तम् Again रुय्यक (at अप्रस्तुतप्रशमा)—तत्र (पर्यायोक्ते) हि कारणापेक्षया कार्यस्यातिशयेन सौन्दर्यमिति तदेव वर्णितम्।

 **St. 113 व प्रेक्ष्य etc** Illustrates पर्यायोक्त Description of the plight of ऐरावण and हरि (इन्द्र) who at the sight of हयग्रीव (or रावण १) were weaned of ichor and pride respectively Construe –यं प्रेक्ष्य मदेन ऐरावण

मुखे चिररूढापि निवासप्रीतिरुज्झिता। मानेन हरे (= इन्द्रस्य) हृदये चिररूढापि निवासप्रीतिरुज्झिता। य हयग्रीव or रावण चिररूढापि निवासप्रीति i e. ऐरावण’s मद had never subsided upto now, for want of one who could tame him continued to be vain until now for want of a match हरि=इद्र ऐरावण or ऐरावत-Indra s elephant. So called because he was churned out of the ocean (= ऐराबत्)

 Here the व्यङ्ग्य as the वृत्ति says is that ऐरावण and इन्द्र were respect ively weaned of ichor and vanity. But it is conveyed by the words, मदमानाभ्या निवासप्रीतिरुज्झिता, which are indeed more charming than the व्यङ्ग्य Note that here the वाच्य is कारण, व्यग्य कार्य, निवासप्रीतित्याग being responsible for मदमानमुक्ति Thus is therefore पर्यायोक्त

 But if the व्यङ्ग्य were viewed as conquest of स्वर्ग by इयग्रीव, the वाच्यार्थ will be the कार्य, the effect of स्वर्गजय, the कारण In other words, the stanza will describe the effects on ऐरावण and इन्द्र of the conquest of heaven by हयग्रीव This indeed would be a better appreciation But the वृत्ति appreciates it in the way given in the preceding para

 वृत्ति—अत्र ऐरावणशक्तौ etc. व्यङ्ग्यमपि शब्देनोच्यते Though suggested is yet expressed by the words the व्यङ्ग्य K is almost the same as वाच्य अपि and shows that normally व्यङ्ग्य and वाच्य stand wide apart, but here they are almost Identical. तेन यदेव उच्यते तदेव म्यग्नयम् Note that वाच्य and व्यङ्ग्य, even in पर्यायोक्त are not absolutely one, but almost one This is due to the gulf between them being narrowed down to its minimum in this figure यया तु व्यङ्ग्य न तया उच्यते i e. because वाच्य is expressed in a different mode,प्रकार, भङ्गि In simple words, वाच्य IS presented in the उदाहरण, as कारण of the व्यङ्ग्य which as कार्य Thus थया etc., so far as the instance goes, means यथा कार्यरूपं व्यङ्ग्य न तथा उच्यो, कारमरूपेण उच्यमानत्वात् In short there is अर्थाभेद but प्रकारभेद

 यथा गौ etc. If anyone asks how we can have the same and as both वाच्य and व्यङ्ग्य the reply is that it is possible owing to प्रकारभेद i e वाच्य being expressed in a different way from the one in which the व्यङ्ग्य is felt. To show how प्रकारभेद can lead to the same अर्थ, मम्मट adduces the instance of निर्विकल्पक and सविकल्पक प्रभेदा in the field of adJust as we apprehend the same cow, in two ways in the form of निर्विकल्पक and सविकल्पक प्रभेदा, so the same अर्थ may be conveyed by adopting different suls i e eवध्यmay be वर्ष, the व्यङ्ग्य प्रकरण and so on.  

 निर्विकल्पक and सविकल्पक प्रभेदा.—These are two successive stages of.... or direct perception Before we get a determinate cognition (.......)like ...., wheren we asociate with the particular obyct faffeers and nfners

attributes like गोत्व शुक्लत्व चलत्व we must have passed through a stage wherein the object is comprehended merely as existing (किमपि इव) related to any distinctive attributes like गोत्व etc. That is why निर्विकल्पकज्ञान is defined as विशेषण-विशेष्यसम्बन्धानवगाहि ज्ञानं विकल्प being the disti-nctive attributes.

 सविकल्पक प्रत्यक्ष follows निर्विकल्पक and consists in the particular object being viewed in relation to particular attributes like गोत्व etc. That is why it is defined as विशेषण विशेष्यसबधावगाहि ज्ञानम् Read **साङ्ख्यतत्वकौमुदी**— समुग्ध वस्तुमात्र तु प्राग्गृह्णात्यविक-ल्पनम्। तत्सामान्यविशेषाभ्या कल्पयन्ति मनीषिण।

 गौ शुक्लश्चलति इति विकल्प Referrang to सविकल्पक stage **विकल्प** is **प्रकार यदेव दृष्ट तदेव विकल्पयति** i e यत्रेव निर्विकल्पकत्वेन दृष्ट तत्रेव सविकल्पकत्वेन पश्यति। i e the same object is विषय for both निर्विकल्पक and सविकल्पक

 न तु यथा दृष्ट तथा In निर्विकल्पक stage it is perceived unrelated to any attributes In सविकल्पक it is perceived as related to them.The wording is apt to mislead one into thinking that in सविकल्पक one apprehends the object not exactly as it is seen. But the meaning actually sought to be conveyed is येन प्रकारण निर्विकल्पकज्ञानविषयीक्रियते न तेनैव प्रकारेण सविकल्पकज्ञानविषयीक्रियते।

 **यत्र अभिन्न-अससृष्टत्वेन दृष्ट भेद-ससगाभ्या विकल्पयति—** In निर्विकल्पक stage the object as perceived as undistinguished(अभिन्न) by जाति or गुण and uncornected (अससृष्ट) with क्रिया In सर्विकल्पक stage it is perceived as distinguished (विकल्पयति ) by distinctions (भ.. ) like जाति or गुण (गौ शुक्ल) and connection (ससर्ग ) with क्रिया (चल\*)

 This interpretation is based on **सम्प्रदायप्रकाशिनी** with shght modifications The modifications were necessary as regards जाति It seems that acc to the Commentary जाति is perceived in the निर्विकल्पक stage But this is unwarranted Read गो शुक्लत्वे चलत्वेऽपि प्रथमाक्षसनिपाते सामान्यावगति। भेदा व्यावृत्ति गुणकृता। ससर्गोऽन्वय क्रियाकृत। क्रोगकृतविशेष सामान्य अभिन्न— अससृष्टत्वेन पश्यति। अथ विकल्पम्य शब्दोल्लखनियतत्वाद् विकल्पयति।

 There is another interpretation which seems too laboured to be true According to it the author gives the views of the Buddhists and Grammarians both regarding निर्विकल्पक and सविकल्पक. According to the former in निर्विकल्पक stage, the object is apprehended not as market of (अभिन्न-अध्यावृत्त) from others सविकल्पक the object is perceived as marked off (भिन्न) from others Acc. to them when we get the सविकल्पक गौ अयम् we have reached a stage where the object is marked off from others i e we realise नाय अश्वः नाय महिष etc

This stage of marking off is not reached in निर्विकल्पक. Acc. to the वैयाकरणऽ, on the other hand, in निर्विकल्पक stage, the object is unconnected (अससृष्ट ) with जाति, गुण or क्रिया In सविकल्पक stage it is so connected (ससृष्ट).

** ३३ A –उदात्त (१)**

 भामह and दण्डिन् place समाहित between पर्यायोक्त and उदात्त and so does उद्भट also It could be safely concluded therefore that मम्मट was following his predecessors in placing उदात्त after पर्यायोक्त He treats of समाधि, which corresponds roughly to the समाहित of his predecessors, later (Fig. 46 )

 उदात्त (2) is exuberance, superabundance. The poet, the प्रजापति of अपार काव्यसंसार, ( अपारे काव्यसंसारे कबिरेकः प्रजापति) has always taken delight in regaling the सहृदय with exaggerated descriptions of the superabundance of things, maybe wealth, flora and fauna, virtues or penance. The descriptions of pearls and diamonds galore in the Arabian Nights, extravagantly fabulous as they are, have never ceased to charm the young and old alike. How we like the description of his mansion by the यक्ष (मेघदूत, उत्तरमेष, १३, १४, १६) with its well of emeralds, its lotuses with stalks of lapis lazuli, its क्रीडाशैल made of sapphires, and its hedge of plantain trees of gold’ The description of the कुमारीपुर (Princess Chamber) in बाण's कादम्बरी particularly where directions are given to the परि..न, is another case in point. One servant is ordered to spread
रत्नवालुका’ Another is asked to protect दालिमीफल from the parrots by covering them with pearl nets’ A third is ordered to sweep a dais of emerald with a broom of gold’ These descriptions have for the सहृदय the same lure as the buccaneers of the Spanish main had for the wealth of El Dorado!

 But as said above, उदात्त (१) is not confined only to gold and jewels. The poet may sometimes give a less glamorous description of the superabundance of other things also Wealth of virtues, wealth of penance wealth of flowers, fruits, animals, etc. when described hyperbolically result in this figure. One feature is common to all these cases and it is that the poet exaggerates things therein. That is what distinguishes उदात्त of this vanety from स्वभावोक्ति and भाविक where, as in उदात्त we get descriptions, but such as are वास्तविक, never अतिशयोक्ति मूलक Read **रुय्यक**—स्वभावोक्ती भाविके व यथावत् वस्तुवर्णनम्।तद्विपक्षत्वेन आरोपित.... उदात्तस्य अवसर। ..............युक्तस्य वस्तुनो वर्गनं ........ उदात्तम्। Note that the superabundance

should be of good things only It will be a misnomer if super abundance of bad things is called उदात्त

 **उदात्त (१) vs अतिशयोक्ति**—This brings us to the question of the relation between these figures उदात्त (१) does necessarily depend upon अतिशयोक्ति, and to be more specific, अतिशयोक्ति of असम्बधे सम्बध class, for it is असम्बधे सम्बध when the poet credits one with wealth etc not really possessed by one It will be proper, therefore to conclude that अतिशयोक्ति that has superabundance of good things for its विषय is necessarily उदात्त(२) In other words उदात्त(२) isअतिशयोक्ति, that has developed into a separate figure

 उदात्त(१) is a figure dating from भामह and दण्डिन् भामह, how ever, seems to attribute it to others ( एतदेवापरेऽन्येन व्याख्यानेनान्यथा विदु। नानारत्नादियुक्त सत्तत् किलोदात्तमुच्यते॥) उद्भट recognised it Neither बामन nor रूद्रट recognised it—possibly thinking it to be अतिशयोक्ति रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, and अप्पयदीक्षित recognised it. The figure is not to be found in the extant portion of जगन्नाथ’s रसगं-गाधर मम्मट admitted it.

 **Essentials**— \[ उदात्त ad\] full of exuberance Used as noun. A figure based on exuberance उत्कर्षेण मादीयते गृह्यते स्म इत्युदात्तं झळ्कीवर,(उत्+आत्त)\] (।) The poet must describe the superabundance of things (॥) The superabundance must be exaggerated (अतिशयोक्ति मूलक), and not वास्तविक cp **जयरथ**—सामान्यमानविभूतिक्तस्य तु वर्णन नैतदङ्गम्। (III) The things whose exuberance is described must be good or noble or dignified Thus a poet may give an exaggerated description of a forest teeming with lions or tigers or deer, but the description of a mosquito nidden pool would become ludicrous (iv) The theme for उदात्त may be riches virtues, flora and fauna etc (cp **दण्डिन् आशयस्य** विभूतेर्वा य महत्त्वमनुत्तमम्। आशय—मनोव्यापारविशेष )

 § सू ५२ वस्तुन सपत् Superabundance of a thing As seen above, the things must be good noble सपत् means here सपद्वर्णनम्

 **वृत्ति समृद्धियोग** सपत् explained Possession of superabundance, by a party, an object etc.

 ** St 114 मुक्ता केलिविसूत्र** etc. Illustrates (२) Description of the fabulous wealth that the learned possessed thanks to the generosity of that patron of learning भोज **केलिविसूत्रहारगलिता** The pearls that had dropped down due to the threads of necklaces snapping by the violent jerks of dallian-ce. The pearls that fell down were later swept into the courtyard. There they got coloured with the

red lac-dye of the maidens’ feet and were mistaken therefore for pomegranate seeds by the parrots. Construe यद् विद्वद्भवनेषु......मुक्ता केलीशुका कर्षन्ति तद् भोजनृपनेस्त्यागलीलायितम् । **त्यागलीलायितम्** Play of the generosity **भोज** (1010-1055 A. D.) of famous parmar family. a famous patron of learning and author of सरस्वतीकण्ठाभरण, a work,on Rhetonics.

 Here we have the wealth of the learned given in such colours, that it is too good to be true. Hence it is उदात्त (2)

**३३ B-उदात्त (२)**

 The second variety of उदात्त consists in the poet’s attempt to exalt the matter under consideration by making some eminent person an adjunct to it “This is the place where Tänaji died fighting ”—says the guide to a visitor to Sinhagad, and we have an उदात्त. “thus is the spot where Columbus first set his foot on American soil, says the guide to the tourist, and we get an उदात्त. In such cases, the object under consideration happens to be exalted by its lofty assoc-iations with some eminent personahty When in the opening stanza of the **मेघदूत** the रामगिर्याश्रम is described as जनकतनयास्नानपुण्योदय, we get a like effect owing to जानकी being presented as an adjunct to the रामगिर्याश्रम In the **विक्रमोर्वशीय,** the सगमनीय मणि certainly looks sublime when described as शैलसुताचरणरागयोनि \[सगमनीयो मणिरिह शैलसुताचरणरागयो-निरयम्।\]. How lofty does the lion कुम्भोद्भव look when he describes himself as being in the service of शिव \[ कैल्यासगौर शरमा-रुमक्ष्य पादार्पणानुग्रहपूतपृष्ठम्। अवेहि मां किङ्करमष्टमूर्ते॥( रघुवंश २३५) \] बाण while describing the शाल्म-लीवृक्ष refers to a तरुखण्ड which was shattered by the arrows of राम (राघवशरप्रहारजर्दरिननीर्णताल-खण्ड) His description of the अगस्त्याश्रम is bristling with such august associations.

 In short, in this variety of उदात्त the प्रकृत is presented with some eminent person as its adjunct. To put it otherwise, in such cases the being the theme of description is the अगिन् or उपल्य and the emunent personality (उदात्तपुरुष) is its अङ्ग or उपक्षग. And that explains the name a उदात्त Read रुय्यकः—महापुरुषाणाम् उदात्त........भावेन उपनिबध्यमानं चरितम् उदात्तम्। ..........

 **उदात्त(१) vs.उदात्त (२)**— उदात्त(१), as we saw,isessentially bacsed on अतिशयोक्ति. उदात्त(२) on the other hand, may or may not be based onअतिशयोक्ति. When the poet describes an historical fact, there will be no अतिशयोक्ति. But when the fact is not historical, who can say that

there is no अतिशयोक्ति involved? Thus शैलसुताचरणरागयोनि as applied to सगमनीय, is likely to involve an, though there may be none in जनकतनयास्तानपुण्योदकेषु as applied to रामगिर्याश्रम But one thing is clear that while उदात्त (१) depends on अतिशयोक्ति for its charm, उदात्त (२) derives it from महता अगमाव.

 That way they are two distinct figures Even भामह He admitted उदात्त (2) but was non-commattal as regardsउदात्त (१) दण्डिन्,उद्भट admitted both वामन did not admit it रुद्रट like भामह admitted उदात्त (२) but not उदात्त (१) seems to take it as a separate figure when he says: उदात्तशब्दसाम्यादिह अभिधानम्। विश्वनाथ and अप्पय्यदीक्षित admitted both उदात्त (१) and उदात्त (2) जगन्नाथ’s **र.गं**, as said above, has no उदात्त मम्मट admitted both

 ***Essentials—*** \ उदात्तचरिताना अङ्गत्वात् उदात्तम्– **रुय्यक** \[^3] The poet must sublime an object by recalling its associations with lofty persons (ii) This may or may not involve अतिशयोक्ति. (iii) When the fact is historical, or vouched for by tradition or other authority, there will be no अतिशयोक्ति But अतिशयोक्ति is sure to be there when no authority is available for the fact mentioned (iv) The object described is the अगिन् and the great person an अग thereto

[^3]: i

 **§ सू ५३ महता etc Construe** महता उपलक्षण च (उदात्ताल्पारो भवति)। उपलक्षण=अङ्गभाव as the वृत्ति says. It consists in presenting the great as adjuncts to the प्रकृत

 **वृत्ति— **अर्थादुपलक्षणीये अर्धेः—i e प्रकृते, the matter to be described If the great are to be उपलक्षण, naturally (अर्थात्) they are to be उपलक्षण to the प्रकृत, which is उपलक्षणीय,

 **St 115 तदिदमरण्य** etc illustrates उदात्त (२) (3) A forest has its associations with राम recalled दशरथ etc who courted distress(व्यसन) in order to enable his father to keep his word (viz. promise to कैकेयी) बाहुसहाय i e there was none else to help him

 Here the forest in question happens to be exalted as the poet recalls its associations with राम, who therefore becomes अग thereto Note that the stanza is taken from रुद्रट's काव्यालवार (७१०४) Was रुद्रट indebted to भामह for the idea? Cp भामह's उदाहरण— उदात्तशक्तिमान् रामो गुरूवाक्यानुरोधकः। विहायोपनत राज्य यथा वनमुपागमत्॥

 **न चाग्र वीरो रस** etc. As the stanza embodies a reference to the extirpation of the demons by राम single-handed, one is likely to suppose that here we have a काव्य wherein वीररस is sugge-sted promi

nently. And if रम becomes प्रधान, the काव्य will belong to ध्वनि class, while it is given here to illustrate an अर्थालङ्कार which means that the काव्य is taken to be अवर (or अधम cp शब्दचित्र वाच्यचित्र अव्यङ्ग्य त्ववर स्मृतम्। उ।) So मम्मट is nodding here, one might feel

 But justifies the instance as one of अधमकाव्य He says In the stanza is not conspicuous, but holds only a secondary postion This is due to the fact that the प्रकृत, अरण्य, is in the forefront in the stanza, and whatever reference to वीररस is there is naturally sub servient to it

 And if रस is only subservient well can the काव्य be अधम, as it is claimed to be by मम्मट

 रस and अलकार.—There is a controversy as to the status of रस as an अलङ्कार One school led by उद्भट says that when रस is prominent the figure is रसवत्, when रस is subservient the figure is उदात्त ( २ ) As against thus the ध्वनि-school holds that रसवत् अलङ्कार occurs only where रस is subservient This is उदात्त ( २), they suggest Read स प्र .—येषा **उद्भटादीना** प्रधानभूतो रस एव रसवान्, तन्मते अङ्गभूतो रसो द्वितीयोदात्तत्वेन सभवति। **ध्वनिदर्शने** तु प्रधानभूतस्य रसस्य अलङ्कार्यत्वात् अगभूतो रसो रसवान्। स एव द्वितीयोदात्तत्वेन व्यवह्रियते। **मम्मट** does not recognise रसवत् अलङ्कार at all **विश्वनाथ** gives in detail different views re रस and अलङ्कार, while treating of भावोदय, भावसधि and भावशवल अलङ्कारऽ One thing is clear that in उदात्त (२), the recalling of the associations results in रसोत्पत्ति, may-be of a mild type

**३४A --समुच्चय (१)**

 समुच्चय is combination and the first variety of the figure does indeed consist in a combination of causes acting in concert When we speak of combination of causes in समुच्चय we must not suppose that they together form the सामग्री or totality of causes necessary to produce the effect. Because here each कारण is by itself capable of producing the कार्य They are placed in a combination by the poet only to heighten the poetical effect .The poet never means nor says that by their coming together the effect is hastened or expedited as in the figure समाधि आलङ्कारिकऽ say that the causes come together in समुच्चय in the same way in which pigeons swarm together on the threshing floor (खलेकपोतिकान्याय) Read **र ग**—अस्मिंस्तु समुच्चयप्रभेदे यत्र एककार्यं सपादयितु युगपदनेके खले कपोता इव अहमहमिकया सपतन्ति कार्यस्य च न कोऽपि अतिशय।

 शुकनास says to चन्द्रापीड **(कादम्बरी)** गर्भेश्वरत्व-अभिनवयौवनत्व अप्रतिमरूपत्व अमानुषशक्तित्व चेति महती इय खलु अनर्थपरपरा। सर्वाविनयानामेकैकमपि एषा आयतन किमुत

समवाय। Here we have समुच्चय, as so many causes, each equally potent to produce the effect, are described as acting in concert कुल रूप वयो विद्या धन च मदयन्त्यमुम्। (कु ) embodies a समुच्चय, as each one कुल etc., is equally potent enough to produce मद In राज्यभ्रशो, वनेवासौ, दूरे माता, पिता मृत। एवैकमपि तद्दुःख यदन्धिमपि शोषयेत्॥( cited by नमिसाधु on अ स ७२३ ) we get a very effective समुच्चय, descnbing राम’s lot when he was exited into the forest. When the ऋषिऽ in कुमारसम्भव (६८२) say उमा वधूर्भवान् दाता याचितार श्मे वयम्। वर शभुरल ह्येष तद्कुलोद्भूतये विधि॥we get another happy समुच्चय

 These causes may be all good सन् or रम्य, all bad, असत् or अरम्य, or each one of them may be good and bad from different angles, सदसत् or रम्यारम्य Thus we get सद्योग असद्योग or सदसद्योग in समुच्चय (१) A good thing is such as Is उपादेय, a bad one is such as Is अनुपादेय Sometimes, however, सत् 1s whatever 13 helpful to the particular matter in hand Whatever thwarts it is bad Thus नव वय though not bad in itself is bad indeed for the विरहिणी Thus we get the following scheme—

समुच्चय ( १ )        

 ————————————————————————

          सद्योगे                  
असद्योगे सदसद्योग  
          (st. 117) (st. 116) ( st. 118 )

 र ग gives another division also, असत्सना or अरमणीयरमणीयानां योगे e g शरीर ज्ञानजनन, रोगा विष्णुस्मृतिप्रद। विपद् वैराग्यजननी त्रय सुसकरं सताम्॥In सदसद्योग, we have a set of things each one of which is normally good, but bad under certain conditions In असत्सद्योग, on the other hand, we have things normally bad becoming good under certain conditions.

 For समुच्चय (१) vs समाधि see समाधि

 रुद्रट was possibly the first to conceive this variety of the figure. After hira मम्मट, रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पयदीक्षित all admitted it practically on the same lines with सद्योग, असद्योग and सदसद्योग as the basis.

 Essentials — \[ हेतूनां (एस्पोनिकान्यायेन ) समुच्चय अत्र \] (।) There must be circumstances, two or more in number, all equally potent to produce the effect in question (॥) The more the circumstances the better the poetical charm. (…) The circumstances may all of them be good or all bad or each one good and bad under different

aspects (iv) Good circumstances should not be mixed up with bad ones, lest there might result क्रमभेददोष —violation of harmony (v) The poet gives such an array of हेतुऽ, when even one is able to produce the effect, in order to lend colour to the statement (vi) The poet will not say or suggest that the कारणसमुच्चय hastens the कार्ये

 § **सू ५४ तत्सिद्धि** etc Construe—यत्र एकस्मिन् तन्सिद्धिहेतौ अन्यत् तत्कर हेत्वन्तर भवेत् असी समुच्चय। **एकस्मिन् तत्सिद्धिहेतौ'** This makes it clear that all the हेतुऽ together are not सामग्री or totality of causes सामग्री, all the कारणऽ must come together and then only will the कार्य be produced, while here each हेतु is **कार्यसाधनममर्थं** तत् =कार्यं **अन्यत्** ( साधकान्तर or हेत्वन्तर ) Note that the singular should not be taken as strictly indicating the presence of one other cause As said above, the more numerous the हेतुऽ, the better So, in all there may be two or more हेतुऽ See how the वृत्ति, interprets अन्यत् as साधकान्तराणि (Plural)

** वृति—साधकान्तराणि** Note how the singular अन्यत् of the सूत्र is interpreted as plural As said above numerous हेत्वन्तरऽ lend charm to the figure

** St 116 दुर्वारा** etc Illustrates असद्योगे समुच्चय The plight of a विरहिणी when all circumstances conspire to make विरह unbearable **कुल निर्मलम्** that comes in the way of wanton behaviour **स्त्रीत्व धैर्यं विरोधि** i e a female cannot remain patient **काल वसन्त कृतान्त अक्षम** Because he does not hasten her death which would have spared her this worry **चतुरा** like बकुलावलिका in **मालविकाग्निमित्र**, managing to bring about the union

 Here स्मरमार्गणा दूरत्व of प्रियतम अस्त्युत्सुक्त्व of मनस्, etc are all हेतुऽ acting in concert to make विरह unbearable when each one was enough to produce the same result Note that नव वय, निर्मल कुल are असत् in the particular context Read **रुय्यक**—नववयप्रभृतीना च यद्यपि स्वतशोभनत्य, तथापि विरहविषयत्वेन अत्र अशोभनत्व श्रेयम्। This is therefore असद्योगे समुच्चय

** अत्र विरहासहत्व** etc i e each हेतु like स्मरमागणा is enough to make विरह unbearable **तदुपरि** over and above that By giving such an array of हेतुऽ the poet shows how desperate the case is एष एव etc मम्मट means to say that the very समुच्चय embraced by the definttion (एष एवं) may turn out to be सद्योगे, असद्योगे and सदसद्योगे in specific cases These three do not call for separate definitions (न पृथक् लक्ष्यते) because they fit into the definition already given **मा चं** says

रुद्रटवत् न लक्ष्यते ( But रुद्रट does not define them separately. He only enumerates these as standing exclusive of सुखापत, दुःखावह समुच्चय.

 **St** **117 कुलममलिनं** etc Illustrates सद्योगे समुच्चय A poet's conpliment to a king that enviable circumstances like nobility etc. that would have made another person insolent make him a man of restraint अल भुतबल (अर Indeclinable ) enough strength of arms, स्फीता copious. अगण्डित प्रभुत्व sovereignty that has not suffered a break अङ्कुशा i. e prevent him from deviating from the proper path

 Here अमलिन कुल, भद्रा मूर्ति etc. are all हतुऽ that restrain the king from becoming wayward Each one of these is enough to produce the result in question Since all these circumstances are सत, it is सद्योगे समुच्चय Note how अमलिन कुल is सद्धेतु here, while it was, due to context, असद्धेतु in the preceding stanza (निर्मल कुल) त एव तवाङ्कुशा embodies रूपक that theकार्य is only implted in अकुशा

 उक्तोदाहरणे i e St. 116 दुर्वारा etc

 **St.** **118 शशी दिवसधूसरो** etc. Illustrates सदसद्योगे समुच्चय The poet feels sorry for some things that are normally good, turning bad when conditioned by certain circumstances **दिवसधूसर** the moon be comes dim duning daytime मुखमनक्षर स्वाकृते ref to a handsome person without learning अनक्षरं because he knows nothing

  Here शशिन्, that is generally सत्, becomes असत् dunng daytime,कामिनी that is सत् becomes असत् owing to ग...यैवनत्व सरस् which is सत् becomes असत् when without lotuses, the lovely face of the handsome which is सत् is असत् owing to अनक्षरत्व, प्रभु who is सत् (because he maintains people) is असत्, because of धनपरायणत्व, and सजन who is सत् becomes असत् (i e his lot is unenviable) owing to his constant musenes. These are therefore सत् things that become असत् under certain conditions. Each one of these is capable of becoming a शक्य and still the poet gives a number of them. Hence it is सदसद्योगे समुच्चय. Note that ......... is a रुपक कार्य of the हेतुऽ viz rankling is implicit in the word इज्य Note that the stanza would have become तुल्ययोगिता 'n with the reading मनसि ......, because then the धर्म in singular stated once only would have conveyed आपम्य between the units. With सत and the plural इत्यादि गम्य सादृश्य, en was becomes impossible.

........ has to be overlooked because it is not in tune with the rest of the genes. For...., is not सत् becoming असत् under some

circumstances Not only that, but its presence constitutes the flaw of प्रक्रमभग which consists in an abrupt break of the motif, due to failure to cornvey an idea with uniformity Read **रुय्यकः** —ननु नृपाङ्गणगत खल इत्य शोमनो अन्ये तु शोमना इति कथ समुच्चीयमानस्य सत तादृशेन असता योग। नैतत्। 'नृपाङ्गणगत खल' इति प्रत्युत प्रक्रमभङ्गाद्दृष्टमेव। न तु सौन्दर्यनिमित्तमित्युपेक्ष्यमेवैनत्।

 Could we not make नृपाङ्गणगत खुल. fit into the scheme of the stanza by saying that खल is असत् while the rest are सत् and that hence the stanza well illustrates सदसद्योगे समुच्चय? That is to say, सदसद् should be interpreted as a द्वद्व, सद् च असत् च ( सत् and असत् ), and not as a कर्मधारय सत् च तत् असत् च ( whatever is सत् becoming असत्)

 But आलङ्कारिकऽ like जगन्नाथ point out that if सदसत् were taken as a द्वद्व ( सत् च अमत् च), समुच्चय would present a heterogeneous assortment of सत and असत्, and then it will suffer from सहचरभिन्नता दोष which results in प्रक्रमभगदोष Therefore we should take सदसत् as a कर्मधारय not a द्वन्द्व Read र
ग— रमणीयारमणीयशब्दे कर्मधारय आश्रीयते न द्वद्व। सहृचरभिन्नत्वदोषापत्ते। And one can easily see how there is greater poetical charm in pre senting one and the same thing as good and bad Read **रा** **तर्कवागीश** ( सा द ) —तथा च अत्र विशेषणविशेष्ययोर्योग एवं वैचेयाक्हा न तु विशिष्टाना योग। क्रमभेद रूपदोषावहत्वादिति भाव।

 ** अत्र शशिनि** etc. शल्ये i e when each one is enough to prove a शल्य, others are presented **इति शोभनाशोभनयोग** The वृत्ति does not show clearly whether सदसत् is taken as a द्वद्व or कर्मधारय by मम्मट

**३४B–समुच्चय (२)**

 If in ससुच्चय (१) the charm lies in the यौगपद्य of several हेतुऽ in समुच्चय ( २ ) it consists in the यौगपद्य of गुणऽ or क्रियाऽ or गुणक्रियाऽ. अग्निमित्र in his cajolery of इरावती says वर्धयसि विलसित त्व दासजनायात्र कुप्यसि च॥(**मालविकाग्निमित्र** ३ २२ ) you heighten the romance what time you get angry Here there is simultanesty between the क्रियाऽ विलसित वर्धयसि and कुप्यसि con veyed by च In प्रादुर्भवति पयोक्ते कज्जलमलिन बभूव नम। रक्त च पथिकहृदय (र. ग) we get simultanenty of गुणऽ In आपूर्णश्च कलाभिरिन्दुरमलो यातश्च राहोर्मुख ( **मालतीमाधव** ९ ) we get simultanenty of गुण (1) आपूर्णत्व and क्रिया राहुमुखगमन

 But when we speak of simultanesty in समुच्चय (२) we must not be too ngorous आलकारिकऽ like **जयरथ** and **जगन्नाथ** extend the scope of the figure even to cases where गुणऽ or क्रियाऽ follow each other in

close successton Says र ग —युगपदिति क्रमव्यावृत्त्यर्थम्। न त्वेकक्षणप्रतिपत्त्यर्थन्। तेन किञ्चित्कालभेदेऽपि न समुच्चयभङ्ग। Again read **जयरथः**— यद्यप्यन्त्र ( i e in the उदाहरण in अ स—शेते शुष्यति etc.) शयनादीना न यौगपद्येन अवस्थान, तथापि तन्नेरनर्येण शेयम्। About the same stanza **कुवलयानद** says अत्र कामाचित् क्रियाणा किञ्चित्कालभेदमभवेऽपि शतपत्रपत्रशनभदन्यायेन योगपद्य।

** समुच्चय (२) vs कारकदीपक** —But a relaxation of the rule of यौगपद्य is likely to make समुच्चय ( २ ) – of क्रियाऽ encroach upon the field of वद्धकदीपक For, then what is there to distinguish between स्विद्यति कूणति, वेलति etc (st 67), given as उदाहरण of कारकदीपक by **मम्मट,** and शेते शुष्यति ताम्यति प्रल्पति प्रम्लयति भ्राम्यति। प्रेरक्युल्लिखति प्रणश्यति दलत्युन्मूर्च्छति त्रुश्यति॥gaven to illustrate क्रियाममुच्चय by **रुय्यक** ? To say that there is गम्य औषम्य III कारकदीपक and none in समुच्चय is futile, as we have already seen under कारकदीपक So it is obvious that any relaxation of the requirement of यौगपद्य will be on peril of tresspassing on the sphere of कारकदीपक Thus जयरथ's appraisement of स्विद्यति कूणनि etc. as a समुच्चय cannot hold water \[ See जयरथ cited at p 124 Notes \] मम्मट to judge from his instances, is very scrupulous about यौगपद्य
