 समुच्चय (२), in so far as it relates to क्रियाऽ (not गुणेऽ), bears resemblance to कारकदीपक, where several क्रियाऽ have one कारक whereas (1) in समुच्चय (२), the क्रियाऽ are simultaneous, in कारकदीपक they follow one after another (ii) In समुच्चय ( २ ) the charm lies in यौगपद्य in कारकदीपक it consists in the कारक being the same (iii) in समुच्चय, the क्रियाऽ may be sometimes भिन्नकर्तृक in कारकदीपक they must be एककर्तृक (iv) In समुच्चय (२) even two क्रियाऽ simultaneously occurring, can make the figure charming In कारकदीपक the charm consists in several क्रियाऽ being related to the same कारक

 For **समुच्चय** vs **असगति** see **असङ्गति**

 **समुच्चय vs अतिशयोक्ति–** Though no आलङ्कारिक except विश्वनाथ, is explicit on the point, a good many instances of गुणयौगपद्य and क्रिया यौगपद्य are found to resolve into कारणगुणस्य-कार्यगुणस्य च यौगपद्य and कारणक्रियाया- कार्यक्रियायाश्च यौगपद्य In plain words, we find, in a good many विवाया कार्यकिरयाच योगान cases of this figure one गुण producing another and yet both described as simultaneous and one क्रिया producing another and yet both describ. ed as simultaneous. Let us take मम्मट's unstance of गुणयौगपद्य विगलितसकल etc. (St. 119) Here the वैमन्ययुग of the victors and मलिन्ययुग of the vanquished are described as simultaneous, and it is not difficult to see how victors बैमन्य is the cause of the मलिन्य of the faces of the

vanquished कार्य and कारण are described as simultaneous for hinting at the शीघ्रकारिता of the कारण Or, m the words of **तर्कवागीश,** झटिति फलोत्पादनप्रतिपत्त्यर्थम् Similarly, in the instance, धुनोति चासिं तनुते च कीर्तिम्, one action-flourishing of the sword—is evidently the कारण of the other action, viz spread of glory Thus it is clear that in such cases of समुच्चय (२), we have अतिशयोक्ति (iv ) where there is यौगपद्य between कार्य-कारण Read सा द.— एते हि गुणक्रियायौगपद्ये समुच्चयप्रकारा नियमेन कार्य-कारण कालनियमविपर्ययरूपातिशयोक्तिमूल।

 How to distinguish then such समुच्चय from अतिशयोक्ति ? For मम्मट this difficulty would not arise Because his अतिशयोक्ति (iv ) is कार्यस्य चारणात् पूर्वम् उक्तौ, not कार्यस्य कारणेन समकाल उक्तौ So when one गुण produces another and yet both are simultaneous, or when a क्रिया produces another and is समानकालीन, मम्मट will at once class it under समुच्चय and not अतिशयोक्ति

 But what about other आलङ्कारिकऽ who bring कार्यकारणयो यौगपद्य also within the scope of अतिशयोक्ति? They give no clue to their view अतिशयोक्ति point Possibly they would say that such समुच्चय is only अतिशयोक्ति, that has developed into a separate figure So यौगपद्ये अतिशयोक्ति would, according to them embrace cases not covered by गुणयौगपद्य and क्रियायौगपद्य

 It should not be supposed, however, that all cases of गुणसमुच्चय and क्रियासमुच्चय, are cases कार्यं-कारणयौगपद्य There are cases of समुच्चय (२) where two गुणऽ or two क्रियाऽ are simultaneous, but there is no र्काय-कारणभाव involved in them Thus in the stanza from **विक्रमोर्वशीय** **४३— **अयमेरपदे etc ( St 120), the क्रियाऽ, viz वियोगोपनति and रम्यीभवन of the days, are not related by कार्य-कारणभाव and yet the figure is समुच्चय ( २ ) Simularly in नश्यन्ति पश्चात् पश्यन्ति त्रस्यन्ति च मवदूद्विष given to illustrate क्रियायौगपद्य by **कुवलयानन्द**, the क्रियाऽ are not related as cause and effect This shows that विश्वनाथ's insistence on अतिशयोक्ति (cp नियमेन अतिशयोक्तिमूल in the quotation given above ) is based on too narrow a view of the figure. It must be admitted, however, that समुच्चय (२), based on अतिशयोक्ति is more poetical

समुच्चय ( २ ), like समुच्चय (१), was first admitted by रुद्रट रद्रट's definmtion confines its scope to वैयभिकरण्ये समुच्चय only मम्मट, रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित all admitted the figure. All except अप्पय्यदीक्षित are positive about the figure being present in सामानाधिकरण्य and वैयधिकरण्य both

Note the following scheme—

**समुच्चय (२)**

**।**

**——————————————————————**

   सामानाधिकरण्ये                          
         वैयधिकरण्ये

गुणयो यौगपद्ये क्रियाय यौगपद्ये गुणक्रिया यौगपद्ये , गुणयो यौगपद्ये क्रियाय यौगपद्ये  
    1 2 3       
       4 5 उभयोयौगपद्ये

    6

 वैयधिकरण्ये समुच्चय is where the गुणऽ or क्रिया's present thernselves in different abodes सामानाधिकरण्ये समुच्चय is where they present them selves in the same abode मम्मट is indifferent to सामानाधिकरण्य and वैयधिारण्य, though he recognuses both in समुच्चय According to some वैयधिकरण्ये समुच्चय is more poetical than सामानाधिकरण्य समुच्चय रामचरण तर्कवागीश says अस्य वैयधिकरण्ये एव प्रयोगप्राचुर्य चिच्छित्तिविशेषजनकत्व च। रूद्रट admitted वैयधिकरण्ये समुच्चय only

 रसगङ्गाधर gives a scheme comprehensive enough to include the first vanety of समुच्चय also—  


**समुच्चय**

**————————————————**

** भिन्नेषु धर्मिषु                    
             अमित्रे धर्मिणि**

** गुणयौगपद्ये क्रियायौगपद्ये उभययौगपद्ये अकाररूपे
                 कारणरूपे**

**                              
गुणयौगपद्यै क्रियायौगपद्ये उभययौगपद्ये रमणीयाना समुच्चये            
                                                                      
                                                                      
                                                        
अमर........समुच्चये रमणीयारमणीयाना समुच्चये**

**समुच्चये(२)                           
समुच्चय(२) समुच्चय(1) ** 

 The scheme is ingenious enough.But it is rather strange that कार्यकारणयौगपद्यमूल समुच्चय to which विश्वनाथ drew attention escaped aजगन्नाथ 'spenetrating mught. For neather his divisions of समुच्चय (२) nor his of then are based on बदकार्य-कारणबोध O is he studiously

keeping समुच्चय from encroachung on अतिशयोक्ति ? जयरथ also is silent over this point

***Essentials —*** \[ समुच्चय ( गुणक्रियाणा) यौगपद्य अत्र \] (1) There must be two or more गुणऽ or क्रियाऽ or गुणक्रियाऽ coming into being simultaneously (2) The गुणऽ orक्रियाऽ or गुणक्रियाऽ may appear in the same abode or different abodes (3) When they appear in different abodes they are more charming (4) The गुणऽ or क्रियाऽ may or may not be related as कार्यकारण (5) When related as कार्यकारण the figure is based on अतिशयोक्ति and is more charming than the other variety not based on कार्य-कारणभाव (6) Simultaneity is necessary though आलङ्कारिकऽ differ on the point otherwise the figure will shade off into कारकदीपक (7) When based on अतिशयोक्ति the अतिशयोक्ति charms us Otherwise mere simultaneity is the source of charm (8) Simultaneity is usually marked by च-व or single च though other variants are also possible It is significant that in one and all the instances of मम्मट we find च व used

** § सू ५५ स त्वन्यो** etc Construe— या युगपद् गुणक्रिया स त्तु अन्य ( समुच्चय )। तु marks transition from the 1st variety **गुणक्रिया**। i e गुणौ ( or गुणा ) च क्रिये ( or क्रिया ) च गुणक्रिये (or गुणक्रिया ) च There may be गुणऽ two or more than two क्रियाऽ two or more than two and गुण क्रियाऽ two or more **अन्य** other than the preceding समुच्चय

 **वृत्ति** गुणौ etc The dual only shows the possibility of two गुणऽ etc but does not exclude the possibility of three or more गुणऽ etc

 **St** **119 विदलित** etc. Illustrates गुणयो यौगपद्य No sooner did the victor's army look bright with triumph than the vanquished looked sullen Construe तव इव बल आशु विमल अभवत् च तानि प्रखलमुखानि मलिनानि जातानि च। प्रखल
reference to the enemies

 Here the two गुणऽ वैमल्य and मालिन्य appear simultaneously Their यौगपद्य Is conveyed by च-च Note that here वैमल्य and मालिन्य are कारण and कार्य respectively described as simultaneous in order to convey कारणस्य शीत्रकारित्व This is therefore अतिशयोक्तिमूल समुच्चय Note again that here we have वैयधिकरण्ये समुच्चय with विमल्य abiding in तब बल and मालिन्य in प्रखलमुखानि, The stanza is taken from रूद्रटऽ काव्यालकार ७ २८

 **St** **120 अयमेकपदे** etc Illustrates क्रिययो यौगपद्य विक्रम's wail over his separation from उर्वशी which he feels acutely now that the rainy season has set in एकपदे all of a sudden अहोभि निरातपत्वरम्यै

भवितव्यं L e.अहानिº रम्यानि भवन्ति। The days have become pleasant as there is no heat

 Here वियोगोपनति and अह्नां रम्यीभाव are क्रियाऽ that take place simultaneously as is indicated by च च Hence is it क्रियायौगपद्य Note that there is a वेयधिकरण्य due to वियोगोपनति being in relation to विक्रम, and रम्यीभाव in relation to अहानि Here there is no कार्य-कारणभाव, much less अतिशयोक्ति. The charm lies in simple यौगपद्य

 ** St. 121**.कलुषं etc. Illustrates गुण क्रियया यौगपद्य The king only cast an angry glance at the enemy and the enemy was plunged in distress. Construe महीपतीन्द्र अकरमात् तत्र सितपकेरूहमोदरश्रि चक्षु अहितेषु कलुष अभवत्) च सेवा वपुषि आपदा कटाक्षै प्रस्फुटं पतित च कलुष Angry सित etc.Having beauty (श्री) similar to (सादर) that of a white lotus,

 Here there is यौगपद्य of the गुण,कालुष्य and the क्रिया, आपत्कटाक्षपात यौगपद्य indicated by च च. Hence it is गुणक्रियायौगपद्य Note that again we have कालुष्य, the of आफ्कटाक्षपात, the कार्य Both being simultane ous, we have अतिशयोक्तिमूलं समुच्चय. As वालुष्य abides in the चक्षु of the king and as आपत्कटाक्षऽ fall on the enemy, it is वैयधिकरण्ये समुच्चय

 धुनोति etc. मम्मट here criticizes the definition of समुच्चय (2) given by रूद्रट as being too narrow रुद्रट's definition is व्यधिकरणे वा यस्मिन् गुणक्रियै चैवकालमेकस्मिन्। उपजायेते देवी समुच्चय स्यात्तदन्योभौ॥

 In the definition there are two requirements (1) व्यधिकरणे (2) एकस्मिन् देश When they are strictly adhered to, we shall have to call that alone as gras समुच्चय where (1) the गुण क्रिया appear simultaneously in different abodes and (2) on the same scene (एकस्मिन् देश) This standard will result in the exclusion of instances like धुमेति चर्मिन् etc. and ....... etc. which are acknowledged to be समुच्चयऽ.

 Because, in धुनेति ...तनुत च कीर्तिम्, the क्रियाऽ appear in the same अधिकल्प the soldier This shows how the requirement of वैयधिकरण्य unnecessarily limits the scope of the figure.

Further this will show how futile it is to interpret समाने दशे as referring to सामानाधिकरण्ये and to suppose that there was some view of समुच्चय which confined it only to सामानाधिकरण्य The words are clearly from रुद्रट and embody no view of सामानाधिकरण्ये एव समुच्चय. In fact,we are aware of none who held this narrow view Note again how नमिसाधु also takes समाने देशे गुणक्रियाधिकरणयोर्वस्तुनो देशाधिकरणमेक-मेवेति कृत्वा

**३५ A-पर्याय (१)**

 If the charm of समुच्चय (१) and ( २ ) consisted in यौगपद्य or simultaneaty of कारणऽ, गुणऽ etc, the charm of both the varieties of पर्याय consists in क्रम or sequence That is why पर्याय is given a place immediately after समुच्चय in the order of the figures.

 **पर्याय (१)**—This consists in one and the same thing abrding in turn (पर्याय = क्रम ) in different abodes Thus, in स्थिता क्षण पक्ष्मसु ताडिताधरा पयोधरात्सेधनिपातचूर्णिता। वपीषु तस्या स्खलिना प्रपेदिरे, क्रमेण नामि प्रथमोदविन्दव॥(कु
स ५ २४), the poet describes how the first rain-drops passed from place to place on the body of पार्वती, practising penance But this is a simple पर्याय (१), because it involves no अतिशयोक्ति It depends for its charm on the minute study of the course of the drops Another instance of simple पर्याय will be विसष्टरागादधरान्निवर्तित स्तनाङ्गरागारुणिताच्च बन्दुवात्। कुशाङ्कुरादान-परिक्षताङ्गुलि कृतोऽक्षसूत्रप्रणयौ तथा कर॥where पार्वती uses her hand, until now used to the rouge and the ball, for holding the as cetic's rosary

 But पर्याय (१) becomes more striking when based on अतिशयोक्ति Says a poet to a lang मक्रालयस्य कुक्षौ स्थित्वा हृदये (?) Sमृताशिना च चिरम्। सप्रति निर्दोषे ते राजन् वदनाम्बुजे सुधा वसति॥( र र्ग ) where nectar is described as abiding in turn in the bosom of the ocean, the heart of gods, and on the king's lips Here the सुधा on the lips of the king i. e huis sweet tongue is अध्यवमित as the same as the सुधा in ocean and heaven Hence there is अतिशयोक्ति, underlying the पर्याय Not only that. There is an additional charm due to the successive positions being higher and higher higher than कुक्षि is हृदय, higher up still is वदन Sometimes, the successive positions may be lower and lower Sometimes however, there may be no such untformity. In fact, जगन्नाथ insists upon the presence of a poetical touch like this in पर्याय Read यत्र आधाराधेयतत्सम्व धक्रमेषु क्वचिदपि कविकल्पनापेक्षा तत्रैवायमलङ्कार। यत्र तु सर्वांशे लोकसिद्धत्वं न तत्र कश्चिदलङ्कार। In all these cases there is of course the basic विरोध of one thing being found in several places.

 पर्याय is a figure dating from रुद्रट who called पर्यायोक्ति also by the same name रुद्रट also recognised the two varebes (१) and (२) of the figure admitted by later आसकारिकऽ. After him the figure in both forms was admitted by all मम्मट,रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, and अप्पय्यदीक्षितम् अप्पय्यदीक्षित gives three sub-divisions of पर्याय (१) viz सकोचपर्याय विकासपर्याय, and शुद्धपर्याय based upon the succession आश्रयऽ becoming smaller and smaller, or bigger and bigger or neither of the two

Note the following scheme—

पर्याय(१)

वस्तुन अनकत्र रक्त भवने (St 122) वस्तुत अनेकत्र अन्येन करणे (St 124)



  This principle of division मम्मट borrowed from रुद्रट Of the later आलङ्कारिकऽ only विश्वनाथ takes note of it The object may itself occupy different abodes or some agent may place it in different abodes.

**For पर्याय ( १ ) is सार see सार  
For पर्याय (१) is विशेष see विशेष**

  **Essentials**— \[पर्याय क्रम क्रमाश्रयणात् पर्याय इत्यन्वर्थमभिधानम्। रुय्यक \] (1) The poet must describe one and the same thing as abiding or being placed in different places in *succession* not simultaneously (2) The fact must be charming in itself (3) It appears still more charrmung when based on अतिशयोक्ति (4) There may or may not be some system in the choice of the successive positions. They may Indicate gradual descent (अवरोह) or ascent (आरोह) or neather (5) The thing in question must leate one आश्रय before resorting to another जगन्नाथ insists on this. Otherwise it is no पर्याय, says he. Read व्याप्तसबन्धनात्तोत्तर अपरसबन्धे सत्येव पर्यायपर्यस्य लोके प्रयोगात्।(6) There 19 of course the basic charm of RN viz. one thing being found th seteral places

 ** § सू ५६** एक कमेण etc. Construe एक (वस्तु) क्रमेण अनैकस्मिन् आधारे (भवति का क्रियते वा) (स) पर्याय। क्रमेण Thus ts the essence of the figure. The name पर्याय also means क्रम भवति या क्रियते वा The thing may abide in शिक्षाकारऽ by itself or at the instance of others. This explanation is abynously based or रूद्रट s साद् वा क्रियते वा

 **St 122** नव्याश्रय etc Illustrates वस्तुन स्वत अनेकत्र भवने पर्याय (१ ) The words of the wicked carry venom (कालकुट) in them This is

conveyed by tracing the course of कालकूट from the ocean to the wicked tongue ab Construe कालकूट, इय उत्तरोत्तरविशिष्टपदा आश्रयस्थिति तव केन ननु उपदिष्टा। कालकूट n (कालस्व मृयो कूटः छद्मद्यूत इव) Deadly poison उत्तरात्तर विशिष्टपदा आश्रयस्थिति manner of staying in abodes in which the successive positions are more and more exalted कण्ठ is higher than हृदय वाक् is higher up still वृषलक्ष्मण ( कण्ठे ) Gen. sing of वृषलक्ष्मन्, शिव whose bull is his symbol (लक्ष्मन् n ) Siva drank the हालाहल (= कालकूट) अथ later खलाना वाचि because the words churned out of the ocean अथ latter खलाना वाचि because the words are deadly like poison

 Here कालकूट is described as remaining in the different आश्रयऽ in succession VIZ अर्णवहृदय शिवकण्ठ and खलवाच् The stanza has the charm of being अतिशयोक्तिमूल It is अतिशयोक्ति to conceive the venom on the lip of the wicked as the same as कालकूट There is further आरोहक्रम in evidence in the stanza as seen above which makes it still more charming As कालकूट itself occupies the आश्रयऽ in succession this is वस्तुन स्वत अनेकत्र भवने पर्याय

 **St.123 बिम्बोष्ठ एव** etc Illustrates पर्याय (१) of the same subdivision as illustrated by St 122 but based on ऐष 4 lady of sanguine lips becomes sanguine at heart

 Here राग described as being present successively in the different आश्रयऽ VIz बिम्बोष्ठ and हत्य This is therefore वस्तुन स्वत अनेकत्र भवने पर्याय There is again अतिशयोक्ति based on श्लेष in concerving राग (ruddy hue) as identical with राग (love) In the instance above there was अतिशयोक्ति based on साधर्म्य here it is based on रेष र ग takes exception to the validity of the पर्याय here पर्याय cannot occur where the former आश्रय is not left by the object before it occupies a newer one Here राग has not left the ओष्ठ

 रागस्य वस्तुत भेदे etc. See above

 Additional Stanza (See Text p 150 bottom) श्रोणीवधस्त्यननि etc. is read by a good many mss. The Tvm edition reads it and the commentaries there presuppose it illustrates once more the same variety of it as Sts. 122 123 Description of changes wrought in a maiden s figure by the onset of youth सम्प्रदायप्रकाशिनी interprets c as त्यक्तमातृकुचसाचिव्य वक्त्र अद्वितीय अनुपम भवति। वक्ष becomes कुचसचिव The वक्त्र that was up to now (मातृ )कुचसचिव (because she was a suckling) becomes अद्वितीय (without a match ) So कुचसचिक्त्व passes from वक्त्र to बक्ष

 Here, the गुणऽ, तनुता, सरलगतित्व and कुचसाचिव्य are found by themselves passing from one abode to another Hence this is वस्तुन स्वतः अनेकत्र भवने पर्याय Note that there is अतिशयोक्ति underlying the figure. Because the तनुत्व of the श्रोणी is not the same as तनुत्व of the मध्यभाग Nor is theसरलगतित्व of the पादऽ the same as that of the लोचनऽ. So कुचसचिवत्व of the मुख is not the same as that of the वक्ष

 **St. 124** त ताण etc. illustrates एकस्य अन्येन अनेकत्र करणे पर्याय The demons so long engrossed in रानोद्धरण the from the ocean lapse into a spell of love-making श्रीसहोद्धररत्नाभरणे उद्यात takes this to mean कौस्तुभावरण=विष्णु It may as well be श्रीमहोदरत्नाहरणे (.एकरस) solely absorbed in collecting the jewels found along with Laksmi This makes a better sense and is adopted by साहित्यचूडामणि प्रियाणां बिम्बाधरे viz मनोरमा, the form assumed by विष्णु to lure away the demons. Or प्रियाणां refers to their beloveds whose memories they conjured up at the sight of मनोरमा ? मा. चं says विक्रम परित्यज्य मन चुम्बनादिसक्त कृतमित्यर्थ। There is a suggestion of व्यतिरेक also Read मा च विम्बाधर सकलरत्नेभ्योऽधिक इति व्यङ्ग्यव्यतिरेकोऽपि।

 Here कुसुमवाण—Cupid—is described as transferring the heart of the demons from रत्नोद्धरण to प्रियाणां बिम्बाधर Thus it is एकस्य अनेकत्र अन्येन करणे पर्याय

**३५ B-पर्याय (२)**

 If in the first variety of the figure the charm lay in one thing coming to abide in different abodes the second variety derives its charm from different things coming in turn (क्रम) to abide in one abode In पयाय (१) the was in respect of the आधारऽ, in पर्याय(२), it is in respect of the आधेयऽ जयरथ looks upon them as two distinct figures and not divisions of one and the same figure Acc to him काव्यप्रकाशकार also means likewise Read—अतश्च द्वौ पयायौ । न युनरेक एव सामान्यलक्षणायोगात्। अन्य काव्यप्रकाशकृता पृथगेन लक्षितौ।

 In पयाय (2), the poet is found depicting the vicissitudes through which a thing passes. The object assumes one aspect now and another later. The aspects are the आश्रितऽ and the object is आश्रय The once glorious अयोध्या had fallen on bad days .Her streets once used to the sweet jungle of the anklets of अभिसारिकाऽ now echoed the ominous howls of the jackals निशासु भास्वत्वलनूपुराणाय सचरोऽभूदभिसारिकाणाम्। नदन्मुखोत्काविचितामियाभिः स बाह्यते शतपथ शिबाभिः॥(रघु १६१२) Here as the अभिसारिकाऽ and शिवाऽ resort in succession to the same शतपथ, we get

पर्याय (२) पार्वती, once a jolly princess, turns an ascetic she once wore ornaments now she dons a bark विमित्यपाम्याभरणानि यौवन धृत त्वया वार्धक्यशोभितबल्कल (कु स ५४४), asks शिव in disguise This is again पर्याय (2) because आभरणऽ and बल्कलऽ are described as आश्रितऽ in turn in relation to the same आश्रय, viz पार्वती Similarly, in नभसोऽपससार तम बभूव तस्मिन्नथालोक (रुद्रट ७४५) there is पर्याय (२), owing to the two आश्रितऽ viz तमस् and आलोक occupying in turn the same आश्रय VIZ नभस् In short, in the 2nd variety, one आश्रय is resorted to by different आश्रितऽ in turn As in the first variety, there is here a vein of विरोध between one आश्रय and several आश्रितऽ But पर्याय (२) leaves no scope for अतिशयोक्ति which could underhe पर्याय(१)

 ** पर्याय (२) vs परिवृत्ति**– In both one आश्रय is resorted to by भिन्न आश्रयिन्ऽ at different times Just as आभरणऽ and बल्कल are found in turn on the same पार्वती, so in the परिवृत्ति ( St 108), वसुन्धरा and प्रहार are found in turn to be with the same दृप्तारिबिसर But whereas (1) in पर्याय (२) there is no idea of exchange, but merely a description of phases of one object in परिवृत्ति, there is the idea of exchange.That is to say in पर्याय, the implication is. This object assumed this other aspect after this one. While in परिवृत्ति the implication is This object exchanged this for that. Thus in the former क्रम interests us, in the latter विनिमय (2) In पर्याय , the thing leaving the object is not taken up by someone else In परिवृत्ति, strictly considered it is taken up by someone else who gives something else in return. (3) In पर्याय , there is only one आधार or party In परिवृत्ति, strictly taken there are two parties between whom the bargain is struck *Vide* परिवृत्ति Read सा द.– अस्य (पर्यायस्य) विनिमयाभावात् परिवृत्ते (भद) ( 4 ) In पर्याय ( २ ) the आश्रितऽ that abide in turn can be two or more than two the thing that is given up and the thing taken

For पर्याय (२) *vs* सार, see सार  
For the development of the figure see पयाय ( १ )  
Note the following scheme—

पर्याय (२)

————————————————————————

        एकस्मिन् अनेकस्य स्वतः भवने एकस्मिन् अनेकस्य अन्येन करणे  

 These divisions correspond to those in पर्याय (१) रुद्रट was their onginator विश्वनाथ also admitted them.

 **Essentials**— \[ पर्याय= क्रम, एकस्मिन् अनेकक्रमेण भवतीति \] There must be one आश्रय and two or more आश्रितऽ (2) The must abide or be made to abide in the आश्रय in turn one after another (3) This vanety generally embodies the vicissitudes of an object (4) The theme must be poetically striking; पुरा यत्र घत तत्र अधुना पट is not पर्याय (5) This varity of पर्याय does not leave much scope for अतिशयोक्ति (6) The आश्रितऽ must abide in the abode one after another One must leave and then the other enter Otherwise there will be no क्रम and therefore no पर्याय  

 ** § सू ५७ अन्य** etc Construe ततोऽन्यथा अन्य ( पर्याय )। The other varity is just the other way is the other way i e अनेक एकस्मिन् पर्याय ( १ ) was एक अनेकस्मिन्

 **वृत्ति— कमेण** not युगपद् This would distinguish thus variety not only from विशेष but also समुच्चय ( १ ) and ( २) where, in case of सामानाधि करण्य, numerous things are present in one abode but simullaneously

 ** St 125 मधुरिम etc** illustrates पर्याय (2) अनेकस्य एकस्मिन् स्वत भवने, The words of the wicked tho-ugh felt as agreeable at first prove disastrous later. मधुरिम्णा रुचिर ..... वच पृथु qualifies अमृत con-strue अथ तदेव अन्नगतमिव मोहहेतु हालहल विष कथयति i e On second thoughts, the words pierce the vitals

 Here खलना वच is आश्रय अमृत and हालहल by themselves abide in it in turn. Hence It is अनेकस्य एकस्मिन् स्वतः भवने पर्याय

 ** St. 126 तद्गेह** etc. illustrates पर्याय (२) अनेकस्य एकस्मिन् अन्येन करणे The startling changes made in the worldly circumstances of a when fortune smiled on him Some like भीमसेन see in it a refe-rence to the well known episode of सुदामन् सद्गेह said in contempt of the petty house of the past. दिव लब्धावकाश मन्दिरं A slky-scraper। जरती refers probably to the premature old age brought about by under feeding वरिणा एता घनाभा घटा \[ घटा rows \] Elephant symbolized the height of affluence in those days Cp Marathi गजान्त लक्ष्मीक्षुद्र मुसलध्वनि See st. 95 क्षुद्र contemptible or indistinct, low ( due to the action being without vigour) d Construe ............. आश्चर्यं दिवस Time has done the miracle.

 Here दिवसऽ make the same द्विज in turn the आश्रय of each one from the following pairs गेह-मन्दिर धनु-बरिघटा मुसलध्वनि-.... Hence it is अनेकस्य एकस्मिन् अन्येन करणे पर्याय

 अत्र एकस्यैव etc. मम्मट wants to distinguish between परिवृत्ति and पर्याय. In परिवृत्ति there can be a barter (हानोपपन्न) because there are

two parties Here since there is one party only, such barter can never be intended (अविवक्षित) हानापादान is barter. Tvm. edition refers to a reading हानोपादानयोर्विवक्षितत्वात् Thus would make a better sense In this figure, हान and उपादान are intended with reference to one party only, therefore it cannot be परिवृत्ति where हान and उपादान are between two parties. This reading makes it unnecessary to interpret हानोपादान as barter which is rather a free rendering e. in अत्र i e. in पर्याय as illustrated by तद्गेह नतभित्ति etc

**३६ - अनुमान**

 अनुमान In सर्व means the process ( प्रमाण ) that leads to and includes अनुमिति I see smoke on the hill, I infer fire on it. What made me arrive at the knowledge of fire from the sight of the smoke is the process of अनुमान ( cp अनुमितिकरण अनुमानम् ) The knowledge of fire is the अनुमिति धूम is साधन in relation to the अनुमिति which is साध्य

 अनुमान in the realm of poetry runs on similar lines. The तार्किक sees the धूम The कवि sees how the कटाक्षऽ of the woman pierce the heart of the man. The तार्किक infers that there is fire on the moun tain The कवि deduces that ahead of the woman runs Cupid with an arrow fixed on the bow! यत्र पतत्यवल्गना दृष्टिर्निशिता पनन्ति तत्र शरा। तच्चापरोपितशरो धावत्यासा पुर स्मरो मन्ये॥ ( सा द ) The fire is not seen but inferred by the तार्किक स्मरस्य पुरोधावन् is not seen but inferred by the poet Then there moves a cloud in the sky To the poet it is smoke pervading the sky He infers therefrom that the fire of love has been burning down the trees in the form of travellers यथा रन्ध्र व्योम्नश्चलजलद धूम स्थगयति। तथा मन्ये लग्न पथिकतरुखण्ड स्मरदव॥ रुय्यक \[cp मेघालोक भवति सखिनोऽप्य-न्यथावृत्ति चेतः। कण्ठा श्लेषप्रणयिनि जने किं पुनर्दूरसस्थ॥— मेघदूत १३ \] This is again अनुमान more poet-ical than यत्र पतत्यवल्गना etc, because it is based on रूपक मघ एवं धूम स्मर एव दव, पथिका एवं तरव Such are poetical अनुमानऽ some more poetical than others र ग —अस्य च कविप्रतिभोलिवितत्वेन चमत्कारित्वे काव्यालङ्कारता ।  

 The अनुमान becomes poetical when the theme is poetical enough and when blended with other figures. The poet does not care for all the varied details like व्यप्तिज्ञान and उदाहरण for whi-ch the तार्किक has the highest respect. His process is simpler. The poet looks to the fact and dr-aws his own poetical inference. The inference may not be strictly true, but is bound to be poe-tical. Who can impugn the validity of the poet's conclusions? Who can challenge the validity of तच्चापरोपितशरा धावत्यामा पुर स्मरो मन्ये? And yet the poet does nothing more

than give a particular reason for arriving at a particular conclusion. Read रुय्यक यत्र साधन साध्यप्रतीतये निर्दिश्यते सोऽनुमानालङ्कार।

 **अनुमान vs काव्यलिङ्गः**—In अनुमान, we get a साधन or हेतु and साध्य In काव्यलिङ्ग also we get a हेतु and कार्य To that extent there is similarity between the two But (1) In अनुमान, the साध्य-साधनभाव is explicit, in काव्यलिङ्ग the हेतु-कार्यभाव is implicat or suggested. That is to say in अनुमान, the हेतु or साधन is clearly stated to be such, by the use of Ablative, Instrumental or particles like यत In काव्यलिङ्ग, as we saw, the हेतुत्व is left to suggestion (2) In अनुमान, the हेतु is within experience. The साध्य is a matter of conjecture. In काव्यलिङ्ग, the हेतु and कार्य are both within experience ( 3 ) In अनुमान, the हेतु indicates something, in other words, it is ज्ञापक In काव्यलिङ्ग, the हेतु accounts for something in other words, it is समर्थक ( 4 ) In अनुमान our thoughts proceed from the साधन to the साध्य. in काव्यलिङ्ग, our thoughts proceed from the कार्य to the हेतु that is to say, in अनुमान, the question arises what does this (साधन) indicate? The reply thereto is the साध्य. in काव्यलिङ्ग, on the other hand, the question arises how does this (कार्य)come about? and the answer thereto is the काव्य or हेतु (5) closely analysed the हेतु and कार्य in काव्यलिङ्ग become साध्य and साधन respectively in अनुमान If प्रियाहृतमना प्रणिपातलङ्घन सेवा मन्यते is काव्यलिङ्ग, the corresponding अनुमान will be यत असौ प्रणिपातलघन सेवा मन्यते तस्मादसौ प्रियाहृतमना (6) In अनुमान, there figure words like मन्ये, जाने etc. markang Inference Atleast they are implied In काव्यलिङ्ग there is no room for them

 **अनुमान VS अर्थान्तरन्यास**— If the साधन in अनुमान is in the form of a वाक्य, we get two वाक्यऽ as those in अर्थान्तरन्यास In both, the हेतुत्व in explicitly stated But whereas (1) in अनुमान, the हेतु is ज्ञापक, in अर्थान्तरन्यास, it is समर्थक (2) In अनुमान the two वाक्यऽ are syntactically dependent due to the use of यत् यस्मात् etc, in अर्थान्तरन्यास, they are ndependent ( 3 ) In अनुमान, the two वाक्यऽ are related as कारण-कार्य In अर्थान्तरन्यास they are related as सामान्य विशेष (4) In अनुमान, we get words like मन्ये etc. to mark the deduction made in अर्थान्तरन्यास, there is no occasion for them

 **अनुमान vs उत्पेक्षा**— In both, there figure words like मन्ये, ऊहे etc, But whereas (1) in अनुमान, there is certainty in the deduction, in उत्प्रेक्षा, there is uncertainty. (2) in अनुमान, there is no सादृश्यप्रतीति in उत्प्रेक्षा, there is सादृश्यप्रतीति Read सा द — उत्प्रेक्षाया अनिश्चिततया प्रतीतिः। इह तु निश्चिततया इत्युभयोर्भेदः। र ग — मन्ये शङ्के अवैमि जाने इत्यादिपदानां यत्र सादृश्यातिनिमित्तसद्भाव तत्र उत्प्रेक्षाबोधकता इनि विवेक।

 अनुमान was first recognised by रुद्रट , though faint traces of the figure are to be found in उद्भट’s काव्यलिङ्ग रुद्रट divides it according as साध्य and साधन are placed first or next Another अनुमान of रुद्रट consists in describing a thing as past or future, even when it is not so, owing to some strong circumstance No later आलङ्कारिक admitted this variety मम्मट, रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ all admit the figure मम्मट’s saw no charm in place-shifting It was to him immaterial whether साध्य or साधन was placed first or next. His followers also assume the same attitude. अप्पय्यदीक्षित does not define the figure but classes it among the प्रमाणालङ्कारऽ

 **Essentials**— \[ अनुमान Deduction of course, poetical\] (1) There must be some circumstance (साधन) on the basis of which the poet makes the deduction ( साध्य ) (2) Both the साध्य and साधन must be poetically striking (3) They may be poetical because of some other underlying अलङ्कार like रूपक or simply because of dignified style (प्रौढोक्ति) (4) The साधन is clearly indicated by Ablative or Instrumental or words like यत्, यस्मात् and not suggested (5) The अनुमान often assumes the form यस्मादिद भवति तस्मादिदं मन्ये (or वाने or विभावये etc.) (6) मन्ये etc. may someti-mes be implied but clearly enough (7) The poet will not bother about the logicians details like व्याप्ति or लिङ्गपरामर्श nor is it of any use to judge the accuracy of the साध्य by तार्किक standards. Because, after all, it is poetical

 ** § सू ५८ अनुमान** etc. Construe यत् साध्य-साधनयो वच तत् अनुमानम् उक्तम्. अनुमान is the staternent of the साध्य and साधन साध्य is the thing inferred, the deduction. In the means thereto  

 **वृत्ति– पक्षधर्मान्वयव्यतिरेकित्वेन विरूपो हेतु साधनम्।** साधन Is a reason that has three aspects i. e. must satisfy three requirements in order to be valid–( 1 ) पक्षधर्मत्व, (2) अन्वयित्व, ( 3 ) व्यतिरेकित्व पक्षधर्मत्व Being present in the particular case under consideration. The धूम is valid साधन for हेतु because it is present on the पक्ष or particular case viz. पर्वत अन्वयित्व or more clearly, सपक्षे अन्वयित्व i e सपक्षे सत्व (अन्वय = Presence) Being present in a similar case.धूम is valied because it is present in the सपक्ष i e. similar instance of महानस— kitchen महानस is a सपक्ष because साध्य वह्णि is present there in such सपक्षऽ, धूम also is present, व्यतिरेकिच or, more fully, विपक्षाद् व्यतिरेकिच or व्यवृत्तत्व Being absent (व्यतिरेक=व्यावृत्ति=absence) from a dissimilar instance. धूम is absent from महाह्रद a great lake which is aविपक्ष because बह्नि is absent there

 A valid logical हेतु must satisfy these three requirements (त्रिरूप) But a poet has scant respect for these. He will never wait to see if these are or are not fulfilled by his हेतु or साधन

 धर्मिणि अयोगव्यवच्छेदो व्यापकस्य साध्यं साध्य of the सूत्र explained साध्य \[e g पर्वतो वह्निमान् \] is the demal ( व्यवच्छेद) of absence (अयाग ) of the more extensive (व्यपक i e वह्नि) from the particular case (धर्मिन् i e पर्वत) In plan words In पर्बतो वह्निमान् वह्नि is stated to be present on the पर्वत Denial of Absence is Presence व्यापक and व्याप्य Relative terms वह्नि is व्यापक or the more extensive one in relation to धूम, the वाप्य or the less extensive one वह्नि is more extensive because it is found not only in all cases where धूम is present but also in others where धूम is absent as in a red hot iron ball (तप्तायोगोल) धूम is therefore less extensive  

 ** St 127 यत्रैता** etc illustrates अनुमान The glances of women pierce the hearts of men because of Cupid their escort, aiming an arrow at them (men) लहरीचलाचलदृश with glarices as unsteady (चलाचल) as waves अमी मार्गणा Ref to मन शरऽ cd Construe तत् सत्य बोधन, अचितशरप्रेङ्खत्कर आसा (सुदृशा ) शामनधर स्मर' चक्रीकृतचाप अग्रत एव धावति। सत्य indeed अचितशरप्रेङ्खत्कर With his hand quivering on the arrow set on the bow (अचित) Quivering due to tension of his mind or bow चक्रीकृतचाप adverb The bow is bent when the arrow is to be discharged A compliment also to the arched eyebrows referred to in a. शासनधर very obedient

 साध्यसाधनयो etc. मम्मट, once more differs from रुद्रट. रुद्रट had divided अनुमान on the basis of the पौर्वापर्य of साध्य-साधन That is to say साध्य first साधन next, was one variety the other being साधन first, साध्य next \[cp वस्तु परोक्ष यस्मिन् साध्यमुपन्यस्य साधनं तस्य। पुनरन्यदुपन्यस्यदे् विपरीत पौर्वापर्यानुमानम्॥७५६ \] मम्मट finds no particular charm in this place-shifting

 It is significant that रूय्यक,विश्वनाथ,जगन्नाथ also did not accept divi stons based on पौर्वापर्यविकल्प \[ पौर्वापर्यविकल्प lit. alternatives in sequence.\] मम्मट implies similar disregard for this principle of पौर्वापर्यविकल्प in the matter of the धर्म in दीपक and in the matter of the समर्थ्य-समर्थक in अर्थान्तरन्यास

**३७ —परिकर**

 परिकर means 'whatever *contributes* to something else— and the name is aptly given to the figure where the central charm consists in

the use of a single adjective or attribute or an array of them that contributes to the sense of the sentence कण्व says to शकुन्तला ( शा ४१८) अभिजनवतो भर्तु श्लाघ्ये स्थिता गृहिणीपदे, विभवगुरुभि कृत्यैस्त-स्य प्रतिक्षणमाकुला।तनयमचिरात् प्राचीवार्कं प्रसूय च पावन, मम विरहजा न त्व वत्से शुच गणयिष्यसि॥That preeminent sage promises very bright prospects for शकुन्तला in abc — To be the consort of one of noble parentage (अभिजनवत्) is enviable (श्राव्य) indeed To be preoccupied, every moment, with his acts great with glory ( विभवगुरु ) is no small fortune. To bear him a son and then too a sacred (पावन) one will be indeed the height of good fortune. Here each one of the विशेषणऽ• अभिजनवत, श्लाघ्य, विभवगुरु, पावन makes a splendid contribution to the issue on hand viz a picture of the rosy future before शकुन्तला This is therefore a परिकर, The यक्ष wants to describe his beloved as a paragon of beauty Says he (मेघदूतः २१९) तन्वी श्यामा शिखरिदशना पक्वबिम्बाधरोष्ठी मध्ये क्षामा चकितहरिणीप्रेक्षणा निम्ननाभि। श्रोणीभारादलसगमना स्तोकनम्रा स्तनाभ्यां या तत्र स्याद् युवतिविषये सृष्टिराद्येव धातु॥Here as each of the epithets contributes to the central theme viz the picture of a beauty, we get a परिकर

 In परिकर, the adjectives or epithets contribute to the वाच्यार्थ by the suggestion which each one carries. Thus each one तन्वी, श्यामा etc. suggests सौन्दर्य and thereby contributes to the वाच्यार्थ which aims at describing a beautiful dame. Not that the वाच्यार्थ would have remained incomplete in their absence. But certainly its charm is heightened by their presence As the suggestion is subservient to वाच्यार्थ there is no ध्वनि here. Read र ग — एव वाच्यार्थस्य सत्यामपि निष्पत्तौ सौन्दर्यविशेषाधानाय साकूत विशेषणम् ....। Again रय्यक विशेषणानां साभिप्रायश्च प्रतीयमानार्थगर्भकारः। ... एवं च प्रतीयमानाशस्य वाच्योन्मुखत्वात् परिकर इति सार्थक नाम।

 परिकर and काव्यलिङ्ग— we have seen how काव्यलिङ्ग usually assumes the form of विशेषणऽ that are significant In परिवर also the विशेषणऽ are significant But the sigruficance of the विशेषणऽ in काव्यलिङ्ग in परिकर the विशेषणऽ are significant in so far as they lend an additional charm to the वाच्यार्थ which as we saw above, would not be defective in their absence In काव्यलिङ्ग on the other hand the विशेषणऽ are significant because they explain or account for the वाच्यार्थ whi-ch, hence in their absence would be rendered defective अत्र says( रघुवंश...)......................। here if were merely to इन्दुमती .... should pain ... but the ... ...(with the hair lying

dishevelled) and विश्रान्तकथ (with the talk come to a standstill) make the matter clear to us. Thus, in short, in परिकर, the विशेषणऽ are वाच्यस्य उत्कर्षाधायक, in काव्यलिङ्ग they are वाच्यमिध्द्यङ्ग or वाच्यनिर्वाहक,

 Is परिकर a *figure or mere* दोषाभाव ?—आलङ्कारिकऽ raise the question Is a positive figure or does it merely mean an immunity from the दोष of अपरिपुष्टार्थत्व? काव्य must be निर्दोष (cp तत् अदोषौ शब्दार्थौ) अपुष्टत्व of अर्थ 1s a दोष, to which a काव्य becomes open, if therein is huddled up matter that has no bearing (अपुष्ट ) on the issue on hand And if in परिकर we get significant विशेषणऽ, that means, it may be argued, that here we have a काव्य free from that दोष of अपुष्टार्थत्व. Thus according to thus view, what is looked upon as a figure ( परिकर) amounts to no more than a दोषाभाव

 As against this, it is argued by मम्मट and others, that though significant adjectives are nothing more than a bare necessity for a काव्य, still there is undemably some particular poetic charm (विच्छित्तिविशेष) when we are presented with an array of such विशेषणऽ. That charm justifies परिकर as a positive figure Read जयरयः—विशेषणाना चात्र बहुत्वमेव विवक्षितम्। अन्यथा ह्यपुष्टार्थस्य दोषत्वाभिधानात् तन्निराकरणेन स्वीकृतस्य पुष्टार्थस्याय विषयस्मात्। एव एवविधानेकविशेषणोपन्यासद्वारेण वैचित्र्यातिशय समवतीत्यस्य अलङ्कारत्वम्।

 It is not clear if there was any आलङ्कारिक that preceded मम्मट who thus questioned the status of परिकर as an अलङ्कार, मम्मट’s predecessor रुद्रट actually admitted the figure. It is just likely, therefore, that मम्मट refers in his वृत्ति not to an actual view like this, but just to a theoretical possibility कुवल्यानन्द refers to it as actually the view of some ( केचित्) अलङ्कारिकऽ like जयरथ and जगन्नाथ are entirely in agreement with मम्मट on this point

 Are numerous विशेषणऽ a necessity for परिकर—This brings us to another question as to whether many significant विशेषणऽ are necessary for the figure. मम्मट In his वृत्ति and जयरथ (*vide* the quotation given above) hold that the figure will be there only if there ts an impressive pile of विशेषणऽ, not otherwise As against these जगन्नाथ and अप्पय्यदीक्षित regard even a single विशेषण as enough to constitute परिकर अप्पय्यदीक्षित for instance, says वस्तुतस्तु अनेकविशेषणो एवं परिकर इति न नियम। श्लेष-यमकादिषु अपुष्टार्थ (ल) दोषाभावेन तत्र। जगन्नाथ says that many विशेषणऽ only make the figure more impressive। Possibly जगन्नाथ is right Fven a single like

( in अभिजनवती भर्तु etc. ) is enough to lend charm and hence to make

 परिकर was first admitted by रुद्रट who divided it on the basis of the fourfold सङ्केत Viz जाति, गुण, क्रिया and द्रव्य मम्मट, रूय्यक विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित all admitted it अप्पय्यदीक्षित concerv-ed in addition a development of the figure परिकराकुर wherein the विशेष्य is significante e g चतुर्णां पुरुषार्थानां दाता देवश्चतुर्भुज where the विशेष्य, चतुर्भुज is significant in view of the पुरुषार्थऽ being four आलङ्कारिकऽ, however arereluctant to accord recogrition to this scion of परिकर

 **Essentials**— \[ परिकरोति व्यङ्ग्येन वाच्यार्थम् उपस्करोति, इति परिकर \] (1) There must be used by the poet significant विशेषणऽ, to quality the विशेष्य (2) The more the विशेषणऽ the more effective the figure. (3) The विशेषणऽ will be significant provided they have a व्यङ्ग्यार्थ which heightens the charm of the वाच्यार्थ They must not be direct as in, प्रधाना विनयाधानाद् रक्षणाद् भरणाद्यपि। म पिता पितरस्तामा केवलं नमहेतव॥ (र ग १ २४ ) (4) The विशेषणऽ only give additional significance for even without them the वाक्यार्थ would be complete (3) Thus they are बाच्यार्थोपस्वनरव and not वाक्यार्थनिर्वाहक as in काव्यलिङ्ग

 **§ सू ५९ विशेषणै** etc Construe – यत् साकूतै विशेषण उक्तिः स तु परिकर। तु Marks transition from the earlier figure to this साकूतै विशेषणै साकूतै=साभिप्रायै Full of significance. Significance is the sugge-stion which contributes to the वाच्यार्थ Read रुय्यक—विदग्धणानां साभिप्रायत्व प्रतीयमानार्थगभीकार। आकूलम् \[ आ + / कू to intend\] Intention

 वृत्ति–अर्थाद् विशष्यस्य (उक्तिः) The सूत्र amplified साकूतै विशेषणै उक्तिः The word विशेषणै makes it clear that the उक्ति or statement relates of course (अर्थात् ) to the विशेष्य For विशेषणऽ appear only in relation to a विशेष्य Thus the सूत्र means साकूतै विशेषणै विशेष्यस्य उक्तिः परिकर

 **St. 128. महौजसो** etc illustrates परिकर A spy reports to युधिष्ठिर how दूर्योधन has on his side soldiers endowed with all those virtues that go to make a soldier a tower of strength धनार्चिता honoured with wealth To be inserted in the Translation after ‘treasure’ सयाति लब्धकीर्तय who have made a name for themselves in battles (सयत् /) सहत who is in a conspiracy न भेदवृत्तय Not bent on dissensions. d construe तस्य ( in c) प्रियाणि अनुभि समहेतु बध्नाति They seek to secure his (i e of दुर्योधन) arms even at the cost of their lives. धनुर्भृत is the विशेष्य

  Here the adjectives, महौनस, मानधना, धनाचता, सयति लब्धकीर्तय, न सहता and न भेदवृत्तय। all suggest virtues (nde उद्योत) that go to make the most dependable soldier This suggestion contributes to the वाच्यार्थ in that it shows how दुर्योधन's cause is being defended by warriors of the most dependable sort Since the व्यग्य 1s subservient to वाच्यार्थं there is no ध्वनि अव्यङ्ग्यस्य वाच्योन्मुखत्व इनि न ध्वनिविषयता -- भा च

 **यद्यप्यपुष्टार्थस्य** etc See above मम्मट Justifies परिकार as a positive अलकार and not more दोषाभाव He says Though by declaring अपुष्टार्थत्व a दोष, we have naturally accepted that a काव्य should be पुष्ठार्थ, still, there does result some striking effect (वैचित्र्य) when many विशेषणऽ are arrayed as in the instance महीतसौ etc (एव) applying to the same विशेष्य (एकनिष्ठत्वेन) Hence is परिकर counted among सकरअलकारऽ अपुष्टार्थस्य दोषताभिधान In उ ७ मम gives अपुष्टार्थत्व as a दोष It consists in the use of words that have no relevancy to the matter on hand अपुष्ठार्थ on hand सपुष्टार्थ as used here is a noun न पुष्ट अर्थ येन तद् अपुष्टार्थम् Irrelevant matter पुष्टार्थ also noun here पुष्ठ अर्थ येन Relevant matter निराकरणेन" स्वीकार कृत When it 19 declared that अपुष्टार्थ is a दोष, it means that पुष्टार्थ is a necessity एकनिष्ठत्वेन i e एक विशेष्यनिष्ठत्वेन एव as illustrated in the stanza

 Two points emerge from this argument ( 1 ) that परिकार is an अलङ्कार, not mere दोषाभाव ( 11 ) that परिकार requires many sigmuficant विशेषणऽ \[ See above \]

**३८ — व्याजोक्ति**

 व्याजोक्ति is a statement based on pretence and is not to be mistaken for व्याजस्तुति stictly confined to diegunsed स्तुनिनिन्दा In व्याजोक्ति the poet mahes a party resort to some pretence (ब्याज) with the intent of concealing the real fact from others.उदयन sheds tears when put in mind o fवासदत्ता whom he regards as dead To conceal the fact that the tears were caused by the memory of वासवदत्ता,उदयन says to पद्मावती that they were caused by the pollen of काल flowers शरच्छशाङ्कगौरण साश्रुदान मुप मम॥ ( स्वप्नवासवदत्तम् ४ ७) This is व्याजोक्ति अग्निमित्र and the विदुषक have succeeded in securing the release of मालविका and from the cellar where they were loched up by धारणे finds them both in the समुद्रगृह with अग्निमित्र When challenged to account for their presence in the समुद्रगृह, अग्निमित्र
pleads। इति ( मा ४ १७ ) Here अग्निमित्र gaves a false reason in order to keep back the real fact from इत्येव This is

व्याजाक्ति again That way व्याजोक्ति has affinities with छेकापह्नुति and the second variety of पर्यायोक्त concerved by अप्पय्यदीक्षित \[ Vide अपह्नुति and पर्यायोक्त \]

 व्याजोक्ति can hardly claim any great poetical merit Its central charm does not consist in anything very poetical like अतिशयोक्ति व्याजोक्ति may give rise to some irony. it may get a certain loftiness due to the प्रौरोक्ति in which it is conveyed. But more than that there is nothing very poetical about the figure. Of course one व्याजोक्ति may be nicer than another according to the greater or lesser degree of ingenuity and subtlety involved in inventing the excuse

 **व्याजोक्ति vs अपह्नुति** In व्याजोक्ति the real fact is concealed and some unreal excuse advanced In order that the excuse advanced may seem plausible it is necessary that there should be सादृश्य between the excuse given and the real fact शिव is in horripilation due to emotion. He wants to conceal that he is seized by emotion. He therefore avers that the horripilation is due to cold Here there is This सादृश्य between emotion and cold in so far as both can equally cause horripilation Thus between व्याजोक्ति and अपह्नुति two points are common (1) Intentional (अगवर्य) concealment of the real fact and (2) Similarity between the fact desired to be kept back and the one established But whereas (1) in व्याजोक्ति the thing is concealed by the speaker keeping silent over it in अपह्नुति the thing is concealed by being actually denied. It can be easily seen how it would become ludicrous if शिव in the above instance were to say. this is *not due to emotion* but to cold .The real issue is thus suggested only in व्याजोक्ति A denial is necessary on the contrary in अपह्नुति नेद सुख चन्द्रोऽयम् Read तर्कवागीश – अत्र तु गोपनाकरिभिटन्नेन काव्याश्रितो विषयो व्यज्यत एव न स्वभिधीयत इति भाव। द्वितीयायां गोपनकृता गोवन प्रथममभिधीयते अग्र तु तवेति भाव। (॥) In व्याजोक्ति, the speaker’s aim is practical viz to keep the hearer in the dark about the real issue In अपह्नुति it is poetical argit viz to drive at the सादृश्य between the thing deried and the one affirmed and thus to exalt it Read जयरथ—अपह्नुति हि प्रकृतमेव कर्षयितुम प्रकृतस्योपपन्नम् । भरतमेव चर्पयतुम महतस्य पणनम्। इह तु उद्भिन्न सत् प्रकृत वस्तु वस्त्वन्तरेण अप्रकृतेव निगृह्य इति अनयोर्नलान् भेदः।

 ** व्याजोक्ति — छेकापह्नुति** In both one thing is concealed and another affirined But while in व्याजो-क्ति there is no equivocation or ambiguity based on द्वेष in छेकापह्नुति the charm consists in the amb-

guity due the use of श्लिष्ट words. Read कुवलयानन्द—तस्या (छेकापह्नुतौ) वचनस्य अन्यथानयनेन अपह्न-व। अस्यामाकारस्य हेचन्तरवर्णनेन गोपनम्।

 व्याजोक्ति was first admitted by वामन The figure must have been earlier still as वामन says in his वृत्ति या मायोक्ति इत्यादु। रुद्रट did not recognise it. मम्मट, रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षिन all admitted it. According to रुय्यक, व्यानोक्ति was included by उद्भट under अपह्नुति who took a wider view of the figure अपह्नुनि साम्यायापह्नवो यत्र सा विज्ञया त्वपह्नुति। अपह्नवाय सादृश्य यस्मिन्नापण्यपह्नुति। This is not borne out by उद्भट’s काव्यालङ्कार

 ** Essentials** —\[ वस्त्वन्तरप्रक्षेपरूपस्य व्यानस्य वचनाद् व्यानोक्तिः - रुय्यक \] (1) There must be a statem-ent by someone, other than the poet. (2) The statement must relate to a matter that has come to light. (3) The speaker must attribute the matter to a cause which, though probable, is not the real one (4) Thus the speaker does to keep bach the real cause (5) The real cause is sought to be concealed because the speaker feels abastied or is afraid or for some such reason

 **§ सू ६० ब्याजोक्ति** etc Construe उद्भिन्नवस्तुनः छद्मना रूपनिगूहन ब्याजोक्तिः This will make clear tha-t छद्मना goes with रूपनिगूहन in the compound उद्भिन्नवस्तु A thing that has come to light. छद्मना रूपनिगूहन concealing its real nature (रूप) by having recourse to some false pretext or excuse

 वृत्ति—निगूढमपि actually means निगुढामपि i e whatever is meant to be kept a secret कथमपि प्रभिन्न comes to light somehow केनापि etc is concealed under some pretext ( व्यपदेश )

 न चैषा etc मम्मट shows the difference between ब्याजोक्ति and अपह्नुति ब्याजोक्ति is not to be mistaken for अपह्नुति as in ब्याजोक्ति ( इह ) there is no room (....) for similarity between two viz the प्रकृत and अप्रकृत being conveyed owing to the mention of the अप्रकृत only. Emphasis on उभयनिष्ठ,

 What मम्मट means is that since in ब्याजोक्ति only the अप्रकृत is stated, there is no room for... being conveyed In अपह्नुति since both are stated there is room for it अननवत् = प्रतिपत्ततया असभक्तम्।

 मम्मट does not mean that there is no सादृश्य between प्रकृत and अप्रकृत in ब्याजोक्ति. As we saw above without such सादृश्य ब्याजोक्ति will be impossible, because the pretext advanced will seem probable only provided there is सादृश्य between the cause assigned and the actual cause Read मा धम् अदौ उभयनिह सम्यम्। अत्र तु प्रवृत्त्यैव सद्भाव तु प्राप्तनिहनेव सम्यम्।

 **St 129 शैलेन्द्र** etc illustrates व्यानोक्ति Looks like a नान्दीश्लोक.शिव who is in hompilation owing to the touch of पार्वती's hand at the nuptials explains the hompilation as due to the cold touch of the hands off हिमालय who was giving his daughter in marriage ab Dissolve शैलेन्द्रण ( हिमवता ) प्रतिपाद्यमाना या गिरिजा (पार्वती), तस्या य हस्त, तस्य यद् उपगूढ (उपगूहन = ग्रहण), तेन उल्लसन् य रोमाञ्चादि तेन विसष्टुल (upset) च असौ अखिलविधिव्यासङ्गभङ्गावुधश्च (कर्मधारय) शिव was upset by the hompi-lation and confused (आकुल) with the consequent interruption in the performance (व्यासङ्ग) of the ceremony Construe भङ्गाकुल, हा तुहिनाचलस्य करयो शैत्य इति ऊचिवान् (च), शेलन्तपुरमातृमण्डलगणै सस्मित दृष्ट (च), शिव व अवतात् हा ऊचिवान् Who exclaimed ‘How cold the hands of the Snowy Mou-ntain’ शैलान्त दृष्ट Who was smilingly looked at by the Mothers in the harem of शिव, and his गणऽ मातृमण्डल The मातृऽ or Divine Mothers are generally stated to be 7 in number Connected with शिव they attend upon his son, स्कन्द They are Energies personified गणऽ are the famous attend-ants of शिव

 ** सस्मित दृष्ट.** These Mothers were seasoned veterans too experienced to be gulled by the wily bridegroom’ They knew too well that not cold, but something clse, was at the root of the रोमाञ्च Hence their smile which is significantly mischievous’ सस्मित् does not go with उचित्रान्

 Here the रोमाञ्चादि (आदि indicates tremor ) was caused by emotion (सात्त्विकरूपतया प्रसूतौ ) but was explained away by शिव as being due to the cold touch This रोमाञ्च caused by emotion though निगूह्य, came to light and was deceitfully attributed to शैल्य by शिव Hence it is व्याजोकि

 अत्र पुलकवेपथू etc. पुळकवेपथू=वेपथू tremor Explains आदि in रोमाञ्चादि सात्विकरूपतया प्रसूतौ that had developed emotional ( सात्त्विक ) in nature ( रूप) They were due to emotion सात्त्विक Attrib-uted to सत्त्वगुण being dominant in one s dispostion सत्त्वेत्कटे मनसि ये प्रभवन्ति भक्ते सात्त्विका इति विदुर्मुनियुगवाष्पे॥Note the complete list of सात्त्विक भावऽ —.......रोमाञ्च स्वरभगोडथ वेपथु। वैधर्म्यमश्रुप्रलय इत्यष्टौ सात्त्विका मता॥शैत्यकारगतया प्रकाशितस्या दपलपितस्वरूपौ Their real nature was concealed (अपरचित्र lit. deried) as they were declared as beang due to cold ब्याजोक्तिं प्रयोजयत cause the figure ब्याजोकि

**३९ — परिसङ्ख्या**

 For the figures दृष्टान्त अनुमान and, to some extent, काव्यलिङ्ग, the आलङ्कारिकऽ were indebted to the ... who had ther दृष्टान्त, अनुमान and

हेतु For the figure परिसङ्ख्या (and another viz अर्थापत्ति, not admitted by मम्मट ), the आसङ्कारिकऽ are indebted to the पूर्वमीमांसकऽ The पूर्वमीमांसकऽ divided a विधि into three classes , अपूर्वविधि, नियमविधि and परिसङ्ख्याविधि, And it was this परिसङ्ख्या that was the prosaic forerunner of the poetical परसङ्ख्या अलङ्कार

 परिसङ्ख्या is very ingemously explained by the मीमांसकऽ परि as परिवर्तन and सङ्ख्या as बुद्धि So परिसङ्ख्या is that विधि wherein stress is laid on the that as gf Sot is that wherein stress is laid on the idea of exclusion and the परिसङ्ख्या अलङ्कार also derives its charm from its suggestion of exclusion When याण a describing King शूद्रक says योग्य य फलोद्भय, अन्तपुरिकालवेषु भङ्ग नूपुरषु मुफपरता, विवाहेषु करग्रहण....अभूत्, he wants to show (शुदकस्य ) शत्रा भय नासीत्, शत्री नासीत्, प्रजानां नासीत् प्रजानां करग्रहणं नासीत् In other words while saying यस्य etc he wanted to exclude things that characterise an ordinary king by he sought to suggest that q\*7 was an extraordinary king कालिदास says of अत्र — भयोपशान्तये ममतये बहुश्रुतम्। (र ध ८ २१ ) suggesting how अज was बल was not for what परपीढन् his विद्या for विवाह as they are in the case of an ordinary mortal Here also since the poet wants to suggest the exclusion of things that are normally found with other mortals, the figure is परिसङ्ख्या

 Thus in short, in परिसङ्ख्या the poet states one thing in order to suggest the exclusion of its likes. The thing stated is striking, not so much by itself, as owing to the suggestion of exclusion it gives rise to In fact, as मम्मट says the statement of an ordinary fact made in परिसङ्ख्या makes us look out for suggestion तद constitutes Its central charm

  श्लेष in परिसङ्ख्या Though it to is possible to have परिसङ्ख्या without श्लेष the figure becomes more picturesque when it is blendal with a caused by The two meanins that a fe word possesses make it a very convenient medium for परसङ्ख्या Tale for instance विवाहषु करग्रहणं in the instance given above करग्रहण has two meanings (1) Taking the hand and (2) Levying of taxes The first ricaring apples to विवाहऽ the second meaning apples to the thing excluded viz Read रुय्यक —           

 some are reluctant to recognise as poetical a परिसङ्ख्या where the thing excluded is actually expressed Read र ग—अन्य तु व्यावृत्तेरार्थत्वे एव परिसङ्ख्यालङ्कार, अन्यथा तु शुद्धा परिसख्यैव इत्याहु। रद्भट only recognused व्यावृतेरार्थत्वे परिसङ्ख्या अन्यत्र तु तदभाव प्रतीयते–says he

Thus we get the following scheme—

परिंसख्या

     ————————————————————————

प्रश्नपूर्विका शुद्धा

                                                                      
                     ————————————        
  —————————————

व्यापोह्यमानस्य व्यापोह्यमानस्य व्यापोह्यमानस्य
व्यापोह्यमानस्य   
शाब्दत्वे आर्थत्वे                  
शाब्दत्वे आर्थत्वे  
   १ २                   
    ३ ४  

For परिसङ्ख्या vs उत्तर, see उत्तर

 परिसङ्ख्या dates back from रुद्रट He admitted all the divisions above excepting व्यपोह्यमानस्य शाब्दले, (१ and ३ ) मम्मट, रय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ all admitted the figure and its divisions on the lines of मम्मट, अप्पय्यदीक्षित अप्पय्यदीक्षित admitted the figure but does not give its divisions

**Essentials** - \[कस्यचित् परिवर्तनेन कुत्र (१ कस्य) चित् सङ्ख्यान वर्गनीयत्वेन गणनम् - रय्यक who explains the name as meaning Counting something परिवर्तन। तव कस्यापि परिवर्तनेन कस्यचित् सरया वर्ण्यत्वेन गणनम्। सम्यक् प्रथनं वा। See above for a different explanation and possibly a better one, because it brings out वर्ज्यमानस्य प्राधान्य \] (1) The poet must state a fact too well known (2) The statement of a such a well known fact naturally suggests exclusion of facts similar to it (तादृगन्यव्यपोह ) (3) The charm lhes in the suggestion of exclusion (4) The statement may or may not be in reply to a query (5) The matter sought to be excluded, may be actually mentioned or merely suggested. (6) When suggested it is more poetical than when mentioned (7) When blended with श्लेषमूलक अतिशयोक्ति परिसङ्ख्या becomes more picturesque. (8) Sometimes the thing excluded is such as characterises an ordinary being As such its exclusion  
may exalt the matter under consideration e g यस्य च परस्पराद् भयं etc.

 ** § सू ६१ किञ्चित्** etc Construe — पृष्ट भाव) (सत्) तादृगन्वयव्यपोहाय प्रपत्न्यो सा तु परिसङ्ख्या स्मृता। पृष्टम् अपृष्टं या य-त् कथितं The thung may be stated in the form of a reply to a query, or it may be barely

 stated without a query तादृगन्य यपोहाय प्रकल्पते Leads to the exclusion (व्यापोह=व्यावृत्ति) of things sim-ilar to it ( i e thung mentioned) The mention of one thing naturally means the exclusion of other similar things. तु marks transition to a new figure

 वृत्ति—प्रमाणान्तरावगतमपि वस्तु etc. When a matter is mentioned (प्रतिपादित) even when it was already known from other sources of knowledge (प्रमाणान्तर) it results in the exclusion (व्यवच्छेद) of other matters similar to the one mentioned प्रयोजनान्तराभावात् Explains how the mention of one re-sults in the exclusion of others When a plain matter happens to be mentioned by the poet we cannot help conclud ing that by mentioning it the poct means to exclude others. What other purpose could there be we ash ourselves but this प्रमाणान्तरावगतमपि means that the matter is so well known from other sources that its mention would be quite uncalled for unless there lay some motive behind it And this motive is वस्त्वन्तरव्यवच्छेद प्रमाणान्तर source of knowledge अत्र च ete gives the divisions See the scheme gmen above तदन्यथा i e without पेज उभयत्र— i e प्रश्नपूर्वबन्धे अन्यथा वा व्यपोद्यमानस्य प्रतीयमानता वाच्यता च The thing excluded may be suggested or expressed

 ** § सूत्र ६२** Construe पूर्वस्य पूर्वस्य अर्थस्य यथोत्तर हेतुता चेत् तदा कारण माला स्यात् यथोत्तर पूर्वस्य हेतुता i e each preceding unit is the हेतु of each succeeding one. Note how our author recognises only this हेतु-कार्य variety, not कार्य-हेतु variety

 यथोत्तर उत्तर उत्तर प्रति

 **St. 134 जितेन्द्रियत्वं** etc. Illustrates कारणमाला, पूर्वपूर्वस्य हेतुत्वे A king's prosperity is the ultimate result of his self restraint

 Here the pars जितेन्द्रियत्वं विनय, विनय गुणप्रकर्ष, गुणप्रकर्ष-जनानुराग, जनानुराग सपदा are interlinked, and each one of them is made up of units related as कारण-कार्य It is therefore पूर्वपूर्वस्य हेतुत्वे कारणमाल. जगन्नाथ criticises this instance as being vitiated by भग्नप्रक्यमदोष which arises as a result of the abrupt break in style in the matter of the clauses beginning with गुणप्रकर्षो विनयादवाप्यते The style would have been uniform and therefore flawless if the wording were जितेन्द्रियत्व विनयस्य करणम्, विनय गुणप्रकर्षस्य कारणं गुणप्रकर्ष जनानुरागस्य कारणम्, जनानुरागश्च सपदा कारणम्-IS जगन्नथ’s mew That जगन्नथ’s criticism is just will have to be admitted in view of the fact that figures like कारणमाला depend for their charm more on form than substance Since uniformity of style is a very important aspect of form one can easily understand why जगनाथ insists upon it Read र ग -सर्वथैव य शब्द कार्य-कारणदोष स्थापक आदौ प्रयुक्त स एव निवाद्य। एव क्रमेण निवधन आकाक्षानुरूपत्वाद् रमणीयम्। अन्यथा तु भग्नप्रक्यम्य स्यात्। यथा प्राचीनाना पद्य-जितेन्द्रियत्व सपदा’ etc. The stanza is found in सरस्वतीकण्ठाभरण ३ रुद्रट has put the same idea आर्या form

 हेतुमता सह etc. The author attacks रुद्रट for having admitted as an अलङ्कार the identification of a cause with its effect, which he (रुद्रट) styles as हेतु अलङ्कार Such identification which is in ess-ence no more than शुद्धा सारोपा (e g आयुर्घृतम् ) or शुद्धा साध्यवसाना ( आयुरेव इदम्) based on कार्य कारणभाव is not charming enough to be styled an अलङ्कार, says he. And of the instance अविर-लकमलविकास etc. where cause is identified with its effect is recognised as good काव्य, it is due to the soft alliteration and not the कार्य-कारणयोरभेद therein argues अल्लट

 हेतुमता सह etc. Note रुद्रटऽ definition हेतुमता सह हेतोरभिधानमभेद कृद्भवेद्यत्र। सोऽलङ्कारो हेतु स्यादन्येभ्यः पृथक्भूत॥हेतु is a figure distinct from others, in which there is a statement (अभिधान) going to identify (अभेदकृत) the cause with its effect ( हेतुमत् ) \[ प्रदीप, through mstake (?) attributes thus view to उद्भट मा चं, स प्रकाशिनी and सा चू are amanmmous in attributing it to रुद्रट \] हेत्वालङ्कार is only कार्यकारणभावनूला

शुद्धा सारोपा or साध्यवसाना काल कमलविभाम etc Is शुद्धा सारोपा It would i e शुद्धा साध्यवसाना, if mod-ified as अय कमलविभाग etc. इति हेत्वलङ्कारो न See काव्यलिङ्ग Development of the figure Prof. Ban-hatti uction to उद्भट’s काव्यालङ्कारसारसङ्ग्रह pp XIX-xx) shows how the man आलङ्कारिकऽ, भामह उन्द्भट and even मम्मट ( or अल्लट ?) did not तु sponsored by the वैदर्भ school of दण्डिन् and others रुद्रट e lone Kashmirian who admitted it, but his हेतु is different i e हेतुऽ given in detaal by दण्डिन् आयुर्घृत etc .... says that onceived by रुद्रट consists in the identification of कार्य and ie आयु-र्घृतम् and is too colourless (वैचित्र्याभाव ) to be styled an भूषणता=अलङ्कारता

 *e* fails to see why अल्लट should have condemned हेललकार in ..y आयुर्घृत may not look poetical But कोकिलानन्द काल, सकला, etc have some poetical charm in them. आलङ्कारिकऽ like अलङ्काकार appreciate such cases as अतिशयोक्ति शुद्धा सारोपा like तदीय ...न and its corresponding साध्यवसाना form, समोहनमिद, based on ...., with sutable subject matter, are poetical enough Notes p 104 In place of ‘or like तदीय रूप कामिनीना समोहन’ (2nd para 2nd line), read or शुद्धा सारोपा like तदीय रूप कामिनीना समोहन and its corresponding साध्यवसाना’ \] उद्योत will call it अतिशयोक्ति, रुद्रट will say that it is हेतु But both agree in seeing some charm therein So अल्लट does not seem just in his criticism of हेत्वालङ्कार as unpoetical

 ** St. 135 अविरल** etc रुद्रट gave it to illustrate हेतु Here ( वसन्त) चाल is described as कमलविकास, अलिमद and कोकिलानन्द while in fact वसन्त is the हेतु of the कार्यऽ viz of कमलविकास, of अलिमद and of कोकिलानन्द Thus here the charm consists in describing the हेतु identical with the कार्य This is हेतु अलङ्कार of रुद्रट \[Note that विकास, मद and आनन्द are not to be interpreted as विकासयतीति विकास मदयतीति मद, आनन्दयतीनि आनन्द as this will make them actually हेतुवाचकऽ not कार्यवाचकऽ \]

 इत्यत्र काव्यरूपता etc Tradition has gaven (समाम्नासिषु ) this as a काव्य in nature only due to its soft alliteration and not because they fancted (कल्पना) any हेत्वालङ्कार beng there कोमलानुप्रा-स Soft aliteration of syllables like व्-व्, ल्-ल्-ल्, म्-म्, द्-द् कोमल whatever does not jar on the ear अनुप्रास aliteration, repetition of वर्णऽ (वर्णावृत्ति) समाम्नासिषु सम् + आ + √ म्ना Given traditionally स प्र says उद्भटादय उद्भट’s काव्यालङ्कारसारसङ्ग्रह does not refer to it मा. च. आमह उद्भटाद्या (?)

 It is difficult to determine the आलङ्कारिकऽ areas who are thus hinted at by अल्लट. Neither भामह, nor दण्डिन् nor उद्भट has the stanza in question

these are put as replies to queries, we get प्रश्नपूर्विका परिसङ्ख्या— व्यपोह्यमानस्य शाब्दत्वे.

 **St.132. कौटिल्य** etc.Illustrates शुद्धा परिसङ्ख्या—व्यमोह्यमानस्य आर्थत्वे. A compliment to the beauty of a lady and to her immunity from faults to which the heart of an ordinary being is susceptible.

 Here by कौटिल्यं कचनिचये is suggested न तु हृदि; by करचरणाधरदलेषु रागः is suggested न तु हृदि ; and so काटिन्यं कुचयुगले and तरलत्वं नयनयोः also suggest the absence of काठिन्य and तरलत्व from the heart. These are not given as replies to queries, nor are the things excluded mentioned. Hence शुद्धा परिसङ्ख्या-व्ययोह्यमानस्य आर्थत्वे. Note hoy there is श्लेष in कौटिल्य, राग, काठिन्य and तरलत्व. This makes the परिसङ्ख्या picturesque Note.again how the परिसङ्ख्या exalts the lady as being above the normal—A vein of व्यतिरेक. Stanza found in **रुद्रटऽ काव्यालङ्कार. ७.८१.**

 **St. 133. भक्तिर्भवे** etc. Illustrates in abe शुद्धा परिसङ्ख्या, व्यपोह्यमानस्य शाब्दत्वे. Some salient traits of great men. **भव = शिव न युवतिकामास्त्रे**. युवतिः एव कामास्त्रं \[cp. बाणाः पञ्चशिलीमुखस्य ललना in St. 33\].

 Here न विभवे, न युवतिकामास्त्रे, न वपुषि state the things that are sought to be excluded. भक्तिर्मवे etc. are not replies to questions. Hence शुद्धा परिमख्या, व्यपोह्यमानस्य शाब्दत्वे.

**४० — कारणमाला**

 मालदीप, कारणमाला, एकावली and सार are रशनालङ्कारऽ ( Or शृङ्खलालङ्कारऽ ) since all of them are made of units that are interlinked. like the links in a girdle. While examining the significance of the term माला, in मालादीपक, it was pointed out how it meant रशना (= शृङ्खला). The same is true of the term माला in कारणमाला also since कारणमाला is no more than कारणरशना One wonders why अल्लट ( after परिकर, अल्लट is the author) should not have put together in a group the last three at least ( कारणमाला, एकावली and सार), if he was not able to disturb the position of मालादीपक, which मम्मट was perhaps justified in placing after दीपक. If anything the assortment presented by अल्लट is promiscuous and arbitrary.

 In कारणमाला, in its generally recognised form, each preceding. thing is the cause of each succeeding one. Thus the norm becomes a (cause)-b ( effect),— b ( cause)-c (effect), —c ( cause)—d (effect) ........But **जयरथ, जगन्नाथ and अप्पय्यदीक्षित** recognise also another variety, wherein each preceding thing is the effect of each succeeding

*thing.* Thus the scheme of this variety would be a (effect)—b (cause), b (effect)—c (cause), c (effect)—d (cause).........In लभ्येत पुण्यैर्गृहिणी मनोज्ञा, तया सुपुत्रा परितः पवित्राः। स्फीतं यशस्तैः समुदेति नून, तेनास्य नित्य. खलु नाकलोकः॥(र. गं.) The series is पुण्य (C.) — गृहिणी (E.), गृहिणी (C.)—सुपुत्रा (E.), सुपुत्राः (C) —यशः (E.), यशः (C) —नाकलोकः (E.). In भवन्ति नरवाः पापात् दारिद्र्यसम्भवम्। दारिद्यमप्रदानेन तस्माद्दानपरो भवेत्॥(कु.) the series is नखा (E) – पाप (C.), पाप (E.) —दारिद्र्य (C.), दारिद्र्य (E.) – अप्रदान (C.).

 कारणमाला was first recognised by रूद्रट who did betray a *penchant* for figures in रशना form as illustrated by his रशनोपमा and रशनारूपक, अलट, रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षिन all admitted the figure. जयरथ, जगन्नाथ and अप्पय्यदीक्षित admitted an additional variety viz. where each preceding unit in the series was the कार्य of each following one.Others had admitted only that variety where each preceding unit was the कारण of each succeeding one. Read **जयरथः**— क्वचिद् विपर्ययेणापि भवति। यथा.........। अत्र हि पूर्वस्य उत्तरोत्तर कारणतयोपनिबद्धम् \।

** कारणमाला vs. मालादीपकः— **Both depend for their charm on the interlinking of units But whereas in कारणमाला the pairs of units are related as कारणचार्य or कार्यकारण in मालादीपक, there is between the two units only that shadow of connection as is due to their being related to the same धर्म. *e. g.* कोदण्डैनशराः शरै परिशिर....... **समासादितम्.** That is, in कारणमाला, the charm hes only in the शृङ्खला based on कार्यकारणभाव. In मालादीपक, it hes in a शृङ्खला based on साधर्म्य, though superficial due to theधर्म being stated once only.

** Essentials:— **\[ कारणानां माला शृङ्खला. Though there is कार्यमाला also, the name is accounted for as being due to the poet's greater emphasis on कारणऽ Read अत्र यद्यपि कार्याणामपि माला वियते तथापि कारणगुणवर्णनमेव वदिसंरम्भ इति शिक्षापूर्विका हि शब्दार्थप्रतिपति इति न्यायन कारणमालेत्यभिधानम्। — **पण्डित दुर्गाप्रसाद on सा. द.\]** (1) There must be a chain of कारण-कार्यऽ or कार्य-कारणऽ. (2) The larger the chaun the more striking it becomes (3) The charm 29 inherent in the cham of कार्य-कारणऽ also. (4) Therefore even such a chain based on moral truths like जितेन्द्रियत्वं विनयस्य कारण, गुणप्रकर्षो विनयादवाप्यते is deemed poetical enough. (5) There should be uniformuty of expression throughout the series, to convey कार्य-कारणभाव.See how this as violated in the expressions विनयम्य कारण and विनयादवाप्यते.(6) Agrin, in the case of interfinking words, they should be the same.Synonyms if used detract from the charm. Thus if गृहिणी is the word in the preceding pair, in the following गृहिणी should be used, not a synonym like कल्प or भार्या.

Or does अल्लट merely refer to the stanza being in vogue among सहृदयऽ ? In the abensce any defimte data, we can nesther trust or distrust अल्लट's कोमलानुप्रासमहिम्नैव समाम्नासिषु न तु हेत्वलङ्कारकल्पनया' Today, the stanza will. be appreciated as much for its कोमलानुप्रास as for its अतिशयोक्ति

 One thing is certain After रुद्रट, विश्वनाथ alone accepted the figure हेतु on रुद्रट's lines आलङ्कारिकऽ called it अतिशयोक्ति based on कार्यकारणमूला शुद्धा सारोपा or कार्यकारणमूला शुद्धा साध्यवसाना लक्षणा उद्भट's innovation of काव्यहेतु (or काव्यलिङ्ग) became popular and ousted हेत्वालङ्कार, अप्पय्यदिक्षित has हेत्वलङ्कार where a cause and its effect are givene g असौ उदेति शीताशुर्मानच्छेदाय सुभ्रुवाम्

 **इति पूर्वोक्त काव्यलिङ्गमेव हेतु'** What अल्लट means is that a poetical हेतु should be something different from कार्यकारणयोरैक्य underlying रुद्रटऽ हेतु-And if one asks what it should be like,अल्लट points to the figure काव्यलिङ्ग, es coming up to the expected standard of a poetical हेतु

**४१ — अन्योन्य**

 We have seen how, an उपमेयोपमा, the charm hes at least partly, in the reciprocaty of उपमानत्व and उपमेयत्व, and आलङ्कारिकऽ, as we saw, attempt to incorporate reciprocity even in विनोक्त to lend it an addıtional charm In the figure अन्योन्य, we find reaprocity as the principal feature

 The poet in अन्योन्य, describes two objects as doing the same action (क्रिया), one in relation to the other, or bestowing the same attribute (गुण), one upon the other This reciprocity looks poetical because it involves the विरोध implicit in the subject of an action be coming its object and *vice versa* In त्रियामा शशिना भाति शशी भाति त्रियामया ( कु ), where the poet describes त्रियामा and शशिन् as lending grace to each other, we have अन्योन्य The figure will look more striking if adentical words are used to describe the objects, किया etc cp शशिन् शशिन्, त्रियामा नियामा, भाति-भाति in the instance above If the words were शशी-चन्द्र, त्रियामारात्रि, भाति-शोभते, the figure would have been there, but not in a striking form Read **रा तर्कवागीश** – अन कारणयो क्रिययोश्च शब्दैक्य वैचित्र्यपरिपोषकमेव न तु नियामकम् \।

 The figure **अन्योन्य** dates back from रुद्रट उद्भट ( काव्यप्रकाश ), रुय्यक,विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ अप्पय्यदीक्षित all adimutted at

 **Essentials — **\[ अन्योन्य क्रियाधान, गुणाधान वा अत्र \] ( 1 ) It is the description of two things (2) They must do the same क्रिया to each other,

or bestow the same गुण on each other Read जयरथ —क्रियाशब्देन धर्मो लक्ष्यते। Again **रा तर्कवागीश—** क्रियापद गुणम्याप्युपत्रणम्। तेन विशया कविरश्चन्द्र चन्द्रण रविरा निशा इत्यप्राति अयम् अलङ्कार (3) If the actions are differ चिरचिरा निशा' इत्यत्रानि अर्थ अवार ent, the figure will not be there, as in बार्षिक सञ्जहरिन्गे धनुर्जैग रघुर्दधौ (र थं ४ १६) where इन्द्र withdraws his bow and रघु
brings his into use (4) Identity of words to describe the धर्मिन् and धर्म will be more poetical though this is not vital to the figure. (5) The form may be A does x to B, B does x to A Or it may be A and B do x to each other. That is अन्योन्य may be embodied in two वाक्यऽ or one वाक्य When embodied in two वाक्यऽ it is more direct. (6) Sometimes there may be the description of two objects producing each other which is no more than a विरोधभास, as it can be accounted for This .....................................I, is अन्योन्य because धन and प्रज्ञा belong to जयरथ यदि पुनरत्र विरोधसमधि ......... (7) The क्रिया or गुण should be उपपर्व An अन्योन्य based on अपरप वप क्रिया or गुण would be ironical

 ** § सू ६३ क्रियया** etc. Construe य तुनो परम्परं क्रियया तु जयते अन्योन्यं भाति वस्तुनो परस्परं क्रियया जनने When there is mutual creation by two things by their action What is meant by mutual creation by action' is विरोधन The idea is When two things beston the same विशेष on each other the thing appearing with a विरोध is conceived is being born (नर) therewith Note that the sta emert does not refer to actual mutual creation which being fiz विरूद्ध cannot be the theme of poetry Even in the well known Vedic lines........। there is only विरोधभाग not real forविरोध क्रियया शिवपा Note that the fi- must be the name in both the ensee

But the वृत्ति connects the word with सार. Cp मिथ श्रीसारता सपादनद्वारेण By making the beauty of each other exalted (सार )

 Here the Swans and ponds are described as exalting each other's glory (श्री सार्यते) They do the same action to each other, hence अन्योन्य

 Lookang to the definitions of अन्योन्य by different आलङ्कारिकऽ, it would seem that the figure will be possible both where the गुणक्रियाऽ are उत्कर्षावह or अपकर्षावह But their instances are confined to उत्कर्ष only An अन्योन्य based on अपकर्षावह क्रिया or गुण would be more comic than poetical

**४२ — उत्तर**

 उत्तर is a reply, a retort a rejoinder, and the figure denives its name from the reply or replies it embodies Sometimes the poet gives the bare reply from which the question could be imagined (उन्नेय) by an easy guess This is उन्नेयप्रश्न उत्तर In this variety प्रश्न is to be understood as a question or merely as a remark that occa sioned the particular उत्तर Sometimes, however, the figure takes the form of several questions and answers the answers being too striking to occur to ordinary intelligence. This is निबद्धप्रश्न उत्तर.आलङ्कारिकऽ specify one reply as being enough to constitute the first variety But the second variety cannot be charming according to them, unless we have an array of questions and answers **रुय्यक — **यत्र च प्रश्नपूर्वक असभावनीय उत्तर तच्च न सकृत्। तावन्मात्रे चारुत्वाप्रतीते। र ग **— **उन्नीतप्रश्ने सकृदुत्तरस्य चारुत्वम्। निबद्धप्रश्ने तु प्रश्नोत्तरयोरसकृदुपन्यासे तदिति प्राञ्च। अतश्च असकृन्निवधे द्वितीयमुत्तरम्। Some, however, say that even in the second variety, one उत्तर would be striking enough, if the प्रश्न and the उत्तर are साकूत i e. full of significance Read **र ग** वस्तुतस्तु प्रश्नोत्तरयोराकूतगर्भत्वे तावतेव चमत्कारात् न असकृदुपादानापेक्षा। आकूताविरहे तु असकृदुपादानकृत चमत्कारोऽपेक्ष्यते निबद्धप्रश्ने।

 In the second vanety, the उत्तरऽ being striking enough and numerous enough may give rise to the figure But why should the in the first varety (उन्नयप्रश्न) be looked upon as poetical? The answer lies in the fact that here also the though one is very poetical, so much so that it at once suggests the प्रश्न Such उत्तरऽ (of the 1st variety) are often साकूत i e. significant and embody शृगार of the most unorthodox type And it is not an idle exaggeration to say that a good many गाथाऽ are उत्तरऽ of this class. These उत्तरऽ and.

theirs प्रश्नऽ that could be easily guessed could well find a place in any short story of Maupassant. This will explain the significance of the आलङ्कारिकऽ' statement that from the उत्तर of the first kind, the प्रश्न could be guessed. Of course, there is bound to be a difference of degrees between one उत्तर and another. Some may be more शृङ्गारिक than others. The second vanety of उत्तर is, in a way, less poetical because शृङ्गार may or may not be there. There it is not so indispensable as in the 1st variety.

 In वीक्षितु न क्षमा श्वश्रूः स्वामी दूरतर गत। अहमेकाविनी बालग तवैह वसतिः कुत॥(स.द), where we can guess a proposal by someone to stay with the speaker, we have उत्तर (2) In किं मरण दारिद्र्य कौ व्याधिजीवित दरिद्रस्य। कः स्वर्गः सन्मित्र मुकलत्र सुप्रभु सुमुत॥(रुद्रट ८.७३. ) we get उत्तर ( २ ).

 Thus we get the following scheme :—

**उत्तर**

**।**

**———————————————————————**

      उन्नेयप्रश्ने                         
           निबद्धप्रश्ने  
        (1)                          
              (2)  

 र ग gives further sub divisions based on साकूतत्व being present or absent in the प्रश्नऽ and उत्तरऽ

** उत्तर (१) vs काव्यलिङ्ग—** In उत्तर (१), the उत्तर may be taken as समर्थ्य and the प्रश्न (which is guessed) its समर्थक, just as in काव्यलिङ्ग,one thing isसमर्थक of another. But whereas (1) in उत्तर (2), only the समर्थ्य is mentioned, in काव्यलिङ्ग, both the समर्थ्य and समर्थक are mentroned (2) In उत्तर (१), उत्तर 1s the शापकहेतु of the प्रश्न, because it indicates the latter, in काव्यलिङ्ग, one thing is the कारकहेतु of another. *e g.* अनमन as the कारकहेतु of अपराध,

 ** उत्तर (१) Vs अनुमानः— **Just as in अनुमान, धूम is the शापकहेतु of वह्नि, similarly, in उत्तर (1), Bar is the शापकहेतु of प्रश्न . But while (1) in उत्तर (१), the उत्तर and प्रश्न abide in different individuals (one questions, another replies) in अनुमान the वह्नि and धूम abide in the same धर्मिन् or पक्ष (1) While in उत्तर (१), only the हेतु viz.उत्तर is stated, and प्रश्न is to be guessed, अनुमान in, both ar साध्य and साधन are stated.

 ***उत्तर (2) vs. प्रश्नपूर्विका परिसङ्ख्याः— ***In उत्तर (१) there is a series of questions and answers as in प्रश्नपरिसङ्ख्या. But whereas in उत्तर ( २ ), the उत्तरऽ themselves are felt to be striking, inपरिसङ्ख्या, the charm lies

in the suggestion of exclusion of similar things When the व्यपोह्यमान is शाब्द (e. g किं भूषण सुदुढमत्र, यशो न रत्नम्), there is no room for प्रश्नपरिमरया being mistaken for उत्तर ( २ ) But when the व्यपोह्यमान is आर्य (e g. किमासेव्य etc.), it is likely to be confused with उत्तर (२) And though the difference between the two has been specified, in actual practice, the line of demarcation between the figures may be too faint In such cases, one has only to justify one's viewpoint Thus if St. 138 का विषमा etc is looked upon by some as परिसङ्ख्या, suggesting the exclusion of प्रभोरिच्छा, धन etc. it will involve no error

 उत्तर was first conceived by रुद्रट He had two independent figures of the name (793 and 872) which by अदृष्ट रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ and जगन्नाथ came to be looked upon as two sub-divisions of the same figure. जयरथ, however, holds them to be two separate figures अपय्यदीक्षित has the first variety, but instead of the second he has one in which प्रश्न and उत्तर are embodied in the same words

 ** Essentials –** \[ उत्तर, उत्तराणि वा अस्मिन् \] ( 1 ) in the first vanety,the उत्तर must be stated (2) It must be sinking enough and from it the सहृदय must be able to guess the प्रश्न or remark which occasioned it ( 3 ) More often than not, its keynote is शृगार (4) When it is साकूत=साभिप्राय and conveys a veled suggestion of शृङ्गार, it is more arresting e. g यत्रासौ वेतसी पान्थ तत्रेय सुतरा सरित्॥\[ सरित्तरणमार्गे पृच्छन्त प्रति त वामनमानाया उत्तरमिदम्। वेतसीक्रुजे स्वाच्छन्दमिति आकूतगर्भम्।—कु \] (5) In the second variety of उत्तर there must be a series of questions and answers (6) These answers must be लोकातिक्रान्तगोचर i e too striking to occur to ordinary intelligence (7) The theme in this variety may or may not be शृङ्गार (8) Though in this variety, प्रश्नऽ and उत्तरऽ are generally numerous still if there is आकूत in them even one प्रश्न and its उत्तर would look poetical

** § सू ६४ उत्तरश्रुति etc**. Construe यत्र उत्तरश्रुतिमात्रत प्रश्नस्य उन्नयन क्रियते तत् (एक) उत्तरम्। असकृत् तत्र (=प्रश्ने) वा सति यद् असकृत् असभाव्य उत्तर स्यात् तत् (द्वितीय) उत्तरम्। प्रश्नस्य उन्नयन = प्रश्नस्य कल्पनम् प्रश्न need not be strictly a query It may also be a simple remark which occasioned the उत्तर in question रूद्रट has पूर्ववचनाता Instead of प्रश्न The वृत्ति rightly renders प्रश्न by पूर्नवाक्य उन्नयन is found in रुद्रट's definition

 तत्र = तस्मिन् — प्रश्ने असकृत् goes with this and उत्तर The प्रश्नऽ must be many and so the replies thereto **असभाव्य** The उत्तर must be striking लोकातिक्रान्तगोचर as the वृत्ति says This is what makes this variety charming even when there be no शृङ्गार therein असकृत् this

is another source of charm A solitary question and answer will have no charm unless they are साकूत

 ** वृत्ति – प्रतिवचनोपलम्भादेव उपलम्भ = ज्ञान** The उत्तर must enable the सहृदय to guess the प्रश्न or पूर्वक्वय We have seen why the प्रश्न is drawn attention to Both प्रश्न and उत्तर in this variety are साकूत equally

 ** St 137 वाणिअअ** etc Illustrates उत्तर ( १ ) The reply made by an aged श्वशुर showing how his son has ceased to take interest in a huntsman s pursuits ever since he got wedded to a lovely lass Addressed to a tradesman dealing in ivory and hides Sanskrit rendering वाणिनक हस्तिना कुतऽस्माक व्याग्रकृत्तयश्च। यावगुल्लिाहरमुसा गृहे शरिष्वकति स्नुषा \। **परिप्वक्वति** moves about **लुलिनाटकमुखी** explains why the son has lost interest in his calling!

 This one can see is a reply to a query made by some merchant for hides and ivory The undertone of शृङ्गार due to which the son has become an idler cannot escape the notice of the सदृश्य It is therefore उत्तर (१)

 अह अधो I requre them ता हस्तिवन्त and व्याप्रकृत्तिऽ (Tiger skins ) मूल्येन प्रयच्छ Because he was a dealer क्रेतुर्वचन क्रेतृ the purchaser समुन्नीयते can be guessed
