 ** न चैतत् काव्यलिङ्ग जनको हेतु** — The author differentiates be tween उत्तर (१) and काव्यलिङ्ङ्ग In काव्यतिङ्ग, the poet gives a circumstance which produces something else or in other words is a जनक हेतु Here the reply cannot be viewed as a जनक हेतु of the प्रश्न The reply only indicates the प्रश्न and is therefore its व्यापक हेतु एतत् i e उत्तर (१) **उत्तरस्य ताद्रूप्यानुपपत्ते** It would be improper (अनुपपत्ति) to hold the reply as being of that nature (ताद्रूप्य — काव्यलिङ्गरूपत्व) **नहि** etc explans why there is no काव्यलिङ्गरूपत्व Construe प्रतिवचन प्रश्नस्य जनको हेतु न The reply is not the जनकहेतु of the प्रश्न It is its ज्ञापक हेतु \[ See above काव्यलिङ्ग *vs* उत्तर (१)

 **नापीदमनुमानम् अनिदशात्— **उत्तर (१) distingushed from अनुमान If उत्तर is ज्ञापकहेतु one mmght feel that the figure उत्तर ( १ ) is अनुमान where we get धूम as the ज्ञापकहेतु of बह्नि But in अनुमान साध्य (वह्नि) and साधन (धूम) are stated as abiding in one धर्मिन् VIZ पर्वत Here साध्य (प्रश्न ) abides in a different person (धर्मिन्) from the one in whom the साधन (उत्तर) abides Because question is made by one reply by another

Nor are *both* साध्य-साधन stated in उत्तर (१) as they are in अनुमान. Here उत्तर, the साधन alone is stated Hence the two are different

 **इति अलङ्कारान्तरमेव उत्तर साधीय —** Therefore better to take उत्तर as a separate figure This suggests that there was a view that was disinclined to look upon उत्तर (१) as an अलङ्कार

 **प्रश्नादन्तर** etc 2nd variety explained असभाव्य 15 लोकानिकान्तगोचर Every question must have a striking reply.**रूद्रट** calls this शातात् अन्यत् other than what is commonly known **अनयो** प्रश्नोत्तरयो **सकृदुपादाने न चारुताप्रतीति** But in case there is आकृत in the question and reply, even a single query and reply are enough to make this figure

 **St 138 का विसमा** etc Illustrates उत्तर (२) Some worldly truths given in the form of rephes to questions Sanskrit rendering — का विषमा दैवगति किं दुर्लभ यत् जनो गुणग्राहा। किं सौख्य मुकलत्र किं दुःख यत् खले लोक॥कि दुर्लभ etc It is rare to come across a person who can appreciate ments

 Here, दैवगति गुणग्राही नन, सुकलत्र and खले लोक are repres that could not have occurred to an ordinary mind They are striking and more so with the series they present Thus it is उत्तर (२)

** प्रश्नपरिसङ्ख्याया** etc उत्तर (२) distinguished from प्रश्नपरिसङ्ख्या, where the thing excluded is left to suggestion and not expressed In उत्तर(2), In the charm lies in the array itself of उत्तरऽ In other words, in उत्तर (2) the mind does not go further than the वाच्यार्थ (वाच्ये एव विश्रान्ति ) In प्रश्नपरिसङ्ख्या (cp किमासेव्य पुंसा etc.), on the other hand, the only aim (तात्पर्य) of the poet is to suggest the exclusion of other similar things (अन्यव्यपोह) Hence its scope extends further than the वाच्यार्थ This is the difference (विवेक) between the two figures e In इह i e in उत्तर (२) \[ See उत्तर (२) vs प्रश्नपूर्विका परिसख्या given above \]

**४३ — सूक्ष्म**

 The essence of the figure‘सूक्ष्म’ would be more easily comprehended when it is realised that सूक्ष्म means in fact सूक्ष्मप्रकाशन The charm of the figure lies first in noticing a matter which is subtle enough to baffle an ordinary observer, and then in communicating this, or something relevant to it, to another in a very picturesque way Says a gallant मयि पश्यति सा केशै सीमन्तमणिमावृणोत् (कु) The नायिका in question looks to the नायिका and from his appearance understands the subtle fact that the नायक seeks to know the सङ्केतकाल, when they

might meet She covers up the सीमान्तमणि and thereby signifies that the time should be when the sun (दिनमणि) sets Here firstly the lady observes the subtle desire on the part of the नायक, and then in response thereto she has recourse to the picturesque action of closing the सीमन्तमणि with her hatr This is सूक्ष्म

 We have only to look to the different instances of सूक्ष्म to under stand how, likeउत्तर (१), it is primarily concerned with शृङ्गार It might be even said that in a good many cases of its occurrence, though not in all उत्तर (१) is a verbal message of love while सूक्ष्म is a message by symbols And just as उत्तर (१) arrests our attention with its note of शृङ्गार, so does सूक्ष्म too

 In सूक्ष्म the following are the two stages clearly marked out A party comes to know some subtle matter regarding another due to some peculianty in his form (आकार) or some significant gesture (इगित) on his part This is the first stage In the second stage, the party has recourse to some significant gesture, (like closing of the सीमन्तमणि in the instance above, or closing of the day lotus) indicating sometimes merely his knowledge of the subtle fact or sometimes his reply to the issues raised by the subtle fact. In प्रिय गृहागते प्रात कान्ता तत्पमकल्पयत् (कु), the wife merely indicates by preparing the bed that she has known स्वपत्नीगृहे जागरण on the part of her husband In मयि पश्यति etc., on the other hand, the नायिका by her gesture of closing the सीमान्तमणि gives a reply to the gallant whose स्त्रोत्रकात् जिज्ञासा she has seen through by his gestures etc जयरथ. speaks of a view acc to which another variety of सूक्ष्म consists merely in the detection of the सूक्ष्म thing and does not refer to its प्रकाशन. Read तत्र अयमाशय। यत्सूक्ष्मस्य अर्थस्य संलक्षणमत्रं प्रकाशनमात्र वा अपि अयमेवलकारः।

 सूक्ष्म is an old figure.भामह refuses to recognise it while दण्डिन् admitted it. Neither उद्भट् nor वामन recogrused it. रूद्रट's सूक्ष्म was of an altogether different kind मम्मट, रूय्यक, विश्वनाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित admitted it The last covered the scope of सूक्ष्म by two figures सूक्ष्म and पिहित रसगङ्गाधर has no सूक्ष्म

 Note the following scheme—

सूक्ष्म

————————————————————————

शब्दार्थे हो                               
  इति शब्दार्थे हो

 **Essentials** \[ सूक्ष्म तीक्ष्णमतिसवेद्य वस्तु ज्ञायते प्रकाश्यते च अत्र \] (1) By some pecuharity in the form (आकार) or by some gesture of one party, another must come to know of a thing which is of a subtle nature (2) As soon as the thing is known, the party must resort to a gesture of a significant nature like the closing of a lotus etc.(3) This gesture may indicate merely the knowledge of the subtle thing by the other party (4) Or the gesture may be in reply to some issue raised by the knowledge of the subtle matter, such as a desire on the part of the first party to know something (5) The figure becomes charming (a) due to knowledge of the subtle thang, and (b) due to the striking way in which the knowledge or message is Indicated (6) शृङ्गार is the heynote of the figure

 ** § सू ६५ कुतोऽपि** etc Construe— यत्र कुतोऽपि लक्षित सूक्ष्मो अपि अर्थ केनचिद् धर्मेण अन्यस्मै प्रकाश्यते तत् सूक्ष्म ( इनि) परिचक्षत। कुतोऽपि लक्षित The matter though subtle happens to be detected due to some circumstance (कुत अपि ) The वृत्ति gives the circumstance to be either आकार or इङ्गित सूक्ष्म subtle i e such as cannot be observed by anyone but an astute सहृदय **केनचिद् धमण अन्यस्मै प्रकाश्यते** is brought to the notice of another party by having recourse to some matter केनचिद् धर्मेण only refers to a gesture like सीमन्तमणिपिधान or लीलापद्मनिमीलन साहित्यदर्पण calls this कयाचिद् भग्या Note that the word धर्म here has the shade of meaning it possesses in the Buddhistic Plulosophy viz existence, matter पदार्थ etc.

 वृत्ति— कुतोऽपि explained as आवाराद् इगिताद् वा The subtle matter may be known from the आकार or इगित of the other party आकार is particular detail in the form eg broken saffron or paleness of appearance etc. इङ्गित 1s a significant gesture of the eye etc Note that दण्डिन् has इङ्गिताकारलक्ष्योऽर्थं सौक्ष्म्यात् सूक्ष्म इति स्मृत।** तर्कवागीश (सा द )** explains आकारो धर्म \। इगित क्रियाभङ्गी विदग्धचेष्टा **सूक्ष्म** is explained as such as could be marked only by one of acute intelligence (तीक्ष्णमति)

 **St 139 वक्त्रस्यदि** etc. Illustrates सूक्ष्म, अकाराद् अर्थे ज्ञाने Descnption as to how a certan female detected 'पुरुषायित' of her friend by the broken saffron at the throat and indicated her knowledge of the fact by drawing the likeness of a sword on her friend's hand. Construe कपि वयस्था तन्व्या कण्ठे स्वेदषि दुप्रबन्धे युयुम मिश्र दृष्ट्वा तन्व्या सुस्त्वं व्यजयन्ती स्मित्वा पणौ स्वङ्गलेखा लिलेख।

 **पुस्त्य or पुरुषायित** an abnormal form of contion (विपरीतमुख) suggested by the स्वेदेत कण्ठकुसुमभेद **Read महेश्वरभट्ट cated by झलकीकार—**

प्रसिद्धरतौ उत्तानायाः नायिकायाः बक्त्राद् गलितस्य स्वेदस्य पृष्ठभागे एव गमनम्। कण्ठे तद्गमनं तु विपरीतरौ एव इति वक्त्रस्यदि-इत्यादेरभिप्रायः।

 Here पुरपायित of a lady, though subtle, is yet detected by her friend, from the आकार viz. the saffron at the throat getting washed out by perspiration. The friend lets the lady know that she has knowledge of it by drawing the likeness of a sword on her friend's palm. Thus a subtle fact being known from आकार is here conveyed to the other party by the stnking mode of असिलनालैसा.This is therefore सूक्ष्म, आकारादर्थे इति।

** अत्र etc. आकृति आलोक्य** The आकृति refers to the कुसुम at the कण्ठ getting washed out by perspiration. **कयापि वितर्कितं पुरुषायितं** i.e.one lady guessed that her friend had indulged in a पुरुषायित असिलता etc. And she brought her knowledge of the fact to the notice of her friend, in the most shrewd manner (वैदग्ध्यात्) viz by drawing on her friend's palm the likeness of a sword वैदग्ध्यात् As we saw above, the charm of सूक्ष्म lies also in the picturesque action resorted to.पुसामेष etc. Shows the propriety of the symbol of the sword to convey her knowledge of her friend's पुरुषायित A sword is carned by a पुष्प. Hence the symbol indicated that her friend had indulged m an act (viz. पुरुषायित) fit for a पुरुष. Tvm Ed. reads पुंसामेव कृपाणस्य पाणियोग्यत्वात्

 **St. 140 सङ्केतकाल** etc Illustrates सूक्ष्म, इतिताद् अर्थे इति A flirt responds by a significant gesture to the desire on the part of her paramour to fix up a सङ्केलकाल ईषर्थेयार्पितकृतं (v. 1. हसमेत्रार्पिताकृतं) qualifies विट The आकूत (=अभिप्राय) of the विट could be seen from the sly movements of his eye Construe ईषन्नेप्रार्पिताकूतं विट सङ्केतकालमनस शञ्च विदग्धया लीलापद्म निमीलितम्। लीलापद्म निमीलितं Thus indicated that the time of सङ्केत would be night when lotuses close विट a gallant.लीलापद्म a lotus taken for amusement or sport.

 Here the सूक्ष्म अभिप्राय of the विट was known from his ईगित or gestures (cp ईषत्रार्पितकृत) by the lady. And in response thereto she makes the sien of लीलापद्मनिमीलन suggesung सन्ध्यासमय as सङ्केतकाल.Hence this is a, सूक्ष्म इगितात् अर्थे इति Note that here the sign is not merely to and cate knowledge of the subtle matter viz अभिप्राय but to respond to that अभिप्राय Response naturally presupposes knowledge of the matter.

 कयाचिद् इङ्गितमात्रेण विदित —The इगित or gesture with his eye by the विट, implied in ईषन्नेत्र वेगकूतम् इगितेन, says रुय्यक-

निशासमयशसिना कमलनिमीलनेन By closing the lotus she indicated निशासमय 25 सङ्केतकाल Lotuses close at night लीलया cP वैदग्ध्यात् above. Gracefully The way to indicate her response was graceful indeed Note that these signs were resorted to, to keep others in the dark about the affair !

**४४ — सार**

 सार means the cream or the pick of things and the figure consists in giving a senes of things, that by a graded rise leads us to the very psck of them. Says the poet मधुर मधु तन्माच्च सुधा तस्या' क्वेर्ववः। (कु) Here the series, मधु — सुधा—वत्वेर्वेच', consists of unmts of which each following is graded higher than each preceding in the matter of माधुर्य, and culmunates in कत्रेर्वच which is the pick of मधुर things This Is सार. In सतारे चेतनाम्ना विद्वांसस्तत्र साधव। माधुष्वपि स्पृहाहीनास्तेषु धन्या निरासया॥ ( र. ग ), we have another instance of सार, because here we समार —चेतन —विद्वान् —साधु—निस्पृह—निराशय—consisting of omts that show a graded nse in धन्यत्व and culminate in निराशय the very cream of धन्य things. In both these instances, the gradation was in point of धर्म Viz माधुर्य धन्यत्व Sometimes the gradation does not hinge upon धर्म, but स्वरूप of the things constituting the series.The well known lines महत परमव्यक्त अव्यक्तात्पुरुष पर। पुरुषान्न पर किञ्चित् सा काष्ठा सा परा गति give us a series wherein the graded rise is in the matter of the स्वरूप of the different units Read जयरथ —उत्तरोत्तरसमुच्चय स्वरूपेण धर्मे वा भवति। Again र ग — गुणस्वरूपाभ्या पूर्वपूर्ववैशिष्ट्ये सार।

 Since, in सार we get series of interlinked units it is one of the अलङ्कारऽ based on रशना form. It therefore forms a class with मालदीपक कारणमाला, and एकावली

 एकविषय सार *Should it be admitted *? आलङ्कारिकऽ like जयरथ and जगन्नाथ have admitted another vanety of सार. It consists in describing the gradual nse of the same object to the very peak.In other words, in this variety, the poet describes how the same object passes through successive stages until it reaches the climax. In नम्बोरश्रियमतिलध्य लोल्यैव व्यानश्रीकृतकमनीयहमकुम्भौ। नीलाम्मोहनयनेऽधुना कुचौ ते स्पर्धेते खलु कनकाचलेन सार्धन्॥we find how the कुचऽ of a particular सुन्दरी attained larger and larger size until they evalled the golden मरू This जयरथ and जगन्नाथ call एकविषय सार, while the one in मधुर मधु etc. or m ससारे चेतना etc. or in महत परमव्यक्तं etc. as अनेकविषय, according to them, because it is based on a series of different units Read जयरथ— एतच्च एकस्यैव वस्तुन बहूना वा स्यादिति अस्य द्वैधम्।

 एकविषय सार has no रशना therean while अनेकविषय सार is रचना in form Hence there was a school of आलकारिकऽ that was reluctant to admit एकविषय सार जगन्नाथ actually refers to such a school -- एकविषये शृङ्खलया अचारुत्वात् तद्नुप्राणित सारो न चारुता धत्ते। तस्या स्वाभाविकभेदापेश्विन्वेन अवस्था अनुलामात्। अत एव अस्मिन् विषये वर्धमानकालवारोऽन्ये अङ्गीकृतः।

 ** एकविषय सार vs पर्याय (१)** -This brings us to the difference between these figures एकविषय सार is very similar to पर्याय (१) wherein there is a regular आरोह in the different positions of one ob ect. एकविषय सार US But (1) whereas in पर्याय (१) the object leaves one position before assuming another in सार the object need not leave its original position Thus विम्बोष्ठ एव रागस्त etc. (St 123) is taken by जगन्नाथ as a case of एकविषय सार because there राग does not leave the विम्बोष्ठ when it goes to the हृदय (2) In पर्याय (2)— the charm lies in the same object shifting its places In एकविषय सार, it hes in the gradual ascent But it must be admitted that, in some cases. leading to the climax the stanza may be as much an instance of one figure as that of the other

 **एकबिषय सार *vs* पर्याय (२)** – In both we get different cond tions of the same object But while in एकविषय सार, the poet has his eye on the uniform आराह in पर्याय (2) the poet is concerned merely with Whether the variety of phases through which the object passes these phases are of regular ascent or not is immaterial to him in पर्याय (२) Here also the chances of both the figures being equally present in the same stanza are equal as in the case of पर्याय (१)

 Looking therefore to the chances of एकविषय सार being confused with पर्याय (१) and (२), it is safer not to admit एकविषय सार at all as आलङ्कारिकऽ have generally done

 **उत्कर्ष and अपकर्ष— **Up to now, we have been talking of सार wherein there is a grided उत्कर्ष अपकर्ष can be of things good or bad Therefore अप्पय्यदीक्षित says that we can have सार based on अध्यगुणेाकार्य Or मरायगुणात्कर्ष This is the same as जगन्न 4's सार based on उत्कर्ष and अपकर्ष all or wat Thus in सार we can have climax and also antich max Says a poet ..........च याचक। वायुना किं न ....... समय प्रष्ययेदिति॥(कु) here we get a graded descent leading to that ... of..... things-the needy beggar

 रूद्रट first concerved the figure सम्ब अल्लट (काव्यप्रकाश) रूय्यक विश्वनाथ जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित all admitted it and रूय्यक called it उपर अप्पय्यदीक्षित and जयरथ had सार based on अपकर्ष or both.

 **Essentials - ***\[* धाराधिराहितया भार उत्कर्ष अत्र \] (1) There must be a शृखल of interlinked units (2) These units must rise by graded ascent (आरोह) till the climax is reached ( 3 ) The rise may be in the matter of some attribute (धर्म) of the units or the units them selves (स्वरूप) (4) There can be a सार of उत्कृष्ट or भपकृत्र things (5) सार of अपकृष्ट things leads to anticlimax (6) When the सार is एकविषय, there is no शृखला only the gradually rising phases of the object are set forth (7) It is not necessary that the series should close only at the end of the stanza It may be completed even in half the stanza Read **तर्कवागीश** – केचित्तु आष्टोक्समापन उत्तरोत्तरोत्कर्षेण अयम् अलङ्कारो भवति तथात्वे एव वैचित्र्यविशेषप्रतिपत्ते इत्याहुः। तन्न। श्लोकार्थममाप्तिपर्यन्त उत्कर्षसत्त्वन अपि अस्य अलङ्कारस्य सम्भवात्।

 ** § सू ६६ उत्तरोत्तर** etc. Construe परावधि उत्तरोत्तर उत्कर्ष सार परावधि The उत्कर्ष comes to a stop when it reaches the पर, the highest of the series ( पर अवधि यस्य ) **उत्तरोत्तर उत्कर्ष** It is a graded rise We have seen how जगन्नाथ and अप्पय्यतीक्षित have a सार of अपकृष्ट things also

 **वृत्ति** – पर = पयन्तभाग The peal chmax apex **पर अवधि यस्य परावधि** explained अवधि terminus limit The उत्कर्ष does not go further than the chmax **धाराधिरोहितया विश्रान्त** Explains why पर Is अवधि The rise comes to a stop there ( तत्रव i e पयन्तभाग ) through a graded ascent ( धाराधिरोहिता ) **धाराधिरोहिता—**Graded or gradual ascent उत्तरोत्तरधारावाही उत्कर्ष — says स्वप्रदायप्रकाशिनी The धारा of तैल, for instance typifies a gradual tapering

 **St 141 राज्ये सार** etc Illustrates सार The poets estimate of a paradise on earth **सार** sc वस्तु सार adj The best the choicest राज्ये सार वसुधा The choicest kingdom will be one comprising the whole earth **अनगसर्वस्व** in apposition to बरागना सौध palace

 Here the series राज्य - वसुधा पुर सौध-ताप बरागन presents a gradually rising ascent to the chmax based on सारत्व or chorceness Each following unit is सारतर than each preceding one वरागना is the climax This is erefore सार based on धर्म (सारत्व )

**४५ — असङ्गति**

 असङ्गति as आलङ्कारिकऽ 1 ke our author and **रुय्यक** understand the ternm is a break in association And in the figure is delineated the break of the association between कार्य and कारण which normally make their appearance in the *same* abode but are in this figure found to

part company Here कार्य and कारण are presented as appearing in different abodes यदातु अन्यदेशस्थ कारण अन्यदेशस्थ च कार्य उपनिबध्यते तदा उचित सगतिनिवृत्ते असगत्याख्यो अलकार — **रुय्यक** स्वभावोत्पन्नपरस्परसगतित्यागात् असगति says the author of the काव्यप्रकाश

 This does not mean that the poet, in this figure regales us with anything absurd or impossible Just as we saw sense in the infraction of कार्यकारणभाव as set forth in विभावना and विशेषोक्ति, so there is sense in the violation of कार्य-कारणभाव as presented in असङ्गति In other words, as In विभावना विशेषाक्ति, here also there is only विरोधाभास not real विरोध. Like them असङ्गति is based on विरोधभास in the field of causation

 When the poet, in असङ्गति, describes the cause as appearing in one place and effect in another, really speaking he connects the effect. in question with a cause not properly its own but with something wuch is usually its cause सा पीनोजनिमत् पयोधरभर धत्ते सस्वेदा वयम्। (रुय्यक), says one looking wistfully at the पीनपयोधरऽ of some beauty Generally the rule is that whosoever carries the burden feels the strain the lovely lady carries the पानीन्नतपयोधरभर and the strain is felt by the wistful looker-on This is असङ्गति Here स्वेद of the looker-on which is really due to मदनवाधा is attributed to पयोधरभर, because the usual rule is भर रौद्राय भवति Thus when analysed in असङ्गति a कार्य is attributed to something other than its proper कारण, and in doing so the poet exploits the usual connection between particular causes and their effects

 This is exactly what happens in विभावना, where an effect is des cribed as taking place even in the absence of its cause There also the real cause is different from the one the poet denies and in denying the cause the poet only depends upon the normal connection between particular causes and effects \[ See विभावना \] Read र. ग. :-- अस्या व विभावनायामिव कार्यांशे अतिशयोक्त्यनुप्रणन आवश्यकम् इति अलङ्कारसर्वस्व करातीना मतम्।

 This is possible owing to अतिशयाक्ति (or in the opinion of जगन्नाथ अध्यवसाय) underlying the figure and it is in the matter of the कार्य the à due to Thus in the above instance सा पीनोन्नतिमत् etc the स्वेद due to मदनबाधा is अध्यवसित as the same as the स्वेद due to भर, when one knows too well how they are भिन्न Similarly when about the चल it is said अन्यग्य दर्शति क्षेत्रमन्य प्राणैर्विपुज्यते। the प्राणविषयेण due to the mischief of the खल isअध्यवसित as the same as the प्राणवियग caused by खल It is owing to this अध्यवसाय, that the poet is enabled to make it appear

that the cause and effect appear in भिन्न आश्रयऽ, when as a matter of fact the two things in question are not at all कार्य-कारण in the particular case Neither is रमणी स्तनभर the cause of the onlooker's स्वत, nor श्रावदश of one the cause of the प्राणवियोग of a thurd party, their immediate causes are, as we saw, मदनबाधा and खलकपट Read र ग— येन केनापि प्रकारेण कार्यांशे अभेदाध्यवसान आवश्यकम्। This अध्यवसाय may some-times be based on श्लेष In दृष्टिर्मृगीदृशोऽत्यन्त श्रुत्यन्तपरिशालिनी। मुच्यन्ने बधनात् केशा॥(र ग), (केशवधन ) मोक्ष IS अध्यवसित as ससारान्मुक्ति This is made posable by the श्लेष in मुच्यन्ते बाधनात् Sometimes, there may be no श्लेष, as in the cases above There may or may not be अध्यवसाय in respect of the कारण In दृष्टिर्मृगीदृश etc there is कारणाशे अध्यवसाय, दृष्टि ( eye) being अध्यवसित as दृष्टि insight In सा पीनोन्नतिमत् etc. there is no कारणाशे अध्यवसाय

 Thus in short, in असङ्गति, the poet describes two things that are present in two different abodes as cause and effect, and for doing so, he has to resort to अध्यवसान of the कार्य of one cause as being the same as that of another

 **असङ्गति *vs* समुच्चय (२*****)*** – That there is विरोधाभास in असङ्गति, as in विरोध, is admitted But whereas (1) in असङ्गति, the विरोधाभाम, is confined to the sphere of कार्य-कारणभाव,in विरोध, it is in other spheres except कार्य-कारणभाव (2) In असङ्गति, the विराधाभास is between कार्य-कारण because they abide in भिन्न आश्रयऽ. In विरोध, it is between things like, जाति, गुण, क्रिया - because they abide in the same आश्रय र ग says व्यधिकरणत्वेन प्रसिद्धयो समानाधिकरणत्वन उपनिबाधने विरोधालकार \। समनााधिकरणत्वेन प्रसिद्धयो द्वयोः वैयधिकरण्यन उपनिवधने असङ्गति।

 ***असङ्गति\_vs समुच्चय*** ( २ ) There is some simulanty between असगति and व्यधिकरणे समुच्चय (२), only when the गुणऽ or क्रियाऽ are related as कार्यं कारण Because as in असगति, 11 व्यधिकरणे समुच्चय (२) of this kind कार्य and कारण are found in भिन्न आश्रय But whereas ( 1 ) in असङ्गति, the कार्य and कारण are not described as simultaneous, but as one following the other in समुच्चय (२), the emphasis is on यौगपद्य ( 11 ) In असगति, the violation of कार्य-कारणभाव interests us In समुच्चय (२) the कार्य-कारणभाव berng only suggested, the यौगपद्य of गुणऽ etc. interests us

 असङ्गति dates back from रुद्रट After fhum अल्लट (का प्र), रुय्यक विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पयदीक्षित all admitted it अप्पय्यदीक्षित admits two more varieties of the figure but जगन्नाथ reduces one to असगति of the usual type and the other to विभावना

  **Essentials— **\[Seer रुय्यक and our author cited above\] (1) The poet must descnbe two things which are usually related as cause and effect as appearing in भिन्न आश्रयऽ, after an interval just enough to make them appear as cause and effect (2) The strikingness consists in their being present in भिन्न आश्रयऽ when normally cause--effect are present in the sameआश्रय (3) There must be apparent विरोध between the two If the huntsman discharges the arrow, and the deer is killed, it cannot be असगति, even though कार्य-कारण are inभिन्न आश्रयऽ because there is no विरोधाभास here Read **कुवलयानद** — यत्र कार्य हेत्वोर्भिन्न देशत्व न विरुद्ध तत्र नासगति। (4) There must be अध्यवसाय of the कार्य of one thing as being the same as that of another (5) This अध्यवसाय may be based on श्लेष also (6) There may or may not be अध्यवसाय of कारण In सा बाल, वयम्प्रगल्ममनस (र) there is no अध्यवसाय in the कारणाश viz बाला, but there is अध्यवसाय in अप्रगव्यमनस
in विष जलधरै पीत, मूर्च्छिता पथिकाना the (कु) there is अध्यावसाय in the कार्याश viz मूर्च्छितात्व And in addition there is अध्यावसाय in the कारणाश
also विष ( water) being by श्लेष अध्यावसित as विष potson

 ** § सू ६७ भिन्नदेशतया** etc Construe —यत्र कार्यकारणभूतयो धर्मयो अत्यन्तभिन्नदेशतया युगपत् रयाति सा असगति स्यात्। **कार्यकारणभूतयो धर्मयो'** Two things which are normally related as कार्यकारण Note that the word धर्म is used in the sense of a thing, a matter, as in Buddhistic" philosophy Cp the definition of सूक्ष्म for a similar use of the word अत्यन्तभिन्नदेशतया This is the keynote of the figure Note, however, that where there is no विरोध in भिन्नदेशत्व of कार्य-कारण, the figure will have no scope \[Vide Essential 3\] युगपत् ख्याति Statement or Perception (ख्या to see) What doesयुगपत् mean? It only implies that the two things occur within such a short interval as would make them seem कार्य-कारण युगपत्
cannot mean simultaneously' Because then there will be no difference between असगति and वैयधिकरण्य समुच्चय 3 which गुणऽ or क्रियाऽ occur युगपद् The word युगपद् has been inserted in the definition in blind imitation of रूद्रट who has सप्तकाल—cp विरपटे समकाल कारणमन्यत्र कार्यमन्ययत्स्यार मुपभ्येने
विपयासगति संयम्॥\[९४८ \] रा तर्कवागीश ( सा द ) rightly criticizes काव्यप्रकाश for inserting युगपद् in the definition Says he क्वेचित्तु काव्यभेदेन तदुभयस्यातिवरणाय युहपदिति। तत्र। दन्तक्षत वध्वा समनीना इत्यत्र स्तोत्रोदाहरणे वथकाभावेन कार्यकारणयोयुगपदुत्पत्रत्वय्यतिविरहात्। रुय्यक विश्वनाथ जगन्नाथ अप्पय्यदीक्षित make no reference to यौगपद्य in असङ्गति

 **वृत्ति— इह, दृष्ट यथा धूमादि** in our in our everyday life (इह) we find and are कार्य abiding in the sameआश्रय or देश बद्वि on शैल does not

produce धूम in महानस, नहि शैले अग्निः महानसे धूम जनयतीति भावः—**भा. चं.** हेतु फलयोरपि धर्मयोः etc. where two matters or things (धर्म) though normally related as cause and effect appear within a brief interval. (युगपद्) in different abodes, due to some abnormal feature (केनापि अतिशयेन). **केनापि अतिशयेन** is the same as विशेषाभिधित्सया that has been referred to in figures like विशेषोक्ति, विभावना, आक्षेप. What is the अतिशय or विशेष in असङ्गति? It is to make conspicuous some particular aspect of the कारण \[ कारणस्य कारणान्तराद् बैलक्षण्य — says मा. चं. \]. In अन्यस्य दशति etc., the poet, for instance, wants to bring out the deadly nature of the खलदश. In सा पीनोन्नति etc., the poet wants to bring out the powerful appeal that स्तनऽ have for the on-looker.**युगपदवभासनम्ः** See above. **सा.. असङ्गतिः** The propriety of the name असङ्गति given. It is असङ्गति because of स्वभावोत्पन्नपरस्परसगतित्याग i. e. here is the negation ( त्याग ) of the mutual association of cause and effect, that is inherent (स्वभावोत्पन्न). **स्वभावोत्पन्नसङ्गति** cause and effect, are thus associated in one abode by the very nature of things Note that in this explanation, अ in असगति is taken as negative.रुय्यक also interprets in the same way. There could be another explanation also of असगति as Paradoxical Association, अ implying विरुद्धस्थ

 ** St. 142** जस्सेअ etc Jllustrates असगति The marks of dalliance-on the newly wed bride make her co-wives jealous. Sanskrit rendering -यस्यैव व्रण तस्यैव वेदना भणति जन तदलीकम्। दन्तक्षत कपोले वश्वाः वेदना सपत्नीनाम्॥

** तत् अलीकम्** is false **दन्तक्षत **Tooth bite. a sequel to dalliance.बध्वा Ref. to newly-wed bride.वेदना am due to jealousy

 Here दन्तक्षत, the कारण, and वेदना, the कार्य, abide inभिन्न आधारऽ. They appear after just that interval that would make them seem as कार्य-कारण. Note that they cannot appear simultaneously (युगपद) as तर्कवागीश rightly says The कार्य VIz. वेदना is based on अध्यवसाय, असूयावेदना being अध्यवसित as दन्तक्षतवेदना Hence there is असङ्गति in the 2nd half.

 **एषा च विरोधबाधिनी, न विरोध।** असगति also has विरोध in it due to कार्य-कारणयों. वैयधिकरण्य. Therefore it ought to be brought under the figure विरोध. But it is a case of विरोध, that has developed into a separate figure In other words, it is an exception (बाधिनी=बाधक) to विरोध in general. भिन्नाधारतया etc. In असगति, the विरोध is only due to two things (कार्य-कारण) appearing in different abodes, (when normally they are found in the same abode). This naturally means that in विरोधलङ्कार, the विरोध should be due to two things appearing in the same abode

(एकाश्रयनिष्ठ विरोधित्वम्), when they normally appear in different abodes "This, though not expressly mentioned in the definition of विरोधालकार (अनुक्तमपि), follows as a matter of course (पर्यवसितम्) when we define the scope of असङ्गति in the manner given above अपवादविषय etc. In this way we can form an idea of the scope of विरोधालकार as being that which is not covered by असङ्गति, its exception. Because, the scope of the general rule (उत्सर्ग) is settled (व्यवस्थिति) by excluding therefrom (परिहार) the province covered by the exception (अपवाद). In short, what the अपवाद does not cover forms the province of उत्सर्ग As—असङ्गति, the अपवाद covers भिन्नाश्रयनिष्ठ विरोध, विरोधालकार, the उत्सर्ग will cover एकाश्रयनिष्ठ विरोध विरोधालङ्कार is उत्सर्ग or general rule, because it forms the broader class उत्सर्ग = General rule सामान्यशास्त्रम् अपवाद = Exception विशेषशास्त्रं तथा चैक निदर्शितम् And the figure विरोध is dlustrated (निदर्शितम्) accordingly e by taking into account the विरोध between एकाश्रयनिष्ठ things only. St 91, for instance (अभिनवलिनी etc.) gives us the विरोध of नलिनीत्व vs दहनराशित्वin the same abode Viz नलिन्यादीनि St 92. ( गिरय etc ) gives the विरोध between गिरित्व and अनुशतत्व as abiding in the same abode viz.गिरिऽ and so on. Read जयरथ —अत एव च तयो भिन्नदेशत्वात् इय विषयभेदेन भवति इति एकविषयात्
विरोधादस्य भेद। See र = ग cated at असङ्गति vs विरोध

**४६ — समाधि**

 समाधि is facilitation and the figure consists in the description of a situation in which the achievement of an object is facilitated by the fortuitous occurrence of some particular circumstance. The gallant wants to reconcile his n who is in huff To achieve this purpose, he is just thinking of falling prostrate at her feet As luck would have it, there becomes audible, just that moment, the rumbling of clouds And it is easy to guess how this rumbling must have facilitated the task of reconciliation Because no मालिनी , howsoever stiff, can help yearning for her lover when she hears a cloud rum blet मानमस्या निराकर्तुं पादयोर्मे पतिव्यत। उपकाराय दिष्टभेदमुदीर्णे धनगर्जितम्॥ says the gallant and we get a case of ,समाध्यलङ्कार The beloved was wending her way in dark to her beloved s home, spurred on by love There flashed lightning all at once, rendering it easier for her to reach her destination स्मरदीपदीप्तदृष्टेर्घनान्धकारेऽपि पतिगृह यान्त्या। झटिति प्रादुरभूवन् सङ्ख्या दिव शृङ्खला पथिन॥ र ग Here is समाधि again, happily blended with उत्प्रेक्षा--सत्यादिव In short in समाधि, the poet refers to the occurrence of some new circumstance (बारणान्तर) which acting in concert with circumstances already operating hastens the result armed at.

some other वारण कराणान्तर shows that other cause or causes are already operating दण्डिन् and विश्वनाथ say that the accidental occurrence is दैववशात् सुकर shows that the achievement would have proved difficult in the absence of the वारणान्तर तर्कवागीश — एतेन प्रकान्तरारणेन कार्यस्य दुष्करत्व प्रतीयते ।

 **वृत्ति— साधनान्तरोपकृतेन** Beng helped by a new circumstance Note that there is another circumstance already operating यद् etc Construe आरब्ध कार्य यद् अक्लेशन आधीयते When the thing undertaken is achieved (आधीयते) with facility (अक्लेशेन) This also explains the name समाधि सम्यक् (अक्लशेन) आधीयते ( क्रियते ) कार्य अत्र

 ** St 143 मानमस्या** etc Illustrates समाधि reconciliation is facilitated by the rumbling of a cloud Construe— अस्या मानं निराकर्तुं पाठयो पनिष्यते मे उपकाराय श्लिष्ट्या इदं घनगर्वित वर्णनम् मान huff the sense of injured feelings **उपकाराय** shows that the task was rendered easy by घनगर्वित

 Here माननिवाकरण which was sought to be achieved by ग्रणिपात is facilitated by the rumbling of clouds that appears by sheer chance Hence oneकारण being already there a कारणान्तर appears making the कार्यं easter Hence समाधि

**४७ — सम**

 सम is equal, well-suited well matched and the figure consists in the poets description of a combination that is well matched In समुच्चय (१) we indeed get a combination of circum stances but the poet is not concerned with their mutual suitability as in सुम In समाधि also there combine circumstances, but the poet worries the least as to whether they are well matched or not. In सम however the poets effort is to focus the attention of the सुहृदय on a combination in which the things that have gravitated together have a mutual suitability In a world of inequalities and disparities, suited combinations and well matched pairs do have an attraction as much for the poet as for the सहृदय

  As the world consists of things good and bad well matched com binations may be of things which are good or of things which are bad When the citizens appreciate the wedding of अत्र and इन्दुमती, because both the parties were well matched we get a सम of the first varietyg When the poet describes an assembly

of monkeys with well matched appurtenances, we get सम of the other sort युक्त सभाया खलु मर्कटाना शाखास्तरुणा मृदुलासनानि सुभाषित चित्कृतिरातिथेयी दन्तैर्नखाग्रैश्च विपाटनानि॥( र ग )

  When the poet appreciates a combination of good things, it tends to exalt them In other words it is स्तुतिपर्यवसायिन् as in शशिनमुपगतेय etc. When, on the other hand, the appreciation' relates to the com bination of bad things, it becomes निन्दापर्यवसायिन् In the latter case, therefore, not infrequently does सम get embodied in a व्याजस्तुति as in युक्त सभाया etc where the total effect is to pour ndicule on the gathering of monkeys Sometimes, however, the poet may merely show how two bad things have come together.

  Thus we get the following scheme—

सम  
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

                सद्योगे                
 असद्योगे

                                                                
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

                                 शुद्ध
        व्याजस्तुतिपर्यवसायिन्  

जगन्नाथ and अपय्यदीक्षित admit two more divisions of the figure which answer corresponding divisions of विषम (Vide विषम) They are (1) कारणात् अनुरूपकार्योत्पत्ति, and ( 11 ) वाछिनार्थप्राप्ति The first is illustrated by मन्त्रापितहविदीप्तहुताशनतनूभुव। शिसास्पर्शेन पाचान्या स्थाने दग्ध सुयोधन\। (र. ग) where सुयोधनदाह is well accounted for by hts having clasped the शिखा (hatr: flame) of द्रौपदी born from यज्ञाग्नि The second is illustrated by युक्तो वारणलाभोऽय स्यान्न ते वारणार्थिन। ( कु ) Here one was in quest of a वारण (elephant ) and indeed got a वारण ( rebuff )। This is वाञ्छितार्थप्राप्ति based on श्लेष Read कु —यत्र अतर्किनोत्कटानिष्टसत्त्वे क्षेषमहिम्ना इष्टार्थत्वप्रतीति तत्रापि समालङ्कार अप्रतिहत एव।

 **रय्यक and जयरथ** were not for admitting these divisions For, they said that अनुरूपकार्योत्पत्ति or वाञ्छितार्थप्राप्ति have no charm in them पूर्वभेदद्वय विपर्ययस्य अनलङ्कारत्वात् — said रुय्यक, the reason being कारणात् अनुरूपकार्योपत्तिहिं लोकप्रसिद्ध।(र ग) जगन्नाथ, however, nghtly points out that mere अनुरूपकार्योत्पत्ति or वाञ्छिताथप्राप्ति may not be striking But they become stnkang enough when blended with अभेदाभ्यवसाय with or without क्षेत्र Thus in दग्ध सुयोधन मरण of सुयाधन 13 अध्यवसित् as दाह And in युक्त्तो etc वारण (rebuff ) is अध्यवसित as वारण ( elephant ) Read र ग— वस्तुन अननुरूपयोरपि कार्यकारणयो श्लेषादिना धर्मैक्यमपादनद्वारा अनुरूपनवर्णने, वस्तुतो

अनिष्टस्यापि तेनैव उपायन इष्ट्यैसपत्तौ च चारुताया अनुपदमेव दर्शितत्वात्। तस्मात् सममपि त्रिवविधमेव।

 **सम *vs* समुच्चय (1)**— In both there assemble, in one place, arcumstances, two or more If these circumstances are well matched in, they are all , or allअसत् inसमुच्चय (1) **र गं i**n fact refers to a view acc to which रमणीयानां समुच्चय and जरमणीयाना समुच्चय come under सम But whereas (t) in सम, the poet stresses the परस्परयोग्यता of the things, in समुच्चय (१), his emphasis is on यौगपद्य (॥) In सम, the circumstances coming together are not हेतुऽ of a parti cular कार्य In समुच्चय (1), they are हेतुऽ, each one of which is capable enough to produce the कार्य in question \[*Vide* र. ग nn समुच्चय)

 सम seems to have been first admitted by our author and then too as a sequel toविषम already admitted by रुद्रट That विषम was earlier in the eyes of the आलङ्कारिकऽ is also bome out by the fact that रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ and अप्पय्यदीक्षित deal with विषम first and then with सं जगन्नाथ
and अप्पय्यदीक्षित admit two additional vaneties that answer corresponding varieties of विषम

 **Essentials—** \[ सम =परस्परयोग्य) (1) There must be a combi nation of things more than one (2) This combination must be of things good or of things bad (3) The things must be well suited one for the other (4) And the poet s aim must be to stress their mutual suitability (5) When the combination is of good things, the poet exalts them by showing their परस्परयोग्यता (6) When it is of bad things the poet is usually ironical in showing how they are stuted one for the other (7) This irony may sometimes take the form of व्याजस्तुति (8) In the two additional varieties recognised by जगन्नाथ, the themes are अनुरूपकार्योपत्ति and वाञ्छितार्थप्राप्ति But both these must be such as are based on एष or अध्ययवसाय. An अनुरूपकार्योत्पत्ति or वाछितार्थप्राप्ति which is too matter of fact cannot be poetical

 ** § सू ६२ समं etc**. Construc -- यदि क्वचित् योग (योग्यतया सम्मार्पित (तर्हि तत्) सर्प (भवति) योग Combination of things योग्यता समार्पित is viewed (by the poet) as well suited

 **वृत्ति — इदम् अनयो** साध्य etc. Construe - सम्बन्धस्य इदम् अनये इति योग्यतया अध्यवसनं चेत् तत् समय। सम्बन्धस्य नियतविषय अध्यवसानं A determination of a peafic nature regarding a con nection between two things. What this determination of a specific nature' is is made clear in इदम् अनयो इति योग्यतया That the con rection is fitting since it (इदं = रगर्त) as between the two parties is

felt to be admirable. In short, there is an apprehension that the association between two particular parties is worthy, well suited Would it not have been better of the reading were सम्बन्धस्य नियतविषयस्य, some particular connection? तत् = समम्.

** St 144** धातु etc. Illustrates सद्योगे सम Two parties well matched get married and hold out prospects of the highest romance.धातु शिल्पाविशयनिकषस्थान The supreme creative art of the Creator can be tested (निकष) by looking at the bnde **स्मरस्य दत्तपात्र** = स्मरेण दत्तपत्र The charter (पत्र) of beauty held so long by Cupid is now surrendered by him in favour of the bridegroom Tvm Ed has दत्तपाद स्मरस्य –Who has set his foot on i e eclipsed him cd Construe यत् दैवात् अनयो एतत् सदृश सगत जात, तत् अधुना शृगारस्य एकातपत्र राज्यम् उपनतम् Since they are united, there is promised the highest romance in near future, **शृङ्गारस्य एकातपत्र राज्यम् i. e** शृङ्गार is going to be the dominant note.

 Here since the bride and bridegroom are worthily matched, we have सद्योगे सम Note that it is स्तुति in tenor

 **St 145** चित्र चित्र etc. Illustrates असद्योगे सम The bitter निम्न bernes have found worthy connoisseurs in the crows 'जातो विधाता The विधातृ or Creator is generally blamed for bringing about ill sorted combinations In the present case, at least, he is उचितरचनासविधाता Dispenser of a fitting arrangement. The fitting arrangement is given in cd cd The bitter निव berries, growing in abundance (स्फोति), have at last become tasty And the party that relishes them is the class of crows

 Here, since the bitter निव beres are associated with the loathsome crow world in a fitting combination, we have a असद्योगे सम ,Note the irony It is also a निन्दापर्यवसायिनी व्याजस्तुतिt because of its being a satire disguised as praise. It may also be an अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा (अन्योक्ति)

**४८ — विषम**

 विषम means 'unequal', 'disparate", and the figure in all its three sub-divisions lays an emphasis on some disparity. It may be the disparity between two things that have come to be associated, st may be the disparity between the गुणक्रियाऽ of the कारण and those of the कार्य or it may be the disparity between one's expectations and their frustration all come under विषम This would also show that विषम

derives its charm from विरोध between things and is, therefore, a scion of विरोधालङ्कार, that has attained an independent status

 *Three sub divisions of the figure* — (1) The first variety of विषम consists in the association between two things which are mostil sunted one for the other Says a poet, चेय शिरीषमृगी क्व तावमन्नज्वर (क्व) Showing how utterly ill suited the delicate frame of the नायिका is to stand the agony of love fever Says सेना to her daughter पार्वती is, who had resolved to practise penance तप क्व वत्से क्व च तावक वपु (कु स ५ ४), bringing out the utter disparity between penance and पार्वतीऽ delicate body. Sometimes, however, the disparity may be between a कार्य and its कारण When अग्निमित्र says क रुजा हृदयप्रमायिनी के च ते विश्वसनीयमायुधम्। he expresses dismay at the dispanty between the वारण viz the delicate कुसुम, the arrow of Cupid, and its कार्य viz This instance is not to be confused as one the heart rending agony of the third division \[See below\]

 In all these cases, we have seen how the disparity between the And there two things in association is conveyed by the use of क्व क्व -was a school of आलकारिकऽ that believed that only क क can convey the Am तयानी idea implicit in the first variety of विषम तर्कवागिश refers to it— चतुर्थ प्रकार (corresponding to our first ) कद्वयप्रयोग एव सभवतीति वदन्त etc. No doubt, the use of th does indicate विषम (१), but it is also possible to have this variety without it When a poet says अहोसेय सीता शिव शिव परीता श्रुतिचलतुकरोटिभिर्वसति खलु रक्षोयुवतिभि॥र ग he brings out the disparity between the two सीता and रक्षोयुवतिभिऽ without employing क्व क र
ग further suggests that bare क्व क going with things that are वास्तविक will not constitute the figure as in क्व शुक्तय क्व चा मुक्ता The matter must be कविप्रतिभात्थावित, he urges But this When, for instance, the poet speaks of क्व शुक्तय श वा मुक्ता is not quile justifiable o xei o a g, he indeed picks out a dispanty which though वास्तविक, is striking because it is d sparity

 (11) The second variety consists in the disparity between one's How often do we find one, after expectations and their frustration making an effort to secure something not only balked of his hopes but involved in some unexpected trouble? The hungry rat, in search of food, rubbles its way into a basket, there to be swallowed by a cobra! दृष्टानुस्तेन भक्षित। ( कु) lo burn Mărutis tail, Råvana sets it on fire only to get his capital burt thereby द्विधक्षन् मास्तेर्बाल तनदर्पाद्दशानन। आत्मीयस्य पुरस्यैव सघो दहनमन्बभूत्॥

(कु) Thus, in this vanety, not only does one fail to achieve one's aim, but, on the contrary, lands oneself in troubles. In other words, here we have a double disappointment र ग makes an attempt to give an exhaustive classification of one's aims and troubles. Read इष्ट च (1) स्वस्य किञ्चित् सुखमाधनवस्तुप्राप्ति, (2) दुःखसमाधनवस्तु निवृत्तिश्च (3) परस्य दुःखसमाधनवस्तुप्रापण (4) परस्य सुखसाधननिवृत्तिश्च इति चतुर्विधम् अनिष्ट च (1) स्वस्य दुःखसाधनवस्तुप्राप्ति (2) परस्य सुखसाधनवस्तुप्राप्ति (3) परस्य दुःखसाधनवस्तुनाशश्च इति त्रिविधम्।

 But we can have विषम (3), even without a double disappointment as in the case of the rat or Ravana. Thus there may be only इष्टाप्राप्ति or only अनिष्टप्राप्ति Thus, when the elephant rubs its temples against a poisonous tree only to suffer inflammation of the body, we get an instance of विषम only with अनिष्टाप्राप्ति – मुकुलितनयन बरिणो खणड es चण्डूयतो विषद्रुतटे। उदभूदवाणडदहनज्वालातानाकुलो देह॥( र ग )

 (11) The third vanety of विषम consists (A) in the dispanty between the गुण go of the कार्य and that of the कारण Or (B) in the dispanty between the क्रिया of the कार्य and that of theकारण

 (A) The general rule is that the qualities of the effect are like those of the cause. From white threads we get a white piece of cloth, from red threads we get a red one The poet, however, who is ever on the look-out for paradoxes, sometimes gives us cases where is गुणऽ of the कार्य are divergent from those of the कारण The sword of the wamor is black, the glory it causes is bright white कीर्ति प्रसूत धवलं श्यामा तव कृपालिका (कु) This is कारयकारणयोर्गुणविरोधे विषम

  (B) Sometimes विषम (३) is based on the विरोध between the क्रिया of the कार्य and क्रिया of the कारण. The lovely damsel brings so much joy to her lover But separation from her causes such acute agony आनन्दममन्दं etc. (St 149 )

  In short, the third variety is based on विरोध of the कार्य and कारण. This is not the same as the विरोध between कारण and कार्य as illustrated by एष रूद्र हृदयप्रतापिनी etc. \[ (विषम (१)\].

  Thus we get the following scheme—

 Is there anything in common between these three divisions to justify their common designation as विषम? जयरथ for one thinks them to be independent figures Says he एवमिति अभिन्यता ग्रन्थरता विषमाणां भिन्नत्वमुक्तम्। न प्रकार प्रकारित्वम्। सामान्यलक्षणस्य असम्भवात्। But रुय्यक says that all have the common feature of अननुरूपसंसर्ग जगन्नाथ develops this अननुरूपसंसर्ग and shows how all the three divisions have some unifying principle running through them Analysed जगन्नाथ's remarks give us the following scheme—

**विपम = अननुरूपसंसर्ग**

              उपत्तिलक्षण                
संयोगाविलक्षण

कारणात् स्वगुणविलक्षणार्थोत्पत्त्या \[विषम (३)\]

             इष्टसाधनतया निश्चिातात् कारणात् अनिष्टकार्यात्पत्तिभि \[ विषम (२) \]  

विषम dates back from रूद्रट And it is significant that even when रूद्रट had no सम he admitted विषम रूद्रट recognised no less than five varieties \[VII 47-55 & IX. 45-47\] which on a closer examination reduce themselves to fourअल्लट admitted three of these That all आलङ्कारिकऽ that followed the काव्यप्रकाशकार, रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित admit the three divisions adopted by the काव्यप्रकाश is an eloquent testimony to the hold का प्र
had on later arduftes,आलङ्कारिकऽ विषम seems to have been earlier than सम which possibly was an invention of the काव्यप्रकाश as a sequel to विषम involving a paradox is likely to have arrested the आलङ्कारिकऽ attention first. It is noteworthy that all आलङ्कारिकऽ of note except काव्यप्रकाश first deal with विषम and then proceed to सम

 Essentials \[विषम= अननुरूप (संसर्ग)--रुय्यक) (1) In the first tantly there is an extreme dispanty between two things that have come together (2) This extreme dispanty is generally conveyed by क्व क्व (3) Sometimes however it may be conveyed even without छ छ (4) In the second rarely the poet describes some party making an effort to achieve some object, not only failing to secure it but also getting involved in some unexpected trouble In other words, there is a double disappointment of इष्टप्राप्ति and अनिष्टप्राप्ति (5) Sometimes, however, there may be only इष्टप्राप्ति or only अनिष्टप्राप्ति (6) Sometimes the poet may describe even an agreeabe matter in this form.C R परिकल्पते॥( र ग ) This

is more poetical than real इष्टाप्राप्ति and अनिष्टापत्ति (7) In the *third variety,* there is वैषम्य between the कारणगुणऽ and कार्यगुणऽ or between कारणक्रिया and कार्य क्रिया (8) This गुणवैषम्य or क्रियावैषम्य is only poetically conceived

  **§ सू ७० क्वचिद्यत्** etc Construe – (1) यत् क्वचित् श्लेष अनिवैषम्यात् घटना न इयात्। (2) यत् वस्तु क्रियाफलावाप्ति नैव भवेत् अनर्रश्व भवेत्। (3-4) यत् च कारणस्य गुणक्रिये क्रमण कार्यस्य गुणक्रियाभ्या विरुद्ध स एष विषमो मत। यत् श्लेष अतिवैधर्म्यात् घटना न इयात् When the association (श्लेष ) between two things is found to be incongruous due to extreme dispanty of nature. What is this अतिवैधर्म्य Read जगन्नाथ - ससर्गिणी अन्यतरगुणस्वरूपतिरस्कार्य अन्यतरगुणास्वरूपतया अयोग्यत्वम् i e The two things possess attributes standing in sharp contrast.श्लेष Association between things They may be even कार्यकारण as in क्व रुना etc When the cause and effect are found incongruous, it will be the first vanety When their गुण क्रियाऽ are found incongruous it will be the third variety घटना न इयात् This is generally expressed by क्व क्व There are some like तर्कवागीश, however, who believe that other varieties also may be conveyed by क क Thus क वन तरुवल्कभूषण नृपलक्ष्मी क्व महेद्रवन्दिता Is assessed by तर्कवागीश, against विश्वनाथ, as illustrating इष्टाप्राप्ति and अनिष्ठापत्ति If thus क्व क्व- does not specifically indicate the 1st variety, even our instance of क्व रुजा हृदयप्रमाथिनी etc. may perhape belong to कार्य-कारणयोर्गुण वैषम्य, ( हृदयप्रभाविनी vs. विश्वसनीय ) One thing is certain that the use of क्व क्व is a sure indication of विषम – to wluchever class it may belong यत् कर्तु etc There is a double disappointment इष्टाप्ताप्ति and अनिष्टापत्ति But, as we saw, each one singly viz इष्टाप्राप्ति, अनिष्टापत्ति can give rise to the figure. क्रियाफल The party as described as making an effort (क्रिया) to attaan some aim ( फल ) फल is इष्ट, अनर्थ 15 अनिष्ट See रगं cited above for an exhaustive enumeration of these. यत् च etc. Here the वैषम्य is between कारणगुणऽ and कार्यगुणऽ or between कारणक्रियाऽ and कार्यक्रियाऽ. क्रमेण i e. वैषम्य between गुणऽ and गुनऽ, क्रियाऽ and क्रियाऽ only For वैषम्य cannot be of the form of कारणगुण vs कार्यक्रिया or कारणक्रिया vs कार्यगुण Note that the गुणवैषम्य or क्रियावैषम्य only poetically possible.

 वृत्ति — द्वयोः It may be possible to have three things also अनुपपद्यमानतया योग Ther combination is founxl incongruous क्रियाया प्रगल्भात् Because his work is undane frustrated रुद्रट has क्रियावैषम्य ममार्पितमपि अनर्थ विषय some disastrous (अनर्थे adj. ) matter he never soughtfor सत्यपि कार्यस्य कारणरूपास्यानुकारे Though as a rule the कार्य re sembles (अनुकर) in nature (रूप) the कारण This is true only in a

logical view of causation, स समविपर्ययामा the name explained. विषम is समविपर्यय, the reverse of सम चतुरूप In four vaneties Sinctly speaking they are three But गुणबैषम्य and क्रियावैषम्य being viewed as the number is given as four

 **St. 14G** शिरीपादपि etc. Mustrates विषम (१) A lady of delicate frame is smitten hard by love कुकुलाग्निकर्कश As tormenting as chaff fire Chaff fire is more effective than ordinary fire two independent classes

 Here the two things that have come together viz. the lady of the most delicate frame and the fire of love are most ill suited, one for the other Their utter dispanty is conveyed by क - क This is therefore विषम (१)

 **St 147.** सिहिकासुत etc Illustrates विषम (2) The hare, getting afraid of the cub of a hon, resorted to the moon But the moon and the hare were both gulped down by राहु सिहिकासुतसत्रस्त scared by the cub of a honess शाश्रय with them आश्रय, the moon सिंहिकासुत i e. राहु सिंहिका was the wife of कदयप and mother of राहु.

 Here the शश made an effort to escape from the hon's cub But thus only resulted in his being swallowed up by राहु Thus there are इष्टाप्राप्ति and अनिष्टप्राप्ति to the शश Hence विषम (२) There is an irony अनिम्रपति शश वियम (२) based on श्लेष in सिंहिकासुत The hare escaped one सिंहिकासुतonly to fall into the jaws of another सिंहिकासुत

 ** St. 148 सद्य** etc. Illustrates विषम (३), कार्य—कारणगुणयोः वैषम्ये, A king wins शुभ्र यश with his नील कृषाण चित्र O wonder! \[Add 'O शुभ यश कृपाण wonder' in the Translation after "hand') करस्पर्शमवाप्य ref to his vielding the sword यस्य नृपस्य तमालनीला adj of कृपा तरेणा (sword) श्लिलोकयामरण. In apposition to यश. Glory which is an ornament to the three worlds (त्रिलोको

 Here is वरण यश its कार्य The is तमालनील while यश is इन्दुपाण्डु Hence कार्य-कारणयो गुणवैषम्य —नीलत्व beng opposed to पाण्डुत्व Hence विषम
(३),कार्य-कारणयो गुणवैषम्य Note that this is not to be mistaken for विरोध Because only गुणवैषम्य can come within its (of विरोध) purview, not कार्यकारणयो गुणवैषम्य

  **Sl 149 आनन्दममन्द etc**. Illustratesविषम (१) कार्यकारणयोः गुणवैषम्य .A lady is lovely enough to give delight But separation caused by her (i. c. from her) acute pain समन्द आनन्द Tremendous delight तथैव जनित विरह i e, separation from you

 Here the is the cause, विरह from her is the effect. While the performs the क्रिया of the विरह . the effect, team form performs the क्रिया of Hence कार्यकारणयो क्रियावैषम्य (विषम ३)

 **St 150 विपुलेन** etc Illustrates, according to some like तर्कवागीश, विषम (२) *without* क्व क्व According to others like प्रदीप, उद्योत, प्रभा, it illustrates a vanety not covered by the definition, and has been given by the author to suggest how विषम may manifest itself in forms other than those specified in the definition The latter seems to be the correct view Description कृष्ण of being drunk up' by a lady with but the corner of her eye, that very कृष्ण who at the end of the युग holds in hus belly the whole world. Construe सागरशयस्य यस्य विपुलन कुक्षिणा युगक्षये भुवनानि पपिरे, स पुन एक्तमया पुरस्त्रिया मदविभ्रमासकलया एकया दृशा पपे। **मदविभ्रमासकलया दृशा** with a glance in which the eye was not fully opened due to the play of passion \[ मदविभ्रम\]

  Here the वैषम्य consists in the *whole* कृष्ण being drunk by a lady with but a corner of her eye that कृष्ण one part of whom viz his belly could drink in (absorb) the whole world at the time of प्रलय To put it more effectively, the वैषम्य is between अवयवकतृकक्रियाया अवयविन कर्मत्वम्। Is it not surprising that the whole (कृष्ण) should be the कर्मन् of that पान क्रिया which a part (कुक्षि) was able to carry out? Thus प्रदीप says this isअववय अवयविनो वैषम्य not covered by the definition Some like तर्कवागीश however say that it is only the 1st variety of अतिवैधम्य put in a different shape Because the imphed meaning is क्व एकदेशेन नगरपानकर्तृ शरीरं क च स्त्रीनेत्रकोणेन तदीय पानम् **सा द** also gives this stanza to illustrate अतिवैधम्यं (i e variety I) **प्रदीप** seems to be nearer the truth and the वृत्ति — विषमत्व यथायोगमवगन्तव्यम् also seems to hint at the definition being not comprehensive enough to cover all cases of वैषम्य

 ** विषमत्व यथायोग अवगन्तव्यम् —**The विषमच should be recognised in such cases, according to the particular context or occasion In other words, it may not be covered by the definition of विषम Vide उद्योत

**४९ — अधिक**

 अधिक means whatever is in excess of another', 'whatever is bigger' and the figure अधिक consists in the poetical description of an आधार as being bigger than its आधेय or of an आधाय being bigger than its आधार When the poet thus describes either theआधेय or आधार as being more extensive or bigger, his sole purpose is to exalt the प्रकृत

  More often than not, the आधेय is प्रकृत And it is easy to under stand how the प्रकृत is exalted when the आधेय is described as bigger than the आधार When the poet says to the patron जले यत्र तत्र न ते गुण (कु), it is easy to see how गुणा, the प्रकृत, are

exalted by being described as too many to be held even in the primordial waters that are capacious enough to hold so many ब्रह्मण्डऽ

 But, it is interesting to see how the poet exalts the प्रकृत even by describing the आधार as bigger than the आधेय when the आधेय is the प्रकृत Says the poet कियद् वागब्रह्मा यौने विश्राम्यन्ति गुणास्तव। ---' How great is the Brahman of Speech that your virtues can rest therem" Here गुणा, the आधेय, are the प्रकृत And they are exalted when the poet describes वाग्ब्रह्मा, their आधार, as being great enough to hold them Because, when the poet refers to the bigness of the आधार, he implies that the आधेय that occupies suchआधार also must be big indeed As कुवलयानन्द Says —अत्र यद्यपि कुयद् वाग्ब्रह्म इति आधारस्य प्रशंसा क्रियते तथापि शब्द ब्रह्मण गुणाधिकरणत्वेन अधिकत्व प्रकत्यैव प्रससा क्रियते इति तत्प्रशंसा प्रस्तुतगुणप्रशंसायामेव पर्यवस्यति। it will be clear from thus that आधाराधिक्य exalts the प्रकृत, in a more suggestive way, than आधाराधिक्य and is hence more poetical

 In some cases, however, the आधार itself may be प्रकृत and by stating its आधिक्य, the poet may exalt the प्रकृत directly A poet wants to praise a हरिमुक्त. Says he गिरामर्वपयो राजन् विस्तारस्तव चेतस। साक्काशतया यत्र शेते विश्वाप्रयो हरि॥( र गं ) Here he says that the heart of the king is vast indeed in that it can accommodate हरि very roomly. In this instance, though हरि is आधेय still चेत, the आधार, is प्रकृत and it comes to be exalted by being described as bigger than हरि himself

 जगन्नाथ very aptly emphasises that the आधिक्य must be poetical, not वस्तुमेव. If परमामन् is described as bigger than, and if the cage is described as bigger than the parrot, there is no figure therein Rend र गं — यत्र व्यापाराधाय अन्यतरस्य न्यूतत्वम् अधिक च नं तत्र Again जयरथ— (अनुरूपत्व

poet's aim is to exalt the प्रकृत In विषम his aim is merely to draw attention to a paradox

 अधिक dates back from रुद्रट् who had two varieties (based on समानकारणभ्य कार्ययो विरोध and आश्रिताश्रयभाव ) one of which alone based on आश्रिताश्रयभाव was adopted by the काव्यप्रवाश While रुद्रट had अधिक based on आधयाधिक्य only काव्यप्रकाश developed one more variety based on आधाराधिक्य also It is once more typical of the influence of the काव्यप्रकाश that रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ and अप्पय्यदीक्षित admitted the varieties as recognised by the का प्र

  Note the following scheme—

**अधिक**

**आधाराधिक्ये आधेयाधिक्ये**

 **Essentials** - \[अधिक 'bigger'\] (1) The poet must describe two things related as आश्रित and आश्रय, both impressively big (2) The आश्रित may be described as bigger than the आश्रय, or vice tersa ( 3 ) Thus is with a view to exalting the प्रकृत (4) The प्रकृत is generally the आश्रित, but sometimes the आश्रय may be the प्रकृत. (5) The आश्रित, the प्रकृत, when described as bigger than the आश्रय naturally gets exalted (6) But when the आश्रय is descnbed as bigger than the आश्रित, it also indirectly suggests the greatness of the आश्रित which is so big as to occupy the आश्रय of the vastness described (7) The bigness must be poetically conceived Bigness of a matter of fact type is of no use

  **§ सू ७१ महतो** etc Construe -महतो आश्रित - आश्रययो यद् प्रमात् आश्रय-आश्रयिणी तनुत्वेपि महीयांसौ स्यावा तत् तु अधिकम् (भवति॥ महतो आश्रित आश्रयो Loc. Absolute The अश्रित and आश्रय being आयमिणो कमात् महीयांसी स्याताम् the आश्रय and आयसेन् (respectively bigger क्रमात्ः i e. आश्रय is bigger (महीयात्) than the आश्रित or आश्रित is bigger than the आश्रय आश्रय =अधर Abode आश्रित=आश्रयिन्=आधेय The thing abrding तनुत्वेऽपि महोयासौ Thus is in imitation of रुद्रट—यत्राधारे मुमदति आधेयमाधिक्य तनीपोऽपि। ( ९ २८) And it is stgmficant that no आलकारिक —रुय्यक विधाय, जगन्नाथ अप्पय्यदीक्षित —inserts this feature in his definition of the figure This suggesta that they thought it as uncalled for. The idea behind) or seems to be like this As we saw रुद्रट refers only to आधायाधिक्य. One can easily see that when one thing is आधार and another आधार, the आधेय is smaller than the And in for the though

*really* तनीयम्, is poetically described as महीयम् This was what रुद्रट thought of There was propriety in his inserting it in his defimtion of अधिक because it was confined to आधयाधिक्य But after काव्यप्रकाश had included आधाराधिक्य also within the scope of the figure तनुत्वेऽपि was not only uncalled for, but inaccurate at least so far as आधाराधिक्य was concerned Because आधार, as it is, can never be तनीय than the आधेय when viewed realshcally And तनुत्वेऽपि no doubt refers to the realistic appreciation of आधार and आधय in contrast to महीयासौ which gives the poetical conception of them Therefore, तनुत्वेऽपि makes sense only when appied to आध्ययाधित्य The आधेय though really smaller than the आधार (as a rule), is poetically described as bigger-this is the idea तनुत्वेऽपि makes no sense on the other hand, when applied to आधाराधिक्य For there is no question of the आधार being really smaller than the आधेय

 वृत्ति— तथो महतो अपि विषये Explanation of Locative Absolute सूत्र विषये i e in the matter of them though they are great.Note that the आधार- आधेय must have bigness about them That is what makes for the स्तुति of the प्रकृत तदपेक्षया तनू अपि This is true only in the case of आधेयाधिक्य The आधेय is by the very nature of things smaller than the आधार How else could it abide in the आधार? It is not true as regards आधाराधिक्य the आधेय being always bigger than the आधेय तनू अपि i e वस्तुत तनू अपि आश्रयाश्रयिणौ Really आश्रयिन् ( = आश्रित) alone is वस्तुत तनु प्रस्ततवस्तुप्रकर्षविवक्षया This is the aim of आधिक It is with a view to exalting the प्रस्तुत which as we saw is generally the अधेय अधिकतरता महीयांसी of the सूत्र explained

 **St 151 अहो विशाल** etc Illustrates आधाराधिक्य आधिक, Com ptment to the glory of a king

 Here theआधार viz. भुवनविषय is described as bigger than the आधेय,महोराशि But the आधार is described as being bigger in such a way as would result in the praise of the आधेय-- मातुमहाकाव्योऽपि यशोराशि As the आधेय यशोराशि is प्सस्तुत there is exaltation thereof, though indirectly by the आधार t being de cnbed as bigger Read कुवलयानदम् अत्र यद्यपि प्रशंसा क्रियते तथापि इति तत्प्रशंसा मन्येवमासा विपर्ययति। Note that मातुमशक्योऽपि should not be taken as proving तत्तुल्य of the आश्रय men tioned in the definition It only daves at the vastness of the प्रकृतयशोराशि दण्डिन् - gives this to illustrate

  **st 252 युगान्त** etc Illustrates अधिक श्रीकृष्ण gets overjoyed at the armval of नारद Construe

कैटभद्विष यस्या तनौ लगन्ति सविकास आसन, तन ( तनौ ) तपाधनाभ्यागमसभवा मुद्र' न ममु। युगान्त आसत In that body, wherein all souls are absorbed at the time of प्रलय and in which the worlds could rest roomly Cp St.150 ab At the time of प्रत्य, the चतुदश वनानि are absorbed by विष्णु तपोधनाम्यागमसभवा मुद Raptures due to the arrival of the sage viz नारद न ममु could not be held

 Here तनु is the आधार मुद the आधय The is stated to be bigger than the आधार by the words न ममु Hence it is आधयाधिक्ये अधिक. It naturally exalts the प्रस्तुत viz the आधेय मुद Note that तनुत्वेऽपि can apply to this आधेय After all आधेय is smaller than the आधार though the poet may describe it as bigger ( न मनु )

**५० —प्रत्यनीक**

 प्रयनीक was first given by दण्डिन् as an उपमाप्रतिपादक शब्द similar to प्रतिपक्ष प्रतिद्वन्द्विन्, विरोधिन् From this it is clear that दण्डिन् attached to the word no other sense than that of an antagonist,' 'a rival’ ‘a compeer' But later आलकारिकऽ seem to understand the word in a somewhat different sense when they employ it to designate the figure. The word is explained by them as meaning अनीकस्य सैन्यस्य प्रतिनिधि प्रत्यनीकम्। तत्तुल्यत्वात् इदमपि प्रत्यनीकम् (का प्र and रुय्यक) Thus they understand the word as meaning quasi hostile' The figure also consists in a party taking revenge not on the professed enemy, but on one who is only connected with the enemy, and is therefore quasi hostile e not really hostile but seemingly so जगन्नाथ seems to understand the name in a different sense प्रत्यनीक is acc. to him, whatever is suited for the army The army being unable to vanquish the enemy himself sometimes tackles the lesser partisan of the enemy And that is what takes place in this figure—लोक प्रतिपक्षस्य तिरस्काराय प्रत्युज्यननत्वात् प्रत्यनीकत्मुच्यते। Thus acc to र ग अनीक refers to the विजिगीषुऽ army, while acc. to का प्र⠀and रुय्यक it refers to the hostile army

 In the figure as in व्यतिरेक or प्रतिक, it is the poets aim to establish the उपमेय as superior to the उपमान This he achieves in a picturesque manner He presents the उपमेय and उपमान as two adversaries, of which the उपमान is inferior The उपमेय offends the उपमान by surpasang it on some point. And because the उपमान cannot directly vanquish the उपमेय which is too strong for it it wreaks its vengeance indirectly by attacking some weaker party connected with the उपमेय The lion has a grievance against a beautiful lady of slim waist (सिद्धार्थ), because

the lady has inflicted a defeat on him owing to her कटी being more graceful than his! The lion is too weak to defeat his adversary viz.the lady. But he has his share of vengeance on one connected with the lady. The elephant is related to her, because his temples are just like the breasts of the lady. So unable to vanquish the professed adversary viz. the lady, the lion vanquishes her partisan, the elephant! :मध्येन तनुमध्या मे मध्य जिनयतीत्ययम्। इभकुम्भौ भिनत्त्यस्याः कुचकुम्भनिमौ हरिः॥(सा.द.). This is प्रत्यनीक and obviously purports to exalt theप्रकृत or उपमेय viz. the सिंहकटी रामा. Again, another lady has defeated the moon with the beauty of her face. The moon wants to vanquish her; but unable to do so, he torments her beloved, the नायक! :—यदि तव तया जिगीषो,तद्वदनमहारि क्रान्तिसर्वस्वम्। मम तत्र किमापतितं तपसि सिताशो यदेवं माम्॥ ( रुद्रट.८.९३).

 Thus, in short, in प्रत्यनीक, the poet, in order to exalt the उपमेय over the उपमान, describes the उपमान as taking revenge on some party connected with the उपमेय, being unable to do so on the उपमेय itself.The party may be directly connected with the उपमेय as in यदि तव etc.,where the नायिका is directly connected with the नायक, or it may be indirectly connected as in मध्येन तनुमध्याetc, where the इम is connected with the सिंहकटी in so far as his कुम्भऽ (temples) are similar to the स्तनकुम्भऽ of the lady.

 Sometimes, however, the two प्रतिपक्षऽ may be real adversaries as in St. 154, not related as उपमान and उपमेय. Then the charm consists only in the surreptitious way in which the weaker प्रतिपक्ष takes revenge on the stronger one, by attacking someone connected with the stronger. Such cases of प्रत्यनीक are not as poetical as the other ones based on औपम्य.

** प्रत्यनीक vs उत्प्रेक्षाः— **It is not unusual to come across cases of प्रत्यनीक which are explicit हेतुत्प्रेक्षाऽ with इव, मन्ये etc. (cp St. 153.) or sometimes they may be implied as in the instances मध्येन etc. and यदि तव etc., where इव, मन्ये etc. are गम्य, But one thing is obvious, that all cases of प्रत्यनीक are also cases of हेतुत्प्रेक्षा. So essentially identical they are that जगन्नाथ actually refers to a view which did not admit प्रत्यनीक because हेतुत्प्रेक्षा was enough to carry out its purpose— हेतुत्प्रेक्षयैव गतार्थत्वात् नैद अलकारान्तर भवितुम् अर्हति।

 Nor is it difficult to understand thus. When the poet explains some fact in life as due to the hostility of the उपमान against the उपमेय, he is indeed indulging in a poctical fancy, because we know that there is no hostility as such between the two, except what the poet

*imagines*, nor are we aware of any real inability of the उपमान to retaliate directly, except what the poet concerves Even in cases where the twoप्रतिपक्षऽ are real adversaries, there may be real hostility, but so far as the revenge is concerned, it is no more than a poetical fancy This is उत्प्रेक्षा and it is inseparably connected with the figure प्रत्यनीक It would be therefore, true to say that प्रत्यनीक is हेतूत्प्रेक्षा itself where the अप्रकृत is fancied to be a weak rival of the प्रकृत , the stronger one, or where the revenge is fancied Read र गं प्रतिपक्षगतवलवत्वस्वामगत दुर्बलत्वयो प्रतीतेर्हेतूत्प्रेक्षान्तरादस्य वैलक्षग्यम्। नैतावना हेतूत्प्रेक्षाया बहिर्भवितुमिद्रभीष्टे।

 प्रत्यनीक was first recognised by रुद्रट, though दण्डिन् gives 'प्रत्यनीक among the different उपमाप्रतिपादक words रुद्रट's प्रत्यनीक was only सादृश्य मूल After रुद्रट all आलकारिकऽ-अल्लट, रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित—admitted the figure, but on broader lines, to include real प्रतिपक्षऽ also अप्पय्यदीक्षित admitted as an additional variety even cases where the प्रतिपक्षिन् vanquishes the opponent himself

  Note the following scheme •—-

**प्रत्यनीक**

  तिरस्कृतस्य तिरस्कृतस्य

           विपक्षेण साक्षात् सबधे विपक्षेण परम्परया सबधे   

  ***Essentials :—***\[ For explanation of the name, see above \] (1) The poet's aim is to exalt the उपमेय cover the उपमान Cp वक्तुमुपमेयमुत्तमम्—रुद्रट ( 2 ) He presents the उपमेय and उपमान as two adversaries (3) The उपमेय by surpassing the उपमान, on some point, causes the hostility (4) The उपमान is imagined to be helpless against the उपमेय \[ प्रकर्ष अप्रताकार्यत्वम् —जयरथ \] This is merely a poetical fancy and no more (5) So the उपमान has its vengeance on something connected with theउपमेय (6) This thing may be directly or indirectly connected with the उपमेय (7) The vengeance of the उपमान is no more than a poetical construction of some plain fact in hfe Cp How the ordinary fact that the elephant is killed by the lion is given the poetical colouring of the lion's revenge in मध्येन तनुमध्या etc. (8) Sometimes श्लषमूल अध्यवसाय may underle the figure Cp जैत्रनेत्रानुगौ कर्णौ उपलाभ्या अध कृतौ ( कु) Where अनुगौ (1 Following, 2 Being at the end of) and अधकृतौ (1 Surpassed, 2 Surmounted) are श्लष्ट (9) When the figure relates to उपमान-उपमेय, it 25 सादृश्यमूल, like उपमा etc (10) Sometimes, however, the two प्रपक्षऽ may be

real adversaries as राहु and कृष्ण in St 154 Then the charm lies in the vicarious punishment, given not to the real adversary, but to someone belonging to him and in the suggestion of बलीयस्त्व of the opponent In this case it may be noted, both the प्रतिपक्षऽ are equally प्रकृत Such cases are not as poetical as those based on औपम्य

 **§ सू ७२ प्रतिपक्ष** etc Construe — प्रतिपक्ष प्रतिकर्तुम् अशक्तेन तटीयस्व तिरस्क्रिया या तत्स्तुत्यै ( भवति ) तत् प्रत्यनीकम् उच्यते। प्रतिपक्ष प्रतिकर्तुम् अशक्तेन Refers to the उपमान which is unable to retaliate against its adversary viz the उपमेय Or it may refer to a real adversary तदीयस्य तिरस्क्रिया i e उपमेयसवद्धस्य or प्रतिपक्षसमबद्धस्य तिरस्क्रिया Contemptuous treatment or punishment meted out to one who is a partisan of the उपमेय or the प्रतिपक्ष या तत्स्तुत्यै भवति तत्तुत्यै i e उपमेयस्तुत्यै, or प्रतिपक्षस्तुत्यै Because it shows how the उपमेय or प्रतिपक्ष itself cannot be assailed by the उपमान or the party in question

 **वृत्ति — न्यक्कृतिपरमपि विपक्ष साक्षान्निरसितु अशक्तेन केनापि** Someone who cannot directly put down the adversary even when the adversary has offended (न्यक्कृति) him तमेव प्रतिपक्ष उत्कर्षयितु The infinitive shows the effect, not purpose The result of the action of the अशक्त विपक्ष is to exalt his opponent His inability to counter his opponent directly proves to be a tribute to the supenonty of the adversary सदाश्रितस्य तिरस्करणं तदाश्रित is only तत्सबद्ध तिरस्करण refers to the retaliatory action तत् अनीकप्रतिनिधितुल्यवाद् उच्यते The narme प्रत्यनीक Justified प्रत्यनीक is अनीकप्रतिनिधि, a representative or proxy of the real enemy (अनीक standing for the hostile army) यथा अनीके etc The अनीकप्रतिनिधित्वelucidated Just as one should out of folly, attack (अभियुज्) not the real enemy (अनीक) but someone else who is his proxy, so here in this figure when the adversary needs to be vanquished someone else belonging to him is vanquished मूढतया अशक्तत्वेन a will be more to the point.

 **St 153 & स्वं विनिर्पित** etc Illustrates प्रत्यनीक, तिरस्कृतस्य विपक्षेण साक्षात् सम्बन्धे The नायिकाऽ torments of love are fancied as due to the revenge of Cupid who unable to varquish the नायक his adversary, attacks the नायिका A compiment by some दूती to the beauty of the नायक and a sly reference to the मदनव्यथा of the नायिका स्वं i e नायक विनि and a sly reference to the मदनव्यथा निर्मितमनोभवरूप that is why Cupid has growm hostile सा भरति अनुरक्त्या expluns how the नायिका is सबद्ध to the प्रतिपक्ष viz नायक भbति (Loc.) to you. पथभि शरै युगपदेव तापयति When love causes the acutest agony, it is explained away as due to all the five arrows being discharged simultaneously अनुशय spite

 Here the two adversanes are मदन and the नायक मदन is unable to attack the नायक directly, hence he attacks the नायिका who is directly connected with the नायक This vicarious revenge exalts the नायक Thus this is प्रत्यनीक, तिरस्कृतस्य विपक्षेण साक्षात् सवन्धे Note how it is also a हेतृत्प्रक्षा, अनुशयादिव तापयति.

  **St. 154** यस्य किञ्चिदपकर्तुं etc. Illustrates प्रत्यनीक, तिरस्कृतस्य विपक्षण परम्परया सबधे राहु oppresses the moon because the moon is similar to कृष्ण's face and कृष्ण has wronged राहु by mutilating his body Construe— कायनिग्रहगृहीतविग्रह कृती राहु यस्य (कृष्णस्य) किञ्चित् अपकर्तुम् अक्षम सन् कान्तवकत्रसदृशाकृतिम् इदु अनुनापि बाधते काय etc Who has been nursing अनुनापि वाधते काय etc ennity (विग्रह) due to his body being mutilated by विष्णु ( = कृष्ण) Ref to विष्णुं's act of sevenng राहु's head from his trunk before अमृत could reach his belly. कृती the capable \[ Add this before Rāhu in the Translation \] राहु is कृती because he would not let his injury go unavenged कान्तरकूनसदृशाकृति इन्दु The moon is similar in form to the lovely face of श्रीकृष्ण A compliment to श्रीकृष्ण's face अधुनापि बाधते Reference to the lunar eclipses अधुनापि shows the long sustained hostility

 Here the two adversaries are राहु and कृष्ण, Not being able to do any harm to कृष्ण, राहु attacks इन्दु who is related to कृष्ण in so far as he (इन्दु ) is similar to कृष्ण's face This naturally exalts कृष्ण As इन्दु is connected with कृष्ण indirectly through the similarity of कृष्णऽ face thereto ( सादृश्यसम्बध ), we get प्रत्यनीक तिरस्कृतस्य परम्परया सबधे Note that here राहु and कृष्ण are not related as उपमान and उपमेय but both are real adversaries The charm is due to the poetical fancy in connecting चन्द्रग्रहण with the विग्रह between राहु and कृष्ण Further कृष्ण's कान्तवकूत्र being described as उपमान in relation to the इन्दु adds to the poetical effect of the stanza

 ** इन्दोरत्र** etc How IS इन्दु तदीय in relation to कृष्ण? कृष्ण's मुख is connected (सबधि ) with कृष्ण And to that मुख इन्दु is related by सादृश्यसवध Thus is इन्दु related to कृष्ण Here we have not साक्षात् सबन्ध but सर्वाधिसवध Read **सा चू –** भगवत्सबद्ध मुख, तत्सबद्धश्च तत्सादृश्याच्चद्र

**५१—मीलित**

 मीलित means 'obscured', 'concealed and the figure consists in some circumstance getting obscured owing to the prevalence of another circumstance of *similar indications* but of greater force The ladies were drunk, their eyes and cheeks red When they were in this condition, they got into a fit of anger But the lovers could

not recognise that they were angry, because they attributed the redness of their eyes and cheeks to drinking which the lovers had witnessed मदिरामदभरपालकपोतललोचनेषु वदनेषु। कोपो मनस्विनीना न लक्ष्यते कामिभि प्रभवन्। (रुद्रट ७१०८) Here काप and पान have the same symptoms But these the lovers attributed to पान which, having been actually witnessed by them, was felt by them with greater force This results in the कोप of the ladies being obscured This is मीलित A lovely lady had ruddy feet When once she paints them with red lac-dye, the on looker fails to make out the lac-dye. The redness he takes for the natural red of her complexion-which being the likelier is also stronger Thus this stronger circumstance obscures the weaker one vw lac-dye रसो नालक्षि लक्षायाश्चरणे सहजारुणे ( कु ) This is मीलित again -Another lady had too much of romance in her While she went to fetch water, she had occasion to indulge in dalliance in a lonely bower As she is coming back, carrying the heavy burden of a full The perspiration he attributes jar, the on looker finds her perspiring to the strain felt in carrying the नलकुम्भ How on earth could he guess the dalliance, which also was responsible for the perspiration, but was obscured by the नलकुम्भानयन which he saw before him, and was therefore stronger ? कुम्भमुभितरस सपदि सरस्या समानयन्त्यान्ते। तटकुञ्जगूढसुरत भगवानेको मनोमनो वेद \।\। र ग) This again is मीलित In chort, in मीलित we have two circumstances producing the same symptoms but of these one gets obscured by the other, which is stronger because it is felt to be likelier In the first case the obscuring symptom—नेत्रक
लयो पालिमा was आगन्तुक i e not innate but due to the adventatious or casual circumstance of पान In the second case, the obscuring symptom—चरणयो सहजारुपत्व —was स्वाभाविक i e innate, natural, the feet being naturally red. In the third case, the obscunng symptom—जलकुम्भनयनेन स्वेदादि , —was आगन्तुक ,जलकुम्भनयन being a casual feature. Thus in मीलित the symptom (or, more properly, the circumstance) obscuring the other may be आगन्तुक or सहज The poet's aim is to strike a note of irony made possible by सादृश्य

 Thus we get the following scheme—

**मीलित**

      सादृश्यमूला                         
आगन्तुकसादृश्यमूला           

 **मीलित *VS* व्याजोक्ति—** In both there are two circumstances at work producing like effects In both there is a note of irony But whereas ( 1 ) in मीलित, the circumstance itself obscures the other because it is slronger, in व्याजोक्ति, one circumstance is intentionally obscured by the party in question, though both the circumstances are of equal force ( 11 ) In मीलित there is natural obscuration in व्याजोक्ति there is a decentful one

**For मीलित *vs* सामान्य, see सामान्य  
For मीलित *vs* भ्रान्तिमान्, see भ्रान्तिमान्.**

 मीलित dates back from रुद्रट, whose पिहित and one vanety of तद्गुण are very similar अल्लट ( का प्र ) also adopted it with the two vanettes first conceived by रुद्रट रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदाक्षितः all admitted the figure The last conceived it on lines broad enough to compre hend सामान्य of others His सामान्य is therefore slightly different from that of others

 **Essentials — **\[ मीलित=तिरस्कृत वस्त्वन्तरेण समेत लक्ष्मणा वस्त्वन्तर मीलित तिरस्कृत अत्र **रुय्यक— **यद् वस्त्वन्तरेण वस्त्वन्तर निगृह्यते तदन्वर्थाभिधान र्मलितम् \] (1) There must be two circumstances at work in the same abode (2) These circumstances must have hke effects, or symptoms, or indications (3) To one of these circumstances the onlooker must attribute the effects in question (4) He attributes them to the one circumstance which he feels possible (5) This is what makes that circum tance stronger in relation to the other which is weaker (6) The stronger circumstance thus obscures the weaker one (7) The circumstance which obscures the other ray be स्वाभाविक ( Innate ) or आगन्तुक ( Adventitious ) to the abode (8) The carctum stance being स्वाभाविक or आगन्तुक its effects also may be स्वाभाविक or आगन्तुक (9) The charm of the figure fhes not so much in the सादृश्य as in the irony resulting therefrom

  **§ सू ७३. समेन** etc Construe—निजन आगन्तुना वा अपि समेन लक्ष्मणा वस्तुना यत् वस्तु निगूह्यते तत् मीलितमिति स्मृतम्। **निजेन आगन्तुना वा अपि** The वृत्ति takes it as adj of लक्ष्मणा See below निज = सहज = what belongs to the object naturally आगन्तु—औषाधिक adventitious casual वस्तु circumstance सम लक्ष्मन् (N ) similar indications or symptoms मीलित /मील 1 P to close conceal

 **वृत्ति— सहज वा आगन्तुक वा किमपि लक्षणम्** Explanation of निजेन आगन्तुना वा taken as qualifying लक्ष्मणा निज is सहज 'or natural annate. लक्षण = लक्ष्मन् of the सूत्र साधारण सम of the सूत्र Though thus the

वृति takes नितेन आगन्तुना वा as qualifying लक्ष्मणा, the more natural way would be to take them as qualifying वस्तुना Because it is the obscuring वस्तु or circumstance which is निज or आगन्तु through the वस्तु not directly साहित्यदर्पण rightly says समानलक्षण वस्तु क्वचित् सहज क्वविद् आगन्तुकम् Others, however, like रूय्यक and even जगन्नाथ take निज and आगन्तु as attributes of लक्ष्मन् रूद्रट , to whom का प्र is indebted for the divisions, has तन्मीलितमिति यस्मिन् समानचिह्वेन हर्षकोपादि। अपरण तिरस्क्रियते नित्येनागन्तु केनापि॥( ७ १०६ ) Here नित्येन आगन्तुकेनापि qualifies अपरेण referring to the वस्तु यत् विचित्र केनचित् वस्तुस्थिस्यैव बलीयस्तया तिरोधीयते when some circumstance is obscured by another owing to the latter being stronger in the very nature of things, वस्तुस्थित्यैक; e as a matter of course \[in place of in fact" in the Translation, insert as a matter of course'\] What is meant by वस्तुस्थित्या is that it is very natural under the circumstances that one वस्तु should be stronger than another Thus the रक्तत्व of the feet in रसो नापक्षि क्षि etc. is naturally (वस्तुस्थित्या) attributed to स्वभावरक्तत्व द्विधा based on सहज or आगन्तु लक्षण (or वस्तु)

  **St 155 अपाङ्गतरलं **etc. Illustrates मीलित सहजलक्ष्मणा निगूहने The innate grace of a lovely maden has obscured the onset of intoxication, the indications of both being alike मधुरवक्रवर्ण गिर words that are sweet and ornate. cd Construe इति मृगदृश अगन्तुके स्त्रीत्या स्वत स्फुरितं लीला A wind auch Elegance, charm grace लीलया स्फुरेव Grace has presented itself
