कृतपदोऽपि मद्रोदय i e Intoxication that has set in She is drunk न सलक्ष्यते Cannot be detected

  Here लीला and मद are at work Both have the same indications VIZ भगङ्गरले दृशौ, मधुरकवर्गा गिर विगसमरमरा गति and वार्न्त मुषम् But as लीला or grace is natural one attributes the symptoms to लील्य, which, thus being stronger, obscures the मद ", which is weaker because it cannot be as easily discerned as लीला Hence मीलित The obscurlng circumstance लीला being सहज, its symptoms also are सहज Hence it is मीलित, सहजलक्ष्मणा विगृहो

 रक्तरलतादिक अङ्गस्य लिङ्गं स्वाभाविकं लिङ्ग indication साधारण च महोदयेन These indications are common to intoxication also because these (भरङ्गतरलत्य etc ) appear (दर्शनात्) in the state of intoxication also

  **St. 156 ये कन्दरासु** etc. Illustrates मीलित, अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा निगूहने wenzen fed The tremor and hompilation of the fuguve foes that had filed into the Himalayas, though equally due to the vicinity of the Snowy mountain and their pinie, were attributed to the vicinity of the Snowy mountain

alone A compliment to a heroic kang who has put his enemies to flight Construe ये त्वत्पातशङ्कित्तधिय विवशा ते द्विष हिमाद्रे कन्दरासु सदा निवसन्ति तेषाम् उपुल्क सकल्प (च) अङ्ग उद्वहतामपि भिया अहो बत बुध अपि अभिशं न। स्वत्पात द्विप your enernies who are helpless ( विवश ) and afraid an ther munds of your onslaught (पात) अहो यत Oh wonder

 Here पुलक and कम्प were as much due to शैत्य of हिमगिरिसान्निध्य (cold made them shudder) as to the भय in their mind But the on looker attributed them to शैत्य which he felt as the hikely cause Thus शैत्यलक्षणऽ obscure भयलक्षणऽ And as हिमगिरिसान्निध्य was only a casual (आगन्तुक) carcumstance, we have here मीलित, आगन्तुकलक्ष्मणा निगृहने

 **अत्र etc. सामर्थ्यादवसितस्य शैत्यस्य आगन्तुकत्वात्** Here since शैत्य is आगन्तुक or casual, शैत्य which is determined (अवसित) as the cause of कम्पपुल्क on the strength ( of probabihty ) तत्प्रभवयोरपि कम्पपुलकयो ताद्रुप्य since शैत्य 1s आगन्तुक the कम्पपुल्कऽ due to it are also आगन्तुक ( तादूप्य = तत्स्वरूपव = आगन्तुक्त्व) The author shows how the लक्ष्मन् can be आगन्तुक Does it not prove that निजेन and आगन्तुना in the सून could have been better taken as qualifying वस्तुना, than लक्ष्मणा? Because लक्ष्मन् is never by itself निज or आगन्तुक. It is so only through the वस्तु or circumstance of which it is an indication समानता च कम्प पुलक are equally present in भय They are equally the symptoms of भय भयेषु casés of fnght तयो कल्प पुलकया

**५२ - एकावली**

 With एकावली we come to the last figure of the शृङ्खला class to which मालादीपक, कारणमाला and सार belonged It is not improbable that here the figure finds its place after मौलित due to the influence of रुद्रट who also treats of the figure immediately after मालित

 एकावली IS एकपङ्क्ति one tine one chan and the figure consists in a chain of (1) विशेष्यऽ-विशेषणऽ or (2) विशेषणऽ -विशेष्यऽ. The word विशेषण is not to be taken in the restricted sense of an adjective It means any circumstance that exalts the other viz विशेष्य *cp* जयरथ— स्वरूपमात्रेण अवगतस्य वस्तुन यत्सवकबलन वैशिष्ट्यमवगम्यते तद्विशेषणम्। When it is a chain of विशष्य - विशेषणऽ each following unit becomes the विशेषण of each preceding one and the विशेषण that figured as such in the preceding pair becomes the विशेष्य In the follo ving one This is सलिलं विवासिकमल, कमलानि सुगन्धिमधुसमृद्धानि। मधु लीनालिकुलाकुल अलिकुलमपि

मधुरराणिनमिह॥ ( रुद्रट ७ ११० ) we find the विशेष्य - विशेषणऽ as सलिल ( विशेष्य ) - कमल (विशेषण), कमल ( विशेष्य ) -मधु (विशेषण), मधु ( विशेष्य ) --अलिकुल (विशेषण), अलिकुल (विशेष्य ) --रणित (विशेषण)

 When the chain is of विशेषणऽ-विशेष्यऽ, each following unit is the विशेष्य in relation to each preceding one which is the विशेषण, and the विशेष्य in the preceding pair becomes the विशेषण in the following one Thus in धर्मेण बुद्धिस्तव देव शुद्धा, बुध्धा निबद्धा सहसैव लक्ष्मी। लक्ष्म्या च तुष्टा भुवि सर्वलोका, लाकैश्च नीता भुवनेषु कीर्ति॥(र ग), the series of विशेषणऽ—विशेष्यऽ is धर्म (विशेषण) — बुद्धि, (विशेष्य), बुद्धि (विशेषण)— लक्ष्मी ( विशेष्य), लक्ष्मी (विशेषण ) – लोका (विशेष्य), लोका (विशेषण) — कीर्ति (विशेष्य )

 In both the varieties above specified, something was affirmed as a विशेषण of some other But where something is *demed* as विशेषण of some other we get the same figure Thus with denial we get two more vaneties (1) विशष्य—विशेषणव्यपोह Or (2) विशेषण—विशेष्यव्यपोह. In the first of these, the विशेषण denied in the preceding pair, becomes the विशेष्य in the following par Thus in नाकुसुम तरुररिमन्नुद्याने नामधूनि कुसुमानि। नारीनालिवुल मधु नामधुरं काणमलिवल्यम्॥(रूद्रट ७ १११), (विशेष्य ) ( न ) तरु —अकुसुम (विशेषणव्यपोह) (न) कुसुमानि (विशेष्य) - अमधूनि (विशेषणव्यपोह), (न) मधु ( विशेष्य) अलीनालिकुल (विशेषणव्यपोह) (न) अलिकुल ( विशेष्य ) -अमधुरवाण--(विशेषणव्यपोह ) is the series of विशेष्यऽ and विशेषणव्यपोहऽ In the second,the विशेष्य denied in the preceding pair becomes विशेषण of the next pair Thus in पुण्यक्षेत्र न सर्वत्र पुण्यक्षत्रे न नास्तिया। नास्तिकेषु न धर्मोऽस्ति न धर्मे दुःखहेतुना॥(तर्कबागीश), सर्वत्र (विशेषण ) -न पुण्यक्षेत्र (विशेष्यव्यपोह), पुण्यक्षेत्र (विशेषण) - न नासिका (विशेष्यव्यपोह) नासिक ( विशेषण ) - धर्म (विशेष्यव्यपोह), धर्म (विशेषण) - न दुःखहेतुना (विशेष्यव्यपोह) is the series

 The relation tetucen विधानमूला *and* व्यपोहमूला एकावली - From these instances it will be clear that what the आलकारिकऽ mean by विशेषणव्यपोह or विशेषणव्यपोह is merely converting the affirmative statement into a This is clearly borne out by the instances given in negative one सा द for affirmation of विशेषण s he gives सरो विकसिताम्भोज अम्भोज भुगसगतन्। भृगा यत्र ससगीता सगीतं सरमरोदयम्॥For demal of विशेषणऽ he gives न तमल यत्र मुन्नारपाज etc (our St 153) These instances are clearly positive and negative statements of the same facts.rightly shows how the idea in both is the same, the difference being only in the matter of the positive and negative forms. Read यद्यपि स्थापके अपि स न पण्ढित इत्यादि, तथा अपोहकेऽपि स्थापकत्वं यो द्वित् स अयं तथापि शब्देन।

Thus we get the following scheme—

एकावली

   उत्तरोत्तरस्य विशेषणत्वे                    
पूर्वपूर्वस्य विशेषणत्वे

  स्थापने अपोहने स्थापने
      अपोहने

 ** एकावली vs मालादीपक— **Both belong to the रशना class of figures, and just as in मालादीपक, the preceding unit bestows the धर्म on the following one, so in एकावली of the विशेषण विशेष्य type, each preceding unrt qualifies each succeeding uut. But whereas ( 1 ) in एकावली each par is a umt complete in itself as विशेषण and विशेष्य, in मालादीपक, all the pairs, being related to the same धर्म which is mentioned once only, have a certain unity running through them (n) The charm of एकाबली bes only in the शुखला, मालादीपक charns us not only by the शुखला but also धर्मस्य सकृद् वृत्ति **अप्पय्यदीक्षित,** in fact, thinks that मालादीपक is a combination of एकावली and दीपक —अत्र ( in सग्राभागण etc ) एक समासादितपद पट्सु अपि वाक्येषु अन्वेति इति दीपकम्। शरादीनामुत्तरोत्तरविशेषणभावा देवावली च इति दीपक एकावलीयोग।

 As we saw at मालादीपक, आलङ्कारिकऽ like **जयरथ** and **जगन्नाथ** are in willing to accept मालादीपक as a variety of दीपक जगन्नाथ proposes to bring it under एकावली itself Says he अस्मिश्च एकावल्या द्वितीये भेदे (विशेषण—विशेष्य) पूर्वपूर्वै परस्य परस्य उपकार क्रियमाण यदि एकरूप स्यात् तदा अयमेव मालादीपक शब्देन व्यवहृियते प्राचीनै ।

 एकावली dates from रुद्रट, who had only उत्तरोत्तस्य विशेषणत्वे (स्थापने and अपोहने) vanely अल्लट ( का प्र ) and रुय्यक have the same variety with its sub divisions विश्वनाथ and जगन्नाथ added the पूर्वपूर्वस्य विशेषणत्व vanety with its two sub-divisions अप्पय्यदीक्षित has both उत्तरोत्तस्य विशेषणत्वे and पूर्वपूर्वस्य विशेषणत्वे does not refer to the sub-divisions based on अपोहन

 ** Essentials — **\[ एकावली = एकपक्ति — पूर्वात्तरयो परस्परानुषक्तत्वेन एकपक्तिरूपत्वात्-**जयरथ** \] (1) There must be शृङ्खला of विशष्य-विशेषणऽ or विशेषण-विशेष्य (॥) विशेषण merely means what by its presence qualifies another thing (1) The figure may be in the form of an affirmative or negative statement (iv) The negative statement is nothing but the positive statement converted and thus any positive एकावली is reducible to a negative one सर साम्भोज, अम्भोज सभृग may be reduced to the

negative form. न सर यत् न साम्भोज, न अम्भोज यज्ञ सभूगे, (v) The विशेषण must exalt the विशेष्य आलङ्कारिकऽ *do not refer to* एकावली based on अपाणीवह विशेषणऽ. (vi) The charm of the figure lies only an the शृखला form

** § सू ७४ स्थाप्यते** etc. Construe यत्र पर पर वस्तु यथापूर्व विशेषणतया स्थाप्यते अपि का अपोह्यते सा द्विधा एकावली ( भवति। प**रं परं यथापूर्वं विशेषणतया स्थाप्यते** Each following thung is affirrned (स्थाप्यते ) as विशेषण of each preceding thing Thus the preceding thing is विशेश्य **अपोह्यते = निषिध्यते.** Note that likeरूद्रट , our author (अल्लट, author of का प्र after परिकर) thought of एकावली only of the form विशेष्य-विशेषण (both स्थापने and निषेधे ),स्थाप्यतेऽपोह्यते are clearly remuniscent of रुद्रट' स्थित्यपोहाम्याम्

 **वृत्ति : वीप्सया** goes with स्थापन निषेधो वा Is repeatedly affirmed or denmed as an attribute (विशेषभाव). **वीप्सा **\[from बीप्स Desider of वि + आप् \] Repetition Hints at the necessity of the chain being long Construe: पूर्वे पूर्व प्रति उत्तरोत्तरस्य बस्तुन विशेषभावेन यत् बीप्सया स्थापन निर्देषो वा सभवति, बुधै सा द्विधा भण्यते। बुधऽ of course, the आलङ्कारिकऽ स्थापनं is विधान as opposed to निषेध or अपोइ.

 **St. 157. पुराणि** etc. lilustrates एकावली, उत्तरोत्तरस्य विशेषगतया स्थापने Description of Ujjain यस्यां = उज्जयिन्या पुराणि Buldings. वरागना Lovely woman रूपपुरस्कृतोग्यः Whose persons are made conspicuous by ther beauty. समुन्मीलितसद्विलास What discloses excellent graces or amours कुसुमायुधस्य अघ्रं because of कामोदीपकत्व

 Here the शृङ्खल is पुर (विशेष्य) -- वराङ्गना (विशेषण), बराङ्गना (विशेष्य )— रूप (विशेषण), रूप (विशेष्य) - विशेष (विशेषण), निगम (विशेष्य) कुसुमायुषम्य अघ्र(विशेषण). Each following is affirmed as the विशेषण of each preceding. lience प्रकावर्ग, उत्तरात्तरस्य विशेषणता स्थापने

 **St. 158, न वत्सलं** etc Illustrates एकावली उत्तरीत्तरस्य विशेषणतया अभेदने The deal water, lotus, bee and hum pictured

 Here the series is (न) जल (विशेष्य ) — न, मुक्कर( विशेषणप्रपदे)

 Each following is a विशेषण denied of each preceding विशेष्य Hence एकावली उत्तरोत्तरस्य उत्तर For the positive forms see the starza from सा द given above

  विधीयते= स्थाप्यो उत्तरत्र i e in St 158 पूर्वत्र। i e in St. 157.

 पूर्वं पोषणम् i e the विशेषण and विशेषणऽ should be understood likewise

**५३ - स्मरण**

 स्मरण as recollection and the figure consists in recollecting a thing at the sight of something similar to it A person looks at a lotus and is put in mind of the face of his wife because of the सादृश्य between the lotus and the face पङ्कत पश्यत कान्तामुख मे गाहते सन। (कु) This IS स्मरण When we see something striking, an impression is left on our mind This impression when stirred by a circumstance, like the sight of something similar, revives the memory of the object This is the actual process behind स्मरण that we get in our everyday experience And the process presupposed by the poet in his स्मरणालङ्कार is much the same But there is a poetical touch about the figure and it consists in the poet's aim to turn स्मरण into a medium for conveying सादृश्य to the सहृदय A fact of our everyday life is by the poet thus turned into a fine instrument of सादृश्य When, for instance विक्रम, in the विक्रमार्वुशीय (४१५ ) says — आरक्तरानिमिरिय कुसुमैकान्दली। सलिलगर्भै। कोपादन्तर्बाष्पे स्मरयति मा लोचने तस्या, we find स्मरण employed as a medium for conveying the सादृश्य between उर्वशी's eves in anger and नवकन्दलीकुसुमऽ

** Scope of the figure — **If स्मरण conveys सादृश्य between the thing seen and the thing remembered, the question anses as to which of the two things is उपमान and उपमेय To judge from the instances given by आल्कारिकऽ, the thing seen may be उपमान or उपमेय, and correspondingly. the thing remembered may be उपमेय or उपमान Thus in जारक्तराजिभि etc the thing seen VIz नवकन्दलीकुसुमऽ are उपमान in relation to the thing remembered कोपादन्तर्बाष्पे नयने which are उपमेय Again when the यक्ष says to the मेघ (**मेघदूत २ १४**) तस्यास्तीरे रचिनशिखर पेशलैरेिन्द्रनीलै क्रीतशैल कनक्कदली वेष्टनप्रेक्षणीय। मद्गेहिन्या प्रिय इति सखे चेतसा कातरेण प्रेक्ष्योपान्तस्फुरिततटित त्वा तमेव स्मरामि॥the मेघ seen is the उपमान in relation to the क्रोटाशैल remembered,which is उपमेय Sometimes, however, the thing seen may be the उपमेय and the thing remembered the उपमान When सुमन्त्र ( **उत्तरराम चरित ५४**) at the sight of लव says अतिशयितमुरासुरप्रभाव शिशुमवलोक्य तथैव तुल्य रूपम्। कुशिकमुनमखाद्विषा प्रमार्थे धृतधनुष रघुनन्दन स्मरामि॥लव who is seen is उपमेय रघुनन्दन ( = राम ), who is remembered, the उपमान Read **जयरथ-अतश्च** स्मर्यमाणेन अनुभूयमानस्य अनुभूयमानेन वा स्मर्यमाणस्य सादृश्य परिकल्पनमयमलकार। यदुक्त — यथा दृश्येन रनिता साम्यधौ स्मयमाणगा \। स्मर्यमाणकृताप्यस्ति तथेय दृश्यगामिनी॥इति।

 र गं comprehends within the scope of the figure even those cases where the thing seen is not itself सदृश to the thing remembered, but is connected with something else which is सदृश to the thing remembered The poet sees a चातक This puts him in mind of मेघ

and this, in its turn, revives the memory of कृष्ण मेघश्याम This is स्मरण acc. to र गं , though here the चातक that was seen is not सदृश to कृष्ण but connected with मेघ that is सदृश to कृष्ण Sometimes it may be the other way श्रीकृष्ण sees in the course of जतकाल a lady of superb grandeur like लक्ष्मी
coming out of water This reminds him of समुद्रमथन Here the lady is similar to लक्ष्मी and this revives the me mory of समुद्रमपन — दिव्यानामपि कृतविस्मया पुरस्तादम्भस्त स्फुरदरविन्दवारहस्ताम्। उद्वीक्ष्य नियमित काचिदुत्तरन्ती अस्मार्थीञ्जलनिधिमन्थनस्य शौरि॥(शिशुपालवध) cated by कुबलयानन्द।

 सा द refers to a view according to which the thing seen may be dissimilar to the thing remembered, and yet give स्मरण, which is therefore, स्मरण by contrast When राम at the sight of the sad phght of सीता in the forest is put in mind of the happy days she once enjoyed in the palace it is स्मरण Read सा द —राघवानन्दमहापात्रास्तु वैसादृश्यात् स्मृतिमपि स्मरणारारमिच्छन्ति। तत्र उदाहरण तेषामेव यथा — शिरीषमृद्वी गिरिषु प्रपेदे यदा यदा दुःखशतानि सीता। तदा तदास्वा सदनेषु सौल्यलक्षाणि दध्यौ गन्दश्रु राम॥

 To all these wider implications of the figure except the last, one fact is common and that is the poet's emphasis on सादृश्य Even in the last it is the antithesis to सादृश्य that plays its part When, there fore, in a स्मरण there is no element of सादृश्य, it cannot be स्मरणालकार Read रुय्यक— सादृश्य विना तु स्मृतिनायमलङ्कार। If by chance राम remembers the miserable days he passed on the banks of the गौदावरी, it is not स्मरणालकार S० स तथेति प्रतिशाय विसृज्य कथमप्युमम् \। ऋषीश्योतिर्मयान् सप्त सरमार रमाशासन॥ (कु स ६३) is not स्मरणालङ्कार Read तर्कवागीश— एव चिन्ता अन्यस्मृतेरपि नालङ्कारत्वम्। If by looking to the Bhavani sword of Sivaji one remembers that eminent hero, that too is not स्मरणच्वार सर्वधिष्ठान जन्यस्मरणस्य नालङ्कारत्वम्— says तर्कवागीश

 रमरण dates back to रुद्रट अलट (Author of का प्र after परिकर), रूय्यक विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित all admitted it अप्पय्यदीक्षित called it स्मृति

 ** स्मरण vs. (भूतविषयक) भावना -- **In both there is contact with the past But while (1) in स्मरण the thing of the past is only remembered, in भाविक, it is actually visualised (i) In स्मरणthere are two सदृश things, one अनुभूत the other स्मृत in भाविक, the thing of the past is the same as the one whose 1 ion is conjured up at present. (in) सादृश्य is the lieynote of स्मरण भावनप्रकर्ष of भाविक

 **स्मरण VS. रूपक — **Both are based on सादृश्य, and in both one thing puts the observer in mind of another So alled they are that one may be easily reduced to the other. The रूपक मुखं चन्द्र may be put

in the form of स्मरण as मुख दृष्ट्वा चन्द्रं स्मरामि or चद्र दृष्ट्वा मुख स्मरामि। But whereas (1) in स्मरण , there is simply the remembrance of the past thing, in रूपक, there is the आरोप of one thing over another So one conveys सादृश्य through the medium of स्मृति, the other through आरोप ( 11 ) In स्मरण, the thing seen may be उपमान or उपमेय (See above) In रूपक, the thing seen is always उपमेय

 **Essentials**— \[सदृशवस्तुत्मरणात् स्मरणम्\] (1) It is a figure essentially based on सादृश्य between two things and purporting to convey that सादृश्य to the सहृदय (2) Of these two things, one is actually अनुभवविषय (3) This recalls the memory of another thing similar to it, which was once an अनुभवविषय, but is not so at the time of स्मरण \[रुद्रट calls it कालान्तरामुभूत् \] (4) Sometimes, the thing seen or अनुभव is not itself सदृश to the thing remembered, but is connected with another which is to the thing remembered Thus चातक reminds the poet first of मेव and then of मेघश्याम This involves double स्मरण (5) Sometimes again the thing that is remembered is an adjunct to the thing which Is सदृश to the thing seen or अनुभूत Thus समुद्रमथन that is remembered is an adjunct to लक्ष्मी who is similar to the beautiful lady swimming This also involves double स्मरण (6) Thus the thing remembered may be an उपमान or उपमेय or something connected therewith (7) The thing that is अनुभवविषय may be प्रत्यक्ष to eather of the senses A blind man by feeling something may get the स्मरण of something of similar touch (8) There is always a clear indication of
स्मरण by the use of the root in one form or another

 ** § सू ७५ यथानुभव** etc Construe - तत्सदृशे दृष्ट अर्थस्य यथानुभव स्मृति स्मरण ( भवति )। यथानुभव स्मृति Remembrance of it as it was once experienced सत्सदृशे दृष्टे When another thing similar to it is seen सादृश्य is the essence of the figure दृष्टे should be taken in the wider sense of अनुभूते That is it may be perceived by चक्षुस्, त्वक् श्रोत्र etc.

 **वृत्ति — केन चिदाकारेण नियत** means only नियतरूप Of a specific form यदा कदाचित् अनुभूतोऽभूत् स्मृति presupposes the stages— (१) अनुभव in the past of a thing (२) The सस्कार ( भावन ) left by the अनुभव (३) The उद्रोष of this संस्कार by the अनुभव at present of something similar कालान्तरे There is no limit as to the interval be tween the sua and its स्मृति One may get vivid memones even from past lives स्मृतिप्रतिबोधाधायिनि तत्समान वस्तुनि दृष्टे सति When something is seen (or cognised) similar to the thing cognised in the past, which awakens memory of it More accurately, संस्कारोद्वोधाधापिनि

समानवस्तुदर्शन stus up the सस्कार which results in स्मृति तथैव स्मर्यते Perverted remembrance will not give the figure

 **St 159 निम्ननाभि etc** Illustrates स्मरण The description of aquatic sports of celestial damsels The water rushing in the cavities of their navels produced a gurgling sound that by its similarity put them in mind of the guttural trill at the time of dalliance Construe— चलदृशा निम्ननाभिकुहरपु यद् अम्म लहरीभिः प्लावित तद्भवै मुरनार्य (v l सुरकन्या) कुमुरतकण्ठताना स्मारिता। निम्न deep निम्ननाभि an item of beauty लहरी Wave Waves forced water into the navels तद्भवै कुहरूतै By the gurgle caused thereby 1 e by the water rushing into the navels Onomatopoeic from the sound of the gurgles Tvm Edition refers to a v l कुहुरूत कुहुरूत refers to the coming of a cuckoo So a suggest on that the gurgle was as sweet a cuckoos cooing

 Here the कुहुरुतऽ of the water remind the damsels of the because the former were सदृण to the latter The poets aim is to drive at the सादृश्य between कुहरुत and सुरतकण्ठरूत Hence स्मरण Which of them is the उपमेय? Possibly कुहरूत The poet wants to pay a tribute to the aquatic sports and in the course of it he suggests that the gurgles were as sweet as सुरतकण्ठरूतऽ!

 **St 160 करजुअ** etc Illustrates स्मरण of a thing of past life श्रीकृष्ण as a suckling sucking यशोदा is breast which he has held in his hands is put in mind of the पाचजन्य that he as विष्णु had occasion to blow in his war on demons Sansknt rendering - करयुगगृहीतयशोदास्तन मुखविनिवेशिताधरपुटस्य। सस्मृतपाचजन्यस्य नमत कृष्णस्य रामाञ्चम्। पाचजन्य the conch of विष्णु that had grown from the bones of a demon by name पाचजन्य सस्मृतपाचजन्य कृष्ण being the 8th अवतार *remembers* the पाचजन्य which was in past ages used by him as विष्णु रोमाञ्चम् The पाचजन्य was blown byविष्णु during his wars on the demons The memory of these causes thrill to विष्णु's अवतार

 Here कृष्ण sucking his mother s breast remembers पाचजन्य which he as विष्णु blew in lives past The poet suggests that the breast is sim lar to the पाचजन्य Hence स्मरण It relates to a thing of the past lives. Read मा च अत्र पूत्रमानुभूतस्य स्मरणम्। पूर्वत्र तु इहभवानुभूतस्य)

**५४ — भ्रान्तिमान्**

 भ्रान्तिमान् or mare properly भ्रान्ति as कुवलयानन्द aptly names it is a figure closely similar to and this in all probabil ty as the reason why it comes to be dealt with immediately after स्मरण

अप्पय्यदीक्षित (कु) also observes the same order Just as, in स्मरण, the poet has turned the everyday occurrence of remembrance (स्मरण ) into a fine medium for conveying सादृश्य, so in भ्रान्तिमान् also, we find the poet pressing into the service of सादृश्य the occasional experience of mistaken identity (विपर्यय) When the lotus is mistaken for the wife's face, it is भ्रान्तिमान् When the lotus puts one in mind of the wife's face, it is स्मरण Thus both are equally helpful to the poet as instruments for conveying सादृश्य Devious indeed are the poet's ways of securing his aims ¹

  Says a poet अय प्रमत्तमथुपस्त्वमुख वेति पञ्जम् \।\। ( कु ) Here the in toxicated bee is described as mistaking the face of a lady for a lotus This is evidently to drive at the सादृश्य between मुख and पक्ज In the course of the praise that he lavishes on his patron, another poet says पालयति त्वयि वसुधां विविधाध्वरधूममालिनी ककुभ पश्यन्तो दूयन्ते घनसमयाशकया हसा॥(रूद्रट ८८८) Here swans are described as getting annoyed, mistaking the smoke from sacrifices for clouds The poet thus hunts at the सादृश्य between अध्वरधूम and घन When विक्रम mistakes the नवशादूल with red insects for उर्वशी's स्तनांशुव we get another भ्रानिमान्—हृतोष्ठरागैर्नयनोदविन्दुभि निमग्ननाभेर्पितद्भिरङ्कितम् \। च्युत रमा भिन्नतेरसशय शुवोदरश्यामा मिद स्तनांशुक्म्॥(विक्रमोर्वशीय ४ १७ ) Another stuhing instance of भ्रान्तिमान् is where the bee perches on the parrot's beak mistaking it for a पलशमुकुल, and the parrot in its turn attempts to pick up the bee mis tahing it for a जम्बूफल पलशमुकुलभ्रान्त्या शुकतुण्डे पतत्यलि। सोऽपि जम्बूफलभ्रान्त्या तमलिं धर्तुमिच्छति॥(कु)

 From these instances it will be clear how सादृश्य is the keynote of the figure. The poet may, not infrequently, also have an eye on the rony that mustaken identity gives rise to and then भ्रान्तिमान् gets an additional charm Thus in the case of the swans the bee and the parrot, and king विक्रम it is not difficult to perceive a vein of irony But the motif of the figure is सादृश्य Read रुय्यक—सादृश्यप्रयुक्ता च भ्रान्ति अस्य विषय। सादृश्यहेतुकापि भ्रान्तिविर्च्छिष्टार्थ कविप्रतिभात्थापितैव गृह्यो। न स्वरसोत्थपिता युक्तिकारतावत् ।

 The figure appears only when the भ्रान्ति based on सादृश्य When there is a perverted notion due not to सादृश्य, but some other circumstance like giddiness, we cannot have भ्रान्तिमान् When the wrestler चर्णुर being punched hard by कृष्ण, becomes giddy and feels like seeing a hundred moons in the sky, it is not भ्रान्तिमान्, though there is a perverted notion. Because there is no suggestion of

 सादृश्य therein—दामोदरकराघातचूर्णिताशेषवक्षसा। दृष्ट चाणूरमल्लेन शतचन्द्र नमस्तलम्॥(रूय्यक) But when on the contrary विक्रम though in the state of one demented mistakes the शूद्रक for the स्तनायुक there is भ्रान्तिमान् because the perverted notion is due to सादृश्य Read रय्यक—गाढमममहारादिना तु भ्रान्ति नास्य अलङ्कारस्य विषय। जयरभ cites some of the curcurnstances that कामशोकभयो मादचौरस्वप्नाद्युप्लुता। अभूतानि (१ ने ) व पश्यन्ति पुरतोऽवस्थितानिव।  

 **भ्रान्तिमान् Vs भाविक**—In both there is a cognition of a thing which is not there But whereas (।) in भ्रान्तिमान् the cognition is erroneous in भाविक it is not so In भाविक the poet gets the vision of the thing exactly as it was Read रुय्यक—न चेय ( भावना in भाविक) भ्रान्ति। भूतमाविन भूतभावितयैव प्रकाशनात्। (॥ ) In भ्रान्तिमान् the poet drives at सादृश्य In भाविक he drives at भावनाप्रकर्ष

 **भ्रान्तिमान् vs. मीलित**—In both there is a concealment of one circumstance due to सादृश्य But whereas (। ) In भ्रान्तिमान्, the thing seen is not there in मीलित the circumstance seen ts there (॥ ) In भ्रान्तिमान् it is error that blinds the party to the real fact In मीलित it is the बलीयस्त्व of one curcumstance that obscures the other

 **भ्रान्तिमान् vs रूपक**—In both there is a sure knowledge of some thung else due to सादृश्य But (1) whereas in भ्रान्तिमान् the idea is due to वास्तविक भ्रम or erroneous identity in रूपक it is due to आहार्य अभेदज्ञान or intentional or simulated identity In other words in रूपक the poet consciously Identifies the उपमेय with the उपमान (॥ ) In भ्रान्तिमान् to the शातृ there is knowledge only of the अप्रकृत In रूपक the शातृ is awáre of both (मुख and चन्द्र )

 **भ्रान्तिमान् vs उत्प्रेक्षा**—In both there is a concept of something else than the प्रकृत due to सादृश्य But whereas (। ) in भ्रान्तिमान् the concept is due to genuine error in उत्प्रेक्षा it is due to आहार्य अभेदज्ञान (॥) In भ्रान्तिमान् the emning party gets a consciousness of one thing only viz the अप्रकृत In उत्प्रेक्षा the poet is conscious of both प्रकृत मुख and अप्रकृत चन्द्र ( 11 ) In भ्रान्तिमान् there is certainty of knowledge In उत्प्रेक्षा there is uncertainty ( सभावन) as though based on error the mind wavers between two al ernatives viz the प्रकृत and अप्रकृत

 ** भ्रान्तिमान् vs अतिशयोक्ति** (१)—In both there is an idea of some thung else due to सादृश्य In both the प्रकृत is not referred to \[ अतिशयोक्ति (१) being चन्द्रोऽयम् \] But whereas in भ्रान्तिमान् the idea of अभेद is due to real error in अतिशयोक्ति it is due to आहार्यज्ञान

 भ्रान्तिमान् dates back from रुद्रट, though earher still, a forerunner of the figure could be found in दण्डिन्'s मोहोपमा अल्लट, रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदाक्षित all admitted the figure अप्पय्यदीक्षित named it भ्रान्ति on the analogy of स्मरण and with greater propriety

 Essentials —\[ भ्रान्ति चित्तधर्म स विद्यते यस्मिन् भणितिप्रकारे स भ्रान्तिमान् More properly the name should have been भ्रान्ति like स्मरण \] (1) The poet employs error based on सादृश्य to convey सादृश्य between two things (2) There must be mistaken identity, one thing being mistaken for another due to सादृश्य (3) The error must be वास्तविक, not आहार्य (4) The thing mistaken is प्रकृत, the thing mistaken for is अप्रकृत Thus when the शुकतुण्ड 1s mistaken for पलाशमुकुल, शुकतुण्ड is प्रकृत, पलाश मुकुल अप्रकृत (5) The mistaken identity may sometimes have a touch of irony, in addition to सादृश्य a which it conveys

 § सूत्र ७६ भ्रान्तिमान् etc Construe तत्तुल्यदर्शने अन्यसवित् भ्रान्तिमान् ( भवति )।When at the sight of something similar there is an apprehersion ( सवित् = ज्ञान ) of something else, we get भ्रातिमान्

 वृति तत् = अप्राकरणिक तेन अप्राकरणिकेन तुल्य यत् प्राकरणिक तस्य दर्शने So तत्तुल्यदर्शने means प्राकरणिकदर्शने अन्यसवित् = अन्यत् is अप्राकरणिक So at the sight of the प्रकृत one gets the cognition of the अप्रकृत तस्य तथा विधस्य—i e
प्राकरणिकस्य अप्राकरणिकतुल्यस्य तथाविध refers to प्राकरणिकतुल्यत्व अप्राकरणिकतया सवेदनम् = अप्राकरणिक इति सवेदनम् Apprehension that it is अप्राकरणिक न चैष etc भ्रान्तिमान् distinguished from रूपक e g मुख चन्द्र and प्रथमा अतिशयोक्ति (निगीर्यो यवसानमूला) e g चन्द्रोऽयम् See above तत्र i e रूपके प्रथमातिशयोक्तौ च वस्तुतो भ्रमस्य अभावात् As said above, in रूपक and अतिशयोक्ति (१) we have आहार्यज्ञान In भ्रान्तिमान् we have real or वास्तविक भ्रम इह च etc In this figure, भ्रम or भ्रान्ति is clearly assumed (प्रतिपन्न ) as is evident from the name भ्रान्तिमान् and as names are given to figures consistently with their purport  

 St. 161 कपाले etc Illustrates भ्रान्तिमान् A ‘comedy of errors’ ! Description as to how the rays of the moon are mistaken by a cat for milk by an elephant for lotus fibres and by a lady for her silken raiment Construe कपाले (स्थितान्) शशिन करान् मार्तार पय इति लेढि। लेढि Licks लिह् तरच्छिद्रप्रोतान् qualifies शशिन वरान् As the rays shot forth to the ground through the interstices of the trees. विसमिति सङ्कलयति The elephant wants to grab them thinking them to be I tus fibres. c Construe................ तान् ...... वनिता.....अशुकमिति हरति। The rays spread on the bed were mistaken for the white silken garment that was taken off

during रत प्रभामत्तश्चन्द्र The moon is conceived as having got intoxicated with his splendour A tribute to the प्रभा वि+प्लु To confound

 Here the शशिकरऽ, the प्रकृत, are, due to their सादृश्य , mistaken for पय, बिस and अशुरू The mistake is genune The poet's aim is to convey the सादृश्य between the प्रकृत and अप्रकृतऽ Hence भ्रान्तिमान् सा चू says it is मालारूप Note the fine touch of irony

५५—प्रतीप

 पुरुरवस्, in the विक्रमोर्वशीय (२३) while layishing praise on the superb beauty of उर्वशी, says उपमानस्यापि सखे प्रत्युपमान वपुस्तस्या। What better tribute could be paid to beauty than suggesting that it (beauty) is the standard of standards? This gives us the spirit of प्रतीप In it the poet ende-avours to exalt the उपमेय by making it a powerful adversary to the उपमान Normally the उपमेय is expected to be less than the उपमान, the standard But here the tables are turned, and not only does the उपमेय cease to be inferior to the उपमान, but becomes its nival nay, sometimes it even usurps the lofty place of the उपमान This idea is implicit in the name प्रतीप itself प्रतीप means प्रतिकूल (adverse), or more significantly still what goes against the tide’

 In व्यतिरेक, we saw how the poet asserts the superiority of the उपमेय over the उपमान In यद्यर्थोक्तौ अतिशयोक्ति (ie 3rd vanety), the superiority was still more pronounced That अतिशयो-क्ति just morks the transition from व्यतिरेक to प्रती in so far as it assigns by suggestion the status of उपमान to the उपमेय As in व्यतिरेक and अतिशयोक्ति the keynote of the figure is सादृश्य

 Four sub divisions of the figure—There are, according to our author, four modes in which the poet realises his aim viz. of making the उपमेय a rival to the उपमान

 (I) Sometimes the poet takes exception to the उपमान itself by condemning it as being superfluous when the उपमेय is there to perform its rôle Says a poet—दृष्ट चेदन तस्या किं पद्येन किमिन्दुना। (कु) If you have seen her face of what use could the lotus or the moon be to you? Here the lotus and the moon that are generally acknow ledged to be उपमानऽ are discarded as superfluous because the face worthily fills their place

 (II) On occasions, the poet may bring the उपमान into contempt by reducing it to the status of the उपमेय and installing उपमेय in its place Says a poet त्वलोचनसम पद्यं त्वद्वक्त्रसदृशो विधु। (कु) where पद्म

and विधु normally recognised as उपमानऽ are reduced to the status of उपमेयऽ, ther place being usurped by लोचन and वक्त्र which become उपमानऽ. When the वैतालिक in मालविकाग्निमित्र com-pliments the king saying सर्वैरुस्रै• समग्रस्त्वमिव नृपगुणै दीप्यते सप्तसप्ति (२ १२), we get प्रतीप, because the sun is reduced to the status of उपमेय , in order to exalt the king who becomes उपमान

 (iii) Sometimes the poet goes one step ahead in his condemnation of the उपमान Not content with reducing it to the status of the उपमेय, and lifting the उपमेय to its ( उपमान’s ) status, he goes to the length of saying that it means degradation for the उपमान to have such a thing as its उपमेय! When the poet says मुधापवादो मुग्धाक्षि त्वन्मुखाभ किलाम्बुजम्। ( कु. ), not only does he reduce the अम्बुज to the status of an उपमेय, but going further he stigmatises the comparison of the अम्बुज to the face as a gratuitous caltumny (मुधापवाद)।

 In (ii) and (iii) the figure takes the form of उपमा with the प्रकृत as the उपमान and अप्रकृत as the उपमेय In ( ii ), the उपमा actually materialises In (iii) it is suggested that the उपमा though attemp-ted does not materialise, as it involves degradation to bring the अप्रकृत into comparison with the प्रकृत if the reference to degradation is dropped, the उपमा materialises and then प्रतीप (iii) becomes प्रतीप (ii) Thus (iii) has close affinmtres with यद्यर्थोक्तौ अतिशयोक्ति.  

 (iv) (A) There is yet another way in which the poet may shove down the उपमान from its high place He boosts up the उपमान as high as possible and then declares that at last it has found its compeer in the उपमेय It is easy to understand how this naturally exalts the उपमेय The mortally afflicted lover says क क्रौर्यदर्पस्ते मृत्यो त्वत्तुल्या सन्ति हि स्त्रिय।(कु) “It is high time, O Death, that you ceased to be vain on the score of your cruelty In woman you have a worthy match।” Here the place of g was first raised high and then woman was made मृत्यु's equal in point of cruelty This naturally results in woman being charged with the maximum cruelty,

 (B) This procedure may be employed in another way also The poet may boost up the उपमेय (not उपमान as in (A)\] high enough and then say that it has the उपमान as its equal Looking to a charming face says the poet, अल गर्वेन ते वक्त्र कान्त्या चन्द्रो भवदृश। (फु.) Here वक्त्र i boosted up by the words and अल गर्वेण and then the poet says that in the चन्द्र it has its match। Note that here also the उपमान and उपमेय exchange places. This sub-division may thus come under (ii)

In fact the instance of (ii)—गर्वमसवाह्य etc. given by our author has been taken by कुवलयानद to illustrate this viz iv ( B ). Our author does not refer to this sub-division (iv B)

Thus we get the following scheme.—

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708526667Screenshot2024-02-21201305.png"/>

IV B is not referred to by काव्यप्रकाश, or रुय्यक or विश्वनाथ But जगन्नाथ and अप्पय्यदीक्षित specify it जयरथ would call these varieties distinct figures and not sub-divisions of one figure एक द्वितीय-मिति अभिदधता ग्रन्थकृता प्रतीपाद्य अलङ्कारद्वय पुन सामान्यलक्षणाभावात् एकमेव द्विप्रकारमित्युक्तम्। जगन्नाथ suggests that of these varieties, II and IV ( A ) ( B ) can come under उपमा, III under व्यतिरेक and I under आक्षेप and so प्रतीप need not be admitted as an additional figure–If at all it is recognised as such, it should comprise only the first vanety ( उपमानस्य आक्षेप ), जगन्नाथ argues argument is not very convincing and is based more on sophistry than logic.

 **प्रतीप vs व्यतिरेक**—In both, the poet’s aim is to establish the उपमेय as superior to the उपमान , but the way in which this is achieved in one figure is different from that in the other I hus —

 **प्रतीप ( १ ) vs व्यतिरेक**—\[ प्रतीप-मुखे सति किं चन्द्रेण व्यतिरेकः— मुख चन्द्र इव न सकलकम् \] (i) in this variety of प्रतीप, the उपमान is declared as superfluous, in व्यतिरेक there is no such challenge to the उपमान. (ii) This प्रतीप is not in the form of an उपमा, व्यतिरेक is in the form of an उपमा, though in favour of the उपमेय

 ** प्रतीप (२) vs व्यतिरेक**—\[ प्रतीप–त्वलोचनम् पद्म, व्यतिरेक—मुख चन्द्र इव न सकलकम् \] Though in both the figures, we are presented with an उपमा, still, in प्रतीप, the उपमा is effected with the अप्रकृत as the उपमेय and प्रकृत as the उपमान In व्यतिरेक, the प्रकृत does remain उपमेय and the अप्रकृत उपमान, as usual

 ** प्रतीप (३) vs व्यतिरेक**—\[ प्रतीप—त्वन्मुखार्भ अबुज इति मुथापवाद। व्यतिरेक—मुख चन्द्र इव न सकलकम् \] Here though in both the figures, we get the form of उपमा, still (i) in प्रतीप, the उपमा does not materiabse (अनिष्पन्न ) due to the suggestion of degradation, while in व्यतिरेक, the उपमा material-ises though in favour of the उपमेय (ii) In प्रतीप, the उपमान becomes उपमेय, the उपमेय उपमान In व्यतिरेक, they retain their normal status

 **प्रतीप (४A ) vs व्यतिरेक**—\[प्रतीप—मृत्यो न त्व निरुपम त्वत्तुल्या स्त्रिय सन्ति। व्यतिरेक—मुख चन्द्र इव न सकलकम् \] Though both are उपमऽ in form in प्रतीप the mode exalts the उपमेय only as ngh as the उपमान, but not higher. In व्यतिरेक, the उपमेय is described as being superior to the उपमान

 **प्रतीप (४B ) vs व्यतिरेक**—\[प्रतीप—वक्त्र न निरुपमम्। तत्सदृश चन्द्र अस्ति। व्यातेरेक—मुख चन्द्र इव न सकलकम् \] Though both are उपमाऽ th form, still (i) in प्रतीप, the प्रकृत is not declared as any high-er than the अकृत In व्यतिरेक, the प्रकृत is superior to the अप्रकृत ( ii) In प्रतीप, the प्रकृत becomes उपमान, the अप्रकृत, उपमेय In व्यतिरेक, they retain their normal status.

 The figure प्रतीप, if not in name, at least in substance, dates from दण्डिन् whose विपर्यासोपमा corresponds to the second vanety of प्रतीप वामन accepted the figure but under the name आक्षेप. After वामन, रुद्रट accepted the figure without specifying the divisions काव्यप्रकाश accepted the figure, in the सूत्र, with the divisions -आक्षेपमूल and उपमानस्य उपमेयत्वमूल which in the वृत्ति, are developed into four as given above रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ अप्पय्यदीक्षित all accepted the figure While रुय्यक and विश्वनाथ give divisions on the lines of the का प्र, the last two give one more division \[ See above \]

 Essentials.—\[ प्रतीपम् = प्रतिकूलम् उपमानप्रतिकूलत्वात् उपमेयस्य प्रतीपमिति व्यपदेश—रुय्यक \] (1) Like व्यतिरेक, it is a figure based on सादृश्य. (2) The poet wants to exalt the उपमेय by declaring it to be superior to the उपमान (A) This he does by challenging the use of the उपमान, when the उपमेय 1s there to perform its rile This is the First variety (B) Or he may exalt the उपमेय over the उपमान, by making उपमान the उपमेय and vice versa Second variety (C) Or, after thus inverting their position he may still further exalt the प्रकृत by suggesting that it is a degradation for it to have the अप्रकृत as its उपमेय Third variety (D) Or he may exalt the उपमान too high and then raise the उपमेय to that level Fourth variety (A) (E) Or he may

 exalt the प्रकृत high enough by turning it into an उपमान and then say that it has found its equal in the अप्रकृत which becomes उपमेय in the inversion Fourth vanety (B) In both these varieties (A) and (B), there must be the feature of raising the उपमान or उपमेय too high Read सा द तदनुक्तौ (उत्कषानुक्तौ ) तु नायमलङ्कार।

 § सु ७७ Construe—उपमानस्य आक्षेप प्रतीपम् (भवति)। यदि वा तस्यैव (उपमानस्यैव) तिरस्कारनिबन्धना उपमेयता कल्प्या (तर्हि प्रतीप भवति।) आक्षेप Objection Exception वामन actually called the figure आक्षेप तिरस्कारनियन्धना उपमेयता कल्प्या (= कल्प्यते) If the उपमान, as a matter of contempt is turned into the उपमेय Note that the सूत्र refers to two broad divisions. The वृत्ति gives four varieties the first three of which come under the two given in the सूत्र। The last is not covered by the definition  
  वृत्ति—अस्य धुर आक्षिप्यत Explains the first variety Therein the उपमान is taken exception to on grounds of futility ( कैमर्थ्येन), because it is suggested that the उपमेय is adequate (प्रौढ) to perform the role ( धुर वह् ) of the उपमान (अस्य) When the उपमेय can do the job of the उपमान latter becomes superfluous यदपि etc Explains उपमानस्य उपमेयत्वे प्रतीप When again the उपमान is turned into an उपमेय in order to bring it into contempt and with a view to showing that there is another thing which is its उपमान this उपमानान्तर is nothing but the उपमेय elevated to that status. So the idea is that it is the poets aim to bring the उपमान into contempt by showing that it is an उपमेय In relation to the प्रकृत which is its उपमान उपमेयस्य उपमान प्रतिकूलवर्तिस्वात् Explains the propnety of the name प्रतीप प्रतीप IS प्रतिकूल In this figure the उपमेय plays the role of an adversary to the उपमान उभयरूप Note that broadly the divisions are (i) उपमानस्य आक्षेप and (ii) उपमानस्य उपमेयत्वे

 st 162 लावण्यौकसि etc Illustrates प्रतीप ( १ ) i e उपमानस्य कैमर्थ्येन आक्षेपे A compliment to the be-auty prowess generosity and capacity for government of a king that are so exalted that they have rendered the उपमानऽ viz इन्दु, पूषन्, चिन्तामणि and कुलपर्वतऽ superfluous ab Construe देव लाचण्यौकसि साप्रतापगरिमणि यतिना अग्रेसरे अवनीमरसमभुजे वयि वेधसा निष्पादिते लावण्यौकस् The sanctum or abode (ओक्स् N) of charm सप्रतापगरिमन् प्रतापगरिम्णा सह बतते असौ **अवनीभरक्षमभुत **Whose arms are capable of sustaining the burden of the earth i e responsibilities of the state इन्दु etc. The moon is उपमान for लावण्य the sun (पूषन्) for प्रताप the चिन्तामणि for त्याग, the कुल्पवैतऽ for भूभारधारण चिन्तारत्न Because it yields everything desired it is उपमान for generosity कुलक्ष्माभृत (कुलक्ष्माभृत् m.) कुलपर्व is seven in number महेन्द्र, मलय, सह्य,शक्तिमान्  


ऋक्षपर्वत, विध्य, पारियात्र कुल—कौ पृथिव्या लीना Lying on earth The mountains keep the earth steady, hence they are उपमान for अवनीभरक्षमत्व

 Here by किं घटित किं विहित etc. the utility of the creation of the उपमानऽ, ViZ इन्दु पूषन्, चिन्तारत्न and कुलपर्वतऽ is called into question because the king the उपमेय is there to play their role Therefore प्रतीप (१) Note that there is यथासख्य also रुय्यक gives it to illustrate यथासख्य and प्रतीप both.

 st 163 ए एहि etc Illustrates प्रतीप (२) i e उपमानस्य उपमेयत्वे उपमित्यनिष्पत्तौ The face of a lovely lady flattered as being superior to the moon Sanskrit rendering—ए एहि तावत् सुन्दरि वर्णं दत्त्वा शृणुष्य वचनीयम्। तव मुखेन कृशोदरि चद्र उपमीयते अनेन॥वचनीयम् Scandal disclosed in the 2nd half 2nd half —People compare the moon with your face.

 Here the चन्द्र the उपमान has not only been reduced to the status of उपमेय in तत्र मुखेन चन्द्र उपमीयते but further it is suggested that it Is scandalous (वचनीय ) for the मुख to have चन्द्र for its उपमेय। Owing to this suggestion the उपमा does not materialise (अनिष्पन्न) Hence उपमानस्य उपमेयत्वे उपमिति अनिष्पत्तौ प्रतीप Note that we have simply to drop the reference to scandal and the उपमा materialises This shows how this variety does not differ much from the one following.

 अत्र etc. Shows how the उपमा does not maternalise मुखेन उपमीयमानस्य शशिन i e मुख becomes उपमान, शशिन्, उपमेय This शशिन् is brought into contempt as suggested by the word वचनीय because this suggests that the comparison does not materialise as the शशिन् is too inferior, in the matter of excellence to enter into comparison with the मुख !

 st 164 गवमसवाह्य etc. Illustrates उपमानम्य उपमेयत्वे उपमिति निष्पत्तौ प्रतीप The eyes of a lovely lady complimented on their beauty लोचनयुगलेन i e on the score of the लोचनयुगल असवाह्य गर्वम् Unbearable vanity

 Here the लोचनयुगल, the उपमेय, usurps the place of the उपमान (in लोचनयुगलसदृशानि नलिनानि) and the नलिनऽ are reduced to the status of उपमेय The उपमा maternalises as is borne out by the word ईदृशानि सन्ति Hence उपमानस्य उपमेयत्वे, उपमिति-निष्पत्तौ प्रतीप Another but simpler instance would be त्वलोचनमम् पद्यं कुवलयानद takes this stanza to illustrate another variety admitted by it and र ग It consists in raising the उपमेय (now tumed into an उपमान) to the pinnacle by suggesting that it is too high to enter into औपम्य with anything whatsoever and then to show that it has a compeer in the उपमान (now tumed उपमेय ) Here लोचनयुगल

 is suggested as possessing the highest beauty by the words असनाह्य and गर्वे and then नील-नलिनऽ are made its compeer (द्वितीय) Thus there is a double compliment to the उपमेय firstly it is exalted as high as possible and secondly it is elevated to the position of उपमान So this may also illustrate अननुभूतौपम्यस्य उपमेयस्य (उपमानीकृतस्य ) औपम्यानुभवे प्रतीप र ग very aptly brings out its essence when he defines it as —उपमेयस्य किञ्चिद्गुणयुक्त अद्वितीयोत्कर्ष परिहर्तु द्वितीयप्रदर्शनेन उल्लास्यमान सादृश्य अपर ( प्रतीपम् ) Note that the उपमेय has necessarily to become उपमान to produce this effect

 अनयैव रीत्या As illustrated in गर्वमसवाह्य etc असामान्यगुणयोगात् न उपमानभावमपि अनुभूतपूर्वि which had not become so much as उपमान be fore owing to its गुणऽ being uncommon That is it stood too high to enter into comparison उपमान is high no doubt but does imply that there is someth-ing else viz उपमेय with which it enters into औपम्य तस्य तत्कल्पना। e what was too high even to become an उपमान is presented (कल्पना) as an उपमान अनुभूतपूर्वि Nom sing Neuter अनुभूत पूर्विन्–अनुभूतं पूर्वे अनेन

 st 165 अहमेव etc. Illustrates प्रतीप अननुभूतीपम्यस्य उपमानत्वे The हालाहल has at long last found its equal in दुर्जनवच तात O dear one (to be added in the Translation after poison )

 Here हालाहल is first described as being too high (अहमेव सुगुरुणानां गुरु) to enter into औपम्य with anything And then दुर्जनवचनानि are stated to be its equal This naturally makes दुर्जनवचनऽ attain that eminence to which हालाहल was lifted Hence अननुभूतौपम्यस्य उपमानत्वे प्रतीप

**५६—सामान्य**

 सामान्य is whatever is assimilated with another merged into an other in the figure we are presented with two (or more) facts that owing to their sim larity of attributes, merge one into the other and become indistinguishable While describing ........s says a poet .......................।अविभाज्या सुत्र यन्ति ............॥(सा द) Here the अविमारिकऽ with the white colour-scheme of their costume become indistinguishable from the moon। ght. This, there fore Is सामान्य Another poet describing the condition of a fugitive woman says— ........................॥ where the woman is described as being in distingushable owing to Iike a tributes, from the creepers in which she had taken shelter This is सामान्य again. It is the poets aim to

stress the गुणसाम्य between two things in this figure And to secure it, he indulges in a slight exaggeration and presents two things as absolutely non-different though really they are not so

 **सामान्य vs मीलित** Thus brings us to the distinction between the two figures In both, similarity of attributes leads to the nonapprehension of one out of two circumstances Thus while in मीलित, मदोदय was not apprehended because its symptoms were similar to those of यौवनलीला in सामान्य, the अभिसारिकाऽ went undetected because their colour was similar to the moonlight But whereas (।) in सामान्य, the non apprehension is due to assimilation, in if it is due to domination Because in , both the circumstances were equally powerful, while, in मीलित, one is more powerful than the other Read रुय्यक—न चाय सामान्यालङ्कार। तस्य हि साधारणगुणयोगाद्भेदानुलक्षण रूपम्। अस्य (मीलितस्य) तु उत्कृष्टगुणेन निकृष्टगुणस्य तिरोधानमिति महाननयो विशेष। (॥) In सामान्य, the distinction between two things becomes imperceptible In मीलित, one of the two things siself is obscured Read कुवलयानद—(केचित्तु) लक्षणसम्भ्यात केनचित् बलीयसा तदन्यस्य स्वरूपतिरोधाने मीलितम्। स्वरूपप्रतीतावपि गुणमाभ्याद् भेदतिरोधाने सामान्यम्। Agaun र गं मीलिते तु निगूह्यमानवस्तु न प्रत्यक्षविषय इति न तत्र अतिव्याप्ति

 The distinction is subtle enough and कुवलयानन्द makes its मीलित comprehend both and goes to the extent of giving as instance of मीलित श्लोक (मल्लिकामाल्य etc. ) wich is similar to what साहित्यदर्पण gives to illustrate सामान्य र ग refers to a view which looked upon मीलित, सामान्य and तद्गुणा as varieties of one figure. The सामान्य in कुवलयानन्द comprehends those cases where, due to सादृश्य, two things cannot be distinguished e g ......विाटानां मुख नालक्षि मुक्षुवान् Note that here no assimilation is involved as in the सामान्य of other आलङ्कारिकऽ.

 तद्गुण (first variety) of रुद्रटwas the forerunner of सामान्य of later आलङ्कारिकऽ अल्लट, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नथ, अप्यय्यदीक्षित all admitted the figure अप्यय्यदीक्षित conceived it on slightly different lines \[S-ee above \]

 Essentials \[सामान्यगुणनिबन्धनम्—काव्यप्रकाश ) (1) There must be present two for more) circu-mstances of identical attributes (2) Due to the attributes being similar, one circumstance must get assimilated with the other (3) One circumstance is perceived owing to its distinctive chara-cteristics being marked, not because it is stronger as in ...... The other arcumstance remains undistinguched owing to its distinctave characteristics remaining unnoticed (4) This means that when

both the objects or circumstances are under notice their distinctness goes unnoticed (5) Both the circumstances must be of equal force not one stronger as in मीलित (6) The poets aim is to drive at the गुणसाम्य between the two circumstances (7) The two things are not absolutely iden-tical in quality but are hyperbolically so described

 § सू ७८ प्रस्तुतस्य etc Construe यत् गुणसाम्यविवक्षया प्रस्तुतस्य अन्येन योगात् ऐकात्म्य बध्यते तत् सामान्यम् इति स्मृतम्। प्रस्तुतस्य अन्येन योगात् एकात्म्य बध्यते where the प्रस्तुत is presented as (बध्यते) assimilated with the other (viz अप्रस्तुत ) due to their coming together (योगात्) Is it proper to describe the two things as प्रस्तुत and अप्रस्तुत ? Perhaps not Because in the instance of the अभिसारिकाऽ the ज्योत्स्ना is as much प्रकृत as the अभिसारिकाऽ At least it is not अप्रकृत It is only विश्वनाथ besides our author who adopts this terminology of प्रस्तुत-अप्रस्तुत,प्रकृत-अप्रकृत in the matter of this figure So प्रस्तुत may apply to the अभिसारिकाऽ only in so far as they are the central figures of the descr-iption

 वृत्ति—अतादृशमपि तादृशतया विवक्षितुम् The two things are not really speaking as absolutely identical as the poet states them to be यत् अप्रस्तुतार्थेन सवृक्त=योगात् in the सूत्र explained Which when it comes into contact (सवृक्त) with the matter not under description तदेकात्मतया निबध्यते Is described as assimilated with the other or merged into the other समानगुणनिय धनात् सामान्यम् The name सामान्य accounted for It is सामान्य because based (निबन्धन) on similar attributes

 St 166 मलयजरस etc Illustrates सामान्य अभिसारिकाऽ camouflaged with their sartorial colour-scheme in white cannot be made out in the moon light and therefore reach safely their destinations viz. the प्रियगृह सिततरदन्तपत्रकृतवक्त्रस्व Ivory pendants (दतपत्र) that are extremely white lend grace to their faces विततधाम्नि शणभृति धरा धवलयति (Locative absolute) when the moo-n with lustre spread out whitens the earth सुखमेव too comfortably निरस्तभिय अभिसारिकाऽ had to evade the public eye. They were looked upon as wantons.

 Here the अभिसारिकाऽ are described as getting merged into (अविभाव्यता गत) the moon light both being equally white (though really speaking their whiteness is not absolutely the same ) Hence सामान्य अत्र etc. प्रस्तुत
-प्रबन्ध i. e. प्रस्तुत-अप्रस्तुत अन्यून- अनातेरिकतया निबद्ध. धवलार्थ the whitene-ss of each described (निबद्ध) as being ...... less nor more than that of the other एकात्मताहेतु is responsible for their assimilation. अत न पृथकभावेण उपलभ्यणं Hence they are not noticed as distinct

relation between them to account for one being immediately followed by the other

 When it is said that there is अतिशय in all the three varieties of विशेष, we are not very much near any precise idea of the figure For, अतिशयोक्ति is the life of practically all figures. And should we attempt to find out any more specific feature in the three divisions of विशेष we are sure to be disappointed In other words the three varieties of the figure present a motley assortment not governed by any common characteristic. जयरथ, for instance, says विशेषाश्च अत्र त्रय) न पुनरेकत्रिविध। i e he would look upon them as three distinct विशेषऽ, so different they are

 That way the varieties bear resemblance to the varieties of विषम which are equally heterogeneous And just as रुद्रट was responsible for the heterogeneous varieties of विषम so is he responsible for those of विशेष  

 The three varieties of the figure— (1) In the first variely a thing is described as existing even in the absence of its acknowledged आश्रय Sometimes it may be presented as existing without any आश्रय whatsoever On occasions however it may be presented as abiding in other than the usual abode Thus it is said of the fame of an eminent king that even when he is no more his fame persists - इदं तु चित्र भुवनावकाशे निराश्रया .... सस्य कीर्ति (र ग ) This illustrates आश्रयराहित्य आश्रयान्तरगतत्व is illustrated by–गतेऽपि सूर्ये दीपरथास्तमरिछ... तत्वरा। (कु) said by one in praise of the brilliance of a lamp. Even when the sun has set, its rays coming to abide in the lamp dispel darkness. Here the rays are described as remaining in an abode different from their usual one. This variety could very well come under (असम्बन्धे सम्बन्ध) अतिशयोक्ति।

 (ii) in the second variety one and the same thing is described as abiding in the same capacity in different abodes at one and the same time Says a lover अन्तर्बहि पुर पश्चात् सर्वस्थिति सैव मे (पुः) She is to me everywhere inside, outside in front behind in all quarters Here the same नायिका is presented as being present simultaneously in different places. This also will come under (असम्बध सम्बध) अतिशयोक्ति।

 विशेष (२) vs पर्याय (१)—In both, one thing abides in different places. But while in विरोध it abides there simultaneously, in पर्याप it does so in turns (क्रम), one after another Thus .... and ..... strike us in विशेष while क्रम and ........ strike us in .....

 Read र ग—अत्र आद्यलक्षणे प्रागुक्तविशेषालङ्कारद्वितीयभेद अतिप्रसगवारणाय क्रमेणेति। तत्र ( विशेष ) आधयस्य युगपदनेकाधारसद्वधात् नातिप्रसङ्ग।

 विशेष (२) vs यथासख्य—In यथासख्य like एकस्त्रिधा वससि etc. we find one “आधेय” abiding simult-aneously in द्विष विदुष, मृगीदृश Just as in विशेष, we find one आधेय simultaneously abiding in diff-erent abodes. But whereas in विशेष the आधेय abides in the same capacity ( एकात्मा ) in the different abodes in यथासख्य it does so in different capacities (cp त्रिधा वससि)

 (iii) In the third variely the poet describes someone doing onething and in doing it securing the accomplishment of something else which is impossible (or also possible acc to सा द ) The lovely lady when she sketches the decorative designs on her cheeks sketches her image on the mind of her lover, an impossible feat—लिखित बालमृगाक्ष्या मम मनसि तया शरीरमात्मीयम्। स्फुटमात्मनो लिखन्त्या तिलक विमले कपोलतले। (रुद्रट ९१० ) This means that her decoration has made the lover love her so fervently This is विशेष (३) Says a poet to his patron–त्वा पश्यता मया लब्ध कल्पवृक्षनिरीक्षणम् ( कु.)— In my act of seeing you I have had the privilege of seeing the कल्पवृक्ष itself an impossible thing Here the poet is represented as achieving कल्पवृक्षनिरीक्षण while coming across the king This means that the sight of the king will prove as fruitful to the poet as that of the कल्पवृक्ष This is विशेष (३) अज, in his lamentations over the death of इन्दुमती, says — O Death when you deprived me of her, you deprived me of my wife adviser, a friend a lovable pupil in fine arts—गृहिणी सचिव सखी मिश्र प्रियशिष्य ललिते कलाविधौ। करुणाविमुखेन मृत्युना हरता त्वा बत किं न मे हृतम्॥Here by taking away इन्दुमती death has taken away अत्रऽ everything an impossible matter This is therefore विशेष (३) \[ See below \] This also will corne under (असम्बधे सबन्ध) अतिशयोक्ति

 The relation between the thing done and the other thing achieved in विशेष (३) जगन्नाथ nghtly says that the thing done must be कारण in relation to the other thang achieved which is its कार्य Thus to take the first instance कपोलतिलकलेख resulis in मनसि स्वशरीरलेख Similar is (किं नाम तेन न कृत मुक्त पुरारे येनार्चिताऽसि कलणाकर हेलयापि॥(र ग ) If on the other hand the two were merely सदृश the figure will not be विशेष but अभव सवधा वाक्यार्थनिदर्शना Because in a निदर्शना like व्योमनि बीजा कुरुते—यस्तु खले चरति सकारम् the idea is of सादृश्य VIz that खलसत्कार is like व्योमबीजावाप

 Thus while विशेष (2) is based on कार्य-कारणभाव, निदर्शना is based on सादृश्य That even रुद्रट must have conceived विशेष (2) as not concerned with सादृश्य is clear from the fact that वि-शेष is taken by him as an अलङ्कार based on अतिशय not औपम्य आलङ्कारिकऽ like अप्पय्यदीक्षित overlook this point of distinction and therefore the instances they have given to illustrate विशेष prove to be those of निदर्शना \[See below\] Thus त्वा पश्यता etc. is really speaking,निदर्शना because it drives at औपम्य ( तव दर्शन कल्पवृक्षदर्शनमिव \]

 **विशेष (३)vs निदर्शना** (१)—In both two facts are put side by side, in two syntactically related sentences But while in विशेष the two facts are related as कार्य-कारण in निदर्शना, they are unrelated and hence suggest सादृश्य

 अशक्यवस्त्वन्तरकरण in विशेष (३)— If the वस्त्वन्तर is अशक्य, how is it that it is said to be the कार्य of the thing which is actually done? In the answer to this question exactly lies the significance of the figure When it is stated that the कार्य, though अशक्य, comes to be achieved on doing a particular thing it naturally results in the exaltation of the thing actually done Because, it means that the particular thing is so eminent that by doing it even the impossible is achieved Not that the impossible thing is actually accomplished, but it is suggested that by doing the particular thing in question the impossible thing is as good as realised This involves अतिशयोक्ति and is the source of the charm of the figure This further shows that the other thing must be अशक्य as a matter of poetic necessity The view of विश्वनाथ which also includes शक्यवस्तु within the scope of the figure is therefore ruled out

We get the following scheme----

**विशेष**

.

.

अनाधारे आधारान्तरे .... तथैव युगपत् पर कार्यं कुर्वण  
वा आधेयस्य स्थिति अनेकत्र स्थिति       
अशक्यस्य कार्यान्तरस्य करणं

 विशेष was first recognised by रुद्रट who also gives the three subdivisions adopted by later अलङ्कारिकऽ अल्लट admitted it and so did रूय्यक विश्वानाथ जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित

 Essentials—\[विशेष= अतिशय\] (i) In the first variety, the आधेय must be presented poetically as abiding without any abode, or in an abode other than the usual one (ii) In the second variety, one

thing must abide poetically, in the same form, at the same time, in different abodes ( iii ) In the third variety, while doing one thing, another thing must be achieved along with it (iv) The other thing must be impossible and its achievement must be not actual but poetically conceived, in order to exalt the thing done. If it is not impossible, there is no अतिशयोक्ति and no charm But some आलङ्कारिकऽ include शक्य वस्त्वन्तर also, under the figure (v) The thing done and the other poetically claimed as accomplished must be related as कारण and कार्य respectively (vi) There must not be सादृश between them (vii) The thing done gets exalted when related to the impossible effect (viii) अतिशयोक्ति is present in all the three varieties  


 § सू ७९ विना प्रसिद्धं etc Construe —प्रसिद्ध आधार विना आधेयस्य व्यवस्थिति (१), एकस्य एकात्मा अनेक-गोचरा युगपद् वृत्ति ( २ ), अन्यत् कार्यं प्रकुर्वत अशक्यस्य अन्यवस्तुन तथैव करणम् (३) च, —इति त्रिविध विशेष स्मृत। प्रसिद्ध आधार विना In the absence of the recognised abode This may result in either absen-ce of any abode whatsoever, or presence of the आधेय in some other abode एकात्मा अनेकगोचरा युगपर् वृत्ति–The simultaneous existence of a thing in the same form ( एकात्मा ), in different abodes ( अनेकगोचरा ) एकात्मा (Fem Adj वृत्ति ) excludes यथासख्य \[ See above \] युगपद् excludes पर्याय (१) \[ See above \] अशक्यस्य अन्यवस्तुन तथैव करणम् The accomplishment of some other impossible thing by the same stroke (तथैव तेनैव प्रयनेन ) We saw how the other thing must be impossible, then only will it exalt the thing done also includes शक्यस्य The other thing may be also शक्य to it. A शक्यवस्त्वन्तर lacking अतिशयोक्ति cannot be so poetical as अशक्यवस्त्वन्तर जयरथ says that the वस्त्वन्तर may be चिकर्षित or अचिकीर्षित, though अशक्य जयरथ illustrates अशक्य but चिकीर्षित वस्त्वन्तर by —माघ शिशुपालवध विदधत् कविमदवध विदधे। रत्नाकर स्वविजय हरविनय वर्णयन् व्यवृणोत्॥

 वृत्ति—विशिष्टा स्थिति Hints at the propriety of the name विशेष. विशिष्ट्य=अतिशययुक्त

 st 168 दिवमपि etc Illustrates विशेष (१) Homage to the poets whose words survive them Cons-true—दिव उपयाताना अपि यिषा अनल्पगुणगणा गिर आकल्य जगन्ति रमयन्ति ते कवय इह कथ न वन्द्या। आकल्प Till the time of the destruction of the Universe

 Here the गिर continue to exast even when their प्रसिद्ध आधार viz the poets are dead गिर exist without any abode whatsoever, after the poets are no more. Hence विशेष (१), आधारराहित्ये

 एकमपि वस्तु अपि shows the paradox involved in one thing coming to stay in various abodes at the same time. एकेनैव स्वभावेन एकात्मा of the सूत्र explained In the same capacity

 St 169 सा वसइ etc Illustrates विशेष (२) The rival to some lady complains to the lady's lover that he is too preoccupied with the lady to pay any attention to herself! Sanskrit rendering :—सा वसति तव हृदये सैव अक्षिषु सा च वचनेषु। अस्मादृशीना सुन्दर अवकाश कुत्र पापानाम्॥

 Here the same lady is described as simultaneously abiding in the heart, the eyes and words of the lover She does so in the same form or capacity Hence विशेष (२)

 यदपि etc. Introduces the third variety रमसेन आरभमाण Doing a thing vehemently This only shows that the act described as being done is conspicuous enough to be the basis of अतिशयोक्ति भरभमाण Perhaps possesses the तार्किक meaning Producang (cp आरम = उत्पत्ति, तन्तव पट आरभन्ते) तेनैव यत्नेन Explains तथैव of the सूत्र By the same stroke of action अशक्यमपि कार्यान्तर आरभते Achieves (आरभ=उत्पादय) another result though it is impossible अशक्य e g मनसि आत्मीयशरीरलेखनम् In लिखित कालमृगाक्ष्या etc Practically every आलङ्कारिक insists on this requirement, because therein lies अतिशयोक्ति सा द wants to include शक्य वस्त्वन्तर also But this being void of अतिशयोक्ति cannot be as charming as the अशक्यवस्त्वन्तर And since अतिशयोक्ति is the keynote of विशेष, it is easy to see how शक्य वस्वन्तर is not in tune with it.

 st 170 स्फुरदद्भुत etc. Illustrates विशेष, वस्त्वन्तरकरणे अशक्यवस्त्वन्तरस्य सपादन A tribute to the beauty, prowess and learning of a king, Construe—स्फुरदद्भुनरूप उत्प्रतापज्वलन अनवद्यविद्य च त्वां सृजता सत्य विधिना नवो मनोभू (नव ) सविता (नव) वृहस्पतिश्च भुवि ससृजे। नव New Different from their counterparts in heaven मनोभू in respect of रूप, सविता in respect of prowess बृहस्पति in respect of learming सत्य Indeed (Adv )

 Here in the act of creating the king the Creator is described as having achieved the creation of नवमदन, नवमविता\] and नववृहस्पति, which as an अशक्य वस्त्वन्तर To create a new मदन etc. is अशक्य note that there is यथासङ्ख्य-रूप, प्रताप and विद्या being in the same order connected with मनोभू सविता and बृहस्पति

 But is नृपसृष्टि the cause of ( नव) मदनादिसृष्टि or सदृश to it? In other words, does it mean नृपसृष्टया नवमदनादय सृष्टा or नृपसृष्टिः नवमदनादि सृष्टिसदृशी। Plainly enough, it means the latter So the sta-rza should be properly speaking an instance of निदर्शना, not विशेष

 What is true of this instance is also true of some instances given by other आलङ्कारिकऽ, as we saw in the case of त्वा पश्यता etc. And this confusion was bound to anse due to the anomalous position of विशेष (3) What is meant by anomalous position will be clear from the following

 विशेष in all its varieties was रुद्रट’s innovation Earlier आलङ्कारिकऽ had no विशेष and the काव्यप्रकाश borrowed it directly from रुद्रट But while borrowing, the काव्यप्रकाश overlooked a very important fact and it was that it had already borrowed from उद्भट the figure निदर्शना (उद्भट’s निदर्शना) which was in form identical with रुद्रट’s विशेष (३), though in substance, निदर्शना is based on सादृश्य, while रुद्रट’s विशेष is based on अतिशय (which रुद्रट conceived as distinct from सादृश्य) रुद्रट had no निदर्शना whatsoever, and therefore he had no fear of his विशेष (3) overlapping निदर्शना

 So, after the का प्र had borrowed विशेष (3) from रुद्रट and निदर्शना from उद्भट, it should have made them as exclusive of each other as possible, by drawing a line of demarcation between the two, as only जगन्नाथ did later with some success Its definition of विशेष (3) ought to have be-en categorical in the matter of exclusion of सादृश्य from the field of विशेष (३) This was not done and hence the ambiguity

 But even supposing such an attempt at demarcation were made, it is not certain that it would have cleared all ambiguity Because कार्यकारणभाव when based on अतिशयोक्ति often shades off into सादृश्य Is there any sharp difference between नृपसृष्टया नवमदनादय सृष्टा and नृपसृष्टि नवमदनादिष्टिसदृशी? Actually कार्यकारणभाव may be distinct from सादृश्य तन्तु पटसवध is quite distinct from मुखकमलसवध But when the वार्यकारणभाव is अतिशयोक्तिमूल it is not so easy to mark off one from the other Hence better would it have been if the का प्र had admitted only one, निदर्शना widering its scope to comprehend कार्यकारणमाव, or विशेष (३) made broad enough to include सादृश्य It is typical of the influence of the का प्र that once it had admitted विशेष, though in excess of निदर्शना, no later आलङ्कारिक thought of improving upon it

 St 171 गृहिणी etc. Illustrates the same i e विशेष, वस्त्वन्तरकरणे अशक्यवस्त्वन्तरस्य सपादने अज’s la-ments over the death of us wife इन्दुमती मिथ सखी a friend to whom he confided all matters howsoever secret.

 Here death, in robbing अज of इन्दुमती is described as having robbed him of all Thus इन्दुमतीहरण is one क्रिया, in doing it मृत्यु is

described as having done the other क्रिया of सर्वस्वहरण सर्वस्वहरण IS अशक्य, at least to the extent that it involves अतिशयोक्ति And it could be said that इन्दुमनीहरण has resulted in सर्वस्वहरण So कार्य-कारणभाव Hence विशेष (३), वस्त्वन्तरकरणे अशक्यवस्त्वन्तरस्य सपादन

 सर्वत्र etc The वृत्ति refers to विशेष beng based on अतिशयोक्ति which underlies a good many अलङ्कारऽ As we saw, विशेष itself means अतिशय एवंविघविषये refers with greater probability, to विशेष in general than only to its third varity अतिशयोक्ति Exaggeration not the figure अतिशयोक्ति though we have seen how all the three varieties could even come under the figure अतिशयोक्ति (असबधे सबध) ता विना अतिशयोक्तिं विना प्रायेण अलङ्कारवायागात् It is not possible, generally speaking to have a figure ( of speech) without it This makes it clear that अतिशयोक्ति is used here in the general sense of exaggeration and not अतिशयोक्ति अलङ्कार What is suggested is that an अलङ्कार generally involves a hyperbole and it is this that makes it charming प्रायेण In स्वभावोक्ति we have no अतिशयोक्ति Even, on occasions भाविक काव्यलिङ्ग, स्मरण, भ्रान्तमान् or तद्गुण may have no अतिशयोक्ति in them.

 अत एव उक्तं सैषा सर्वत्र etc. भामहऽ tribute to अतिशयोक्ति cited सैषा etc. The stanza will be better understood when read with the preceding one which is इत्येवमादिरुदिता गुणातिशययोगन। सर्वैवातिश-योक्तिस्तु तर्कयेत् तां यथागमम्॥सैषा सर्वत्र वक्रोक्ति etc. It is clear from this that वक्रोक्ति is the same as अतिशयोक्ति referred to in the preceding श्लेष वक्रोक्ति Lit speech with a twist i e ornate speech अतिशयोक्ति वक्रिमा is the same as अतिशय says सा चू सैषा सर्वत्र वक्रोक्ति. This ornate expression or अतिशयोक्ति is present in all figures (generally) अनया अर्थ विभाव्यते a ( plain) fact is made to look charming (विभाव्यते) by it What are all figures? They are simple facts overstated or presented in exaggerated colours विभाव्यते विशिष्टो भाव्यते—प्रदीप, विचित्रतया भाव्यते—सा चू Is rendered striking यत्नोऽस्या कविना कार्य The poet should make an effort to achieve अतिशयोक्ति कोऽलङ्कारोऽनया विना Bears out ता विना प्रायेण अलङ्कारत्वायोगात् of the वृत्ति It is interesting to see how दण्डिन् also lavishes unstinted prause on अतिशयोक्ति— अलङ्कराणामप्येकमाहु परायणम्। बागीशमहितमुत्तिमिमान.......॥(का द २. २२०) Read जयरथ— इह हि अतिशयस्थ द्वयौ गति .................... सामान्यात्मा भवति भेदेऽप्यनेद इत्येवनादि रूप विशेषात्मा। तत्राद्य सर्वैरव ......... अभ्युपगतः।

५८-तद्गुण

 We saw in मौलिन how a thing was obscured by another, we saw in सामान्य, how a thing was assimilated with another, and now

in तद्गुण we shall see how quality of a thing is suppressed and obscured by the more powerful quality of another thing. If in मीलित and सामान्य, the charm lay in a धर्मिन् being dominated or assimilated by another, in तद्गुण, the charm lies in the धर्म of one thing being dominated and obscured by the stronger of another thing. And it is easy to see how in such cases, at least in some of them, the poet's aim will be to bring into limelight the धर्मिन् credited with the powerful धर्म. Occasionally, however, the poet's aim may be simpler, to wit, to draw attention to the kaleidoscopic changes in the quality of a thing wrought under the influence of some other contiguous thing.

 Thus a poet who wants to lavish praise on the golden complexion of a नायिका refers to the change wrought in the white colour of roses that appear yellow as they come in contact with her goldencoloured neck ! —कुब्जकमालापि कृता कार्तस्वरभास्वरे त्वया कण्ठे। एतत्प्रमानुलिप्ता चम्पकदा-मम्रमं कुरुते॥( रुद्रट ९.२५ ). Another poet flatters the बिम्बाधर of the नायिका saying :—पद्मरागायते नासामौक्तिकं तेऽधरत्विषा। ( कु. )—How the pearl in the nose has turned into a ruby thanks to the (ruddy) lustre of your lower lip ! These are cases of तद्गुण अलङ्कार.

 **तद्गुण vs. मीलित :**—In both, one contiguous thing influences another. In both, the stronger obscures the weaker. But whereas (i) in तद्रुण, one धर्म obscures another and takes its place, in मीलित one धर्मिन् obscures another and becomes conspicuous. Read रुय्यक :—न चेदं मीलितम्। तत्र हि प्रकृतं वस्तु वस्त्वन्तरेण आच्छादितत्वेन प्रतीयते, इह तु अनपह्नुनरवरूपमेव प्रकृतं वस्त्वन्तरगुणोपरक्ततया प्रतीयते इति अनयोर्भेदः। Again र. गं. :—तद्गुणे वस्स्वन्तरगुणाना भिन्नत्वेन अग्रहणे अपि वस्त्वन्तरस्य ग्रह-णमस्ति एव इति न तत्र प्रसङ्गः। (ii) In तद्रुण the quality obscuring another must be dissimilar to it. Otherwise there cannot be any questions of replacement. In मीलित, the धर्मऽ of both the धर्मिन्ऽ must be similar Cp. रुद्रटः असमानगुणं यस्मिन्नतिवहलगुणेन वस्तुना वस्तु। संसृष्टं तद्गुणतां धत्ते...........॥Again तर्कवागीशः—मीलिते स्वजातीयोत्कृष्टगुणनिरोधानम्। अत्र तु विजातीयोत्कृष्टगुणेनापकृष्टगुणतिरोधानम्।

 **तद्गुण vs. सामान्य :**—In both there is non-perception of a fact as it is. But while (i) in तद्गुण, the nonperception relates to the गुण of an object which is ousted by the stronger गुण of another object, in सामान्य, the non-perception relates to the thing itself which is assimilated with another. (ii) In तद्गुण, the गुणऽ of the two contiguous things are dissimilar. In सामान्य, they are similar (iii) In

तद्गुण the गुण of one is stronger than that of another In सामान्य, गुणऽ of the two contiguous things are of the same intensity

 तद्गुण dates back to रुद्रट who recognised two varieties of it, the first of which became the सामान्य of other आलङ्कारिकऽ, while the second has come down as the तद्गुण of later is, beginning with अल्लट It is once more a testimony to the influence of the का प्र that what ever innovations it initiated were accepted without demur by his successors And we find रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ and अप्पय्यदीक्षित all ad mitting as तद्गुण the second variety of रुद्रट's तद्गुण

 ** Essentials** —\[ तस्य अप्रकृतस्य (? प्रकृतस्य वा ) गुण अत्र अस्ति —**का प्र** \] (1) Two things must be contiguous enough to influence each other (2) They must possess is that are dissimilar (3) The गुणऽ may be colour or such other qualities Read **कुवलयानन्द** —तद्गुण अतद्गुणयो पुन गुणशब्द रूपरसग धादिगुणवाची। (4) The of one must be stronger than that of the other which it obscures (5) The thing with the stronger quality may be प्रकृत or अप्रकृत (6) When it is प्रकृत the poet's aim is evidently to exalt it When it is not प्रकृत, the poet is aim is only to note the striking kaleidoscopic changes (7) There is also charm due to the विराध implicit in a धर्मिन् losing, though temporarily, its धर्म (8) The terms प्रकृत and अप्रकृत respectively indicate the central theme and what is contiguous thereto Because, strictly speaking both the things are प्रकृत as they enter into the picture (9) The बलीयान् गुण is that which obscures the other (10) The thing has its quality obscured only so long as the other thing is by its side

 **§ सू ८० स्वमुत्सृज्य** etc Construe —अत्युज्ज्वलगुणस्य योगाद् यद् वस्तु स्व गुण उत्सृज्य तद्गुणतामेति स तु तद्गुण इति मण्यते। **अत्युज्ज्वलगुणस्य योगात्** Owing to its contact with another thing of very brilliant attributes अत्युज्ज्वलत्व suggests that the गुण outshines the other स्व गुण उत्सृज्य Note that the स्वगुणत्याग is not permanent but temporary lasting only so long as the other thing is beside it तु marks transition to the new figure Note that the सूत्र is significantly vague on the point of प्रकृतत्व or अप्रकृतत्व of the two वस्तुऽ. As we saw, the thing with stronger quality may be either प्रकृत or अप्रकृत the two terms being used rather freely, as said above The वृत्ति however unjustifiably specifies the thing with stronger quality as अप्रकृत  


 ** वृत्ति—तिरस्कृतनिजरूप** Note that by interpreting गुण in the सूत्र as रूप the वृत्ति unnecessarily limits the scope of the figure to the one

गुण viz. रूप. See agam how वर्ण quiis alone referred to in the वृत्ति to अतद्गुण As we saw (Ess-ential 3), कुवलयानन्द rightly interprets गुण as not confined to रूप only But the उदाहरणऽ of the आलङ्कारिकऽ we find confined to रूप only **प्रगुणया स्वगुणसपदा उपरक्त** Tinged or influenced by the powerful (प्रगुण=उत्कृष्ट) quality possessed copiously by it **स्वगुणसपत्** cp उदात्त वस्तुन सपत् सपत् superabundance **तत्प्रतिमास समासादयति** Assumes the semblance or likeness of the other thing त**स्य अप्रकृतस्य गुण अत्र अस्ति**—अत्र = हीनगुणे प्रकृते Note that this portion of the वृत्ति makes the उत्कृष्टगुण as अप्रकृत, हीनगुण as प्रकृत But this is unwarranted In कुब्जकमालापि etc. and पद्मरागायते etc, the उत्कृष्टगुण वस्तुऽ VIZ कण्ठ, अधर are properly speaking the प्रकृत Again to talk in terms of प्रकृत-अप्रकृत in the matter of such figures is not very accurate, though it may be convenient, as we saw already

 **St 172 विभिन्न** etc Illustrates तद्गुण रैवतक a mountain described as possessing green emer-alds so abundantly that the colour of the sun's horses underwent a change as they passed over it Construe—यत्र (रैवतके ) गरुडाग्रजेन ( = अरुणेन ) परित सुरन्त्या रुचा विभिन्नवर्णा सूर्यस्य रथ्या वशक-रीनीलै रत्नै परित स्फुरन्त्या रुचा स्वा रुच पुन आनिन्यिरे। The sun's horses are green They were rendere-d red in the first instance by the ruddy hue of अरुण the sun's charioteer But they were again restored to their original green hue by the emeralds on the रैवतक गरुडाग्रज = अरुण who is proverbially red परित स्फुरन्स्या रुचा indicates the रुच् as being powerful fafted with colour changed यत्र= रेवतके **सूर्यस्य रथ्या** the horses of the Sun seven in number and of green colour वशकरीनीलै Green (नील) the the shoots of bamboos करीर (m & n) shoot Perhaps the poet even wants to suggest that the रत्नऽ appeared green due to the lustre of वशकरीरऽ' This appears unlikely though looking to the word हरि मणीना of the वृत्ति The reference seems to be to emeral-ds in particular A vein of उदात्त  

 Here the colour of the horses is first obscured and replaced by the colour of अरुण This is one तद्गुण When they are again given the green tinge by the green shade of the jewels there is another तद्गुण And, if the jewels are not emeralds but any jewels made green by the colour of the bamboo shoots we have one more तद्गुण The stanza will be पूर्वरूप acc to कुवलयानन्द Where a thing losing its quality for sometime again regains it we get पूर्वरूप acc to it र गं refers to a view which admits a figure called गर्वरूप embracing cases where a thing assumes one quality after another

 अत्र etc अरुणऽ (गरुडाग्रज) colour is here described as stronger (प्रगुण) than that of the horses, and the वर्ण of the green jewels as stronger than that of अरुण How else could the horses that appeared red become green once more? Note that here the प्रकृत if at all we were to think in those terms will be the रत्नऽ Because the subject of description being the रैवतक, the poet wants to pay a compliment to the jewels thereon This shows how the वृत्ति तस्य अप्रकृतस्य गुण अस्ति is too narrow to cover all cases because here रत्नऽ are not अप्रकृत

**५९ अतद्गुण**

 अतद्गुण was an innovation first introduced by the **काव्यप्रकाश** as a sequel to तद्गुण As the name indicates the figure consists in the failure of an object to get tinged by the stronger quality of another contiguous object Thus the figure consists in the violation of exactly that principle which underlies तद्गुण viz that the stronger गुण of an object must obscure and replace the weaker गुण of another contiguous object When a नायिका says to the lover चिर रागिणि मच्चित्ते निहितोऽपि न रजसि (कु ) we get an अतद्गुण because the नायक even when placed for long in the रक्त हृदय does not become रक्त

 **अतद्गुण vs विशेषोक्ति —**Involving as it does the violation of causation viz the non realization of an expected result अतद्गुण bears very close resemblance to विशेषोक्ति where also we find the effect not materialising even when the causes are there So close is the resemblance that it would not be rash to suggest that in initiating this figure the काव्यप्रकाश overlooked how its scope was already covered by विशेषोक्ति र ग in fact refers to a school that looked upon अतद्गुण as a subdivision of विशेषोक्ति—सति गुणग्रहणहेतौ उत्कृष्टगुणवस्तुसनिधाने तद्गुणग्रहणरूपकार्यामावामक अयमतद्गुणो विशेषोक्तेरवान्तरभेद, न त्वलकारान्तरं जयरथ mentions and supports a similar view point Read—वतुतस्तु समवत्येव (अतद्गुणे ) कार्यकारणभाव। अत एव अलङ्कारसारकृता विशेषोक्त्यन्तमान एव उक्त। रुय्यक says that in अतद्गुण we have no कार्यकारणभाव as in विषम But, as जयरथ points out that there is कार्यकारणभाव

 Now that it is recognised as an independent figure (i) अतद्गुण covers those cases where a गुण does not tinge another in spite of favourable circumstances विशेषोक्ति on the other hand will cover such cases of arred कारणसत्त्वे कार्याभाव as are not covered by अतद्गुण Read सा द —अत्र च गुणाग्रहणरूपविच्छित्तिविशेषाश्रयात् विशेषोक्तैर्भेद। (ii) There is a greater element of अतिशयोक्ति in अतद्गुण than in विशेषोक्ति (See below)

When one गुण, that should have otherwise dislodged another, fails to do so, we naturally feel surprised This tends to draw our attention to the circumstance that comes in the way, as in विशेषोक्ति Thus in the instance चिर रागिणि etc, we ask ourselves what is it that comes in the way of the नायक becoming रक्त? And when we realise that it is उदासीनता towards the नायिका, we perceive the charm and significance of the violation of causation

 In अतद्गुण, moreover, there is a greater element of अतिशयोक्ति than in विशेषोक्ति In विशेषोक्ति, the causes stated such as निद्रानिवृत्ति etc. (St 83) are वास्तविक causes In अतद्गुण , on the other hand, they are अध्यवसित Thus in चिर रागिणि etc, रमणध्यान is अध्यवसित as रमणस्य चित्ते निधान—Contemplation of the lover is अध्यवसित an as placing of the lover in the passionate heart. And this makes the अतद्गुण possible How otherwise could we expect the नायक to be रक्त if he is merely meditated upon by the नायिका? But when we think in terms of the नायक being placed in the रक्त heart, we do expect him to be रक्त because we know that a thing contracts the colour in which it is dipped' This अध्यवसाय of course leaves scope for श्लेष  

 अतद्गुण was first admitted by the का प्र obviously as a sequel to तद्गुण admitted by रुद्रट After का प्र रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ जगन्नाथ, अप्पय्यदीपिन all admitted the figure

 Essentials—\[ न विद्यते तस्य (प्रकृतस्य अप्रकृतस्य वा) गुण अत्र \] ( i ) There must be two objects one of which possesses a गुण in a potent form, whose contiguity is poetically conceived like रमण and हृदय in the instance given above (ii) The contiguity, though poetical of one possessing the particular quality in a potent form must make us expect its reaction on the other object (iii) Though expected, this reaction must not show itself (iv) This non realisation of the expected result is charming because it is अतिशयोक्तिमूल and draws our attention to the arcumstance that comes in the way (iv) The thing possessing the quality in a potent form may be प्रकृत or अप्रकृत (v) It is unnecessary for the poet to state the qualities of both, as How in तद्गुण But it is presumed that the qualities are dissimilar else could there be any scope for one contracting the quality of the other?

 § सूत्र ८१ तद्रूपाननुहार etc. Construe –अस्य तद्रूपाननुहार चेत तत् अतद्गुण स्यात्। अस्य तद्रूपाननुहार If that thing does not contract the गुण of the other अस्य refers to वस्तु only of the preceding कारिका It does not

stand either for प्रस्तुत or अप्रस्तुत तद्रूपाननुहार तत् refers to अत्युज्ज्वलगुण वस्तु of the preceding कारिका अननुहार. Non-contraction non assumption ( अनुहार = अनुहरण receiving from another ) Note that रूप unnecessarily restricts the scope of the figure as we saw under तद्गुण
