 वृत्ति—तदीय वर्ण The colour of the object of potent quality. रूप=वर्ण must be taken as indicating गुण cp How in the instance from र ग there is no रूप—कुचाभ्यामालीढ सहजकठिनाभ्यामपि रमे। न काठिन्य धत्ते तब हृत्यमत्यन्तमृदुलम्॥सभवन्त्यामपि योग्यताया Even when this was to be properly expected (in view of the गुण being प्रकृष्ट in the other object) इदं न्यूनगुण Note that the poet will never say that the thing is न्यूनगुण Whatever does not contract the quality which it ought to is न्यूनगुण

 St 173 धवलोऽसि जह etc. Illustrates अतद्गुण in the 2nd half A नायिका makes a grnevance that the नायक does not respond to her love for him Sanskrit rendering—धवलोऽसि यद्यपि सुन्दर तथापि त्वया मम रज्जित हृदयम्। रागभरितेऽपि हृदये सुभग निहितो न रक्तोऽसि॥रागभरित Filled with राम भरित brings to us the notion of an object filled with राम and shows how there was scope for नायक to be रक्त

 Here in the second half the नायक is stated as having been placed in the रागभरित हृदय and still not getting रक्त Hence अतद्गुण. Here हृदय can be described as प्रकृत because the नायिका wants to describe it to the नायक and the नायक as अप्रकृत So here we get अप्रकृतेन प्रकृतस्य गुणाननुहार In the first half we have विषम, acc to मा च—पूर्वाधे धबलस्य विरुद्धरागजनकत्वेन विषमालङ्कारोऽपि। सा चू says it us विरोध But मा चं 's assessment seems better

 किं च तदिति etc The सूत्र made applicable to those cases also where the अप्रकृत is अधिकगुण प्रकृत हीनगुण तद्रूपाननुहार thus would mean अप्रकृतरूपाननुहार अस्य=प्रकृतस्य That is the न्यूनगुण प्रकृत does not assume the quahty of the अधिकगुण अप्रकृत \[ In St 173, न्यूनगुण was अप्रकृत—नायक, अधिकगुण प्रकृत-हृदय \] कुतोऽपि निमित्तात् —As we saw thus violation of कार्य-कारणभाव is based on अतिशयोक्ति इत्यपि अपि Thus also us अतद्गुण in addition to the one llustrated in St 173

 St. 174 गामवतु etc Illustrates अतद्गुण where the हीनगुण is प्रकृत, अधिकगुण अप्रकृत The swans hue remains unaffected whether he dives in the white water of the Ganges or the black water of the Jumna Construe गाङ्ग अम्बु सितम्, यामुन अम्बु कञ्जलामम्। राजहंस उभयत्र मञ्जत

तव शुभ्रता सैव (सा)। न चीयते, न अपचीयते च। चीयते is heightened अपचीयते is lessened गाग = of the Ganges

 Here even when the waters are so white and so black, राजहंस’s hue rernains unaffected राजहंस, the हीनगुण is प्रकृत, भाग अबु and यामुन अबु, the अधिकगुण, are अप्रकृत. Hence अतद्गुण, प्रकृतस्य हीनगुणत्वे, अप्रकृतस्य च अधिकगुणत्वे Note that there is अप्रस्तुतप्रंशसा (अन्योक्ति) also in the Stanza

**६०-व्याघात**

 व्याघात is a super stroke and the figure is occasioned when an antagonist deals a super-stroke to his opponent and thereby establishes his supremacy over him. The super stroke imagined by the poet is ingenious enough. It consists in the adversary undoing a thing done by his opponent, and then too undoing it by the same means or instrument as was employed by his opponent for doing it. It is a double trumph Firstly, the adversary undoes of a thing in the hand of his rival, he turns into an instrument for frustration The wicked injure the poor with words, but with words do the good make the poor happy–दीनद्रुमान् वचौभि खलनिकरैरनुदिन दलितान्। पल्लवयन्त्युलसिता नित्य तैरेव सज्जनधुरीणा॥(र ग ) this is व्याघात where a thing is actually done (निष्पादित) and undone

 Sometimes the व्याघात may present itself in another form. The thing may not have been done, but is sought to be done (निष्पिपादयियित) by a particular means, by someone. And since it is not done, there cannot be any actual undoing of it, so here it is only sought to be undone by the same means by the rival. ‘How feeble you are. my darling, to undertake a journey with me’? said the lover to his beloved, who scores him off as she aptly retorts —‘That is why I insist upon accompanying you, my dear’ Forsooth I am too fceble to stand the pangs of separation!” इहैव त्व निष्ट... मृदुरसि न चायाससहना। मृदुत्वं मे हेतु सुभग भवता गन्तुमधिक न मृदी सोम यद्विरहकृतमायाममसमम्॥ (सा द) Here feebleness that was urged by the नायक as a cause for गमननिवृत्ति —only sought to be achieved—comes to be stressed by the नायिका as the reason for गमन—When the father refused consent to his small son to accompany him when he (the father) was going on a military expedition, because he (the son) was too tender, the son turns the tables on his father, saying that his tenderness was the reason why he should not be left behind—दया

चेद् बाल इति मय्यपरित्याज्य एव ते। (कु) This second vanety is not to be found in the का प्र रुय्यक seems to have initiated it

 Thus we find, in this variety something which is stated by one party as capable of producing a particular result happens to be proposed by the other party as a means for securing exactly the opposite result In the first vanety, on the other hand, something was actually achieved by one means by one party, and was upset by that very means by the other party Read जयरथ—तत्र (= प्रथमे व्याघाते) हि येनकेनचिदुपायेन निष्पादित सद्वस्तु तत्रैव अन्येन अन्यथाक्रियते इत्युक्तम्। इह तु किञ्चिद् निष्पादयितु सभाव्यमानस्य कारणस्य तद्भिरुद्धनिपादकत्वेन समर्थनम्।

 The two nval parties in व्याघात of the first vanety may, as in प्रत्यनीक, be sometimes related as उपमान and उपमेय, the party undoing the work of another being उपमेय in relation to the other whose work is andone which is उपमान Then the figure gets a deep tinge of व्यतिरेक, the उपमेय being suggested as superior to the उपमान जयरथ says अतश्चास्य सर्वात्मना व्यतिरेको निमित्तत्व यायात्। र ग suggests that व्याघात will come under व्यतिरेक—नहि अस्य उत्तमकाव्यतिरेकनिर्मुक्त प्रकारोऽस्ति येन स्वातन्त्र्यमभ्युपगच्छेम। In St 175 दृशा दग्ध etc, the poet thinks of the कमलोचनऽ as उपमेद In relation to विरूपाक्ष, the उपमान \[ See below \] Sometimes, however, as in प्रत्यनीक the two parties may be genune adversarnes Thus in दीनद्रुमान् etc., the खलऽ and सज्जनऽ are actual opponents If we also get a comparative measure of their powers in the stanza, and hence a suggestion of औषम्य, t is incidental And to the extent that there is a suggestion of औपम्य, here also we have a vein of व्यतिरेक

 In the second variety, on the other hand there is not rivalry in action, as in the first, but rivalry in reportee Here we find two parties engaged in a dialogue one of which worsts the other by a smart repartee In this variety therefore, there is no element of व्यतिरेक, but a semblance of विषम (२)—कर्तुं कियाफ्नवाप्ति अनर्थप्राप्तिश्च \[रुय्यक rightly marks off विषम (२) from व्याघात (२)—अत एव द्वितीयाद् विषमाद् भेद। तत्र हि कार्यस्य अनुत्पत्ति, अनर्थस्य च उद्गमनम्। इह तु कार्यकार्यमेव न भवति। तद्विरुद्धस्य अनर्थस्य व्यतिरेकिणोऽपि अत्र सुप्तकार्यत्वात् In विषम, the desired thing cannot be achieved, the undesired is achieved Here the desired thing can be achieved though with difficulty But the undesired can be achieved with ease Thus गमननिवृत्ति can be realised on the score of मार्दव, but with difficulty गमन, on the other hand, can be realised with greater ease\]

Thus we get the following scheme

<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1708677896Screenshot2024-02-23141429.png"/>

 रुद्रट was the first आलङ्कारिक to conceive the figure व्याघात, but his व्याघात was altogether diffe-rent from that of the का. प्र which, therefore, seems to have initiated the figure on the present lines का प्र thought of the first variety only रुय्यक discovered the second variety for the first time And विश्वनाथ, जगन्नाथ, अप्य्यदीक्षित all followed him in admitting two varieties of the figure

 Essentials–\[ निष्पादितवस्तुव्याहतिहेतुत्वात्, निष्पिपादयिषितकार्यव्याहतिहेतुत्वात् वा। का प्र रुय्यक and जग-न्नाथ with slight modifications \] (1) In the first variety, there must be two parties related as उपमान and उपमेय or as genuine opponents. (2) One of these must have achieved something by a particular means (3) The second must upset it by the same means (4) The second is therefore made conspicuous (5) If the two things are related as उपमान-उपमेय , it is the उपमेय which thus outstrips the उपमान and gives rise to व्यतिरेक (6) In such cases, the poet's aim is obviously to exalt the उपमेय. (7) Also if the two parties are real adversanes, the poet's aim is to exalt one over the other, but here the औपम्य is only incidental (8) In the second vanety, the two are parties to an argument, engaged in a ‘battle of wits’ (9) In this argument, one party suggests a particular circumstance for pursuing some course of action, while the other party gives that very circumstance for not pursuing that course of action and thus turns the tables on the other party (10) If in the first variety, the charm lies in the same means undoing a thing done, in this variety it lies in the same circumstance being proposed to achieve a result contrary to the one which the other party expected to achieve therewith.

 § सू ८२ यद्यथा etc Construe केनापि यद् यथा साधितं तद् अपरेण तथैव अन्यथा यद् विधीयेत स व्याघात इति स्मृत। As said above, का प्र has व्याघात only of the 1st variety (निष्पन्नवस्तुविषय) केनापि by उपमान or a real opponent. यथा साधितं तथैव अन्यथा विधीयेत Should be upset (अन्यथा विधा) by he same means (तथा) by which it has been achieved by the other party. साधित=निष्पादित—Shows how the definition does not comprehend the other variety based on निष्पन्नवस्तुविषय thing

 वृत्ति :—येन उपायेन यथा of सूत्र explained उपकल्पितम् Achieved जिगीपुतया. The poet’s aim is to show one as superior to the other. This he secures by presenting the party that he wants to exalt as desirous of scoring a success on the other. तदुपायकमेव अन्यधाकरणम् i e. तेनैव उपायेन अन्यथाकरणम्. साधितवस्तुव्याहतिहेतुत्वात् Explains the name of the figure. The figure is occasioned by the undoing ( व्याहति ) of a thing that is achieved

 St 175 दृशा दग्ध etc Illustrates व्याघात, निष्पादितवस्तुविषय A tribute to वामलोचनाऽ suggesting that they outstrip even शिव Construe —या (विरूपाक्षेण ) दृशा दग्ध मनसिज दृशैव जीवयन्ति ता विरूपाक्षस्य जयिनी वामलोचना स्तुवे। मनसिज or मदन was burnt down by शिव with a glances of his third eye. He is revived by women with glances विरूपाक्ष = शिव. Lat ‘of ugly eyes’ With his third eye, शिव looks ugly cp वपुर्विरूपाक्ष ( कु. स ५ ७१ ) वैरूप्य च त्रिनेत्रत्वात् इlf क्षीरस्वामी—मल्लिनाथ note the contrast between वामलोचना and विरूपाक्ष

 Here वामलोचना. are proved to outstrip शिव in that they succeed in reviving with glance मदन who was burnt down by a look of शिव The poet wants to suggest वामलोचना as उपमेय, शिव as उपमान, because otherwise there is no real antipathy between them Hence व्याघात, with a vein of व्यतिरेक, which is also suggested by बामलोचना and विरूपाक्षस्य Read रुरयकः—सोऽपि (व्याघात) व्यतिरेकनिमित्तत्वेन अत्र उक्त। ‘विरूपाक्षस्य’ ‘चारुलोचना’ इति व्यतिरेकगर्भावेव वाचकौ।

**६१-ससृष्टि**

 ससृष्टि is combination', 'aggregation and, as the name suggests, in the figure we are presented with a combination of two or more of the figures given heretoforeThe figures that enter into the combination im संसृष्टि may be शब्दालङ्कारऽ, dealt with in उ. IX, or अर्थालङ्कारऽ In उल्लास X, or both Thus we may get a संसृष्टि of two or more शब्दालङ्कारऽ, or a संसृष्टि of two or more अर्थालङ्कारऽ, or a संसृष्टि of शब्दालङ्कारऽ and अर्थालङ्कारऽ The first will be called शब्दालङ्कारसंसृष्टि, the second अर्थालङ्कारसंसृष्टि, the last शब्दार्थालङ्कारसंसृष्टि

 In संसृष्टि, to whichever of the three classes it may belong, the अलङ्कारऽ stand mutually indepe-ndent That is to stay, they are incorporated in such a way that if one is detached, the other or others will not be affected To take the instance, लिम्पतीव तर्मोगानि etc. in the first half we have उत्प्रेक्षा quite independent of the उपमा in the second viz. असत्पुरुषसेवेव If we were to drop the उपमा, the उपेक्षा will not suffer or if we drop the उत्प्रेक्षा, the उपमा will not suffer

 Thus what is meant by the different figures remaining independ ent in संसृष्टि is that they are detachable one from the other, and are not orgamcally connected as in सङ्कर that follows this figure \[vide सङ्कर \] To use the नैयायिक terminology, there is संयोग between the figures as they stand in संसृष्टि And as we know in तर्क, सयुक्त things can be detached one from the other, without affecting the integrity of either The कपि in वृक्षकपिसंयोग can be detached from the वृक्ष and neither the कपि nor the वृक्ष loses its integrity So also the figures in संसृष्टि can be separated, one from the other, and none loses its integrity The आलङ्कारिकऽ put it less technically, but all the same, more effectively, when they say that in संसृष्टि, the different figures are mixed up in the same way in which sesamum and rice get mixed, (तिलतण्डुलन्याय) The housewife never finds it difficult to sift तिलऽ from the तण्डुलऽ। Read रुय्यक—अलङ्कारान्तरत्वेऽपि च संयोगन्यायेन स्फुटावगमो भेद। अत एव तिलतण्डुलन्याय तस्य यथार्थतामवगमयति।

 The question naturally arises as to the limits within which the individual figures must occur to give rise to संसृष्टि It is necessary to define these limits in view of the fact that we may get a good many figures in the space of a few stanzas. Supposing there is one figure in one stanza, and another in the following one, will it be ससृष्टि?

 आलङ्कारिकऽ have thus fixed the limits If the Rs अलङ्कारऽ in one वाक्य, it is संसृष्टि Also if they occur in one श्लेष or stanza, though it may embody अनेकवाक्यऽ it is संसृष्टि This naturally makes it clear that if in a युग्म i e a couple of stanzas that together embody one वाक्य, we have more than one अलङ्कार, mutually independent, we get संसृष्टि because though the stanzas are two, the वाक्य is one. If these figures occur in a कुलक i e a group of three to five stanzas, embodying one sustained वाक्य, we get संसृष्टि Read जयरथ —स...मानत्वेन च प्रतिपत्ति अलङ्काराणाम् एकस्मिन् वास्ये तत्तच्छन्दसि वा भवति। यदाहु —वाक्यार्थभेदेऽपि एक....कान्तर्गतत्वेन अलङ्कारस्य अलङ्कारान्तरसाहित्यं प्रतिभाति एव। अतिदूरत्वात् विभिन्नश्लोकगतत्वेन वाक्यभेद व्यवहितत्वान्न भवति संसृष्टि इति। कुलकादौ अपि अलङ्काराणां वाक्यैकवाक्यतया यदि अविच्छेदन प्रतिपत्तिप्ररोह स्यात्तदा अत्रापि संसृष्ट्यद्यभ्युपगमे न कश्चिद्दोष।

 Is संसृष्टि a separate figure? —Since in संसृष्टि, we get merely a combination of the figures, mutually independent, the question arises as to whether such a combination can be viewed as a separate figure. There seems to be a consensus of opinion among आलङ्कारिक’s on this point of allowing संसृष्टि a separate status. In the concept of चित्ररूप, admitted by the तार्किकऽ, they find a handy analogy to support

their view point Just as चित्ररूप, though made up of separate individual रूपऽ is still regarded as an independent रूप, so संसृष्टि, though made up of separate individual अलङ्कारऽ, should be regarded as an independent अलङ्कार, they argue Read जयरथ :—चित्ररूपमपि एकमेव वस्तुरूप भास-ते। तथैव भिन्नकक्ष्याणामलङ्काराणां सधटमानत्वेन प्रतीतौ एकता (ध्य) वसाय इति युक्तमेव ससृष्टया घलकारान्तर-त्वम्। एकत्रैव एकस्य द्वयोर्बहूना वालकाराणामवगमे यथायथ अतिशयोत्कर्षस्य स्वसक्तिसाक्षिकत्वेन वैद्यमानत्वात्

 संसृष्टि was recognised by भामह whose definition of the figure was wide enough to include सङ्कर also दण्डिन्'s संसृष्टि is made up of two divisions one clearly corresponding to सङ्कर of later आलङ्कारिकऽ, the other to their संसृष्टि उद्भट was the first to separate the two and make them ind-ependent figures वामन has संसृष्टि alone but wide enough to comprehend सङ्कर रुद्रट just goes the other way He has सङ्कर, in two varieties, one of which corresponds to संसृष्टि अल्लट, विश्वनाथ, अप्पय्यदीक्षित have admitted सङ्कर and संसृष्टि as independent figures जगन्नाथ's र गं does not treat of them, evidently due to the work having ended abruptly.

Note the following scheme—

                         संसृष्टि  
शब्दालङ्कारसंसृष्टि अर्थालङ्कारसंसृष्टि        
शब्दा-अर्थालङ्कारसंसृष्टि  

 ** Essentials**—\[ संसृष्टि संसर्ग सम्बन्ध —वामन \] (1) There must be two or more अलङ्कारऽ in the same वाक्य (which may even extend over two or more श्लोकऽ) or the same श्लोक (2) All of them may be शब्दालङ्कारऽ, all अर्थालङ्कारऽ, or there may be a combination of शब्दालङ्कारऽ and अर्थालङ्कारऽ (3) The different अलङ्कारऽ must be mutually independent i. e, separable one from the other without affecting the integrity of either (4) This combination of अलङ्कारऽ is regarded as giving rise to a neto figure ..... संसृष्टि.

 § सू ८३ सेष्टा etc Construe —यत् एतेया इह भेदेन स्थिति सा संसृष्टेः इष्टा। एतेषां Refers not only to the अर्थालङ्कारऽ dealt with in the course of the present उल्लास, but also शब्दालङ्कारऽ treated of in the preceding उल्लास Thus एतेषां=शब्दालङ्काराणाम् अर्थालङ्काराणां च इदं The वृत्ति makes it refer to शब्दोश्च or अर्थोश्च or उपयोश्च of वाच्य This means that if it is शब्दालङ्कारसंसृष्टि, it will be present in the शब्दऽ of the काव्य If it be अर्थालङ्कारसंसृष्टि, it will be present in the अर्थ of the काव्य If it is उभयालङ्कारसंसृष्टि it will be present in शब्दऽ and अर्थ of the काव्य भेदेन स्थितिः Separate existence

 i e. independent appearance As we saw this means that one अलङ्कार must not be organically connected with another, on the analogy of the तिल-तण्डुल्ऽ \[ रुद्रट was the first to refer to this analogy as also the other नीर क्षीरन्याय of सङ्कर \] इष्टा = स्मृता said to be

 वृत्ति—एतेषां of the सूत्र explained as समनन्तरमेव उक्तस्वरूपाणाम् अलङ्काराणां समनन्तरमेव Refers to the 10th उल्लास and the 9th as well In the tenth अर्थालङ्कारऽ were dealt with in the ninth शब्दालङ्कारऽ यथासम्भव According to the occasion or possibility i e on some occasions we may get संसृष्टि of शब्दालङ्कारऽ only or of अर्थालङ्कारऽ only, and on others of both together सम्प्रदायप्रकाशिनी construes यथासम्भव with शब्दभागे एव ( for शब्दालङ्कारसंसृष्टि) and अर्थविषये एवं ( for अर्थालङ्कारसंसृष्टि) अन्योन्यनिरपेक्षतया भेदेन of the सूत्र explained एकत्र-शब्दभागे, अर्थविषये, उभयत्र अपि वा Explains इह of the सूत्र See explanation of इह given above. एकार्थ समवायस्वभावा Gives the propriety of the name संसृष्टि. It is so called because in essence it consists in a grouping (समवाय) (of figures ) in the frame of the same idea That is all the diverse figures are there to embellish the one idea present in the वाक्य or श्लोक \[ एकार्थे समवाय अलङ्काराणाम् \] A more accurate word would have been संयोग, as used by रुय्यक to describe the mutual connection between the अलङ्कारऽ in संसृष्टि समवाय strictly speaking means inseparable connection, while here between the अलङ्कारऽ the connection is separable

 St. 176 वदनसौरभ etc Illustrates संसृष्टि of शब्दालङ्कारणऽ A lovely lady sends forth the tumult of her gardle Construe—वदनसौरभलोभ परिभ्रमद्भ्रमरसभूतशोभया अलकलोलदृशा अन्यया चलितया (मत्या) ....... विदधे। वदन शोभया whose beauty was heightened by the bees that hovered about in their greed of the fragrance of the lady's mouth v I कुसमसौरभ अलकलोलदृशा whose eses moved with the dangling locks. चलितया etc i e वनितया when she moved the girdle tinkled अन्यया वनितया

 Here there is यमक VIZ ..... / ..... in d अनुप्राम of भ्, म्, र्, in ab and of ल् in c So यमक being present in d and अनुप्रास in abc the two are independent As यमक and अनुप्राम are शब्दालङ्कारऽ embodied in the same श्लोक ( and also वाक्य ) this is शब्दालङ्कारसंसृष्टि

 St 177 लिम्पतीव etc Illustrates संसृष्टि of अलङ्कारऽ. Vide notes p 35 St. 26

 Here in the first half we have उत्प्रेक्षा, in the second, उपमा Both are independent, one of the other To wit, supposing we eliminate

the उपमा असत्पुरुषसेवेव the उत्प्रेक्षा will not suffer or if we eliminate the उत्प्रेक्षा the उपमा will not suffer As उपमा and उत्प्रेक्षा are अर्थालङ्कारऽ we have अर्थालङ्कारसंसृष्टि

 पूर्वत्र etc परस्परनिरपेक्षो clearly evident from the fact that यमक is present in d while अनुप्रास is present in abc यमक a शब्दालङ्कार काव्यप्रकाश (IX) defines it as अर्थे सत्यर्थभिन्नानां वर्णानां सा पुनश्रुति। यमकम् i e there must be a repetition of the same set of syllables (लकलोलकले) in relation to different meanings (the first लकल is connected with कलकलो the second with अलकलोसदृशा) दण्डिन्'s is a more direct definition—आवृत्ति वर्णसङ्घातगोचरा यमक विदु। —यमक is the repetition of (गोचर) a set of syllables (वर्गसङ्घात) अनुप्रास का प्र (IX) defines it as वर्णसाम्यमनुप्रास Alliteration repetition of the same syllables Note that the repetition must be of the consonants If the vowels are not repeated it does not matter Thus म—मे मा—मु will be अनुप्रास though the vowels are different Read का प्र (वृत्ति) —स्वरवैसादृश्यऽपि व्यञ्जनमात्रसदृशव वर्णसाम्यम्। उत्तरत्र तथाविधे उपमोत्प्रेक्षे उत्तरत्र in St 177 तथाविधे i e परस्परनिरपेक्षे (See above)

 st 178 सो णथि etc Illustrates संसृष्टि of शब्दालङ्कार and अर्थालङ्कार Compliment to the beauty of a lovely lass that has not so far found a suitable match and hence gads about। Sanskrit reading—स नास्ति अत्र ग्रामे य एना महमहायमानलावण्याम्। तरुणानां हृदयलुण्ठाकी परिष्वकमाणा निवार यति॥महमहायमानलावण्याम् (v / आमोत्मानलावण्याम्) whose grace is being celebrated √ मह् to esteem highly हृदयलुण्ठाकी Heart robber परिष्वकन्तीम् √ ष्वक्क to go (1A)

 Here there is अनुप्रास of स्थ ण and म in abcd There is रूपक in हृदयलुण्ठाकी—Heart robbe-r the अनुप्रास and रूपक are mutually in dependent Hence it is शब्दार्थालङ्कारसंसृष्टि अनुप्रास being शब्दालङ्कार and रूपक अर्थालङ्कार Note that this is to be sharply distinguished from सङ्कर (३) where we get a blend of शब्दालङ्कार and अर्थालङ्कार in the same प

 अत्रानुप्रासो etc ससर्गश्च तयोरेकत्र वाक्ये छन्दसि वा समवेतवात् And these two viz अनुप्रास and रूपक are looked upon as combined because they are embodied in one sentence or one stanza (छन्दस्) Above we saw how the limits specified for संसृष्टि are वाक्य or श्लोक Here सो णथि णिवरेह is one वाक्य and also one श्लोक So one may with equal justice say that here is संसृष्टि because the two figures occur in the space of a वाक्य or छन्दस् (=श्लोक) समवेतत्वात् As saud above संयुक्तत्वात् will be better समवाय of figures is in evidence in सङ्कर that follows.

**६२—सङ्कर**

 सङ्कर is ‘a blend’ ‘a compound’, and the figure, in all its three varieties has the feature of bei-ng a blend or compound of figures In संसृष्टि, we had merely the combination of figures standing mutually distinct In सङ्कर, on the other hand the different figures are not mutually distinct or independent They are organically connected, one with the other, because here one figure actually goes into the very fabric of another, that is, forms its integral part Thus here one figure could not be detached from the other, without impairing the other Here is in evidence the क्षीर-नीरन्याय, according to the आलङ्कारिकऽ Just as milk and water get blended inseparably, so the अलङ्कारऽ get blended inseparably in this figure There is समवाय between them

 Three varieties of सकर —(1) अगागिभावसकर - In the *first variety*, one figure is subservient (अङ्ग) to the other which is principal (अगिन्) To put it otherwise one causes the other, as तन्तुऽ cause the पट. The अङ्गिन् cannot exist apart from the अग so here the principal अलङ्कार cannot isolate itself from the subsidiary one The अङ्ग on the other hand can exist apart from the अङ्गिन् and so the subsidiary figure could exist apart from the principal But it must not be forgotten that, though the subsidiary can thus exist apart from the principal it cannot do so without losing its grace which it derives in its relation to the अगिन् तन्तुऽ can exist apart from the पट, but when they exist apart they cannot claim the grace they have when they go into the texture of the पट Thus the अग and अगिन् in this variety are mutually dependent one upon the other, for the resultant grace The two (or more) अलङ्कारऽ may be अर्थालङ्कारऽ or शब्दालङ्कारऽ \[There cannot be अङ्गाङ्गिभाव between a शब्दालङ्कार and अर्थालङ्कार\]

 (ii) सन्देहसङ्कर—The second tariety of सङ्कर comprehends those cases of अर्थालङ्कारऽ in which due to the characteristics of two (or more) figures being present in the same stanza, we are in doubt as to which figure should be accepted to the exclusion of others. This variety arises owing to the absence of a determining arcumstance for accepting one figure or excluding another When such a circumstance is there, one figure is naturally accepted to the exclusion of the other and so no room is left for सन्देह जयरथ refers to and supports a view which questioned the validity of सन्देहसङ्कर What are given as सन्देहसङ्करऽ,

he says reduce themselves to one particular figure and leave no room for सन्देह Read —न चैतद् अलङ्कारसारकारातीना मतम्। अलङ्काराणां सन्देहायोगात्। सर्वदा सर्वत्र सर्वान् प्रति च अलङ्कारलक्षण प्रणयनम्। तथात्वे च सन्देहो अयुक्त। तस्य नियतदेशकालप्रमातृगतत्वात्। \[See below\]

 (iii) एकवाचकानुप्रविष्टसङ्कर—In the third variety we find one शब्दालङ्कार and another अर्थालङ्कार in the body of the same ... This is the limited scope assigned to this division in the का प्र वृत्ति the सूत्र ( एकत्र विषये ) being vague on the point Later आलङ्कारिकऽ bke रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ and अप्पय्य-दीक्षित do not limit the scope of this variety to ... Acc. to them it occurs in एकवाचक, which may be or part of a sentence The सूत्र in the काव्यप्रकाश—स्फुटमेकत्र विषय Indeed supports this broad view of this variety \[See below at सू ८६ and the वृत्ति thereon\]

   Thus we get the following scheme—

 As we saw at संसृष्टि सङ्कर (अङ्गागि) was included by भामह and अङ्गिन् under their संसृष्टि सन्देहसङ्कर and एकवाचकानुप्रविष्ट सङ्कर were not possibly conceived by them उद्भट. was the first to give सङ्कर of which he gave four varieties रुद्रट comprehended संसृष्टि under his सङ्कर He has nothing to correspond to the present divisions of the figure सङ्कर का प्र reduced उद्भटऽ four divisions to three the fourth (एकवाक्य .......) having been covered by संसृष्टि रुय्यक admits the same varieties as का प्र but anstead of एकवाक्यप्रविष्ट सङ्कर of का प्र ( वृत्ति not सूत्र ) he gives एकवाक्यप्रविष्ट सङ्कर विश्वनाथ and अप्पय्यदीक्षित tollow रुय्यक-जगन्नाथ has no सङ्कर evidently oving to the work having ended abruptls

 Essentials —\[ क्षीरनन्यायेस सर्वा.... अलङ्कााराणाम् अत्र \] ( i ) In the first variety one अर्थालङ्कार must be subservient to another or one
शब्दालङ्कार must be subservient to another \[रुय्यक, however rightly says that one शब्दालङ्कार cannot be subserment to another See below\] (ii) In the second for want of a conclusive arcumstance for accepting one figure (रुय्यक) or for revecting another (रुय्यक) there is an ambiguity

as to which of the two (or more) possibles अर्थालङ्कारऽ should be accepted since there are feat-ures that would justify the assumption of either (iii) The second variety–सन्देहसङ्कर—is possible only in the case of अर्थालङ्कारऽ (iv) In the third vanety we have aशब्दालङ्कार and अर्थालङ्कार incorp-orated in the same शब्द ( or group of शब्दऽ )

 ई सू ८४ अविश्रान्ति etc Construe —आत्मनि अविश्रान्तिजुषा अङ्गाङ्गित्व तु सङ्कर। Defines the first variety आत्मनि अविश्रान्तिजुषा = आत्मनि अविश्रान्ताना—आत्मनि अस्वतत्राणा those that are not self sufficient as they are in संसृष्टि where the different figures are स्वतन्त्र अङ्गाङ्गित्व Relation as subsidiary (अङ्ग अनुग्राहक ) and principal (अङ्गिन् = अनुग्राह्य) Some अलङ्कारऽ are subservient to some other which is the principal

 वृत्ति –एते एव 1 e शब्दालङ्कारऽ and अर्थालङ्कारऽ आत्मनि अनासादित स्वतप्रभावा आत्मनि अविश्रान्तिजुषा of the सूत्र explained Are not in them selves independent as in संसृष्टि परस्पर अनुग्राह्य अनुग्राहकता दधति One is the अनुग्राह्य or principal in relation to which the others are अनुग्राहक or subservient सकीर्यमाणस्वरूपत्वात् The propnety of the name सङ्कर explained सकीर्यमाण 1 e where the figures are blended one into the other In संसृष्टि they are ससृज्यमान Thus the आलङ्कारिकऽ beginning with रुद्रट gave the analogy of क्षीर-नीर as applicable to सङ्कर while they had given तिल-तण्डुलन्याय in support of संसृष्टि In सङ्कर to put it differently there is समवाय between the अङ्ग-अङ्गिन् figures In संसृष्टि there is between one figure and another In brief in सङ्कर (particularly variety 1) one figure goes into the very structure of another

 st 179 आत्ते सीमन्तरत्ने etc. Illustrates अङ्गाङ्गिसङ्कर of two अर्थालङ्कार A paean addressed to a king by the poet The fugitive women of the enemy are by robbers mulcted of all save their pearl necklaces which appearing red with the ruddy hue of their lower lips are mistaken for strings of red bernes by the robbers and therefore left untouched। Construe रातन् अरण्ये इत्वरीणा त्वपरिमृग-दृशा मरकतिनि सीमन्तरत्ने आत्ते, हेमताम्रपत्रे हृते झटिति मेघलायां लुप्ताया मणितुलाकोटियुग्मे गृहीते शबरा बिम्बोष्ठकान्त्या शोन हार गुञ्जाफलना स्रत इति ( मत्वा ) नैव हरन्ति इत्वरीणा त्वदरिमृगदृशा Of the deer-eyed (1 e lovely) women of your foes that were fleeing इत्वर √ इ Fugative cp the formations √ गत्वर √गं सृत्वर √सृ हेम ताटकपत्रे हृते After they had removed the golden earring ताटकपत्र–(ताटक = तारक ) Lit Earring of the shape of a palm leaf earring in general मणितुलाकोटि (f ) Jewelled an-klets. \[तुल्य सादृश्य कोटयते इति—what is beyond comparison in grace\] .... Aborigines wild tribes

 Here, the principal figure is भ्रान्तिमान् which arises due to the शबरऽ mistakang the हारऽ for गुञ्जाफलाना स्रत But this भ्रान्तिमान् would not have been there, if the हारऽ were not tinged red by the ruddy lustre of the lower lips of the women which involves तद्गुण Hence to भ्रान्तिमान् तद्गुण is subsidiary, भ्रान्तिमान् being अङ्गिन् तद्गुण its अङ्ग Hence अङ्गाङ्गिभावसङ्कर of two अर्थालङ्कारऽ

 अत्र तद्गुणमपेक्ष्य etc अपेक्ष्य by depending upon तदाश्रयेण च तद्गुण सचेतसा प्रभूतचमत्कृतिनिमित्त Though भ्रान्तिमान् has thus to depend upon तद्गुण for its existence, it does not mean that तद्गुण is absolutely inde pendent. Because, even तद्गुण looks much more charming when it appears as an adjunct to the भ्रान्तिमान् than it would have done had it stood alone The तन्तु going into the texture of पद looks more graceful than when it stands by itself, तदाश्रयेण = भ्रान्तिमदाश्रयेण

 St. 180 जटामाभि etc Illustrates अर्थालङ्काराणाम् अङ्गाङ्गिमकर of a more complex kind The moon conceived as an anchorite Construe—नदामामि भाभि (उपलक्षित) करधृतकलकाक्षवलय, वियोगिव्या-पत्तेरिव कलितवैराग्यविशद भस्मापाण्डु शशी परिप्रेङ्खतारापरिकपाला..... व्योम्नि पितृवने व चरति। जटाभाभि भाभि ( उपलक्षित ) with lustre that appears like matted hair करधृतकलकाक्षवलय who has taken the rosary in the form of the spot by its कर (1 ray 2 hand) कलितवैराग्यविशद That appears clear owing to the immunity it is secured from ruddy hue (By ... who appears serene with the immunity he has won from passions) वैराग्य 1 Immumty from the ruddy hue of evening twilight, 2 Immunity from the passions विशद clarity of appearance, as applied to the moon Serenity of mind, as applied to an ascetic वियोगिथ्यपत्तेरिव (who seems to have turned a recluse) as if owing to someone's death (व्यापत्ति) that has brought about separation (from him) \[One turns a सन्यासिन् due to the death of, and consequent separation from someone dear to one\] The explanation by उद्योत— वियोगिन विरया नेपा व्यापत्ते विनाशाय इव is too laboured परिप्रेङ्खत् etc The sky the surface of which is dotted with the skulls in the shape of the host (परिकर) of stars that are hanging about. परिप्रेङ्खत् Possibly refers to the flickering lustre of the stars. पितृयन=दमशान.................. .............. as applied to the moon. The ascetic also is.............. white with ashes.  

 Here the उप्रेक्षा, वियोनिध्यापसेरिव, makes possible the श्लेष,... विशद because it accounts for .... (....) This श्लेष with its emphasis on वैराग्य becomes possible also with the उपमा—.....

and the रूपक करधृत यत् कलर एव अक्षवलयम्, because तटा and अक्षवलय are symbols of वैराग्य and thus serve to sustain the idea of वैराग्य. \[ प्रदीप takes श्लेष as अङ्ग of the रूपक and उपमा Not very cogent जटा and अक्षवलय are symbols of वैराग्य and hence make a case for it. Thus they must be subsidiary to the श्लेष—कलितवैराग्य \] Thus here श्लेष is the अङ्गिन् and उपमा, रूपक and उत्प्रेक्षा are its अङ्गऽ This श्लेष is further sustained by the उपमा, पितृवने इव व्योम्नि, which in its turn is supported by the उपमा, ताराणां परिवर कपालानि इव \[Generally commentators take this as रूपक But when we have पितृवने इव व्योम्नि, as उपमा, with प्राधान्य to उपमेय— व्योम्नि, the तारापरिकरक-पालानि also must be उपमा, with प्राधान्य to तारापरिकर \] Thus here we get श्लेष as अङ्गिन् and उत्प्रेक्षा, उपमाऽ (जटाभाभि भाभि and पितृवने इव व्योम्नि), and रूपक (करधृत etc ) as अङ्गऽ m relation to it To the उपमा, पितृवने इव व्योम्नि, the उपमा, नारापरिक्रकपाल is subsidiary \[ Could we not take the उपमा, तारा कपालनीव as caused by पितृवने इव व्योम्नि, 1 e as अङ्गिन् relation to the latter? Possibly not Greater prominence attaches to the crematorium than its details like skulls etc So the poet's eye is on securing the effect viz पितृवने इव ब्योम्नि to which naturally तारापरिकरकपाल becomes subsidiary It must be admitted, however, that in deciding aſ one cannot be dogmatic. There is ample room for divergence between one as sessment and another \]

   पूर्ववत् as in St 179

 कलक एव मुख्येऽपि उपचार एवं शरण स्यात्। We have seen above that कलङ्काक्षवलय was interpre-ted as a रूपक (कलङ्कऽ एव अक्षवलयम्) Could we not take it as an उपमा (कलङ्कऽ एव अक्षवलयमिव)? For, we have seen already (Notes, p 44 ) how such compounds lend themselves to a double interpretation as an उपमा and रूपक, unless there is some specific circumstance confining us to one of the two Is there any such circumstance in the present case confining us to रूपक and रूपक alone ?

 Yes. The adjective करधृत in करधृत कलङ्काक्षवलय is a circumstance that makes a case for रूपक and it happens like this  

 वरधूत applies to अक्षवलय Because we know that the rosary is carned in the hand. It does not apply so aplly to कलङ्क, since really speaking कलङ्क is not borne by the moon by its करऽ (rays) but on its form (मूर्ति) We thus see that करधृत applies better to अक्षवलय than to कलङ्क To कलङ्क, करधृत will apply only secondanly, 1. e. after कर is by a twist interpreted as मूर्ति, on the justification that कर (rays ) and मूर्ति (form)

 being close enough, one viz. कर is used to signify the other viz. मूर्ति. Taking this into consi-deration, we have to interpret कलङ्काक्षवलय in such a way as would lay greater emphasis on अक्षवलय than कलङ्क. And this is done, when we interpret it as a रूपक. Because we have seen \[p. 48 B. Notes) that in रूपक greater prominence attaches to the उपमान than to the उपमेय : and here the उपमान being अक्षवलय is naturally brought into prominence when we interpret the compound as a रूपक ( कलङ्कः एव अक्षवलयम्).

 If, on the other hand, the compound were dissolved as an उपमा (कलङ्कः अक्षवलय इव ), greater prominence will attach to the उपमेय, कलङ्क , as the rule is that in उपमान, उपमेय is प्रधान. And, as we saw above, करधृत applies to it only secondarily (उपचार). So if we take कलङ्काक्षवलय as an उपमा, the adjective will have to be given a twist and then made applicable to कलङ्क, the wa thing.

 As with रूपक, the adjective applies primarily to the प्रधान and with उपमा only secondarily, we have to prefer रूपक to उपमा Because primary significance is always preferred to secondary. उपमा is thus ruled out. Thus करधृतत्व has helped us to prefer one figure to another

** करधृतत्वमेव साधकप्रमाणतां प्रतिपद्यते** There are tiro hands of प्रमाणऽ or probative circumsta-nces that enable us to accept one figure to the exclusion of another They are (i) साधकऽ, those that make a case for a particular figure and (ii) वाधकऽ, those that help us to reject a particular figure, thereby clearing the way for the acceptance of the other. (See below at § सू, ८५.) अस्य हि ...प्रसिद्धि Because (हि), if कलङ्काक्षवलय (अस्य=कलङ्काक्षवलयस्य ) is taken as a रूपक, अक्षवलय becomes मुख्य, being the उपमान And that viz अक्षवलय is universally known as suited for being carried in the hand. श्लेषच्छायया....उद्वहनात् Since कलङ्क being उपमेय is प्रकृत, the adjective करधृत must apply to it also. But we find that it applies to कलङ्क secondarly. To begin with करधारण as applied to ... means .... ( कर = ....). If in place of “करधृत,” one were to use 'रश्मिभूत,' the statement would look incoherent, as कलङ्क is not held by रश्मिऽ. It looks coherent under the shadow of ..; because कर also means hand, and things are generally camed in the hand. Thus ... first lends an appearance of plausibility to करधारण as applied to यमक. And afterकरधृत is thus used in relation to कलङ्क, though really speaking कलङ्क is not करधृत, we interpret it secondanly (उपचर्य) as meaning मूर्तिभूत, on the justification that rays (...) and figure (मूर्ति)

are close enough (प्रत्यासत्ति) to allow us to use one for the otherम(प्रत्यासत्त्या due to proximity, as गङ्गा it is used to convey गङ्गातट because the two are in proximity उपचर्य योज्यते is interpreted secondarily उपचर्य =उपचारेण = लक्षणया शशाङ्केन उद्वहनात् —Explains करधारण असदेव of the prece-ding sentence The moon carries the कलङ्क on its body, not on its rays

 कलङ्कोऽ उत्कटतया प्रतिपत्ति–Because in उपमा, उपमेय IS उत्कट or प्रधान न चास्य (कलङ्कस्य) करधृत-त्व etc And as करधृत, as seen above does not really (तत्त्वत ) apply to कलङ्क , but does so only secondarily, we shall be resorting to (शरण) उपचार or a secondary way of interpretation even in the matter of the मुख्य Viz कलङ्क \[ कलङ्क becomes मुरय, if we take कलकाक्षवलय as an उपमा. \] मुख्येऽपि when we take it as रूपक, the उपचार has to be resorted to only in the matter of the अमुख्य (because कलङ्क the उपमेय becomes अमुख्य in रूपक) एवंरूप सङ्कर 1e. अङ्गागिसङ्कर शब्दालङ्कारयोरपि above we saw how it featured in अर्थालङ्कारयो or अर्थालङ्कारेषु

 St 181 राजति तटीय etc Illustrates शब्दालङ्कारयो अङ्गागिभावे सङ्कर Description of a mountain slope and a herd of elephants Construe अभिहतदानवरासा-अतिपाति-सारावनदा इय लटी राजति। अविरतदानवरा, वनदा, सारा, गजता च यूथ अतिपाति। **अभिहतदानवरासातिपातिसारावनदा** adj of तटी On which there is a noisy (साराव) river (नद), which flows rapidly (अतिपाति) and which (by its roar) beats the tumult (रास √ रस् to sound) of deimons. गजता A group of bull-elephants अविरतदानवरा Emment with its continuous ichor वनदा (दो to cut) that fells trees सारा Powerful यूथ अतिपाति Looks after the herd very assiduously (अति)

 Here we have यमक दानवरासातिपातिसारावनदा—दानवरासातिपातिसारावनदा Again, we have अनुलोम प्रतिलोम, because दानवरासातिपातिसारावनदा, reads the same both ways read inversely it gives the same effect as when read in due order

 Here, as the वृत्ति says, यमक and अनुलोम प्रतिलोम in the two पादऽ are mutually dependent and hence we get अङ्गाङ्गिभावसङ्कर of शब्दालङ्कारऽ

 रुय्यक rather justifiably criticises this view about शब्दालङ्कारऽ being related as अङ्ग and अङ्गिन् His argument seems to be that one शब्दालङ्कार cannot be subsidiary to another Both remain mutually independent, when they appear in different places, or both are mutually dependent when they appear in the same place, as in the present instance When mutually dependent, one cannot be अङ्गिन् in relation to another So cases of two शब्दालङ्कारऽ like this will come under संसृष्टि (if they occur in different places), or एकत्वाचकानुप्रविष्ट सङ्कर, if they occur

in the same place Read रुय्यक—शब्दालङ्कारसकरस्तु कैश्चिद् उदाहृतौ यथा—राजनि तटीय 
  वनदा \।\। अत्र यमक अनुलोम-प्रतिलोमपौ शब्दालङ्कारयो परम्परा पेक्षत्वेन अगागिसवर इति। एतत्तु न सम्यगावर्तकम् \। शब्दालङ्कारयो शब्दवदुपकार्य-उपकारकत्वाभावन अगागिमावाभावात्। शब्दालङ्कारसंसृष्टि तु अत्र श्रेयसी। यद्वा अत्र शब्दालङ्कारद्वयमेक वाचकानुप्रविष्टमिति तृतीय सङ्करो शय।

 Commentators like प्रदीप also hesitate to accept शब्दांलकारयो अगाविसावे शङ्कर माणिक्यचन्द्र also seems to follow रूय्यक Read मिथो विवेकेन द्वयोरिह वृत्तेरभावात्। अपक्षैव च परस्पर अनुग्राह्य अनुग्राह्यकभाव। अथवा असौ अनुग्राह्य इति निर्णेतुमशक्यत्वे मिथ यापेक्षा शब्दालकारयो इति संसृष्टि एव अत्र युक्ता \। यदा शब्दालङ्कारयस्य एकवाचकगतत्वे तृतीय सङ्कर \।

  अत्र यमक etc अनुलोम-प्रतिलोप This is a poetical feat consisting in giving a वाक्य or part of it श्लोक in such words that they read alike both ways The 9thउल्लास does not refer specifically to this variety of चित्रकाव्य रुद्रट \[५ ३\] specifies it. चित्रभेद चित्र obviously, शब्दचित्र परस्परापेक्षे Mutually dependent As it is not easy to deade which is अग and which अगिन्, they are indeed परस्परापेक्ष See मा च cated above Vide प्रदीप also

  § सू ८५ एकस्य च ग्रहे etc Defines सङ्कर ( २) Construe न्याय दोपाभावात् एकस्य ग्रहे अनिश्चय च (सङ्कर भवति।) एकस्व =एकालवारस्य न्याय दोषा भावात् For want of a circumstance agreeable for the acceptance of one figure, or one which rules out the other figure This variety occurs where the chances for figures more than one are equal and there is no न्याय or दोष that would help us to admit one only ( एकस्य ग्रह) to the exclusion of the other Note that सन्देहसङ्कर 13 confined to अर्थालङ्कारऽ only शब्दालङ्कारऽ cannot be confused one with the other The defination is borrowed practically terbatim from उद्धट. Cp एकस्य च ग्रहे न्याय-दोषाभावे च सङ्कर। न्याय (नीयते प्राप्यते विवक्षितार्थसिद्धि अनेन) is साधक or a circumstance that supports a particular figure e g करधूत S180 is साधक of रुपक One cannot say with certainty whether उद्भट was the first to use this term in this sense in अलङ्कार The same is true of दोष also दोष 15 रुपक that rules out a particular figure and thus strengthens the claims of the other I Note that when features of two or more figures are present in a stanza, a INT circumstance supports a figure A वाधक circumstance rules out one figure and thereby makes a case for the other When neither of the circumstances is there naturally there remains ambiguity as to the figure in the starrza and this as सदेहसङ्कर \]

 वृत्ति— अलङ्काराणां ....... अर्थालङ्काराणां समावेशोऽपि विरोधात् The princ... ple being that more than one अर्थालङ्कार cannot be present simulta

are close enough (प्रत्यासत्ति) to allow us to use one for the other प्रत्यासत्त्या due to proximity, as गङ्गा is used to convey गङ्गानद because the two are in proximity. उपचर्य योज्यते is interpreted secondarily. उपचर्य = उपचारेण = लक्षणया शशाङ्केन.....उद्वहनात्ः— Explains करधारण असदेव of the pr-eceding sentence The moon carries the कलम on its body, not on its rays

 कलङ्कोऽ. .उत्कटतया प्रतिपत्ति—Because in उपमा, उपमेय is उत्कट or प्रधान न चास्य (= कलङ्कस्य) करधृततत्त्वं etc. And as करधृत, as seen above, does not really (तत्त्वत) apply to कलङ्क, but does so only secondarily, we shall be resorting to (शरण) उपचार or a secondary way of interpretation even in the matter of the मुख्य Viz कलङ्क. \[ कलङ्क becomes मुख्य, if we take कलकाक्षवलय as an उपमा.\] मुख्येऽपि when we take it as रूपक, the उपचार has to be resorted to only in the matter of the अमुख्य (because कलङ्क the उपमेय becomes अमुख्य in रूपक) एवंरूप सङ्कर I e अङ्गाङ्गिसङ्कर शब्दालङ्कारयोरपि above we saw how it featured in अर्थालकारयो.or अर्थाल्कारेषु

 St 181 राजति तटीय etc Illustrates शब्दालङ्कारयो अङ्गाङ्गिभावे सङ्कर Description of a mountain slope and a herd of elephants Construe अभिहतदानवरासाअतिपाति-सारावनदा इय लटी राजति। अविरतदा-नवरा, वनदा, सारा, गजता च यूथ अतिपाति। अभिहतदानवरासातिपातिसारावनदा adj of तटी On which there is a noisy (साराव) river (नद), which flows rapidly (अतिपाति) and which (by its roar) beats the tu-mult (रास √ रस् to sound) of deimons. गजता A group of bull-elephants अविरतदानवरा Emment with its continuous ichor वनदा (दो to cut) that fells trees सारा Powerful यूथ अतिपाति Looks after the herd very assiduously (अति)

 Here we have यमक दानवरासातिपातिसारावनदा—दानवरासातिपातिसारावनदा Again, we have अनुलोम प्रतिलोम, because दानवरासातिपातिसारावनदा, reads the same both ways read inversely it gives the same effect as when read in due order

 Here, as the वृत्ति says, यमक and अनुलोम प्रतिलोम in the two पादऽ are mutually dependent and hence we get अङ्गागिभावसङ्कर of शब्दालङ्कारऽ

 रुय्यक rather justifiably criticises this view about शब्दालङ्कारऽ being related as अङ्ग and अङ्गिन् His argument seems to be that one शब्दालङ्कार cannot be subsidiary to another Both remain mutually independent, when they appear in different places, or both are mutually dependent when they appear in the same place, as in the present instance When mutually dependent, one cannot be अङ्गिन् in relation to another So cases of two शब्दालङ्कारऽ like this will come under संसृष्टि (if they occur in different places), or एकवाचकानुप्रविष्ट सङ्कर, if they occur

in the same place Read रुय्यक —शब्दालङ्कारसङ्करस्तु कैश्चिद् उदाहृतो यथा—‘राजति तटीय वनदा’॥अत्र यमक-अनुलोम-प्रतिलोमयो शब्दालङ्कारया परस्परा पेक्षत्वेन अङ्गाङ्गिसङ्कर इति। एतत्तु न सम्यगावर्तकम्।शब्दालं-कारयो शब्दवदुपकार्य-उपकारकत्वा भावन अङ्गाङ्गिभावाभावात्। शब्दालङ्कारसंसृष्टि तु अत्र श्रेयसी। यद्वा अत्र शब्दालङ्कारद्वयमेकवाचकानुप्रविष्टमिति तृतीय सङ्करो ज्ञेय।

 Commentators like प्रदीप also hesitate to accept शब्दालङ्कारयो अङ्गाङ्गिमावे सङ्कर **माणिक्यचन्द्र** also seems to follow रुय्यक Read मिथो विवेकेन द्वयोरिह वृत्तेरभावात्। अपक्षैव च परस्पर अनुग्राह्य अनुग्राहकभाव। अथवा असौ अनु ग्राह्य इति निर्णेतुमशक्यत्वे मिथ कापेक्षा शब्दालङ्कारयो इति संसृष्टि एव अत्र युक्ता। यद्वा शब्दालं-कारद्वयस्य एकवाचकगतत्वे तृतीय सङ्कर।

 अत्र यमक etc अनुलोम-प्रतिलोम This is a poetical feat consisting in giving a वाक्य or part of it or श्लोक in such words that they read alike both ways The 9th उल्लास does not refer specifically to this variety of चित्रकाव्य रुद्रट \[५ ३\] specifies it चित्रभेद चित्र obviously शब्दचित्र परस्परापेक्षे Mutually dependent As it is not easy to decide which is अङ्ग and which अङ्गिन् they are indeed परस्परापेक्ष See मा च cated above Vide प्रदीप also

 § सू ८५ एकस्य च ग्रहे etc Defines सङ्कर (२) Construe न्याय दोषाभावात् एकस्य ग्रहे अनिश्चय च (सङ्कर भवति।) एकस्य=एकालङ्कारस्य न्याय दोषा भावात् For want of a circumstance agreeable for the acceptance of one figure, or one which rules out the other figure This variety occurs where the chances for figures more than one are equal and there is no न्याय or दोष that would help us to admit one only ( एकस्य ग्रह) to the exclusion of the other Note that सन्देहसङ्कर as confined to अर्थालङ्कारऽ only शब्दालङ्कारऽ cannot be confused one with the other The definition is borrowed practically verbatim from उद्भट. Cp एकस्य च ग्रहे न्याय दोषाभावे सङ्कर। न्याय ( नीयते प्राप्यते विवक्षितार्थमिद्धि अनेन) IS साधक or a circumstance that supports a particular figure e g करधृत in St. 180 1s साधक of रुपक One cannot say with certainty whether उद्भट was the first to use thus term in this sense in अलङ्कार The same is true of दोष also दोष Is वाधक that rules out a particular figure and thus strengthens the claims of the other \[Note that when features of two or more figures are present in a stanza a साधक circumstance supports a figure A वाधक circumstance rules out one figure and thereby makes a case for the other When neither of the circumstances is there naturally there remains ambiguity as to the figure in the stanza and this is सन्देहसङ्कर \]

 वृत्ति—अलङ्काराणां I e अर्थालङ्काराणां समावेशेऽपि विरोधात् The princaple being that more than one and अर्थालङ्कार cannot be present simulta

 (i) न्याय (= साधक) —A particular circumstance may make for a particular figure This is न्याय or साधक In करधृतकलकाक्षवलय we saw how करधृत supported रूपक. But when it makes for one figure it does not mean that it rules out the other To put it otherwise, because a circumstance is साधक of one figure it does not mean it is necessarily बाधक of another Thus करधृत, though it is साधक of रूपक is not still बाधक of उपमा Because करधृत may make sense though secondarly in उपमा also \[as we saw that करधृत will apply secondarily to कलङ्क \]

 (ii) दोष (=बाधक) —A particular circumstance may rule out one figure and thereby strengthen the case of the other This is बाधक Thus in मुखचन्द्र चुम्बति चुम्बति is बाधक of रूपक (मुखमेव चन्द्र ) because चुम्बति cannot apply to चन्द्र

 When a circumstance is बाधक of a particular figure, it is as a rule also साधक of another, e g चुम्बति in मुखचन्द्र चुम्बति which is बाधक of रूपक is साधक of उपमा (मुख चन्द्र इव) When a circum-stance is thus बाधक of one and साधक of another the poetical convention is to describe it only as बाधक of the particular figure though it is साधक of another This is due to the universally acknowledged principle that negation is more emphatic than affirmation and बाधक is negative in significance Thus चुम्बति will be described as बाधक of रूपक and not साधक of उपमा, though it is so

 यत्र तु etc. अवतार Presence एकतरस्य sc अलङ्कारस्य न संशय Doubt as to which figure to be acce-pted न्याय explained as साधक or अनुकूलI e. a circumstance that is agreeable. दोष=बाधक=प्रतिकूल

 सौभाग्य etc Illustrates a साधक of one figure which is not बाधक of another A tribute to the bright smile of a brilliant fice Con strue ज्योत्स्नेव हासद्युति वक्त्रशशिन सौभाग्य वितनोति

 Here when we are in doubt as to whether वक्त्रशशिन is an उपमा or रूपक, the word हासद्युति comes to our help हासद्युति primarily applies to वक्त्र So वक्त्र in वक्त्रशशिन् must be प्रधान This gives a clue to वक्त्रशशिन् being an उपमा, because in उपमा उपभय (viz वक्त्र) is प्रधान

 Though thus हासद्युति is साधक of उपमा, it is not so unfavourable to रूपक, Because हासद्युति may also apply to far, though secondarly It may be, by a twist, interpreted as smiling lustre and then apply to the moon So हासद्युति applying as it does though not primarily. to शशिन् cannot be बाधक of रूपक.

 **मुख्यतया अवगम्यमाना हासद्युति मुख्यतया—**ज्यात्स्नेव हासद्यले is an उपमा and hence the उपमय-हासद्युति is प्रधान, न तथा प्रतिकूला तथा shows that हासद्युति is to some extent प्रतिकुल and that is when taken in its primary Significance But it is not absolutely प्रतिकुल because in its secondary significance it may apply to the शशिन्

  वक्त्रेन्दौ तत्र etc Illustrates the same viz a साधक of one figure which is not बाधक of another The propriety of giving one more instance probably consists in the fact that in the preceding instance we got उपमासाधक while here we get a रूपकसाधक A tribute to the beauty of a नायिकाऽ face Construe यत् नव वक्त्रेन्दौ सति अग्र अपर शीतानु अभ्युद्यन अभ्युद्यत Arisen

 Here when we are in doubt as to whether वक्त्रन्दु is उपमा or रूपक the word अपर is decisive अपर applies primarily to इन्दु because we speak of a second moon in the light of another moon This moon is naturally the इन्दु in वक्त्रेन्दु  
Thus इन्दु becomes प्रधान Hence it is साधक of रूपक (वक्त्र ण्व इन्दु) where the उपमा इन्दु , becomes prominent

 Though अपर ISसाधक of रूपक it is not बाधक of उपमा Because नपर A may secondanly apply to वक्त्र also in so far as वक्त्र and moon are similar, there may be some propriety in looking upon the face as second to the moon Though the author is not clear as to how अपर apples to वक्त्र he seems to suggest that अपर is not powerful enough to be वाधक of उपमा Commentators like मा च and प्रदीप are at sea wlule explaining how अपर applies to मु

 राजनारायणं etc filustrates a बाधक Compliment to a king that he is closely embraced by लक्ष्मी Construe—लक्ष्मी राजनारायण त्वा निभर आलिङ्गति निर्भर ads closely  

 Here when we are undecided as to whether राजनारायणं embodies an उपमा or रूपक आलिङ्गति rules out उपमा L e is बाधक Of उपमा Because if we take राजनारायणं as उपमा,राजा becomes मुख्य and the sentence would purport to mean that लक्ष्मं embraces राजा This becomes an absurd position particularly when नारायण (= विष्णु) is there in the compound For is it not strange that should be supposed as embracing the king when there is the alternative before us to suppose that she embraces नारायण her spouse? (सङ्ख्या प्रति etc.It is improbable that the consort of one will embrace another similar 10 her husband ! \]

neously in the same place **सा चू** द्वयोरनेकेषा वा भूषणाना एकत्र वाक्ये छन्दसि वा समावेशमावसद्भावेऽपि विरोधशका न पार्यते प्रक्षालयितु एकस्य अलकारस्य गोचर इतर कथमाक्रामतु इति। विरोधश्च सहानवस्थानस्वभाव **स** **प्र** says that such अलङ्कारऽ if they are allowed to stand in one place at the same time, set off each other **न चैकतरस्य** etc Nor is there any circumstance supporting the acceptance of one figure nor any circumstance ruling out the other figure so that one of the two could alone be accepted **समुच्चयेन सङ्करस्य एव आक्षेपात्** The copulative (च) in the सूत्र (एकस्य च etc) Indicates that this definition also continues the same figure सङ्कर as was defined in the earlier सूत्र The idea is when the author uses च in the सूत्र, he indicates that he is continuing the theme of the preceding सूत्र And as the preceding सूत्र defined one vanety of सङ्कर, this सूत्र also must be defining सङ्कर of another variety समुच्चय The copulative viz च in the सूत्र, एकस्य च

 st 182. जह गहिरो etc. Illustrates सन्देहसङ्कर (समासोक्ति and अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा The ocean is deep, is rich in gems But, alas’ its water is salin’ Sanskrit rendering—यथा गम्भीरो यथा रत्ननिर्भरa यथा च निर्मलच्छाय। तथा किं विधिना एष सुरसपानीय न कृत॥**सुरसपानीय** Of fresh water

 One may detect herein a समासोक्ति नलनिधि being प्रस्तुत and a patron of rough words अप्रस्तुत Like the जलनिधि, the patron is रत्ननिर्भर ( rich in jewels ) and निर्मलच्छाय ( of clean appearance ) And like the नलनिधि he is not सुरसवाणीओ (सुरसवाणीक) Thus the विशेषणऽ alone being श्लिष्ट and the विशेष्य (जलनिधि) अश्लिष्ट we have features of समासोक्ति, on the assumption that जलनिधि is प्रस्तुत

 But it may be also an अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, on the assumption that जलनिधि is अप्रस्तुत and a patron of rough words प्रस्तुत \[ Vide Notes p 91 अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा vs. समासोक्ति \] As there is no circumstance supporting one figure or ruling out another, we here get सन्देहसङ्कर

 विशेषणसाम्यात् अप्रस्तुतार्थप्रतीते रत्ननिर्भर, निर्मलच्छाय and ‘सुरसवाणीअ’ are transparent enough to suggest a patron of rough words. अब्धे अप्रस्तुतस्य मुखेन Through (मुखेन) अब्धि as अप्रस्तुत तत्समगुणतया गुणऽ being रत्ननिर्भरत्व निर्मच्छायत्व and अ‘सुरसवाणीअ’त्व

 St. 183 नयनानन्द etc. Illustrates सन्देहसङ्कर of greater possibilities. Description of the moon rise and the still undispelled darkness. a Construe एतत् इन्दो नयनानन्दनयि बिम्वं प्रसीदति। निरुद्धाशं तम Darkness that has blocked the quarters.

 Thus may be taken as (1) पर्यायोक्त or (2) अतिशयोक्ति, or (3) रूपक, or (4) दीपक, or (5) तुल्ययोगिता, or (6) समभेत्ति, or (7) अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा

 (1) Since moon stimulates passion, a reference to its rise may indicate that the time is well suited for wooing’ Its suitability is further heightened by the opportunities for privacy suggested by अविशीर्णं तम। Thus the stanza may be taken as embodying पर्यायोक्त suggesting how the time is opportune for stimulating love (कामस्य उद्दीपक काल)

 (2) Or it may be अतिशयोक्ति (१) in the first half एतत् बिम्ब may be मुक itself निगीर्ण by चन्द्र, as it is in अतिशयोक्ति चन्द्रोऽयम् In other words, here वदन may be अध्यवसित as इन्दुबिम्ब

 (3) Or it may be रूपक in the first half, एतत् being taken as used for pointing out the मुख So एतत् बिम्ब will be as good as मुख इन्दुविम्बम् In other words we have here the आरोप of the इन्दुरूप on मुख (तद्रूपारोप) and thus it may be a रूपक

 (4) Or it may be a दीपक, in the first half, एतत् standing for प्रकृत, मुख बिम्व being अप्रकृत, and both being related to the same धर्म, प्रसीदति

 (5) Or it may be तुल्ययोगिता, in the first half, if एतद् goes with बिम्ब are both प्रस्तुत, owing to the poet praising the मुख and इन्दु at nightfall (प्रदोषसमय )

 (6) Or it may be समासोक्ति in the first half, if एतद् goes with बिम्ब and एतद् बिम्ब Is प्रकृत, the विशेषणऽ of which Viz नयनानन्ददायि and प्रसीदति are transparent enough to suggest the अप्रकृत Viz मुख Possibly a cajolery when he finds the मानिनी getting reconciled \[ प्रदोषसमये विशेषणसाम्यात् आननस्य अवगतौ, अवगतौ=व्यञ्जने suggestion When at nightfall the face is suggested as the अप्रकृत owing to the विशेषणऽ equally applying to both इन्दु and मुख \]

 (7) Or it may be अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा in the first half, if बिम्ब is अप्रस्तुत and मुख प्रस्तुत, and the reference is to an occasion (प्रस्ताव) when the face is regaining its serenity (नैर्मल्य) after having become कलुष with कोष  

 As there is no conclusive arcumstance to decide in favour of one figure to the exclusion of others this is again सन्देहसङ्कर अयमेव agua i e सन्देहसङ्कर

 न्याय—दोष—सन्देहसङ्कर is occasioned because there is no decisive evidence for accepting one particular figure

As seen earlier, this evidence may be of two kinds —(1) .......
............

 Thus आलिङ्गति is बाधक of उपमा Is it not साधक of रुपक ? It is because आलिङ्गति goes well with नारायण But though it is बाधक of of another, the circumstance will be recognised as बाधक one and साधक only of the particular figure

  पादाम्बुज Illustrates another वाधक The propriety of giving an additional instance consists in the fact that, in the preceding उदाहरण we had उपमाबाधक here we get रूपकबाधक A prayer to the feet of अम्विका to lead the speakers to success Construe-मञ्जुमञ्जीरशिभितमनोहर अम्विकाया पादाम्बुज नो वि याय भवतु। मञ्जु मनोहर Charming with the sweet tinkling (शिञ्जित) of the anklets (मवीर m n ) अम्विका = पार्वती

  Here मञ्जिरशिञ्जित, not applying to अम्बुन rules out रूपक Though it is साधक of उपमा owing to शिथिल applying to पाद , it is not so designated it would be designated as बाधक of रुपक

  (अम्बुने) प्रतिकूल = बाधक असम्भवात् Tinkling canno refer to a lotus रूपकस्य बाधक sc इनि अभिधीयते न तु etc Thought (मञ्जीरशिञ्जिन) suts the पाद and therefore supports उपमा, it is not designated as साधक of उपमा विध्युपमादन etc. Gives the reason why of two possible designa tions in such cases the designation बाधक is preferred to साधक बाधक is negative in effect Negation is stronger than affirmation, because it sets at naught (उपमर्दिन् ) the thing affirmed (विधि) We deny some thing that is affirmed and hence denial has greater force than affirm ation So when a circumstance deres something and affirms another, it is natural to designate it more on the basis of its capacity to deny than its capacity to affirm. Read मा च—प्रधानेन व्यपदेशाभान्ति इति न्यायात्। साधकत्वुपमया वषिष्ठत्वेन उत्वटतया प्रवर्ते बाघक प्रधानम्। विध्युपमर्दिन बाधकस्य A बाधक because it sets at naught तदपेक्षया उत्कण्ठत्वेन प्रतिपत्ते Is तदपेक्षया उत्स्टपेन प्रतिपते is felt with greater emphasis than साधक (तदपेक्षय =साधकापेक्षया) एवम् अन्यत्र सुधिमि परीक्ष्यम् The antelligent ones सहृदयऽ should acrutinise साधक and बाधक, on these lines elsewhere also अन्यत्र Elsewhere e in other cases of doubt about figures

This is also true of a doubt about उपमा and रूपक they say One must have sufficient acumen and one is sure to decide in favour of one figure to the exclusion of the other

 The school seems to think of what is possible only in theory and not practice All later आलकारिकऽ have recogmsed सदेहसङ्कर

 § सू ८६ स्फुटमेकत्र etc. Defines the third vanety of सङ्कर, known as एकपदप्रतिपाद्य सङ्कर Construe -एकत्र विषये स्फुट शब्दार्यालकृतिद्वय व्यवस्थित च (सङ्कर भवति।) एक विषये in the same place Note that the मूत्र does not refer to एकन्मिन् पदे as the वृत्ति does Later आल्कारिकऽ like रुय्यक, विश्वनाथ and अप्पय्यतीक्षित also follow the सूत्र when they recognise एकवाचकानु प्रविष्ठ सङ्कर का प्र here reduces two varieties admitted by उद्भट to one उद्भट had एक वाक्ये शब्दार्थालकारयो सङ्कर and एक वाक्यांशे शब्दार्थीलवारयो सङ्कर, for both of which का प्र invented एकविषयानुप्रविष्ट शब्दार्थालकारयो सङ्कर which was the same as एकवाचकानुप्रविष्ठसङ्कर of later आलङ्कारिकऽ But later आलङ्कारिकऽ beginning with रूपक widened its scope to include two शब्दालङ्कारऽ and two अर्थालङ्कारऽ also while उद्भट and का प्र confined it to one शब्दालकार and one अर्थालङ्कार present in the same place \[See below \] स्फुट clearly Not ambiguously as an सन्देहसङ्कर स्फुट ( निश्चयेन) stands in antithesis to अनिश्चय of the precedingसूत्र Note that when we have a शाब्दालङ्कार and another of अर्थालङ्कार both are bound to be sharply marked off one from the other एकत्र विषये The वृत्ति specifically confines the scope of विषय to प This naturally rules out एकस्मिन् वाक्ये शाब्दार्थालङ्कार सङ्कर admitted by उद्भट Because वाक्यगत सङ्कर like this Is not different from शब्दार्थालंवृत्तिद्वय Note how the word द्वयु makes it clear that का प्र like उद्भट conceived this variety as confined to oneशब्दालङ्कार and one अर्थालङ्कार The varieties of two शब्दालङ्कारऽ and twoअर्थालङ्कारऽ each given by रुय्यक and others were later innovations व्यवस्थित That present themselves (in the same विषय ) च indicates that this too is a variety of सङ्कर

 वृत्ति—अभिन्न एवं पदे Specifies the scope of एकत्र विषये of the सूत्र This was quite necessary Because विषय may also stand for वाक्य and वाक्यगत शब्दार्थालङ्कारद्वय comes under ससृष्ठि Read मा च— शब्दार्थवति अलङ्कार सवरस्तु उद्भटोक्त ससृष्टिरेव। But पदे may be taken elastically to stand for वाक्यांश which may mean one पद or more but part of a वाक्य —not the wholeउद्भट , as seen above had वाक्यांश and, as the वृत्ति here zunterprets विषय of the सूत्र as एकत्र so प्रतिहारेन्दुराज explans वाक्यांश as

एकशब्द Later आलङ्कारिकऽ are not clear as to whether वाक्य comes within the purview of their एकवाचकानुप्रविष्ट सङ्कर One thing is clear that वाक्यगत अलङ्कार standing independently are nothing but संसृष्टि

 शब्दार्थालङ्कारा 1 e one शब्दालङ्कार and one अर्थालङ्कार रुय्यक and others admit varieties with two शब्दालङ्कारऽ and two अर्थालङ्कारऽ also Thus राजति तटीयमभिहत् etc (St 181 ) is proposed by रुय्यक as illustrating एकवाचकानु प्रविष्टशब्दालङ्कारयो सङ्कर It must be said to the credit of the सूत्र and वृत्ति here that by confining the scope to एकपद and to one शब्दालङ्कार and one अर्थालङ्कार this variety is sharply marked off from संसृष्टि The varieties of two शब्दालङ्कारऽ and two अर्थालङ्कारऽ each recognised by रुय्यक, on the other hand may be easily confused with संसृष्टि व्यवस्था समासादयत व्यवस्थित of the सूत्र explained present themselves

 st 184 स्पष्टोल्लसत् etc Illustrates एकपादप्रतिपाद्य सङ्कर The close of the day pictured as the closing of a lotus Construe—अथो स्पष्टाल्लसात्करणकेसरसूर्यबिम्वविस्तीर्णकर्णिक श्लिष्टाष्टदिग्दलकलापसु-खावतारबद्धाधकारमधुपावलि दिवसा रविन्द सचुकोच। अधो = अध स्पष्टोल्लसत् etc adj of दिवसारविन्द स्पष्ट उल्लसन्त किरणा एव केसरा यस्य सूर्यबिम्ब एव विस्तीर्णकर्णिका यस्य The lotus of day of which the filaments are the rays shooting up conspicuously and of which the ample pericarp ( कर्णिका ) is the orb of the sun श्लिष्टाष्ट etc adj of ....भारविन्दं श्लिष्ट अष्टदिश एव दलकलाप तस्य ( °कलापस्य ) मुखे अवतारे बद्धा अन्धकार एव मधुपावलि यस्य (........) In which the row of bees in the form of darkness gets enclosed as it gets into (अवतार) the interior of the numerous tight petals in the form of the eight quarters When the lotus closes the bee gets enclosed therein The lotus of day also enclosed the darkness

 Here in किरणकेसर we have the शब्दालङ्कार अनुप्रास of ... and .... and अर्थालङ्कार of रूपक—किरणा एव केसरा This is therefore सङ्कर (३)

 In सूर्यबिम्बविस्तीर्णकर्णिक also we have अनुप्रास of र् ण क and रूपकविम्बमेव विस्तीर्णकर्णिा So in दिग्दलकलाप we have अनुप्रास of द्-द् and ल्-ल् These are two more cases of सङ्कर ( ३ ) Thus in all we have thres cases of सङ्कर (३) in the stanza. Note that we have also समस्तवस्तुविषय रूपक here

 एकपदानुप्रविष्टौ एकपद VIZ किरणकेसर, सूर्यबिम्वविस्तीर्णकणिक and दिग्दलकलाप

 § सू ८७ तेनासौ etc. Therefore ( i e in view of the divisions given ) it is threefold असौ = सङ्कर त्रिरूप (1) अङ्गाङ्गि or अनुग्राह्य-अनुग्राहक (2) सन्देह (3) एकपदप्रतिपाद्य विप्रकार एव Only in three varieties एव and the following प्रकारान्तरेण etc. are possibly meant to एव प्रकारान्तरेण contradict the view of रुद्रट Who said—एषां तु चतुर्णामपि सकीना स्यु

अगणिता भेदा। (१० २४) प्रकारान्तरेण etc It is not possible to classify (व्याकर्तुं) सङ्कर in any other way, because that would give infinite varieties See रुद्रट cited above रुद्रट spoke of different vaneties of सङ्कर based on its embodying this figure and that But this principle will gove us innumerable vaneties of सङ्कर, (आनन्त्यात् तत्प्रभेदानाम्) as रुद्रट himself admitted Hence, says our author, it is more convenient to divide the figure सङ्कर in the way he has done

 इति प्रतिपादिता Said with ref to the figures in उल्लासऽ IX-X in IX, there are शब्दालङ्कारऽ and in X अर्थालङ्कारऽ dealt with What are उभयालङ्कारऽ? सा चू admits that they are not many \[ औमयिकाना अप्राचुर्येऽपि \] उभयालङ्कारऽ like श्लिष्ट परम्परित रूपक, श्लिष्ट समासोक्ति or अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, लाटानुप्रास ( IX ), पुनरुक्तवदाभाम ( उ IX ), श्लिष्ट विरोध etc All अर्थालङ्कारऽ it may be seen will become उभालङ्कारऽ if श्लेष enters into them काव्यप्रकाश nowhere deals with उभयालङ्कारऽ as a class भोज ( in स क ) has a whole chapter on उभयालङ्कारऽ and they are none other than our अर्थालङ्कारऽ like उपमा This is due to भोजऽ concept of उभयालङ्कार being broader शब्देभ्य य पदार्थेभ्य उपमादि प्रतीयते। विशिष्टोऽयं कवीना ता उभयालङ्क्रिया प्रिया॥( स क ४१ ) Judged by this standard उपमा in so far as it requires words like इव or अपह्नुति requiring words like छल will be उभयालङ्कारऽ \[छलादिशब्दहेतुवा अपह्नुतिप्रभृतयश्च—स प्र\] In the following para our author sets forth his view about उभयालङ्कारऽ

 कुत पुन etc कुत पुन व्यवस्थाप्यते।—The आलङ्कारिकऽ have divided अलङ्कारऽ into three classes those of शब्द those of अर्थ and those of both An objector might question the validity of this threefold division
कार्यभेदात् कारणभेद is the rule and since, what are divided as शब्दालङ्कारऽ, अर्थालङ्कारऽ, and उभयालङ्कारऽ equally produce the same effect viz of lending charm to poetry \[तुल्येऽपि काव्यशोभातिशयहेतुत्वे\], the objector might urge that the threefold division \[ नियम v / प्रतिनियम \] is unwarranted

 Our author refers the objector back to the principle that was enunciated in उ IX, where it was stated that the classification of figures excellences (गुण) and defects is governed by the principle of Presence and Absence \[अन्वय व्यतिरेकौ\] In simpler words it was laid down in उ IX that an अलङ्कार will be शब्दालङ्कार if it occurs when a particular शब्द or शब्दऽ are there failing which it does not present itself It would be अर्थालङ्कारऽ if it occurs when a particular अर्थ or अर्थऽ are there failing which it does not appear. It will be उभयालङ्कार case its presence or absence is determined by the particular शब्दऽ and अर्थऽ both.

 उभयस्य i e शब्दस्य अर्थस्य च. उक्तमत्र In उ IX, while discussing श्लेष, मम्मट says—ननु स्वरितादिगुणभेदात् मित्रप्रयत्नोच्चार्याणा तदभावात् अभिन्नप्रयत्नोच्चार्याणां च शब्दाना बन्धे अलङ्कारान्तरप्रतिभोत्पत्तिहेतु शब्दश्लेष अर्थशेषश्च इति दिविधोऽपि अय अर्थालङ्कार मध्ये गणिनोऽन्यैरिति कथमय शब्दालङ्कार। उच्यते। इह दोषगुणालङ्काराणां शब्दार्थगतत्वेन यो विभाग स अन्वयव्यतिरेकाभ्यामेव व्यवतिष्ठते। अन्वयव्यतिरेकौ एव प्रभवत The presence (अन्वय) and absence (व्यतिरेक) of शब्द and अर्थ are alone the determining factor in the classification (व्यवस्था) of अलङ्कारऽ, गुणऽ and दोषऽ as शब्दालङ्कारऽ, अर्थाल्ङ्कारऽ etc. निमित्तान्तरस्य अभावात् For want of any other circumstance, to account for the classification Others say, howe-ver, that it is not अन्वयव्यतिरेक but आश्रय आश्रयिभाव that governs this classification {See below} ततश्च etc योऽलङ्कार यदीयौ अन्वयव्यतिरेकौ अनुविधत्ते स तदलकार A figure will pertain to that viz शब्द, अर्थ or शब्दार्थ, whose presence and absence determine its own presence or absence अनुविधत्ते follows, is governed by एवं च यथा etc एवं च ie judged by thus standard of अन्वय व्यतिरेक पुनरुक्तवदाभास etc पुनरुक्तवदाभास and परम्परितरूपक are शब्दार्थालङ्कारऽ because their presence or absence is determined by the presence or absence of शब्द and अर्थ both. पुनरुक्तवदाभास Semblance of Tautology Dealt with in उ IX. Defined as पुनरुक्तवदाभासो विभिन्नाकारशन्दगा। एकार्थतेव. It consists in the use of two words, one of which appears tautologous (पुनरुक्त) but is not really so, since it is used in a sense other than the one that would make it tautologous, thus तनुवपु is पुनरुक्तवदाभास, because तनु and वपु appear as synonyms but here तनु is used in the other sense of ‘slender,’ the meaning being ‘one of slim body’ If तनु were to mean ‘body, there would have arisen the पुनरुक्ततादोष or tautology पुनरुक्तवदाभास can be of शब्द only or of शब्दार्थऽ together In the present discussion the reference is to the latter viZ शब्दार्थमूलक पुनरुक्तवदाभास तनुवपु is शब्दार्थमूलक, because it depends on the use of particular words used in different sens-es परम्परितरूपक Ref is to श्लिष्ट परम्परितरूपक like विद्वन्मानसहस which also is उभयालङ्कार \[Vide Notes pp 57-58\] तथा शब्दहेतुकार्थान्तरन्यासप्रभृतयोऽपि द्रष्टव्या Just as परम्परितरूपक and पुनरुक्त-वदाभास are उभयालङ्कारऽ, so also अर्थान्तरन्यास based upon the use of a particular word like दाक्षिण्य ( 1 southerliness, 2 gatiantry ) in उत्पादयति लोकस्य प्रीतिं मल्यमारुत। ननु दाक्षिण्यसपन्न सर्वस्य भवति प्रिय॥ ( दण्डिन्का २ १७४ ) Trnvendrum Edition has a reading हिशब्दहेतुकार्थान्तरन्यास. We have seen \[ p 166 Notes Essential 7 \] how दि features generally in अर्थान्तरन्यास With this reading, it would seem that our author, like भोज, takes a broader view of उभयालङ्कारऽ. For, if an अर्थान्तरन्यास depending upon a wond like हि becomes उभयालङ्कार, a श्रौती

उपमा depending upon इव and अपह्नुति depending upon छल etc will be उभयालङ्कारऽ With the other reading without हि viz शब्दहेतुक etc, the view about उभयालङ्कारऽ becomes limited to those based on श्लेष Because पुनरुक्तवदाभ्यास, श्लिष्टपरम्परित रूपक and शब्दहेतुकअर्थान्तरन्यास all have शब्दश्लेष them Of the two readings ‘शब्दहेतुक’ appears preferable The idea in काव्यप्रकाश about उभयालङ्कार is confined to cases having शब्दश्लेष in them And so शब्दहेतुकअर्थान्तरन्यास refers to such as is based on श्लेष अर्थस्य etc परम्परितरूपक अर्थान्तरन्यास,based on हि etc though उभयालङ्कारऽ are dealt with as अर्थालङ्कार (वाच्य=अर्थ) because it is the strikingness of sense that is conspicuous in them पुनरुक्तवदाभ्यास is dealt with among शब्दालङ्कार—not वाच्यालङ्कर वस्तुस्थिति अनपेक्ष्यैव ie though as a matter of fact, they are उभयालङ्कारऽ , they have found a place among अर्थालङ्कार, without regard for actual facts वस्तुस्थिति is that they are उभयालङ्कारऽ

 योऽलङ्कार etc. We have seen that acc to the view of our author, शाब्दत्व etc of अलङ्कारऽ is gove-rned by अन्यय व्यतिरेक. This view is some what different from the view of others who say that शाब्दत्व etc is determined by आश्रय-आश्रयिभाव and not अन्यय व्यतिरेक They say that a शब्दाश्रित अलङ्कार is अर्थाश्रित is अर्थालङ्कार उभयाश्रित is उभयालङ्कार

 Our author does not openly call into question the validity of आश्रयाश्रयिभाव as a principle of division He only suggests that even in आश्रयाश्रयिभाव, अन्वयव्यतिरेक does assert itself His argument seems to be that when the other school says that in शब्दालङ्कार, for instance शब्द is आश्रय does it not suggest that the figure will be there if the आश्रय is there and the figure will not be there if the आश्रय not there? Is this not अन्वय-व्यतिरेक of a particular type (विशिष्ट) So closely scruti mused even the आश्रयाश्रयिभाव of the other school becomes अन्वय-व्यतिरेक of a special class This proves therefore how अन्वय-व्यतिरेक is a principle broad enough to comprehend आश्रयाश्रयिभाव and that is why it should be accepted, urges the काव्यप्रकाश योऽलङ्कार यदाश्रित e g य अलङ्कार शब्दाश्रित स शब्दाल्ङ्कार। तदाश्रयणमन्तरेण=अन्वय व्यतिरेवाश्रयणमन्तरेण, यथोक्तनिमित्त एव परस्परव्यतिरेको ज्यायान्—It is more cogent (ज्यायान्) to distinguish the different classes of figures from each other on the basis of अन्वय-व्यतिरेक as already given in उ IX व्यतिरेक = भेद।

 Relation betiween आश्रयाश्रयिभाव and अन्वय-व्यतिरेक—On the face of it, the two appear to be almost identical relations Just as between तन्तुऽ and पट, there as आश्रयाश्रयिभाव तन्तु berng आश्रय, पट आश्रयिन्, so also there is अन्वय-व्यतिरेक that determines their relation the अन्वय or व्यतिरेक of तन्तुऽ determining the अन्वय-व्यतिरेक of पट And when it is bome in

mind that आश्रय आश्रितऽ are here *tnseparably* related (eg शब्दालङ्कार is so because inseparably connected with शब्द ), and not separably con nected as in the case of देवदत्त the आश्रित and गृह the आश्रय, the difficulty of distinguishing between आश्रयाश्रयिभाव and अन्वयव्यतिरेक becomes obvious Just as अन्वय-व्यतिरेक determines the causal relation between two things so आश्रयाश्रयिभाव determnes it This shows the futility of the suggestion made by रुय्यक and endorsed by जयरथ when they say that अन्वयव्यतिरेक features in causation, आश्रयाश्रयिभाव features in location जयरथ misses the point when he gives the analogy of कर्णभूषण कर्णभूषण, he argues, is so called not because it is governed by the अन्वय-व्यतिरेक of कर्ण but because its आश्रय is कर्ण The instance is fallacious When शब्दालङ्कार is so called it is because it is made of शब्द and that is the idea underlying आश्रयाश्रयिभाव in अलङ्कार, as पट is made of तन्तुऽ and is therefore described as तन्त्वाश्रित Whereas कर्णालङ्कार is not made of कर्ण, so that it could be कर्णाश्रित tn the same sense in which शब्दालङ्कार is शब्दाश्रित The relation between them is not inseparable as that between शब्द and शब्दालङ्कार

 Read रय्यक—लेकवदाश्रयाश्रयिभावश्च तत्तदलङ्कारनिवधनम्। अन्यन्यव्यतिरेकौ तु त... प्रयोतकौ। न तदलङ्कारते। तदलङ्कारत्वप्रयो..वत्वे तु श्रौतोपमादेरपि शब्दालङ्कारत्वप्रसङ्गात्। तस्मादाश्रयाश्रयिभावेनैव चिरन्तनम-तानुसृतिरिति भद्रम्। See जयरथ's comments—लेके हि योऽलङ्कारो यदाश्रित स तदलङ्कारतयोच्यते, यथा कुण्डलदि वर्णाद्याश्रित तदलङ्कार। एव मिहापि शब्दाद्याश्रितस्तदलङ्कार इति सिद्ध एवं विषयविभागरूप प्रतिनियम। यत्तु अन्यै अन्वय व्यतिरेकौ तदलङ्कारनिबन्धनत्वेनोक्तौ तद्युक्तमवन्याह—अन्वयेति। एव हि श्रौतोपमाणाम् इवादिश-ब्दान्वय व्यतिरेकानुवर्तनात् तत्कार्यमव, न पुन तदलङ्कारत्वम्। तस्याविशेषात्। अर्थस्य पुन अलङ्कृत्वं तदलङ्का-रत्वमेव युक्तमिति तापर्यार्थ। जयरथ says elsewhere—न पुन अन्वयव्यतिरेकाभ्याम्। ताभ्या हि यस्य यद्धेतुक-त्व नम्य तत्वार्यत्व स्यान्न पुन तदलङ्कारत्वम्। लोके हि यथा वर्णाश्रितकुण्डलादि वर्णालङ्कार उच्यते न पुन सुवर्णका-रणाहेतुत्वात् तदलङ्कार।

 Note that there is no force in the suggestion that if अलङ्कारऽ were to be governed by अन्वय-व्यतिरेक, श्रौतोपमा will be शब्दालङ्कार, because it depends on इव Thus objection is pure sophistry, because श्रौतोपमा really speaking, is not determined by the presence or absence of the word इव It is determined by the अन्वय-व्यतिरेक of the sense of इव and so we can have श्रौतोपमा even with a used in the sense of इव How could one pretend then that श्रौतोपमा will be शब्दालङ्कार because of इव etc.

 From this discussion, it will appear that अन्वय-व्यतिरेक and आश्रयाश्रयिभाव are practically the same. उद्भट seems to have been the first to sponsor आश्रयाश्रयिभाव, though indications of this are not to be found in his काव्यालङ्कार जयरथ says that thus आश्रयाश्रयिभाव has been developed in detail by राजानकतिलक in उद्भटविवेक \[ Read एतस्य .... राजानकतिलकेन ..... ..... इति ग्रन्थ..... .....\]....... Deems to be

referring to and contradicting this view, proposing अन्वय व्यतिरेक instead. As we saw, रुय्यक is for आश्रयाश्रयिभाव विश्वनाथ and जगन्नाथ are non-com mittal. अप्पय्यदीक्षित’s view could be tentatively guessed by looking to the fact that सभग and अभगरेष are both taken by him as अलङ्कारऽ Does It mean that he was for आश्रयाश्रयिभाव ? He refers to चित्रमीमांसा on the point But unfortunately the extant portion of the work does not contain क्षेत्र Read वैद्यनाथ तत्सत्- उत्प्रेक्षाग्रन्थानन्तर चित्रमीमांसा न क्वापि दृश्यते।

अलङ्कारदोषऽ

 काव्यप्रकाशकार, after having dealt with the शब्दालङ्कारऽ and अर्थालङ्कारऽ, proceeds to tackle the question of अलङ्कारदोषऽ Corresponding to the शब्दालङ्कारऽ and अर्थालङ्कारऽ it is possible to have शब्दालङ्कारदोषऽ and अर्थालङ्कारदोषऽ And gong bach to the days of **भामह** and **दण्डिन्** we do find अलङ्कारिकऽ attempting to ascertain the दोषऽ of figures भामह refers to मेधाविन्ऽ view about seven उपमादोषऽ हीनतासभवो लिङ्गवचोभेदो विपर्यय। उपमानाधिकत्व च तेनासदृशतापि च॥त एते उपमादोषा सप्त मेधाविनादिता। (भा अ २ ३९ ४० )—(1) हीनता (2) असभव (3) लिङ्गभेद (4) वचनभेद (5) विपर्यय, (6) उपमानाधिकत्व, (7) उपमानासदृशता **भामह** is Critical of some of them **दण्डिन्** only makes a passing reference to and criticises लिङ्गवचनभेद and हीनता and अधिकत्व in उपमा **रुद्रट** admitted four उपमादोषऽ (1) सामान्यशब्दभेद, (2) वैषम्य, (3) असभव (4) अप्रसिद्धि in place of मेधाविन्'s seven वामन admits six उपमादोषऽ almost on the lines of मेधाविन् They are (1) हीनत्व (2) अधिकत्व, (3 4)लिङ्ग-वचनभेद (5) असादृश्य (6) असभव

 When the **काव्यप्रकाशकार** refers to उपमादोषऽ, he seems to refer to practically the same दोषऽ as were discovered by मेधाविन् But it is not clear as to who was responsible for stating the अनुप्रासदोषऽ which काव्यप्रकाश discusses It seems likely that some earlier आलङ्कारिक had referred to them when we take into consideration the names given to them प्रसिद्ध्यभाव, वृत्तिविरोध, वैफल्य, which seem to have tradition The दोष of यमक VIZ पादनययमन may be of the discovery of the का प्र दण्डिन्ऽ different varieties of यमक actually in clude one based upon पादनययमन behind them And का प्र alone seems to condemn it The दोषऽ in उत्प्रेक्षा, समासोक्ति, and अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा are again likely to be of the discovery of the का प्र because no conventional name was given to these दोषऽ

 Whatever be the दोषऽ of शब्दालङ्कार or अर्थालङ्कार the काव्यप्रकाश proves that they are ultimately the same as शब्ददोषऽ or अर्थदोषऽ and thus no separate अलङ्कारदोषऽ need be admitted according to it



**अलङ्कारदोषाः**



<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1711005833KAVYAPRAKASHA.png"/>









                                
<MISSING_FIG href="../books_images/U-IMG-1712138830123.png"/>

 §सू ८८. एषां दोषा etc. Construe एषा यथायोग सभवन्तोऽपि केचन दोषा उत्तेषु अन्तर्भवन्ति इति न पृथक् प्रतिपादिता। एषां I. c. of शब्दालङ्कारऽ and अर्थालङ्कार सभवन्तोऽपि केचन दोषाः अपि shows that the author admits that अलङ्कारदोषऽ are possible, but his contention is that they can well come under शब्ददोषऽ or अर्थदाषऽ केचन दोषा. See the scheme gaven above उक्तेषु अन्तर्भवन्ति They can be comprehended by those शब्ददोषऽ and अर्थदोषऽ already specified in उ VII. See the scheme न पृथक प्रतिपादिता In the earlier works, भामह only referred to मेधाविन्'s seven उपमादोषऽ. दण्डिन् seems to casually refer to three or four of the same दोषऽ. उद्भट has no अलङ्कारदोषऽ वामन has only उपमादोषऽ on the lines of मेधाविन् रुद्रट has four दोषऽ of his own. So it is evident that nesther भामह, nor दण्डिन्, nor was उद्भट nterested in अलङ्कारदोषऽ And when the काव्यप्रकाशकार reduces the so-called अलङ्कारदोषऽ to शब्ददोषऽ or अर्थदोषऽ, he seems to be following in the footsteps of his redoubtable predecessors.

**शब्दालङ्कारदोष**

 वृत्तिः— Two शब्दालंरकारऽ referred to अनुप्रास and यमक.

**1 अनुप्रासदोषऽ**

 ** (1) प्रसिद्ध्यभाव**—Absence of established usage or convention. प्रसिद्धि is convention. When, therefore, something unwaranted by convention happens to be stated, only to secure an alliterative effect, we get the दोष, प्रसिद्ध्यभाव, of अनुप्रास, This is reducable to प्रसिद्धिविरुद्धता here an अर्थदोष in उ VII ( thoughit ts also a शब्ददोष ).

 St. 185 चक्री etc Illustrates प्रसिद्ध्यभाव of अनुप्रास A benedictory stanza addressed to the chanot of the sun. Construe:-यस्य ( रथस्य ) चक्रारपङ्क्ति चक्री स्तौति, यस्य हरीन् हरि अपि स्तौति, यस्य धू...ज्ञान्तात् धूर्जटि स्तौति, यस्य अक्ष नक्षत्रनाथ स्तौति,यस्त अरुण वरुण, यस्य कुवराग्रं कुवर स्तौति, यस्य रह सुरा-णां सध स्तौति, स जगदुपकृतये नित्ययुक्तस्य अहिमरुचे (सूर्यस्य) स्यन्दन प्रीतिप्रसन्न (सन्) a ...... चक्रारपं-क्ति wheel and numerous spokes ( अर m. ) Read ‘wheel’ for wheels’ in the Translation Sun's chariot has got one wheel only. चक्री विष्णु हरीन् The seven bay steeds of सूर्य हरि इन्द्र धूर्ध्वजान्त The top of the yoke-banners. धूर्जटि=शिव \[ धूर्-सारभूता जटि (जटा) यस्य \].क्ष (m.) axle. नक्षत्रनाथ = चन्द्र यस्य अरणम् अरुण is सूर्य's सारथि कुयराग्र - यूश्चर (m.n. ) The pole of the chanot, to which the yoke is fixed ..... { Accu Sing इदम् n. ) Speed प्रीतिप्रमदा Pleased due to gratification. जगदुपकृतये नित्ययुक्तस्य can be better taken as qualityang .... than .....( रथस्य ) The Translation takes the latter.

 Here the different gods are pressed into the service of the chanot only with an eye on the अनुप्रास resulting therefrom. There

 isअनुप्रास of अम्-अम् in रह and सद्य also To take an instance, विष्णु is described as praising the wheel etc simply to secure the अनुप्रास of चक्री-चक्र. विष्णु and others are not प्रसिद्ध in पुराणऽ, महाभारत (= इतिहास ) etc, as being सातृऽ of the सूप्ररथात्रयवऽ

 कर्तृकर्मप्रतिनियमेन स्तुति Uniform praise with particular gods a subjects and particular charnot parts as objects \[ प्रतिनियम Umformity\]
