 (ii) अनुप्रासस्य वैफल्य i e futility of alliteration anses where alliteration does not fend any charm to the theme This is reducable to अपुत्रर्थता—an अर्थदोष अपुष्टार्थता is irrelevancy यस्य अनुपादानऽपि प्रतिपाद्यमान अर्थ न बाध्यते To have futile alliteration means to base it on words that do not bear up-on the central theme, and this is the same as अपुष्टार्थता

 Sts 186-187 भण तहणि etc Illustrates वैफल्य of अनुप्रास युग्म A disappointed lover expresses his annoyance when the नायिका goes to the house of her beloved Construe—आन दस्यन्दिमुन्द-रेन्दुमुखि सल्लीलोल्लापिनि तरुणि, भण, यदि त्वं रमणमन्दिर गच्छसि तदा अरुणचरणे अनणुरणन्मणिमेखल अविरतशिञ्जान मञ्जुमञ्जीर तव परिसरण मे किम् अकारण रणरणरक कुरुते। आनन्द etc You whose face is charming hke the moon that diffuses delight. सल्लीलोल्लापिनि speaking very gracefully अविरत et-c with the sweet anklet tinkling constantly परिसरण = Movement रणरणक (m n ) anxiety, worry. The stanza is given in रद्रटालङ्कार (२ २२-२३ \]

 Here the author of का प्र suggests that the अनुप्रास does not contnbute to the charm of the वाच्य =अर्थ and hence it Is अपुष्टार्थता वाच्यस्य अर्थस्य विचिन्त्यमान चाव charm that could be imagined From Ed reads विवेच्यमान charm that could be discerned

 Here the का प्र criticizes as faulty that very instance which रुद्रट gives to illustrate मधुरा वृत्ति of अनुप्रास And looking to the stanzas the criticism appears unjustifiable आनन्दस्यन्दिमुन्दरेन्दुमुखि सल्लीलोल्लापिनि अनणुरणन्मणिमेखल अविरतशिञ्जानमञ्जुमञ्जीर are अनुप्रासऽ that are embodied in words that are significant enough to bear on the central theme viz. आत्सुक्यवर्धन The same is true also of the line परिसरणमरुणचरणे रणरणचमवारण कुरुते Looking to this criticism one is reminded of दण्डिन्ऽ criticism of अनुप्रास which he says bings about पारुष्य and शैथिल्य of composition अतो नैवमनुप्रासं दक्षिणा या प्रयुञ्जते। says दण्डिन् १६० अपुष्टार्थतैव etc. Construe अनुप्रासस्य वैफल्यम् अपुष्टार्थता एव।

 (iii) अनुप्रासस्य वृत्तिविरोध वृत्तिऽ are styles modes रुद्रट speaks of पत्र वृतिऽ of अनुप्रास ( २ १० ) अनुप्रासस्य वृतविरोध, therefore, amounts

to the employment of such alliteration as is not in tune with the theme Particular themes demand particular types of अनुप्रासऽ, soft or hard मम्मट illustrates such रसाननुगुण जनुप्रास in उ VII. Soft अनुप्रास will tone down वीररस, and hard अनुप्रास will appear Jarring in शृङ्गार Such वृत्तिविरोध of अनुप्रास is reducible to प्रतिकूलवर्णता, acc to का. प्र. प्रतिकूवर्णता is a शब्ददोष It consists in the use of syllables that are not suited for particular रसऽ रसाननुगुण प्रतिकूलवर्णम् Note that the name वृत्तिविरोध, as given to the defect, leaves an impression of being traditional.

 st 188 अकुण्टोत्कण्ठया etc llustrates अनुप्रासस्य वृत्तिविरोध A lovers request to the go between, to bring about his meeting with the नायिका Construe—हे कलकण्ठि, अकुण्ठोत्कण्ठया आकण्ठ पूर्णं मा क्षण कम्बुकण्ठ्या कण्ठे कुरु। कण्ठार्तिमुद्धर (च)। **कलकण्ठि** addressed to the दूती An apt epithet for those *messengers* of love Once अग्निमित्र talks of the दता, बकुलावलिका, as मधुरवा परभृतिका। **अकुण्ठोत्कण्ठया आकण्ठ पूर्ण मा** me who am filled to the brim with irresistible (**अकुण्ठ**) yearning क्षण for a moment कम्बुकण्ट्या कण्ठे कुरु Give me an opportumty for the कण्ठारेष of that lovely lady कम्बुकण्ठी=कम्बुग्रावा whose neck in wateness and bulging shape resembles the conch (कम्बु m ) कण्ठार्ति i e वण्ठाश्लषौत्सुक्य The eagerness for embrace and the consequent uneasiness उद्धर rid (me) of

 Here there is the अनुप्रास of the harsh रु which is ill-suited for the delicate love making This, which others (?) style as वृत्तिविरोध is none else than प्रतिकृतवर्णता i e use of syllables out of tune with the prevailing sentiment

 **अत्र शृङ्गारे** The शृङ्गार in the stanza is too patent **परपवर्णाडम्बर** the pompous use of the harsh syll-ables viz, आडम्बर suggests that such syllables become an encumbrance ट-वर्ग was always looked upon as ill-suited for producing a delicate effect like शृङ्गार Note how रुद्रट excludes टवर्ग from प्रौढा वृत्ति अन्त्य-टवर्गात् मुक्त्वा (२ २४) पूर्वोक्तरीत्या विरुध्यते Is found inconsistent with शृङ्गार, in the way already given in उ VII, where मम्मट gives different illustrations to show how certain syllables are not in tune with certain रसऽ अकुण्ठोत्कण्ठया also figures there as an illustration of प्रतिकूट-वर्णत in शृङ्गार अन प्रतिकूलवर्णतैव वृत्तिविरोधHere what is looked upon as वृत्तिविरोध is प्रतिकूलवर्णता itself

**II यमकदोष**

 यमक is, as we say, a वर्णमघात repeating itself in different senses लकलो/लकलो was यमक in St 176. Here the अलङ्कारदोष of having the यमक in three out of four quarters of a stanza is brought under the दोष of अप्रयुक्तत्व.

 पादत्रयगतत्वेन यमन To put it in three out of four quarters of a stanza It should be noted that of the several varieties of यमक recognised as poetical, in काव्यादर्श ३, one actually consisted in पादत्रययमन cp the instance करणे ते रणेष्वन्तकरणे द्विपतां हता। करणेव क्षरद्रता भान्ति सध्यायना इव॥ काव्यादर्श ३ २६ And this very class of यमकऽ comes to be condemned here अप्रयुक्तव दोष It may be a शब्ददोष or अर्थदोष It consists in the use of words etc. not generally in use It is explained in उ VII as तथा आम्नानमपि कविभि न आदृतम्—What is not in poetical vogue, though it may have the sanction of ancient authorities पादत्रययमन may have the sanction of दण्डिन् and others but it is not an vogue and so is अप्रयुक्त, says the काव्यप्रकाश

 St 189 भुतगमस्येव etc Illustrates यमकस्य पादत्रययमन The wily Jure away the heart of men and bring them into troubles is भुतगमस्येव etc. like a snake's jewel that is full of lustre सदम्भा Nom Sing मत् अम्भ (= तत् ) यस्य cp Marathi पाणीगर b ग्राहाबकीर्णेव etc Like a river that has good water but is infested by crocodiles सदग्भा Nom Sing. Fem. सम्म्भस् = साधुनम् cd Construe - सदम्भा दुरततां निर्णयन अपि रत्नो चेत प्रसभ वर्षान्त The wily ones (म + दम्भ ) lure away ‘he heart of a person (जन्तु) even when he realises that they would lead him to disaster (दुरलता)

 Here the ..... VIZ सदम्भा repeats itself in three (abd) out of four पादऽ This isअप्रयुक्तत्व of यमक, since it is not a fashion to have यमकऽ in three out of four quarter Though as said above, दण्डिन् had विषादयमक, there is surely some sort of abruptness when the यमक occurs like this.

**अर्थालङ्कारदोष**

Four अर्थालङ्कारऽ referred to— यमक,उपमा, समासोक्ति,अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा

**१- उपमादोष (१२)**

 Here by comparing यूय with the निकृष्ट ...., there is no exaltation, but degradation Note that this will be a दोष, only when the poet aims at exalting the उपमेय . But, if the poet's aim is to condemn the act by becoming ironical, it will be a व्याजस्तुति(fनिन्दापर्यवसायिनी), and then there will be no दोष. नमिसाधु (रुद्रट ११, २४.) very aptly remarks तथा योऽपि हीनताधिक्यविशिष्ट विपर्यय उक्त सोऽपि न तावन्मात्रेण दोषहेतु। अतिप्रपप्तगात्अन्यथा हि निन्दास्तुती यत्र चिकीर्षिते भवत तत्रापि यथाक्रम निकृष्टस्य उत्कृष्टस्य च उपमानस्य दुष्टत्व स्यात्।

 (ii) उपमानस्य प्रमाणन्यूनत्व It consists in the उपमान beng too small in size for the उपमेय This also is reducable to अनुचितार्थत्व, Note that, as in the above case, it will not be a दोष if it is व्याज-स्तुति(fनिन्दापर्यवसायिनी) Read नमिसाधु cited above

 वह्निस्फुलिङ्ग etc. Illustrates उपमानस्य प्रमाणन्यूनत्व The sun compared to a fiery spark

 Here it is obvious how the sun, instead of being exalted, as the poet certainly wants to do, is brought into contempt by being compared to a स्फुलिङ्ग

 (iii) उपमानस्य जात्यधिकत्व—It consists in the उपमान belonging to a class too high as compared to the उपमेय This also is reducible to अनुचितार्थत्व Here also there will be no दोष if there is व्याज-स्तुति

 st 190. अय पद्मासनासीन etc Illustrates उपमानस्य जात्यधिकत्व. A चक्रवाक compared to ब्रह्मा पद्मास-नासीन The चक्रवधू is perched on a lotus ब्रह्मान्ऽ seat is lotus वेधस्=ब्रह्मा

 Here the चक्रवाक is compared with ब्रह्मा , when about to create the world The चक्रवाक belongs to a class too trivial as compared to the eminent class of ..... Hence उपमानस्य जात्यधिकत्व which can well come under अनुचितार्थत्व But when the poet wants to regale us with an anti-climax, there is no दोष Pope's ‘Rape of the Lock’ involves no दोष।  

 (iv) उपमानस्य प्रमाणाधिकत्व It consists in the उपमान being too big in size as compared to the उपमेय Also reducible to अनुचितार्थत्व If it is a व्याजस्तुति , however no दोष

 St 191 पातालमिव etc Illustrates उपमानस्य प्रमाणाधिकत्व A poet compliments a नायिका on the beauty of her beautiful limbs पातालमिव to निम्नत्व of the नाभि स्तनी क्षिति...रोपमौ- (See the उदाहरण of पदविषयसार Notes p 262) कालिन्दीपातमग्रिम .............—यमुना known for its black colour

 Here the उपमानऽ, पाताल, क्षितिधर, कालिन्दापात, are too big in size for the particular उपमेयऽ viz नाभि, स्तन, वेणीदण्ड respectively And hence there is a दोष , it is urged which is reducible to अनुचितार्थत्व Is this strictly true? We have already seen from the instance of एकविषयसार, how the idea of क्षितिधरोपमौ स्तनौ is not uncommon in Sanskrit poetry Again we have seen at St 41, how नाभि is described as कुहर In such matters अतिशयोक्ति is bound to feature Nay it must feature Who can set limits to अतिशयोक्ति? And if we become hypercritical in these, matters we might even get startled to find a gu compared to a कमल। Particularly this is true of प्रमाणगताधिक्य भामह says (३ ४९ ५० ५१ )—यस्यातिशयवानर्थं कथ साऽसम्भवो मत। ... चातिशयात्वमुपमात्प्रेक्षया वधा॥and mark the instance he gives पुञ्जाभूतमिव ध्वान्तमष भाति मतङ्गन। सर शरत्प्रसन्नाम्भो न भखण्डमिव ज्झिनम्।Read again अखण्डमण्डल केन्दु क काताननमद्युति। यत्किञ्चित्कान्ति सामान्याच्छशिनैवापमीयत ॥२ ४४ It must be conceded however, that poetic convention is the best standard to judge such cases The उपमानऽ may be अतिशयोक्तिपूर्ण but they must not transgres, the limits of poetic conventions किञ्च काव्यानि नेयानि लक्षणेन महात्मनाम्-Poems are to be understood in the light of the conventions set down by the greatpoets-says भामह (२ ४५)  

 अत्र etc. ...... उपमानै आदि includes बहिस्फुलिङ्ग, ..., पातालितिधर कालिन्दीपात कदथित Is brought into contempt Note that this is not only true of उपमानन्यूनत्व but also उपमानाधिकत्व The चक्रवाक by being compared to वेझस् is equally ....

 (V) उपमानस्य धर्मन्यूनत्व—it consists in the failure of the poet to refer to a particular item (धर्म) in the उपमान when he refers to a corresponding item in the उपमेय This is reducible to हीनपदत्व says the का प्र हीनपदत्व a वाक्यदोष results when a necessary word remains unembodied ther-ein Read in Translation (p 227) So far as the excess or deficiency of attributes (धर्माश्रय) is concerned in place of so far as the inferiority or superiority abiding in the Common Property is concerned

  St 192. स मुनि etc. Illustrates उपमानम्य धर्मन्यूनत्व Asage des cribed as appearing like the sun मौञ्ज्या लाछित a marked by a girdle made of Munja grass कृष्णातिनय.. his garment was the ski-n of a black antelope, व्यरातत् etc. He shone like the sun (अंशुमान्) wrapped in a fragment of da-rk (नील) cloud जीमूत

 Here मुनि corresponds to अनुमान् कृष्णाजिनपत to नीलजीमूत But while the poet has mentioned the मौनी as an item (धर्म) of the उपमेय,

he fails to mention तडित् the corresponding item in the उपमान. thus is therefore उपमानस्य धर्मन्यूनत्व

 (v) **उपमानस्य धर्माधिकत्व**—It consists in the mention of an item of the उपमान, without mentioning the corresponding item of the उपमेय It is reducible to अधिकपदत्व, says का प्र. अधिकपदत्व consists in the use of a पद that is unnecessary

 St 193 **स पीतवासा** etc. Illustrates उपमानस्य धर्माधिकत्व Description of श्रीकृष्ण **पीतवासस्** wearing a yellow garment प्रगृहीतशार्ङ्ग-शार्ङ्ग the bow of विष्णु **मनोज्ञभीर्म वपु **form, charming and awful 2nd half—शनहृदेन्द्रायुधवान् मेघ निशाया शशिना समृज्यमान इव-Iike a cloud coming in contact with (समृज्यमान) the moon, at night, and having lightning (शतहृदा) and a rainbow (इन्द्रायुध) about it

 Here मेघ is उपमान कृष्ण उपमेय शतहृदा of the उपमान, corresponds to the पानवासस् of the उपमेय इन्द्रायुध of the उपमान corresponds to the शाङ्ग of the उपमेय But corresponding to शशिन् of the उपमान , there is nothing stated of the उपमेय when, as a matter of fact शख, a of the उपमेय could well have been mentioned So this is उपमानस्य धर्माधिकत्व which can well come under अधिकपदत्व

 Note that this उपमानस्य धर्माधिकत्व or the preceding धर्मन्यूनत्व are दोषऽ because the poet fails to mention a corresponding धर्म of the उपमेय or उपमान when such धर्म is there In other words, the poet is blamed in such cases for failing to strike a balance between the उपमान and उपमेय even when there is room for it When there is no Toom for such a perfect equality between the उपमान or उपमेय, if the poet mentions some item of the उपमान or उपमा in excess, there will be no दोष Hence does भामह say
-सर्वं सर्वेसु सारुप्य नाम्नि भावस्य कस्यचित्। यथोपपत्ति कृतिभिपमासु प्रयुज्यते॥( २ ४३ )  

 शग्वादेरनिदेशे शशिनो ग्रहणमतिरिच्यते The mention (ग्रहण ) of शशिन् is in excess because the corresponding धर्म viz शख of the उपमेय is not mentioned शखादे आदि is evidently superfluous इत्यस्य will be more appropriate

 VII-VIII **लिङ्गवचनभेदात् दोष**–When there is a divergence between the उपमान or उपमेय, m punh of gender or number, the उपमा sometimes becomes defective Because, such divergence may affect the साधारणधर्म which may cease to be perfectly साधारण In मुखा चन्द्र इव रम्यम्, रम्य goes well with the उपमेय, but does not grammatically fit in with चन्द्र which is masculine.Thus what is looked upon as a साधारण

धर्म is found not to be in perfect grammatical accord with the उपमान, as it ought to Hence, this is a flaw,

 का .प्र reduces the flaw to भग्नप्रक्रम, the same as प्रक्रमभग a वाक्यदोष प्रक्रम=प्रकान्त अर्थ is molif, the aim that the poet has in view. When the poet's alm is not achieved, it is भग्नप्रक्रम दण्डिन् is Critical of such दोषऽ based on लिङ्गवचनभेद Or उपमानन्यूनल्व and अधिकत्व Says he—न लिङ्गवचने भिन्ने हीनताऽधिकतापि वा । उपामादूपणाद्याल यवोद्वेगो न धीमताम्॥( का द २ ५१) दण्डिन्'s view point seems just particularly with the saving clause यवोद्वेगो न धीमताम्॥Here also we must take into consideration poetic convention Thus लिङ्गवचनभेद may not be दोष in some cases, but will be so in others दण्डिन् admits this---अस्त्येव ..... प्रयागे वाग्विदा यथा। इसीव ..... सरासीवामल नभ। भर्तृभक्तो भट श्चेव ग्यद्योतो भाति भानुवत्॥ईदृश .... सद्भि। \[ का. द २५४-५५\]

 लिङ्गवचनभेदोऽपि etc साधारण धर्म अन्यरूप कुर्यात् i e अन्यरूप ie, असाधारण In मुख चन्द्र इव रम्य the धर्म, रम्य does not go with चन्द्र in the masculine and hence it becomes असाधारण एकतरस्यैव सद्धर्म-समन्वयावगते Since we find that only with one (viz the उपमेय) the af goes एकतरस्यैव VIz उपमेयस्यैव Because the साधारणधर्म in उपमा agrees with the उपमेय, \[ which is an indication of उपमेय being प्रधान in उपमा \] In मुख चन्द्र इव रम्य, रम्यं agrees with the उपमेय, मुख

 सविशेषणस्यैव तस्य उपमानत्वम् उपमेयत्वं वा प्रतीयमानेन धर्मेण प्रतीयते-The sentence must be construed as प्रतीयमानेन धर्मेण सविशेषणस्यैव तस्य उपमानत्व उपमेयत्व वा प्रतीयते। Since the धर्म, in such cases goes only with the उपमेय, we have to understand (प्रतीयमान) its counterpart such as would go with the उपमान and then only will the two be उपमान and उपमेय in the real sense of the term The idea is So long as the धर्म Is not साधारण, how can there be औपम्य, theoretically speaking ? And if there be no औपम्य, how could one thing be उपमेय and another उपमान ? So here to bring about औपम्य, we have to depend upon a धर्म understood (प्रतीयमान), not mentioned, and this involves प्रक्रमभग as the poet's aim is not fully realised (प्रकान्तस्य अर्थस्य स्फुट अनिर्वाहात्) प्रकान्त अर्थ the matter the poet aims at viz उपमानस्य उपमेयमस्य च समानधर्मसबध अनिर्वाह non realization

 चिन्तारत्न etc Illustrates लिङ्गभेददोष The laments of one who has lost something he carned in his hand that was very useful to him चिन्तारत्न= चिन्तामणि, a jewel credited with the powers of fulfilling the expectations of the आर्थिन्, करत च्युत shpped from the hand

 Here the उपमेय, त्व, is masculine as can be judged from च्युत, the ‘उपमान is चिन्तारत्न, neuter च्युत goes with त्व, but cannot agree with

 चिन्तारत्नम् Hence लिङ्गभेददोष in which is reducible toप्रक्रमभग Note that if the उपमान were चिन्तामनि (masculine), the दोष would not have arisen सक्तवो etc illustrates वचनभोद One says that he has eaten barley meal Construe देव, कुलवधूरिव शुद्धा सक्तव भक्षिता। कुलवधूरिव शुद्धा pure hke a wife of good family. सक्तव सक्तु m Barley mea।  

 Here सक्तव, the उपमेय, is in the plural, while the उपमान, वधू, is in
the singular \[There is लिङ्गभेद also But it is immaterial, because if the वचनभेद were not there, and we had बध्व in place of वधू, there would have ansen no दोष due to लिङ्गभेद, as शुद्धा is Nom PI of masculine as well as feminine forms\] This वचनभेद, has made शुद्धा agree with the उपमेय, सक्तव, only, not उपमान Hence वचनभेद becomes a दोष which is the same as भग्नप्रक्रम , acc to का प्र

 यत्र तु etc. But लिङ्गभेद and वचनभेद are not दोषऽ always They are दोषऽ only when the साधारणधर्म agrees in grammar with one, not both If, however, in spite of लिङ्गभेद and वचनभेद , the साधारणधर्म agrees gram matically with both the उपमान and उपमेय, then लिङ्गभेद and वचनभेद do not involve दोष

 सामान्याभिधायि पद् the word conveying theसाधारणधर्म स्वरूपभेद नापद्यते when there is no dive-rgence in grammatical form ie the same form may apply to the two genders or two numbers उभयथापि अस्य अनुगमक्षमस्वभावत्वात् As the साधारणधर्मपद is capable of being in concord with both viz. उपमान and उपमेय

 गुणैरनर्ध्यौ etc Illustrates how लिङअगभेद may not be a दोष one complimented on the score of his invaluable virtues

 Here ...., the साधारणधर्म, is in grammatical concord with the उपमान,रत्नै,and उफमेय,गुणी both, though रत्न is neuter and गुण masculine Hence no दोष art despite लिङ्गभेद.

 St 194 तद्वेषो etc Illustrates how वचनभेद may not be a दोष A fashionable lady complimented on the elegance of her dress and graces Construe— ......................... or Plu) Replete with elegance.......... amp (Sing or Plu) unlike those of other women

 Here...the.... is in singular. .... the .... is in plural Still, the साधारणधर्म's can agree with both. They are.... which may be singular of .... or plural of .... so also ...

may be Present 3rd per Sing of दध् IA to hold or 3rd per. Plu of धा to hold

 ।X-X-XI **कालभेददोष-पुरुषभेददोष विधिभेददोष** Just as the साधारणधर्म tends to be असाधारण, due to लिङ्गवचनभेद, so it becornes असाधारण, due to difference in Tense (काल), Person (पुरुष), and Mood (विधि), as apphed to the उपमान and उपमेय. This also comes under भग्नप्रक्रमदोष

 **पुरुष** i e 1st or 2nd, or 3rd person **विधि** a mood that enjoins like the Imperative or Potential or which merely states a thing, like the Indicative **विध्यादि** आदि Commentators interpret as referring to आशीर्वाद ( blessing) It may also refer to combinations of two or all of the three viz काल, पुरुष, विधि **नमिसाधु** (रु ११-२४) refers to काल-कारक विभक्तिभेदा। **न तथा प्रतीति** **अस्खलितरूपतया विश्रान्तिमासादयति** i e the understanding of idea does not become as free from abruptness, and complete as one would expect it to be-तथा “कोविन्द प्रसिद्धप्रकारेण मा च अस्खलितरूपतया As being free from abruptness, this is why it comes under प्रक्रमभग विश्रान्तिम् आसादयति=निरपेक्षा भवति be comes complete असौ अपि i e वाल पुरुष विधिभेददोष भग्नप्रक्रमतयैव व्याप्त Comes under भग्नप्रक्रम In short, कालादिभेद leaves some gap in our comprehension of the idea on hand and hence occasions प्रक्रमभग

 ** st 195 अतिथिं** etc Illustrates कालभेददाष कुमुद्रती bore a son, अतिथि by name, to कुश 2nd half construe पश्चिमात् यामिनीयानाच चेतना यथा प्रसाद (आप्नोति) तथा। **पश्चिमाद् यामिनीयामाद्** In the last watch of the night चेतना प्रसाद प्राप्नोति तथा After good sleep one's intellect becomes quite clear काकुत्स्थ Refers to कुश

 Here कुमुद्वती पुत्र आप is भूतकाल which does not apply to the उपमान, चेतना Because it is an everyday occurrence we require वर्तमान चेतना प्रमाद आप्नोति Hence कालभेददोष

 st 196 प्रत्यग्र etc Illustrates पुरुषभेददोष वासवदत्ता वासवदत्ता while worship ping cupid is compared to a creeper Construe-प्रयग्र, मूर्ति, कौत्सुम्भ,कान्ता, मकरचेतन अर्चयन्ती वालप्रवालविग्यप्रभवा लतेव विभ्राजसे। For the two meanings of प्रत्यग्य मूर्ति and कौस्तुभ कान्ता, see the Translation कौसुम्भ of कुसुम्भ m. saffron कौमुम्भ इव (कौसरस्य इव ) राग तेन रुचिरा च स्फुरद्भि अशुभि कान्ता च-as applied to लता अशु m filaments. (Read 'filaments for ‘pollen’ in Translation) कौसुम्भन रागेण रुचिर स्पुरन् च अशुकस्य अन्त यस्या as applied to वासवदत्ता प्रयग्य Recent, मज्जन (N) - Watering as applied to लता. Bath, as applied to वामवदत्ता बालप्रवालविटपप्रभवा applied to लता-That is the origin of tender sprouts and tendrils. A hint at the tenderness of बासवदत्ता's limbs.

 Here विभ्रातसे \[ 2nd person \] goes withत्व ( वासवदत्ता ), the उपमय It cannot go with the उपमान, लता which requires a third person. Hence पुरुषभेददोष

 अत्र etc इति सम्बोध्यमाननिष्टस्य परभागस्य The परभाग i e प्रत्ययभाग in विभ्रातसे as applying to ( निष्ठ) the party addressed (सबोध्यमान) The 2nd person termination in विभ्रातसे has to be modified (व्य-त्यास) into 3rd person in order to make it applicable to लता, the उपमान, that is not being addr-essed ( असबोध्यमानविषयतया ), and this gives rise to पुरुषभेददोष coming under भग्नप्रक्रम

 गङ्गेव प्रवहतु etc Illustrates विधिभेददोष. A blessing for continued fame Here the Imperative of प्रवहतु agrees with the उपमेय VIz कीर्तिं, but does not agree with the उपमान, गङ्गा Nobody can order the Ganges to flow Hence विधिभेददोष, Imperative being the विधि which does not suit the उपमान which requires the Indicative प्रवहति इति अप्रवृत्तप्रवर्तनामनविधे (भेद) Here the विधि of प्रवहततु applying to कीर्तिं, the उपमेय, which consists in bringing about something not actually in being (अप्रवृत्त) is different from the विधि viz indicative required for ग्गा, the उपमान अप्रवृत्तप्रवर्तन the कीर्तिं is yet to flow forth by the blessing. This thing is to be brought about in the case of the उपमेय The Ganges on the other hand is not to flow it is already flowing

 एवजातीयकस्य च अन्यस्य etc एवजातीयक मा. च Thinks that this refers to combination of काल, पुरुष, विधि—Read ‘गमिष्याम्युपहास्यता वामन इव इत्यादौ श्व क्वचित्काल्पुरपविध्यादियोगेनापि दोष स्यात्। उपमानग-तस्य असम्भवात् If it will not apply to the उपमान

 ननु समानम् (vi साक्षात् ) etc We saw how दण्डिन् maintained that लिङ्गवचनभेद is not enough to make an उपमा defective As against such views our author wants to urge that these भेदऽ do ca-use a gap in our comprehension of the idea on hand and hence should be looked upon as प्रक्रमभग.

 ननु सत्यवादी अयम् इति अर्थावगमात्। This sets forth the case of those who like दण्डिन्, would say न लि.वने भिन्ने..उपमादूषणाय अलम्,

 They might urge The poet's primary aim in उपमा is to connect the उपमेय with the particular धर्म, ( धनान्तर= य कश्चित् धर्म ) which may be expressly stated \[साक्षादुश्चारित Trvim Ed \] as in मुख चन्द्र इव रम्य or merely suggested \[प्रतीयमान\] as in मुख चन्द्र इव After this connection of the उपमेय with the धर्म is realised, the उपमा is concluded and then the difference in tense etc. are of no conse-quence (न कश्चित् .....) and hence

 they cannot be said to vitiate the उपमा After the धर्म is construed with the उपमेय it is by easy guess that we construe the धर्म with the उपमान, despite कालादिभेद. This leaves no room for any दोष

 How कालादिभेद, continues the objector, does not constitute a दोष will be clear from an instance. We usually say युधिष्ठिर इव अय सत्य वदति, where the साधारणधर्म is mentioned (उपात्त = उच्चारित )not suggested Here there is कालभेद But our understanding of the वाक्यार्थ is never marred by the कालभेद As soon as we have construed सत्य वदति with the उपमेय viz अय, by an easy guess we understand that the साधारणधर्म सत्यवादित्व and so the sentence युधिष्ठिर इव अय सत्य वदति is apprehended by us as युधिष्ठिर इव सत्यवादा अय सत्य वदति

 As against this, suggests the objector, it might be contended by some that in our attempt to disprove कालभेददोष, we have unwittingly exposed ourselves to another दोष viz पौनरुक्त्य, such as is evident in सत्यवादी सत्य वदति. But such a contention will be found to be untenable on closer examination सत्यवादी सत्य वदति involves no पौनरुक्त्य (Tautology) because there is the nice shade of meaning implicit in सत्यवादी It implies one who by nature is truth-speaking So with सत्यवादी सत्य वदति we get the idea that the person in question speaks the truth, not casually, but naturally. If the mere repetition of the same word were to land us into tautology व्याकरण would not have given its approval to णमुल्ऽ like पार्थसचार सचरति or रैपोष पुष्णाति There is a fine distinction between राय इव पुष्णाति and रैपोष पुष्णाति, because the latter So if there is no tautology (पौनरुक्त्य ) in रैपोष पुष्णाति , there is likewise none in सत्यवादी सत्य वदति— argues the objector

 सत्यमेवैतत् etc The author sticks to his view point that कालादिभेद does involve a दोष

 He says Whatever the objector says about रैपोष पुष्णाति and such other usages is indeed true The objector is quite justified says the author, in saying that रैपोष पुष्णाति involves no पौनरुक्त्य, णमुल्ऽ and such other forms are usages that have come to stay (स्थित) and hence have to be justified But this justification which may be cogent for usages that have come to stay (स्थितेषु प्रयोदेषु) in language will not be entirely apt ( सर्वथा निरवद्य) in the case of कालदिभेद in उपमा. Because men of taste do admit (सचेतन एव अत्र प्रमाणम्) that there is a gap in our comprehension of the idea on hand due to कालादिभेद \[ प्रस्तुतवस्तुप्रतीतिन्या-(वि)वातात्). Hence कालादिभेद do vitiate a काव्य, says the का प्र \[ Read

 सत्यम् एवमेतत्। किन्तु स्थितेषु प्रयोगेषु समर्थनमिद, न तु सर्वथा निरवद्य प्रस्तुतवस्तुप्रतीतिव्या (v i वि ) घातात् इति सचेतम एव अत्र प्रमाणम्। \]

 Note that स्थितेषु प्रयोगेषु refers only to case hke रैपोष पुष्णाति It does not apply to युधिष्ठिर इवाय सत्य वदति

 Note that लिङ्गभेद, वचनभेद, कालभेद, विधिभेद are दोषऽ because they make the साधारणधर्म असाधा-रण, in so far as it agrees with the उपमेय only

 X॥ असादृश्यदोष anses when there is no resemblance whatsoever between the उपमेय and the proposed उपमान This is brought under अनुचितार्थत्वदोष

 ग्रन्थामि etc Illustrates असादृश्य One sets about witing a poem which is to him like the moon dif-fusing the rays of its meaning

 Here the काव्य and its अर्थ are respectively compared to शशिन् and रश्मिऽ But, says our author such comparison is unwarranted as nowhere is there सादृश्य between them The author says so perhaps in the light of poetic convention

 Xiii असभवदोष This is the last उपमादोष It anses where the proposed उपमान is such as is improbable In असभव, the thing given as उपमान is So impossible that it appears absurd to state it, and this is enough to distinguish असभव from अतिशयोक्ति अतिशयोक्ति in its most extravagant forms never becomes absurd असभव , on the other hand, is grotesquely absurd Says भामह २ ५० यस्यातिशयवानर्थं कथ सोऽसम्भबो मत। This असभवदोष reduces itself to अनुचिताथत्व, acc to the का प्र

 St 197 निपेतुरास्या etc Illustrates उपमानस्य असभवदोष The impression left by an archer shooting sharp Construe.—दिनार्धमान परिवेषिग अर्कात् नाज्वल्यमाना वारिधारा एव धनुर्मण्डलमध्यभान तस्य आस्या-दिव दीप्ता शरानिपेतु \। दिनार्धभाज’ अर्कात् from the sun when it is on the merrdian, at midday परिवेषिण Gen Sing परिवेषिण् having a परिवष Ie halo of of lustre round it जाज्वल्यमाना वारिधारा Burning jets of water धनुर्मण्डलमध्यभाज adj of आस्यात्The face stood at the centre of the extended bow आस्यादिव The arrows seemed to proceed from the mouth itself, as the mouth was at the centre of the bow

 Burning tets of water mentioned here as उपमान are impossible, and equally impossible is their emergence from the orb of the sun. Heace this which is stated to be असभवदोष, ts the sa-me as अनुचितार्थत्व उपनिवध्यमान अर्थ अनौचित्यमव पुष्णाति Ths matter which is being stated tends to occasion the flaw of impropriety itself

**२-उत्प्रेक्षादोषऽ (२)**

 (1) The उत्प्रेक्षा is vitiated if in place of इव the poet uses यथा. This is due to the fact that the po-etical fancy implicit in उत्प्रेक्षा can be conveyed only by इव, ध्रुव, मन्ये etc, यथा is incapable of conveying that element of fancy. Hence, when instead of इव or ध्रुव etc. the poet uses यथा, there results a defect which is none other than अवाचकत्व. अवाचकत्व is a पददोष. It anses when a पद is incapable of conveying the idea it is meant to convey.

 सम्भावन Vide Notes pp. 34-35 ध्रुवेवादयः See the list of उपेत्क्षावाचकऽ gven at p. 33 Notes. The idea of सम्भावन can be conveyed by इव, ध्रुव etc., not by यथा यथा is capable of conveying साधर्म्य only and not सम्भावन. \[ दण्डिन् makes a useful list of उपमावाचकऽ \[ का. द. २,५७-६५.\]:— इव वत् वा यथाशब्दाः समान-निभ-सन्निभाः। तुल्य सकाश नीकाश प्रकाश-प्रतिरूपकाः॥प्रतिद्वन्दि-प्रत्यनीक-विरोधिन। सदूक्-सदृश-मवादि-सनादि-सनानीय-अनुवादिनः॥प्रतिबिम्व-प्रतिच्छन्द्र-सुरुप-सम-समिता। सलक्षण्-सुदक्ष-आय-सुरक्ष-उपमित-उपमाः॥कल्प-देशीय देश्यादिः प्रख्य प्रतिनिधी अपि। सवर्ण-तुलिणौ शब्दौ ये चान्यूनार्थवादिन॥समासश्च बहुव्रीहिः शशाङ्कवदनादिषु। स्पर्धन जयति द्वेष्टि द्रुह्यति प्रतिगर्जति॥आक्रोशति आवजानातिय कदर्थयति निन्दति।विडम्बयति सधत्ते इमनि ईर्ध्यति असूयति॥ तस्य मुष्णाति सौभाग्य भाग्य तस्य कान्ति वि...। तेन सार्धं विगृह्णानि, तुलां तेनाधिरोहति॥तत्पदव्या पद धत्ते, तस्य कक्षा विगाहते । नमन्वेति, अनुरध्नाति, तच्छील तन्निधेयति॥तनिषेति॥तस्य चानुकरोनीति शब्दाः सादृश्यमूलका। उपमायामिमै प्रोक्ता कवीनां बुद्धिसौख्यदा ॥\] This difference in connotative powers is due to शब्दशक्तिमहिमा only. one word possesses a power wiuch another may not possess, तस्य=साधर्म्यस्य. अस्यां= उत्पक्षाया In उत्प्रेक्षा, the poet's aum is not to convey साधर्म्य but सम्भवना Perhaps it would be truer to say that in उत्पेक्षा, the poet's aim is to convey साधर्म्य by सम्भावन. तत्र अस्य अशक्तिः सम्भावने यथापदस्य अशक्ति Incapacity of यथा to convey सम्भावना अवाचकत्वं दोषः thus सम्भावने यथापदस्य अशक्ति is the same as the defect ......

 St. 198. तद्ययौ etc. Illustrates उत्पेक्षादोष due to the use of यथा instead of इव Lotus bud coming out of a fotus pond fancied as shrinking in fear of the charm of a lady's eye मेघ...... a blue lotus. मुकुलं yet a bud and hence closed. This becomes the basis of the उत्पेक्षा in the 2nd half. नारी etc. which seemed to have shrunk from fear of the charm (..... ) of a lady's eye

 In the 2nd half we get a हेतुत्प्रेक्षा due to the ...... closing of the bud being attributed to the fanc-ied हेतु viz. ........... Here we require "........ इव in place of “....... यथा Since यथा does not connote सम्भावन but सादृश्य, here we have ....  


 Here the attribute (करै स्पृशति) and the genders ( व्यक्ति ), masculine and ferminine, of the तिग्मरुचि and ककुभ्ऽ are transparent enough to suggest that they are compared to a नायक and नायिका so also the विशेषणऽ विजृम्भिततापया, अतनुमानपरिग्रहया and चिरयाऽपि रुचिरया and the femini-ne gender of दिनश्री are transparent enough to suggest that दिनश्री is compared to a प्रतिनायिका When the प्रतिनायिकात्व of दिनश्री IS thus suggested why make use of दयितया इव in the श्लोक? This becomes अपुष्टार्थता or पुनरुक्ततादोष व्यक्तिविशेष=लिङ्गविशेष cp कालो व्यक्तिः स्वरादय p 66 उ॥. व्यक्तिः Suggestion (as नायक and नायिका) किं स्वशब्दोपादानेन What is the point in employing the wond for itself ( स्वशब्द ) when it is suggested ?

 श्लेषोपमाया etc An objector mught urge—Why not call सशति etc as श्रेषोपना–उपमा based on श्लेष In श्लेषोपमा, the उपमान has to be stated, and thus requirement is satisfied by the use of दयितयेव, which therefore involves no दोष

 Our author replies that स्पृशति etc cannot be श्लेषोपमा Because, in श्लेषोपमा the attributes are not transparent enough to suggest the उपमान, which hence has to be stated In स्पृशति etc on the other hand the attributes are transparent enough to suggest the उपमान which, hence, requ-ires no mention

 यत्र etc. उपमानस्य उपादानमन्तरेण Unless the उपमाT i e. अप्रकृत is mentioned साधारणेष्वपि विशेष-णेषु As in समासोक्ति, in श्लेषोपमा the विशेषणऽ being श्लेष apply both to the उपमान and उपमेय But still they are not transparent enough to suggest the उपमान as in समामोति न तथा प्रतीति = न समा-सोक्तौ इव उपमानस्य प्रतीति

 st 201 स्वयं च etc. illustrates श्लेषोपमा गौरी compared to morning-twilight स्वयं च i e. You are great not only because your father, हिमालय, is great but also because you yourself are पल्लवाताग्र भास्वष्करविराजिता (1) Shining with your hands that are bnght (भास्वत्) and ruddy like sprouts -as applied to ﬁt (11) Shining with the rays of the sun (भास्वत् ) that are ruddy like sprouts-as apphed to प्रमधसन्ध्या अस्वापफललुब्धेहितप्रदा (।) Grving the desired object (ईहितप्रदा) to those wra banker after (लुब्ध ) fruts not easy to obtain (भ+सु+अ.प.)-of गौरौ (॥ ) Fulfilling the craving of those that want (क्त) to be free from sleep (अ+स्वप) i e bringing sleep to an end— of प्रभातसन्ध्या The stanza occurs in उद्भट ४१५ pence at must be from his ...—कुमारसम्भव not to be confused with...... s कुमारसम्भव

 Here the विशेषणऽ, पल्लवाताम्र etc and अस्वाप etc though श्लिष्ट and applying to both viz गौरौ the उपमेय, and प्रभातसन्ध्या, the उपमान, are not transparent enough to suggest the उपमान ,प्रभातसन्ध्या , which, therefore, has to be mentioned and is mentioned This is, therefore श्लेषोपमा not समासोक्ति

**४- अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसादोष (१)**

 If in समासोक्ति, the mention of the अकृत becomes a दोष, in अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा, the mention of the प्रकृत becomes a दोष for the same reasons When we understand how समासोक्ति and अप्रस्तुतप्रशं-सा are practically identical in form \[Vide Notes p 91\], it is not difficult to understand the genesis of this दोष If in समासोक्ति was, the अप्रकृत IS suggested by the transparent विशेषणऽ, in, अप्रस्तुतप्र-शंसा the प्रकृत (=उपमेय ) is suggested by the विशेषणऽ Hence just as, in समासोक्ति, the mention of the अप्रकृत constitutes अपुष्टार्थना or पौन्यरुक्त्यऽo the mention of the प्रकृत, in अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसा leads to अपुष्टार्थना or पौन्यरुक्त्य.

 अप्रस्तुतप्रशंसायामपि etc. अनयैव रीत्या प्रतीतं i e suggested as in समासोक्कि, from the transparent विशेषणऽ In समासोक्ति, उपमान is suggested; in अ प्र उपमेय is suggested पुन प्रयोगेण at By menti-oning it over again when it is already suggested न कदर्थता नेयम् उपमेय should not be made fuule (कदर्थता मी) by being mentioned over again That is, it will involve अपुष्टार्थतादोष or पुनरुक्तादोष

 st 202 आहूतेषु etc Illustrates अप्रस्तुनप्रशसाया प्रस्तुतस्य उपादानात् दोष As Genus embraces the worthy and worthless alike, so the undis criminating patron patronises the worthy and worthless alike. a None can prevent the fly if it comes forward as a विहगम, when विहगमऽ are called out In other words विहगम is a class that comprehends worthy birds and the worthless fly also The fly with its wings can claim to be a विहगम b The तृणमणि (7), abiding in the रत्नाकर ( वारिधि), claims the glory of jewels. The class of jewels comprehends the worthy diamond and also the worthless तृणमनि What is तृणमनि? One thing is certain that it is a worthless substance. Does तृण here convey the idea of contempt implicit in the word (ep तृणेन कार्य भवताश्चाराणाम्) तृणमनि being a worthless मनि? स प्र interprets तृणमनि as .... मा चं says तृणप्राय मणि अप्रधान .... This conveys the idea of worthlessness, but what about मध्येपरिधि वसन्? Is it coral as उद्योत Says? e Fven the glow worm is not afraid to move in the midst of things luminous. That is, the class of तेजस्विन् things compreherds the most emment luminaries like the sun and also the insignificant ...

 (॥) The उत्प्रेक्षा may be vitiated if in support of an उत्प्रेक्षा, an अर्थान्तरन्यास is given This is due to the fact that उत्प्रेक्षा being only a poctical fancy has no reality about it, and when there is no reality, why should there be any attempt at corroboration in the form of अर्थान्तरन्यास? So an अर्थान्तरन्यास has no room in an and, therefore, its employment there is अनुचितार्थता Imp-ropriety

 उत्प्रेक्षितमपि whatever is poetically fancied तात्विकेन रूपेण परिवर्जितत्वात् Because such fancied things have no reality of form They are “cobwebs of poetical fancy” निरुपाख्यप्रख्यं निरुपाख्य nonexistent, अमत्य \[उपाख्य observable \] निरपाख्यप्रख्य=निरुपाख्यतुल्य \[ प्रख्य appearing like.\]. तत्सम-र्थनाय etc. The उत्प्रेक्षित being imaginary and unreal should not be corroborated An attempt at corroboration is extremely improper like an attempt to sketch a portrait in the air \[cp. चित्र निर्माति सुन्दर पवने \] समीचीन \[from सम् + √ अञ्च\] proper, fit cp सम्यक् निर्विपयन्त्र एतस्य Absence of scope for this viz अर्थान्तरन्यास Absence of scope for thus is the same as अनुचितार्थता That is, अर्थान्तरन्यास, in support of उत्प्रेक्षा results in the दोष, अनुचितार्थता

 st 199 दिवाकरात् etc. illustrates the निर्विषयत्वदोष of अर्थान्तरन्यास in support of उत्प्रेक्षा The caves of the Himalayas are impenetrable to the rays of the sun Construe—य दिवा भीत व गुहासु लौन अन्धकार दिवाकरात् रक्षति। नून उच्चै शिरसा शरण प्रपन्न क्षुद्रेऽपि अतीव ममत्वम्। Who protects from the Sun Darkness that hes hidden in his caves during day time उच्चै शिरस् The lofty ones. As applied to the हिमालय, whose peak is lofty

 Here darkness is imagined to be भीत \[भीतमिव\] and taking shelter in the caves. When भय is imaginary, the act of taking shelter also becomes imaginary Hence an attempt to corroborate it by अर्थान्तरन्यास is nothing short of futile.

 अत्र अचेतनस्य etc. तमस् being अ.. cannot be at all afraid of the sun Much less could there be any protection by the पर्वत, which (protection) is a sequel to भय \[तत्प्रयोजित=......\] When such fear and protection present themselves in an imaginary form (मेधाविन रूप), there cannot be any impropriety Because, what could be improper when based on pure imagination? Hence it is futile to make an attempt to corroborate it i c. परिव्रण, by having recourse to an अर्थान्तरन्या-स \[............परित्राणसमर्थना \].

 Note that we get उत्प्रेक्षा only when we read .... भीतमिव as two separate words. If we take them together, they will mean ‘an owl. Then the idea being who protects Darkness hiding in his caves

like an owl’, there will be no उत्प्रेक्षा \[Read ग्रिवा भीतमिव as two separate words, in the Text Read in the Translation for sheltering by the mountain’, “sheltering by the mountain as a sequel to it (i e.भय )”

३—समासोक्तिदोष ( १ )

 In समासोक्ति we saw how the विशेषणऽ are transparent enough to suggest the अप्रकृत When the अप्रकृत can be suggested by the विशेषणऽ. it is unnecessary to mention it Wherever the is अप्रकृत thus mentioned in समासोक्ति, the unnecessary mention results in अपुष्टार्थस्य or पुनरुक्तता अपुष्टार्थत्व we have already seen while discussing अनुप्रासस्य वैकल्य पुनरुक्तता, an अर्थदोष, is Tautology, Repetition. The mention of the अप्रकृत results in अपुष्टार्थत्व in so far as it has no contri-bution to make to the significance of the वाक्य the वाक्य being complete even without it.The fault becomes पुनरुक्तता in so far as the अप्रकृत which is already suggested is expressed again Between अपुष्टार्थत्व and पुनरुक्तता the difference lies in the fact that the former is irrelevant, the latter tautologous.The former arises even where the thing is mentioned once, if it does not bear upon the matter on hand, the latter arises only when there is repetition of the same idea

 साधारणविशेषणवशादेव etc (Vide Notes p 74) The adjectives or attributes are transparent enou-gh to suggest the अप्रकृत, though it is not mentioned उपमान। e अप्रकृत तस्य उपमानम्य अत्र समासो-क्तौ प्रयोजना भावात् अनुपादेयता Its mention is improper as it is uncalled for Construe—प्रयोजनाभावात् यत् अनुपादेयता तत् अपुष्टार्थत्व पुनरुक्तता वा दोष भवति।

 st 200 स्पृशति etc illustrates समासोक्तौ अप्रकृतस्य उपादानात् दोष Description of summer construe तिग्मरुचौ कुकुभ ...स्पृशति, विजृम्भिततापया, अननुमानपरिग्रहया चिरयाऽपि रुचिरया, दिनाश्रिया दयितया इव स्थितम्। तिग्मरुचै कुकुभः करै स्पृशति—Locative Absolute. When the sun touched the quarters with its rays. Note how the attribute is transparent enough with the Feminine of कुकुभः, with the Mas. of तिग्मरुचि and with the pun on कर, to suggest a नायक touching with his hands (कर) the नायिका विजृम्भिततापया (1) the warmth of which (दिनश्री) had become more intense, (॥) whose (of the प्रतिनायिका) annoyance had been aggravated अननुमानपरिग्रहया (1) which (दिनश्री) had assum-ed a great length (the days in summer are long), (॥) whose (of the प्रतिनायिका) मान or huffiness was great fenest विरयाऽपि रुचिरया (1) which (दिनश्री) was bright though long (विरा), (॥) who (प्रतिनायिका) though of a long standing was yet charming. (The general rule being नव वस्तु रुचिर भवति। Here though the नायिका was not नवपरिणीत, she was yet .....\]

d Fie upon the Genus which like a patron ( प्रभु इव ), does not take cognizance of (अनामृष्ट) disti-nction between things ( तत्त्वान्तर ), and which is non-sentient (अचेतन ) like the patron who is ins-ensible (अचेतन )

 Here the mention of प्रभु इव, the प्रवृत्त, is superfluous. Because such insensible ( अचेतन=अवि-वेकिन्) lord is suggested by the अप्रस्तुत सामान्य distinguished (विशिष्ट ) by the same attributes, अचेतनत्व and अनामृष्टतत्त्वान्तरत्वअप्रस्तुत etc. अप्रस्तुत यत् ( अचेननत्व) (अनामृष्टतत्त्वान्तरत्व) विशिष्ट सामान्य तद्द्वारेण अभिव्यक्ति Suggestion So प्रभु इव 15 पुनरक्त or अपुष्टार्थ

 It should not be supposed that the का प्र is here stating something unlikely. Let us imagine the sentence to be धिक् सामान्य अचेतन अनामृष्टतत्त्वान्तरम् Here from सामान्य which comes to be conde-mned for अचेतनत्व and अनामृष्यत्वान्तरत्व, it is indeed by an easy guess that one gets at the idea of an अचेतन प्रभु For, we ask ourselves where is the point in the poet pouring scorn on the सामा-न्य ? So when the poet does like that, it must be with an eye upon something else-the प्रभूत and that प्रभूत a सहृदय can well judge to be प्रभु For अतिवेकित्व is a usual point of grievance against प्रभुऽ

 तदेते etc. अयेऽप्येवनातीयका Other defects also of this hind read उद्योत पूर्वोत्तयैव दोषताया अन्तर्भाविता Can come under the दोषऽ given already in उ VII

 सपूर्णमिद वाच्यलक्षणम् We have seen in उ I that the Ten Ullasas of the काव्यप्रकाश are devoted to a detailed explanation of the definition of चाव्य given by मम्मट in उ I—तददोषौ शब्दार्थौ सगु-णावनलङ्कृती पुन कापि। इत्यष मार्गो etc. Concluding stanza प्रदीप उद्योत, प्रभा सुधासागर of भीमसेन take no cognizance of it. But all others including the very old commentators रुय्यक ( सङ्केत- Appendix A) and माणिक्यचन्न्द्र comment upon it What is more significant is that रुय्यक and मा चं are among those who see in the stanza a sly reference to the double authorship of the काव्यप्रकारा रुय्यक says एतेन च महामनीनां प्रवरतराहेतुरेष ग्रन्थो प्रवतृना अनेन वयमपि असमप्तत्वात् परेण च ...................... \[ Vide Appendix A-p 7\] मा च says— अध च अयं ग्रन्थ ग्रन्थेन आरब्ध परेण समाषित इति..................................। सप्रदायप्रकाशिनी refers to ........... as the author who completed मन्मथऽ \[=मम्मट\]। निदर्शना a tributed the portion beginning with ....................... from all these references two facts emerge Firstly that  


the view about double authorship of the का प्र is as old as रूय्यक (a contemporary of मम्मट) and माणिक्यचद्र who was separated from मम्मट by no more than about fifty years And secondly, it is निदर्शना that specifies the portions going to the credit ofमम्मट and अत’

 All commentators are at one in holding that primarily the stanza conveys the idea that in theवाक्यप्रकाश, the author (or authors) have succeeded in presenting the divergent view ponts ( विदुषां विभिन्न अपि मार्गं ) In भस्कारशास्त्र as one organic whole (अभिन्नरूप), and that this is entirely due to the happy fusion (सम्यग् विनिर्मिता सघटना) of the different view points Construe ष्ण विदुर्षां निमित्त अनि मार्ग यत् अभिन्नरूप प्रति मागो, तद् न विचित्रम्। यद् अमुत्र सम्यग् विनिर्मिता समना एवं हेतु (भवति )। एष विदुषां विभिन्नं मार्गं Ref to the divergent schools in Sanskrit Poetics Vide Introduction pp x xii Read the remarks made onमम्मट’s definition of क्तव्य Notes on उधृत्य I pp. 17-19 अभिन्नरूप यत् प्रति भासते Certainly the claim is justified There is an organic unity about the work. It does not present a motley assortment of diver gent views सम्यग् विनिर्मिता सघटना The different views have been happily blended

 As said above, there may be a secondary and sly reference to double authorship also Then विदुषां मार्ग would refer to the काव्यप्रकाश which is the ‘rath’ to the wise leading to the अपारशास्वज्ञान विभिन्न अरि अभिग्नरूप will then mean which though vaned because of double authorship, still appears as one सम्यग् विनिर्भिता सघटना will refer to भट्टऽ and मम्मटऽ contributions having been well fused together in an organic whole  


**\।\। शुभ भूयात् \।\।**

\]
